1_26_19: Also Isaac's servants dug in the valley and found a well of fresh spring water there.
1_26_20: But the herdsmen of Gerar quarreled with Isaac's herdsmen, saying, "The water is ours," so he called the name of the well Esek, which means contention, because they argued with him.
1_26_21: Then they dug another well, and they quarreled over that one also, so he named it Sitnah.
1_26_22: And he moved from there and dug another well, and they did not quarrel over it, so he called its name Rehoboth, saying, "For now at last the LORD has made room for us, and we shall be fruitful in the land."
1_26_23: Then he went up from there to Beersheba.
1_26_24: And the LORD appeared to him the same night and said, "I am the God of your father Abraham; do not be afraid, for I am with you. I will bless you and multiply your offspring for My servant Abraham's sake."
1_26_25: So he built an altar there and called upon the name of the LORD, and he pitched his tent there; and there Isaac's servants dug a well.
1_26_26: Then Abimelech came to him from Gerar with Ahuzzath his adviser, and Phichol the commander of his army.
1_26_27: And Isaac said to them, "Why have you come to me, seeing that you hate me and have sent me away from you?"
1_26_28: They said, "We have clearly seen that the LORD is with you, so we said, 'Let there now be a sworn oath between us, between you and us, and let us make a covenant with you.
1_26_29: that you will do us no harm, just as we have not touched you, and since we have done nothing to you but good and have sent you away in peace. You are now the blessed of the LORD.
1_26_30: So he prepared a feast for them, and they ate and drank together.
1_26_31: Then they arose early in the morning and exchanged oaths with one another; and Isaac sent them on their way, and they departed from him in peace.
1_26_32: That same day Isaac's servants came and told him about the well they had dug, saying to him, "We have found water."
1_26_33: So he called it Shebah, therefore the name of the city is still Beersheba to this day.
1_26_34: When Esau was forty years old, he married Judith the daughter of Beeri the Hittite, and Basemath the daughter of Elon the Hittite.
1_26_35: And they were a grief of spirit to Isaac and Rebekah.
1_27_1: Now it came to pass, when Isaac was old and his eyes were so weak that he could not see, that he called Esau his older son and said to him, "My son." And he answered him, "Here I am."
1_27_2: Then he said, "Behold now, I am old and I do not know when I may die.
1_27_3: Now therefore, please take your weapons, your quiver and your bow, and go out to the field and hunt some wild game for me.
1_27_4: And make me delicious food, such as I love, and bring it to me that I may eat, that my soul may bless you before I die.
1_27_5: Now Rebekah was listening when Isaac spoke to Esau his son, and Esau went to the field to hunt for game and bring it home.
1_27_6: So Rebekah spoke to Jacob her son, saying, "Listen, I overheard your father speak to Esau your brother, saying,
1_27_7: Bring me game and prepare delicious food for me, that I may eat it and bless you in the presence of the LORD before I die.
1_27_8: Now therefore, my son, listen to me and obey my voice according to what I command you.
1_27_9: Go now to the flock and bring me from there two fine young goats, and I will make delicious food from them for your father, such as he loves.
1_27_10: Then you shall bring it to your father, that he may eat it, and that he may bless you before he dies.
1_27_11: And Jacob said to Rebekah his mother, "Look, Esau my brother is a hairy man, and I am a man with smooth skin.
1_27_12: Perhaps my father will touch me, and I shall seem to be a deceiver to him, and I will bring a curse upon myself and not a blessing.
1_27_13: But his mother said to him, "Let your curse fall on me, my son; only obey my voice, and go, get them for me."
1_27_14: So he went and got them and brought them to his mother, and his mother prepared savory food, just the way his father loved.
1_27_15: Then Rebekah took the best clothes of her older son Esau, which were with her in the house, and put them on Jacob her younger son.
1_27_16: And she put the skins of the young goats on his hands and on the smooth part of his neck.
1_27_17: Then she gave the delicious food and the bread, which she had prepared, into the hand of her son Jacob.
1_27_18: So he came to his father and said, "My father." And he said, "Here I am. Who are you, my son?"
1_27_19: Jacob said to his father, "I am Esau your firstborn; I have done as you told me; please sit up and eat some of my game, that your soul may bless me."
1_27_20: But Isaac said to his son, "How is it that you have found it so quickly, my son?" And he said, "Because the LORD your God granted me success."
1_27_21: Isaac said to Jacob, "Please come near, that I may touch you, my son, to know whether you are really my son Esau or not."
1_27_22: So Jacob went near to Isaac his father, and he felt him and said, "The voice is Jacob's voice, but the hands are the hands of Esau."
1_27_23: And he did not recognize him, because his hands felt hairy like his brother Esau's hands; so he blessed him.
1_27_24: Then he said, "Are you really my son Esau?" And he answered, "I am."
1_27_25: He said, "Bring it near to me, and I will eat of my son's game, that I may bless you." So he brought it near to him, and he ate; and he brought him wine, and he drank.
1_27_26: Then his father Isaac said to him, "Please come near now and kiss me, my son."
1_27_27: So he came near and kissed him, and when Isaac smelled the smell of his garments he blessed him and said, "Ah, the smell of my son is like the smell of a field which the LORD has blessed!"
1_27_28: Therefore may God give you of the dew of heaven, of the richness of the earth, and an abundance of grain and new wine.
1_27_29: Let peoples serve you and nations bow down to you, be master over your brothers and let your mother's sons bow down to you, cursed be everyone who curses you and blessed be those who bless you.
1_27_30: Now it happened, as soon as Isaac had finished blessing Jacob, and Jacob had barely gone out from the presence of Isaac his father, that Esau his brother returned from his hunting.
1_27_31: He also had made delicious food and brought it to his father, and said to his father, "Let my father arise and eat of his son's game, that your soul may bless me."
1_27_32: And his father Isaac said to him, "Who are you?" He answered, "I am your son, your firstborn, Esau."
1_27_33: Then Isaac trembled violently and said, "Who was it then that hunted game and brought it to me? I ate it all before you came, and I have blessed him—and indeed he shall be blessed."
1_27_34: When Esau heard the words of his father, he cried out with an exceedingly great and bitter cry, and said to his father, "Bless me, even me also, O my father!"
1_27_35: But he said, "Your brother came deceitfully and has taken away your blessing."
1_27_36: Esau said, "Is he not rightly named Jacob, for he has cheated me these two times—he took away my birthright, and now look, he has taken away my blessing!" Then he said, "Have you not reserved a blessing for me?"
1_27_37: Then Isaac answered and said to Esau, "Indeed I have made him your master, and all his brothers I have given to him as servants; with grain and wine I have sustained him. What then can I do for you, my son?"
1_27_38: And Esau said to his father, "Do you have only one blessing, my father? Bless me—me also, O my father!" And Esau lifted up his voice and wept.
1_27_39: Then Isaac his father answered and said to him, "Behold, away from the richness of the earth shall your dwelling be, and away from the dew of heaven from above.
1_27_40: By your sword you shall live, and you shall serve your brother; and it shall come to pass, when you grow restless, that you shall break his yoke from your neck.
1_27_41: So Esau bore a grudge against Jacob because of the blessing with which his father blessed him, and Esau said in his heart, "The days of mourning for my father are approaching; then I will kill my brother Jacob."
1_27_42: And when the words of Esau her older son were reported to Rebekah, she sent and called Jacob her younger son, and said to him, "Listen, your brother Esau is consoling himself concerning you by plotting to kill you."
1_27_43: Now therefore, my son, listen carefully to my voice: arise, flee to my brother Laban in Haran.
1_27_44: And stay with him for a few days, until your brother's fury subsides.
1_27_45: Until your brother's anger turns away from you and he forgets what you have done to him, then I will send and bring you back from there. Why should I lose both of you in one day?
1_27_46: Rebekah said to Isaac, "I loathe my life because of the daughters of Heth; if Jacob marries a wife of the daughters of Heth, like these who are the daughters of the land, what good will my life be to me?"
1_28_1: Then Isaac called Jacob and blessed him, and commanded him, saying, "You must not take a wife from the daughters of Canaan.
1_28_2: Arise, go at once to Padan Aram, to the house of Bethuel your mother's father, and take yourself a wife from there from the daughters of Laban your mother's brother.
1_28_3: May God Almighty bless you and make you fruitful and multiply you, that you may become an assembly of peoples.
1_28_4: And may He give you the blessing of Abraham, to you and your descendants with you, that you may possess the land where you are living as a foreigner, which God gave to Abraham.
1_28_5: So Isaac sent Jacob away, and he went to Paddan-aram to Laban the son of Bethuel the Aramean, the brother of Rebekah, the mother of Jacob and Esau.
1_28_6: Esau saw that Isaac had blessed Jacob and sent him away to Paddan Aram to take himself a wife from there, and that as he blessed him he commanded him, saying, "You shall not take a wife from the daughters of Canaan."
1_28_7: and that Jacob had listened to his father and his mother and had gone to Paddan Aram.
1_28_8: Also Esau realized that the daughters of Canaan did not please his father Isaac.
1_28_9: So Esau went to Ishmael and married Mahalath the daughter of Ishmael, Abraham's son, the sister of Nebajoth, in addition to the wives he already had.
1_28_10: Now Jacob departed from Beersheba and traveled toward Haran.
1_28_11: So he came to a certain place and spent the night there because the sun had set, and he took one of the stones of the place and put it under his head and lay down in that place to sleep.
1_28_12: Then he dreamed, and behold, a stairway was set up on the earth, and its top reached to heaven; and there the angels of God were ascending and descending on it.
1_28_13: And behold, the LORD stood above it and said, "I am the LORD, the God of your father Abraham and the God of Isaac; the land on which you lie I will give to you and your descendants.
1_28_14: Your descendants shall be as numerous as the dust of the earth; you shall spread abroad to the west and the east, to the north and the south; and in you and in your seed all the families of the earth shall be blessed.
1_28_15: Behold, I am with you and will watch over you wherever you go, and will bring you back to this land; for I will not leave you until I have done what I have promised you.
1_28_16: Then Jacob awoke from his sleep and said, "Surely the LORD is in this place, and I was not even aware of it."
1_28_17: And he was afraid and said, "What an awesome place this is! This is none other than the house of God, and this is the very gate of heaven!"
1_28_18: Then Jacob rose early in the morning, and took the stone that he had put under his head, set it up as a memorial pillar, and poured oil on top of it.
1_28_19: And he called the name of that place Bethel, which means "house of God," but the name of that city had been Luz previously.
1_28_20: Then Jacob made a vow, saying, "If God will indeed be with me and keep me on this journey that I am going, and give me bread to eat and clothing to wear,
1_28_21: so that I return safely to my father's house in peace, then the LORD shall be my God.
1_28_22: And this stone which I have set up as a pillar shall be God's house, and of all that You give me I will surely give a tenth back to You.
1_29_1: So Jacob went on his journey and came to the land of the people of the east.
1_29_2: And he looked, and saw a well in the field, and behold, there were three flocks of sheep lying beside it, for out of that well they watered the flocks, and a large stone covered the well's mouth.
1_29_3: When all the flocks were gathered there, the shepherds would roll the stone from the well's mouth, water the sheep, and put the stone back in its place on the well's mouth.
1_29_4: And Jacob said to them, "My brothers, where do you come from?" And they said, "We are from Haran."
1_29_5: Then he said to them, "Do you know a man named Laban the son of Nahor?" And they said, "We know him."
1_29_6: So he said to them, "Is he well?" And they said, "He is well, and here is his daughter Rachel coming with the sheep."
1_29_7: Then he said, "Look, it is still broad daylight; it is not time for the livestock to be gathered together. Water the sheep, and go and pasture them."
1_29_8: But they said, "We cannot until all the flocks are gathered together, and the stone is rolled from the mouth of the well; then we water the sheep."
1_29_9: Now while he was still talking with them, Rachel arrived with her father's sheep, for she was a shepherdess.
1_29_10: As soon as Jacob saw Rachel the daughter of Laban his mother's brother, and the sheep of Laban his mother's brother, Jacob went near and rolled the stone from the well's mouth and watered the flock of Laban his mother's brother.
1_29_11: Then Jacob kissed Rachel, and lifted up his voice and wept aloud.
1_29_12: And Jacob told Rachel that he was her father's relative and that he was Rebekah's son, so she quickly ran and told her father.
1_29_13: When Laban heard the news about Jacob his sister's son, he ran to meet him and embraced him and kissed him and brought him to his house, and Jacob told Laban all these things.
1_29_14: And Laban said to him, "Surely you are my own bone and flesh," and he stayed with him for about a month.
1_29_15: Then Laban said to Jacob, "Because you are my relative, should you therefore work for me for nothing? Tell me, what should your wages be?"
1_29_16: Now Laban had two daughters: the name of the older was Leah, and the name of the younger was Rachel.
1_29_17: Leah's eyes were weak, but Rachel was beautiful in form and appearance.
1_29_18: Now Jacob loved Rachel, so he answered, "I will work for you seven years for Rachel your younger daughter."
1_29_19: Laban said, "It is better that I give her to you than that I should give her to any other man. Stay and work with me."
1_29_20: So Jacob served seven years for Rachel, and they seemed to him only a few days because of the love he had for her.
1_29_21: Then Jacob said to Laban, "Give me my wife, for my time is completed, that I may go in to her."
1_29_22: So Laban gathered together all the men of the place and prepared a feast.
1_29_23: Now it came to pass in the evening that he took Leah his daughter and brought her to Jacob, and he slept with her.
1_29_24: And Laban gave his female servant Zilpah to his daughter Leah as a maid.
1_29_25: So it came to pass in the morning, that behold, it was Leah! And he said to Laban, "What is this you have done to me? Was it not for Rachel that I served with you? Why then have you deceived me?"
1_29_26: And Laban said, "It is not the custom in our place to give the younger before the firstborn."
1_29_27: Complete her week, and we will give you this one also in return for the service which you will serve with me another seven years.
1_29_28: Then Jacob did so and completed her week of celebration, and Laban gave him his daughter Rachel as wife also.
1_29_29: And Laban gave his female servant Bilhah to his daughter Rachel as a maid.
1_29_30: Then Jacob also went in to Rachel, and he loved Rachel more than Leah, and he worked for Laban another seven years.
1_29_31: When the LORD saw that Leah was unloved, He opened her womb and enabled her to have children, but Rachel was barren.
1_29_32: So Leah conceived and bore a son, and she named him Reuben, for she said, "The LORD has surely seen my affliction; now therefore my husband will love me."
1_29_33: Then she conceived again and bore a son, and said, "Because the LORD has heard that I am unloved, He has therefore given me this son also," and she named him Simeon.
1_29_34: She conceived again and bore a son, and said, "Now this time my husband will become attached to me, because I have borne him three sons." Therefore he was named Levi.
1_29_35: And she conceived again and bore a son, and said, "This time I will praise the LORD." Therefore she named him Judah. Then she stopped bearing.
1_30_1: Now when Rachel saw that she bore Jacob no children, she became jealous of her sister and pleaded with Jacob, "Give me children, or else I die!"
1_30_2: Jacob became angry with Rachel and said, "Am I in the place of God, who has withheld from you the fruit of the womb?"
1_30_3: So she said, "Here is my maid Bilhah; go in to her, and she will bear a child on my knees, that through her I too may have children."
1_30_4: So she gave him Bilhah her servant as wife, and Jacob went in to her.
1_30_5: And Bilhah conceived and bore Jacob a son.
1_30_6: Then Rachel said, "God has vindicated me and has also heard my voice and given me a son." Therefore she called his name Dan.
1_30_7: And Rachel's servant Bilhah conceived again and bore Jacob a second son.
1_30_8: Then Rachel said, "With mighty wrestlings I have wrestled with my sister, and indeed I have prevailed." So she called his name Naphtali.
1_30_9: When Leah saw that she had stopped bearing children, she took her servant Zilpah and gave her to Jacob as wife.
1_30_10: And Leah's servant Zilpah bore Jacob a son.
1_30_11: Then Leah said, "Good fortune has come!" So she called his name Gad.
1_30_12: And Leah's servant Zilpah bore Jacob a second son.
1_30_13: Then Leah said, "I am happy, for the women will call me blessed." So she named him Asher.
1_30_14: Now Reuben went out in the days of wheat harvest and found mandrakes in the field, and brought them to his mother Leah, and Rachel said to Leah, "Please give me some of your son's mandrakes."
1_30_15: But she said to her, "Is it a small matter that you have taken away my husband? Would you take away my son's mandrakes also?" And Rachel said, "Therefore he will lie with you tonight in exchange for your son's mandrakes."
1_30_16: When Jacob came home from the field in the evening, Leah went out to meet him and said, "You must come in to me, for I have surely hired you with my son's mandrakes." So he lay with her that night.
1_30_17: God listened to Leah, and she conceived and bore Jacob a fifth son.
1_30_18: Leah said, "God has rewarded me with my wages, because I gave my maid to my husband." So she called his name Issachar.
1_30_19: Then Leah became pregnant again and bore Jacob a sixth son.
1_30_20: And Leah said, "God has endowed me with a good gift; now my husband will honor me, because I have borne him six sons." So she called his name Zebulun.
1_30_21: Afterward she bore a daughter and named her Dinah.
1_30_22: Then God remembered Rachel, and God answered her and opened her womb.
1_30_23: And she conceived and bore a son, and said, "God has taken away my disgrace."
1_30_24: So she named him Joseph, and said, "May the LORD add to me another son."
1_30_25: After Rachel had borne Joseph, Jacob said to Laban, "Send me away, that I may go to my own home and to my country."
1_30_26: Give me my wives and my children for whom I have served you, and let me go, for you know very well the service I have rendered you.
1_30_27: And Laban said to him, "Please stay, if I have found favor in your eyes, for I have learned by divination that the LORD has blessed me because of you."
1_30_28: Then he said, "Name me your wages, and I will pay it."
1_30_29: So Jacob said to him, "You yourself know how I have served you and how your livestock has fared with me.
1_30_30: For what you had before I came was little, and it has increased abundantly; the LORD has blessed you wherever I turned. And now, when shall I also provide for my own household?
1_30_31: So he said, "What shall I give you?" And Jacob said, "You don't need to give me anything. If you will do this one thing for me, I will again pasture and keep your flocks.
1_30_32: Let me pass through your entire flock today, removing from there all the speckled and spotted sheep, and all the brown ones among the lambs, and the spotted and speckled among the goats; and these shall be my wages.
1_30_33: So my honesty will testify for me in the future when you come to check on my wages: every one that is not speckled and spotted among the goats, and black among the lambs, if found with me will be considered stolen.
1_30_34: And Laban said, "Good, let it be according to your word!"
1_30_35: So he removed that very day the male goats that were striped and spotted, all the female goats that were speckled and spotted, every one that had white on it, and all the dark-colored ones among the lambs, and placed them in the care of his sons.
1_30_36: Then he put a distance of three days' journey between himself and Jacob, and Jacob continued to care for the rest of Laban's flocks.
1_30_37: Now Jacob took for himself fresh branches of poplar and almond and plane trees, peeled white streaks in them, and exposed the white which was in the branches.
1_30_38: Jacob set the branches which he had peeled in front of the flocks in the watering troughs where the flocks came to drink, and they mated when they came to drink.
1_30_39: So the flocks mated in front of the branches, and the flocks brought forth young that were streaked, speckled, and spotted.
1_30_40: Then Jacob separated the lambs and made the flocks face toward the streaked and all the brown in the flock of Laban, but he put his own herds apart by themselves and did not put them with Laban's flock.
1_30_41: And it came to pass, whenever the stronger of the flock were mating, that Jacob placed the rods before the eyes of the livestock in the gutters, that they might breed among the rods.
1_30_42: But when the flocks were feeble, he did not put them in; so the weaker sheep belonged to Laban and the stronger ones to Jacob.
1_30_43: Thus the man became exceedingly prosperous and had large flocks, female and male servants, and camels and donkeys.
1_31_1: Now Jacob heard the words of Laban's sons, saying, "Jacob has taken away all that was our father's, and from what belonged to our father he has gained all this wealth."
1_31_2: And Jacob saw the countenance of Laban, and indeed his attitude was not favorable toward him as before.
1_31_3: Then the LORD said to Jacob, "Return to the land of your fathers and to your relatives, and I will be with you."
1_31_4: So Jacob sent and called Rachel and Leah out to the field where his flock was.
1_31_5: And said to them, "I see your father's attitude, that it is not favorable toward me as before, but the God of my father has been with me."
1_31_6: And you know that I have worked for your father with all my strength.
1_31_7: Yet your father has cheated me and changed my wages ten times, but God did not permit him to harm me.
1_31_8: If he said thus, 'The speckled shall be your wages,' then all the flocks bore speckled, and if he said thus, 'The streaked shall be your wages,' then all the flocks bore streaked.
1_31_9: So God has taken away your father's livestock and given them to me.
1_31_10: And it happened at the time when the flocks were mating, that I lifted my eyes and saw in a dream, and behold, the male goats which leaped upon the flocks were streaked, speckled, and spotted.
1_31_11: Then the Angel of God said to me in the dream, "Jacob," and I replied, "Here I am."
1_31_12: And He said, Lift your eyes now and see, all the male goats which mate with the flock are streaked, speckled, and spotted; for I have seen all that Laban has been doing to you.
1_31_13: I am the God of Bethel, where you anointed the pillar and made a vow to Me. Now arise, leave this land, and return to the land of your birth.
1_31_14: Then Rachel and Leah answered and said to him, "Is there still any portion or inheritance left for us in our father's house?
1_31_15: Are we not regarded as strangers by him, for he has sold us and also completely devoured our money.
1_31_16: For all these riches which God has taken away from our father are really ours and our children's; now then, whatever God has said to you, do it.
1_31_17: Then Jacob rose and put his children and his wives on camels.
1_31_18: And he drove away all his livestock and all his possessions which he had acquired, the livestock in his possession which he had gained in Padan Aram, to go to his father Isaac in the land of Canaan.
1_31_19: Now Laban had gone to shear his flock, and Rachel had stolen the household idols that were her father's.
1_31_20: And Jacob deceived Laban the Aramean, in that he did not tell him that he intended to flee.
1_31_21: So he fled with all that he had, and he arose and crossed the Euphrates, and set his face toward the hill country of Gilead.
1_31_22: And Laban was told on the third day that Jacob had fled.
1_31_23: Then he took his relatives with him and pursued him for seven days' journey, and he overtook him in the hill country of Gilead.
1_31_24: But God came to Laban the Aramean in a dream by night and warned him, "Be careful that you speak to Jacob neither good nor bad."
1_31_25: So Laban caught up with Jacob. Now Jacob had pitched his tent in the hill country, and Laban with his kinsmen pitched in the hill country of Gilead.
1_31_26: And Laban said to Jacob, "What have you done, that you have deceived me and carried away my daughters like captives taken with the sword?"
1_31_27: Why did you flee away secretly and deceive me, and not tell me, so that I might have sent you away with joy and songs, with tambourine and harp?
1_31_28: And you did not allow me to kiss my grandchildren and my daughters farewell, so you have acted foolishly in doing this.
1_31_29: It is in my power to do you harm, but the God of your father appeared to me last night, saying, 'Be careful not to say anything to Jacob, either good or bad.'
1_31_30: And now you have surely gone because you longed intensely for your father's house, but why did you steal my gods?
1_31_31: Then Jacob answered and said to Laban, "Because I was afraid, for I thought you would take your daughters from me by force."
1_31_32: With whomever you find your gods, do not let him live; in the presence of our relatives point out what I have of yours and take it with you, for Jacob did not know that Rachel had stolen them.
1_31_33: And Laban went into Jacob's tent, into Leah's tent, and into the tent of the two female servants, but he did not find them. Then he went out of Leah's tent and entered Rachel's tent.
1_31_34: Now Rachel had taken the household idols, hidden them in the camel's saddle, and sat on them, and Laban felt through all the tent but did not find them.
1_31_35: And she said to her father, "Let not my lord be angry that I cannot rise before you, for the manner of women is upon me." So he searched but did not find the household idols.
1_31_36: Then Jacob became angry and rebuked Laban, and Jacob said to Laban, "What is my offense, what is my sin, that you have so hotly pursued me?"
1_31_37: Although you have searched through all my goods, what part of your household things have you found? Set it here before my relatives and your relatives, that they may judge between us both!
1_31_38: These twenty years I have been with you; your ewes and your female goats have not miscarried their young, and I have not eaten a single ram of your flock.
1_31_39: That which was torn by wild beasts I did not bring to you; I bore the loss of it myself. You demanded payment from my hand, whether stolen by day or stolen by night.
1_31_40: There I was—by day the scorching heat consumed me, and the frost by night, and my sleep fled from my eyes.
1_31_41: These twenty years I have been in your house; I served you fourteen years for your two daughters and six years for your flock, and you changed my wages ten times.
1_31_42: Unless the God of my father, the God of Abraham and the Fear of Isaac, had been on my side, surely now you would have sent me away empty-handed. God has seen my affliction and the hard work of my hands, and rebuked you last night.
1_31_43: Then Laban answered and said to Jacob, "These daughters are my daughters, and these children are my children, and this flock is my flock; all that you see is mine. But what can I do this day for these my daughters or for their children whom they have borne?"
1_31_44: Now therefore, come, let us make a covenant, you and I, and let it be a witness between you and me.
1_31_45: So Jacob took a stone and set it up as a pillar.
1_31_46: Then Jacob said to his kinsmen, "Gather stones." And they took stones and made a heap, and they ate there by the heap.
1_31_47: Laban named it Jegar Sahadutha, but Jacob called it Galeed.
1_31_48: And Laban declared, "This heap is a witness between you and me this day." Therefore its name was called Galeed.
1_31_49: Also Mizpah, because he said, "May the LORD keep watch between you and me when we are out of sight of one another."
1_31_50: If you mistreat my daughters, or if you take other wives besides my daughters, although no one is with us—see, God is witness between you and me!
1_31_51: Then Laban said to Jacob, "See this heap and this monument which I have set between you and me.
1_31_52: This heap is a witness, and this pillar is a witness, that I will not pass beyond this heap to you, and you will not pass beyond this heap and this pillar to me, to do harm.
1_31_53: The God of Abraham, the God of Nahor, and the God of their father judge between us." So Jacob swore by the Fear of his father Isaac.
1_31_54: Then Jacob offered a sacrifice on the mountain and called his kinsmen to eat a meal, and they ate and spent the night on the mountain.
1_31_55: Early in the morning Laban arose, and kissed his grandchildren and daughters and blessed them, then Laban departed and returned home to his place.
1_33_1: Now Jacob lifted his eyes and looked, and behold, Esau was coming toward him, and with him were four hundred men, so he divided the children among Leah, Rachel, and the two maidservants.
1_33_2: And he put the maidservants and their children in front, Leah and her children next, and Rachel and Joseph last of all.
1_33_3: Then he himself passed on ahead of them and bowed to the ground seven times until he came near to his brother.
1_33_4: But Esau ran to meet him and embraced him and threw his arms around his neck and kissed him, and they both wept.
1_33_5: And he lifted his eyes and saw the women and children, and said, "Who are these people with you?" So he said, "The children whom God has graciously given to me, your servant."
1_33_6: Then the maidservants came near with their children and bowed down.
1_33_7: And Leah likewise came near with her children, and they bowed down. Afterward Joseph and Rachel came near, and they bowed down.
1_33_8: Then Esau said, "What do you mean by all this procession which I met?" And Jacob answered, "These are to find favor in the sight of my lord."
1_33_9: But Esau said, "I have plenty, my brother; keep what you have for yourself."
1_33_10: Jacob said, "No, please, if I have now found favor in your sight, then accept my gift from my hand, for I have seen your face as one sees the face of God, and you have received me favorably.
1_33_11: Please accept my gift that is brought to you, because God has dealt graciously with me, and because I have everything I need, so he urged him, and he took it.
1_33_12: Then Esau said, "Let us take our journey and move on our way, and I will go ahead of you."
1_33_13: But Jacob said to him, "My lord knows that the children are frail, and the nursing flocks and herds are a care to me, and if they are driven hard for one day, all the flock will die."
1_33_14: Please let my lord go on ahead before his servant, and I will proceed slowly at a pace suited to the livestock that go before me and to the children, until I come to my lord in Seir.
1_33_15: And Esau said, "Now let me leave with you some of the people who are with me." But Jacob said, "What need is there? Let me find favor in the sight of my lord."
1_33_16: So Esau turned around and started back that day on his way to Seir.
1_33_17: And Jacob journeyed to Succoth, built himself a house, and made shelters for his livestock, therefore the name of the place is called Succoth.
1_33_18: Then Jacob came safely to the city of Shechem, which is in the land of Canaan, after he came from Padan Aram, and he camped before the city.
1_33_19: And he bought the parcel of land where he had pitched his tent from the sons of Hamor, Shechem's father, for one hundred pieces of silver.
1_33_20: Then he erected an altar there and called it El Elohe Israel, which means God, the God of Israel.
1_35_1: Then God said to Jacob, "Arise, go up to Bethel and settle there, and make an altar there to God, who appeared to you when you fled from the face of Esau your brother."
1_35_2: So Jacob said to his household and to all who were with him, "Get rid of the foreign gods that are among you, purify yourselves, and change your garments.
1_35_3: Then let us arise and go up to Bethel, and I will make an altar there to God, who answered me in the day of my distress and has been with me wherever I have gone.
1_35_4: So they gave Jacob all the foreign gods which were in their hands, and the earrings which were in their ears, and Jacob buried them under the terebinth tree which was near Shechem.
1_35_5: And they journeyed, and a terror from God fell upon the cities that were all around them, and they did not pursue the sons of Jacob.
1_35_6: So Jacob came to Luz, that is Bethel, which is in the land of Canaan, he and all the people who were with him.
1_35_7: And he built an altar there and called the place El Bethel, because there God had revealed himself to him when he fled from his brother.
1_35_8: Now Deborah, Rebekah's nurse, died and was buried below Bethel under the oak tree, so he called its name Allon Bachuth, which means oak of weeping.
1_35_9: Then God appeared to Jacob again after he returned from Padan Aram, and blessed him.
1_35_10: And God said to him, "Your name is Jacob; your name shall no longer be called Jacob, but Israel shall be your name." So He called his name Israel.
1_35_11: Also God said to him, "I am God Almighty. Be fruitful and multiply; a nation and a company of nations shall come from you, and kings shall come forth from your body.
1_35_12: The land which I gave to Abraham and Isaac I will give to you, and I will give the land to your descendants after you.
1_35_13: Then God went up from him in the place where he had spoken with him.
1_35_14: So Jacob set up a stone pillar to mark the place where God had talked with him, and he poured out a drink offering on it and anointed it with oil.
1_35_15: And Jacob called the name of the place where God had spoken with him, Bethel.
1_35_16: Then they journeyed from Bethel, and when there was still some distance to go to Ephrath, Rachel went into labor and she had hard labor.
1_35_17: Now it came to pass, when she was in severe labor, that the midwife said to her, "Do not fear; you have another son."
1_35_18: And so it was, with her last breath as her soul was departing, for she died, that she named him Ben-Oni, but his father called him Benjamin.
1_35_19: So Rachel died and was buried on the way to Ephrath, that is, Bethlehem.
1_35_20: Jacob set up a pillar over her grave, which is the pillar of Rachel's tomb to this day.
1_35_21: Then Israel journeyed on and pitched his tent beyond the tower of Eder.
1_35_22: And it happened, while Israel was living in that land, that Reuben went and lay with Bilhah his father's concubine, and Israel heard about it. Now the sons of Jacob were twelve.
1_35_23: The sons of Leah were Reuben, Jacob's firstborn, then Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, and Zebulun.
1_35_24: The sons of Rachel were Joseph and Benjamin;
1_35_25: The sons of Bilhah, Rachel's servant, were Dan and Naphtali.
1_35_26: The sons of Zilpah, Leah's maidservant, were Gad and Asher. These are the sons of Jacob who were born to him in Paddan Aram.
1_35_27: Then Jacob came to his father Isaac at Mamre, or Kirjath Arba (that is, Hebron), where Abraham and Isaac had sojourned.
1_35_28: Now the days of Isaac were one hundred and eighty years.
1_35_29: So Isaac breathed his last and died, and was gathered to his people, an old man full of days, and his sons Esau and Jacob buried him.
1_36_1: Now this is the account of the generations of Esau, who is Edom.
1_36_2: Esau took his wives from the daughters of Canaan: Adah the daughter of Elon the Hittite; Oholibamah the daughter of Anah, the granddaughter of Zibeon the Hivite.
1_36_3: and Basemath, daughter of Ishmael, sister of Nebaioth.
1_36_4: Adah bore Eliphaz to Esau, and Basemath bore Reuel.
1_36_5: Aholibamah gave birth to Jeush, Jaalam, and Korah. These were the sons of Esau who were born to him in the land of Canaan.
1_36_6: Then Esau took his wives, his sons, his daughters, and all the members of his household, his cattle and all his livestock, and all his possessions which he had acquired in the land of Canaan, and went to another land away from his brother Jacob.
1_36_7: For their possessions were too great for them to dwell together, and the land where they sojourned could not support them because of their livestock.
1_36_8: So Esau settled in the hill country of Seir, for Esau is Edom.
1_36_9: This is the genealogy of Esau the father of the Edomites in the hill country of Seir.
1_36_10: These are the names of Esau's sons: Eliphaz the son of Adah the wife of Esau, and Reuel the son of Basemath the wife of Esau.
1_36_11: The sons of Eliphaz were Teman, Omar, Zepho, Gatam, and Kenaz.
1_36_12: Now Timna was the concubine of Eliphaz, Esau's son, and she bore Amalek to Eliphaz. These were the descendants of Adah, Esau's wife.
1_36_13: These were the sons of Reuel: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah, who were the sons of Basemath, Esau's wife.
1_36_14: These were the sons of Aholibamah, Esau's wife, the daughter of Anah and granddaughter of Zibeon, and she bore to Esau Jeush, Jaalam, and Korah.
1_36_15: These were the chiefs of the sons of Esau: The sons of Eliphaz, the firstborn son of Esau, were Chief Teman, Chief Omar, Chief Zepho, Chief Kenaz.
1_36_16: Chief Korah, Chief Gatam, and Chief Amalek were the chiefs descended from Eliphaz in the land of Edom, and they were the sons of Adah.
1_36_17: These were the sons of Reuel, Esau's son: Chief Nahath, Chief Zerah, Chief Shammah, and Chief Mizzah; these were the chiefs descended from Reuel in the land of Edom, and these were the sons of Basemath, Esau's wife.
1_36_18: These were the sons of Aholibamah, Esau's wife: Chief Jeush, Chief Jaalam, and Chief Korah, the chiefs born of Aholibamah the daughter of Anah, Esau's wife.
1_36_19: These were the sons of Esau, who is Edom, and these were their clan chiefs.
1_36_20: These were the sons of Seir the Horite, the inhabitants of the land: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah.
1_36_21: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan. These were the chiefs descended from the Horites, the sons of Seir, in the land of Edom.
1_36_22: The sons of Lotan were Hori and Hemam, and Lotan's sister was named Timna.
1_36_23: These were the sons of Shobal: Alvan, Manahath, Ebal, Shepho, and Onam.
1_36_24: These were the sons of Zibeon: Aiah and Anah. This was the Anah who discovered the hot springs in the wilderness while he was pasturing the donkeys of his father Zibeon.
1_36_25: These were the children of Anah: his son Dishon and his daughter Aholibamah.
1_36_26: These were the sons of Dishon: Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.
1_36_27: These were the sons of Ezer: Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan.
1_36_28: These were the sons of Dishan: Uz and Aran.
1_36_29: These were the chiefs descended from the Horites: Chief Lotan, Chief Shobal, Chief Zibeon, Chief Anah,
1_36_30: Chief Dishon, Chief Ezer, and Chief Dishan. These were the chiefs descended from the Horites, according to their various chiefs in the land of Seir.
1_36_31: Now these are the kings who reigned in the land of Edom before any king ruled over the children of Israel.
1_36_32: Bela the son of Beor ruled in Edom, and the name of his city was Dinhabah.
1_36_33: When Bela died, Jobab the son of Zerah from Bozrah became king in his place.
1_36_34: When Jobab died, Husham from the land of the Temanites became king in his place.
1_36_35: When Husham died, Hadad the son of Bedad, who defeated Midian in the country of Moab, reigned in his place, and the name of his city was Avith.
1_36_36: When Hadad died, Samlah from Masrekah became king in his place.
1_36_37: And when Samlah died, Shaul from Rehoboth on the Euphrates River reigned in his place.
1_36_38: When Saul died, Baal-Hanan the son of Achbor became king in his place.
1_36_39: When Baal-Hanan the son of Achbor died, Hadar became king in his place and ruled from the city of Pau, and his wife's name was Mehetabel, the daughter of Matred, the granddaughter of Mezahab.
1_36_40: And these were the names of the chiefs descended from Esau, according to their clans and their dwelling places, by their names: Chief Timnah, Chief Alvah, Chief Jetheth.
1_36_41: Chief Oholibamah, Chief Elah, Chief Pinon,
1_36_42: Chief Kenaz, Chief Teman, Chief Mibzar,
1_36_43: Chief Magdiel, and Chief Iram—these were the chiefs of Edom, according to their settlements in the land of their possession, and Esau was the father of the Edomites.
1_37_1: Now Jacob settled in the land where his father had sojourned as a stranger, in the land of Canaan.
1_37_2: This is the account of Jacob. Joseph, being seventeen years old, was tending the flock with his brothers, and the young man was working with the sons of Bilhah and the sons of Zilpah, his father's wives, and Joseph brought a bad report about them to his father.
1_37_3: Now Israel loved Joseph more than all his other sons, because he was the son of his old age, and he made him a robe of many colors.
1_37_4: But when his brothers saw that their father loved him more than all his brothers, they hated him and could not bring themselves to speak peaceably to him.
1_37_5: Now Joseph had a dream, and when he told it to his brothers they hated him even more.
1_37_6: So he said to them, "Please listen to this dream which I have dreamed:
1_37_7: There we were, binding sheaves of grain in the field, and suddenly my sheaf arose and stood upright, and your sheaves gathered all around and bowed down to my sheaf.
1_37_8: His brothers said to him, "Are you actually going to reign over us? Or shall you indeed have dominion over us?" So they hated him even more for his dreams and for his words.
1_37_9: Then he dreamed still another dream and told it to his brothers, and said, "Look, I have had another dream, and this time the sun, the moon, and the eleven stars were bowing down to me."
1_37_10: So he told it to his father and his brothers, and his father rebuked him and said to him, "What kind of dream is this that you have dreamed? Shall your mother and I and your brothers actually come to bow down to the ground before you?"
1_37_11: And his brothers envied him, but his father kept the saying in mind.
1_37_12: Then his brothers went to pasture their father's flock near Shechem.
1_37_13: And Israel said to Joseph, "Are not your brothers pasturing the flock at Shechem? Get ready, I will send you to them." So he said to him, "Here I am."
1_37_14: Then he said to him, "Please go now and see if it is well with your brothers and well with the flocks, and bring back word to me." So he sent him out of the Valley of Hebron, and he went to Shechem.
1_37_15: Now a certain man found him wandering in the field, and the man asked him, "What are you looking for?"
1_37_16: So he said, "I am seeking my brothers. Please tell me where they are pasturing their flocks."
1_37_17: And the man said, "They have moved on from here, for I heard them say, 'Let us go to Dothan.'" So Joseph went after his brothers and found them at Dothan.
1_37_18: Now when they saw him from a distance, even before he came near them, they plotted against him to kill him.
1_37_19: Then they said to one another, "Look, here comes this master of dreams!"
1_37_20: Come now therefore, let us kill him and throw him into one of the pits, and we shall say, 'Some wild beast has devoured him.' Then we shall see what will become of his dreams!
1_37_21: But when Reuben heard it, he rescued him out of their hands and said, "Let us not take his life."
1_37_22: Reuben said to them, "Shed no blood, but throw him into this pit here in the wilderness, and do not lay a hand on him"—intending to rescue him out of their hands and restore him to his father.
1_37_23: So it came to pass, when Joseph arrived at his brothers, that they stripped Joseph of his tunic, the beautiful tunic of many colors that he wore.
1_37_24: Then they took him and threw him into the pit, and the pit was empty with no water in it.
1_37_25: And they sat down to eat a meal, and when they lifted up their eyes and looked, there was a caravan of Ishmaelites coming from Gilead with their camels bearing spices, balm, and myrrh, on their way to carry them down to Egypt.
1_37_26: So Judah said to his brothers, "What profit is there for us if we kill our brother and cover up his blood?"
1_37_27: Come and let us sell him to the Ishmaelites, and let not our hand be upon him, for he is our brother, our own flesh, and his brothers agreed.
1_37_28: Then Midianite traders passed by, so the brothers pulled Joseph up and lifted him out of the cistern and sold him to the Ishmaelites for twenty pieces of silver, and they took Joseph to Egypt.
1_37_29: Then Reuben returned to the pit, and when he saw that Joseph was not in the pit, he tore his clothes.
1_37_30: And he returned to his brothers and lamented, "The boy is gone; and I, where shall I go?"
1_37_31: So they took Joseph's tunic, slaughtered a young goat, and dipped the tunic in the blood.
1_37_32: Then they sent the robe of many colors and brought it to their father and said, "We have found this. Please examine whether it is your son's robe or not."
1_37_33: And he recognized it and said, "It is my son's tunic. A wild beast has devoured him. Joseph is without doubt torn to pieces."
1_37_34: Then Jacob tore his clothes, put sackcloth around his waist, and mourned deeply for his son many days.
1_37_35: And all his sons and all his daughters arose to comfort him, but he refused to be comforted, and he said, "No, I shall go down to the grave to my son mourning," and thus his father wept for him.
1_37_36: Meanwhile the Midianites had sold him in Egypt to Potiphar, an officer of Pharaoh and captain of the guard.
1_38_1: It came to pass at that time that Judah went down from his brothers and stayed with a certain Adullamite whose name was Hirah.
1_38_2: And Judah saw there a daughter of a certain Canaanite whose name was Shua, and he took her as a wife and went in to her.
1_38_3: So she conceived and bore a son, and he named him Er.
1_38_4: She conceived again and bore a son, and she named him Onan.
1_38_5: She conceived yet again and bore a son, and named him Shelah, and Judah was at Chezib when she bore him.
1_38_6: Then Judah took a wife for Er his firstborn, whose name was Tamar.
1_38_7: But Er, Judah's firstborn, was evil in the sight of the LORD, so the LORD put him to death.
1_38_8: Then Judah said to Onan, "Go in to your brother's wife and perform your duty as a brother-in-law to her, and raise up offspring for your brother."
1_38_9: But Onan knew that the offspring would not be his, and it came to pass when he went in to his brother's wife that he spilled his semen on the ground so as not to give offspring to his brother.
1_38_10: And what he did was evil in the sight of the LORD, therefore He took his life also.
1_38_11: Then Judah said to Tamar his daughter-in-law, "Remain a widow in your father's house till my son Shelah grows up," for he feared that he also might die like his brothers, and Tamar went and remained in her father's house.
1_38_12: Now in the course of time the daughter of Shua, Judah's wife, died, and when Judah had finished mourning, he went up to his sheepshearers at Timnah, he and his friend Hirah the Adullamite.
1_38_13: And it was told Tamar, saying, "Look, your father-in-law is going up to Timnah to shear his sheep."
1_38_14: So she took off her widow's garments, covered herself with a veil and wrapped herself, and sat at the entrance to Enaim which was on the road to Timnah, for she saw that Shelah had grown up and she had not been given to him in marriage.
1_38_15: When Judah noticed her, he thought she was a prostitute, because she had covered her face.
1_38_16: Then he turned aside to her by the roadside and said, "Please let me come in to you," for he did not know that she was his daughter-in-law, so she said, "What will you give me, that you may come in to me?"
1_38_17: And he said, "I will send you a young goat from the flock." So she said, "Will you give me a pledge until you send it?"
1_38_18: Then he said, "What pledge shall I give you?" So she replied, "Your signet and cord, and your staff that is in your hand." Then he gave them to her, and went in to her, and she conceived by him.
1_38_19: So she arose and went away, and removed her veil and put on her widow's garments.
1_38_20: And Judah sent the young goat by the hand of his friend the Adullamite to take back his pledge from the woman's hand, but he did not find her.
1_38_21: Then he asked the men of that place, saying, "Where is the cult prostitute who was openly by the roadside at Enaim?" And they said, "There was no cult prostitute in this place."
1_38_22: So he returned to Judah and said, "I couldn't find her anywhere, and furthermore the men of the place said there was no prostitute here."
1_38_24: About three months later Judah was told, "Tamar your daughter-in-law has played the harlot; furthermore she is with child by harlotry." So Judah said, "Bring her out and let her be burned to death!"
1_38_25: As she was being brought out, she sent word to her father-in-law saying, "I am pregnant by the man to whom these items belong," and she said, "Please identify whose these are—the signet and cord and staff."
1_38_26: So Judah recognized them and said, "She is more righteous than I, because I did not give her to Shelah my son." And he never knew her again.
1_38_27: Now it came to pass, at the time of her labor, that behold, twins were in her womb.
1_38_28: And so it was, when she was in labor, that one of them put out his hand, and the midwife took a scarlet thread and tied it on his hand, saying, "This one came out first."
1_38_29: Then it happened, as he pulled back his hand, that his brother came out instead; and she said, "What a breach you have made for yourself!" Therefore his name was called Perez.
1_38_30: Afterward his brother came out who had the scarlet thread tied on his hand, and his name was called Zerah.
1_39_1: Now Joseph had been taken down to Egypt, and Potiphar, an officer of Pharaoh, captain of the guard, an Egyptian, bought him from the Ishmaelite traders who had taken him down there.
1_39_2: The LORD was with Joseph, so he became a successful man, and he was in the house of his master the Egyptian.
1_39_3: And his master saw that the LORD was with him and that the LORD caused all he did to succeed in his hand.
1_39_4: So Joseph found favor in his sight and became his personal attendant, and he made him overseer of his house, and all that he had he put under his authority.
1_39_5: From the time that he put him in charge of his household and all that he owned, the LORD blessed the Egyptian's house for Joseph's sake, and the blessing of the LORD was on all that he had in the house and in the field.
1_39_6: Thus he left all that he had in Joseph's charge, and he did not concern himself with anything except the food he ate. Now Joseph was handsome in form and appearance.
1_39_7: And it came to pass after these events that his master's wife looked with desire at Joseph, and she said, "Lie with me."
1_39_8: But he refused and said to his master's wife, "Look, my master does not concern himself with anything in the house because of me, and he has committed all that he has to my charge.
1_39_9: There is no one greater in this house than I, nor has he withheld anything from me but you, because you are his wife. How then can I do this great wickedness and sin against God?
1_39_10: So it was, as she spoke to Joseph day after day, that he refused to listen to her, to lie with her or to be with her.
1_39_11: But one day when Joseph went into the house to do his work, none of the men of the household was inside.
1_39_12: She caught him by his garment, demanding "Lie with me," but he left his garment in her hand and fled and ran outside.
1_39_13: When she saw that he had left his garment in her hand and had fled out of the house,
1_39_14: She called to the men of her household and spoke to them, saying, "See, he has brought in to us a Hebrew to make fools of us. He came in to me to lie with me, and I cried out with a loud voice.
1_39_15: And it happened, when he heard that I lifted up my voice and screamed, that he left his garment beside me, and fled and got outside.
1_39_16: So she kept his garment beside her until his master came home.
1_39_17: Then she told him the same story, saying, "The Hebrew slave whom you brought to us came in to me to mock me;
1_39_18: So it happened, as I raised my voice and screamed, that he left his garment beside me and fled outside.
1_39_19: So it was, when his master heard the story which his wife spoke to him, saying, "Your servant treated me in this manner," that his anger was kindled.
1_39_20: Then Joseph's master took him and threw him into the prison, the place where the king's prisoners were confined, and he was there in the prison.
1_39_21: But the LORD was with Joseph and extended mercy to him, and He gave him favor in the sight of the chief keeper of the prison.
1_39_22: The warden of the prison committed to Joseph's charge all the prisoners who were in the prison, and whatever was done there, he was responsible for it.
1_39_23: The keeper of the prison did not supervise anything that was under Joseph's charge, because the LORD was with him; and whatever he did, the LORD made it succeed.
1_40_1: It came to pass after these things that the cupbearer and the baker of the king of Egypt committed an offense against their lord, the king of Egypt.
1_40_2: Pharaoh was angry with his two officials, the chief cupbearer and the chief baker.
1_40_3: So he put them in custody in the house of the captain of the bodyguard, in the prison, the same place where Joseph was confined.
1_40_4: The captain of the guard assigned Joseph to be with them, and he attended them, so they remained in custody for some time.
1_40_5: Then the cupbearer and the baker of the king of Egypt, who were confined in the prison, had a dream, both of them, each man's dream in one night and each man's dream with its own meaning.
1_40_6: When Joseph came in to them in the morning and observed them, he saw that they looked troubled.
1_40_7: So he asked Pharaoh's officers who were with him in custody in his master's house, saying, "Why do your faces look so sad today?"
1_40_8: And they said to him, "We each have had a dream, and there is no one to interpret it." So Joseph said to them, "Do not interpretations belong to God? Please tell them to me."
1_40_9: Then the chief cupbearer told his dream to Joseph and said to him, "In my dream there was a vine before me,
1_40_10: On the vine were three branches, and as soon as it budded, its blossoms shot forth, and its clusters ripened into grapes.
1_40_11: Then Pharaoh's cup was in my hand, and I took the grapes and squeezed them into Pharaoh's cup, and placed the cup in Pharaoh's hand.
1_40_12: And Joseph said to him, "This is the interpretation: The three branches represent three days.
1_40_13: Within three days Pharaoh will lift up your head and restore you to your office, and you will put Pharaoh's cup in his hand according to the former manner when you were his cupbearer.
1_40_14: But remember me when it goes well with you, and please show kindness to me by mentioning me to Pharaoh, and get me out of this prison.
1_40_15: For indeed I was kidnapped from the land of the Hebrews, and even here I have done nothing that they should put me into the dungeon.
1_40_16: When the chief baker saw that the interpretation was favorable, he said to Joseph, "I also was in my dream, and there were three white baskets on my head.
1_40_17: In the uppermost basket were all sorts of baked goods for Pharaoh, but the birds were eating them out of the basket on my head.
1_40_18: So Joseph answered and said, "This is what it means: The three baskets are three days.
1_40_19: Within three days Pharaoh will lift up your head from you and hang you on a tree, and the birds will eat your flesh from you.
1_40_20: Now it came to pass on the third day, which was Pharaoh's birthday, that he prepared a feast for all his servants and lifted up the head of the chief cupbearer and of the chief baker among his servants.
1_40_21: Then he restored the chief cupbearer to his position again, and he placed the cup in Pharaoh's hand.
1_40_22: But he hanged the chief baker, just as Joseph had interpreted to them.
1_40_23: Yet the chief cupbearer did not remember Joseph, but forgot him completely.
1_42_1: When Jacob learned that there was grain in Egypt, Jacob said to his sons, "Why do you keep staring at one another?"
1_42_2: And he said, "I have heard that there is grain for sale in Egypt; go down there and buy some for us, that we may live and not die."
1_42_3: So Joseph's ten brothers went down to Egypt to buy grain.
1_42_4: But Jacob did not send Joseph's brother Benjamin with his brothers, for he feared that some calamity might befall him.
1_42_5: And the sons of Israel went to buy grain among the others who journeyed, for the famine was in the land of Canaan.
1_42_6: Now Joseph was governor over the land, and he was the one who sold grain to all the people of the land, and Joseph's brothers came and bowed down before him with their faces to the ground.
1_42_7: Joseph saw his brothers and recognized them, but he disguised himself to them and spoke harshly to them, and he said to them, "Where do you come from?" And they said, "From the land of Canaan to buy food."
1_42_8: So Joseph recognized his brothers, although they did not recognize him.
1_42_9: Then Joseph remembered the dreams which he had dreamed about them, and said to them, "You are spies! You have come to see the weakness of the land!"
1_42_10: And they said to him, "No, my lord, your servants have simply come to buy food."
1_42_11: We are all one man's sons; we are honest men, sir; your servants are not spies.
1_42_12: But he said to them, "No, you have come to see how vulnerable and undefended the land is."
1_42_13: And they said, "Your servants are twelve brothers, the sons of one man in the land of Canaan, and the youngest is now with our father, and one is no longer living."
1_42_14: But Joseph said to them, "It is just as I told you, saying, 'You are spies!'
1_42_15: In this manner you shall be tested: As surely as Pharaoh lives, you shall not leave this place unless your youngest brother comes here.
1_42_16: Send one of you, and let him get your brother; and you shall be kept in prison, that your words may be tested to see whether there is any truth in you; or else, by the life of Pharaoh, surely you are spies!
1_42_17: So he put them all together in custody for three days.
1_42_18: On the third day Joseph said to them, "Do this and you will live, for I am a God-fearing man:
1_42_19: If you are honest men, let one of your brothers remain confined in your prison house, but as for the rest of you, go and carry grain to relieve the famine of your households.
1_42_20: And bring your youngest brother to me; so your words will be verified, and you shall not die, and they consented to this.
1_42_21: Then they said to one another, "We are truly guilty concerning our brother, for we saw the anguish of his soul when he pleaded with us for his life, and we would not listen; therefore this distress has come upon us."
1_42_22: And Reuben answered them, saying, "Did I not tell you not to sin against the boy, and you would not listen? Therefore behold, now comes the reckoning for his blood."
1_42_23: But they did not realize that Joseph understood them, for he spoke to them through an interpreter.
1_42_24: Joseph turned away from them and wept, then he returned to them and spoke to them, and he took Simeon from them and bound him before their eyes.
1_42_25: Then Joseph gave orders to fill their containers with grain, to restore every man's money to his sack, and to give them provisions for the journey, and thus it was done for them.
1_42_26: So they loaded their donkeys with the grain and headed for home.
1_42_27: But as one of them opened his sack to give his donkey feed at the lodging place, he saw his money; and there it was, in the mouth of his sack.
1_42_28: So he said to his brothers, "My money has been restored, and there it is, in my sack!" Then their hearts sank and they turned trembling to one another, saying, "What is this that God has done to us?"
1_42_29: When they came to their father Jacob in the land of Canaan, they told him everything that had happened to them, saying:
1_42_30: The man who is lord of the land spoke harshly to us and accused us of being spies of the country.
1_42_31: But we said to him, We are true men; we are not spies.
1_42_32: We are twelve brothers, sons of our father; one is no longer alive, and the youngest is with our father today in the land of Canaan.
1_42_33: Then the man, the lord of the land, said to us, 'By this I will know that you are honest men: Leave one of your brothers here with me, take grain for the famine of your households, and go your way.
1_42_34: And bring your youngest brother to me; then I shall know that you are not spies, but that you are honest men. I will deliver your brother to you, and you may trade in the land.
1_42_35: Then it happened as they emptied their sacks, that behold, every man's bundle of money was in his sack, and when they and their father saw the bundles of money, they were terrified.
1_42_36: And Jacob their father said to them, "You have robbed me of my children: Joseph is no more, Simeon is no more, and now you would take Benjamin. All these things are against me."
1_42_37: Then Reuben spoke to his father, saying, "You may kill my two sons if I do not bring him back to you; put him in my care, and I will return him to you."
1_42_38: But he said, "My son shall not go down with you, for his brother is dead, and he alone is left. If any harm should befall him along the journey in which you go, then you would bring down my gray hair with sorrow to the grave."
1_43_1: Now the famine continued severe in the land.
1_43_2: And it came to pass, when they had finished eating the grain which they had brought from Egypt, that their father said to them, "Go back, buy us a little more food."
1_43_3: But Judah spoke to him, saying, "The man solemnly warned us, saying, 'You shall not see my face again unless your brother is with you.'"
1_43_4: If you send our brother with us, we will go down and buy food for you.
1_43_5: But if you will not send him, we will not go down, for the man said to us, 'You will not see my face again unless your brother is with you.'
1_43_6: And Israel said, "Why did you treat me so badly as to tell the man whether you had still another brother?"
1_43_7: But they said, "The man questioned us carefully about ourselves and our family, saying, 'Is your father still alive? Have you another brother?' And we answered him according to these questions. Could we possibly have known that he would say, 'Bring your brother down'?"
1_43_8: Then Judah said to Israel his father, "Send the boy with me, and we will arise and go, that we may live and not die of starvation, both we and you and also our little ones.
1_43_9: I myself will be surety for him; from my hand you shall require him, and you may hold me personally accountable. If I do not bring him back to you and set him before you, then let me bear the blame forever.
1_43_10: For if we had not delayed, surely by now we would have returned this second time.
1_43_11: Then their father Israel said to them, "If it must be so, then do this: take some of the choice fruits of the land in your bags and carry down a present to the man—a little balm and a little honey, aromatic gum and myrrh, pistachio nuts and almonds."
1_43_12: Take double the money in your hand, and carry back the money that was returned in the mouth of your sacks; perhaps it was a mistake.
1_43_13: Take your brother also, and arise, go back again to the man.
1_43_14: May God Almighty grant you mercy before the man, so that he may release your other brother and Benjamin, and as for me, if I am bereaved of my children, I am bereaved.
1_43_15: So the men took that present and Benjamin, and they took double the money in their hand, and arose and went down to Egypt and presented themselves before Joseph.
1_43_16: When Joseph saw Benjamin with them, he said to the steward of his house, "Bring these men into my house, and slaughter an animal and prepare a feast, for these men will dine with me at noon."
1_43_17: Then the man did as Joseph told him, and the man brought the men into Joseph's house.
1_43_18: Now the men were afraid because they were brought into Joseph's house, and they said, "It is because of the money that was returned in our sacks the first time that we are brought in, so that he may seek occasion against us and fall upon us, to make us slaves and seize our donkeys."
1_43_19: When they drew near to the steward of Joseph's house, they spoke with him at the entrance of the house.
1_43_20: And said, "O sir, we really did come down the first time to buy food;
1_43_21: But it happened, when we came to the lodging place, that we opened our sacks, and behold, each man's money was in the mouth of his sack, our money in full weight; so we have brought it back with us.
1_43_22: And we have brought down additional money in our hands to buy food, and we have no idea who put our money in our sacks.
1_43_23: But he said, "Peace be with you, do not be afraid. Your God and the God of your father has put treasure in your sacks; I received your money." Then he brought Simeon out to them.
1_43_24: So the steward brought the men into Joseph's house and gave them water, and they washed their feet, and he gave their donkeys feed.
1_43_25: Then they prepared the present for Joseph's arrival at noon, for they had heard that they would eat a meal there.
1_43_26: When Joseph came home, they brought him the gift which was in their hand into the house, and bowed down before him to the ground.
1_43_27: Then he asked them about their well-being, and said, "How is your father, the old man of whom you spoke? Is he still alive?"
1_43_28: They answered, "Your servant our father is well; he is still alive." And they bowed their heads down and paid homage.
1_43_29: Then he lifted up his eyes and saw his brother Benjamin, his mother's son, and said, "Is this your youngest brother of whom you spoke to me?" And he said, "May God be gracious to you, my son."
1_43_30: Now Joseph's heart yearned for his brother, so he hurried out and sought a place to weep, and he went into his chamber and wept there.
1_43_31: Then he washed his face and came out, and controlling himself he said, "Serve the meal."
1_43_32: So they served him by himself, and them by themselves, and the Egyptians who ate with him by themselves, because the Egyptians could not eat food with the Hebrews, for that is an abomination to the Egyptians.
1_43_33: And they sat before him in order by age, the firstborn according to his birthright and the youngest according to his youth, and the men looked at one another in astonishment.
1_43_34: Then he took portions to them from his own table, but Benjamin's portion was five times as much as any of theirs, so they feasted and drank freely with him.
1_44_1: Then Joseph commanded the steward of his house, saying, "Fill the men's sacks with as much food as they can carry, and put each man's money in the mouth of his sack.
1_44_2: Also put my personal cup, the silver cup, in the mouth of the sack of the youngest, along with his grain money, and he did as Joseph had told him.
1_44_3: As soon as the morning was light, the men were sent on their journey, they and their donkeys.
1_44_4: When they had gone out of the city and were not yet far off, Joseph said to his steward, "Get up, pursue the men, and when you overtake them, say to them, 'Why have you repaid evil for good?
1_44_5: Is not this the cup from which my lord drinks and which he indeed uses for divination? You have done wrong in doing this.
1_44_6: So he overtook them, and he spoke to them these words as he had been instructed.
1_44_7: And they said to him, "Why does my lord speak such words as these? Far be it from your servants to do such a thing.
1_44_8: Look, we even brought back to you from the land of Canaan the money which we found in the mouth of our sacks, so how then could we steal silver or gold from your master's house?
1_44_9: With whomever of your servants it is found, let him die, and the rest of us also will be my lord's slaves.
1_44_10: And he said, "Now also let it be according to your words; he with whom it is found shall be my slave, and the rest of you shall be blameless."
1_44_11: Then each man quickly lowered his sack to the ground, and each opened his sack.
1_44_12: So he searched, beginning with the oldest and ending with the youngest, and the cup was found in Benjamin's sack.
1_44_13: Then they tore their clothes in despair, and each man loaded his donkey and returned to the city.
1_44_14: So Judah and his brothers came to Joseph's house, and he was still there, and they fell to the ground before him.
1_44_15: Joseph said to them, "What deed is this you have done? Did you not know that a man like me can certainly practice divination?"
1_44_16: Then Judah said, "What shall we say to my lord? What shall we speak? Or how can we justify ourselves? God has found out the iniquity of your servants; here we are, my lord's slaves, both we and he also in whose possession the cup was found."
1_44_17: But he said, "Far be it from me that I should do this; the man in whose possession the cup was found, he shall be my slave, and as for you, go up in peace to your father."
1_44_18: Then Judah approached him and said, "O my lord, please let your servant speak a word personally in my lord's hearing, and do not let your anger burn against your servant, for you are even like Pharaoh himself."
1_44_19: My lord asked his servants, saying, 'Do you have a father or a brother?'
1_44_20: And we said to my lord, We have a father, an elderly man, and a younger brother, a child of his old age; his full brother is dead, and he alone is left of his mother's children, and his father loves him.
1_44_21: Then you said to your servants, 'Bring him down to me, that I may see him with my own eyes.'
1_44_22: And we said to my lord, The boy cannot leave his father, for if he were to leave his father, his father would die.
1_44_23: But you told your servants, 'Unless your youngest brother comes down with you, you shall not see my face again.'
1_44_24: So it was, when we went back to your servant my father, that we told him the words of my lord.
1_44_25: And our father said, 'Go back again and buy us a little food.'
1_44_26: But we said, 'We cannot go down unless our youngest brother is with us, then we will go down; for we cannot see the man's face unless our youngest brother is with us.'
1_44_27: Then your servant my father said to us, You know that my wife bore me two sons.
1_44_28: And the one went out from me, and I said, "Surely he is torn to pieces," and I have never seen him since.
1_44_29: But if you take this one also from me, and harm befalls him, you shall bring down my gray hair with sorrow to Sheol.
1_44_30: Now therefore, when I come to your servant my father, and the boy is not with us, since his life is bound up in the boy's life,
1_44_31: When he sees that the boy is not with us, he will die, and your servants will bring down the gray hairs of your servant our father with sorrow to the grave.
1_44_32: For your servant became surety for the boy to my father, saying, 'If I do not bring him back to you, then I shall bear the blame before my father forever.'
1_44_33: Now therefore, please let your servant remain here instead of the boy as a slave to my lord, and let the boy go back with his brothers.
1_44_34: For how can I go back to my father if the boy is not with me, lest I see the anguish that would overtake my father?
1_45_1: Then Joseph could no longer control himself before all those who stood by him, and he cried out, "Make everyone go out from me!" So no one stood with him while Joseph made himself known to his brothers.
1_45_2: And he wept so loudly that the Egyptians heard it, and the household of Pharaoh heard it.
1_45_3: Then Joseph said to his brothers, "I am Joseph; is my father still alive?" But his brothers could not answer him, for they were terrified in his presence.
1_45_4: And Joseph said to his brothers, "Please come closer to me." So they came near. Then he said, "I am Joseph your brother, whom you sold into slavery in Egypt."
1_45_5: But now, do not be grieved or angry with yourselves because you sold me here, for God sent me ahead of you to preserve life.
1_45_6: For these two years the famine has been in the land, and there are still five more years in which there will be neither plowing nor harvesting.
1_45_7: God sent me before you to preserve a remnant for you in the earth and to keep you alive by a great deliverance.
1_45_8: So now it was not you who sent me here, but God, and He has made me an adviser to Pharaoh, and lord of all his household, and a ruler throughout all the land of Egypt.
1_45_9: Hurry and go up to my father and tell him, 'Thus says your son Joseph: God has made me master of all Egypt; come down to me, do not delay.
1_45_10: You shall settle in the land of Goshen, and you shall be near to me, you and your children, your children's children, your flocks and your herds, and all that you have.
1_45_11: There I will provide for you, for there are still five more years of famine to come, so that you and your household and all that you have do not come to poverty.
1_45_12: And behold, your eyes and the eyes of my brother Benjamin can see for yourselves that it is my mouth that speaks to you.
1_45_13: So you shall tell my father of all my honor in Egypt, and of all that you have seen, and you shall hurry and bring my father down here.
1_45_14: Then he fell on his brother Benjamin's neck and wept, and Benjamin embraced him and wept on his neck.
1_45_15: Moreover he kissed each of his brothers and wept over them, and after that his brothers talked freely with him.
1_45_16: Now the news was heard in Pharaoh's house, saying, "Joseph's brothers have come," and it pleased Pharaoh and his servants well.
1_45_17: And Pharaoh said to Joseph, "Say to your brothers, 'Do this: Load your pack animals and go back to the land of Canaan.
1_45_18: Bring your father and your households and return to me; I will give you the best of the land of Egypt, and you will eat the richness of the land.
1_45_19: Now you are commanded—do this: Take wagons out of the land of Egypt for your little ones and your wives; bring your father and come.
1_45_20: Also do not be concerned about your personal belongings, for the best of all the land of Egypt is yours.
1_45_21: Then the sons of Israel did so, and Joseph gave them carts according to the command of Pharaoh and gave them provisions for the journey.
1_45_22: He gave to each of them changes of garments, but to Benjamin he gave three hundred shekels of silver and five changes of garments.
1_45_23: And he sent to his father these things: ten donkeys loaded with the best things of Egypt, and ten female donkeys loaded with grain, bread, and provisions for his father for the journey.
1_45_24: So he sent his brothers away, and they departed; and he said to them, "See that you do not quarrel along the journey."
1_45_25: Then they went up out of Egypt and returned to the land of Canaan to Jacob their father.
1_45_26: And they told him, saying, "Joseph is still alive, and he is ruler over all the land of Egypt." And Jacob's heart became numb, for he did not believe them.
1_45_27: But when they repeated to him all the words which Joseph had said to them, and when he saw the wagons which Joseph had sent to carry him, the spirit of their father Jacob revived.
1_45_28: Then Israel said, "It is enough! Joseph my son is still alive. I will go and see him before I die."
1_46_1: So Israel set out with all that he had, and came to Beersheba, and offered sacrifices to the God of his father Isaac.
1_46_2: Then God spoke to Israel in the visions of the night and said, "Jacob, Jacob!" And he replied, "Here I am."
1_46_3: So He said, "I am God, the God of your father; do not be afraid to go down to Egypt, for there I will make you into a great nation.
1_46_4: I will go down with you to Egypt, and I will also surely bring you back again, and Joseph will close your eyes.
1_46_5: Then Jacob set out from Beersheba, and the sons of Israel carried their father Jacob, their little ones, and their wives in the wagons which Pharaoh had sent to carry him.
1_46_6: So they took their livestock and their possessions, which they had acquired in the land of Canaan, and came to Egypt, Jacob and all his descendants with him.
1_46_7: His sons and his grandsons, his daughters and his granddaughters, and all his offspring he brought with him to Egypt.
1_46_8: Now these were the names of the descendants of Israel, Jacob and his sons, who went to Egypt: Reuben was Jacob's firstborn.
1_46_9: The sons of Reuben were Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi.
1_46_10: The sons of Simeon were Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar, and Shaul, the son of a Canaanite woman.
1_46_11: The sons of Levi were Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
1_46_12: The sons of Judah were Er, Onan, Shelah, Perez, and Zerah, though Er and Onan had died in the land of Canaan, and the sons of Perez were Hezron and Hamul.
1_46_13: The sons of Issachar were Tola, Puvah, Job, and Shimron.
1_46_14: The sons of Zebulun were Sered, Elon, and Jahleel.
1_46_15: These were the sons of Leah, whom she bore to Jacob in Padan Aram, together with his daughter Dinah, and altogether the persons, his sons and his daughters, numbered thirty-three.
1_46_16: The sons of Gad were Ziphion, Haggi, Shuni, Ezbon, Eri, Arodi, and Areli.
1_46_17: The sons of Asher were Jimnah, Ishuah, Isui, Beriah, and their sister Serah, and the sons of Beriah were Heber and Malchiel.
1_46_18: These were the sons of Zilpah, whom Laban gave as a servant to Leah his daughter, and these she bore to Jacob: sixteen persons.
1_46_19: The sons of Rachel, Jacob's wife, were Joseph and Benjamin.
