45_1_16: For I am not ashamed of the gospel, for it is the power of God for salvation to everyone who believes, to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
45_1_17: For in it the righteousness of God is revealed from faith to faith, as it is written: "The righteous shall live by faith."
45_1_18: For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of men, who by their unrighteousness suppress the truth.
45_1_19: For what can be known about God is plain to them, because God has made it clear to them.
45_1_20: For His invisible attributes — His eternal power and divine nature — have been clearly seen ever since the creation of the world, being understood through the things that have been made. So they are without excuse.
45_1_21: For although they knew God, they did not honor Him as God or give thanks to Him, but they became futile in their thinking, and their foolish hearts were darkened.
45_1_22: Claiming to be wise, they became fools,
45_1_23: and exchanged the glory of the immortal God for images resembling mortal man and birds and four-footed animals and creeping things.
45_1_24: Therefore God gave them up in the desires of their hearts to impurity, to the dishonoring of their bodies among themselves,
45_1_25: because they exchanged the truth of God for a lie, and worshiped and served the creature rather than the Creator, who is blessed forever. Amen.
45_1_26: For this reason God gave them up to dishonorable passions. For their women exchanged natural relations for those that are contrary to nature;
45_1_27: and the men likewise, abandoning natural relations with women, burned with passion toward one another, men committing shameless acts with men and receiving in themselves the due penalty for their error.
45_1_28: And since they did not see fit to acknowledge God, He gave them over to a debased mind to do things that ought not to be done.
45_1_29: They were filled with all manner of unrighteousness, evil, covetousness, malice; full of envy, murder, strife, deceit, and malice — they are gossips,
45_1_30: slanderers, haters of God, insolent, arrogant, boastful, inventors of evil, disobedient to parents,
45_1_32: Though they know God's righteous decree that those who practice such things deserve to die, they not only do them but give hearty approval to those who practice them.
45_2_1: Therefore you have no excuse, O man, every one of you who judges. For in passing judgment on another you condemn yourself, because you who judge practice the very same things.
45_2_2: But we know that the judgment of God rightly falls on those who practice such things.
45_2_3: Do you suppose, O man — you who judge those who do such things and yet do the same yourself — that you will escape the judgment of God?
45_2_4: Or do you despise the riches of His kindness, restraint, and patience, not knowing that God's kindness is meant to lead you to repentance?
45_2_5: But because of your stubborn and unrepentant heart you are storing up wrath for yourself on the day of wrath when God's righteous judgment will be revealed.
45_2_6: He will render to each one according to his deeds:
45_2_7: eternal life to those who by perseverance in doing good seek for glory, honor, and immortality;
45_2_8: but wrath and indignation to those who are self-seeking and do not obey the truth, but obey unrighteousness.
45_2_9: There will be tribulation and distress for every soul who does evil, for the Jew first and also for the Greek;
45_2_10: but glory, honor, and peace to everyone who does good, to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
45_2_11: For there is no partiality with God.
45_2_12: For all who have sinned without the law will also perish without the law, and all who have sinned under the law will be judged by the law.
45_2_13: For it is not the hearers of the law who are righteous before God, but the doers of the law who will be justified.
45_2_14: For when Gentiles, who do not have the law, by nature do what the law requires, they are a law to themselves, even though they do not have the law.
45_2_15: They show that the work of the law is written on their hearts, while their conscience also bears witness, and their conflicting thoughts either accuse or even excuse them
45_2_16: on the day when God judges the secrets of men through Christ Jesus, according to my gospel.
45_2_17: But if you call yourself a Jew and rely on the law and boast in God,
45_2_18: and know His will and approve what is excellent because you are instructed from the law,
45_2_19: and if you are confident that you yourself are a guide to the blind, a light to those in darkness,
45_2_20: an instructor of the ignorant, a teacher of the immature, having in the law the embodiment of knowledge and truth—
45_2_21: you then who teach another, do you not teach yourself? You who preach that a man should not steal, do you steal?
45_2_22: You who say, "Do not commit adultery," do you commit adultery? You who detest idols, do you rob temples?
45_2_23: You who boast in the law, do you dishonor God by breaking the law?
45_2_24: For, as it is written, "The name of God is blasphemed among the Gentiles because of you."
45_2_25: For circumcision is of value if you observe the law; but if you are a lawbreaker, your circumcision has become uncircumcision.
45_2_26: So if a man who is uncircumcised keeps the requirements of the law, will not his uncircumcision be counted as circumcision?
45_2_27: Then he who is physically uncircumcised but keeps the law will judge you who, though having the written code and circumcision, are a transgressor of the law.
45_2_28: For no one is a Jew who is merely one outwardly, nor is true circumcision something visible in the flesh.
45_2_29: But a Jew is one inwardly, and circumcision is a matter of the heart — by the Spirit, not the letter. His praise is not from man but from God.
45_3_1: Then what advantage has the Jew? Or what is the value of circumcision?
45_3_2: Much in every way! To begin with, they were entrusted with the oracles of God.
45_3_3: What then? If some were unfaithful, does their faithlessness nullify the faithfulness of God?
45_3_4: Absolutely not! Let God be true though every man a liar, as it is written: "That You may be justified in Your words, and triumph when You are judged."
45_3_5: But if our unrighteousness serves to show the righteousness of God, what shall we say? Is God unjust to inflict wrath on us? I am speaking in human terms.
45_3_6: Absolutely not! For then how could God judge the world?
45_3_7: But if through my lie God's truth abounds to His glory, why am I still being judged as a sinner?
45_3_8: And why not say — as some people slanderously charge us with saying — "Let us do evil that good may come"? Their condemnation is just.
45_3_9: What then? Are we Jews any better off? Not at all! For we have already charged that all, both Jews and Greeks, are under sin,
45_3_10: as it is written: "There is none righteous, no, not one;
45_3_11: there is none who understands; there is none who seeks after God.
45_3_12: All have turned aside; together they have become worthless; there is none who does good, not even one."
45_3_13: "Their throat is an open grave; with their tongues they practice deceit." "The venom of vipers is under their lips."
45_3_14: "Their mouth is full of cursing and bitterness."
45_3_15: "Their feet are swift to shed blood;
45_3_16: ruin and misery are in their paths,
45_3_17: and the way of peace they have not known."
45_3_18: "There is no fear of God before their eyes."
45_3_19: Now we know that whatever the law says, it speaks to those who are under the law, so that every mouth may be stopped and the whole world may be held accountable to God.
45_3_20: For by works of the law no human being will be justified in His sight, since through the law comes knowledge of sin.
45_3_21: But now the righteousness of God has been revealed apart from the law, although the Law and the Prophets bear witness to it—
45_3_22: the righteousness of God through faith in Jesus Christ to all who believe. For there is no distinction:
45_3_23: for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God,
45_3_24: and are justified freely by His grace as a gift, through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus,
45_3_25: whom God put forward as an atoning sacrifice by His blood, received through faith. This was to show God's righteousness, because in His forbearance He had passed over sins previously committed.
45_3_26: It was to show His righteousness at the present time, so that He would be just and the justifier of the one who has faith in Jesus.
45_3_27: Where then is boasting? It is excluded. By what kind of law? By a law of works? No, but by the law of faith.
45_3_28: For we conclude that a person is justified by faith apart from the works of the law.
45_3_29: Or is God the God of Jews only? Is He not the God of Gentiles also? Yes, of Gentiles also,
45_3_30: since God is one — who will justify the circumcised by faith and the uncircumcised through faith.
45_3_31: Do we then overthrow the law by this faith? By no means! On the contrary, we uphold the law.
45_4_1: What then shall we say that Abraham, our forefather according to the flesh, has found?
45_4_2: For if Abraham was justified by works, he has something to boast about — but not before God.
45_4_3: For what does the Scripture say? "Abraham believed God, and it was credited to him for righteousness."
45_4_4: Now to the one who works, his wages are not credited as a gift but as something owed.
45_4_5: But to the one who does not work but believes in Him who justifies the ungodly, his faith is credited as righteousness.
45_4_6: Just as David also speaks of the blessing of the person to whom God credits righteousness apart from works:
45_4_7: "Blessed are those whose lawless acts are forgiven, and whose sins are covered;
45_4_8: blessed is the man whose sin the Lord will never count against him."
45_4_9: Is this blessing then only for the circumcised, or also for the uncircumcised? For we say that faith was credited to Abraham for righteousness.
45_4_10: How then was it credited? Was it before or after he had been circumcised? It was not after, but before he was circumcised.
45_4_11: He received the sign of circumcision as a seal of the righteousness that he had by faith while still uncircumcised, so that he might be the father of all who believe without being circumcised, that righteousness might be credited to them as well,
45_4_12: and the father of the circumcised — not only those who are circumcised, but who also walk in the footsteps of the faith that our father Abraham had while still uncircumcised.
45_4_13: For the promise to Abraham and his offspring that he would be heir of the world did not come through the law, but through the righteousness that comes by faith.
45_4_14: For if those who are of the law are heirs, faith is made void and the promise is nullified,
45_4_15: because the law brings about wrath; but where there is no law, there is no transgression.
45_4_16: That is why the promise is by faith — so that it may rest on grace and be guaranteed to all Abraham's offspring, not only to those who are of the law but also to those who share the faith of Abraham, who is the father of us all.
45_4_17: As it is written: "I have made you a father of many nations" — in the presence of the God in whom he believed, who gives life to the dead and calls into existence the things that do not exist.
45_4_18: Against all hope, Abraham in hope believed, so that he became the father of many nations, according to what was spoken: "So shall your descendants be."
45_4_19: He did not weaken in faith when he considered his own body — already as good as dead since he was about a hundred years old — and the deadness of Sarah's womb.
45_4_20: He did not waver in unbelief at God's promise, but grew strong in faith, giving glory to God,
45_4_21: being fully convinced that what God had promised He was also able to perform.
45_4_22: That is why his faith was credited to him as righteousness.
45_4_23: Now the words "it was credited to him" were not written for his sake alone,
45_4_24: but for ours also. It will be credited to us who believe in Him who raised Jesus our Lord from the dead,
45_4_25: who was delivered up for our trespasses and raised for our justification.
45_5_1: Therefore, since we have been justified by faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ.
45_5_2: Through Him we have also obtained access by faith into this grace in which we stand, and we rejoice in the hope of the glory of God.
45_5_3: Not only that, but we also rejoice in our sufferings, knowing that suffering produces endurance,
45_5_4: and endurance produces proven character, and proven character produces hope.
45_5_5: And this hope does not put us to shame, because God's love has been poured into our hearts through the Holy Spirit who has been given to us.
45_5_6: For while we were still weak and helpless, at the right time Christ died for the ungodly.
45_5_7: For one will scarcely die for a righteous man — though perhaps for a good man someone might even dare to die.
45_5_8: But God demonstrates His own love for us in this: while we were still sinners, Christ died for us.
45_5_9: Much more then, since we have now been justified by His blood, we shall be saved from wrath through Him.
45_5_10: For if while we were enemies we were reconciled to God through the death of His Son, much more, now that we are reconciled, shall we be saved by His life.
45_5_11: And not only that, but we also rejoice in God through our Lord Jesus Christ, through whom we have now received this reconciliation.
45_5_12: Therefore, just as sin entered the world through one man, and death through sin, and so death spread to all people, because all sinned—
45_5_13: for sin indeed was in the world before the law was given, but sin is not charged to a person's account where there is no law.
45_5_14: Yet death reigned from Adam to Moses, even over those who did not sin in the likeness of Adam's transgression. He is a type of the One who was to come.
45_5_15: But the free gift is not like the trespass. For if many died through one man's trespass, how much more have the grace of God and the free gift by the grace of the one man Jesus Christ overflowed to the many.
45_5_16: And the gift is not like the result of that one man's sin. For the judgment following one trespass brought condemnation, but the free gift following many trespasses brought justification.
45_5_17: For if, because of one man's trespass, death reigned through that one man, much more will those who receive the overflow of grace and the gift of righteousness reign in life through the one man Jesus Christ.
45_5_18: Therefore, as one trespass led to condemnation for all men, so one act of righteousness leads to justification and life for all men.
45_5_19: For as by one man's disobedience the many were made sinners, so by the one man's obedience the many will be made righteous.
45_5_20: Now the law came in to increase the trespass, but where sin increased, grace abounded all the more,
45_5_21: so that as sin reigned in death, so also grace might reign through righteousness to eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord.
45_6_1: What shall we say then? Should we continue in sin so that grace may abound?
45_6_2: Absolutely not! How can we who died to sin still live in it?
45_6_3: Or are you unaware that all of us who were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into His death?
45_6_4: We were buried therefore with Him through baptism into death, so that just as Christ was raised from the dead by the glory of the Father, we too might walk in newness of life.
45_6_5: For if we have been united with Him in a death like His, we will certainly also be united with Him in a resurrection like His.
45_6_6: We know that our old self was crucified with Him in order that the body of sin might be rendered powerless, so that we would no longer be enslaved to sin.
45_6_7: For one who has died has been set free from sin.
45_6_8: Now if we died with Christ, we believe that we will also live with Him,
45_6_9: knowing that Christ, having been raised from the dead, will never die again. Death no longer has dominion over Him.
45_6_10: For the death He died, He died to sin once for all; but the life He lives, He lives to God.
45_6_11: So you also must consider yourselves dead to sin but alive to God in Christ Jesus.
45_6_12: Therefore do not let sin reign in your mortal body, making you obey its desires.
45_6_13: Do not offer any part of yourself to sin as an instrument of unrighteousness. Instead, as those who are alive from the dead, offer yourselves to God, and all the parts of yourselves as instruments of righteousness to God.
45_6_14: For sin will not have dominion over you, since you are not under law but under grace.
45_6_15: What then? Should we sin because we are not under law but under grace? Absolutely not!
45_6_16: Do you not know that if you present yourselves to anyone as obedient slaves, you are slaves of the one you obey — either of sin leading to death, or of obedience leading to righteousness?
45_6_17: But thank God that, although you used to be slaves of sin, you obeyed from the heart that pattern of teaching to which you were entrusted,
45_6_18: and having been set free from sin, you became enslaved to righteousness.
45_6_19: I am using a human analogy because of the weakness of your flesh. For just as you once offered the parts of yourselves as slaves to impurity and to lawlessness leading to more lawlessness, so now offer them as slaves to righteousness, resulting in holiness.
45_6_20: For when you were slaves of sin, you were free from the obligation to righteousness.
45_6_21: But what fruit were you producing then from the things of which you are now ashamed? For the outcome of those things is death.
45_6_22: But now, having been set free from sin and enslaved to God, you have your fruit resulting in holiness — and the outcome is eternal life.
45_6_23: For the wages of sin is death, but the free gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.
45_7_1: Or do you not know, brothers and sisters — for I am speaking to those who know the law — that the law is binding on a person only as long as he lives?
45_7_2: For a married woman is legally bound to her husband while he lives; but if her husband dies, she is released from the law concerning the husband.
45_7_3: Accordingly, she will be called an adulteress if she marries another man while her husband is alive. But if her husband dies, she is free from that law, and if she marries another man she is not an adulteress.
45_7_4: Likewise, my brothers and sisters, you also were put to death in relation to the law through the body of Christ, so that you may belong to another — to Him who was raised from the dead — in order that we may bear fruit for God.
45_7_5: For while we were living in the flesh, the sinful passions aroused by the law were at work in us to bear fruit for death.
45_7_6: But now we have been released from the law, having died to what held us captive, so that we serve in the newness of the Spirit and not in the oldness of the written code.
45_7_7: What shall we say then? Is the law sin? Absolutely not! On the contrary, I would not have known sin except through the law. For I would not have known what it is to covet if the law had not said, "You shall not covet."
45_7_8: But sin, seizing an opportunity through the commandment, produced in me coveting of every kind. For apart from the law sin lies dead.
45_7_9: I was once alive apart from the law, but when the commandment came, sin sprang to life and I died.
45_7_10: The very commandment that was meant for life proved to be death for me.
45_7_11: For sin, seizing an opportunity through the commandment, deceived me and through it killed me.
45_7_12: So then, the law is holy, and the commandment is holy and righteous and good.
45_7_13: Did that which is good, then, bring death to me? Absolutely not! It was sin, in order to be shown as sin, that was producing death in me through what is good — so that through the commandment, sin might become sinful beyond measure.
45_7_14: For we know that the law is spiritual, but I am of the flesh, sold as a slave to sin.
45_7_15: For I do not understand what I am doing, because I do not practice what I want to do, but I do what I hate.
45_7_16: Now if I do what I do not want to do, I agree with the law that it is good.
45_7_17: So now it is no longer I who do it, but sin that dwells within me.
45_7_18: For I know that nothing good lives in me — that is, in my flesh. For the desire to do what is good is with me, but the ability to carry it out is not.
45_7_19: For the good that I want to do, I do not do; but the evil I do not want is what I keep on doing.
45_7_20: Now if I do what I do not want to do, it is no longer I who do it, but sin that dwells within me.
45_7_21: So I discover this principle: when I want to do what is right, evil is present with me.
45_7_22: For I delight in the law of God in my inner being,
45_7_23: but I see another law in the parts of my body, waging war against the law of my mind and taking me prisoner to the law of sin that dwells in my members.
45_7_24: What a wretched man I am! Who will rescue me from this body of death?
45_7_25: Thanks be to God through Jesus Christ our Lord! So then, with my mind I myself serve the law of God, but with my flesh the law of sin.
45_8_1: There is therefore now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.
45_8_2: For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has set you free from the law of sin and death.
45_8_3: For what the law could not do, weakened as it was through the flesh, God did: sending His own Son in the likeness of sinful flesh and as a sin offering, He condemned sin in the flesh,
45_8_4: in order that the righteous requirement of the law might be fulfilled in us, who walk not according to the flesh but according to the Spirit.
45_8_5: For those who live according to the flesh have their minds set on the things of the flesh, but those who live according to the Spirit have their minds set on the things of the Spirit.
45_8_6: For the mind-set of the flesh is death, but the mind-set of the Spirit is life and peace.
45_8_7: For the mind set on the flesh is hostile to God, because it does not submit to God's law — indeed, it cannot.
45_8_8: Those who are in the flesh cannot please God.
45_8_9: You, however, are not in the flesh but in the Spirit, if in fact the Spirit of God dwells in you. Anyone who does not have the Spirit of Christ does not belong to Him.
45_8_10: But if Christ is in you, although the body is dead because of sin, the Spirit gives life because of righteousness.
45_8_11: If the Spirit of Him who raised Jesus from the dead dwells in you, He who raised Christ from the dead will also give life to your mortal bodies through His Spirit who dwells in you.
45_8_12: So then, brothers and sisters, we are not obligated to the flesh to live according to the flesh.
45_8_13: For if you live according to the flesh you are going to die; but if by the Spirit you put to death the deeds of the body, you will live.
45_8_14: For all who are led by the Spirit of God are sons of God.
45_8_15: For you did not receive a spirit of slavery to fall back into fear, but you received the Spirit of adoption by whom we cry out, "Abba, Father!"
45_8_16: The Spirit Himself testifies together with our spirit that we are children of God,
45_8_17: and if children, then heirs — heirs of God and fellow heirs with Christ, if indeed we suffer with Him so that we may also be glorified with Him.
45_8_18: For I consider that the sufferings of this present time are not worth comparing with the glory that is going to be revealed to us.
45_8_19: For the creation eagerly waits with anticipation for the revealing of the sons of God.
45_8_20: For the creation was subjected to futility — not willingly, but because of Him who subjected it — in hope
45_8_21: that the creation itself will also be set free from the bondage to decay into the glorious freedom of the children of God.
45_8_22: For we know that the whole creation has been groaning together with labor pains until now.
45_8_23: And not only the creation, but we ourselves, who have the Spirit as firstfruits, groan inwardly as we eagerly wait for adoption, the redemption of our bodies.
45_8_24: For in this hope we were saved. But hope that is seen is not hope — for who hopes for what he already sees?
45_8_25: But if we hope for what we do not see, we eagerly wait for it with patient endurance.
45_8_26: Likewise the Spirit also helps us in our weakness. For we do not know what we should pray for as we ought, but the Spirit Himself intercedes for us with groanings too deep for words.
45_8_27: And He who searches our hearts knows what the mind of the Spirit is, because He intercedes for the saints according to the will of God.
45_8_28: And we know that God causes all things to work together for good to those who love God, who are called according to His purpose.
45_8_29: For those He foreknew, He also predestined to be conformed to the image of His Son, so that He would be the firstborn among many brothers and sisters.
45_8_30: And those He predestined, He also called; those He called, He also justified; and those He justified, He also glorified.
45_8_31: What then shall we say about these things? If God is for us, who can be against us?
45_8_32: He who did not spare His own Son but offered Him up for us all — how will He not also with Him graciously give us all things?
45_8_33: Who shall bring a charge against God's elect? It is God who justifies.
45_8_34: Who is the one who condemns? Christ Jesus is the one who died — and even more, who was raised — who is at the right hand of God and who intercedes for us.
45_8_35: Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? Shall tribulation, or distress, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or danger, or sword?
45_8_36: As it is written: "For Your sake we are being put to death all day long; we are counted as sheep to be slaughtered."
45_8_37: No, in all these things we are more than conquerors through Him who loved us.
45_8_38: For I am persuaded that neither death nor life, nor angels nor rulers, nor things present nor things to come, nor powers,
45_8_39: nor height nor depth, nor any other created thing, will be able to separate us from the love of God that is in Christ Jesus our Lord.
45_9_1: I am speaking the truth in Christ — I am not lying; my conscience confirms it by the Holy Spirit—
45_9_2: that I have great sorrow and unceasing anguish in my heart.
45_9_3: For I could wish that I myself were cursed and cut off from Christ for the sake of my brothers, my own flesh and blood.
45_9_4: They are Israelites, and to them belong the adoption, the glory, the covenants, the giving of the law, the temple service, and the promises.
45_9_5: To them belong the patriarchs, and from their race, according to the flesh, is the Christ, who is God over all, blessed forever. Amen.
45_9_6: But it is not as though the word of God has failed. For not all who are descended from Israel are truly Israel.
45_9_7: Nor are they all children of Abraham because they are his descendants. But as it is written: "Through Isaac shall your offspring be named."
45_9_8: This means that it is not the children by physical descent who are God's children, but the children of the promise are counted as the offspring.
45_9_9: For this is what the promise said: "At this time I will return, and Sarah shall have a son."
45_9_10: And not only so, but also when Rebekah had conceived children by one man, our forefather Isaac —
45_9_11: though they were not yet born and had done nothing either good or bad, so that God's purpose of election might stand, not because of works but because of Him who calls —
45_9_12: she was told, "The older will serve the younger."
45_9_13: As it is written: "Jacob I loved, but Esau I hated."
45_9_14: What shall we say then? Is there injustice with God? Absolutely not!
45_9_15: For He says to Moses: "I will have mercy on whom I have mercy, and I will have compassion on whom I have compassion."
45_9_16: So then it does not depend on human will or effort, but on God who shows mercy.
45_9_17: For the Scripture says to Pharaoh: "For this very purpose I have raised you up, that I might display My power in you, and that My name might be proclaimed in all the earth."
45_9_18: So then He has mercy on whomever He wills, and He hardens whomever He wills.
45_9_19: You will say to me then, "Why does He still find fault? For who can resist His will?"
45_9_20: But who are you, O man, to answer back to God? Will what is molded say to its molder, "Why have you made me like this?"
45_9_21: Has the potter no right over the clay, to make from the same lump one vessel for honorable use and another for common use?
45_9_22: What if God, desiring to display His wrath and to make His power known, endured with much patience vessels of wrath prepared for destruction?
45_9_23: What if He did this to make known the riches of His glory for vessels of mercy, which He had prepared beforehand for glory—
45_9_24: even us whom He has called, not from the Jews only but also from the Gentiles?
45_9_25: As He says in Hosea: "Those who were not My people I will call 'My people,' and her who was not beloved I will call 'Beloved.'"
45_9_26: "And in the very place where it was said to them, 'You are not My people,' there they will be called sons of the living God."
45_9_27: And Isaiah cries out concerning Israel: "Though the number of the sons of Israel be as the sand of the sea, only a remnant will be saved,
45_9_28: for the Lord will carry out His sentence on the earth, completely and decisively."
45_9_29: And as Isaiah predicted: "If the Lord of Hosts had not left us offspring, we would have been like Sodom and become like Gomorrah."
45_9_30: What shall we say then? That Gentiles who did not pursue righteousness have attained it — the righteousness that comes from faith;
45_9_31: but Israel, who pursued a law of righteousness, did not arrive at that law.
45_9_32: Why? Because they pursued it not by faith, but as if it were by works. They stumbled over the stumbling stone,
45_9_33: as it is written: "Behold, I am laying in Zion a stone of stumbling and a rock of offense; and the one who believes in Him will not be put to shame."
45_10_1: Brothers and sisters, my heart's desire and prayer to God for Israel is for their salvation.
45_10_2: For I can testify that they have a zeal for God, but not according to knowledge.
45_10_3: For, being ignorant of the righteousness of God and seeking to establish their own, they did not submit to God's righteousness.
45_10_4: For Christ is the end of the law for righteousness to everyone who believes.
45_10_5: For Moses writes about the righteousness that is based on the law: "The person who does these things shall live by them."
45_10_6: But the righteousness that comes from faith says: "Do not say in your heart, 'Who will ascend into heaven?'" — that is, to bring Christ down —
45_10_7: or, "'Who will descend into the abyss?'" — that is, to bring Christ up from the dead.
45_10_8: But what does it say? "The word is near you, in your mouth and in your heart" — that is, the message of faith that we proclaim:
45_10_9: because if you confess with your mouth that Jesus is Lord and believe in your heart that God raised Him from the dead, you will be saved.
45_10_10: For with the heart one believes and is justified, and with the mouth one confesses and is saved.
45_10_11: For the Scripture says: "Everyone who believes in Him will not be put to shame."
45_10_12: For there is no distinction between Jew and Greek, since the same Lord of all richly blesses all who call on Him.
45_10_13: For "everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved."
45_10_14: How then will they call on Him in whom they have not believed? And how can they believe in Him of whom they have not heard? And how can they hear without someone preaching?
45_10_15: And how can they preach unless they are sent? As it is written: "How beautiful are the feet of those who bring good news!"
45_10_16: But not all have obeyed the gospel. For Isaiah says, "Lord, who has believed our message?"
45_10_17: So faith comes from hearing, and hearing through the word of Christ.
45_10_18: But I ask: have they not heard? Yes indeed! "Their voice has gone out to all the earth, and their words to the ends of the world."
45_10_19: But I ask: did Israel not understand? First Moses says, "I will make you jealous of those who are not a nation; I will provoke you to anger by a foolish nation."
45_10_20: Then Isaiah is so bold as to say: "I was found by those who were not looking for Me; I revealed Myself to those who were not asking for Me."
45_10_21: But concerning Israel He says: "All day long I have held out My hands to a disobedient and defiant people."
45_11_1: I ask then, has God rejected His people? Absolutely not! For I myself am an Israelite, a descendant of Abraham, from the tribe of Benjamin.
45_11_2: God has not rejected His people whom He foreknew. Or do you not know what the Scripture says about Elijah, how he pleads with God against Israel?
45_11_3: "Lord, they have killed Your prophets and torn down Your altars, and I alone am left, and they are trying to take my life."
45_11_4: But what is God's answer to him? "I have kept for Myself seven thousand men who have not bowed the knee to Baal."
45_11_5: So too at the present time there is a remnant, chosen by grace.
45_11_6: But if by grace, then it is no longer on the basis of works; otherwise grace would no longer be grace.
45_11_7: What then? Israel did not obtain what it was seeking. The elect obtained it, but the rest were hardened,
45_11_8: as it is written: "God gave them a spirit of stupor, eyes that could not see and ears that could not hear, down to this very day."
45_11_9: And David says: "Let their table become a snare and a trap, a stumbling block and a retribution to them.
45_11_10: Let their eyes be darkened so that they cannot see, and bend their backs forever."
45_11_11: So I ask, did they stumble in order that they might fall? Absolutely not! Rather, through their trespass, salvation has come to the Gentiles, so as to make Israel jealous.
45_11_12: Now if their trespass means riches for the world, and their failure riches for the Gentiles, how much more will their fullness bring!
45_11_13: Now I am speaking to you Gentiles. Inasmuch as I am an apostle to the Gentiles, I magnify my ministry
45_11_14: in hopes that I might make my own people jealous and save some of them.
45_11_15: For if their rejection means the reconciliation of the world, what will their acceptance mean but life from the dead?
45_11_16: If the firstfruit is holy, so is the whole batch; and if the root is holy, so are the branches.
45_11_17: But if some of the branches were broken off, and you — though a wild olive branch — were grafted in among them and have come to share in the rich root of the cultivated olive tree,
45_11_18: do not boast over the branches. If you do boast, remember that it is not you who supports the root, but the root that supports you.
45_11_19: Then you will say, "Branches were broken off so that I might be grafted in."
45_11_20: True enough — they were broken off because of unbelief, but you stand by faith. Do not be arrogant, but beware,
45_11_21: for if God did not spare the natural branches, He will not spare you either.
45_11_22: Therefore consider the kindness and severity of God: severity toward those who have fallen, but God's kindness toward you — if you continue in His kindness. Otherwise you also will be cut off.
45_11_23: And even they, if they do not remain in unbelief, will be grafted in, for God has the power to graft them in again.
45_11_24: For if you were cut from what is by nature a wild olive tree and grafted, contrary to nature, into a cultivated olive tree, how much more will these natural branches be grafted back into their own olive tree?
45_11_25: I do not want you to be unaware of this mystery, brothers and sisters — so that you will not be conceited: a partial hardening has come upon Israel until the fullness of the Gentiles has come in.
45_11_26: And in this way all Israel will be saved, as it is written: "The Deliverer will come from Zion; He will turn away ungodliness from Jacob;
45_11_27: and this will be My covenant with them when I take away their sins."
45_11_28: Regarding the gospel, they are enemies for your sake; but regarding election, they are beloved for the sake of the patriarchs,
45_11_29: for the gracious gifts and the calling of God are irrevocable.
45_11_30: For just as you were once disobedient to God but have now received mercy because of their disobedience,
45_11_31: so they too have now been disobedient, in order that by the mercy shown to you they also may now receive mercy.
45_11_32: For God has imprisoned all in disobedience so that He may have mercy on all.
45_11_33: Oh, the depth of the riches both of the wisdom and knowledge of God! How unsearchable are His judgments and how untraceable His ways!
45_11_34: "For who has known the mind of the Lord? Or who has been His counselor?"
45_11_35: "Or who has given a gift to Him that He should be repaid?"
45_11_36: For from Him and through Him and to Him are all things. To Him be the glory forever. Amen.
45_12_1: I appeal to you therefore, brothers and sisters, by the mercies of God, to present your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and pleasing to God — this is your true and proper worship.
45_12_2: Do not be conformed to this age, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind, so that you may discern what is the good, pleasing, and perfect will of God.
45_12_3: For by the grace given to me I say to everyone among you: do not think of yourself more highly than you ought, but think with sober judgment, each according to the measure of faith God has assigned.
45_12_4: For as in one body we have many members, and all the members do not have the same function,
45_12_5: so we, though many, are one body in Christ, and individually members of one another.
45_12_6: Having gifts that differ according to the grace given to us, let us use them: if prophecy, in proportion to our faith;
45_12_7: if service, in our serving; if teaching, in our teaching;
45_12_8: if exhortation, in our exhortation; if giving, with generosity; if leading, with diligence; if showing mercy, with cheerfulness.
45_12_9: Let love be without hypocrisy. Detest what is evil; cling to what is good.
45_12_10: Love one another deeply as brothers and sisters. Outdo one another in showing honor.
45_12_11: Do not lack diligence in zeal; be fervent in spirit; serve the Lord.
45_12_12: Rejoice in hope; be patient in affliction; be persistent in prayer.
45_12_13: Share with the saints in their needs; pursue hospitality.
45_12_14: Bless those who persecute you; bless and do not curse.
45_12_15: Rejoice with those who rejoice; weep with those who weep.
45_12_16: Live in harmony with one another. Do not be proud, but associate with the humble. Do not be wise in your own estimation.
45_12_17: Repay no one evil for evil, but give thought to do what is honorable in the sight of all people.
45_12_18: If possible, as far as it depends on you, live at peace with everyone.
45_12_19: Beloved, do not avenge yourselves, but leave room for God's wrath, for it is written: "Vengeance is Mine; I will repay, says the Lord."
45_12_20: To the contrary: "If your enemy is hungry, feed him; if he is thirsty, give him something to drink; for by doing this you will heap burning coals on his head."
45_12_21: Do not be conquered by evil, but conquer evil with good.
45_13_1: Let every person be subject to the governing authorities. For there is no authority except from God, and the authorities that exist have been instituted by God.
45_13_2: Therefore whoever resists the authorities resists what God has appointed, and those who resist will bring judgment on themselves.
45_13_3: For rulers are not a terror to good conduct, but to bad. Do you want to be unafraid of the one in authority? Then do what is good, and you will receive his approval,
45_13_4: for he is God's servant for your good. But if you do wrong, be afraid, for he does not carry the sword for nothing. He is God's servant, an avenger who brings wrath on the one who does wrong.
45_13_5: Therefore you must submit, not only to avoid wrath but also for the sake of your conscience.
45_13_6: For because of this you also pay taxes, for the authorities are God's servants, attending continually to these very duties.
45_13_7: Pay to all what is owed them: taxes to whom taxes are owed, revenue to whom revenue is owed, respect to whom respect is owed, honor to whom honor is owed.
45_13_8: Owe no one anything except to love one another, for the one who loves another has fulfilled the law.
45_13_9: For the commandments — "You shall not commit adultery, You shall not murder, You shall not steal, You shall not covet" — and any other commandment are summed up in this word: "You shall love your neighbor as yourself."
45_13_10: Love does no wrong to a neighbor; therefore love is the fulfillment of the law.
45_13_11: Besides this, you know the time — it is already the hour for you to wake up from sleep, because our salvation is nearer now than when we first believed.
45_13_12: The night is nearly over; the day is near. So let us discard the deeds of darkness and put on the armor of light.
45_13_13: Let us walk with decency, as in the daytime: not in carousing and drunkenness, not in sexual immorality and promiscuity, not in quarreling and jealousy.
45_13_14: But put on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make no plans to gratify the desires of the flesh.
45_14_1: Welcome anyone who is weak in faith, but do not argue about disputed matters.
45_14_2: One person believes he may eat anything, while one who is weak in faith eats only vegetables.
45_14_3: The one who eats must not look down on the one who does not eat, and the one who does not eat must not judge the one who eats, for God has accepted him.
45_14_4: Who are you to judge another's household servant? Before his own master he stands or falls — and he will stand, for the Lord is able to make him stand.
45_14_5: One person considers one day to be more important than another; another considers every day the same. Let each one be fully convinced in his own mind.
45_14_6: The one who observes the day does so to the Lord. The one who eats does so to the Lord, since he gives thanks to God; the one who abstains, abstains to the Lord and also gives thanks to God.
45_14_7: For none of us lives for himself, and no one dies for himself.
45_14_8: If we live, we live for the Lord; and if we die, we die for the Lord. So whether we live or die, we belong to the Lord.
45_14_9: Christ died and returned to life for this very purpose — that He might be Lord over both the dead and the living.
45_14_10: But you, why do you judge your brother or sister? Or why do you look down on your brother or sister? For we will all stand before the judgment seat of God.
45_14_11: For it is written: "As I live, says the Lord, every knee will bow to Me, and every tongue will give praise to God."
45_14_12: So then, each of us will give an account of himself to God.
45_14_13: Therefore let us no longer judge one another. Instead, decide never to put a stumbling block or pitfall in the way of your brother or sister.
45_14_14: I know and am persuaded in the Lord Jesus that nothing is unclean in itself. But to someone who considers a thing to be unclean, to that person it is unclean.
45_14_15: For if your brother or sister is hurt by what you eat, you are no longer walking in love. Do not destroy with your food someone for whom Christ died.
45_14_16: Therefore do not let what you regard as good be slandered.
45_14_17: For the kingdom of God is not a matter of eating and drinking, but of righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit.
45_14_18: Whoever serves Christ in this way is acceptable to God and receives human approval.
45_14_19: So then, let us pursue what promotes peace and what builds up one another.
45_14_20: Do not tear down God's work because of food. Everything is clean, but it is wrong to make someone stumble by what he eats.
45_14_21: It is a good thing not to eat meat or drink wine or do anything that makes your brother or sister stumble.
45_14_22: The faith you have, keep between yourself and God. Blessed is the one who has no reason to condemn himself for what he approves.
45_14_23: But whoever doubts stands condemned if he eats, because his eating is not from faith — and everything that is not from faith is sin.
45_15_1: Now we who are strong have an obligation to bear with the weaknesses of those without strength, and not to please ourselves.
45_15_2: Let each of us please his neighbor for his good, to build him up.
45_15_3: For even Christ did not please Himself. As it is written: "The insults of those who insult You fell on Me."
45_15_4: For whatever was written in former days was written for our instruction, so that through endurance and through the encouragement of the Scriptures we might have hope.
45_15_5: May the God who gives endurance and encouragement grant you to live in harmony with one another, according to Christ Jesus,
45_15_6: so that together you may with one voice glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
45_15_7: Therefore accept one another, just as Christ also accepted you, to the glory of God.
45_15_8: For I tell you that Christ became a servant to the circumcised to show God's truthfulness, in order to confirm the promises made to the fathers,
45_15_9: and so that the Gentiles might glorify God for His mercy. As it is written: "Therefore I will praise You among the Gentiles, and sing to Your name."
45_15_10: And again it says: "Rejoice, O Gentiles, with His people!"
45_15_11: And again: "Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles, and let all the peoples extol Him!"
45_15_12: And again, Isaiah says: "The root of Jesse will appear — the one who rises to rule the Gentiles; in Him the Gentiles will hope."
45_15_13: Now may the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace as you believe, so that you may overflow with hope by the power of the Holy Spirit.
45_15_14: I myself am convinced about you, my brothers and sisters, that you are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, and able to instruct one another.
45_15_15: Nevertheless, I have written to you quite boldly on some points as a reminder, because of the grace given to me by God
45_15_16: to be a minister of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles, serving as a priest of the gospel of God, so that the offering of the Gentiles might be acceptable, sanctified by the Holy Spirit.
45_15_17: Therefore in Christ Jesus I have reason to boast about what pertains to God.
45_15_18: For I will not dare to speak of anything except what Christ has accomplished through me to bring the Gentiles to obedience — by word and deed,
45_15_19: by the power of signs and wonders, and by the power of the Spirit of God — so that from Jerusalem and all the way around to Illyricum I have fully proclaimed the gospel of Christ.
45_15_20: And so I make it my ambition to preach the gospel where Christ has not yet been named, so that I would not be building on someone else's foundation,
45_15_21: but as it is written: "Those who were not told about Him will see, and those who have not heard will understand."
45_15_22: That is why I have so often been hindered from coming to you.
45_15_23: But now, since I no longer have any room for work in these regions, and since I have longed for many years to come to you,
45_15_24: whenever I journey to Spain — for I hope to see you in passing, and to be helped on my way there by you, once I have first enjoyed your company for a while —
45_15_25: but now I am traveling to Jerusalem to serve the saints.
45_15_26: For Macedonia and Achaia were pleased to make a contribution for the poor among the saints in Jerusalem.
45_15_27: Yes, they were pleased to do it, and indeed they are indebted to them. For if the Gentiles have shared in their spiritual blessings, they are obligated to minister to them in material needs.
45_15_28: When therefore I have completed this and have safely delivered this fruit to them, I will go by way of you to Spain.
45_15_29: And I know that when I come to you, I will come in the fullness of the blessing of Christ.
45_15_30: I appeal to you, brothers and sisters, through our Lord Jesus Christ and through the love of the Spirit, to strive together with me in your prayers to God on my behalf,
45_15_31: that I may be rescued from the unbelievers in Judea, and that my ministry to Jerusalem may be acceptable to the saints,
45_15_32: so that by God's will I may come to you with joy and be refreshed together with you.
45_15_33: May the God of peace be with you all. Amen.
45_16_1: I commend to you our sister Phoebe, a servant of the church in Cenchreae,
45_16_2: so that you may receive her in the Lord in a manner worthy of the saints, and assist her in whatever she may need from you; for she has indeed been a benefactor of many — and of myself as well.
45_16_3: Give my greetings to Prisca and Aquila, my coworkers in Christ Jesus,
45_16_4: who risked their own necks for my life, to whom not only I give thanks but also all the churches of the Gentiles.
45_16_5: Greet also the church that meets in their home. Greet my beloved Epaenetus, who was the first convert to Christ from Asia.
45_16_6: Greet Mary, who has worked very hard for you.
45_16_7: Greet Andronicus and Junia, my fellow Jews and fellow prisoners. They are well known among the apostles, and they were in Christ before me.
45_16_8: Greet Ampliatus, my dear friend in the Lord.
45_16_9: Greet Urbanus, our coworker in Christ, and my dear friend Stachys.
45_16_10: Greet Apelles, who is approved in Christ. Greet those who belong to the household of Aristobulus.
45_16_11: Greet Herodion, my fellow Jew. Greet those in the Lord who belong to the household of Narcissus.
45_16_12: Greet Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who have worked hard in the Lord. Greet my dear friend Persis, who has worked very hard in the Lord.
45_16_13: Greet Rufus, chosen in the Lord, and his mother — who has also been a mother to me.
45_16_14: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers and sisters with them.
45_16_15: Greet Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints who are with them.
45_16_16: Greet one another with a holy kiss. All the churches of Christ send you greetings.
45_16_17: Now I urge you, brothers and sisters, to watch out for those who cause divisions and create obstacles contrary to the teaching you have learned. Avoid them,
45_16_18: for such people do not serve our Lord Christ but their own appetites. By smooth talk and flattery they deceive the hearts of the unsuspecting.
45_16_19: For your obedience has reached everyone. I rejoice over you, but I want you to be wise in what is good and innocent in what is evil.
45_16_20: The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus be with you.
45_16_21: Timothy, my coworker, sends you greetings, as do Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater, my fellow countrymen.
45_16_22: I, Tertius, who wrote this letter, greet you in the Lord.
45_16_23: Gaius, who is host to me and to the whole church, greets you. Erastus, the city treasurer, and our brother Quartus greet you.
45_16_24: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.
45_16_25: Now to Him who is able to strengthen you according to my gospel and the preaching of Jesus Christ — according to the revelation of the mystery that was kept silent for long ages
45_16_26: but has now been revealed and made known through the prophetic Scriptures, according to the command of the eternal God, to advance the obedience of faith among all the Gentiles—
45_16_27: to the only wise God, through Jesus Christ, be the glory forever. Amen.
48_1_1: Paul, an apostle — not sent from men nor through human authority, but through Jesus Christ and God the Father who raised Him from the dead —
48_1_2: and all the brothers and sisters who are with me, To the churches of Galatia:
48_1_3: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ,
48_1_4: who gave Himself for our sins to rescue us from this present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father,
48_1_5: to whom be glory forever and ever. Amen.
48_1_6: I am amazed that you are turning away so quickly from Him who called you in the grace of Christ, to a different gospel —
48_1_7: not that there is another; but there are some who trouble you and want to distort the gospel of Christ.
48_1_8: But even if we, or an angel from heaven, should preach to you a gospel contrary to what we have preached to you, let him be accursed.
48_1_9: As we have said before, so I say again: if anyone is preaching to you a gospel contrary to what you received, let him be accursed.
48_1_10: For am I now seeking the approval of people, or of God? Or am I trying to please people? If I were still trying to please people, I would not be a servant of Christ.
48_1_11: But I want you to know, brothers and sisters, that the gospel which was preached by me is not of human origin.
48_1_12: For I neither received it from any man, nor was I taught it, but it came through a direct revelation of Jesus Christ.
48_1_13: For you have heard of my former way of life in Judaism, how I violently persecuted the church of God beyond measure and tried to destroy it.
48_1_14: And I was advancing in Judaism beyond many of my contemporaries among my own people, being extremely zealous for the traditions of my fathers.
48_1_15: But when God, who had set me apart from my mother's womb and called me through His grace, was pleased
48_1_16: to reveal His Son in me, so that I might preach Him among the Gentiles, I did not immediately consult with anyone of flesh and blood,
48_1_17: nor did I go up to Jerusalem to those who were apostles before me; but I went to Arabia, and returned again to Damascus.
48_1_18: Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to get to know Peter, and stayed with him fifteen days.
48_1_19: But I saw none of the other apostles except James, the Lord's brother.
48_1_20: Now in what I write to you, I assure you before God that I am not lying.
48_1_21: Afterward I went into the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
48_1_22: And I was still personally unknown to the churches of Judea which were in Christ.
48_1_23: They only kept hearing: "He who formerly persecuted us now preaches the faith he once tried to destroy."
48_1_24: And they glorified God because of me.
48_2_1: Then after fourteen years I went up again to Jerusalem with Barnabas, and also took Titus with me.
48_2_2: I went up by revelation, and set before them the gospel which I preach among the Gentiles — but privately to those recognized as leaders — so that I might not be running, or had run, in vain.
48_2_3: Yet not even Titus who was with me, though he was a Greek, was compelled to be circumcised.
48_2_4: But because of false brothers secretly brought in — who slipped in to spy on the freedom we have in Christ Jesus, so that they might bring us into bondage —
48_2_5: to them we did not yield in submission even for a moment, so that the truth of the gospel might be preserved for you.
48_2_6: But from those who seemed to be something — whatever they were makes no difference to me; God shows no partiality — those who seemed important added nothing to me.
48_2_7: On the contrary, when they saw that the gospel for the uncircumcised had been entrusted to me, just as the gospel for the circumcised was to Peter,
48_2_8: for the same God who worked effectively through Peter for the apostleship to the circumcised also worked effectively through me for the Gentiles —
48_2_9: and when James, Cephas, and John — who were recognized as pillars — perceived the grace that had been given to me, they gave me and Barnabas the right hand of fellowship, that we should go to the Gentiles and they to the circumcised.
48_2_10: They asked only that we should remember the poor, the very thing I was also eager to do.
48_2_11: But when Peter came to Antioch, I opposed him to his face, because he stood condemned.
48_2_12: For before certain men came from James, he would eat with the Gentiles; but when they came, he withdrew and separated himself, fearing those of the circumcision party.
48_2_13: And the rest of the Jews joined him in that hypocrisy, so that even Barnabas was led astray by their hypocrisy.
48_2_14: But when I saw that they were not walking in step with the truth of the gospel, I said to Peter before them all, "If you, being a Jew, live like a Gentile and not as a Jew, how can you compel the Gentiles to live like Jews?"
48_2_15: We who are Jews by birth, and not Gentile sinners,
48_2_16: knowing that a person is not justified by the works of the law but through faith in Jesus Christ, even we have believed in Christ Jesus, so that we might be justified by faith in Christ and not by the works of the law; for by the works of the law no one will be justified.
48_2_17: "But if, while we seek to be justified in Christ, we ourselves are also found to be sinners, is Christ therefore a promoter of sin? Absolutely not!
48_2_18: For if I rebuild what I tore down, I prove myself to be a lawbreaker.
48_2_19: For through the law I died to the law, so that I might live for God.
48_2_20: I have been crucified with Christ; it is no longer I who live, but Christ lives in me; and the life which I now live in the flesh I live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave Himself up for me.
48_2_21: I do not set aside the grace of God; for if righteousness comes through the law, then Christ died for nothing."
48_3_1: O foolish Galatians! Who has bewitched you? Before your very eyes Jesus Christ was publicly portrayed as crucified!
48_3_2: Let me ask you this one thing: Did you receive the Spirit by the works of the law or by believing what you heard?
48_3_3: Are you so foolish? Having begun by the Spirit, are you now finishing by your own human effort in the flesh?
48_3_4: Have you experienced so many things for nothing — if indeed it was for nothing?
48_3_5: So then, does He who supplies the Spirit to you and works miracles among you do it by the works of the law, or by believing what you heard —
48_3_6: just as Abraham "believed God, and it was credited to him as righteousness"?
48_3_7: Therefore know that those who are of faith are the sons of Abraham.
48_3_8: And the Scripture, foreseeing that God would justify the Gentiles by faith, proclaimed the gospel to Abraham beforehand, saying, "In you all the nations shall be blessed."
48_3_9: So then, those who are of faith are blessed along with Abraham, the man of faith.
48_3_10: For all who rely on the works of the law are under a curse; for it is written, "Cursed is everyone who does not continue in all things written in the book of the law, to do them."
48_3_11: But that no one is justified before God by the law is evident, for "the righteous shall live by faith."
48_3_12: Yet the law is not based on faith, but "the one who does them shall live by them."
48_3_13: Christ redeemed us from the curse of the law by becoming a curse for us — for it is written, "Cursed is everyone who hangs on a tree" —
48_3_14: so that in Christ Jesus the blessing of Abraham might come to the Gentiles, and that we might receive the promised Spirit through faith.
48_3_15: Brothers and sisters, I speak in terms of everyday life: even a human covenant, once it has been ratified, no one annuls or adds conditions to it.
48_3_16: Now the promises were spoken to Abraham and to his Seed. He does not say, "And to seeds," as of many, but as of one, "And to your Seed," who is Christ.
48_3_17: And this is what I mean: the law, which came four hundred and thirty years later, cannot annul the covenant previously ratified by God, so as to make the promise void.
48_3_18: For if the inheritance is based on the law, it is no longer based on the promise; but God graciously gave it to Abraham through the promise.
48_3_19: What purpose then does the law serve? It was added because of transgressions, until the Seed should come to whom the promise was made; and it was ordained through angels by the hand of a mediator.
48_3_20: Now a mediator is not for one party only, but God is one.
48_3_21: Is the law then contrary to the promises of God? Absolutely not! For if a law had been given which could give life, then righteousness would indeed be by the law.
48_3_22: But the Scripture has shut up everyone under sin, so that the promise by faith in Jesus Christ might be given to those who believe.
48_3_23: But before faith came, we were held captive under the law, imprisoned until the coming faith would be revealed.
48_3_24: Therefore the law became our guardian to lead us to Christ, so that we might be justified by faith.
48_3_25: But now that faith has come, we are no longer under a guardian.
48_3_26: For you are all sons of God through faith in Christ Jesus.
48_3_27: For as many of you as were baptized into Christ have clothed yourselves with Christ.
48_3_28: There is neither Jew nor Greek, there is neither slave nor free, there is neither male nor female; for you are all one in Christ Jesus.
48_3_29: And if you belong to Christ, then you are Abraham's seed, and heirs according to the promise.
48_4_1: Now I say that the heir, as long as he is a child, differs in no way from a slave, though he is master of all,
48_4_2: but is under guardians and trustees until the date set by his father.
48_4_3: Even so we, when we were children, were in bondage under the elementary principles of the world.
48_4_4: But when the fullness of the time had come, God sent forth His Son, born of a woman, born under the law,
48_4_5: to redeem those who were under the law, so that we might receive adoption as sons.
48_4_6: And because you are sons, God has sent forth the Spirit of His Son into our hearts, crying out, "Abba, Father!"
48_4_7: Therefore you are no longer a slave but a son, and if a son, then an heir of God through Christ.
48_4_8: But then, when you did not know God, you were enslaved to those that by nature are not gods.
48_4_9: But now that you have come to know God — or rather to be known by God — how can you turn back again to the weak and worthless elementary principles? Do you want to be enslaved to them all over again?
48_4_10: You are carefully observing days and months and seasons and years.
48_4_11: I fear for you, that perhaps I have labored for you in vain.
48_4_12: Brothers and sisters, I urge you: become like me, for I also became like you. You have done me no wrong.
48_4_13: You know that it was because of a bodily infirmity that I first preached the gospel to you.
48_4_14: And though my physical condition was a trial to you, you did not despise or reject me, but received me as an angel of God, even as Christ Jesus Himself.
48_4_15: Where then is the blessing you felt then? For I testify to you that, if possible, you would have plucked out your own eyes and given them to me.
48_4_16: Have I therefore become your enemy because I tell you the truth?
48_4_17: Those people are zealously courting you, but not for good; they want to shut you out so that you will pursue them.
48_4_18: But it is always good to be pursued for a good purpose, and not only when I am present with you.
48_4_19: My little children, for whom I am again suffering labor pains until Christ is formed in you,
48_4_20: I wish I could be present with you now and change my tone; for I am perplexed about you.
48_4_21: Tell me, you who desire to be under the law, do you not hear what the law actually says?
48_4_22: For it is written that Abraham had two sons: one by a slave woman and the other by a free woman.
48_4_23: But the son of the slave woman was born according to the flesh, and the son of the free woman through promise.
48_4_24: These things are to be understood figuratively: the women represent two covenants. One is from Mount Sinai, bearing children into bondage — this is Hagar.
48_4_25: For Hagar represents Mount Sinai in Arabia and corresponds to the present Jerusalem, for she is in slavery with her children.
48_4_26: But the Jerusalem above is free, and she is our mother.
48_4_27: For it is written: "Rejoice, O barren woman who does not bear! Break forth and shout, you who are not in labor! For the children of the desolate woman will be many, more numerous than those of the one who has a husband."
48_4_28: Now you, brothers and sisters, like Isaac, are children of promise.
48_4_29: But just as at that time the one born according to the flesh persecuted the one born according to the Spirit, so it is now.
48_4_30: But what does the Scripture say? "Drive out the slave woman and her son, for the son of the slave will never share the inheritance with the son of the free woman."
48_4_31: So then, brothers and sisters, we are not children of the slave woman but of the free.
48_5_1: For freedom Christ has set us free; stand firm therefore, and do not submit again to a yoke of slavery.
48_5_2: Take note — I, Paul, say to you that if you become circumcised, Christ will be of no benefit to you at all.
48_5_3: And I testify again to every man who becomes circumcised that he is obligated to keep the whole law.
48_5_4: You who seek to be justified by law are severed from Christ; you have fallen from grace.
48_5_5: For we through the Spirit, by faith, eagerly wait for the hope of righteousness.
48_5_6: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision nor uncircumcision means anything, but only faith working through love.
48_5_7: You were running well. Who hindered you from obeying the truth?
48_5_8: This persuasion does not come from the One who calls you.
48_5_9: A little leaven leavens the whole lump of dough.
48_5_10: I have confidence in you in the Lord that you will take no other view; but whoever it is that is troubling you will bear the penalty, whoever he is.
48_5_11: And I, brothers and sisters, if I still preach circumcision, why am I still being persecuted? In that case the offense of the cross has been abolished.
48_5_12: I wish that those who are unsettling you would go even further and mutilate themselves!
48_5_13: For you, brothers and sisters, have been called to freedom; only do not use your freedom as an opportunity for the flesh, but through love serve one another.
48_5_14: For the whole law is fulfilled in one statement: "You shall love your neighbor as yourself."
48_5_15: But if you bite and devour one another, watch out — you will be consumed by one another!
48_5_16: I say then: walk by the Spirit, and you will certainly not carry out the desire of the flesh.
48_5_17: For the flesh desires what is against the Spirit, and the Spirit what is against the flesh; these are opposed to each other, so that you do not do the things you wish.
48_5_18: But if you are led by the Spirit, you are not under the law.
48_5_19: Now the works of the flesh are evident: sexual immorality, moral impurity, sensuality,
48_5_20: idolatry, sorcery, hatreds, strife, jealousy, outbursts of anger, selfish ambitions, dissensions, factions,
48_5_21: envy, drunkenness, carousing, and things like these — of which I warn you, as I warned you before, that those who practice such things will not inherit the kingdom of God.
48_5_22: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness,
48_5_23: gentleness, self-control. Against such things there is no law.
48_5_24: And those who belong to Christ Jesus have crucified the flesh with its passions and desires.
48_5_25: If we live by the Spirit, let us also keep in step with the Spirit.
48_5_26: Let us not become conceited, provoking one another, envying one another.
48_6_1: Brothers and sisters, if someone is overtaken in any trespass, you who are spiritual should restore that person with a spirit of gentleness, keeping watch on yourself so that you also will not be tempted.
48_6_2: Bear one another's burdens, and so fulfill the law of Christ.
48_6_3: For if anyone thinks he is something when he is nothing, he deceives himself.
48_6_4: But let each one examine his own work, and then his reason for boasting will be in himself alone and not in comparing himself with another.
48_6_5: For each one will have to carry his own load.
48_6_6: Let the one who is taught the word share all good things with the one who teaches.
48_6_7: Do not be deceived, God is not mocked; for whatever a person sows, that he will also reap.
48_6_8: For the one who sows to his own flesh will from the flesh reap destruction, but the one who sows to the Spirit will from the Spirit reap eternal life.
48_6_9: And let us not grow weary of doing good, for in due season we will reap if we do not give up.
48_6_10: Therefore, as we have opportunity, let us do good to all people, and especially to those who are of the household of faith.
48_6_11: See with what large letters I have written to you with my own hand!
48_6_12: As many as desire to make a good showing in the flesh are the ones who compel you to be circumcised — only so that they will not be persecuted for the cross of Christ.
48_6_13: For not even those who are circumcised keep the law themselves; they want to have you circumcised so that they can boast about your flesh.
48_6_14: But as for me, may I never boast except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, by which the world has been crucified to me and I to the world.
48_6_15: For neither circumcision nor uncircumcision counts for anything; what matters is a new creation.
48_6_16: And as many as walk by this rule — peace and mercy be upon them, and upon the Israel of God.
48_6_17: From now on let no one cause me trouble, for I bear on my body the marks of Jesus.
48_6_18: Brothers and sisters, the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.
50_1_1: Paul and Timothy, servants of Christ Jesus, To all the saints in Christ Jesus who are in Philippi, including the overseers and deacons:
50_1_2: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
50_1_3: I thank my God in every remembrance of you,
50_1_4: always in every prayer of mine for you all, making my prayer with joy,
50_1_5: because of your partnership in the gospel from the first day until now.
50_1_6: And I am confident of this very thing: that He who began a good work in you will carry it on to completion until the day of Christ Jesus.
50_1_7: It is right for me to feel this way about all of you, because I hold you in my heart; inasmuch as both in my chains and in the defense and confirmation of the gospel, you are all partners with me in grace.
50_1_8: For God is my witness, how deeply I long for you all with the tender affection of Christ Jesus.
50_1_9: And this I pray: that your love may abound still more and more in real knowledge and every kind of discernment,
50_1_10: so that you may approve the things that are excellent, that you may be pure and blameless until the day of Christ,
50_1_11: being filled with the fruit of righteousness that comes through Jesus Christ, to the glory and praise of God.
50_1_12: Now I want you to know, brothers and sisters, that what has happened to me has actually served to advance the gospel,
50_1_13: so that it has become known throughout the whole imperial guard, and to everyone else, that my imprisonment is for Christ.
50_1_14: And most of the brothers and sisters, having gained confidence in the Lord by my imprisonment, dare even more to speak the word of God fearlessly.
50_1_15: Some indeed preach Christ even from envy and rivalry, but others also from goodwill.
50_1_16: These preach out of love, knowing that I have been appointed for the defense of the gospel.
50_1_17: The others proclaim Christ out of selfish ambition, not sincerely, thinking they will cause me trouble in my imprisonment.
50_1_18: What does it matter? Only that in every way — whether from false motives or true — Christ is proclaimed, and in this I rejoice. Yes, and I will continue to rejoice,
50_1_19: for I know that this will lead to my deliverance through your prayers and the provision of the Spirit of Jesus Christ,
50_1_20: according to my earnest expectation and hope that in nothing I will be ashamed, but that with all boldness, as always, so now also Christ will be magnified in my body, whether by life or by death.
50_1_21: For to me, to live is Christ, and to die is gain.
50_1_22: But if I live on in the flesh, this will mean fruitful labor for me; yet what I shall choose I cannot tell.
50_1_23: For I am torn between the two, having a desire to depart and be with Christ — which is far better —
50_1_24: but to remain in the flesh is more necessary for your sake.
50_1_25: And being confident of this, I know that I will remain and continue with you all for your progress and joy in the faith,
50_1_26: so that your boasting in Christ Jesus may abound through my coming to you again.
50_1_27: Only let your manner of life be worthy of the gospel of Christ, so that whether I come and see you or am absent, I may hear that you are standing firm in one spirit, striving side by side with one accord for the faith of the gospel,
50_1_28: and not frightened in anything by your opponents. This is a sign of destruction for them, but of your salvation — and this from God.
50_1_29: For to you it has been granted on behalf of Christ, not only to believe in Him, but also to suffer for His sake,
50_1_30: since you are engaged in the same struggle that you saw in me and now hear is in me.
50_2_1: Therefore if there is any encouragement in Christ, if any comfort of love, if any fellowship of the Spirit, if any affection and compassion,
50_2_2: make my joy complete by being of the same mind, having the same love, united in spirit, intent on one purpose.
50_2_3: Let nothing be done through selfish ambition or empty conceit, but in humility consider others as more important than yourselves.
50_2_4: Let each of you look out not only for his own interests, but also for the interests of others.
50_2_5: Let this same attitude be in you that was in Christ Jesus,
50_2_6: who, existing in the form of God, did not consider equality with God something to be exploited,
50_2_7: but emptied Himself, taking the form of a servant, being made in the likeness of men.
50_2_8: And being found in appearance as a man, He humbled Himself and became obedient to the point of death, even death on a cross.
50_2_9: Therefore God also has highly exalted Him and given Him the name which is above every name,
50_2_10: so that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow — of those in heaven, and of those on earth, and of those under the earth —
50_2_11: and every tongue confess that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father.
50_2_12: Therefore, my dear friends, as you have always obeyed — not only as in my presence but even more in my absence — work out your own salvation with fear and trembling;
50_2_13: for it is God who is at work in you, both to will and to work for His good purpose.
50_2_14: Do all things without grumbling or disputing,
50_2_15: so that you may be blameless and pure, children of God without fault in the midst of a crooked and perverse generation, among whom you shine like stars in the world,
50_2_16: holding firm the word of life, so that in the day of Christ I may rejoice that I have not run in vain or labored in vain.
50_2_17: But even if I am being poured out as a drink offering upon the sacrifice and service of your faith, I am glad and rejoice with you all.
50_2_18: And in the same way you also be glad and rejoice with me.
50_2_19: But I hope in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to you soon, so that I also may be encouraged when I learn how you are doing.
50_2_20: For I have no one like him who will be genuinely concerned for your welfare.
50_2_21: For they all seek their own interests, not those of Jesus Christ.
50_2_22: But you know his proven character — how as a son with his father he served with me in the gospel.
50_2_23: Therefore I hope to send him as soon as I see how things will go with me.
50_2_24: But I trust in the Lord that I myself will also come to you shortly.
50_2_25: Yet I considered it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus — my brother, fellow worker, and fellow soldier, and your messenger who ministered to my need —
50_2_26: since he was longing for you all, and was deeply distressed because you had heard that he was sick.
50_2_27: For indeed he was sick, to the point of death; but God had mercy on him — and not only on him but on me also, so that I would not have sorrow upon sorrow.
50_2_28: Therefore I sent him all the more eagerly, so that when you see him again you may rejoice, and I may be less anxious.
50_2_29: Receive him therefore in the Lord with all joy, and hold such people in high esteem,
50_2_30: because he came close to death for the work of Christ, risking his life to make up what was lacking in your service toward me.
50_3_1: Finally, my brothers and sisters, rejoice in the Lord. For me to write the same things to you is no trouble at all, and it is a safeguard for you.
50_3_2: Watch out for the dogs, watch out for the evil workers, watch out for those who mutilate the flesh.
50_3_3: For we are the circumcision — those who worship by the Spirit of God, boast in Christ Jesus, and put no confidence in the flesh —
50_3_4: though I myself have reasons for confidence in the flesh. If anyone else thinks he has grounds for confidence in the flesh, I have even more:
50_3_5: circumcised the eighth day, of the nation of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin, a Hebrew of Hebrews; as to the law, a Pharisee;
50_3_6: as to zeal, a persecutor of the church; as to the righteousness which is in the law, found blameless.
50_3_7: But whatever things were gain to me, those I have counted as loss for the sake of Christ.
50_3_8: Indeed, I count all things as loss because of the surpassing value of knowing Christ Jesus my Lord. For His sake I have suffered the loss of all things and count them as rubbish, so that I may gain Christ
50_3_9: and be found in Him, not having a righteousness of my own from the law, but that which comes through faith in Christ — the righteousness from God based on faith —
50_3_10: that I may know Him and the power of His resurrection, and the fellowship of His sufferings, being conformed to His death,
50_3_11: in hopes that somehow I may attain to the resurrection from the dead.
50_3_12: Not that I have already obtained this or am already perfect; but I press on to take hold of that for which Christ Jesus has also taken hold of me.
50_3_13: Brothers and sisters, I do not consider myself to have apprehended it; but one thing I do — forgetting what lies behind and reaching forward to what lies ahead —
50_3_14: I press on toward the goal for the prize of God's heavenly call in Christ Jesus.
50_3_15: Therefore let all of us who are mature have this same attitude; and if in anything you think otherwise, God will reveal even this to you.
50_3_16: In any case, let us live up to the truth we have already attained.
50_3_17: Join in imitating me, brothers and sisters, and pay careful attention to those who walk according to the example you have in us.
50_3_18: For many walk, of whom I have often told you — and now tell you even weeping — that they are enemies of the cross of Christ:
50_3_19: whose end is destruction, whose god is their appetite, and whose glory is in their shame — they have their minds set on earthly things.
