47_6_5: beatings, imprisonments, riots, labors, sleepless nights, and hunger;
47_6_6: by purity, knowledge, patience, kindness, the Holy Spirit, and genuine love;
47_6_7: by truthful speech and the power of God; with the weapons of righteousness for the right hand and for the left;
47_6_8: through honor and dishonor, through slander and praise. We are treated as impostors, and yet are true;
47_6_9: as unknown, and yet well known; as dying, and behold, we live; as chastened, and yet not killed;
47_6_10: as sorrowful, yet always rejoicing; as poor, yet making many rich; as having nothing, yet possessing everything.
47_6_11: We have spoken freely to you, Corinthians; our heart is wide open.
47_6_12: You are not restricted by us, but you are restricted in your own affections.
47_6_13: In return — I speak as to children — open wide your hearts to us also.
47_6_14: Do not be unequally yoked with unbelievers. For what partnership has righteousness with lawlessness? Or what fellowship has light with darkness?
47_6_15: What accord has Christ with Belial? Or what portion does a believer share with an unbeliever?
47_6_16: What agreement has the temple of God with idols? For we are the temple of the living God; as God said, 'I will make my dwelling among them and walk among them, and I will be their God, and they shall be my people.'
47_6_17: 'Therefore go out from their midst, and be separate from them, says the Lord, and touch no unclean thing; then I will welcome you,'
47_6_18: 'and I will be a father to you, and you shall be sons and daughters to me, says the Lord Almighty.'
47_7_1: Since we have these promises, beloved, let us cleanse ourselves from every defilement of body and spirit, bringing holiness to completion in the fear of God.
47_7_2: Make room for us in your hearts. We have wronged no one, we have corrupted no one, we have taken advantage of no one.
47_7_3: I do not say this to condemn you, for I said before that you are in our hearts, to die together and to live together.
47_7_4: I am acting with great boldness toward you; I have great pride in you; I am filled with comfort. In all our affliction, I am overflowing with joy.
47_7_5: For even when we came into Macedonia, our bodies had no rest, but we were afflicted at every turn — conflicts on the outside, fears within.
47_7_6: But God, who comforts the downcast, comforted us by the coming of Titus,
47_7_7: and not only by his coming but also by the comfort with which he was comforted by you, as he told us of your longing, your mourning, your zeal for me, so that I rejoiced still more.
47_7_8: For even if I made you grieve with my letter, I do not regret it — though I did regret it, for I see that that letter grieved you, though only for a while.
47_7_9: As it is, I rejoice, not because you were made to grieve, but because your grief led you to repentance. For you felt a godly grief, so that you suffered no loss through us.
47_7_10: For godly grief produces a repentance that leads to salvation without regret, whereas worldly grief produces death.
47_7_11: For see what earnestness this godly grief has produced in you — what eagerness to clear yourselves, what indignation, what fear, what longing, what zeal, what justice! At every point you have proved yourselves innocent in the matter.
47_7_12: So although I wrote to you, it was not for the sake of the one who did the wrong, nor for the sake of the one who was wronged, but in order that your earnestness for us might be revealed to you before God.
47_7_13: Therefore we are comforted. And besides our own comfort, we rejoiced even more at the joy of Titus, because his spirit has been refreshed by you all.
47_7_14: For whatever boasts I made to him about you, I was not put to shame. But just as everything we said to you was true, so also our boasting before Titus has proved true.
47_7_15: And his affection for you is even greater as he remembers the obedience of you all, how you received him with fear and trembling.
47_7_16: I rejoice, because I have complete confidence in you.
47_8_1: We want you to know, brothers, about the grace of God that has been given among the churches of Macedonia,
47_8_2: for in a severe test of affliction, their abundance of joy and their extreme poverty have overflowed in a wealth of generosity on their part.
47_8_3: For they gave according to their means — I testify to this — and even beyond their means, of their own free will,
47_8_4: begging us earnestly for the grace and the privilege of sharing in this ministry to the saints —
47_8_5: and this, not as we expected, but they first gave themselves to the Lord and then by the will of God to us.
47_8_6: Accordingly, we urged Titus that as he had started, so he should complete among you this act of grace.
47_8_7: But as you excel in everything — in faith, in speech, in knowledge, in all earnestness, and in our love for you — see that you excel in this act of grace also.
47_8_8: I say this not as a command, but to prove by the earnestness of others that your love also is genuine.
47_8_9: For you know the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that though He was rich, yet for your sake He became poor, so that you by His poverty might become rich.
47_8_10: And in this matter I give my judgment: this benefits you, who a year ago started not only to do this work but also to desire to do it.
47_8_11: So now finish doing it as well, so that your readiness in desiring it may be matched by your completing it out of what you have.
47_8_12: For if the readiness is there, it is acceptable according to what a person has, not according to what he does not have.
47_8_13: For I do not mean that others should be eased and you burdened, but that as a matter of fairness
47_8_14: your abundance at the present time should supply their need, so that their abundance may supply your need, that there may be equality.
47_8_15: As it is written, 'Whoever gathered much had nothing left over, and whoever gathered little had no lack.'
47_8_16: But thanks be to God, who put into the heart of Titus the same earnest care I have for you.
47_8_17: For he not only accepted our appeal, but being himself very eager he is going to you of his own accord.
47_8_18: With him we are sending the brother who is famous among all the churches for his preaching of the gospel.
47_8_19: And not only that, but he has also been appointed by the churches to travel with us as we carry out this act of grace — for the glory of the Lord Himself and to show our good will.
47_8_20: We take this course so that no one should blame us about this generous gift that is being administered by us,
47_8_21: for we aim at what is honorable not only in the sight of the Lord but also in the sight of men.
47_8_22: And with them we are sending our brother whom we have often tested and found earnest in many matters, but who is now more earnest than ever because of his great confidence in you.
47_8_23: As for Titus, he is my partner and fellow worker for your benefit. As for our brothers, they are messengers of the churches, the glory of Christ.
47_8_24: So give proof before the churches of your love and of our boasting about you to these men.
47_9_1: Now it is unnecessary for me to write to you about this ministry to the saints,
47_9_2: for I know your readiness, of which I boast about you to the people of Macedonia, saying that Achaia has been ready since last year. And your zeal has stirred up most of them.
47_9_3: But I am sending the brothers so that our boasting about you may not prove empty in this matter, so that you may be ready, as I said you would be —
47_9_4: otherwise, if some Macedonians come with me and find that you are not ready, we would be humiliated — to say nothing of you — for being so confident.
47_9_5: So I thought it necessary to urge the brothers to go on ahead to you and arrange in advance for your promised gift of blessing, so that it may be ready as a willing gift and not as something grudgingly given.
47_9_6: The point is this: whoever sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and whoever sows generously will also reap generously.
47_9_7: Each one must give as he has decided in his heart, not reluctantly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver.
47_9_8: And God is able to make all grace abound to you, so that having all sufficiency in all things at all times, you may abound in every good work.
47_9_9: As it is written, 'He has distributed freely, He has given to the poor; His righteousness endures forever.'
47_9_10: He who supplies seed to the sower and bread for food will supply and multiply your seed for sowing and increase the harvest of your righteousness.
47_9_11: You will be enriched in every way to be generous in every way, which through us will produce thanksgiving to God.
47_9_12: For the ministry of this service is not only supplying the needs of the saints but is also overflowing in many thanksgivings to God.
47_9_13: By their approval of this service, they will glorify God because of your submission that flows from your confession of the gospel of Christ, and the generosity of your contribution for them and for all others,
47_9_14: while they long for you and pray for you, because of the surpassing grace of God upon you.
47_9_15: Thanks be to God for His inexpressible gift!
47_10_1: I, Paul, myself appeal to you by the meekness and gentleness of Christ — I who am humble when face to face with you, but bold toward you when I am away!
47_10_2: I beg of you that when I am present I may not have to be bold with the confidence with which I intend to show boldness against some who suspect us of walking according to the flesh.
47_10_3: For though we walk in the flesh, we are not waging war according to the flesh.
47_10_4: For the weapons of our warfare are not of the flesh but have divine power to destroy strongholds.
47_10_5: We destroy arguments and every lofty opinion raised against the knowledge of God, and take every thought captive to obey Christ,
47_10_6: being ready to punish every act of disobedience, once your obedience is complete.
47_10_7: Look at what is before you. If anyone is confident that he is Christ's, let him remind himself that just as he is Christ's, so also are we.
47_10_8: For even if I boast a little too much of our authority, which the Lord gave for building you up and not for tearing you down, I will not be put to shame.
47_10_9: I do not want to appear to be frightening you with my letters.
47_10_10: For they say, 'His letters are weighty and strong, but his bodily presence is weak and his speech of no account.'
47_10_11: Let such a person understand that what we say by letter when absent, we do when present.
47_10_12: Not that we dare to classify or compare ourselves with some of those who commend themselves. But when they measure themselves by one another and compare themselves with one another, they are without understanding.
47_10_13: But we will not boast beyond limits, but will boast only within the area of influence God assigned to us — a sphere that reaches even to you.
47_10_14: For we are not overextending ourselves, as though we did not reach you. We were the first to come all the way to you with the gospel of Christ.
47_10_15: We do not boast beyond limit in the labors of others. But our hope is that as your faith grows, our area of influence among you may be greatly enlarged,
47_10_16: so that we may preach the gospel in lands beyond you, without boasting of work already done in another's area.
47_10_17: 'Let the one who boasts, boast in the Lord.'
47_10_18: For it is not the one who commends himself who is approved, but the one whom the Lord commends.
47_11_1: I wish you would bear with me in a little foolishness. Do bear with me!
47_11_2: For I feel a divine jealousy for you, since I betrothed you to one husband, to present you as a pure virgin to Christ.
47_11_3: But I am afraid that as the serpent deceived Eve by his cunning, your thoughts will be led astray from a sincere and pure devotion to Christ.
47_11_4: For if someone comes and proclaims another Jesus than the one we proclaimed, or if you receive a different spirit from the one you received, or if you accept a different gospel from the one you accepted, you put up with it readily enough.
47_11_5: Indeed, I consider that I am not in the least inferior to these super-apostles.
47_11_6: Even if I am unskilled in speaking, I am not so in knowledge; in every way we have made this plain to you in all things.
47_11_7: Or did I commit a sin in humbling myself so that you might be exalted, because I preached God's gospel to you free of charge?
47_11_8: I robbed other churches by accepting support from them in order to serve you.
47_11_9: And when I was with you and was in need, I did not burden anyone, for the brothers who came from Macedonia supplied my need. So I kept myself from being a burden to you in any way, and will continue to do so.
47_11_10: As the truth of Christ is in me, this boasting of mine will not be silenced in the regions of Achaia.
47_11_11: And why? Because I do not love you? God knows I do!
47_11_12: And what I am doing I will continue to do, in order to cut off the opportunity of those who want an opportunity to be regarded just as we are in the matter they boast about.
47_11_13: For such men are false apostles, deceitful workers, disguising themselves as apostles of Christ.
47_11_14: And no wonder, for even Satan disguises himself as an angel of light.
47_11_15: So it is no surprise if his servants also disguise themselves as servants of righteousness. Their end will correspond to their deeds.
47_11_16: I repeat, let no one think me foolish. But even if you do, accept me as a fool, so that I too may boast a little.
47_11_17: What I am saying with this boastful confidence, I say not as the Lord would, but as a fool.
47_11_18: Since many boast according to the flesh, I too will boast.
47_11_19: For you gladly put up with fools, being so wise yourselves!
47_11_20: For you bear it if someone enslaves you, or devours you, or takes advantage of you, or puts on airs, or strikes you in the face.
47_11_21: To my shame, I must say, we were too weak for that! But whatever anyone else dares to boast of — I am speaking as a fool — I also dare to boast of that.
47_11_22: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they offspring of Abraham? So am I.
47_11_23: Are they servants of Christ? I am a better one — I am talking like a madman — with far greater labors, far more imprisonments, with countless beatings, and often near death.
47_11_24: Five times I received at the hands of the Jews forty lashes less one.
47_11_25: Three times I was beaten with rods. Once I was stoned. Three times I was shipwrecked; a night and a day I was adrift at sea;
47_11_26: on frequent journeys, in danger from rivers, danger from robbers, danger from my own people, danger from Gentiles, danger in the city, danger in the wilderness, danger at sea, danger from false brothers;
47_11_27: in toil and hardship, through many a sleepless night, in hunger and thirst, often without food, in cold and exposure.
47_11_28: And apart from other things, there is the daily pressure on me of my anxiety for all the churches.
47_11_29: Who is weak, and I am not weak? Who is made to stumble, and I am not indignant?
47_11_30: If I must boast, I will boast of the things that show my weakness.
47_11_31: The God and Father of the Lord Jesus — He who is blessed forever — knows that I am not lying.
47_11_32: At Damascus, the governor under King Aretas was guarding the city of Damascus in order to seize me,
47_11_33: but I was let down in a basket through a window in the wall and escaped his hands.
47_12_1: I must go on boasting. Though there is nothing to be gained by it, I will go on to visions and revelations of the Lord.
47_12_2: I know a man in Christ who fourteen years ago was caught up to the third heaven — whether in the body or out of the body I do not know, God knows.
47_12_3: And I know that this man was caught up into paradise — whether in the body or out of the body I do not know, God knows —
47_12_4: and he heard things that cannot be told, which no man is permitted to utter.
47_12_5: On behalf of this man I will boast, but on my own behalf I will not boast, except of my weaknesses.
47_12_6: Though if I should wish to boast, I would not be a fool, for I would be speaking the truth. But I refrain from it, so that no one may think more of me than he sees in me or hears from me.
47_12_7: So to keep me from becoming conceited because of the surpassing greatness of the revelations, a thorn was given me in the flesh, a messenger of Satan to harass me, to keep me from becoming conceited.
47_12_8: Three times I pleaded with the Lord about this, that it should leave me.
47_12_9: But He said to me, 'My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.' Therefore I will boast all the more gladly of my weaknesses, so that the power of Christ may rest upon me.
47_12_10: For the sake of Christ, then, I am content with weaknesses, insults, hardships, persecutions, and calamities. For when I am weak, then I am strong.
47_12_11: I have been a fool! You forced me to it, for I ought to have been commended by you. For I was not at all inferior to these super-apostles, even though I am nothing.
47_12_12: The signs of a true apostle were performed among you with utmost patience, with signs and wonders and mighty works.
47_12_13: For in what were you less favored than the rest of the churches, except that I myself did not burden you? Forgive me this wrong!
47_12_14: Here for the third time I am ready to come to you. And I will not be a burden, for I seek not what is yours but you. For children are not obligated to save up for their parents, but parents for their children.
47_12_15: I will most gladly spend and be spent for your souls. If I love you more, am I to be loved less?
47_12_16: But granting that I myself did not burden you, I was crafty, you say, and got the better of you by deceit.
47_12_17: Did I take advantage of you through any of those whom I sent to you?
47_12_18: I urged Titus to go, and sent the brother with him. Did Titus take advantage of you? Did we not act in the same spirit? Did we not take the same steps?
47_12_19: Have you been thinking all along that we have been defending ourselves to you? It is in the sight of God that we have been speaking in Christ, and all for your upbuilding, beloved.
47_12_20: For I fear that perhaps when I come I may find you not as I wish, and that you may find me not as you wish — that perhaps there may be quarreling, jealousy, anger, hostility, slander, gossip, conceit, and disorder.
47_12_21: I fear that when I come again my God may humble me before you, and I may have to mourn over many of those who sinned earlier and have not repented of the impurity, sexual immorality, and sensuality that they have practiced.
47_13_1: This is the third time I am coming to you. Every charge must be established by the evidence of two or three witnesses.
47_13_2: I warned those who sinned before and all the others, and I warn them now while absent — as I did when I was present on my second visit — that if I come again I will not spare them,
47_13_3: since you seek proof that Christ is speaking in me. He is not weak in dealing with you, but is powerful among you.
47_13_4: For He was crucified in weakness, but lives by the power of God. For we also are weak in Him, but in dealing with you we will live with Him by the power of God.
47_13_5: Examine yourselves, to see whether you are in the faith. Test yourselves. Or do you not realize this about yourselves, that Jesus Christ is in you? — unless indeed you fail to meet the test!
47_13_6: I hope you will find out that we have not failed the test.
47_13_7: But we pray to God that you may not do anything wrong — not that we may appear to have passed the test, but that you may do what is right, even if we seem to have failed.
47_13_8: For we cannot do anything against the truth, but only for the truth.
47_13_9: For we rejoice when we are weak and you are strong. Your restoration is what we pray for.
47_13_10: For this reason I write these things while I am away from you, so that when I come I may not have to be severe in my use of the authority that the Lord has given me for building up and not for tearing down.
47_13_11: Finally, brothers, rejoice. Aim for restoration, comfort one another, agree with one another, live in peace; and the God of love and peace will be with you.
47_13_12: Greet one another with a holy kiss.
47_13_14: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ and the love of God and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with you all.
52_1_1: Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy, To the church of the Thessalonians in God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
52_1_2: We give thanks to God always for all of you, constantly mentioning you in our prayers,
52_1_3: remembering before our God and Father your work of faith, labor of love, and steadfastness of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ.
52_1_4: For we know, beloved brothers and sisters, loved by God, that He has chosen you,
52_1_5: For our gospel did not come to you in word only, but also in power, and in the Holy Spirit and with full assurance, as you know what kind of men we were among you for your sake.
52_1_6: And you became imitators of us and of the Lord, having received the word in much affliction, with the joy of the Holy Spirit.
52_1_7: so that you became an example to all the believers in Macedonia and Achaia.
52_1_8: For from you the word of the Lord has sounded forth, not only in Macedonia and Achaia, but also in every place. Your faith in God has gone out, so that we do not need to say anything.
52_1_9: For they themselves report concerning us what kind of reception we had among you, and how you turned to God from idols to serve the living and true God,
52_1_10: and to wait for His Son from heaven, whom He raised from the dead, Jesus who delivers us from the wrath to come.
52_2_1: For you yourselves know, brothers and sisters, that our coming to you was not in vain.
52_2_2: But even after we had suffered before and were shamefully treated at Philippi, as you know, we were bold in our God to speak to you the gospel of God in much conflict.
52_2_3: For our exhortation did not come from error or impurity or by way of deceit.
52_2_4: But as we have been approved by God to be entrusted with the gospel, so we speak, not as pleasing men, but God who tests our hearts.
52_2_5: For we never used flattering words, as you know, nor a pretext for greed—God is witness.
52_2_6: Nor did we seek glory from men, either from you or from others, even though as apostles of Christ we might have asserted our authority.
52_2_7: But we were gentle among you, just as a nursing mother cherishes her own children.
52_2_8: So, affectionately longing for you, we were well pleased to impart to you not only the gospel of God, but also our own lives, because you had become dear to us.
52_2_9: For you remember, brothers and sisters, our labor and toil; for laboring night and day, that we might not be a burden to any of you, we preached to you the gospel of God.
52_2_10: You are witnesses, and God also, how devoutly and justly and blamelessly we behaved ourselves among you who believe.
52_2_11: As you know how we exhorted, comforted, and charged every one of you, like a father does his own children.
52_2_12: That you would walk worthy of God who calls you into His own kingdom and glory, encouraging you to live in a manner worthy of God.
52_2_13: For this reason we also thank God without ceasing, because when you received the word of God which you heard from us, you welcomed it not as the word of men, but as it truly is, the word of God, which effectively works in you who believe.
52_2_14: For you, brethren, became imitators of the churches of God in Christ Jesus that are in Judea, for you also suffered the same things from your own countrymen, just as they did from the Jews.
52_2_15: Who killed both the Lord Jesus and their own prophets, and have persecuted us; they do not please God and are contrary to all men.
52_2_16: Forbidding us to speak to the Gentiles that they may be saved, so as always to fill up the measure of their sins; but wrath has come upon them at last.
52_2_17: But we, brethren, having been taken away from you for a short time in presence, not in heart, endeavored more eagerly to see your face with great desire.
52_2_18: Therefore we wanted to come to you—even I, Paul, time and again—but Satan hindered us.
52_2_19: For what is our hope, or joy, or crown of rejoicing? Is it not even you in the presence of our Lord Jesus Christ at His coming?
52_2_20: For you are our glory and joy.
52_3_1: Therefore, when we could no longer endure it, we thought it good to be left in Athens alone.
52_3_2: And sent Timothy, our brother and minister of God, and our fellow laborer in the gospel of Christ, to establish you and encourage you concerning your faith.
52_3_3: That no one should be shaken by these afflictions; for you yourselves know that we are appointed to this.
52_3_4: For, in fact, we told you before when we were with you that we would suffer tribulation, just as it happened, and you know.
52_3_5: For this reason, when I could no longer endure it, I sent to know your faith, lest by some means the tempter had tempted you, and our labor might be in vain.
52_3_6: But now that Timothy has come to us from you, and brought us good news of your faith and love, and that you always have good remembrance of us, greatly desiring to see us, as we also to see you.
52_3_7: Therefore, brethren, in all our affliction and distress we were comforted concerning you by your faith.
52_3_8: For now we live, if you stand fast in the Lord.
52_3_9: For what thanks can we render to God for you, for all the joy with which we rejoice for your sake before our God.
52_3_10: Night and day praying exceedingly that we may see your face and perfect what is lacking in your faith.
52_3_11: Now may our God and Father Himself, and our Lord Jesus, direct our way to you.
52_3_12: And may the Lord make you increase and abound in love for one another and for all people, just as we do for you.
52_3_13: So that He may establish your hearts blameless in holiness before our God and Father at the coming of our Lord Jesus with all His saints.
52_4_1: Finally then, brethren, we urge and exhort in the Lord Jesus that you should abound more and more, just as you received from us how you ought to walk and to please God.
52_4_2: For you know what commandments we gave you through the Lord Jesus.
52_4_3: For this is the will of God, your sanctification: that you should abstain from sexual immorality.
52_4_4: That each of you should know how to possess his own vessel in sanctification and honor.
52_4_5: Not in passion of lust, like the Gentiles who do not know God.
52_4_6: That no one should take advantage of and defraud his brother in this matter, because the Lord is the avenger of all such, as we also forewarned you and testified.
52_4_7: For God did not call us to uncleanness, but in holiness.
52_4_8: Therefore he who rejects this does not reject man, but God, who has also given us His Holy Spirit.
52_4_9: But concerning brotherly love you have no need that I should write to you, for you yourselves are taught by God to love one another.
52_4_10: And indeed you do so toward all the brethren who are in all Macedonia. But we urge you, brethren, that you increase more and more.
52_4_11: That you also aspire to lead a quiet life, to mind your own business, and to work with your own hands, as we commanded you.
52_4_12: That you may walk properly toward those who are outside, and that you may lack nothing.
52_4_13: But I do not want you to be ignorant, brethren, concerning those who have fallen asleep, lest you sorrow as others who have no hope.
52_4_14: For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again, even so God will bring with Him those who sleep in Jesus.
52_4_15: For this we say to you by the word of the Lord, that we who are alive and remain until the coming of the Lord will by no means precede those who are asleep.
52_4_16: For the Lord Himself will descend from heaven with a shout, with the voice of an archangel, and with the trumpet of God. And the dead in Christ will rise first.
52_4_17: Then we who are alive and remain shall be caught up together with them in the clouds to meet the Lord in the air. And thus we shall always be with the Lord.
52_4_18: Therefore comfort and encourage one another with these words.
52_5_1: But concerning the times and the seasons, brothers and sisters, you have no need that I should write to you.
52_5_2: For you yourselves know perfectly well that the day of the Lord will come like a thief in the night.
52_5_3: For when they say, “Peace and safety!” then sudden destruction will come upon them, as labor pains upon a pregnant woman. And they shall not escape.
52_5_4: But you, brethren, are not in darkness, so that this day should surprise you like a thief.
52_5_5: You are all children of light and children of the day. We are not of the night nor of darkness.
52_5_6: Therefore let us not sleep, as others do, but let us stay awake and be sober.
52_5_7: For those who sleep, sleep at night, and those who get drunk are drunk at night.
52_5_8: But let us who are of the day be sober, putting on the breastplate of faith and love, and as a helmet the hope of salvation.
52_5_9: For God did not appoint us to wrath, but to obtain salvation through our Lord Jesus Christ,
52_5_10: who died for us, that whether we wake or sleep, we should live together with Him.
52_5_11: Therefore encourage each other and build one another up, just as you are already doing.
52_5_12: And we urge you, brethren, to recognize those who labor among you and are over you in the Lord and admonish you,
52_5_13: and to esteem them very highly in love for their work’s sake. Be at peace among yourselves.
52_5_14: Now we exhort you, brethren, warn those who are unruly, comfort the fainthearted, uphold the weak, be patient with everyone.
52_5_15: See that no one repays evil for evil to anyone, but always pursue what is good both for yourselves and for all.
52_5_18: in everything give thanks; for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus for you.
52_5_21: Test everything; hold fast to what is good.
52_5_22: Abstain from every kind of evil.
52_5_23: Now may the God of peace Himself sanctify you completely; and may your whole spirit, soul, and body be kept blameless at the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
52_5_24: He who calls you is faithful; He will surely do it.
52_5_25: Brothers and sisters, pray for us.
52_5_26: Greet all the brothers and sisters with a holy kiss.
52_5_27: I charge you by the Lord to have this letter read to all the brothers and sisters.
52_5_28: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.
53_1_1: Paul, Silvanus, and Timothy, To the church of the Thessalonians in God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ:
53_1_2: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
53_1_3: We ought always to give thanks to God for you, brothers and sisters, as it is fitting, because your faith is growing abundantly, and the love of every one of you for one another is increasing.
53_1_4: Therefore, we ourselves boast about you among the churches of God for your perseverance and faith in all your persecutions and afflictions that you are enduring.
53_1_5: This is manifest evidence of the righteous judgment of God, that you may be considered worthy of the kingdom of God, for which you are also suffering;
53_1_6: Since it is a righteous thing with God to repay with affliction those who trouble you,
53_1_7: And to give you who are troubled rest with us when the Lord Jesus is revealed from heaven with His mighty angels,
53_1_8: In flaming fire taking vengeance on those who do not know God, and on those who do not obey the gospel of our Lord Jesus Christ.
53_1_9: These shall be punished with everlasting destruction, away from the presence of the Lord and from the glory of His power,
53_1_10: When He comes, in that Day, to be glorified in His saints and to be admired among all those who believe, because our testimony among you was believed.
53_1_11: Therefore we also pray always for you that our God would count you worthy of this calling, and fulfill all the good pleasure of His goodness and the work of faith with power,
53_1_12: That the name of our Lord Jesus Christ may be glorified in you, and you in Him, according to the grace of our God and the Lord Jesus Christ.
53_2_1: Now, brethren, concerning the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ and our gathering together to Him, we ask you,
53_2_2: Not to be soon shaken in mind or troubled, either by spirit or by word or by letter, as if from us, as though the day of the Lord had come.
53_2_3: Let no one deceive you by any means; for that Day will not come unless the rebellion comes first, and the man of lawlessness is revealed, the son of destruction,
53_2_4: Who opposes and exalts himself above all that is called God or that is worshiped, so that he sits as God in the temple of God, proclaiming himself to be God.
53_2_5: Do you not remember that when I was still with you I told you these things?
53_2_6: And now you know what is restraining, that he may be revealed in his own time.
53_2_7: For the mystery of lawlessness is already at work; only He who now restrains will do so until He is taken out of the way.
53_2_8: And then the lawless one will be revealed, whom the Lord Jesus will consume with the breath of His mouth and destroy with the brightness of His coming.
53_2_9: The coming of the lawless one is according to the working of Satan, with all power, false signs, and lying wonders,
53_2_10: and with all unrighteous deception among those who are perishing, because they did not receive the love of the truth, so that they might be saved.
53_2_11: And for this reason God will send them strong delusion, so that they should believe what is false,
53_2_12: that they all may be condemned who did not believe the truth but had pleasure in unrighteousness.
53_2_13: But we are bound to give thanks to God always for you, brethren beloved by the Lord, because God from the beginning chose you for salvation through sanctification by the Spirit and belief in the truth,
53_2_14: to which He called you by our gospel, so that you may obtain the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
53_2_15: Therefore, brethren, stand fast and hold the traditions which you were taught, whether by word or our letter.
53_2_16: Now may our Lord Jesus Christ Himself, and our God and Father, who has loved us and given us eternal comfort and good hope by grace,
53_2_17: comfort your hearts and establish you in every good word and work.
53_3_1: Finally, brethren, pray for us, that the word of the Lord may run swiftly and be glorified, just as it is with you,
53_3_2: and that we may be delivered from wicked and evil men; for not all have faith.
53_3_3: But the Lord is faithful, who will establish you and guard you from the evil one.
53_3_4: And we have confidence in the Lord concerning you, that you are doing and will continue to do the things we command.
53_3_5: Now may the Lord direct your hearts into the love of God and into the patience of Christ.
53_3_6: But we command you, brethren, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that you withdraw from every brother who walks disorderly and not according to the tradition which he received from us.
53_3_7: For you yourselves know how you ought to follow us, for we were not idle among you;
53_3_8: nor did we eat anyone’s bread free of charge, but worked with labor and toil night and day, that we might not be a burden to any of you,
53_3_9: not because we do not have the right, but to make ourselves an example of how you should follow us.
53_3_10: For even when we were with you, we commanded you this: If anyone is not willing to work, neither shall he eat.
53_3_11: For we hear that there are some who walk among you in a disorderly manner, not working at all, but are busybodies.
53_3_12: Now those who are such we command and exhort through our Lord Jesus Christ to work quietly and earn their own living.
53_3_13: But as for you, brothers and sisters, do not grow weary in doing good.
53_3_14: If anyone does not obey our word in this letter, take note of that person and do not associate with him, so that he may be ashamed.
53_3_15: Yet do not count him as an enemy, but admonish him as a brother.
53_3_16: Now may the Lord of peace Himself give you peace always in every way. The Lord be with you all.
53_3_17: I, Paul, write this greeting with my own hand, which is a sign of genuineness in every letter; this is how I write.
53_3_18: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.
54_1_1: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the commandment of God our Savior and Christ Jesus our hope,
54_1_2: To Timothy, my true son in the faith: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
54_1_3: As I urged you when I went into Macedonia, remain in Ephesus so that you may instruct certain people not to teach any different doctrine,
54_1_4: nor pay attention to myths and endless genealogies, which promote speculations rather than godly edification which is in faith.
54_1_5: Now the purpose of the commandment is love from a pure heart, a good conscience, and sincere faith.
54_1_6: from which some, having strayed, have turned aside to fruitless discussion,
54_1_7: desiring to be teachers of the law, understanding neither what they say nor the things about which they make confident assertions.
54_1_8: But we know that the law is good if one uses it lawfully,
54_1_9: knowing this: that the law is not made for a righteous person, but for the lawless and rebellious, for the ungodly and sinners, for the unholy and profane, for those who kill their fathers and mothers, for murderers,
54_1_10: for the sexually immoral, for homosexuals, for kidnappers, for liars, for perjurers, and whatever else is contrary to sound doctrine,
54_1_11: according to the glorious gospel of the blessed God which was entrusted to me.
54_1_12: And I thank Christ Jesus our Lord who has strengthened me, because He considered me faithful, putting me into the ministry,
54_1_13: although I was formerly a blasphemer, a persecutor, and an insolent man; but I received mercy because I acted ignorantly in unbelief.
54_1_14: And the grace of our Lord was exceedingly abundant, with the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
54_1_15: This is a trustworthy saying and worthy of all acceptance, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners, of whom I am the foremost.
54_1_16: However, for this reason I obtained mercy, that in me, as the foremost, Jesus Christ might display His perfect patience as a pattern to those who are going to believe in Him for eternal life.
54_1_17: Now to the King eternal, immortal, invisible, the only God, be honor and glory forever and ever. Amen.
54_1_18: This charge I commit to you, son Timothy, according to the prophecies previously made concerning you, that by them you may wage the good warfare,
54_1_19: having faith and a good conscience, which some having rejected, concerning the faith have suffered shipwreck,
54_1_20: of whom are Hymenaeus and Alexander, whom I have delivered to Satan that they may learn not to blaspheme.
54_2_1: Therefore I exhort first of all that supplications, prayers, intercessions, and giving of thanks be made for all people,
54_2_2: for kings and all who are in authority, that we may lead a peaceful and quiet life in all godliness and dignity.
54_2_3: For this is good and pleasing in the sight of God our Savior,
54_2_4: who desires all people to be saved and to come to the knowledge of the truth.
54_2_5: For there is one God and one Mediator between God and humanity, the Man Christ Jesus,
54_2_6: who gave Himself as a ransom for all, the testimony given at the proper time.
54_2_7: For this I was appointed a preacher and an apostle—I am telling the truth, I am not lying—a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and truth.
54_2_8: I desire therefore that the men pray everywhere, lifting up holy hands, without anger or quarreling;
54_2_9: in like manner also, that the women adorn themselves in respectable apparel, with modesty and self-control, not with braided hair or gold or pearls or costly clothing,
54_2_10: but with good works, as is proper for women professing godliness.
54_2_11: Let a woman learn quietly with all submission.
54_2_12: And I do not permit a woman to teach or to have authority over a man, but to remain quiet.
54_2_13: For Adam was formed first, then Eve.
54_2_14: And Adam was not deceived, but the woman being deceived, fell into transgression.
54_2_15: Nevertheless she will be saved in childbearing if they continue in faith, love, and holiness, with self-control.
54_3_1: This is a faithful saying: If anyone aspires to the office of overseer, he desires a noble task.
54_3_2: An overseer then must be above reproach, the husband of one wife, self-controlled, sensible, respectable, hospitable, able to teach;
54_3_3: not given to wine, not violent, but gentle, not quarrelsome, not a lover of money.
54_3_4: one who manages his own household well, keeping his children under control with all dignity
54_3_5: (for if a man does not know how to manage his own household, how will he take care of the church of God?);
54_3_6: not a recent convert, lest being puffed up with pride he fall into the same condemnation as the devil.
54_3_7: Moreover, he must have a good reputation among those who are outside, so that he may not fall into disgrace and the snare of the devil.
54_3_8: Likewise deacons must be reverent, not double-tongued, not addicted to much wine, not greedy for dishonest gain.
54_3_9: holding the mystery of the faith with a clear conscience.
54_3_10: But let these also first be tested; then let them serve as deacons if they prove themselves blameless.
54_3_11: Likewise, their wives must be dignified, not slanderers, temperate, faithful in all things.
54_3_12: Let deacons be the husbands of one wife, managing their children and their own households well.
54_3_13: For those who have served well as deacons obtain for themselves a good standing and great confidence in the faith which is in Christ Jesus.
54_3_14: These things I write to you, though I hope to come to you shortly;
54_3_15: but if I am delayed, I write so that you may know how you ought to conduct yourself in the house of God, which is the church of the living God, the pillar and foundation of the truth.
54_3_16: And without controversy great is the mystery of godliness: He was manifested in the flesh, justified in the Spirit, seen by angels, preached among the nations, believed on in the world, taken up in glory.
54_4_1: Now the Spirit expressly says that in later times some will depart from the faith, paying attention to deceitful spirits and doctrines of demons,
54_4_2: speaking lies in hypocrisy, having their own conscience seared with a hot iron,
54_4_3: forbidding to marry, and commanding to abstain from foods which God created to be received with thanksgiving by those who believe and know the truth.
54_4_4: For everything created by God is good, and nothing is to be rejected if it is received with thanksgiving;
54_4_5: for it is sanctified by the word of God and prayer.
54_4_6: If you instruct the brethren in these things, you will be a good minister of Jesus Christ, nourished in the words of faith and of the good doctrine which you have carefully followed.
54_4_7: But reject profane and old wives’ fables, and train yourself toward godliness.
54_4_8: For bodily exercise profits a little, but godliness is profitable for all things, having promise of the life that now is and of that which is to come.
54_4_9: This is a faithful saying and worthy of all acceptance.
54_4_10: For to this end we labor and strive, because we have set our hope on the living God, who is the Savior of all people, especially of those who believe.
54_4_12: Let no one despise your youth, but be an example to the believers in speech, in conduct, in love, in faith, and in purity.
54_4_13: Until I come, give attention to the public reading of Scripture, to exhortation, and to teaching.
54_4_14: Do not neglect the spiritual gift that is in you, which was given to you by prophecy with the laying on of the hands of the elders.
54_4_15: Meditate on these things; give yourself entirely to them, so that your progress may be evident to all.
54_4_16: Pay close attention to yourself and to your teaching. Continue in them, for in doing this you will save both yourself and those who hear you.
54_5_1: Do not rebuke an older man, but exhort him as a father, younger men as brothers,
54_5_2: older women as mothers, younger women as sisters, with all purity.
54_5_3: Honor widows who are truly in need.
54_5_4: But if any widow has children or grandchildren, let them first learn to show godliness at home and repay their parents, for this is pleasing before God.
54_5_5: Now she who is truly a widow, left alone, has set her hope on God and continues in supplications and prayers night and day.
54_5_6: But she who lives in pleasure is dead even while she lives.
54_5_7: Command these things as well, so that they may be above reproach.
54_5_8: But if anyone does not provide for his own, especially for his household, he has denied the faith and is worse than an unbeliever.
54_5_9: Do not let a widow under sixty years old be taken into the number, unless she has been the wife of one husband,
54_5_10: Well reported for good works: if she has brought up children, shown hospitality, washed the saints’ feet, helped the afflicted, and devoted herself to every good work.
54_5_11: But refuse to enroll younger widows, for when they are drawn away from Christ by desire, they want to marry.
54_5_12: Having condemnation because they have cast off their first faith.
54_5_13: And besides, they learn to be idle, wandering from house to house, and not only idle but also gossips and busybodies, saying things they ought not.
54_5_14: Therefore I desire that the younger widows marry, bear children, manage their households, and give no opportunity to the adversary to speak reproachfully.
54_5_15: For some have already turned aside to follow Satan.
54_5_16: If any believing man or woman has widows, let them relieve them, and do not let the church be burdened, so that it may care for those who are truly widows.
54_5_17: Let the elders who rule well be considered worthy of double honor, especially those who labor in preaching and teaching.
54_5_18: For the Scripture says, “You shall not muzzle an ox while it treads out the grain,” and, “The laborer is worthy of his wages.”
54_5_19: Do not receive an accusation against an elder except from two or three witnesses.
54_5_20: Those who continue in sin, rebuke in the presence of all, so that the rest may stand in fear.
54_5_21: I charge you before God and the Lord Jesus Christ and the elect angels to observe these things without prejudice, doing nothing with partiality.
54_5_22: Do not lay hands on anyone hastily, nor share in other people’s sins; keep yourself pure.
54_5_23: No longer drink only water, but use a little wine for the sake of your stomach and your frequent ailments.
54_5_24: Some men’s sins are clearly evident, preceding them to judgment, but those of some men follow later.
54_5_25: Likewise, the good works of some are clearly evident, and those that are otherwise cannot be hidden.
54_6_1: Let as many bondservants as are under the yoke count their own masters worthy of all honor, so that the name of God and His doctrine may not be blasphemed.
54_6_2: And those who have believing masters, let them not despise them because they are brethren, but rather serve them because those who are benefited are believers and beloved. Teach and exhort these things.
54_6_3: If anyone teaches otherwise and does not consent to wholesome words, even the words of our Lord Jesus Christ, and to the doctrine which accords with godliness,
54_6_4: he is proud, knowing nothing, but is obsessed with disputes and arguments over words, from which come envy, strife, slander, evil suspicions,
54_6_5: and constant friction among people of corrupt minds and destitute of the truth, who suppose that godliness is a means of gain.
54_6_6: But godliness with contentment is great gain.
54_6_7: For we brought nothing into this world, and it is certain we can carry nothing out.
54_6_8: And having food and clothing, with these we shall be content.
54_6_9: But those who desire to be rich fall into temptation, a snare, and many foolish and harmful desires which plunge people into ruin and destruction.
54_6_10: For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evil, for which some have strayed from the faith in their greediness and pierced themselves with many sorrows.
54_6_11: But you, O man of God, flee these things and pursue righteousness, godliness, faith, love, perseverance, and gentleness.
54_6_12: Fight the good fight of faith, lay hold on eternal life, to which you were called and have confessed the good confession in the presence of many witnesses.
54_6_13: I urge you in the sight of God who gives life to all things, and before Christ Jesus who testified the good confession before Pontius Pilate,
54_6_14: that you keep this commandment without spot, blameless until the appearing of our Lord Jesus Christ,
54_6_15: which He will manifest in His own time, He who is the blessed and only Sovereign, the King of kings and Lord of lords,
54_6_16: who alone has immortality, dwelling in unapproachable light, whom no one has seen or can see. To Him be honor and eternal dominion. Amen.
54_6_17: Command those who are rich in this present age not to be haughty, nor to trust in uncertain riches but in the living God, who richly provides us with everything to enjoy.
54_6_18: Let them do good, that they be rich in good works, generous and ready to share,
54_6_19: storing up for themselves a good foundation for the future, so that they may take hold of what is truly life.
54_6_20: O Timothy! Guard what was committed to your trust, avoiding profane and idle babblings and contradictions of what is falsely called knowledge—
54_6_21: by professing it some have strayed concerning the faith. Grace be with you. Amen.
55_1_1: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, according to the promise of life in Christ Jesus,
55_1_2: To Timothy, my beloved son: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
55_1_3: I thank God, whom I serve with a clear conscience, as my ancestors did, as I constantly remember you in my prayers night and day,
55_1_4: Greatly desiring to see you, remembering your tears, that I may be filled with joy.
55_1_5: When I call to remembrance the genuine faith that is in you, which dwelt first in your grandmother Lois and your mother Eunice, and I am persuaded is in you also.
55_1_6: Therefore I remind you to stir up the gift of God which is in you through the laying on of my hands.
55_1_7: For God has not given us a spirit of fear, but of power and of love and of a sound mind.
55_1_8: Therefore do not be ashamed of the testimony of our Lord, nor of me His prisoner, but share with me in the sufferings for the gospel according to the power of God,
55_1_9: Who has saved us and called us with a holy calling, not according to our works, but according to His own purpose and grace which was given to us in Christ Jesus before time began,
55_1_10: But has now been revealed by the appearing of our Savior Jesus Christ, who has abolished death and brought life and immortality to light through the gospel,
55_1_11: To which I was appointed a preacher, an apostle, and a teacher.
55_1_12: For this reason I also suffer these things; nevertheless I am not ashamed, for I know whom I have believed and am persuaded that He is able to keep what I have committed to Him until that Day.
55_1_13: Hold fast the pattern of sound words which you have heard from me, in faith and love which are in Christ Jesus.
55_1_14: That good thing which was committed to you, keep by the Holy Spirit who dwells in us.
55_1_15: This you know, that all those in Asia have turned away from me, among whom are Phygellus and Hermogenes.
55_1_16: The Lord grant mercy to the household of Onesiphorus, for he often refreshed me and was not ashamed of my chain;
55_1_17: But when he arrived in Rome, he sought me out very zealously and found me.
55_1_18: The Lord grant to him that he may find mercy from the Lord in that Day—and you know very well how many ways he ministered to me at Ephesus.
55_2_1: You therefore, my son, be strong in the grace that is in Christ Jesus.
55_2_2: And the things that you have heard from me among many witnesses, commit these to faithful men who will be able to teach others also.
55_2_3: You therefore must endure hardship as a good soldier of Christ Jesus, sharing in suffering.
55_2_4: No one engaged in warfare entangles himself with the affairs of civilian life, aiming to please the one who enlisted him.
55_2_5: And also if anyone competes as an athlete, he is not crowned unless he competes according to the rules.
55_2_6: The hardworking farmer must be the first to partake of the crops, having the first share.
55_2_7: Consider what I say, and may the Lord give you understanding in everything.
55_2_8: Remember that Jesus Christ, risen from the dead and of the seed of David, was preached in my gospel.
55_2_9: For which I suffer trouble as an evildoer, even to the point of chains; but the word of God is not chained.
55_2_10: Therefore I endure all things for the sake of the elect, that they also may obtain the salvation which is in Christ Jesus with eternal glory.
55_2_11: This is a faithful saying: For if we died with Him, we shall also live with Him.
55_2_12: If we endure, we will also reign with Him; if we deny Him, He also will deny us.
55_2_13: If we are faithless, He remains faithful; He cannot deny Himself.
55_2_14: Remind them of these things, charging them before the Lord not to strive about words to no profit, which leads to the ruin of the hearers.
55_2_15: Be diligent to present yourself approved to God, a worker who does not need to be ashamed, rightly dividing the word of truth.
55_2_16: But shun profane and idle babblings, for they will lead to more ungodliness.
55_2_17: And their message will spread like gangrene. Hymenaeus and Philetus are of this sort.
55_2_18: Who have strayed concerning the truth, saying that the resurrection is already past; and they overthrow the faith of some.
55_2_19: Nevertheless the solid foundation of God stands, having this seal: “The Lord knows those who are His,” and, “Let everyone who names the name of the Lord depart from iniquity.”
55_2_20: But in a great house there are not only vessels of gold and silver, but also of wood and clay, some for honor and some for dishonor.
55_2_21: Therefore if anyone cleanses himself from the latter, he will be a vessel for honor, sanctified and useful for the Master, prepared for every good work.
55_2_22: Flee also youthful lusts; but pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace with those who call on the Lord out of a pure heart.
55_2_23: But avoid foolish and ignorant disputes, knowing that they generate strife and breed quarrels.
55_2_24: And a servant of the Lord must not quarrel but be gentle and kind to all, able to teach, patient with difficult people.
55_2_25: In humility correcting those who are in opposition, if God perhaps will grant them repentance leading to a knowledge of the truth.
55_2_26: And that they may come to their senses and escape the snare of the devil, having been taken captive by him to do his will.
55_3_1: But know this, that in the last days perilous and difficult times will come.
55_3_2: For men will be lovers of themselves, lovers of money, boasters, proud, disobedient to parents, unthankful, unholy.
55_3_3: Unloving, unforgiving, slanderers, without self-control, brutal, despisers of good.
55_3_4: Traitors, headstrong, haughty, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God.
55_3_5: Having a form of godliness but denying its power. Avoid such people.
55_3_6: For of this sort are those who creep into households and make captives of gullible women loaded down with sins, led away by various lusts.
55_3_7: Always learning and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.
55_3_8: Now as Jannes and Jambres resisted Moses, so do these also resist the truth: men of corrupt minds, disapproved concerning the faith.
55_3_9: But they will progress no further, for their folly will be manifest to all, as theirs also was.
55_3_10: But you have carefully followed my doctrine, manner of life, purpose, faith, patience, love, perseverance.
55_3_11: Persecutions, afflictions, which happened to me at Antioch, at Iconium, at Lystra—what persecutions I endured. And out of them all the Lord delivered me.
55_3_12: Yes, and all who desire to live godly in Christ Jesus will suffer persecution.
55_3_13: But evil men and impostors will grow worse and worse, deceiving and being deceived.
55_3_14: But you must continue in the things which you have learned and been assured of, knowing from whom you have learned them.
55_3_15: And that from childhood you have known the Holy Scriptures, which are able to make you wise for salvation through faith in Christ Jesus.
55_3_16: All Scripture is inspired by God and is profitable for teaching, for reproof, for correction, for instruction in righteousness.
55_3_17: that the man of God may be complete, thoroughly equipped for every good work.
55_4_1: I solemnly charge you before God and the Lord Jesus Christ, who will judge the living and the dead at His appearing and His kingdom:
55_4_2: Preach the word! Be ready in season and out of season. Convince, rebuke, exhort, with all patience and teaching.
55_4_3: For the time will come when they will not endure sound doctrine, but according to their own desires, because they have itching ears, they will heap up for themselves teachers.
55_4_4: and they will turn their ears away from the truth, and be turned aside to myths.
55_4_5: But you be watchful in all things, endure afflictions, do the work of an evangelist, fulfill your ministry.
55_4_6: For I am already being poured out as a drink offering, and the time of my departure is at hand.
55_4_7: I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith.
55_4_8: Finally, there is laid up for me the crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous Judge, will give to me on that Day, and not to me only but also to all who have loved His appearing.
55_4_9: Be diligent to come to me quickly;
55_4_10: for Demas has forsaken me, having loved this present world, and has departed for Thessalonica—Crescens for Galatia, Titus for Dalmatia.
55_4_11: Only Luke is with me. Get Mark and bring him with you, for he is useful to me for ministry.
55_4_12: And Tychicus I have sent to Ephesus.
55_4_13: Bring the cloak that I left with Carpus at Troas when you come—and the books, especially the parchments.
55_4_14: Alexander the coppersmith did me much harm. May the Lord repay him according to his works.
55_4_15: You also must beware of him, for he has greatly resisted our words.
55_4_16: At my first defense no one stood with me, but all forsook me. May it not be charged against them.
55_4_17: But the Lord stood with me and strengthened me, so that the message might be preached fully through me, and that all the Gentiles might hear. Also I was delivered out of the mouth of the lion.
55_4_18: And the Lord will deliver me from every evil work and preserve me for His heavenly kingdom. To Him be glory forever and ever. Amen!
55_4_19: Greet Prisca and Aquila, and the household of Onesiphorus.
55_4_20: Erastus stayed in Corinth, but I left Trophimus, who was sick, at Miletus.
55_4_21: Do your best to come before winter. Eubulus greets you, as do Pudens, Linus, Claudia, and all the brothers and sisters.
55_4_22: The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with you all. Amen.
59_1_1: James, a bondservant of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ, to the twelve tribes dispersed abroad: Greetings.
59_1_2: My brethren, consider it all joy when you encounter various trials,
59_1_3: knowing that the testing of your faith produces endurance.
59_1_4: But let patience have its perfect work, that you may be mature and complete, lacking nothing.
59_1_5: If any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask of God, who gives generously to all without reproach, and it will be given to him.
59_1_6: But let him ask in faith, with no doubting, for he who doubts is like a wave of the sea driven and tossed by the wind.
59_1_7: For that man should not expect to receive anything from the Lord;
59_1_8: he is a double-minded man, unstable in all his ways.
59_1_9: Let the lowly brother glory in his exaltation,
59_1_10: but the rich in his humiliation, because as a flower of the field he will pass away.
59_1_11: For the sun rises with a scorching heat and withers the grass; its flower falls, and its beautiful appearance perishes. So the rich man also will fade away in his pursuits.
59_1_12: Blessed is the man who endures temptation; for when he has stood the test, he will receive the crown of life which the Lord has promised to those who love Him.
59_1_13: Let no one say when he is tempted, “I am tempted by God”; for God cannot be tempted by evil, nor does He Himself tempt anyone.
59_1_14: But each one is tempted when he is drawn away by his own desires and enticed.
59_1_15: Then, when desire has conceived, it gives birth to sin; and sin, when it is fully grown, brings forth death.
59_1_16: Do not be deceived, my beloved brothers and sisters.
59_1_17: Every good gift and every perfect gift is from above, coming down from the Father of lights, with whom there is no variation or shifting shadow.
59_1_18: Of His own will He brought us forth by the word of truth, that we might be a kind of firstfruits of His creatures.
59_1_19: So then, my beloved brethren, let every person be quick to hear, slow to speak, slow to anger;
59_1_20: for the anger of man does not produce the righteousness of God.
59_1_21: Therefore lay aside all filthiness and overflow of wickedness, and humbly receive the implanted word, which is able to save your souls.
59_1_22: But be doers of the word, and not hearers only, deceiving yourselves.
59_1_23: For if anyone is a hearer of the word and not a doer, he is like a man observing his natural face in a mirror.
59_1_24: For he observes himself, goes away, and immediately forgets what kind of person he was.
59_1_25: But he who looks into the perfect law of liberty and continues in it, not being a forgetful hearer but a doer of the work, this one will be blessed in what he does.
59_1_26: If anyone among you thinks he is religious and does not bridle his tongue but deceives his own heart, this one's religion is worthless.
59_1_27: Pure and undefiled religion before God the Father is this: to visit orphans and widows in their trouble, and to keep oneself unstained from the world.
59_2_1: My brethren, do not hold the faith of our glorious Lord Jesus Christ with partiality.
59_2_2: For if there should come into your assembly a man with gold rings, in fine apparel, and there should also come in a poor man in filthy clothes,
59_2_3: and you pay attention to the one wearing the fine clothes and say to him, “You sit here in a good place,” and say to the poor man, “You stand there,” or, “Sit here at my footstool,”
59_2_4: have you not shown partiality among yourselves and become judges with evil thoughts?
59_2_5: Listen, my beloved brethren: Has God not chosen the poor of this world to be rich in faith and heirs of the kingdom which He promised to those who love Him?
59_2_6: But you have dishonored the poor man. Do not the rich oppress you and drag you into the courts?
59_2_7: Do they not blaspheme that noble name by which you are called?
59_2_8: If you really fulfill the royal law according to the Scripture, “You shall love your neighbor as yourself,” you do well.
59_2_9: But if you show partiality, you commit sin and are convicted by the law as transgressors.
59_2_10: For whoever shall keep the whole law, and yet stumble in one point, he is guilty of all.
59_2_11: For He who said, “Do not commit adultery,” also said, “Do not murder.” Now if you do not commit adultery, but you do murder, you have become a transgressor of the law.
59_2_12: So speak and so do as those who will be judged by the law of liberty.
59_2_13: For judgment is without mercy to the one who has shown no mercy. Mercy triumphs over judgment.
59_2_14: What good is it, my brothers and sisters, if someone says he has faith but does not have works? Can such faith save him?
59_2_15: If a brother or sister is poorly clothed and lacking in daily food,
59_2_16: and one of you says to them, “Depart in peace, be warmed and filled,” but you do not give them the things needed for the body, what good is that?
59_2_17: Thus also faith by itself, if it does not have works, is dead.
59_2_18: But someone will say, “You have faith, and I have works.” Show me your faith without works, and I will show you my faith by my works.
59_2_19: You believe that God is one. You do well. Even the demons believe—and tremble!
59_2_20: But do you want to know, O foolish person, that faith without works is useless?
59_2_21: Was not Abraham our father justified by works when he offered Isaac his son on the altar?
59_2_22: You see that faith was working together with his works, and by works faith was made perfect.
59_2_23: And the Scripture was fulfilled which says, “Abraham believed God, and it was accounted to him for righteousness.” And he was called the friend of God.
59_2_24: You see then that a person is justified by works, and not by faith alone.
59_2_25: Likewise, was not Rahab the prostitute also justified by works when she received the messengers and sent them out by another way?
59_2_26: For as the body without the spirit is dead, so faith without works is dead also.
59_3_1: My brethren, let not many of you become teachers, knowing that we shall receive a stricter judgment.
59_3_2: For we all stumble in many ways. If anyone does not stumble in word, he is a perfect man, able also to bridle the whole body.
59_3_3: Indeed, we put bits in horses’ mouths that they may obey us, and we turn their whole body.
59_3_4: Look also at ships: although they are so large and are driven by fierce winds, they are turned by a very small rudder wherever the pilot desires.
59_3_5: Even so the tongue is a little member and boasts great things. See how great a forest a little fire kindles!
59_3_6: And the tongue is a fire, a world of iniquity. The tongue is so set among our members that it defiles the whole body, and sets on fire the course of nature; and it is set on fire by hell.
59_3_7: For every kind of beast and bird, of reptile and creature of the sea, is tamed and has been tamed by mankind.
59_3_8: But no one can tame the tongue. It is a restless evil, full of deadly poison.
59_3_9: With it we bless our Lord and Father, and with it we curse people who are made in the likeness of God.
59_3_10: Out of the same mouth proceed blessing and cursing. My brothers and sisters, these things ought not to be so.
59_3_11: Does a spring send forth fresh and bitter water from the same opening?
59_3_12: Can a fig tree, my brothers, bear olives, or a grapevine produce figs? Thus no spring yields both salt water and fresh.
59_3_13: Who is wise and understanding among you? Let him show by good conduct that his works are done in the gentleness of wisdom.
59_3_14: But if you have bitter jealousy and selfish ambition in your hearts, do not boast and lie against the truth.
59_3_15: This wisdom does not descend from above, but is earthly, unspiritual, demonic.
59_3_16: For where jealousy and selfish ambition exist, there is disorder and every evil practice.
59_3_17: But the wisdom that is from above is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, open to reason, full of mercy and good fruits, without partiality and without hypocrisy.
59_3_18: Now the fruit of righteousness is sown in peace by those who make peace.
59_4_1: Where do wars and fights come from among you? Do they not come from your desires for pleasure that war within you?
59_4_2: You desire and do not have. You murder and covet and cannot obtain. You fight and quarrel. Yet you do not have because you do not ask.
59_4_3: You ask and do not receive, because you ask with wrong motives, that you may spend it on your pleasures.
59_4_4: You adulterous people! Do you not know that friendship with the world is hostility toward God? Whoever therefore wants to be a friend of the world makes himself an enemy of God.
59_4_5: Or do you think that the Scripture says in vain, “The Spirit who dwells in us yearns jealously”?
59_4_6: But He gives more grace. Therefore He says: “God resists the proud, but gives grace to the humble.”
59_4_7: Therefore submit to God. Resist the devil and he will flee from you.
59_4_8: Draw near to God and He will draw near to you. Cleanse your hands, you sinners; and purify your hearts, you double-minded.
59_4_9: Be miserable and mourn and weep. Let your laughter be turned to mourning and your joy to gloom.
59_4_10: Humble yourselves in the sight of the Lord, and He will lift you up in honor.
59_4_11: Do not speak evil against one another, brethren. He who speaks against a brother and judges his brother, speaks against the law and judges the law. But if you judge the law, you are not a doer of the law but a judge.
59_4_12: There is one Lawgiver and Judge, who is able to save and to destroy. Who are you to judge your neighbor?
59_4_13: Come now, you who say, “Today or tomorrow we will go to such and such a city, spend a year there, buy and sell, and make a profit.”
59_4_14: Whereas you do not know what will happen tomorrow. For what is your life? It is even a vapor that appears for a little time and then vanishes away.
59_4_15: Instead you ought to say, “If the Lord wills, we will live and do this or that.”
59_4_16: But as it is, you boast in your arrogance. All such boasting is evil.
59_4_17: Therefore, to him who knows the right thing to do and does not do it, to him it is sin.
59_5_1: Come now, you rich, weep and howl for your miseries that are coming upon you!
59_5_2: Your riches have rotted, and your garments are moth-eaten.
59_5_3: Your gold and silver are corroded, and their corrosion will be a witness against you and will eat your flesh like fire. You have stored up treasure in the last days.
59_5_4: Indeed the wages of the laborers who mowed your fields, which you kept back by fraud, cry out; and the cries of the harvesters have reached the ears of the Lord of Hosts.
59_5_5: You have lived luxuriously on the earth and indulged yourselves; you have fattened your hearts as in a day of slaughter.
59_5_6: You have condemned and murdered the righteous, who does not resist you.
59_5_7: Therefore be patient, brethren, until the coming of the Lord. See how the farmer waits for the precious fruit of the earth, waiting patiently for it until it receives the early and latter rain.
59_5_8: You also be patient. Strengthen your hearts, for the coming of the Lord is near.
59_5_9: Do not grumble against one another, brethren, lest you be judged. Behold, the Judge is standing at the door!
59_5_10: My brethren, take the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord as an example of suffering and patience.
59_5_11: Indeed we count them blessed who endure. You have heard of the perseverance of Job and seen the end intended by the Lord—that the Lord is very compassionate and merciful.
59_5_12: But above all, my brethren, do not swear, either by heaven or by earth or with any other oath. But let your “Yes” be “Yes,” and your “No,” “No,” lest you fall into judgment.
59_5_13: Is anyone among you suffering? Let him pray. Is anyone cheerful? Let him sing praises.
59_5_14: Is anyone among you sick? Let him call for the elders of the church, and let them pray over him, anointing him with oil in the name of the Lord.
59_5_15: And the prayer of faith will save the sick, and the Lord will raise him up. And if he has committed sins, he will be forgiven.
59_5_16: Confess your sins to one another, and pray for one another, that you may be healed. The effective prayer of a righteous person has great power.
59_5_17: Elijah was a man with a nature like ours, and he prayed earnestly that it would not rain; and it did not rain on the land for three years and six months.
59_5_18: And he prayed again, and the heaven gave rain, and the earth produced its fruit.
59_5_19: My brothers and sisters, if anyone among you wanders from the truth, and someone turns him back,
59_5_20: Let him know that he who turns a sinner from the error of his way will save a soul from death and cover a multitude of sins.
60_1_1: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ, to the elect exiles of the Dispersion in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia,
60_1_2: elect according to the foreknowledge of God the Father, through the sanctifying work of the Spirit, for obedience and sprinkling of the blood of Jesus Christ: Grace to you and peace be multiplied.
60_1_3: Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who according to His great mercy has caused us to be born again to a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead,
60_1_4: to an inheritance that is imperishable, undefiled, and unfading, kept in heaven for you,
60_1_5: who are protected by the power of God through faith for a salvation ready to be revealed in the last time.
60_1_6: In this you greatly rejoice, though now for a little while, if necessary, you have been grieved by various trials,
60_1_7: that the tested genuineness of your faith, more precious than gold that perishes though it is tested by fire, may be found to result in praise, honor, and glory at the revelation of Jesus Christ,
60_1_8: whom having not seen you love. Though now you do not see Him, yet believing, you rejoice with joy inexpressible and full of glory,
60_1_9: receiving the outcome of your faith—the salvation of your souls.
60_1_10: Of this salvation the prophets have inquired and searched carefully, who prophesied of the grace that would come to you,
60_1_11: searching what, or what manner of time, the Spirit of Christ who was in them was indicating when He testified beforehand the sufferings of Christ and the glories that would follow.
60_1_12: To them it was revealed that, not to themselves, but to us they were ministering the things which now have been reported to you through those who have preached the gospel to you by the Holy Spirit sent from heaven—things which angels desire to look into.
60_1_13: Therefore gird up the loins of your mind, be sober, and rest your hope fully upon the grace that is to be brought to you at the revelation of Jesus Christ;
60_1_14: as obedient children, not conforming yourselves to the former lusts, as in your ignorance;
60_1_15: but as He who called you is holy, you also be holy in all your conduct,
60_1_16: because it is written, “Be holy, for I am holy.”
60_1_17: And if you call on the Father, who without partiality judges according to each one’s work, conduct yourselves throughout the time of your stay here in fear;
60_1_18: knowing that you were not redeemed with perishable things like silver or gold from your aimless conduct received by tradition from your fathers,
60_1_19: but with the precious blood of Christ, as of a lamb without blemish and without spot.
60_1_20: He indeed was foreordained before the foundation of the world, but was manifest in these last times for you
60_1_21: Through Him you believe in God, who raised Him from the dead and gave Him glory, so that your faith and hope are in God.
60_1_22: Since you have purified your souls by obeying the truth for a sincere love of the brethren, love one another fervently with a pure heart.
60_1_23: Having been born again, not of corruptible seed but incorruptible, through the living and enduring word of God.
60_1_24: For all flesh is like grass, and all its glory like the flower of the grass. The grass withers, and its flower falls away.
60_1_25: But the word of the Lord endures forever. Now this is the word which by the gospel was preached to you.
60_2_1: Therefore, laying aside all malice, all deceit, hypocrisy, envy, and all evil speaking.
60_2_2: As newborn babes, desire the pure milk of the word, that you may grow thereby into salvation.
60_2_3: If indeed you have tasted that the Lord is good.
60_2_4: Coming to Him as to a living stone, rejected by men but chosen by God and precious.
60_2_5: You also, as living stones, are being built up a spiritual house, a holy priesthood, to offer up spiritual sacrifices acceptable to God through Jesus Christ.
60_2_6: Therefore it is also contained in the Scripture: “Behold, I lay in Zion a chief cornerstone, chosen and precious, and he who believes on Him will by no means be put to shame.”
60_2_7: Therefore, to you who believe, He is precious; but to those who are disobedient, “The stone which the builders rejected has become the chief cornerstone.”
60_2_8: And a stone of stumbling and a rock of offense. They stumble because they disobey the word, as they were appointed.
60_2_9: But you are a chosen generation, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, His own special people, that you may proclaim the praises of Him who called you out of darkness into His marvelous light.
60_2_10: Who once were not a people but are now the people of God, who had not obtained mercy but now have obtained mercy.
60_2_11: Beloved, I urge you as sojourners and pilgrims, abstain from fleshly lusts which war against the soul.
60_2_12: Having your conduct honorable among the Gentiles, that when they speak against you as evildoers, they may, by your good works which they observe, glorify God in the day of visitation.
60_2_13: Therefore submit yourselves to every ordinance of man for the Lord’s sake, whether to the king as supreme.
60_2_14: Or to governors, as to those who are sent by him for the punishment of evildoers and for the praise of those who do good.
60_2_15: For this is the will of God, that by doing good you may put to silence the ignorance of foolish men.
60_2_16: Live as free people, yet not using your freedom as a cover-up for evil, but as bondservants of God.
60_2_17: Honor everyone. Love the brotherhood. Fear God. Honor the king.
60_2_18: Servants, be submissive to your masters with all respect, not only to the good and gentle but also to the harsh.
60_2_19: For this is commendable, if because of conscience toward God one endures grief, suffering unjustly.
60_2_20: For what credit is it if, when you are beaten for your faults, you take it patiently? But when you do good and suffer, if you endure it patiently, this is commendable before God.
60_2_21: For to this you were called, because Christ also suffered for you, leaving you an example, that you should follow His steps.
60_2_22: He committed no sin, nor was deceit found in His mouth.
60_2_23: When He was reviled, He did not revile in return; when He suffered, He did not threaten, but entrusted Himself to Him who judges righteously.
60_2_24: He Himself bore our sins in His own body on the tree, that we, having died to sins, might live for righteousness—by whose wounds you were healed.
60_2_25: For you were like sheep going astray, but have now returned to the Shepherd and Overseer of your souls.
