40_4_7: Jesus said to him, 'It is written again, 'You shall not put the Lord your God to the test.'
40_4_8: Again, the devil took Him up on an exceedingly high mountain, and showed Him all the kingdoms of the world and their glory.
40_4_9: And he said to Him, 'All these things I will give You if You will fall down and worship me.'
40_4_10: Then Jesus said to him, 'Away with you, Satan! For it is written, 'You shall worship the Lord your God, and Him only you shall serve.'
40_4_11: Then the devil left Him, and behold, angels came and began to minister to Him.
40_4_12: Now when Jesus heard that John had been put in prison, He departed to Galilee.
40_4_13: And leaving Nazareth, He came and dwelt in Capernaum by the sea, in the regions of Zebulun and Naphtali,
40_4_14: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Isaiah the prophet, saying:
40_4_15: 'The land of Zebulun and the land of Naphtali, by the way of the sea, beyond the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles:
40_4_16: The people who sat in darkness have seen a great light, and upon those who sat in the region and shadow of death, a light has dawned.'
40_4_17: From that time Jesus began to preach and to say, 'Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.'
40_4_18: And Jesus, walking by the Sea of Galilee, saw two brothers, Simon called Peter, and Andrew his brother, casting a net into the sea; for they were fishermen.
40_4_19: Then He said to them, 'Follow Me, and I will make you fishers of men.'
40_4_20: They immediately left their nets and followed Him.
40_4_21: Going on from there, He saw two other brothers, James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, in the boat with Zebedee their father, mending their nets. He called them,
40_4_22: and immediately they left the boat and their father, and followed Him.
40_4_23: And Jesus went about all Galilee, teaching in their synagogues, preaching the gospel of the kingdom, and healing every disease and every affliction among the people.
40_4_24: Then His fame went throughout all Syria; and they brought to Him all sick people who were afflicted with various diseases and torments, and those who were demon-possessed, epileptics, and paralytics; and He healed them.
40_4_25: Great multitudes followed Him from Galilee, and from Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea, and beyond the Jordan.
40_5_1: And seeing the multitudes, He went up on a mountain, and when He was seated, His disciples came to Him.
40_5_2: Then He opened His mouth and began to teach them, saying:
40_5_3: Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
40_5_4: Blessed are those who mourn, for they shall be comforted.
40_5_5: Blessed are the meek, for they shall inherit the earth.
40_5_6: Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they shall be satisfied.
40_5_7: Blessed are the merciful, for they shall obtain mercy.
40_5_8: Blessed are the pure in heart, for they shall see God.
40_5_9: Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called sons of God.
40_5_10: Blessed are those who are persecuted for righteousness' sake, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
40_5_11: Blessed are you when they revile and persecute you, and say all kinds of evil against you falsely for My sake.
40_5_12: Rejoice and be exceedingly glad, for great is your reward in heaven, for so they persecuted the prophets who were before you.
40_5_13: You are the salt of the earth; but if the salt loses its flavor, how shall it be seasoned? It is then good for nothing but to be thrown out and trampled underfoot by men.
40_5_14: You are the light of the world. A city that is set on a hill cannot be hidden.
40_5_15: Nor do they light a lamp and put it under a basket, but on a lampstand, and it gives light to all who are in the house.
40_5_16: Let your light shine before others, so that they may see your good works and give glory to your Father who is in heaven.
40_5_17: Do not think that I came to destroy the Law or the Prophets. I did not come to destroy but to fulfill.
40_5_18: For assuredly, I say to you, till heaven and earth pass away, one jot or one tittle will by no means pass from the law till all is fulfilled.
40_5_19: Whoever therefore breaks one of the least of these commandments, and teaches men so, shall be called least in the kingdom of heaven; but whoever does and teaches them shall be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
40_5_20: For I say to you, that unless your righteousness exceeds that of the scribes and Pharisees, you will by no means enter the kingdom of heaven.
40_5_21: You have heard that it was said to those of old, 'You shall not murder, and whoever murders will be in danger of the judgment.'
40_5_22: But I say to you that whoever is angry with his brother without a cause shall be in danger of the judgment. And whoever says to his brother, 'Raca!' shall be in danger of the council. But whoever says, 'You fool!' shall be in danger of hell fire.
40_5_23: Therefore if you bring your gift to the altar, and there remember that your brother has something against you,
40_5_24: leave your gift there before the altar, and go your way. First be reconciled to your brother, and then come and offer your gift.
40_5_25: Agree with your adversary quickly, while you are on the way with him, lest your adversary deliver you to the judge, the judge hand you over to the officer, and you be thrown into prison.
40_5_26: Assuredly, I say to you, you will by no means get out of there till you have paid the last penny.
40_5_27: You have heard that it was said to those of old, 'You shall not commit adultery.'
40_5_28: But I say to you that whoever looks at a woman to lust for her has already committed adultery with her in his heart.
40_5_29: If your right eye causes you to sin, pluck it out and cast it from you; for it is more profitable for you that one of your members perish than for your whole body to be cast into hell.
40_5_30: And if your right hand causes you to sin, cut it off and cast it from you; for it is more profitable for you that one of your members perish than for your whole body to be cast into hell.
40_5_31: Furthermore it has been said, 'Whoever divorces his wife, let him give her a certificate of divorce.'
40_5_32: But I say to you that everyone who divorces his wife, except on the ground of sexual immorality, causes her to commit adultery; and whoever marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
40_5_33: Again you have heard that it was said to those of old, 'You shall not swear falsely, but shall perform your oaths to the Lord.'
40_5_34: But I say to you, do not swear at all: neither by heaven, for it is God's throne;
40_5_35: nor by the earth, for it is His footstool; nor by Jerusalem, for it is the city of the great King.
40_5_36: Nor shall you swear by your head, because you cannot make one hair white or black.
40_5_37: But let your 'Yes' be 'Yes,' and your 'No,' 'No.' For whatever is more than these is from the evil one.
40_5_38: You have heard that it was said, 'An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.'
40_5_39: But I tell you not to resist an evil person. But if anyone slaps you on your right cheek, turn the other to him also.
40_5_40: If anyone wants to sue you and take away your tunic, let him have your cloak also.
40_5_41: And if anyone forces you to go one mile, go with him two miles.
40_5_42: Give to him who asks you, and from him who wants to borrow from you do not turn away.
40_5_43: You have heard that it was said, 'You shall love your neighbor and hate your enemy.'
40_5_44: But I say to you, love your enemies, bless those who curse you, do good to those who hate you, and pray for those who spitefully use you and persecute you,
40_5_45: that you may be sons of your Father in heaven; for He makes His sun rise on the evil and on the good, and sends rain on the just and on the unjust.
40_5_46: For if you love those who love you, what reward do you have? Do not even the tax collectors do the same?
40_5_47: And if you greet your brethren only, what do you do more than others? Do not even the tax collectors do so?
40_5_48: Therefore you shall be perfect, just as your Father in heaven is perfect.
40_6_1: Take heed that you do not do your charitable deeds before men, to be seen by them. Otherwise you have no reward from your Father in heaven.
40_6_2: Therefore, when you do a charitable deed, do not sound a trumpet before you as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets, that they may have glory from men. Assuredly, I say to you, they have their reward.
40_6_3: But when you do a charitable deed, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing,
40_6_4: that your charitable deed may be in secret; and your Father who sees in secret will Himself reward you openly.
40_6_5: And when you pray, you shall not be like the hypocrites. For they love to pray standing in the synagogues and on the corners of the streets, that they may be seen by men. Assuredly, I say to you, they have their reward.
40_6_6: But you, when you pray, go into your room, and when you have shut your door, pray to your Father who is in the secret place; and your Father who sees in secret will reward you openly.
40_6_7: And when you pray, do not use vain repetitions as the heathen do. For they think that they will be heard for their many words.
40_6_8: Therefore do not be like them. For your Father knows the things you have need of before you ask Him.
40_6_9: In this manner, therefore, pray: Our Father in heaven, hallowed be Your name.
40_6_10: Your kingdom come. Your will be done on earth as it is in heaven.
40_6_11: Give us this day our daily bread.
40_6_12: And forgive us our debts, as we also have forgiven our debtors.
40_6_13: And do not lead us into temptation, but deliver us from the evil one. For Yours is the kingdom and the power and the glory forever. Amen.
40_6_14: For if you forgive others their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.
40_6_15: But if you do not forgive others their trespasses, neither will your Father forgive your trespasses.
40_6_16: Moreover, when you fast, do not look gloomy like the hypocrites, for they disfigure their faces that their fasting may be seen by others. Truly, I say to you, they have received their reward.
40_6_17: But you, when you fast, anoint your head and wash your face,
40_6_18: so that your fasting may not be seen by others but by your Father who is in secret; and your Father who sees in secret will reward you openly.
40_6_19: Do not lay up for yourselves treasures on earth, where moth and rust destroy and where thieves break in and steal;
40_6_20: but lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where neither moth nor rust destroys and where thieves do not break in and steal.
40_6_21: For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
40_6_22: The eye is the lamp of the body. If your eye is healthy, your whole body will be full of light.
40_6_23: But if your eye is bad, your whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light that is in you is darkness, how great is that darkness!
40_6_24: No one can serve two masters; for either he will hate the one and love the other, or he will be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve both God and money.
40_6_25: Therefore I say to you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat or what you will drink; nor about your body, what you will put on. Is not life more than food and the body more than clothing?
40_6_26: Look at the birds of the air, for they neither sow nor reap nor gather into barns; yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Are you not of more value than they?
40_6_27: Which of you by worrying can add one moment to his life?
40_6_28: So why do you worry about clothing? Consider the lilies of the field, how they grow: they neither toil nor spin;
40_6_29: and yet I say to you that even Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these.
40_6_30: Now if God so clothes the grass of the field, which today is alive and tomorrow is thrown into the oven, will He not much more clothe you, O you of little faith?
40_6_31: Therefore do not worry, saying, 'What shall we eat?' or 'What shall we drink?' or 'What shall we wear?'
40_6_32: For the Gentiles seek after all these things, and your heavenly Father knows that you need them all.
40_6_33: But seek first the kingdom of God and His righteousness, and all these things will be added to you.
40_6_34: Therefore do not worry about tomorrow, for tomorrow will worry about itself. Each day has enough trouble of its own.
40_7_1: Judge not, that you be not judged.
40_7_2: For with the judgment you pronounce you will be judged, and with the measure you use it will be measured to you.
40_7_3: And why do you look at the speck in your brother's eye, but do not consider the plank in your own eye?
40_7_4: Or how can you say to your brother, 'Let me remove the speck from your eye'; and look, a plank is in your own eye?
40_7_5: Hypocrite! First remove the plank from your own eye, and then you will see clearly to remove the speck from your brother's eye.
40_7_6: Do not give what is holy to the dogs; nor cast your pearls before swine, lest they trample them under their feet and turn and tear you in pieces.
40_7_7: Ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and it will be opened to you.
40_7_8: For everyone who asks receives, and the one who seeks finds, and to the one who knocks, it will be opened.
40_7_9: Or what man is there among you who, if his son asks for bread, will give him a stone?
40_7_10: Or if he asks for a fish, will he give him a serpent?
40_7_11: If you then, being evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father who is in heaven give good things to those who ask Him!
40_7_12: Therefore, whatever you wish that others would do to you, do also to them, for this is the Law and the Prophets.
40_7_13: Enter by the narrow gate; for wide is the gate and broad is the way that leads to destruction, and there are many who go in by it.
40_7_14: Because narrow is the gate and difficult is the way which leads to life, and there are few who find it.
40_7_15: Beware of false prophets, who come to you in sheep's clothing, but inwardly they are ravenous wolves.
40_7_16: You will know them by their fruits. Do men gather grapes from thornbushes or figs from thistles?
40_7_17: Even so, every good tree bears good fruit, but a bad tree bears bad fruit.
40_7_18: A good tree cannot bear bad fruit, nor can a bad tree bear good fruit.
40_7_19: Every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
40_7_20: Therefore by their fruits you will know them.
40_7_21: Not everyone who says to Me, 'Lord, Lord,' shall enter the kingdom of heaven, but he who does the will of My Father who is in heaven.
40_7_22: Many will say to Me in that day, 'Lord, Lord, have we not prophesied in Your name, cast out demons in Your name, and done many wonders in Your name?'
40_7_23: And then I will declare to them, 'I never knew you; depart from Me, you who practice lawlessness!'
40_7_24: Therefore whoever hears these sayings of Mine, and does them, I will liken him to a wise man who built his house on the rock:
40_7_25: and the rain descended, the floods came, and the winds blew and beat on that house; and it did not fall, for it was founded on the rock.
40_7_26: But everyone who hears these sayings of Mine, and does not do them, will be like a foolish man who built his house on the sand:
40_7_27: and the rain descended, the floods came, and the winds blew and beat on that house; and it fell. And great was its fall.
40_7_28: And so it was, when Jesus had ended these sayings, that the people were astonished at His teaching,
40_7_29: for He taught them as one having authority, and not as the scribes.
40_8_1: When He had come down from the mountain, great multitudes followed Him.
40_8_2: And behold, a leper came and worshiped Him, saying, 'Lord, if You are willing, You can make me clean.'
40_8_3: Then Jesus put out His hand and touched him, saying, 'I am willing; be cleansed.' Immediately his leprosy was cleansed.
40_8_4: And Jesus said to him, 'See that you tell no one; but go your way, show yourself to the priest, and offer the gift that Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.'
40_8_5: Now when Jesus had entered Capernaum, a centurion came to Him, pleading with Him,
40_8_6: saying, 'Lord, my servant is lying at home paralyzed, dreadfully tormented.'
40_8_7: And Jesus said to him, 'I will come and heal him.'
40_8_8: The centurion answered and said, 'Lord, I am not worthy that You should come under my roof. But only speak a word, and my servant will be healed.
40_8_9: For I also am a man under authority, having soldiers under me. And I say to this one, 'Go,' and he goes; and to another, 'Come,' and he comes; and to my servant, 'Do this,' and he does it.
40_8_10: When Jesus heard it, He marveled, and said to those who followed, "Truly, I say to you, I have not found such great faith, not even in Israel!"
40_8_11: And I say to you that many will come from east and west and recline at table with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven.
40_8_12: But the sons of the kingdom will be cast out into outer darkness; there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
40_8_13: Then Jesus said to the centurion, "Go your way; and as you have believed, let it be done for you." And his servant was healed that very moment.
40_8_14: Now when Jesus had come into Peter's house, He saw his mother-in-law lying sick with a fever.
40_8_15: So He touched her hand, and the fever left her; and she arose and began to serve Him.
40_8_16: When evening had come, they brought to Him many who were demon-possessed, and He cast out the spirits with a word and healed all who were sick.
40_8_17: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Isaiah the prophet, saying: "He Himself took our infirmities and bore our sicknesses."
40_8_18: And when Jesus saw great multitudes about Him, He gave a command to depart to the other side.
40_8_19: Then a certain scribe came and said to Him, "Teacher, I will follow You wherever You go."
40_8_20: And Jesus said to him, "Foxes have holes and birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay His head."
40_8_21: Then another of His disciples said to Him, "Lord, let me first go and bury my father."
40_8_22: But Jesus said to him, "Follow Me, and let the dead bury their own dead."
40_8_23: Now when He got into a boat, His disciples followed Him.
40_8_24: And suddenly a great tempest arose on the sea, so that the boat was being swamped by the waves; but He was asleep.
40_8_25: Then His disciples came to Him and awoke Him, saying, "Lord, save us! We are perishing!"
40_8_26: But He said to them, "Why are you fearful, O you of little faith?" Then He arose and rebuked the winds and the sea, and there was a great calm.
40_8_27: So the men marveled, saying, "What sort of man is this, that even winds and sea obey Him?"
40_8_28: When He had come to the other side, to the region of the Gadarenes, there met Him two demon-possessed men, coming out of the tombs, exceedingly fierce, so that no one could pass that way.
40_8_29: And suddenly they cried out, saying, "What have we to do with You, Jesus, You Son of God? Have You come here to torment us before the time?"
40_8_30: Now a good way off from them there was a herd of many swine feeding.
40_8_31: So the demons begged Him, saying, "If You cast us out, permit us to go away into the herd of swine."
40_8_32: And He said to them, "Go." So when they had come out, they went into the herd of swine, and suddenly the whole herd rushed down the steep bank into the sea and perished in the waters.
40_8_33: Then those who kept them fled; and they went away into the city and told everything, including what had happened to the demon-possessed men.
40_8_34: And behold, the whole city came out to meet Jesus, and when they saw Him, they begged Him to depart from their region.
40_9_1: So He got into a boat, crossed over, and came to His own city.
40_9_2: Then behold, they brought to Him a paralytic lying on a bed. When Jesus saw their faith, He said to the paralytic, "Take heart, my son; your sins are forgiven."
40_9_3: And at once some of the scribes said within themselves, "This man blasphemes!"
40_9_4: But Jesus, knowing their thoughts, said, "Why do you think evil in your hearts?
40_9_5: For which is easier, to say, 'Your sins are forgiven you,' or to say, 'Arise and walk'?
40_9_6: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins, then He said to the paralytic, Arise, take up your bed, and go to your house.
40_9_7: And he arose and went home.
40_9_8: Now when the multitudes saw it, they were awestruck and glorified God, who had given such authority to men.
40_9_9: As Jesus passed on from there, He saw a man named Matthew sitting at the tax office. And He said to him, Follow Me. So he arose and followed Him.
40_9_10: Now it happened, as Jesus reclined at the table in the house, that behold, many tax collectors and sinners came and were dining with Him and His disciples.
40_9_11: And when the Pharisees saw this, they said to His disciples, Why does your Teacher eat with tax collectors and sinners?
40_9_12: When Jesus heard that, He said to them, Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick.
40_9_13: But go and learn what this means: I desire mercy and not sacrifice. For I did not come to call the righteous, but sinners, to repentance.
40_9_14: Then the disciples of John came to Him, saying, Why do we and the Pharisees fast often, but Your disciples do not fast?
40_9_15: And Jesus said to them, Can the wedding guests mourn as long as the bridegroom is with them? But the days will come when the bridegroom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast.
40_9_16: No one puts a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old garment, for the patch pulls away from the garment, and the tear is made worse.
40_9_17: Nor do they put new wine into old wineskins, or else the wineskins break, the wine is spilled, and the wineskins are ruined. But they put new wine into fresh wineskins, and both are preserved.
40_9_18: While He spoke these things to them, behold, a ruler came and worshiped Him, saying, My daughter has just died, but come and lay Your hand on her and she will live.
40_9_19: So Jesus arose and followed him, and so did His disciples.
40_9_20: And suddenly, a woman who had a flow of blood for twelve years came from behind and touched the fringe of His garment.
40_9_21: For she said to herself, If only I may touch His garment, I shall be made well.
40_9_22: But Jesus turned around, and when He saw her He said, Be of good cheer, daughter; your faith has made you well. And the woman was made well from that hour.
40_9_23: When Jesus came into the ruler’s house, and saw the flute players and the noisy crowd wailing,
40_9_24: He said to them, Make room, for the girl is not dead, but sleeping. And they ridiculed Him.
40_9_25: But when the crowd was put outside, He went in and took her by the hand, and the girl arose.
40_9_26: And the report of this went out into all that land.
40_9_27: When Jesus departed from there, two blind men followed Him, crying out and saying, Son of David, have mercy on us!
40_9_28: And when He had come into the house, the blind men came to Him. And Jesus said to them, Do you believe that I am able to do this? They said to Him, Yes, Lord.
40_9_29: Then He touched their eyes, saying, According to your faith let it be to you.
40_9_30: And their eyes were opened. And Jesus sternly warned them, saying, See that no one knows it.
40_9_31: But when they had departed, they spread the news about Him in all that country.
40_9_32: As they went out, behold, they brought to Him a man, mute and demon-possessed.
40_9_33: And when the demon was cast out, the mute spoke. And the multitudes marveled, saying, It was never seen like this in Israel!
40_9_34: But the Pharisees said, He casts out demons by the ruler of the demons.
40_9_35: Then Jesus went about all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, preaching the gospel of the kingdom, and healing every sickness and every disease among the people.
40_9_36: But when He saw the multitudes, He was moved with compassion for them, because they were weary and scattered, like sheep without a shepherd.
40_9_37: Then He said to His disciples, 'The harvest truly is plentiful, but the laborers are few.
40_9_38: Therefore pray to the Lord of the harvest to send out workers into His harvest.'
40_10_1: And when He had called His twelve disciples to Him, He gave them authority over unclean spirits, to cast them out, and to heal every kind of sickness and every kind of disease.
40_10_2: Now the names of the twelve apostles are these: first, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew his brother; James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother;
40_10_3: Philip and Bartholomew; Thomas and Matthew the tax collector; James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus;
40_10_4: Simon the Zealot, and Judas Iscariot, who also betrayed Him.
40_10_5: These twelve Jesus sent out and commanded them, saying: 'Do not go into the way of the Gentiles, and do not enter a city of the Samaritans.
40_10_6: But go rather to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.
40_10_7: And as you go, proclaim, saying, 'The kingdom of heaven is at hand.'
40_10_8: Heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse those with leprosy, cast out demons. Freely you have received, freely give.
40_10_9: Provide neither gold nor silver nor copper in your money belts,
40_10_10: nor bag for your journey, nor two tunics, nor sandals, nor staffs; for a worker is worthy of his food.
40_10_11: 'Now whatever city or town you enter, inquire who in it is worthy, and stay there till you go out.
40_10_12: And when you go into a household, greet it.
40_10_13: If the household is worthy, let your peace come upon it; but if it is not worthy, let your peace return to you.
40_10_14: And whoever will not receive you nor hear your words, when you depart from that house or city, shake off the dust from your feet.
40_10_15: Assuredly, I say to you, it will be more tolerable for the land of Sodom and Gomorrah in the day of judgment than for that city!
40_10_16: 'Behold, I send you out as sheep in the midst of wolves. Therefore be wise as serpents and innocent as doves.
40_10_17: But beware of men, for they will deliver you up to councils and scourge you in their synagogues.
40_10_18: You will be brought before governors and kings for My sake, as a testimony to them and to the Gentiles.
40_10_19: But when they deliver you up, do not worry about how or what you should speak, for it will be given to you in that hour what you should speak;
40_10_20: for it is not you who speak, but the Spirit of your Father speaking through you.
40_10_21: 'Now brother will deliver up brother to death, and a father his child; and children will rise up against parents and cause them to be put to death.
40_10_22: And you will be hated by all for My name's sake. But he who endures to the end will be saved.
40_10_23: When they persecute you in this city, flee to another. For assuredly, I say to you, you will not have gone through the cities of Israel before the Son of Man comes.
40_10_24: 'A disciple is not above his teacher, nor a servant above his master.
40_10_25: It is enough for a disciple that he be like his teacher, and a servant like his master. If they have called the master of the house Beelzebul, how much more will they call those of his household!
40_10_26: Therefore do not fear them, for there is nothing covered that will not be revealed, and hidden that will not be known.
40_10_27: 'Whatever I tell you in the dark, speak in the light; and what you hear in a whisper, proclaim on the housetops.
40_10_28: And do not fear those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul. But rather fear Him who is able to destroy both soul and body in hell.
40_10_29: Are not two sparrows sold for a copper coin? And not one of them falls to the ground apart from your Father’s will.
40_10_30: But even the very hairs of your head are all numbered.
40_10_31: Do not fear therefore; you are of more value than many sparrows.
40_10_32: Therefore whoever confesses Me before men, him I will also confess before My Father who is in heaven.
40_10_33: But whoever denies Me before men, him I will also deny before My Father who is in heaven.
40_10_34: Do not think that I came to bring peace on earth. I did not come to bring peace but a sword.
40_10_35: For I have come to set a man against his father, a daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law;
40_10_36: and a man’s enemies will be those of his own household.
40_10_37: He who loves father or mother more than Me is not worthy of Me. And he who loves son or daughter more than Me is not worthy of Me.
40_10_38: And he who does not take his cross and follow after Me is not worthy of Me.
40_10_39: He who finds his life will lose it, and he who loses his life for My sake will find it.
40_10_40: He who receives you receives Me, and he who receives Me receives Him who sent Me.
40_10_41: He who receives a prophet in the name of a prophet shall receive a prophet’s reward. And he who receives a righteous man in the name of a righteous man shall receive a righteous man’s reward.
40_10_42: And whoever gives one of these little ones only a cup of cold water in the name of a disciple, assuredly, I say to you, he shall by no means lose his reward.
40_11_1: Now it came to pass, when Jesus finished commanding His twelve disciples, that He departed from there to teach and to preach in their cities.
40_11_2: And when John had heard in prison about the works of Christ, he sent two of his disciples
40_11_3: and said to Him, Are You the Coming One, or do we look for another?
40_11_4: Jesus answered and said to them, Go and tell John the things which you hear and see:
40_11_5: The blind receive their sight and the lame walk; the lepers are cleansed and the deaf hear; the dead are raised up and the poor have the gospel preached to them.
40_11_6: And blessed is he who is not offended because of Me.
40_11_7: As they departed, Jesus began to say to the multitudes concerning John: What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken by the wind?
40_11_8: But what did you go out to see? A man clothed in soft garments? Indeed, those who wear soft clothing are in kings’ houses.
40_11_9: But what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I say to you, and more than a prophet.
40_11_10: For this is he of whom it is written: Behold, I send My messenger before Your face, Who will prepare Your way before You.
40_11_11: Assuredly, I say to you, among those born of women there has not risen one greater than John the Baptist; but he who is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
40_11_12: And from the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven suffers violence, and the violent take it by force.
40_11_13: For all the prophets and the law prophesied until John.
40_11_14: And if you are willing to receive it, he is Elijah who is to come.
40_11_15: He who has ears to hear, let him hear!
40_11_16: But to what shall I liken this generation? It is like children sitting in the marketplaces and calling to their playmates,
40_11_17: and saying: 'We played the flute for you, and you did not dance; we sang a dirge, and you did not mourn.'
40_11_18: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, 'He has a demon.'
40_11_19: The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they say, 'Look, a glutton and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!' Yet wisdom is justified by her deeds.
40_11_20: Then He began to denounce the cities where most of His mighty works had been done, because they did not repent:
40_11_21: Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the mighty works done in you had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.
40_11_22: But I say to you, it will be more tolerable on the day of judgment for Tyre and Sidon than for you.
40_11_23: And you, Capernaum, who are exalted to heaven, will be brought down to Hades; for if the mighty works which were done in you had been done in Sodom, it would have remained until this day.
40_11_24: But I say to you that it shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom on the day of judgment than for you.
40_11_25: At that time Jesus answered and said, 'I thank You, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that You have hidden these things from the wise and understanding and have revealed them to infants.
40_11_26: Yes, Father, for such was your gracious will.
40_11_27: All things have been delivered to Me by My Father, and no one knows the Son except the Father, nor does anyone know the Father except the Son and anyone to whom the Son chooses to reveal Him.
40_11_28: Come to Me, all you who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.
40_11_29: Take My yoke upon you and learn from Me, for I am gentle and lowly in heart, and you will find rest for your souls.
40_11_30: For My yoke is easy and My burden is light.
40_12_1: At that time Jesus went through the grainfields on the Sabbath. His disciples were hungry, and began to pluck heads of grain and to eat.
40_12_2: But when the Pharisees saw it, they said to Him, 'Look, Your disciples are doing what is not lawful to do on the Sabbath!'
40_12_3: But He said to them, 'Have you not read what David did when he was hungry, he and those who were with him:
40_12_4: how he entered the house of God and ate the bread of the Presence, which it was not lawful for him to eat nor for those who were with him, but only for the priests?
40_12_5: Or have you not read in the law that on the Sabbath the priests in the temple profane the Sabbath and are guiltless?
40_12_6: But I say to you that something greater than the temple is here.
40_12_7: But if you had known what this means, 'I desire mercy and not sacrifice,' you would not have condemned the innocent.
40_12_8: For the Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath.
40_12_9: Now when He had departed from there, He went into their synagogue.
40_12_10: And behold, there was a man who had a withered hand. And they asked Him, saying, 'Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?' that they might accuse Him.
40_12_11: Then He said to them, 'What man is there among you who has one sheep, and if it falls into a pit on the Sabbath, will not lay hold of it and lift it out?
40_12_12: Of how much more value then is a man than a sheep? Therefore it is lawful to do good on the Sabbath.
40_12_13: Then He said to the man, 'Stretch out your hand.' And he stretched it out, and it was restored as whole as the other.
40_12_14: Then the Pharisees went out and plotted against Him, how they might destroy Him.
40_12_15: But when Jesus knew it, He withdrew from there. And great multitudes followed Him, and He healed them all.
40_12_16: Yet He warned them not to make Him known,
40_12_17: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet Isaiah, saying:
40_12_18: Behold! My Servant whom I have chosen, My Beloved in whom My soul is well pleased! I will put My Spirit upon Him, and He will proclaim justice to the nations.
40_12_19: He will not quarrel nor cry out, nor will anyone hear His voice in the streets.
40_12_20: A bruised reed He will not break, and a smoldering wick He will not quench, until He leads justice to victory;
40_12_21: And in His name the Gentiles will hope.
40_12_22: Then one was brought to Him who was demon-possessed, blind and mute; and He healed him, so that the man spoke and saw.
40_12_23: And all the multitudes were amazed and said, 'Could this be the Son of David?'
40_12_24: Now when the Pharisees heard it they said, 'This fellow does not cast out demons except by Beelzebul, the ruler of the demons.'
40_12_25: But Jesus knew their thoughts, and said to them: 'Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation, and every city or house divided against itself will not stand.
40_12_26: If Satan casts out Satan, he is divided against himself. How then will his kingdom stand?
40_12_27: And if I cast out demons by Beelzebul, by whom do your sons cast them out? Therefore they shall be your judges.
40_12_28: But if I cast out demons by the Spirit of God, surely the kingdom of God has come upon you.
40_12_29: Or how can one enter a strong man's house and plunder his goods, unless he first binds the strong man? And then he will plunder his house.
40_12_30: He who is not with Me is against Me, and he who does not gather with Me scatters abroad.
40_12_31: Therefore I say to you, every sin and blasphemy will be forgiven men, but the blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven men.
40_12_32: Anyone who speaks a word against the Son of Man, it will be forgiven him; but whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit, it will not be forgiven him, either in this age or in the age to come.
40_12_33: Either make the tree good and its fruit good, or make the tree bad and its fruit bad; for a tree is known by its fruit.
40_12_34: Brood of vipers! How can you, being evil, speak good things? For out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaks.
40_12_35: A good man out of the good treasure of his heart brings forth good things, and an evil man out of the evil treasure brings forth evil things.
40_12_36: But I say to you that for every idle word men may speak, they will give account of it in the day of judgment.
40_12_37: For by your words you will be justified, and by your words you will be condemned.
40_12_38: Then some of the scribes and Pharisees answered, saying, 'Teacher, we want to see a sign from You.'
40_12_39: But He answered and said to them, 'An evil and adulterous generation seeks after a sign, and no sign will be given to it except the sign of the prophet Jonah.
40_12_40: For as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the great fish, so will the Son of Man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.
40_12_41: The men of Nineveh will rise up in the judgment with this generation and condemn it, because they repented at the preaching of Jonah; and indeed a greater than Jonah is here.
40_12_42: The queen of the South will rise up in the judgment with this generation and condemn it, for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and indeed a greater than Solomon is here.
40_12_43: When an unclean spirit goes out of a man, he goes through dry places, seeking rest, and finds none.
40_12_44: Then he says, 'I will return to my house from which I came.' And when he comes, he finds it empty, swept, and put in order.
40_12_45: Then he goes and takes with him seven other spirits more wicked than himself, and they enter and dwell there; and the last state of that man is worse than the first. So shall it also be with this wicked generation.
40_12_46: While He was still talking to the multitudes, behold, His mother and brothers stood outside, seeking to speak with Him.
40_12_47: Then someone said to Him, 'Look, Your mother and Your brothers are standing outside, wanting to speak to You.'
40_12_48: But He answered and said to the one who told Him, 'Who is My mother and who are My brothers?'
40_12_49: And He stretched out His hand toward His disciples and said, 'Here are My mother and My brothers!
40_12_50: For whoever does the will of My Father in heaven is My brother and sister and mother.'
40_13_1: On that same day Jesus went out of the house and sat beside the sea.
40_13_2: And great multitudes were gathered together to Him, so that He got into a boat and sat; and the whole multitude stood on the shore.
40_13_3: Then He spoke many things to them in parables, saying: 'Behold, a sower went out to sow.
40_13_4: And as he sowed, some seed fell along the path, and the birds came and devoured them.
40_13_5: Some fell on rocky ground, where they did not have much soil; and they immediately sprang up because they had no depth of soil.
40_13_6: But when the sun was up they were scorched, and because they had no root, they withered away.
40_13_7: And some fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up and choked them.
40_13_8: But others fell on good ground and yielded a crop: some a hundredfold, some sixty, some thirty.
40_13_9: He who has ears to hear, let him hear!
40_13_10: And the disciples came and said to Him, 'Why do You speak to them in parables?'
40_13_11: He answered and said to them, 'Because it has been given to you to know the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it has not been given.
40_13_12: For whoever has, to him more will be given, and he will have abundance; but whoever does not have, even what he has will be taken away from him.
40_13_13: Therefore I speak to them in parables, because seeing they do not see, and hearing they do not hear, nor do they understand.
40_13_14: And in them the prophecy of Isaiah is fulfilled, which says: 'Hearing you will hear and shall not understand, and seeing you will see and not perceive;
40_13_15: For the hearts of this people have grown dull. Their ears are hard of hearing, and their eyes they have closed, lest they should see with their eyes and hear with their ears, lest they should understand with their hearts and turn, so that I should heal them.'
40_13_16: But blessed are your eyes for they see, and your ears for they hear;
40_13_17: for assuredly, I say to you that many prophets and righteous men desired to see what you see, and did not see it, and to hear what you hear, and did not hear it.
40_13_18: 'Therefore hear the parable of the sower:
40_13_19: When anyone hears the word of the kingdom, and does not understand it, then the evil one comes and snatches away what was sown in his heart. This is he who received seed by the wayside.
40_13_20: But he who received the seed on rocky ground, this is he who hears the word and immediately receives it with joy;
40_13_21: yet he has no root in himself, but endures only for a while. For when tribulation or persecution arises because of the word, immediately he stumbles.
40_13_22: Now he who received seed among the thorns is he who hears the word, and the cares of this world and the deceitfulness of riches choke the word, and he becomes unfruitful.
40_13_23: But he who received seed on the good ground is he who hears the word and understands it, who indeed bears fruit and produces: some a hundredfold, some sixty, some thirty.
40_13_24: Another parable He put forth to them, saying: 'The kingdom of heaven is like a man who sowed good seed in his field;
40_13_25: but while people were sleeping, his enemy came and sowed weeds among the wheat and went away.
40_13_26: But when the grain had sprouted and produced a crop, then the weeds also appeared.
40_13_27: So the servants of the owner came and said to him, 'Sir, did you not sow good seed in your field? How then does it have weeds?'
40_13_28: He said to them, 'An enemy has done this.' The servants said to him, 'Do you want us then to go and gather them up?'
40_13_29: But he said, 'No, lest while you gather up the weeds you also uproot the wheat with them.
40_13_30: Let both grow together until the harvest, and at the time of harvest I will say to the reapers, 'First gather together the weeds and bind them in bundles to burn them, but gather the wheat into my barn.'
40_13_31: Another parable He put forth to them, saying: 'The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed, which a man took and sowed in his field,
40_13_32: which indeed is the least of all the seeds; but when it is grown it is greater than the garden plants and becomes a tree, so that the birds of the air come and nest in its branches.'
40_13_33: Another parable He spoke to them: 'The kingdom of heaven is like leaven, which a woman took and hid in three measures of flour till it was all leavened.'
40_13_34: All these things Jesus spoke to the multitude in parables; and without a parable He did not speak to them,
40_13_35: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet, saying: 'I will open My mouth in parables; I will utter what has been hidden since the foundation of the world.'
40_13_36: Then Jesus sent the multitude away and went into the house. And His disciples came to Him, saying, 'Explain to us the parable of the weeds of the field.'
40_13_37: He answered and said to them: 'He who sows the good seed is the Son of Man.
40_13_38: The field is the world, the good seeds are the sons of the kingdom, but the weeds are the sons of the evil one.
40_13_39: The enemy who sowed them is the devil, the harvest is the end of the age, and the reapers are angels.
40_13_40: Therefore as the weeds are gathered and burned in the fire, so it will be at the end of this age.
40_13_41: The Son of Man will send out His angels, and they will gather out of His kingdom all causes of sin and those who practice lawlessness,
40_13_42: and will cast them into the furnace of fire. There will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
40_13_43: Then the righteous will shine forth as the sun in the kingdom of their Father. He who has ears to hear, let him hear!
40_13_44: 'Again, the kingdom of heaven is like treasure hidden in a field, which a man found and hid; and for joy over it he goes and sells all that he has and buys that field.
40_13_45: 'Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a merchant seeking fine pearls,
40_13_46: who, when he had found one pearl of great value, went and sold all that he had and bought it.
40_13_47: 'Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a dragnet that was cast into the sea and gathered fish of every kind,
40_13_48: which, when it was full, they drew to shore; and they sat down and gathered the good into vessels, but threw the bad away.
40_13_49: So it will be at the end of the age. The angels will come forth, separate the wicked from among the righteous,
40_13_50: and cast them into the furnace of fire. There will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
40_13_51: Jesus said to them, 'Have you understood all these things?' They said to Him, 'Yes, Lord.'
40_13_52: Then He said to them, 'Therefore every scribe instructed concerning the kingdom of heaven is like a householder who brings out of his treasure things new and old.'
40_13_53: Now it came to pass, when Jesus had finished these parables, that He departed from there.
40_13_54: When He had come to His own country, He taught them in their synagogue, so that they were astonished and said, 'Where did this Man get this wisdom and these mighty works?
40_13_55: Is this not the carpenter's son? Is not His mother called Mary? And His brothers James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas?
40_13_56: And are not all his sisters with us? Where then did this Man get all these things?
40_13_57: And they took offense at Him. But Jesus said to them, "A prophet is not without honor except in his hometown and in his own household."
40_13_58: Now He did not do many mighty works there because of their unbelief.
40_14_1: At that time Herod the tetrarch heard about the fame of Jesus.
40_14_2: and he said to his servants, "This is John the Baptist. He has been raised from the dead; that is why these miraculous powers are at work in him."
40_14_3: For Herod had seized John and bound him and put him in prison for the sake of Herodias, his brother Philip's wife.
40_14_4: because John had been saying to him, "It is not lawful for you to have her."
40_14_5: Although Herod wanted to put him to death, he feared the crowd, because they regarded John as a prophet.
40_14_6: But when Herod's birthday came, the daughter of Herodias danced before the company and pleased Herod.
40_14_7: so that he promised with an oath to give her whatever she might ask.
40_14_8: Prompted by her mother, she said, "Give me the head of John the Baptist here on a platter."
40_14_9: And the king was sorry, but because of his oaths and his guests he commanded it to be given.
40_14_10: So he sent and had John beheaded in the prison.
40_14_11: And his head was brought on a platter and given to the girl, who carried it to her mother.
40_14_12: Then his disciples came, removed the corpse, buried it, and went and reported to Jesus.
40_14_13: When Jesus heard about it, he withdrew from there by boat to a remote place to be alone. When the crowds heard this, they followed him on foot from the towns.
40_14_14: When he went ashore, he saw a large crowd, had compassion on them, and healed their sick.
40_14_15: When evening came, the disciples approached him and said, "This place is deserted, and it is already late. Send the crowds away so that they can go into the villages and buy food for themselves."
40_14_16: But Jesus said to them, "They don’t need to go away; you give them something to eat."
40_14_17: And they said to Him, "We have here only five loaves and two fish."
40_14_18: He said, "Bring them here to Me."
40_14_19: Then He commanded the multitudes to sit down on the grass. And He took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up to heaven, He blessed and broke and gave the loaves to the disciples; and the disciples gave to the multitudes.
40_14_20: So they all ate and were filled, and they picked up twelve baskets full of the fragments that remained.
40_14_21: Now those who had eaten were about five thousand men, besides women and children.
40_14_22: Immediately He made the disciples get into the boat and go ahead of Him to the other side, while He dismissed the crowds.
40_14_23: After dismissing the crowds, he went up on the mountain by himself to pray. Well into the night, he was there alone.
40_14_24: Meanwhile, the boat was already some distance from land, battered by the waves, because the wind was against them.
40_14_25: Now in the fourth watch of the night Jesus came to them, walking on the sea.
40_14_26: When the disciples saw him walking on the sea, they were terrified. "It’s a ghost!" they said, and they cried out in fear.
40_14_27: But immediately Jesus spoke to them, saying, "Take heart; it is I. Do not be afraid."
40_14_28: And Peter answered Him and said, “Lord, if it is You, command me to come to You on the water.”
40_14_29: So He said, “Come.” And when Peter had come down out of the boat, he walked on the water to go to Jesus.
40_14_30: But when he saw that the wind was strong, he was afraid; and beginning to sink he cried out, saying, “Lord, save me!”
40_14_31: And immediately Jesus stretched out His hand and caught him, and said to him, “O you of little faith, why did you doubt?”
40_14_32: And when they got into the boat, the wind ceased.
40_14_33: Then those who were in the boat came and worshiped Him, saying, “Truly You are the Son of God.”
40_14_34: When they had crossed over, they came to the land of Gennesaret.
40_14_35: And when the men of that place recognized Him, they sent word into all that surrounding region, and brought to Him all who were sick,
40_14_36: and begged Him that they might only touch the fringe of His garment. And as many as touched it were made perfectly well.
40_15_1: Then the scribes and Pharisees who were from Jerusalem came to Jesus, saying,
40_15_2: “Why do Your disciples break the tradition of the elders? For they do not wash their hands when they eat bread?”
40_15_3: He answered and said to them, “Why do you also transgress the commandment of God because of your tradition?
40_15_4: For God commanded, saying, ‘Honor your father and your mother’; and, ‘He who curses father or mother must surely be put to death.’
40_15_5: But you say, ‘Whoever says to his father or mother, “Whatever profit you might have received from me is a gift to God”—
40_15_6: then he need not honor his father or mother.’ Thus you have made the commandment of God of no effect by your tradition.
40_15_7: Hypocrites! Well did Isaiah prophesy about you, saying:
40_15_8: ‘These people draw near to Me with their mouth, and honor Me with their lips, but their heart is far from Me.
40_15_9: And in vain they worship Me, teaching as doctrines the commandments of men.’
40_15_10: When He had called the multitude to Himself, He said to them, “Hear and understand:
40_15_11: Not what goes into the mouth defiles a man; but what comes out of the mouth, this defiles a man.”
40_15_12: Then His disciples came and said to Him, “Do You know that the Pharisees were offended when they heard this saying?”
40_15_13: But He answered and said, “Every plant which My heavenly Father has not planted will be uprooted.
40_15_14: Let them alone. They are blind guides of the blind. And if the blind leads the blind, both will fall into a pit.”
40_15_15: Then Peter answered and said to Him, “Explain this parable to us.”
40_15_16: So Jesus said, “Are you also still without understanding?
40_15_17: Do you not yet understand that whatever enters the mouth goes into the stomach and is eliminated?
40_15_18: But those things which proceed out of the mouth come from the heart, and they defile a man.
40_15_19: For out of the heart proceed evil thoughts, murders, adulteries, fornications, thefts, false witness, slander.
40_15_20: These are the things which defile a man, but to eat with unwashed hands does not defile a man.”
40_15_21: Then Jesus went out from there and departed to the region of Tyre and Sidon.
40_15_22: And behold, a Canaanite woman from that region came and cried out to Him, saying, 'Have mercy on me, O Lord, Son of David! My daughter is severely tormented by a demon.'
40_15_23: But He did not answer her a word. And His disciples came and urged Him, saying, 'Send her away, for she is crying out after us.'
40_15_24: But He answered and said, 'I was sent only to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.'
40_15_25: Then she came and knelt before Him, saying, 'Lord, help me!'
40_15_26: But He answered and said, 'It is not right to take the children's bread and throw it to the dogs.'
40_15_27: And she said, 'Yes, Lord, yet even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall from their masters' table.'
40_15_28: Then Jesus answered and said to her, 'O woman, great is your faith! Let it be done for you as you desire.' And her daughter was healed from that very hour.
40_15_29: Jesus departed from there, walked beside the Sea of Galilee, and went up on the mountain and sat down there.
40_15_30: Then great multitudes came to Him, bringing with them the lame, blind, mute, crippled, and many others; and they laid them down at Jesus' feet, and He healed them.
40_15_31: So the multitude marveled when they saw the mute speaking, the crippled restored, the lame walking, and the blind seeing; and they glorified the God of Israel.
40_15_32: Now Jesus called His disciples to Himself and said, 'I have compassion on the crowd, because they have now continued with Me three days and have nothing to eat. And I do not want to send them away hungry, lest they faint on the way.'
40_15_33: Then His disciples said to Him, 'Where could we get enough bread in this desolate place to fill such a great multitude?'
40_15_34: Jesus said to them, 'How many loaves do you have?' And they said, 'Seven, and a few small fish.'
40_15_35: So He commanded the multitude to sit down on the ground.
40_15_36: And He took the seven loaves and the fish, gave thanks, broke them, and gave them to His disciples; and the disciples gave to the crowds.
40_15_37: So they all ate and were satisfied, and they took up seven large baskets full of the fragments that were left.
40_15_38: Now those who ate were four thousand men, besides women and children.
40_15_39: And He sent away the multitude, got into the boat, and came to the region of Magadan.
40_16_1: Then the Pharisees and Sadducees came, and testing Him asked that He would show them a sign from heaven.
40_16_2: He answered and said to them, 'When it is evening you say, 'It will be fair weather, for the sky is red;'
40_16_3: and in the morning, 'It will be stormy today, for the sky is red and threatening.' Hypocrites! You know how to discern the face of the sky, but you cannot discern the signs of the times.
40_16_4: A wicked and adulterous generation seeks after a sign, and no sign shall be given to it except the sign of the prophet Jonah.' And He left them and departed.
40_16_5: Now when His disciples had come to the other side, they had forgotten to take bread.
40_16_6: Then Jesus said to them, 'Watch and beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and the Sadducees.'
40_16_7: And they reasoned among themselves, saying, 'It is because we have taken no bread.'
40_16_8: But Jesus, being aware of it, said to them, 'O you of little faith, why do you reason among yourselves because you have brought no bread?'
40_16_9: Do you not yet understand, or remember the five loaves for the five thousand and how many baskets you took up?
40_16_10: Nor the seven loaves of the four thousand and how many large baskets you took up?
40_16_11: How is it you do not understand that I did not speak to you concerning bread—but to beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees.'
40_16_12: Then they understood that He did not tell them to beware of the leaven of bread, but of the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
40_16_13: When Jesus came into the region of Caesarea Philippi, He asked His disciples, saying, 'Who do people say that I, the Son of Man, am?'
40_16_14: So they said, 'Some say John the Baptist, others say Elijah, and others Jeremiah or one of the prophets.'
40_16_15: He said to them, 'But who do you say that I am?'
40_16_16: Simon Peter answered and said, 'You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.'
40_16_17: Jesus answered and said to him, 'Blessed are you, Simon Bar-Jonah, for flesh and blood has not revealed this to you, but My Father who is in heaven.'
40_16_18: And I also say to you that you are Peter, and on this rock I will build My church, and the gates of Hades shall not prevail against it.
40_16_19: And I will give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven, and whatever you bind on earth will be bound in heaven, and whatever you loose on earth will be loosed in heaven.'
40_16_20: Then He commanded His disciples that they should tell no one that He was Jesus the Christ.
40_16_21: From that time Jesus began to show His disciples that He must go to Jerusalem, and suffer many things from the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be killed, and be raised on the third day.
40_16_22: Then Peter took Him aside and began to rebuke Him, saying, 'Far be it from You, Lord; this shall never happen to You!'
40_16_23: But He turned and said to Peter, 'Get behind Me, Satan! You are an offense to Me, for you are not mindful of the things of God, but the things of men.'
40_16_24: Then Jesus said to His disciples, 'If anyone desires to come after Me, let him deny himself, take up his cross, and follow Me.'
40_16_25: For whoever desires to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for My sake will find it.
40_16_26: For what profit is it to a man if he gains the whole world, and loses his own soul? Or what will a man give in exchange for his soul?
40_16_27: For the Son of Man will come in the glory of His Father with His angels, and then He will reward each according to his works.
40_16_28: Assuredly, I say to you, there are some standing here who shall not taste death till they see the Son of Man coming in His kingdom.
40_17_1: Now after six days Jesus took Peter, James, and John his brother, led them up on a high mountain by themselves;
40_17_2: and He was transfigured before them. His face shone like the sun, and His clothes became as white as the light.
40_17_3: And behold, Moses and Elijah appeared to them, talking with Him.
40_17_4: Then Peter answered and said to Jesus, 'Lord, it is good for us to be here; if You wish, let us make here three tabernacles: one for You, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.'
40_17_5: While he was still speaking, behold, a bright cloud overshadowed them; and suddenly a voice came out of the cloud, saying, 'This is My beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased. Hear Him!'
40_17_6: And when the disciples heard it, they fell on their faces and were greatly afraid.
40_17_7: But Jesus came and touched them and said, 'Arise, and do not be afraid.'
40_17_8: When they had lifted up their eyes, they saw no one but Jesus only.
40_17_9: Now as they came down from the mountain, Jesus commanded them, saying, 'Tell the vision to no one until the Son of Man is risen from the dead.'
40_17_10: And His disciples asked Him, saying, 'Why then do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?'
40_17_11: Jesus answered and said to them, 'Indeed, Elijah is coming first and will restore all things.'
40_17_12: But I say to you that Elijah has come already, and they did not know him but did to him whatever they wished. Likewise, the Son of Man is also about to suffer at their hands.'
40_17_13: Then the disciples understood that He spoke to them of John the Baptist.
40_17_14: And when they had come to the multitude, a man came to Him, kneeling down to Him and saying,
40_17_15: Lord, have mercy on my son, for he has seizures and suffers severely; for he often falls into the fire and often into the water.
40_17_16: So I brought him to Your disciples, but they could not heal him.
40_17_17: Then Jesus answered and said, O faithless and perverse generation, how long shall I be with you? How long shall I bear with you? Bring him here to Me.
40_17_18: And Jesus rebuked the demon, and it came out of him; and the boy was healed instantly.
40_17_19: Then the disciples came to Jesus privately and said, Why could we not cast it out?
40_17_20: So Jesus said to them, Because of your little faith; for assuredly, I say to you, if you have faith like a grain of mustard seed, you will say to this mountain, Move from here to there, and it will move; and nothing will be impossible for you.
40_17_21: However, this kind does not go out except by prayer and fasting.
40_17_22: Now while they were staying in Galilee, Jesus said to them, The Son of Man is about to be betrayed into the hands of men,
40_17_23: and they will kill Him, and on the third day He will be raised up. And they were greatly distressed.
40_17_24: When they had come to Capernaum, those who collected the temple tax approached Peter and said, Does your Teacher not pay the temple tax?
40_17_25: He said, Yes. And when he had come into the house, Jesus anticipated him, saying, What do you think, Simon? From whom do the kings of the earth take customs or taxes, from their sons or from strangers?
40_17_26: Peter said to Him, From strangers. Jesus said to him, Then the sons are free.
40_17_27: Nevertheless, lest we offend them, go to the sea, cast in a hook, and take the fish that comes up first. And when you have opened its mouth, you will find a piece of money; take that and give it to them for Me and you.
40_18_1: At that time the disciples came to Jesus, saying, Who then is greatest in the kingdom of heaven?
40_18_2: Then Jesus called a little child to Him, set him in the midst of them,
40_18_3: and said, Assuredly, I say to you, unless you turn and become like children, you will by no means enter the kingdom of heaven.
40_18_4: Therefore whoever humbles himself like this little child is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
40_18_5: Whoever receives one such child in My name receives Me.
40_18_6: Whoever causes one of these little ones who believe in Me to sin, it would be better for him if a millstone were hung around his neck, and he were drowned in the depth of the sea.
40_18_7: Woe to the world because of offenses! For offenses must come, but woe to that man by whom the offense comes!
40_18_8: If your hand or foot causes you to sin, cut it off and cast it from you. It is better for you to enter into life maimed or lame, rather than having two hands or two feet, to be cast into the eternal fire.
40_18_9: And if your eye causes you to sin, pluck it out and cast it from you. It is better for you to enter into life with one eye, rather than having two eyes, to be cast into hell fire.
40_18_10: Take heed that you do not despise one of these little ones, for I say to you that in heaven their angels always see the face of My Father who is in heaven.
40_18_11: For the Son of Man has come to save that which was lost.
40_18_12: What do you think? If a man has a hundred sheep, and one of them goes astray, does he not leave the ninety-nine on the mountains to seek the one that is straying?
40_18_13: And if he finds it, assuredly, I say to you, he rejoices over that sheep more than over the ninety-nine that did not go astray.
40_18_14: Even so it is not the will of your Father who is in heaven that one of these little ones should perish.
40_18_15: Moreover, if your brother sins against you, go and tell him his fault between you and him alone. If he hears you, you have gained your brother.
40_18_16: But if he does not listen, take one or two others along with you, that every charge may be established by the evidence of two or three witnesses.
40_18_17: And if he refuses to hear them, tell it to the church. But if he refuses even to hear the church, let him be to you like a Gentile and a tax collector.
40_18_18: Assuredly, I say to you, whatever you bind on earth will be bound in heaven, and whatever you loose on earth will be loosed in heaven.
40_18_19: Again I say to you, if two of you agree on earth concerning anything that they ask, it will be done for them by My Father in heaven.
40_18_20: For where two or three are gathered together in My name, I am there in the midst of them.
40_18_21: Then Peter came to Him and said, Lord, how often shall my brother sin against me, and I forgive him? Up to seven times?
40_18_22: Jesus said to him, I do not say to you, up to seven times, but up to seventy times seven.
40_18_23: Therefore the kingdom of heaven is like a certain king who wanted to settle accounts with his servants.
40_18_24: And when he had begun to settle accounts, one was brought to him who owed him ten thousand talents.
40_18_25: But as he was not able to pay, his master commanded that he be sold, with his wife and children and all that he had, and that payment be made.
40_18_26: The servant therefore fell down before him, saying, Master, have patience with me, and I will pay you all.
40_18_27: Then the master of that servant was moved with compassion, released him, and forgave him the debt.
40_18_28: But that servant went out and found one of his fellow servants who owed him a hundred denarii; and he laid hands on him and took him by the throat, saying, Pay me what you owe!
40_18_29: So his fellow servant fell down at his feet and begged him, saying, Have patience with me, and I will pay you all.
40_18_30: And he would not, but went and threw him into prison till he should pay the debt.
40_18_31: So when his fellow servants saw what had been done, they were very grieved, and came and told their master all that had been done.
40_18_32: Then his master, after he had called him, said to him, You wicked servant! I forgave you all that debt because you begged me.
40_18_33: Should you not also have had compassion on your fellow servant, just as I had pity on you?
40_18_34: And his master was angry, and delivered him to the torturers until he should pay all that was due to him.
40_18_35: So My heavenly Father also will do to you if each of you does not forgive his brother his trespasses from the heart.
40_19_1: Now it came to pass, when Jesus had finished these sayings, that He departed from Galilee and came to the region of Judea beyond the Jordan.
40_19_2: And great multitudes followed Him, and He healed them there.
40_19_3: The Pharisees also came to Him, testing Him, and saying to Him, Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife for just any reason?
40_19_4: And He answered and said to them, Have you not read that He who made them at the beginning made them male and female,
40_19_5: and said, For this reason a man shall leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife, and the two shall become one flesh?
40_19_6: So then, they are no longer two but one flesh. Therefore what God has joined together, let not man separate.
40_19_7: They said to Him, Why then did Moses command to give a certificate of divorce, and to put her away?
40_19_8: He said to them, Moses, because of the hardness of your hearts, permitted you to divorce your wives, but from the beginning it was not so.
40_19_9: And I say to you, whoever divorces his wife, except for sexual immorality, and marries another, commits adultery; and whoever marries her who is divorced commits adultery.
40_19_10: His disciples said to Him, If such is the case of the man with his wife, it is better not to marry.
40_19_11: But He said to them, All cannot accept this saying, but only those to whom it has been given:
40_19_12: For there are eunuchs who were born thus from their mother's womb, and there are eunuchs who were made eunuchs by men, and there are eunuchs who have made themselves eunuchs for the kingdom of heaven's sake. He who is able to accept it, let him accept it.
40_19_13: Then little children were brought to Him that He might lay His hands on them and pray, but the disciples rebuked them.
40_19_14: But Jesus said, 'Let the little children come to Me, and do not hinder them; for of such is the kingdom of heaven.'
40_19_15: And He laid His hands on them and went away.
40_19_16: Now behold, a man came and said to Him, 'Good Teacher, what good thing shall I do that I may have eternal life?'
40_19_17: So He said to him, 'Why do you ask Me about what is good? There is only One who is good. But if you want to enter into life, keep the commandments.'
40_19_18: He said to Him, 'Which ones?' Jesus said, 'You shall not murder, You shall not commit adultery, You shall not steal, You shall not bear false witness,'
40_19_19: 'Honor your father and your mother, and you shall love your neighbor as yourself.'
40_19_20: The young man said to Him, 'All these things I have kept from my youth. What do I still lack?'
40_19_21: Jesus said to him, 'If you want to be perfect, go, sell what you have and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; and come, follow Me.'
40_19_22: But when the young man heard that saying, he went away sorrowful, for he had great possessions.
40_19_23: Then Jesus said to His disciples, 'Assuredly, I say to you that it is hard for a rich man to enter the kingdom of heaven.'
40_19_24: And again I say to you, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom of God.
40_19_25: When His disciples heard it, they were greatly astonished, saying, 'Who then can be saved?'
40_19_26: But Jesus looked at them and said, 'With men this is impossible, but with God all things are possible.'
40_19_27: Then Peter answered and said to Him, 'See, we have left all and followed You. Therefore what shall we have?'
40_19_28: So Jesus said to them, 'Assuredly I say to you, that in the regeneration, when the Son of Man sits on the throne of His glory, you who have followed Me will also sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.'
40_19_29: And everyone who has left houses or brothers or sisters or father or mother or children or lands for My name's sake shall receive a hundredfold and inherit eternal life.
40_19_30: But many who are first will be last, and the last first.
40_20_1: 'For the kingdom of heaven is like a landowner who went out early in the morning to hire laborers for his vineyard.'
40_20_2: Now when he had agreed with the laborers for a denarius a day, he sent them into his vineyard.
40_20_3: And he went out about the third hour and saw others standing idle in the marketplace,
40_20_4: and said to them, 'You also go into the vineyard, and whatever is right I will give you.' So they went.
40_20_5: Again he went out about the sixth and the ninth hour and did likewise.
40_20_6: And about the eleventh hour he went out and found others standing idle, and said to them, 'Why have you been standing here idle all day?'
40_20_7: They said to him, 'Because no one hired us.' He said to them, 'You also go into the vineyard, and whatever is right you will receive.'
40_20_8: So when evening had come, the owner of the vineyard said to his steward, 'Call the laborers and give them their wages, beginning with the last to the first.'
40_20_9: And when those came who were hired about the eleventh hour, they each received a denarius.
40_20_10: But when the first came, they supposed that they would receive more; and they likewise received each a denarius.
40_20_11: And when they had received it, they complained against the landowner,
40_20_12: saying, 'These last men have worked only one hour, and you have made them equal to us who have borne the burden and the heat of the day.'
40_20_13: But he answered one of them and said, 'Friend, I am doing you no wrong. Did you not agree with me for a denarius?
40_20_14: Take what is yours and go your way. I choose to give to this last man the same as to you.
40_20_15: Is it not lawful for me to do what I wish with my own things? Or do you begrudge my generosity?
40_20_16: So the last will be first, and the first last. For many are called, but few chosen.
40_20_17: Now Jesus, going up to Jerusalem, took the twelve disciples aside on the road and said to them,
40_20_18: Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be delivered over to the chief priests and scribes; and they will condemn Him to death,
40_20_19: and deliver Him to the Gentiles to be mocked and flogged and crucified. And on the third day He will be raised again.
40_20_20: Then the mother of Zebedee's sons came to Him with her sons, kneeling down and asking something from Him.
40_20_21: And He said to her, 'What do you wish?' She said to Him, 'Grant that these two sons of mine may sit, one on Your right hand and the other on the left, in Your kingdom.'
40_20_22: But Jesus answered and said, 'You do not know what you ask. Are you able to drink the cup that I am about to drink, and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?' They said to Him, 'We are able.'
40_20_23: So He said to them, 'You will indeed drink My cup, and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with; but to sit on My right hand and on My left is not Mine to give, but it is for those for whom it is prepared by My Father.'
40_20_24: And when the ten heard it, they were greatly displeased with the two brothers.
40_20_25: But Jesus called them to Himself and said, 'You know that the rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and their great ones exercise authority over them.
40_20_26: Yet it shall not be so among you; but whoever desires to become great among you, let him be your servant.
40_20_27: And whoever desires to be first among you, let him be your slave—
40_20_28: just as the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve, and to give His life a ransom for many.
40_20_29: Now as they went out of Jericho, a great multitude followed Him.
40_20_30: And behold, two blind men sitting by the road, when they heard that Jesus was passing by, cried out, saying, 'Have mercy on us, O Lord, Son of David!'
40_20_31: Then the multitude warned them that they should be quiet; but they cried out all the more, saying, 'Have mercy on us, O Lord, Son of David!'
40_20_32: So Jesus stood still and called them, and said, 'What do you want Me to do for you?'
40_20_33: They said to Him, 'Lord, that our eyes may be opened.'
40_20_34: So Jesus had compassion and touched their eyes. And immediately their eyes received sight, and they followed Him.
40_21_1: Now when they drew near to Jerusalem, and came to Bethphage, at the Mount of Olives, then Jesus sent two disciples,
40_21_2: saying to them, 'Go into the village opposite you, and immediately you will find a donkey tied, and a colt with her. Loose them and bring them to Me.
40_21_3: And if anyone says anything to you, you shall say, 'The Lord has need of them,' and immediately he will send them.
40_21_4: All this was done that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet, saying:
40_21_5: Tell the daughter of Zion, 'Behold, your King is coming to you, humble, and sitting on a donkey, a colt, the foal of a donkey.'
40_21_6: So the disciples went and did as Jesus commanded them.
40_21_7: They brought the donkey and the colt, laid their clothes on them, and set Him on them.
40_21_8: And a very great multitude spread their clothes on the road; others cut down branches from the trees and spread them on the road.
40_21_9: Then the multitudes who went before and those who followed cried out, saying: Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest!
40_21_10: And when He had come into Jerusalem, all the city was stirred, saying, Who is this?
40_21_11: So the multitudes said, This is Jesus, the prophet from Nazareth in Galilee.
40_21_12: Then Jesus went into the temple of God and drove out all those who bought and sold in the temple, and overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who sold doves.
40_21_13: And He said to them, It is written, My house shall be called a house of prayer, but you have made it a den of robbers.
40_21_14: Then the blind and the lame came to Him in the temple, and He healed them.
40_21_15: But when the chief priests and scribes saw the wonderful things that He did, and the children crying out in the temple and saying, Hosanna to the Son of David! they were indignant.
40_21_16: and said to Him, Do You hear what these children are saying? And Jesus said to them, Yes. Have you never read, Out of the mouth of infants and nursing babies You have perfected praise?
40_21_17: Then He left them and went out of the city to Bethany, and He spent the night there.
40_21_18: Now in the morning, as He returned to the city, He was hungry.
40_21_19: And seeing a fig tree by the road, He came to it and found nothing on it but leaves, and said to it, May no fruit ever come from you again. Immediately the fig tree withered away.
40_21_20: And when the disciples saw it, they marveled, saying, How did the fig tree wither so quickly?
40_21_21: So Jesus answered and said to them, Assuredly, I say to you, if you have faith and do not doubt, you will not only do what was done to the fig tree, but also if you say to this mountain, Be taken up and thrown into the sea, it will be done.
40_21_22: And whatever you ask in prayer, believing, you will receive.
40_21_23: Now when He came into the temple, the chief priests and the elders of the people confronted Him as He was teaching, and said, By what authority are You doing these things? And who gave You this authority?
40_21_24: But Jesus answered and said to them, I also will ask you one thing, which if you tell Me, I likewise will tell you by what authority I do these things:
40_21_25: The baptism of John—where was it from? From heaven or from men? And they reasoned among themselves, saying, If we say, From heaven, He will say to us, Why then did you not believe him?
40_21_26: But if we say, From men, we fear the multitude, for all count John as a prophet.
40_21_27: So they answered Jesus and said, We do not know. And He said to them, Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.
40_21_28: But what do you think? A man had two sons, and he came to the first and said, Son, go work today in my vineyard.
40_21_29: He answered and said, I will not, but afterward he regretted it and went.
40_21_30: Then he came to the second and said likewise. And he answered and said, I go, sir, but he did not go.
40_21_31: Which of the two did the will of his father? They said to Him, The first. Jesus said to them, Assuredly, I say to you that tax collectors and prostitutes go into the kingdom of God before you.
40_21_32: For John came to you in the way of righteousness, and you did not believe him; but tax collectors and prostitutes believed him; and when you saw it, you did not afterward change your minds and believe him.
