40_21_33: Hear another parable: There was a certain landowner who planted a vineyard and set a hedge around it, dug a winepress in it and built a tower. And he leased it to vinedressers and went into a far country.
40_21_34: Now when vintage-time drew near, he sent his servants to the vinedressers, that they might receive its fruit.
40_21_35: And the vinedressers took his servants, beat one, killed one, and stoned another.
40_21_36: Again he sent other servants, more than the first, and they did likewise to them.
40_21_37: Then last of all he sent his son to them, saying, They will respect my son.
40_21_38: But when the vinedressers saw the son, they said among themselves, This is the heir. Come, let us kill him and seize his inheritance.
40_21_39: So they took him and threw him out of the vineyard and killed him.
40_21_40: Therefore, when the owner of the vineyard comes, what will he do to those tenants?
40_21_41: They said to Him, He will destroy those wicked men miserably and lease his vineyard to other vinedressers who will render to him the fruits in their seasons.
40_21_42: Jesus said to them, Have you never read in the Scriptures: The stone which the builders rejected has become the chief cornerstone; this was the Lord's doing, and it is marvelous in our eyes?
40_21_43: Therefore I say to you, the kingdom of God will be taken away from you and given to a people producing its fruits.
40_21_44: And whoever falls on this stone will be broken; but on whomever it falls, it will shatter him.
40_21_45: Now when the chief priests and Pharisees heard His parables, they perceived that He was speaking about them.
40_21_46: But when they sought to lay hands on Him, they feared the multitudes, because the people regarded Him as a prophet.
40_22_1: And again Jesus spoke to them in parables, saying:
40_22_2: The kingdom of heaven may be compared to a king who gave a wedding feast for his son,
40_22_3: and sent his servants to call those who were invited to the wedding feast, but they were not willing to come.
40_22_4: Again, he sent out other servants, saying, Tell those who are invited, See, I have prepared my dinner; my oxen and fatted cattle are killed, and all things are ready. Come to the wedding feast.
40_22_5: But they paid no attention and went away, one to his own farm, another to his business.
40_22_6: And the rest seized his servants, mistreated them, and killed them.
40_22_7: But when the king heard about it, he was furious. And he sent out his armies, destroyed those murderers, and burned their city.
40_22_8: Then he said to his servants, The wedding is ready, but those who were invited were not worthy.
40_22_9: Therefore go into the highways, and as many as you find, invite to the wedding.
40_22_10: So those servants went out into the highways and gathered together all whom they found, both bad and good. And the wedding hall was filled with guests.
40_22_11: But when the king came in to see the guests, he saw a man there who was not dressed in wedding clothes.
40_22_12: So he said to him, Friend, how did you come in here without wedding clothes? And he was speechless.
40_22_13: Then the king said to the attendants, Bind him hand and foot, and cast him into outer darkness; there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
40_22_14: For many are called, but few are chosen.
40_22_15: Then the Pharisees went and plotted how to entangle him in his words.
40_22_16: And they sent their disciples to him, along with the Herodians, saying, Teacher, we know that you are true and teach the way of God truthfully; nor do you care about anyone, for you are not swayed by appearances.
40_22_17: Tell us, therefore, what do You think? Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?
40_22_18: But Jesus, aware of their malice, said, Why do you test me, you hypocrites?
40_22_19: Show me the coin for the tax. So they brought him a denarius.
40_22_20: And He said to them, Whose image and inscription is this?
40_22_21: They said to Him, Caesar's. And He said to them, Render therefore to Caesar the things that are Caesar's, and to God the things that are God's.
40_22_22: When they heard this, they were amazed, and left him and went away.
40_22_23: The same day the Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came to Him and asked Him a question,
40_22_24: saying, “Teacher, Moses said, ‘If a man dies having no children, his brother must marry the widow and raise up offspring for his brother.'
40_22_25: Now there were seven brothers among us. The first married and died, and having no offspring, left his wife to his brother.
40_22_26: So too the second and third, down to the seventh.
40_22_27: Last of all, the woman died.
40_22_28: In the resurrection, therefore, whose wife will she be of the seven? For they all had married her.”
40_22_29: Jesus answered and said to them, “You are mistaken, not knowing the Scriptures nor the power of God.
40_22_30: For in the resurrection they neither marry nor are given in marriage but are like angels in heaven.
40_22_31: But concerning the resurrection of the dead, have you not read what was spoken to you by God, saying,
40_22_32: ‘I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’? He is not the God of the dead, but of the living.”
40_22_33: And when the multitudes heard this, they were astonished at His teaching.
40_22_34: But when the Pharisees heard that He had silenced the Sadducees, they gathered together.
40_22_35: Then one of them, an expert in the law, asked Him a question, testing Him,
40_22_36: “Teacher, which is the great commandment in the Law?”
40_22_37: Jesus said to him, “You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, and with all your mind.”
40_22_38: This is the first and greatest commandment.
40_22_39: And a second is like it: ‘You shall love your neighbor as yourself.’
40_22_40: On these two commandments depend all the Law and the Prophets.”
40_22_41: While the Pharisees were gathered together, Jesus asked them a question,
40_22_42: saying, “What do you think about the Christ? Whose Son is He?” They said to Him, “The Son of David.”
40_22_43: He said to them, “How then does David in the Spirit call Him ‘Lord,’ saying:
40_22_44: ‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at My right hand, till I make Your enemies Your footstool”?
40_22_45: If then David calls Him ‘Lord,’ how is He his Son?”
40_22_46: And no one was able to answer Him a word, nor from that day on did anyone dare question Him anymore.
40_23_1: Then Jesus spoke to the multitudes and to His disciples,
40_23_2: saying: “The scribes and the Pharisees sit on Moses' seat,
40_23_3: therefore whatever they tell you to observe, that observe and do, but do not do according to their works; for they say, and do not do.
40_23_4: For they bind heavy burdens, hard to bear, and lay them on men's shoulders; but they themselves will not move them with one of their fingers.
40_23_5: But all their works they do to be seen by men. They make their phylacteries broad and enlarge the borders of their garments.
40_23_6: They love the best places at feasts, the best seats in the synagogues,
40_23_7: They love greetings in the marketplaces, and to be called 'Rabbi' by others.
40_23_8: But you, do not be called 'Rabbi'; for One is your Teacher, the Christ, and you are all brothers.
40_23_9: Do not call anyone on earth your father; for you have one Father, who is in heaven.
40_23_10: And do not be called teachers; for One is your Teacher, the Christ.
40_23_11: But he who is greatest among you shall be your servant.
40_23_12: And whoever exalts himself will be humbled, and whoever humbles himself will be exalted.
40_23_13: But woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you shut the door of the kingdom of heaven in people's faces; for you neither enter yourselves nor allow those who would enter to go in.
40_23_14: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you devour widows' houses, and for a pretense make long prayers; therefore you will receive greater condemnation.
40_23_15: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you travel across sea and land to make a single proselyte, and when he becomes one, you make him twice as much a child of hell as yourselves.
40_23_16: Woe to you, blind guides, who say, 'Whoever swears by the temple, it is nothing; but whoever swears by the gold of the temple, he is obliged to perform it.'
40_23_17: Blind fools! For which is greater, the gold or the temple that sanctifies the gold?
40_23_18: And you say, 'Whoever swears by the altar, it is nothing; but whoever swears by the gift that is on it, he is bound by his oath.'
40_23_19: Blind people! For which is greater, the gift or the altar that sanctifies the gift?
40_23_20: Therefore, the one who takes an oath by the altar takes an oath by it and by everything on it.
40_23_21: And whoever swears by the temple swears by it and by him who dwells in it.
40_23_22: And the one who takes an oath by heaven takes an oath by God's throne and by him who sits on it.
40_23_23: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you tithe mint and dill and cumin, and have neglected the weightier matters of the law: justice, mercy, and faithfulness. These you ought to have done, without neglecting the others.
40_23_24: Blind guides! You strain out a gnat and swallow a camel!
40_23_25: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you cleanse the outside of the cup and dish, but inside they are full of greed and self-indulgence.
40_23_26: You blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of the cup and the dish, that the outside of them may be clean also.
40_23_27: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you are like whitewashed tombs, which appear beautiful outwardly, but inside are full of dead people's bones and all uncleanness.
40_23_28: In the same way, on the outside you seem righteous to people, but inside you are full of hypocrisy and lawlessness.
40_23_29: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! For you build the tombs of the prophets and decorate the monuments of the righteous,
40_23_30: and you say, 'If we had lived in the days of our ancestors, we wouldn’t have taken part with them in shedding the blood of the prophets.'
40_23_31: So you testify against yourselves that you are descendants of those who murdered the prophets.
40_23_32: Fill up, then, the measure of your ancestors' sins!
40_23_33: You serpents, you brood of vipers! How can you escape being sentenced to hell?
40_23_34: Therefore, I send you prophets, wise men, and scribes; some of whom you will kill and crucify, and some you will flog in your synagogues and persecute from town to town,
40_23_35: so that all the righteous blood shed on the earth will be charged to you, from the blood of righteous Abel to the blood of Zechariah, son of Berechiah, whom you murdered between the sanctuary and the altar.
40_23_36: Truly, I say to you, all these things will come upon this generation.
40_23_37: O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, the city that kills the prophets and stones those who are sent to her! How often I wanted to gather your children together, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings, but you were not willing!
40_23_38: See, your house is left to you desolate;
40_23_39: for I say to you, you will not see Me again until you say, 'Blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord!'
40_24_1: Then Jesus went out and departed from the temple, and His disciples came up to point out to Him the buildings of the temple.
40_24_2: And Jesus said to them, 'Do you not see all these things? Assuredly, I say to you, not one stone will be left here upon another that shall not be thrown down.'
40_24_3: Now as He sat on the Mount of Olives, the disciples came to Him privately, saying, 'Tell us, when will these things be? And what will be the sign of Your coming and of the end of the age?'
40_24_4: And Jesus answered and said to them, 'Take heed that no one deceives you.'
40_24_5: For many will come in My name, saying, 'I am the Christ,' and they will lead many astray.
40_24_6: And you will hear of wars and rumors of wars. See that you are not alarmed, for all these things must come to pass, but the end is not yet.
40_24_7: For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom, and there will be famines and earthquakes in various places.
40_24_8: All these are but the beginning of labor pains.
40_24_9: Then they will deliver you up to tribulation and kill you, and you will be hated by all nations for My name's sake.
40_24_10: And then many will fall away, betray one another, and hate one another.
40_24_11: Then many false prophets will rise up and deceive many.
40_24_12: And because lawlessness will be increased, the love of many will grow cold.
40_24_13: But he who endures to the end shall be saved.
40_24_14: And this gospel of the kingdom will be proclaimed throughout the whole world as a testimony to all nations, and then the end will come.
40_24_15: Therefore when you see the 'abomination of desolation,' spoken of by Daniel the prophet, standing in the holy place (let the reader understand),
40_24_16: then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains.
40_24_17: Let him who is on the housetop not go down to take anything out of his house.
40_24_18: And let him who is in the field not turn back to get his cloak.
40_24_19: But woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing babies in those days!
40_24_20: And pray that your flight may not be in winter or on the Sabbath.
40_24_21: For then there will be great tribulation, such as has not been from the beginning of the world until now, no, and never will be.
40_24_22: And unless those days were shortened, no flesh would be saved; but for the sake of the elect those days will be shortened.
40_24_23: Then if anyone says to you, 'Look, here is the Christ!' or 'There!' do not believe it.
40_24_24: For false christs and false prophets will arise and perform great signs and wonders to lead astray, if possible, even the elect.
40_24_25: See, I have told you beforehand.
40_24_26: Therefore if they say to you, 'Look, He is in the desert!' do not go out; or 'Look, He is in the inner rooms!' do not believe it.
40_24_27: For as the lightning comes from the east and flashes as far as the west, so will be the coming of the Son of Man.
40_24_28: For wherever the carcass is, there the vultures will be gathered together.
40_24_29: Immediately after the tribulation of those days the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light; the stars will fall from heaven, and the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
40_24_30: Then the sign of the Son of Man will appear in heaven, and then all the tribes of the earth will mourn, and they will see the Son of Man coming on the clouds of heaven with power and great glory.
40_24_31: And He will send His angels with a loud trumpet call, and they will gather together His elect from the four winds, from one end of heaven to the other.
40_24_32: Now learn this lesson from the fig tree: When its branch has already become tender and puts forth leaves, you know that summer is near.
40_24_33: So you also, when you see all these things, know that He is near—at the doors!
40_24_34: Assuredly, I say to you, this generation will by no means pass away till all these things take place.
40_24_35: Heaven and earth will pass away, but My words will by no means pass away.
40_24_36: But of that day and hour no one knows, not even the angels of heaven, nor the Son, but My Father only.
40_24_37: But as the days of Noah were, so also will the coming of the Son of Man be.
40_24_38: For as in the days before the flood, they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day that Noah entered the ark,
40_24_39: and did not know until the flood came and took them all away, so also will the coming of the Son of Man be.
40_24_40: Then two men will be in the field: one will be taken and the other left.
40_24_41: Two women will be grinding at the mill: one will be taken and the other left.
40_24_42: Watch therefore, for you do not know what hour your Lord is coming.
40_24_43: But know this, that if the master of the house had known what hour the thief would come, he would have watched and not allowed his house to be broken into.
40_24_44: Therefore you also be ready, for the Son of Man is coming at an hour you do not expect.
40_24_45: Who then is a faithful and wise servant, whom his master made ruler over his household, to give them food at the proper time?
40_24_46: Blessed is that servant whom his master, when he comes, will find so doing.
40_24_47: Assuredly, I say to you that he will make him ruler over all his possessions.
40_24_48: But if that wicked servant says in his heart, 'My master is delayed,'
40_24_49: and begins to beat his fellow servants, and to eat and drink with the drunkards,
40_24_50: the master of that servant will come on a day when he is not looking for him and at an hour that he is not aware of,
40_24_51: and will cut him in pieces and assign him a place with the hypocrites. There shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
40_25_1: Then the kingdom of heaven shall be likened to ten virgins who took their lamps and went out to meet the bridegroom.
40_25_2: Now five of them were wise, and five were foolish.
40_25_3: Those who were foolish took their lamps and took no oil with them,
40_25_4: but the wise took oil in their flasks with their lamps.
40_25_5: But while the bridegroom was delayed, they all slumbered and slept.
40_25_6: And at midnight a cry was heard: 'Behold, the bridegroom is coming; go out to meet him!'
40_25_7: Then all those virgins arose and trimmed their lamps.
40_25_8: And the foolish said to the wise, 'Give us some of your oil, for our lamps are going out.'
40_25_9: But the wise answered, saying, 'No, lest there should not be enough for us and you; go rather to those who sell, and buy for yourselves.'
40_25_10: And while they went to buy, the bridegroom came, and those who were ready went in with him to the wedding feast, and the door was shut.
40_25_11: Afterward the other virgins came also, saying, 'Lord, Lord, open to us!'
40_25_12: But he answered and said, 'Truly, I say to you, I do not know you.'
40_25_13: Watch therefore, for you know neither the day nor the hour in which the Son of Man is coming.
40_25_14: For the kingdom of heaven is like a man traveling to a far country, who called his own servants and delivered his goods to them.
40_25_15: And to one he gave five talents, to another two, and to another one, to each according to his own ability; and immediately he went on a journey.
40_25_16: Then he who had received the five talents went and traded with them, and made five talents more.
40_25_17: And likewise he who had received two gained two more also.
40_25_18: But he who had received one went and dug in the ground, and hid his master's money.
40_25_19: After a long time the master of those servants came and settled accounts with them.
40_25_20: So he who had received five talents came and brought five other talents, saying, 'Master, you delivered to me five talents; look, I have gained five more talents besides them.'
40_25_21: His lord said to him, 'Well done, good and faithful servant; you were faithful over a few things, I will make you ruler over many things. Enter into the joy of your lord.'
40_25_22: He also who had received two talents came and said, 'Lord, you delivered to me two talents; look, I have gained two more talents besides them.'
40_25_23: His lord said to him, 'Well done, good and faithful servant; you have been faithful over a few things, I will make you ruler over many things. Enter into the joy of your lord.'
40_25_24: Then he who had received the one talent came and said, 'Lord, I knew you to be a hard man, reaping where you have not sown, and gathering where you have not scattered seed.'
40_25_25: And I was afraid, and went and hid your talent in the ground. Look, there you have what is yours.'
40_25_26: But his lord answered and said to him, 'You wicked and lazy servant! You knew that I reap where I have not sown, and gather where I have not scattered seed.
40_25_27: So you ought to have deposited my money with the bankers, and at my coming I would have received back my own with interest.
40_25_28: Therefore take the talent from him, and give it to him who has ten talents.
40_25_29: For to everyone who has, more will be given, and he will have abundance; but from him who does not have, even what he has will be taken away.
40_25_30: And cast the unprofitable servant into the outer darkness. There will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
40_25_31: When the Son of Man comes in His glory, and all the holy angels with Him, then He will sit on the throne of His glory.
40_25_32: All the nations will be gathered before Him, and He will separate them one from another, as a shepherd divides his sheep from the goats.
40_25_33: And He will set the sheep on His right hand, but the goats on the left.
40_25_34: Then the King will say to those on His right hand, 'Come, you blessed of My Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world:
40_25_35: for I was hungry and you gave Me food; I was thirsty and you gave Me drink; I was a stranger and you took Me in;
40_25_36: I was naked and you clothed Me; I was sick and you visited Me; I was in prison and you came to Me.'
40_25_37: Then the righteous will answer Him, saying, 'Lord, when did we see You hungry and feed You, or thirsty and give You something to drink?
40_25_38: When did we see You a stranger and take You in, or naked and clothe You?
40_25_39: Or when did we see You sick, or in prison, and come to You?'
40_25_40: And the King will answer and say to them, 'Assuredly, I say to you, inasmuch as you did it to one of the least of these My brothers, you did it to Me.'
40_25_41: Then He will also say to those on the left hand, 'Depart from Me, you cursed, into the eternal fire prepared for the devil and his angels:
40_25_42: for I was hungry and you gave Me no food; I was thirsty and you gave Me no drink;
40_25_43: I was a stranger and you did not take Me in, naked and you did not clothe Me, sick and in prison and you did not visit Me.'
40_25_44: Then they also will answer Him, saying, 'Lord, when did we see You hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in prison, and did not minister to You?'
40_25_45: Then He will answer them, saying, 'Assuredly, I say to you, inasmuch as you did not do it to one of the least of these, you did not do it to Me.'
40_25_46: And these will go away into eternal punishment, but the righteous into eternal life.'
40_26_1: Now it came to pass, when Jesus had finished all these sayings, that He said to His disciples,
40_26_2: 'You know that after two days is the Passover, and the Son of Man will be delivered up to be crucified.'
40_26_3: Then the chief priests and the elders of the people assembled at the palace of the high priest, who was called Caiaphas,
40_26_4: and plotted together to arrest Jesus by stealth and kill Him.
40_26_5: But they said, 'Not during the feast, lest there be an uproar among the people.'
40_26_6: And when Jesus was in Bethany at the house of Simon the leper,
40_26_7: a woman came to Him having an alabaster flask of very costly fragrant oil, and she poured it on His head as He sat at the table.
40_26_8: But when His disciples saw it, they were indignant, saying, 'Why this waste?
40_26_9: For this fragrant oil might have been sold for much and given to the poor.'
40_26_10: But when Jesus was aware of it, He said to them, 'Why do you trouble the woman? For she has done a good work for Me.
40_26_11: For you have the poor with you always, but Me you do not have always.
40_26_12: For in pouring this fragrant oil on My body, she did it to prepare Me for burial.
40_26_13: Assuredly, I say to you, wherever this gospel is preached in the whole world, what this woman has done will also be told as a memorial to her.'
40_26_14: Then one of the twelve, called Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests
40_26_15: and said, 'What are you willing to give me if I deliver Him to you?' And they counted out to him thirty pieces of silver.
40_26_16: So from that time he sought opportunity to betray Him.
40_26_17: Now on the first day of the Feast of Unleavened Bread the disciples came to Jesus, saying to Him, 'Where do You want us to prepare for You to eat the Passover?'
40_26_18: And He said, 'Go into the city to a certain man, and say to him, 'The Teacher says, 'My time is at hand; I will keep the Passover at your house with My disciples.'
40_26_19: So the disciples did as Jesus had directed them; and they prepared the Passover.
40_26_20: When evening had come, He sat down with the twelve.
40_26_21: Now as they were eating, He said, “Truly, I say to you, one of you will betray Me.”
40_26_22: And they were very sorrowful, and each of them began to say to Him, “Lord, is it I?”
40_26_23: He answered and said, “He who has dipped his hand with Me in the dish will betray Me.
40_26_24: The Son of Man indeed goes just as it is written of Him, but woe to that man by whom the Son of Man is betrayed! It would have been better for that man if he had not been born.”
40_26_25: Then Judas, who was betraying Him, answered and said, “Rabbi, is it I?” He said to him, “You have said so.”
40_26_26: And as they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed and broke it, and gave it to the disciples and said, “Take, eat; this is My body.”
40_26_27: Then He took the cup, and after giving thanks, He gave it to them, saying, “Drink from it, all of you.
40_26_28: For this is My blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many for the forgiveness of sins.
40_26_29: But I say to you, I will not drink of this fruit of the vine from now on until that day when I drink it new with you in My Father’s kingdom.”
40_26_30: And when they had sung a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
40_26_31: Then Jesus said to them, “All of you will fall away because of Me this night, for it is written: ‘I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will be scattered.’
40_26_32: But after I have been raised, I will go before you to Galilee.”
40_26_33: Peter answered and said to Him, “Even if all are made to stumble because of You, I will never fall away.”
40_26_34: Jesus said to him, “Truly, I say to you that this night, before the rooster crows, you will deny Me three times.”
40_26_35: Peter said to Him, “Even if I have to die with You, I will not deny You!” And so said all the disciples.
40_26_36: Then Jesus came with them to a place called Gethsemane, and said to the disciples, “Sit here while I go and pray over there.”
40_26_37: And He took with Him Peter and the two sons of Zebedee, and He began to be sorrowful and troubled.
40_26_38: Then He said to them, “My soul is very sorrowful, even to death. Stay here and watch with Me.”
40_26_39: He went a little farther and fell on His face, and prayed, saying, “O My Father, if it is possible, let this cup pass from Me; nevertheless, not as I will, but as You will.”
40_26_40: Then He came to the disciples and found them sleeping, and said to Peter, “What! Could you not watch with Me one hour?
40_26_41: Watch and pray, lest you enter into temptation. The spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.”
40_26_42: Again, a second time, He went away and prayed, saying, “O My Father, if this cup cannot pass away from Me unless I drink it, Your will be done.”
40_26_43: And He came and found them asleep again, for their eyes were heavy.
40_26_44: So He left them, went away again, and prayed the third time, saying the same words.
40_26_45: Then He came to His disciples and said to them, “Are you still sleeping and resting? Behold, the hour is at hand, and the Son of Man is being betrayed into the hands of sinners.
40_26_46: Rise, let us be going. See, My betrayer is at hand.”
40_26_47: And while He was still speaking, behold, Judas, one of the twelve, with a great multitude with swords and clubs, came from the chief priests and elders of the people.
40_26_48: Now His betrayer had given them a sign, saying, “Whomever I kiss, He is the One; seize Him.”
40_26_49: Immediately he went up to Jesus and said, “Greetings, Rabbi!” and kissed Him.
40_26_50: But Jesus said to him, “Friend, why have you come?” Then they came and laid hands on Jesus and took Him.
40_26_51: And suddenly, one of those who were with Jesus stretched out his hand and drew his sword, struck the servant of the high priest, and cut off his ear.
40_26_52: But Jesus said to him, “Put your sword back into its place, for all who take the sword will perish by the sword.
40_26_53: Or do you think that I cannot now pray to My Father, and He will provide Me here and now with more than twelve legions of angels?
40_26_54: How then could the Scriptures be fulfilled that it must happen this way?
40_26_55: In that hour Jesus said to the multitudes, “Have you come out, as against a robber, with swords and clubs to capture Me? I sat daily with you, teaching in the temple, and you did not seize Me.
40_26_56: But all this has taken place that the Scriptures of the prophets might be fulfilled.” Then all the disciples forsook Him and fled.
40_26_57: And those who had laid hold of Jesus led Him away to Caiaphas the high priest, where the scribes and the elders were assembled.
40_26_58: But Peter followed Him at a distance to the high priest’s courtyard. And he went in and sat with the guards to see the outcome.
40_26_59: Now the chief priests, the elders, and all the council sought false testimony against Jesus to put Him to death,
40_26_60: but found none. Even though many false witnesses came forward, they found none. But at last two false witnesses came forward
40_26_61: and said, “This man said, ‘I am able to destroy the temple of God and to rebuild it in three days.’”
40_26_62: And the high priest arose and said to Him, “Do You answer nothing? What is it these men testify against You?”
40_26_63: But Jesus kept silent. And the high priest answered and said to Him, “I put You under oath by the living God: Tell us if You are the Christ, the Son of God!”
40_26_64: Jesus said to him, “It is as you said. Nevertheless, I say to you, hereafter you will see the Son of Man seated at the right hand of the Power, and coming on the clouds of heaven.”
40_26_65: Then the high priest tore his clothes, saying, “He has spoken blasphemy! What further need do we have of witnesses? Look, now you have heard His blasphemy!
40_26_66: What do you think?” They answered and said, “He is deserving of death.”
40_26_67: Then they spat in His face and beat Him; and others struck Him with the palms of their hands,
40_26_68: saying, “Prophesy to us, Christ! Who is the one who struck You?”
40_26_69: Now Peter sat outside in the courtyard. And a servant girl came to him, saying, “You also were with Jesus of Galilee.”
40_26_70: But he denied it before them all, saying, “I do not know what you are saying.”
40_26_71: And when he had gone out to the gateway, another girl saw him and said to those who were there, “This fellow also was with Jesus of Nazareth.”
40_26_72: But again he denied with an oath, “I do not know the Man!”
40_26_73: And a little later those who stood by came up and said to Peter, “Surely you also are one of them, for your speech betrays you.”
40_26_74: Then he began to curse and swear, saying, “I do not know the Man!” Immediately a rooster crowed.
40_26_75: And Peter remembered the word of Jesus who had said to him, “Before the rooster crows, you will deny Me three times.” So he went out and wept bitterly.
40_27_1: When morning came, all the chief priests and elders of the people plotted against Jesus to put Him to death.
40_27_2: And when they had bound Him, they led Him away and delivered Him to Pontius Pilate the governor.
40_27_3: Then Judas, His betrayer, seeing that He had been condemned, was remorseful and brought back the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders,
40_27_4: saying, “I have sinned by betraying innocent blood.” And they said, “What is that to us? You see to it!”
40_27_5: Then he threw down the pieces of silver in the temple and departed, and went and hanged himself.
40_27_6: But the chief priests took the silver pieces and said, "It is not lawful to put them into the treasury, because they are the price of blood."
40_27_7: And they consulted together and bought with them the potter's field as a burial place for strangers.
40_27_8: Therefore that field has been called the Field of Blood to this day.
40_27_9: Then was fulfilled what was spoken by Jeremiah the prophet, saying, "And they took the thirty pieces of silver, the price of him on whom a price had been set by some of the sons of Israel,"
40_27_10: and gave them for the potter's field, as the Lord directed me.
40_27_11: Now Jesus stood before the governor, and the governor asked Him, saying, "Are You the King of the Jews?" Jesus said to him, "It is as you say."
40_27_12: And while He was being accused by the chief priests and elders, He answered nothing.
40_27_13: Then Pilate said to Him, "Do You not hear how many things they testify against You?"
40_27_14: But He answered him not one word, so that the governor marveled greatly.
40_27_15: Now at the feast the governor was accustomed to releasing to the multitude one prisoner whom they wished.
40_27_16: And at that time they had a notorious prisoner called Barabbas.
40_27_17: Therefore, when they had gathered together, Pilate said to them, "Whom do you want me to release to you? Barabbas, or Jesus who is called Christ?"
40_27_18: For he knew that it was out of envy that they had delivered him up.
40_27_19: While he was sitting on the judgment seat, his wife sent to him, saying, "Have nothing to do with that righteous man, for I have suffered many things today in a dream because of Him."
40_27_20: But the chief priests and elders persuaded the multitudes that they should ask for Barabbas and destroy Jesus.
40_27_21: The governor answered and said to them, "Which of the two do you want me to release to you?" They said, "Barabbas!"
40_27_22: Pilate said to them, "What then shall I do with Jesus who is called Christ?" They all said to him, "Let Him be crucified!"
40_27_23: Then the governor said, "Why, what evil has He done?" But they cried out all the more, saying, "Let Him be crucified!"
40_27_24: When Pilate saw that he could not prevail at all, but rather that a tumult was rising, he took water and washed his hands before the multitude, saying, "I am innocent of the blood of this just Person. You see to it."
40_27_25: And all the people answered and said, "His blood be on us and on our children!"
40_27_26: Then he released Barabbas to them; and when he had scourged Jesus, he delivered Him to be crucified.
40_27_27: Then the soldiers of the governor took Jesus into the governor's residence and gathered the whole battalion around Him.
40_27_28: And they stripped Him and put a scarlet robe on Him.
40_27_29: When they had twisted a crown of thorns, they put it on His head, and a reed in His right hand. And they bowed the knee before Him and mocked Him, saying, "Hail, King of the Jews!"
40_27_30: Then they spat on Him, and took the reed and struck Him on the head.
40_27_31: And when they had mocked Him, they took the robe off Him, put His own clothes on Him, and led Him away to be crucified.
40_27_32: Now as they came out, they found a man of Cyrene, Simon by name. Him they compelled to bear His cross.
40_27_33: And when they had come to a place called Golgotha, that is to say, Place of a Skull,
40_27_34: they gave Him sour wine mingled with gall to drink. But when He had tasted it, He would not drink.
40_27_35: Then they crucified Him, and divided His garments, casting lots, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet: "They divided My garments among them, And for My clothing they cast lots."
40_27_36: Sitting down, they kept watch over Him there.
40_27_37: And they put up over His head the accusation written against Him: THIS IS JESUS, THE KING OF THE JEWS.
40_27_38: Then two robbers were crucified with Him, one on the right and one on the left.
40_27_39: And those who passed by derided Him, wagging their heads.
40_27_40: and saying, 'You who would destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days, save Yourself! If You are the Son of God, come down from the cross.'
40_27_41: Likewise the chief priests also, mocking with the scribes and elders, said,
40_27_42: 'He saved others; He cannot save Himself. If He is the King of Israel, let Him now come down from the cross, and we will believe in Him.'
40_27_43: He trusts in God; let God deliver Him now if He desires Him; for He said, 'I am the Son of God.'
40_27_44: Even the robbers who were crucified with Him reviled Him in the same way.
40_27_45: Now from the sixth hour until the ninth hour there was darkness over all the land.
40_27_46: And about the ninth hour Jesus cried out with a loud voice, saying, 'Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?' that is, 'My God, My God, why have You forsaken Me?'
40_27_47: Some of those who stood there, when they heard that, said, 'This Man is calling for Elijah!'
40_27_48: Immediately one of them ran and took a sponge, filled it with sour wine and put it on a reed, and offered it to Him to drink.
40_27_49: The rest said, 'Let Him alone; let us see if Elijah will come to save Him.'
40_27_50: And Jesus cried out again with a loud voice and yielded up His spirit.
40_27_51: Then, behold, the veil of the temple was torn in two from top to bottom; and the earth quaked, and the rocks were split,
40_27_52: and the tombs were opened; and many bodies of the saints who had fallen asleep were raised;
40_27_53: and coming out of the tombs after His resurrection, they went into the holy city and appeared to many.
40_27_54: So when the centurion and those with him, who were guarding Jesus, saw the earthquake and the things that had happened, they feared greatly, saying, 'Truly this was the Son of God!'
40_27_55: And many women who followed Jesus from Galilee, ministering to Him, were there looking on from afar,
40_27_56: among whom were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James and Joseph, and the mother of the sons of Zebedee.
40_27_57: Now when evening had come, there came a rich man from Arimathea, named Joseph, who himself had also become a disciple of Jesus.
40_27_58: This man went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus. Then Pilate commanded the body to be given to him.
40_27_59: When Joseph had taken the body, he wrapped it in a clean linen cloth,
40_27_60: and laid it in his new tomb which he had hewn out of the rock; and he rolled a large stone against the door of the tomb and departed.
40_27_61: And Mary Magdalene was there, and the other Mary, sitting opposite the tomb.
40_27_62: On the next day, which followed the Day of Preparation, the chief priests and Pharisees gathered together to Pilate,
40_27_63: saying, 'Sir, we remember, while He was still alive, how that deceiver said, 'After three days I will rise.'
40_27_64: Therefore command that the tomb be made secure until the third day, lest His disciples come by night and steal Him away, and say to the people, 'He has risen from the dead.' So the last deception will be worse than the first.'
40_27_65: Pilate said to them, 'You have a guard; go your way, make it as secure as you know how.'
40_27_66: So they went and made the tomb secure by sealing the stone and setting a guard.
40_28_1: Now after the Sabbath, as the first day of the week began to dawn, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to see the tomb.
40_28_2: And behold, there was a great earthquake, for an angel of the Lord descended from heaven, rolled back the stone, and sat on it.
40_28_3: His appearance was like lightning, and his clothing was as white as snow.
40_28_4: And the guards shook for fear of him and became like dead men.
40_28_5: But the angel said to the women, 'Do not be afraid, for I know that you seek Jesus who was crucified.
40_28_6: He is not here, for he has risen, as he said. Come, see the place where he lay.
40_28_7: And go quickly and tell His disciples that He has risen from the dead, and indeed He is going before you into Galilee; there you will see Him. Behold, I have told you.
40_28_8: So they departed quickly from the tomb with fear and great joy, and ran to tell his disciples.
40_28_9: And as they went to tell His disciples, behold, Jesus met them, saying, 'Greetings!' So they came and held Him by the feet and worshiped Him.
40_28_10: Then Jesus said to them, 'Do not be afraid; go and tell My brethren to go to Galilee, and there they will see Me.'
40_28_11: Now while they were going, behold, some of the guard came into the city and reported to the chief priests all the things that had happened.
40_28_12: When they had assembled with the elders and consulted together, they gave a large sum of money to the soldiers,
40_28_13: saying, 'Tell them, 'His disciples came at night and stole Him away while we slept.'
40_28_14: And if this comes to the governor's ears, we will appease him and keep you out of trouble.'
40_28_15: So they took the money and did as they were instructed; and this story has been spread among the Jews to this day.
40_28_16: Then the eleven disciples went away into Galilee, to the mountain which Jesus had appointed for them.
40_28_17: When they saw Him, they worshiped Him; but some doubted.
40_28_18: And Jesus came and spoke to them, saying, 'All authority has been given to Me in heaven and on earth.
40_28_19: Go therefore and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit,
40_28_20: teaching them to observe all that I have commanded you; and behold, I am with you always, even to the end of the age.' Amen.
41_1_1: The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.
41_1_2: As it is written in Isaiah the prophet: "Behold, I send My messenger before Your face, Who will prepare Your way before You."
41_1_3: "The voice of one crying in the wilderness: 'Prepare the way of the LORD; Make His paths straight.'"
41_1_4: John came baptizing in the wilderness and proclaiming a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.
41_1_5: Then all the land of Judea, and those from Jerusalem, went out to him and were all baptized by him in the Jordan River, confessing their sins.
41_1_6: Now John was clothed with camel's hair and with a leather belt around his waist, and he ate locusts and wild honey.
41_1_7: And he preached, saying, "There comes One after me who is mightier than I, whose sandal strap I am not worthy to stoop down and loose."
41_1_8: I indeed baptized you with water, but He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit."
41_1_9: It came to pass in those days that Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
41_1_10: And immediately, coming up from the water, He saw the heavens being torn open and the Spirit descending upon Him like a dove.
41_1_11: Then a voice came from heaven, "You are My beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased."
41_1_12: Immediately the Spirit drove Him into the wilderness.
41_1_13: And He was there in the wilderness forty days, tempted by Satan, and was with the wild beasts; and the angels ministered to Him.
41_1_14: Now after John was arrested, Jesus came to Galilee, preaching the gospel of God,
41_1_15: and saying, "The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God is at hand. Repent, and believe in the gospel."
41_1_16: And as He walked by the Sea of Galilee, He saw Simon and Andrew his brother casting a net into the sea; for they were fishermen.
41_1_17: Then Jesus said to them, "Follow Me, and I will make you become fishers of men."
41_1_18: They immediately left their nets and followed Him.
41_1_19: When He had gone a little farther from there, He saw James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, who also were in the boat mending their nets.
41_1_20: And immediately He called them, and they left their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired servants, and went after Him.
41_1_21: Then they went into Capernaum, and immediately on the Sabbath He entered the synagogue and taught.
41_1_22: And they were astonished at His teaching, for He taught them as one having authority, and not as the scribes.
41_1_23: Now there was a man in their synagogue with an unclean spirit. And he cried out,
41_1_24: saying, "Let us alone! What have we to do with You, Jesus of Nazareth? Did You come to destroy us? I know who You are—the Holy One of God!"
41_1_25: But Jesus rebuked him, saying, "Be silent, and come out of him!"
41_1_26: And when the unclean spirit had convulsed him and cried out with a loud voice, he came out of him.
41_1_27: Then they were all amazed, so that they questioned among themselves, saying, "What is this? What new doctrine is this? For with authority He commands even the unclean spirits, and they obey Him."
41_1_28: And immediately His fame spread throughout all the region around Galilee.
41_1_29: Now as soon as they had come out of the synagogue, they entered the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
41_1_30: But Simon's wife's mother lay sick with a fever, and they told Him about her at once.
41_1_31: So He came and took her by the hand and lifted her up, and immediately the fever left her. And she served them.
41_1_32: At evening, when the sun had set, they brought to Him all who were sick and those who were demon-possessed.
41_1_33: And the whole city was gathered together at the door.
41_1_34: Then He healed many who were sick with various diseases, and cast out many demons; and He did not allow the demons to speak, because they knew Him.
41_1_35: Now in the morning, having risen a long while before daylight, He went out and departed to a solitary place; and there He prayed.
41_1_36: And Simon and those who were with Him searched for Him.
41_1_37: When they found Him, they said to Him, "Everyone is looking for You."
41_1_38: But He said to them, "Let us go into the next towns, that I may preach there also, because for this purpose I have come forth."
41_1_39: And He was preaching in their synagogues throughout all Galilee, and casting out demons.
41_1_40: Now a leper came to Him, imploring Him, kneeling down to Him and saying to Him, "If You are willing, You can make me clean."
41_1_41: Then Jesus, moved with compassion, stretched out His hand and touched him, and said to him, "I am willing; be cleansed."
41_1_42: Immediately the leprosy left him, and he was cleansed.
41_1_43: And He sternly warned him and sent him away at once,
41_1_44: and said to him, "See that you say nothing to anyone; but go, show yourself to the priest, and offer for your cleansing what Moses commanded, as a testimony to them."
41_1_45: However, he went out and began to proclaim it freely and spread the news, so that Jesus could no longer openly enter a city, but was outside in deserted places; and they came to Him from every direction.
41_2_1: And when He entered Capernaum again after some days, it was heard that He was in the house.
41_2_2: Immediately many gathered together, so that there was no more room, not even near the door. And He was preaching the word to them.
41_2_3: Then they came to Him, bringing a paralytic who was carried by four men.
41_2_4: And when they could not come near Him because of the crowd, they removed the roof above Him; and when they had broken through, they let down the mat on which the paralytic was lying.
41_2_5: When Jesus saw their faith, He said to the paralytic, "Son, your sins are forgiven."
41_2_6: And some of the scribes were sitting there, questioning in their hearts,
41_2_7: "Why does this Man speak like this? He is blaspheming! Who can forgive sins but God alone?"
41_2_8: But immediately, when Jesus perceived in His spirit that they reasoned thus within themselves, He said to them, "Why do you reason about these things in your hearts?"
41_2_9: Which is easier, to say to the paralytic, 'Your sins are forgiven you,' or to say, 'Arise, take up your bed and walk'?"
41_2_10: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins"—He said to the paralytic,
41_2_11: "I say to you, arise, take up your bed, and go to your house."
41_2_12: Immediately he arose, took up the bed, and went out in the presence of them all, so that all were amazed and glorified God, saying, "We never saw anything like this!"
41_2_13: Then He went out again by the sea; and all the crowd was coming to Him, and He was teaching them.
41_2_14: As He passed by, He saw Levi the son of Alphaeus sitting at the tax booth, and He said to him, "Follow Me." So he arose and followed Him.
41_2_15: Now it happened, as He was reclining at table in Levi's house, that many tax collectors and sinners were reclining with Jesus and His disciples; for there were many, and they followed Him.
41_2_16: And when the scribes of the Pharisees saw Him eating with the sinners and tax collectors, they said to His disciples, "Why does He eat with tax collectors and sinners?"
41_2_17: When Jesus heard it, He said to them, "Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick. I did not come to call the righteous, but sinners."
41_2_18: Now John's disciples and the Pharisees were fasting. Then they came and said to Him, "Why do John's disciples and the Pharisees fast, but Your disciples do not fast?"
41_2_19: And Jesus said to them, "Can the friends of the bridegroom fast while the bridegroom is with them? As long as they have the bridegroom with them they cannot fast."
41_2_20: But the days will come when the bridegroom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast in those days."
41_2_21: No one sews a piece of unshrunk cloth on an old garment; or else the new piece pulls away from the old, and a worse tear is made."
41_2_22: And no one puts new wine into old wineskins; or else the new wine will burst the skins, and the wine is spilled and the skins are ruined. But new wine must be put into fresh wineskins."
41_2_23: Now it happened that He went through the grainfields on the Sabbath; and as they went His disciples began to pluck the heads of grain.
41_2_24: And the Pharisees said to Him, "Look, why are they doing what is not lawful on the Sabbath?"
41_2_25: But He said to them, "Have you never read what David did when he was in need and hungry, he and those with him:"
41_2_26: how he went into the house of God in the days of Abiathar the high priest, and ate the bread of the Presence, which is not lawful for anyone to eat except the priests, and also gave some to those who were with him?"
41_2_27: And He said to them, "The Sabbath was made for man, and not man for the Sabbath."
41_2_28: Therefore the Son of Man is also Lord of the Sabbath."
41_3_1: And He entered the synagogue again, and a man was there who had a withered hand.
41_3_2: So they watched Him closely to see whether He would heal him on the Sabbath, so that they might accuse Him.
41_3_3: And He said to the man who had the withered hand, "Step forward."
41_3_4: Then He said to them, "Is it lawful on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to kill?" But they kept silent.
41_3_5: And when He had looked around at them with anger, being grieved by the hardness of their hearts, He said to the man, "Stretch out your hand." And he stretched it out, and his hand was restored whole as the other.
41_3_6: Then the Pharisees went out and immediately plotted with the Herodians against Him, how they might destroy Him.
41_3_7: But Jesus withdrew with His disciples to the sea. And a great multitude from Galilee followed Him, and from Judea
41_3_8: and Jerusalem and Idumea and beyond the Jordan; and those from around Tyre and Sidon, a great multitude, when they heard how many things He was doing, came to Him.
41_3_9: So He told His disciples that a small boat should be kept ready for Him because of the multitude, lest they should crush Him.
41_3_10: For He had healed many, so that all who had afflictions pressed about Him to touch Him.
41_3_11: And the unclean spirits, whenever they saw Him, fell down before Him and cried out, saying, 'You are the Son of God.'
41_3_12: But He sternly warned them that they should not make Him known.
41_3_13: And He went up on the mountain and called to Him those He Himself wanted. And they came to Him.
41_3_14: Then He appointed twelve, whom He also named apostles, that they might be with Him and that He might send them out to preach,
41_3_15: and to have power to cast out demons:
41_3_16: Simon, to whom He gave the name Peter;
41_3_17: James the son of Zebedee and John the brother of James, to whom He gave the name Boanerges, that is, 'Sons of Thunder';
41_3_18: Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James the son of Alphaeus, Thaddaeus, Simon the Zealot;
41_3_19: and Judas Iscariot, who also betrayed Him.
41_3_20: Then the multitude came together again, so that they could not so much as eat bread.
41_3_21: But when His own people heard about this, they went out to lay hold of Him, for they said, 'He is out of His mind.'
41_3_22: And the scribes who came down from Jerusalem said, 'He is possessed by Beelzebub,' and 'By the ruler of the demons He casts out demons.'
41_3_23: So He called them to Himself and said to them in parables: 'How can Satan cast out Satan?
41_3_24: If a kingdom is divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
41_3_25: And if a house is divided against itself, that house cannot stand.
41_3_26: And if Satan has risen up against himself, and is divided, he cannot stand, but has an end.
41_3_27: No one can enter a strong man's house and plunder his goods, unless he first binds the strong man. And then he will plunder his house.
41_3_28: 'Assuredly, I say to you, all sins will be forgiven the sons of men, and whatever blasphemies they may utter;
41_3_29: but he who blasphemes against the Holy Spirit never has forgiveness, but is guilty of an eternal sin'—
41_3_30: because they said, 'He has an unclean spirit.'
41_3_31: Then His brothers and His mother came, and standing outside they sent to Him, calling Him.
41_3_32: And a multitude was sitting around Him; and they said to Him, 'Look, Your mother and Your brothers are outside seeking You.'
41_3_33: But He answered them, saying, 'Who is My mother, or My brothers?'
41_3_34: And He looked around in a circle at those who sat about Him, and said, 'Here are My mother and My brothers!'
41_3_35: For whoever does the will of God is My brother and My sister and mother.'
41_4_1: And again He began to teach by the sea. And a very large crowd was gathered to Him, so that He got into a boat and sat in it on the sea; and the whole multitude was on the shore facing the sea.
41_4_2: Then He taught them many things by parables, and said to them in His teaching:
41_4_3: 'Listen! Behold, a sower went out to sow.
41_4_4: And it happened, as he sowed, that some seed fell by the wayside; and the birds of the air came and devoured it.
41_4_5: Some fell on stony ground, where it did not have much earth; and immediately it sprang up because it had no depth of earth.
41_4_6: But when the sun was up it was scorched, and because it had no root it withered away.
41_4_7: And some seed fell among thorns; and the thorns grew up and choked it, and it yielded no crop.
41_4_8: But other seed fell on good ground and yielded a crop that sprang up, increased and produced: some thirtyfold, some sixty, and some a hundred.'
41_4_9: And He said to them, 'He who has ears to hear, let him hear!'
41_4_10: But when He was alone, those around Him with the twelve asked Him about the parables.
41_4_11: And He said to them, 'To you it has been given to know the mystery of the kingdom of God; but to those who are outside, all things come in parables,
41_4_12: so that 'Seeing they may see and not perceive, And hearing they may hear and not understand; Otherwise they might turn, And their sins be forgiven them.' "
41_4_13: And He said to them, "Do you not understand this parable? How then will you understand all the parables?"
41_4_15: And these are the ones by the wayside where the word is sown. When they hear, Satan comes immediately and takes away the word that was sown in their hearts.
41_4_16: These likewise are the ones sown on stony ground who, when they hear the word, immediately receive it with joy;
41_4_17: and they have no root in themselves, but endure only for a time. Afterward, when tribulation or persecution arises for the word's sake, immediately they fall away.
41_4_18: Now these are the ones sown among thorns; they are the ones who hear the word,
41_4_19: and the cares of this world, the deceitfulness of riches, and the desires for other things entering in choke the word, and it becomes unfruitful.
41_4_20: But these are the ones sown on good ground, those who hear the word, accept it, and bear fruit: some thirtyfold, some sixty, and some a hundred."
41_4_21: Also He said to them, "Is a lamp brought to be put under a basket or under a bed? Is it not to be set on a lampstand?"
41_4_22: For there is nothing hidden which will not be revealed, nor has anything been kept secret but that it should come to light.
41_4_23: If anyone has ears to hear, let him hear."
41_4_24: Then He said to them, "Take heed what you hear. With the same measure you use, it will be measured to you; and to you who hear, more will be given."
41_4_25: For whoever has, to him more will be given; but whoever does not have, even what he has will be taken away from him."
41_4_26: And He said, "The kingdom of God is as if a man should scatter seed on the ground,"
41_4_27: and should sleep by night and rise by day, and the seed should sprout and grow, he himself does not know how."
41_4_28: For the earth yields crops by itself: first the blade, then the head, after that the full grain in the head."
41_4_29: But when the grain ripens, immediately he puts in the sickle, because the harvest has come."
41_4_30: Then He said, "To what shall we liken the kingdom of God? Or with what parable shall we picture it?"
41_4_31: It is like a mustard seed which, when it is sown on the ground, is smaller than all the seeds on earth;"
41_4_32: but when it is sown, it grows up and becomes greater than all herbs, and shoots out large branches, so that the birds of the air may nest under its shade."
41_4_33: And with many such parables He spoke the word to them as they were able to hear it."
41_4_34: But without a parable He did not speak to them. And when they were alone, He explained all things to His disciples."
41_4_35: On the same day, when evening had come, He said to them, "Let us cross over to the other side."
41_4_36: Now when they had left the multitude, they took Him along in the boat as He was. And other little boats were also with Him."
41_4_37: And a great windstorm arose, and the waves were breaking into the boat, so that it was already filling."
41_4_38: But He was in the stern, asleep on a pillow. And they awoke Him and said to Him, "Teacher, do You not care that we are perishing?"
41_4_39: Then He arose and rebuked the wind, and said to the sea, "Peace, be still!" And the wind ceased and there was a great calm."
41_4_40: But He said to them, "Why are you so fearful? How is it that you have no faith?"
41_4_41: And they feared exceedingly, and said to one another, "Who can this be, that even the wind and the sea obey Him!"
41_5_1: Then they came to the other side of the sea, to the country of the Gerasenes."
41_5_2: And when He had come out of the boat, immediately there met Him out of the tombs a man with an unclean spirit,"
41_5_3: who had his dwelling among the tombs; and no one could bind him, not even with chains,"
41_5_4: because he had often been bound with shackles and chains, and the chains had been pulled apart by him, and the shackles broken in pieces; neither could anyone tame him."
41_5_5: And always, night and day, he was in the mountains and in the tombs, crying out and cutting himself with stones."
41_5_6: When he saw Jesus from afar, he ran and bowed down before Him."
41_5_7: And he cried out with a loud voice and said, "What have I to do with You, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I implore You by God that You do not torment me."
41_5_8: For He said to him, "Come out of the man, unclean spirit!"
41_5_9: Then He asked him, "What is your name?" And he answered, saying, "My name is Legion; for we are many."
41_5_10: Also he begged Him earnestly that He would not send them out of the country."
41_5_11: Now a large herd of swine was feeding there near the mountains."
41_5_12: So all the demons begged Him, saying, "Send us to the swine, that we may enter them."
41_5_13: And at once Jesus gave them permission. Then the unclean spirits went out and entered the swine (there were about two thousand); and the herd ran violently down the steep place into the sea, and drowned in the sea.
41_5_14: So those who fed the swine fled, and they told it in the city and in the country. And they went out to see what it was that had happened.
41_5_15: Then they came to Jesus, and saw the one who had been demon-possessed and had the legion, sitting and clothed and in his right mind. And they were afraid.
41_5_16: And those who saw it told them how it happened to him who had been demon-possessed, and about the swine.
41_5_17: Then they began to plead with Him to depart from their region.
41_5_18: And when He got into the boat, he who had been demon-possessed begged Him that he might be with Him.
41_5_19: However, Jesus did not permit him, but said to him, "Go home to your friends, and tell them what great things the Lord has done for you, and how He has had compassion on you."
41_5_20: And he departed and began to proclaim in Decapolis all that Jesus had done for him; and all marveled.
41_5_21: Now when Jesus had crossed over again by boat to the other side, a great multitude gathered to Him; and He was by the sea.
41_5_22: And behold, one of the rulers of the synagogue came, Jairus by name. And when he saw Him, he fell at His feet.
41_5_23: And begged Him earnestly, saying, "My little daughter lies at the point of death. Come and lay Your hands on her, that she may be healed, and she will live."
41_5_24: So Jesus went with him, and a great multitude followed Him and pressed around Him.
41_5_25: Now a certain woman had a flow of blood for twelve years,
41_5_26: And had suffered many things from many physicians. She had spent all that she had and was no better, but rather grew worse.
41_5_27: When she heard about Jesus, she came behind Him in the crowd and touched His garment.
41_5_28: For she said, "If only I may touch His clothes, I shall be made well."
41_5_29: Immediately the fountain of her blood was dried up, and she felt in her body that she was healed of the affliction.
41_5_30: And Jesus, immediately knowing in Himself that power had gone out of Him, turned around in the crowd and said, "Who touched My clothes?"
41_5_31: But His disciples said to Him, "You see the multitude pressing around You, and You say, 'Who touched Me?'"
41_5_32: And He looked around to see her who had done this thing.
41_5_33: But the woman, fearing and trembling, knowing what had happened to her, came and fell down before Him and told Him the whole truth.
41_5_34: And He said to her, "Daughter, your faith has made you well. Go in peace, and be healed of your affliction."
41_5_35: While He was still speaking, some came from the ruler of the synagogue's house who said, "Your daughter is dead. Why trouble the Teacher any further?"
41_5_36: But Jesus, overhearing what was being spoken, said to the ruler of the synagogue, "Do not be afraid; only believe."
41_5_37: And He permitted no one to follow Him except Peter, James, and John the brother of James.
41_5_38: Then He came to the house of the ruler of the synagogue, and saw a commotion and those who wept and wailed loudly.
41_5_39: When He came in, He said to them, "Why make this commotion and weep? The child is not dead, but sleeping."
41_5_40: And they ridiculed Him. But when He had put them all outside, He took the father and the mother of the child, and those who were with Him, and entered where the child was lying.
41_5_41: Then He took the child by the hand, and said to her, "Talitha, koum," which is translated, "Little girl, I say to you, arise."
41_5_42: Immediately the girl arose and began to walk, for she was twelve years of age. And they were overcome with great amazement.
41_5_43: But He commanded them strictly that no one should know it, and said that something should be given her to eat.
41_6_1: Then He went out from there and came to His own country, and His disciples followed Him.
41_6_2: And when the Sabbath had come, He began to teach in the synagogue. And many hearing Him were astonished, saying, "Where did this Man get these things? And what wisdom is this which is given to Him, that such mighty works are performed by His hands?"
41_6_3: Is this not the carpenter, the Son of Mary, and brother of James, Joses, Judas, and Simon? And are not His sisters here with us?" So they were offended at Him.
41_6_4: But Jesus said to them, "A prophet is not without honor except in his own country, among his own relatives, and in his own house."
41_6_5: Now He could do no mighty work there, except that He laid His hands on a few sick people and healed them.
41_6_6: And He marveled because of their unbelief. Then He went about the villages in a circuit, teaching.
41_6_7: And He called the twelve to Himself, and began to send them out two by two, and gave them authority over the unclean spirits.
41_6_8: He commanded them to take nothing for the journey except a staff—no bag, no bread, no money in their belts—
41_6_9: but to wear sandals and not put on two tunics.
41_6_10: Also He said to them, "Whenever you enter a house, stay there until you leave that place."
41_6_11: And whoever will not receive you nor hear you, when you depart from there, shake off the dust under your feet as a testimony against them. Assuredly, I say to you, it will be more tolerable for Sodom and Gomorrah in the day of judgment than for that city!"
41_6_12: So they went out and proclaimed that people should repent.
41_6_13: And they cast out many demons and anointed with oil many who were sick and healed them.
41_6_14: Now King Herod heard of Him, for His name had become well known. And he said, "John the Baptist has been raised from the dead, and therefore these miraculous powers are at work in him."
41_6_15: Others said, "It is Elijah." And others said, "It is a prophet, like one of the prophets of old."
41_6_16: But when Herod heard, he said, "This is John, whom I beheaded; he has been raised from the dead!"
41_6_17: For Herod himself had sent and laid hold of John, and bound him in prison for the sake of Herodias, his brother Philip's wife; for he had married her.
41_6_18: Because John had been saying to Herod, "It is not lawful for you to have your brother's wife."
41_6_19: Therefore Herodias held it against him and wanted to kill him, but she could not;
41_6_20: for Herod feared John, knowing that he was a righteous and holy man, and he protected him. And when he heard him, he was greatly perplexed, yet he heard him gladly.
41_6_21: Then an opportune day came when Herod on his birthday gave a banquet for his nobles, military commanders, and the leading men of Galilee.
41_6_22: And when Herodias's daughter came in and danced, and pleased Herod and those who sat with him, the king said to the girl, "Ask me whatever you want, and I will give it to you."
41_6_23: He also swore to her, "Whatever you ask me, I will give you, up to half my kingdom."
41_6_24: So she went out and said to her mother, "What shall I ask?" And she said, "The head of John the Baptist."
41_6_25: Immediately she came in with haste to the king and asked, saying, "I want you to give me at once the head of John the Baptist on a platter."
41_6_26: And the king was exceedingly sorry; yet, because of the oaths and because of those who sat with him, he did not want to refuse her.
41_6_27: Immediately the king sent an executioner and commanded his head to be brought. And he went and beheaded him in the prison,
41_6_28: brought his head on a platter, and gave it to the girl; and the girl gave it to her mother.
41_6_29: When his disciples heard of it, they came and took away his corpse and laid it in a tomb.
41_6_30: Then the apostles gathered to Jesus and told Him all things, both what they had done and what they had taught.
41_6_31: And He said to them, "Come aside by yourselves to a quiet place and rest a while." For there were many coming and going, and they did not even have time to eat.
41_6_32: So they departed to a deserted place in the boat by themselves.
41_6_33: But the multitudes saw them departing, and many knew Him and ran there on foot from all the cities. They arrived before them and came together to Him.
41_6_34: And Jesus, when He came out, saw a great multitude and was moved with compassion for them, because they were like sheep without a shepherd. So He began to teach them many things.
41_6_35: When the day was now far spent, His disciples came to Him and said, "This is a deserted place, and already the hour is late."
41_6_36: Send them away, that they may go into the surrounding country and villages and buy themselves bread; for they have nothing to eat."
41_6_37: But He answered and said to them, "You give them something to eat." And they said to Him, "Shall we go and buy two hundred denarii worth of bread and give them something to eat?"
41_6_38: But He said to them, "How many loaves do you have? Go and see." And when they found out they said, "Five, and two fish."
41_6_39: Then He commanded them to make them all sit down in groups on the green grass.
41_6_40: So they sat down in ranks, in hundreds and in fifties.
41_6_41: And when He had taken the five loaves and the two fish, He looked up to heaven, blessed and broke the loaves, and gave them to His disciples to set before them; and the two fish He divided among them all.
41_6_42: So they all ate and were satisfied.
41_6_43: And they took up twelve baskets full of fragments and of the fish.
41_6_44: Now those who had eaten the loaves were about five thousand men.
41_6_45: Immediately He made His disciples get into the boat and go before Him to the other side, to Bethsaida, while He sent the multitude away.
41_6_46: And when He had sent them away, He departed to the mountain to pray.
41_6_47: Now when evening came, the boat was in the middle of the sea; and He was alone on the land.
41_6_48: Then He saw them straining at rowing, for the wind was against them. Now about the fourth watch of the night He came to them, walking on the sea, and would have passed them by.
41_6_49: And when they saw Him walking on the sea, they supposed it was a ghost, and cried out;
41_6_50: for they all saw Him and were terrified. But immediately He talked with them and said to them, "Take courage; it is I; do not be afraid."
41_6_51: Then He went up into the boat to them, and the wind ceased. And they were utterly astounded,
41_6_52: For they had not understood about the loaves, because their heart was hardened.
41_6_53: When they had crossed over, they came to the land of Gennesaret and anchored there.
41_6_54: And when they came out of the boat, immediately the people recognized Him,
41_6_55: ran through that whole surrounding region, and began to carry about on beds those who were sick to wherever they heard He was.
41_6_56: Wherever He entered, into villages, cities, or the country, they laid the sick in the marketplaces, and begged Him that they might just touch the hem of His garment. And as many as touched Him were made well.
41_7_1: Then the Pharisees and some of the scribes came together to Him, having come from Jerusalem.
41_7_2: Now when they saw some of His disciples eat bread with defiled, that is, with unwashed hands, they found fault.
41_7_3: For the Pharisees and all the Jews do not eat unless they wash their hands in a special way, holding the tradition of the elders.
41_7_4: When they come from the marketplace, they do not eat unless they wash. And there are many other things which they have received and hold, like the washing of cups, pitchers, copper vessels, and couches.
41_7_5: Then the Pharisees and scribes asked Him, "Why do Your disciples not walk according to the tradition of the elders, but eat bread with unwashed hands?"
41_7_6: He answered and said to them, "Well did Isaiah prophesy of you hypocrites, as it is written: 'This people honors Me with their lips, But their heart is far from Me.
41_7_7: And in vain they worship Me, Teaching as doctrines the commandments of men.'"
41_7_8: For laying aside the commandment of God, you hold the tradition of men—the washing of pitchers and cups, and many other such things you do."
41_7_9: He said to them, "All too well you reject the commandment of God, that you may keep your tradition.
41_7_10: For Moses said, 'Honor your father and your mother'; and, 'He who curses father or mother, let him be put to death.'
41_7_11: But you say, 'If a man says to his father or mother, "Whatever profit you might have received from me is Corban"—' (that is, a gift to God),
41_7_12: then you no longer let him do anything for his father or his mother,
41_7_13: making the word of God of no effect through your tradition which you have handed down. And many such things you do."
41_7_14: When He had called all the multitude to Himself, He said to them, "Hear Me, everyone, and understand:
41_7_15: There is nothing that enters a man from outside which can defile him; but the things which come out of him, those are the things that defile a man."
41_7_16: If anyone has ears to hear, let him hear!"
41_7_17: When He had entered a house away from the crowd, His disciples asked Him concerning the parable.
41_7_18: So He said to them, "Are you thus without understanding also? Do you not perceive that whatever enters a man from outside cannot defile him,
41_7_19: because it does not enter his heart but his stomach, and is eliminated, thus purifying all foods?"
41_7_20: And He said, "What comes out of a man, that defiles a man.
41_7_21: For from within, out of the heart of men, proceed evil thoughts, adulteries, fornications, murders,
41_7_22: thefts, covetousness, wickedness, deceit, lewdness, an evil eye, blasphemy, pride, foolishness.
41_7_23: All these evil things come from within and defile a man."
41_7_24: From there He arose and went to the region of Tyre and Sidon. And He entered a house and wanted no one to know it, but He could not be hidden.
41_7_25: For a woman whose young daughter had an unclean spirit heard about Him, and she came and fell at His feet.
41_7_26: The woman was a Greek, a Syro-Phoenician by birth, and she kept asking Him to cast the demon out of her daughter.
41_7_27: But Jesus said to her, "Let the children be filled first, for it is not good to take the children's bread and throw it to the little dogs."
41_7_28: And she answered and said to Him, "Yes, Lord, yet even the little dogs under the table eat from the children's crumbs."
41_7_29: Then He said to her, "For this saying go your way; the demon has gone out of your daughter."
41_7_30: And when she had come to her house, she found the demon gone out, and her daughter lying on the bed.
41_7_31: Again, departing from the region of Tyre and Sidon, He came through the midst of the region of Decapolis to the Sea of Galilee.
41_7_32: Then they brought to Him one who was deaf and had an impediment in his speech, and they begged Him to put His hand on him.
41_7_33: And He took him aside from the multitude privately, and put His fingers in his ears, and He spat and touched his tongue.
41_7_34: Then, looking up to heaven, He sighed deeply and said to him, "Ephphatha," that is, "Be opened."
41_7_35: Immediately his ears were opened, and the impediment of his tongue was loosed, and he spoke plainly.
41_7_36: Then He commanded them that they should tell no one; but the more He commanded them, the more widely they proclaimed it.
41_7_37: And they were astonished beyond measure, saying, "He has done all things well. He even makes the deaf to hear and the mute to speak."
41_8_1: In those days, when again the multitude was very great and had nothing to eat, Jesus called His disciples to Him and said to them,
41_8_2: "I have compassion on the multitude, because they have now continued with Me three days and have nothing to eat."
41_8_3: And if I send them away hungry to their own houses, they will faint on the way; for some of them have come from afar."
41_8_4: Then His disciples answered Him, "How can one satisfy these people with bread here in this desolate place?"
41_8_5: He asked them, "How many loaves do you have?" And they said, "Seven."
41_8_6: So He commanded the multitude to sit down on the ground. And He took the seven loaves and gave thanks, broke them and gave them to His disciples to set before them; and they set them before the multitude.
41_8_7: They also had a few small fish; and having blessed them, He said to set them also before them.
41_8_8: So they ate and were filled, and they took up seven large baskets of leftover fragments.
41_8_9: Now those who had eaten were about four thousand. And He sent them away,
41_8_10: immediately got into the boat with His disciples and came to the region of Dalmanutha.
41_8_11: Then the Pharisees came out and began to dispute with Him, seeking from Him a sign from heaven to test Him.
41_8_12: But He sighed deeply in His spirit and said, "Why does this generation seek a sign? Truly, I say to you, no sign shall be given to this generation."
41_8_13: And He left them, and getting into the boat again, departed to the other side.
41_8_14: Now the disciples had forgotten to take bread, and they did not have more than one loaf with them in the boat.
41_8_15: Then He charged them, saying, "Take heed, beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and the leaven of Herod."
41_8_16: And they reasoned among themselves, saying, "It is because we have no bread."
41_8_17: But Jesus, being aware of it, said to them, "Why do you reason because you have no bread? Do you not yet perceive or understand? Is your heart still hardened?"
41_8_18: Having eyes, do you not see? And having ears, do you not hear? And do you not remember?
41_8_19: When I broke the five loaves for the five thousand, how many baskets full of fragments did you take up?" They said to Him, "Twelve."
41_8_20: "Also, when I broke the seven for the four thousand, how many large baskets full of fragments did you take up?" And they said, "Seven."
41_8_21: So He said to them, "Do you not yet understand?"
41_8_22: Then He came to Bethsaida; and they brought a blind man to Him, and begged Him to touch him.
41_8_23: So He took the blind man by the hand and led him out of the town. And when He had spit on his eyes and put His hands on him, He asked him, "Do you see anything?"
41_8_24: And he looked up and said, "I see people, but they look like trees, walking."
41_8_25: Then He put His hands on his eyes again and made him look up. And he was restored and saw everything clearly.
41_8_26: Then He sent him away to his house, saying, "Do not even enter the village."
41_8_27: Now Jesus and His disciples went out to the villages of Caesarea Philippi; and on the way He asked His disciples, "Who do people say that I am?"
41_8_28: So they answered, "John the Baptist; but some say, Elijah; and others, one of the prophets."
41_8_29: He said to them, "But who do you say that I am?" Peter answered and said to Him, "You are the Christ."
41_8_30: Then He strictly warned them that they should tell no one about Him.
