41_8_31: And He began to teach them that the Son of Man must suffer many things and be rejected by the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be killed, and after three days rise again.
41_8_32: He spoke this word openly. Then Peter took Him aside and began to rebuke Him.
41_8_33: But when He had turned around and looked at His disciples, He rebuked Peter, saying, "Get behind Me, Satan! For you are not mindful of the things of God, but the things of men."
41_8_34: When He had called the people to Himself with His disciples also, He said to them, "Whoever desires to come after Me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow Me."
41_8_35: For whoever desires to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for My sake and the gospel's will save it.
41_8_36: For what will it profit a man if he gains the whole world and loses his own soul?
41_8_37: Or what will a man give in exchange for his soul?
41_8_38: For whoever is ashamed of Me and My words in this adulterous and sinful generation, of him the Son of Man also will be ashamed when He comes in the glory of His Father with the holy angels.
41_9_1: And He said to them, "Truly, I say to you that there are some standing here who will not taste death until they see the kingdom of God come with power."
41_9_2: Now after six days Jesus took Peter, James, and John, and led them up on a high mountain apart by themselves; and He was transfigured before them.
41_9_3: His clothes became shining, exceedingly white, as no launderer on earth can whiten them.
41_9_4: And Elijah appeared to them with Moses, and they were talking with Jesus.
41_9_5: Then Peter answered and said to Jesus, "Rabbi, it is good for us to be here; and let us make three tabernacles: one for You, one for Moses, and one for Elijah"—
41_9_6: because he did not know what to say, for they were greatly afraid.
41_9_7: And a cloud came and overshadowed them; and a voice came out of the cloud, saying, "This is My beloved Son. Hear Him!"
41_9_8: Suddenly, when they had looked around, they saw no one anymore, but only Jesus with themselves.
41_9_9: Now as they came down from the mountain, He commanded them that they should tell no one the things they had seen, until the Son of Man had risen from the dead.
41_9_10: So they kept this word to themselves, questioning what the rising from the dead meant.
41_9_11: And they asked Him, saying, "Why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?"
41_9_12: Then He answered and told them, "Indeed, Elijah is coming first and restores all things. And how is it written concerning the Son of Man, that He must suffer many things and be treated with contempt?
41_9_13: But I say to you that Elijah has also come, and they did to him whatever they wished, as it is written of him."
41_9_14: And when He came to the disciples, He saw a great multitude around them, and scribes disputing with them.
41_9_15: Immediately, when they saw Him, all the people were greatly amazed, and running to Him, greeted Him.
41_9_16: And He asked the scribes, "What are you discussing with them?"
41_9_17: Then one of the crowd answered and said, "Teacher, I brought You my son, who has a spirit that makes him mute.
41_9_18: And wherever it seizes him, it throws him down; he foams at the mouth, grinds his teeth, and becomes rigid. So I spoke to Your disciples, that they should cast it out, but they could not."
41_9_19: He answered him and said, "O faithless generation, how long shall I be with you? How long shall I bear with you? Bring him to Me."
41_9_20: Then they brought him to Him. And when he saw Him, immediately the spirit convulsed him, and he fell on the ground and wallowed, foaming at the mouth.
41_9_21: So He asked his father, "How long has this been happening to him?" And he said, "From childhood."
41_9_22: And often he has thrown him both into the fire and into the water to destroy him. But if You can do anything, have compassion on us and help us."
41_9_23: Jesus said to him, "If you can believe, all things are possible to him who believes."
41_9_24: Immediately the father of the child cried out and said, "I believe; help my unbelief!"
41_9_25: When Jesus saw that the people came running together, He rebuked the unclean spirit, saying to it: "Deaf and dumb spirit, I command you, come out of him and enter him no more!"
41_9_26: Then the spirit cried out, convulsed him greatly, and came out of him. And he became as one dead, so that many said, "He is dead."
41_9_27: But Jesus took him by the hand and lifted him up, and he arose.
41_9_28: And when He had come into the house, His disciples asked Him privately, "Why could we not cast it out?"
41_9_29: So He said to them, "This kind can come out by nothing but prayer."
41_9_30: Then they departed from there and passed through Galilee, and He did not want anyone to know it.
41_9_31: For He taught His disciples and said to them, "The Son of Man is being betrayed into the hands of men, and they will kill Him. And after He is killed, He will rise the third day."
41_9_32: But they did not understand this saying, and were afraid to ask Him.
41_9_33: Then He came to Capernaum. And when He was in the house He asked them, "What were you disputing among yourselves on the road?"
41_9_34: But they kept silent, for on the road they had disputed among themselves who would be the greatest.
41_9_35: And He sat down, called the twelve, and said to them, "If anyone desires to be first, he shall be last of all and servant of all."
41_9_36: Then He took a little child and set him in the midst of them. And when He had taken him in His arms, He said to them,
41_9_37: "Whoever receives one of these little children in My name receives Me; and whoever receives Me, receives not Me but Him who sent Me."
41_9_38: Now John answered Him, saying, "Teacher, we saw someone casting out demons in Your name, and we tried to stop him because he does not follow us."
41_9_39: But Jesus said, "Do not forbid him, for no one who works a mighty work in My name can soon afterward speak evil of Me."
41_9_40: For he who is not against us is for us.
41_9_41: For whoever gives you a cup of water to drink in My name, because you belong to Christ, assuredly, I say to you, he will by no means lose his reward.
41_9_42: But whoever causes one of these little ones who believe in Me to stumble, it would be better for him if a heavy millstone were hung around his neck, and he were thrown into the sea.
41_9_43: If your hand causes you to sin, cut it off. It is better for you to enter into life maimed, rather than having two hands, to go to hell, into the unquenchable fire—
41_9_44: where 'Their worm does not die And the fire is not quenched.'
41_9_45: And if your foot causes you to sin, cut it off. It is better for you to enter life lame, rather than having two feet, to be cast into hell—
41_9_46: where 'Their worm does not die, And the fire is not quenched.'
41_9_47: And if your eye causes you to sin, pluck it out. It is better for you to enter the kingdom of God with one eye, rather than having two eyes, to be thrown into hell—
41_9_48: where 'their worm does not die and the fire is not quenched.'
41_9_49: For everyone will be seasoned with fire, and every sacrifice will be seasoned with salt.
41_9_50: Salt is good, but if the salt loses its flavor, how will you season it? Have salt in yourselves, and have peace with one another."
41_10_1: Then He arose from there and went to the region of Judea and beyond the Jordan. And multitudes gathered to Him again, and as He was accustomed, He taught them again.
41_10_2: The Pharisees came and asked Him, "Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife?" testing Him.
41_10_3: And He answered and said to them, "What did Moses command you?"
41_10_4: They said, "Moses permitted a man to write a certificate of divorce, and to dismiss her."
41_10_5: And Jesus answered and said to them, "Because of the hardness of your heart he wrote you this precept."
41_10_6: But from the beginning of the creation, God 'made them male and female.'
41_10_7: 'For this reason a man shall leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife,
41_10_8: and the two shall become one flesh'; so then they are no longer two, but one flesh.
41_10_9: Therefore what God has joined together, let not man separate."
41_10_10: In the house His disciples also asked Him again about the same matter.
41_10_11: So He said to them, "Whoever divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery against her.
41_10_12: And if a woman divorces her husband and marries another, she commits adultery."
41_10_13: Then they brought little children to Him, that He might touch them; but the disciples rebuked those who brought them.
41_10_14: But when Jesus saw it, He was indignant and said to them, "Let the little children come to Me, and do not forbid them; for of such is the kingdom of God.
41_10_15: Assuredly, I say to you, whoever does not receive the kingdom of God as a little child will by no means enter it."
41_10_16: And He took them up in His arms, laid His hands on them, and blessed them.
41_10_17: Now as He was going out on the road, one came running, knelt before Him, and asked Him, "Good Teacher, what shall I do that I may inherit eternal life?"
41_10_18: So Jesus said to him, "Why do you call Me good? No one is good but God alone.
41_10_19: You know the commandments: 'Do not commit adultery,' 'Do not murder,' 'Do not steal,' 'Do not bear false witness,' 'Do not defraud,' 'Honor your father and your mother.'"
41_10_20: And he answered and said to Him, "Teacher, all these things I have kept from my youth."
41_10_21: Then Jesus, looking at him, loved him, and said to him, "One thing you lack: Go your way, sell whatever you have and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; and come, follow Me."
41_10_22: But he was sad at this word, and went away grieving, for he had great possessions.
41_10_23: Then Jesus looked around and said to His disciples, "How hard it is for those who have riches to enter the kingdom of God!"
41_10_24: And the disciples were astonished at His words. But Jesus answered again and said to them, "Children, how hard it is to enter the kingdom of God!
41_10_25: It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom of God."
41_10_26: And they were greatly astonished, saying among themselves, "Who then can be saved?"
41_10_27: But Jesus looked at them and said, "With men it is impossible, but not with God; for with God all things are possible."
41_10_28: Then Peter began to say to Him, "See, we have left all and followed You."
41_10_29: So Jesus answered and said, "Assuredly, I say to you, there is no one who has left house or brothers or sisters or father or mother or wife or children or lands, for My sake and the gospel's,"
41_10_30: who shall not receive a hundredfold now in this time—houses and brothers and sisters and mothers and children and lands, with persecutions—and in the age to come, eternal life."
41_10_31: But many who are first will be last, and the last first."
41_10_32: Now they were on the road, going up to Jerusalem, and Jesus was walking ahead of them; and they were amazed. And as they followed they were afraid. Then He took the twelve aside again and began to tell them the things that would happen to Him:"
41_10_33: "Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be betrayed to the chief priests and to the scribes; and they will condemn Him to death and deliver Him to the Gentiles;"
41_10_34: and they will mock Him, and scourge Him, and spit on Him, and kill Him. And the third day He will rise again."
41_10_35: Then James and John, the sons of Zebedee, came to Him, saying, "Teacher, we want You to do for us whatever we ask."
41_10_36: And He said to them, "What do you want Me to do for you?"
41_10_37: They said to Him, "Grant us that we may sit, one on Your right hand and the other on Your left, in Your glory."
41_10_38: But Jesus said to them, "You do not know what you ask. Are you able to drink the cup that I drink, and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?"
41_10_39: They said to Him, "We are able." So Jesus said to them, "You will indeed drink the cup that I drink, and with the baptism I am baptized with you will be baptized;"
41_10_40: but to sit on My right hand and on My left is not Mine to give, but it is for those for whom it is prepared."
41_10_41: And when the ten heard it, they began to be greatly displeased with James and John."
41_10_42: But Jesus called them to Himself and said to them, "You know that those who are considered rulers over the Gentiles lord it over them, and their great ones exercise authority over them."
41_10_43: Yet it shall not be so among you; but whoever desires to become great among you shall be your servant."
41_10_44: And whoever of you desires to be first shall be slave of all."
41_10_45: For even the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve, and to give His life a ransom for many."
41_10_46: Now they came to Jericho. As He went out of Jericho with His disciples and a great multitude, blind Bartimaeus, the son of Timaeus, sat by the road begging."
41_10_47: And when he heard that it was Jesus of Nazareth, he began to cry out and say, "Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!"
41_10_48: Then many warned him to be quiet; but he cried out all the more, "Son of David, have mercy on me!"
41_10_49: So Jesus stood still and commanded him to be called. Then they called the blind man, saying to him, "Be of good cheer. Rise, He is calling you."
41_10_50: And throwing aside his garment, he rose and came to Jesus."
41_10_51: So Jesus answered and said to him, "What do you want Me to do for you?" The blind man said to Him, "Rabboni, that I may receive my sight."
41_10_52: Then Jesus said to him, "Go your way; your faith has made you well." And immediately he received his sight and followed Jesus on the road."
41_11_1: Now when they drew near Jerusalem, to Bethphage and Bethany, at the Mount of Olives, He sent two of His disciples;"
41_11_2: and He said to them, "Go into the village opposite you; and as soon as you have entered it you will find a colt tied, on which no one has sat. Loose it and bring it."
41_11_3: And if anyone says to you, 'Why are you doing this?' say, 'The Lord has need of it,' and immediately he will send it here."
41_11_4: So they went their way, and found the colt tied by the door outside on the street, and they loosed it."
41_11_5: But some of those who stood there said to them, "What are you doing, loosing the colt?"
41_11_6: And they spoke to them just as Jesus had commanded. So they let them go."
41_11_7: Then they brought the colt to Jesus and threw their clothes on it, and He sat on it."
41_11_8: And many spread their clothes on the road, and others cut down leafy branches from the trees and spread them on the road."
41_11_9: Then those who went before and those who followed cried out, saying: "Hosanna! 'Blessed is He who comes in the name of the LORD!'"
41_11_10: Blessed is the kingdom of our father David that comes in the name of the Lord! Hosanna in the highest!"
41_11_11: And Jesus went into Jerusalem and into the temple. So when He had looked around at all things, as the hour was already late, He went out to Bethany with the twelve."
41_11_12: Now the next day, when they had come out from Bethany, He was hungry."
41_11_13: And seeing from afar a fig tree having leaves, He went to see if perhaps He would find something on it. When He came to it, He found nothing but leaves, for it was not the season for figs."
41_11_14: In response Jesus said to it, "Let no one eat fruit from you ever again." And His disciples heard it."
41_11_15: So they came to Jerusalem. Then Jesus went into the temple and began to drive out those who bought and sold in the temple, and overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who sold doves."
41_11_16: And He would not allow anyone to carry merchandise through the temple.
41_11_17: Then He taught, saying to them, "Is it not written, 'My house shall be called a house of prayer for all nations'? But you have made it a 'den of thieves.'"
41_11_18: And the chief priests and the scribes heard it and sought how they might destroy Him; for they feared Him, because all the people were astonished at His teaching.
41_11_19: When evening had come, He went out of the city.
41_11_20: Now in the morning, as they passed by, they saw the fig tree withered from the roots up.
41_11_21: And Peter, remembering, said to Him, "Rabbi, look! The fig tree which You cursed has withered away."
41_11_22: So Jesus answered and said to them, "Have faith in God."
41_11_23: For assuredly, I say to you, whoever says to this mountain, 'Be taken up and cast into the sea,' and does not doubt in his heart, but believes that what he says will come to pass, he will have whatever he says.
41_11_24: Therefore I say to you, whatever things you ask when you pray, believe that you have received them, and you will have them.
41_11_25: And whenever you stand praying, if you have anything against anyone, forgive him, that your Father in heaven may also forgive you your trespasses.
41_11_26: But if you do not forgive, neither will your Father in heaven forgive your trespasses.
41_11_27: Then they came again to Jerusalem. And as He was walking in the temple, the chief priests, the scribes, and the elders came to Him.
41_11_28: And they said to Him, "By what authority are You doing these things? And who gave You this authority to do these things?"
41_11_29: But Jesus answered and said to them, "I also will ask you one question; then answer Me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things:"
41_11_30: The baptism of John—was it from heaven or from men? Answer Me."
41_11_31: And they reasoned among themselves, saying, "If we say, 'From heaven,' He will say, 'Why then did you not believe him?'"
41_11_32: But if we say, 'From men'"—they feared the people, for all counted John to have been a real prophet.
41_11_33: So they answered and said to Jesus, "We do not know." And Jesus answered and said to them, "Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things."
41_12_1: Then He began to speak to them in parables: "A man planted a vineyard and set a hedge around it, dug a pit for the wine vat and built a tower. And he leased it to tenant farmers and went into a far country."
41_12_2: Now at harvest time he sent a servant to the tenant farmers, that he might receive some of the fruit of the vineyard from them.
41_12_3: And they took him and beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
41_12_4: Again he sent them another servant, and at him they threw stones, wounded him in the head, and sent him away shamefully treated.
41_12_5: And again he sent another, and him they killed; and many others, beating some and killing some.
41_12_6: Therefore still having one son, his beloved, he also sent him to them last, saying, 'They will respect my son.'"
41_12_7: But those tenant farmers said among themselves, 'This is the heir. Come, let us kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.'"
41_12_8: So they took him and killed him and cast him out of the vineyard."
41_12_9: Therefore what will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy the tenant farmers, and give the vineyard to others."
41_12_10: Have you not even read this Scripture: 'The stone which the builders rejected has become the chief cornerstone;"
41_12_11: This came about from the Lord, and it is marvelous in our eyes'?"
41_12_12: And they sought to lay hands on Him, but feared the multitude, for they knew He had spoken the parable against them. So they left Him and went away.
41_12_13: Then they sent to Him some of the Pharisees and the Herodians, to trap Him in His words.
41_12_14: When they had come, they said to Him, "Teacher, we know that You are truthful and care about no one; for You do not show partiality, but teach the way of God in truth. Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?"
41_12_15: Shall we pay, or shall we not pay?" But He, knowing their hypocrisy, said to them, "Why do you test Me? Bring Me a denarius that I may see it."
41_12_16: So they brought it. And He said to them, "Whose image and inscription is this?" They said to Him, "Caesar's."
41_12_17: And Jesus answered and said to them, "Render to Caesar the things that are Caesar's, and to God the things that are God's." And they marveled at Him.
41_12_18: Then some Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came to Him; and they asked Him, saying:
41_12_19: "Teacher, Moses wrote to us that if a man's brother dies, and leaves his wife behind, and leaves no children, his brother should take his wife and raise up offspring for his brother."
41_12_20: Now there were seven brothers. The first took a wife; and dying, he left no offspring.
41_12_21: And the second took her, and he died; nor did he leave any offspring. And the third likewise.
41_12_22: So the seven had her and left no offspring. Last of all the woman died also.
41_12_23: Therefore, in the resurrection, when they rise, whose wife will she be? For all seven had her as wife.
41_12_24: Jesus answered and said to them, "Are you not therefore mistaken, because you do not know the Scriptures nor the power of God?"
41_12_25: For when they rise from the dead, they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven.
41_12_26: But concerning the dead, that they rise, have you not read in the book of Moses, in the passage about the burning bush, how God spoke to him, saying, 'I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob'?
41_12_27: He is not the God of the dead, but the God of the living. You are therefore greatly mistaken."
41_12_28: Then one of the scribes came, and having heard them reasoning together, perceiving that He had answered them well, asked Him, "Which is the first commandment of all?"
41_12_29: Jesus answered him, "The first of all the commandments is: 'Hear, O Israel, the LORD our God, the LORD is one."
41_12_30: And you shall love the LORD your God with all your heart, with all your soul, with all your mind, and with all your strength.' This is the first commandment."
41_12_31: And the second, like it, is this: 'You shall love your neighbor as yourself.' There is no other commandment greater than these."
41_12_32: So the scribe said to Him, "Well said, Teacher. You have spoken the truth, for there is one God, and there is no other but He."
41_12_33: And to love Him with all the heart, with all the understanding, with all the soul, and with all the strength, and to love one's neighbor as oneself, is more than all the whole burnt offerings and sacrifices."
41_12_34: Now when Jesus saw that he answered wisely, He said to him, "You are not far from the kingdom of God." But after that no one dared question Him.
41_12_35: Then Jesus answered and said, while He taught in the temple, "How is it that the scribes say that the Christ is the Son of David?"
41_12_36: For David himself said by the Holy Spirit: 'The LORD said to my Lord, "Sit at My right hand, Till I make Your enemies Your footstool."'"
41_12_37: Therefore David himself calls Him 'Lord'; how is He then his Son?" And the common people heard Him gladly.
41_12_38: Then He said to them in His teaching, "Beware of the scribes, who desire to go around in long robes, love greetings in the marketplaces,"
41_12_39: the best seats in the synagogues, and the best places at feasts,"
41_12_40: who devour widows' houses, and for a pretense make long prayers. These will receive greater condemnation."
41_12_41: Now Jesus sat opposite the treasury and saw how the people put money into the treasury. And many who were rich put in much.
41_12_42: Then one poor widow came and threw in two mites, which make a quadrans.
41_12_43: So He called His disciples to Himself and said to them, "Assuredly, I say to you that this poor widow has put in more than all those who have given to the treasury;"
41_12_44: for they all put in out of their abundance, but she out of her poverty put in all that she had, her whole livelihood."
41_13_1: Then as He went out of the temple, one of His disciples said to Him, "Teacher, see what wonderful stones and what impressive buildings are here!"
41_13_2: And Jesus answered and said to him, "Do you see these great buildings? Not one stone shall be left upon another, that shall not be thrown down."
41_13_3: Now as He sat on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple, Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked Him privately,"
41_13_4: "Tell us, when will these things be? And what will be the sign when all these things will be fulfilled?"
41_13_5: And Jesus, answering them, began to say: "Take heed that no one deceives you."
41_13_6: For many will come in My name, saying, 'I am He,' and will deceive many."
41_13_7: But when you hear of wars and rumors of wars, do not be troubled; for such things must happen, but the end is not yet."
41_13_8: For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. And there will be earthquakes in various places, and there will be famines and troubles. These are the beginning of birth pains."
41_13_9: "But watch out for yourselves, for they will deliver you up to councils, and you will be beaten in the synagogues. You will be brought before rulers and kings for My sake, for a testimony to them."
41_13_10: And the gospel must first be preached to all the nations."
41_13_11: But when they arrest you and deliver you up, do not worry beforehand, or premeditate what you will speak. But whatever is given you in that hour, speak that; for it is not you who speak, but the Holy Spirit."
41_13_12: Now brother will betray brother to death, and a father his child; and children will rise up against parents and cause them to be put to death."
41_13_13: And you will be hated by all for My name's sake. But he who endures to the end shall be saved."
41_13_14: "So when you see the 'abomination of desolation,' spoken of by Daniel the prophet, standing where it ought not" (let the reader understand), "then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains."
41_13_15: Let him who is on the housetop not go down into the house, nor enter to take anything out of his house."
41_13_16: And let him who is in the field not go back to get his clothes."
41_13_17: But woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing babies in those days!"
41_13_18: And pray that your flight may not be in winter."
41_13_19: For in those days there will be tribulation, such as has not been since the beginning of the creation which God created until this time, nor ever shall be.
41_13_20: And unless the Lord had shortened those days, no flesh would be saved; but for the elect's sake, whom He chose, He shortened the days.
41_13_21: Then if anyone says to you, 'Look, here is the Christ!' or, 'Look, He is there!' do not believe it.
41_13_22: For false christs and false prophets will rise and show signs and wonders to deceive, if possible, even the elect.
41_13_23: But take heed; see, I have told you all things beforehand.
41_13_24: But in those days, after that tribulation, the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light;
41_13_25: and the stars of heaven will fall, and the powers in the heavens will be shaken.
41_13_26: Then they will see the Son of Man coming in the clouds with great power and glory.
41_13_27: And then He will send His angels, and gather together His elect from the four winds, from the farthest part of earth to the farthest part of heaven.
41_13_28: Now learn this parable from the fig tree: When its branch has already become tender and puts forth leaves, you know that summer is near.
41_13_29: So you also, when you see these things happening, know that it is near—at the doors!
41_13_30: Assuredly, I say to you, this generation will by no means pass away till all these things take place.
41_13_31: Heaven and earth will pass away, but My words will by no means pass away.
41_13_32: But of that day and hour no one knows, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.
41_13_33: Take heed, watch and pray; for you do not know when the time is.
41_13_34: It is like a man going to a far country, who left his house and gave authority to his servants, and to each his work, and commanded the doorkeeper to watch.
41_13_35: Watch therefore, for you do not know when the master of the house is coming—in the evening, at midnight, at the crowing of the rooster, or in the morning—
41_13_36: lest, coming suddenly, he find you sleeping.
41_13_37: And what I say to you, I say to all: Watch!
41_14_1: After two days it was the Passover and the Feast of Unleavened Bread. And the chief priests and the scribes sought how they might take Him by trickery and put Him to death.
41_14_2: But they said, "Not during the feast, lest there be an uproar of the people."
41_14_3: And being in Bethany at the house of Simon the leper, as He sat at the table, a woman came having an alabaster flask of very costly oil of spikenard. Then she broke the flask and poured it on His head.
41_14_4: But there were some who were indignant among themselves, and said, "Why was this fragrant oil wasted?
41_14_5: For it might have been sold for more than three hundred denarii and given to the poor." And they criticized her sharply.
41_14_6: But Jesus said, "Let her alone. Why do you trouble her? She has done a good work for Me.
41_14_7: For you have the poor with you always, and whenever you wish you may do them good; but Me you do not have always.
41_14_8: She has done what she could. She has come beforehand to anoint My body for burial.
41_14_9: Assuredly, I say to you, wherever this gospel is preached in the whole world, what this woman has done will also be told as a memorial to her."
41_14_10: Then Judas Iscariot, one of the twelve, went to the chief priests to betray Him to them.
41_14_11: And when they heard it, they were glad, and promised to give him money. So he sought how he might conveniently betray Him.
41_14_12: Now on the first day of Unleavened Bread, when they killed the Passover lamb, His disciples said to Him, "Where do You want us to go and prepare, that You may eat the Passover?"
41_14_13: And He sent out two of His disciples and said to them, "Go into the city, and a man will meet you carrying a pitcher of water; follow him.
41_14_14: Wherever he goes in, say to the master of the house, 'The Teacher says, "Where is the guest room in which I may eat the Passover with My disciples?"'"
41_14_15: Then he will show you a large upper room, furnished and prepared; there make ready for us."
41_14_16: So His disciples went out, and came into the city, and found it just as He had said to them; and they prepared the Passover.
41_14_17: In the evening He came with the twelve.
41_14_18: Now as they sat and ate, Jesus said, "Assuredly, I say to you, one of you who eats with Me will betray Me."
41_14_19: And they began to be sorrowful, and to say to Him one by one, "Is it I?" And another said, "Is it I?"
41_14_20: He answered and said to them, "It is one of the twelve, who dips with Me in the dish.
41_14_21: The Son of Man indeed goes just as it is written of Him, but woe to that man by whom the Son of Man is betrayed! It would have been good for that man if he had never been born."
41_14_22: And as they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed and broke it, and gave it to them and said, "Take, eat; this is My body."
41_14_23: Then He took the cup, and when He had given thanks He gave it to them, and they all drank from it.
41_14_24: And He said to them, "This is My blood of the new covenant, which is poured out for many."
41_14_25: Assuredly, I say to you, I will no longer drink of the fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it new in the kingdom of God."
41_14_26: And when they had sung a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
41_14_27: Then Jesus said to them, "All of you will fall away, for it is written: 'I will strike the Shepherd, And the sheep will be scattered.'"
41_14_28: "But after I have been raised, I will go before you to Galilee."
41_14_29: Peter said to Him, "Even if all are made to stumble, yet I will not be."
41_14_30: Jesus said to him, "Assuredly, I say to you that today, even this night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny Me three times."
41_14_31: But he spoke more vehemently, "If I have to die with You, I will not deny You!" And they all said likewise.
41_14_32: Then they came to a place which was named Gethsemane; and He said to His disciples, "Sit here while I pray."
41_14_33: And He took Peter, James, and John with Him, and He began to be troubled and deeply distressed.
41_14_34: Then He said to them, "My soul is exceedingly sorrowful, even to death. Stay here and watch."
41_14_35: He went a little farther, and fell on the ground, and prayed that if it were possible, the hour might pass from Him.
41_14_36: And He said, "Abba, Father, all things are possible for You. Take this cup away from Me; nevertheless, not what I will, but what You will."
41_14_37: Then He came and found them sleeping, and said to Peter, "Simon, are you sleeping? Could you not watch one hour?"
41_14_38: Watch and pray, lest you enter into temptation. The spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak."
41_14_39: Again He went away and prayed, and spoke the same words.
41_14_40: And when He returned, He found them asleep again, for their eyes were heavy; and they did not know what to answer Him.
41_14_41: Then He came the third time and said to them, "Are you still sleeping and resting? It is enough! The hour has come; behold, the Son of Man is being betrayed into the hands of sinners."
41_14_42: Rise, let us be going. See, My betrayer is at hand."
41_14_43: And immediately, while He was still speaking, Judas, one of the twelve, with a crowd with swords and clubs, came from the chief priests and the scribes and the elders.
41_14_44: Now His betrayer had given them a signal, saying, "The one I kiss, He is the One; seize Him and lead Him away safely."
41_14_45: As soon as he had come, immediately he went up to Him and said to Him, "Rabbi!" and kissed Him.
41_14_46: Then they laid their hands on Him and took Him.
41_14_47: And one of those who stood by drew his sword and struck the servant of the high priest, and cut off his ear.
41_14_48: Then Jesus answered and said to them, "Have you come out, as against a robber, with swords and clubs to take Me?"
41_14_49: I was daily with you in the temple teaching, and you did not seize Me. But the Scriptures must be fulfilled."
41_14_50: Then they all forsook Him and fled.
41_14_51: Now a certain young man followed Him, having a linen cloth thrown around his naked body. And the young men laid hold of him,
41_14_52: and he left the linen cloth and fled from them naked.
41_14_53: And they led Jesus away to the high priest; and with him were assembled all the chief priests, the elders, and the scribes.
41_14_54: But Peter followed Him at a distance, right into the courtyard of the high priest. And he sat with the servants and warmed himself at the fire.
41_14_55: Now the chief priests and all the council sought testimony against Jesus to put Him to death, but found none.
41_14_56: For many bore false witness against Him, but their testimonies did not agree.
41_14_57: Then some rose up and bore false witness against Him, saying,
41_14_58: "We heard Him say, 'I will destroy this temple made with hands, and within three days I will build another made without hands.'"
41_14_59: But not even then did their testimony agree.
41_14_60: And the high priest stood up in the midst and asked Jesus, saying, "Do You answer nothing? What is it these men testify against You?"
41_14_61: But He kept silent and answered nothing. Again the high priest asked Him, saying to Him, "Are You the Christ, the Son of the Blessed?"
41_14_62: Jesus said, "I am. And you will see the Son of Man seated at the right hand of Power, and coming with the clouds of heaven."
41_14_63: Then the high priest tore his clothes and said, "What further need do we have of witnesses?"
41_14_64: You have heard the blasphemy! What do you think?" And they all condemned Him as deserving of death.
41_14_65: Then some began to spit on Him, and to blindfold Him, and to beat Him, and to say to Him, "Prophesy!" And the officers struck Him with the palms of their hands.
41_14_66: Now as Peter was below in the courtyard, one of the servant girls of the high priest came.
41_14_67: And when she saw Peter warming himself, she looked at him and said, "You also were with Jesus of Nazareth."
41_14_68: But he denied it, saying, "I neither know nor understand what you are saying." And he went out on the porch, and a rooster crowed.
41_14_69: And the servant girl saw him again, and began to say to those who stood by, "This is one of them."
41_14_70: But he denied it again. And a little later those who stood by said to Peter again, "Surely you are one of them; for you are a Galilean, and your speech shows it."
41_14_71: Then he began to curse and swear, "I do not know this Man of whom you speak!"
41_14_72: A second time the rooster crowed. Then Peter called to mind the word that Jesus had said to him, "Before the rooster crows twice, you will deny Me three times." And when he thought about it, he wept.
41_15_1: Immediately, in the morning, the chief priests held a consultation with the elders and scribes and the whole council; and they bound Jesus, led Him away, and delivered Him to Pilate.
41_15_2: Then Pilate asked Him, "Are You the King of the Jews?" He answered and said to him, "It is as you say."
41_15_3: And the chief priests accused Him of many things, but He answered nothing.
41_15_4: Then Pilate asked Him again, saying, "Do You answer nothing? See how many things they testify against You!"
41_15_5: But Jesus still answered nothing, so that Pilate marveled.
41_15_6: Now at the feast he was accustomed to releasing one prisoner to them, whomever they requested.
41_15_7: And there was one named Barabbas, who was chained with his fellow rebels; they had committed murder in the rebellion.
41_15_8: Then the multitude, crying aloud, began to ask him to do just as he had always done for them.
41_15_9: But Pilate answered them, saying, "Do you want me to release to you the King of the Jews?"
41_15_10: For he knew that the chief priests had handed Him over because of envy.
41_15_11: But the chief priests stirred up the crowd, so that he should rather release Barabbas to them.
41_15_12: Pilate answered and said to them again, "What then do you want me to do with Him whom you call the King of the Jews?"
41_15_13: So they cried out again, "Crucify Him!"
41_15_14: Then Pilate said to them, "Why, what evil has He done?" But they cried out all the more, "Crucify Him!"
41_15_15: So Pilate, wanting to gratify the crowd, released Barabbas to them; and he delivered Jesus, after he had scourged Him, to be crucified.
41_15_16: Then the soldiers led Him away into the hall called Praetorium, and they called together the whole garrison.
41_15_17: And they clothed Him with purple; and they twisted a crown of thorns, put it on His head,
41_15_18: and began to salute Him, "Hail, King of the Jews!"
41_15_19: Then they struck Him on the head with a reed and spat on Him; and bowing the knee, they worshiped Him.
41_15_20: And when they had mocked Him, they took the purple off Him, put His own clothes on Him, and led Him out to crucify Him.
41_15_21: Then they compelled a certain man, Simon a Cyrenian, the father of Alexander and Rufus, as he was coming out of the country and passing by, to bear His cross.
41_15_22: And they brought Him to the place Golgotha, which is translated, Place of a Skull.
41_15_23: Then they gave Him wine mingled with myrrh to drink, but He did not take it.
41_15_24: And when they crucified Him, they divided His garments, casting lots for them to determine what every man should take.
41_15_25: Now it was the third hour, and they crucified Him.
41_15_26: And the inscription of His accusation was written above: THE KING OF THE JEWS.
41_15_27: With Him they also crucified two robbers, one on His right and the other on His left.
41_15_28: So the Scripture was fulfilled which says, "And He was numbered with the transgressors."
41_15_29: And those who passed by blasphemed Him, wagging their heads and saying, "Aha! You who destroy the temple and build it in three days,
41_15_30: save Yourself, and come down from the cross!
41_15_31: Likewise the chief priests also, mocking among themselves with the scribes, said, "He saved others; Himself He cannot save."
41_15_32: Let the Christ, the King of Israel, descend now from the cross, that we may see and believe." Even those who were crucified with Him reviled Him.
41_15_33: Now when the sixth hour had come, there was darkness over the whole land until the ninth hour.
41_15_34: And at the ninth hour Jesus cried out with a loud voice, saying, "Eloi, Eloi, lama sabachthani?" which is translated, "My God, My God, why have You forsaken Me?"
41_15_35: Some of those who stood by, when they heard that, said, "Look, He is calling for Elijah!"
41_15_36: Then someone ran and filled a sponge full of sour wine, put it on a reed, and offered it to Him to drink, saying, "Let Him alone; let us see if Elijah will come to take Him down."
41_15_37: And Jesus cried out with a loud voice, and breathed His last.
41_15_38: Then the veil of the temple was torn in two from top to bottom.
41_15_39: So when the centurion, who stood opposite Him, saw that He cried out like this and breathed His last, he said, "Truly this Man was the Son of God!"
41_15_40: There were also women looking on from afar, among whom were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James the Less and of Joses, and Salome,
41_15_41: who also followed Him and ministered to Him when He was in Galilee, and many other women who came up with Him to Jerusalem.
41_15_42: Now when evening had come, because it was the Preparation Day, that is, the day before the Sabbath,
41_15_43: Joseph of Arimathea, a prominent council member, who was himself waiting for the kingdom of God, took courage and went in to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.
41_15_44: Pilate marveled that He was already dead; and summoning the centurion, he asked him if He had been dead for some time.
41_15_45: So when he found out from the centurion, he granted the body to Joseph.
41_15_46: Then he bought fine linen, took Him down, and wrapped Him in the linen. And he laid Him in a tomb which had been hewn out of the rock, and rolled a stone against the door of the tomb.
41_15_47: And Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses observed where He was laid.
41_16_1: Now when the Sabbath was past, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices, that they might come and anoint Him.
41_16_2: Very early in the morning, on the first day of the week, they came to the tomb when the sun had risen.
41_16_3: And they said among themselves, "Who will roll away the stone from the door of the tomb for us?"
41_16_4: But when they looked up, they saw that the stone had been rolled away—for it was very large.
41_16_5: And entering the tomb, they saw a young man clothed in a long white robe sitting on the right side; and they were alarmed.
41_16_6: But he said to them, "Do not be alarmed. You seek Jesus of Nazareth, who was crucified. He is risen! He is not here. See the place where they laid Him."
41_16_7: But go, tell His disciples—and Peter—that He is going before you into Galilee; there you will see Him, as He said to you."
41_16_8: So they went out quickly and fled from the tomb, for they trembled and were amazed. And they said nothing to anyone, for they were afraid.
41_16_9: Now when He rose early on the first day of the week, He appeared first to Mary Magdalene, out of whom He had cast seven demons.
41_16_10: She went and told those who had been with Him, as they mourned and wept.
41_16_11: And when they heard that He was alive and had been seen by her, they did not believe.
41_16_12: After that, He appeared in another form to two of them as they walked and went into the country.
41_16_13: And they went and told it to the rest, but they did not believe them either.
41_16_14: Later He appeared to the eleven as they sat at the table; and He rebuked their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they did not believe those who had seen Him after He had risen.
41_16_15: And He said to them, "Go into all the world and preach the gospel to every creature."
41_16_16: He who believes and is baptized will be saved; but he who does not believe will be condemned.
41_16_17: And these signs will follow those who believe: In My name they will cast out demons; they will speak with new tongues;
41_16_18: they will take up serpents; and if they drink anything deadly, it will by no means hurt them; they will lay hands on the sick, and they will recover."
41_16_19: So then, after the Lord had spoken to them, He was received up into heaven, and sat down at the right hand of God.
41_16_20: And they went out and preached everywhere, the Lord working with them and confirming the word through the accompanying signs.
42_1_1: Inasmuch as many have undertaken to compile a narrative of those things which have been fulfilled among us,
42_1_2: just as those who from the beginning were eyewitnesses and ministers of the word delivered them to us,
42_1_3: it seemed good to me also, having carefully investigated everything from the very first, to write to you an orderly account, most excellent Theophilus,
42_1_4: that you may know the certainty of those things in which you were instructed.
42_1_5: There was in the days of Herod, the king of Judea, a certain priest named Zacharias, of the division of Abijah. His wife was of the daughters of Aaron, and her name was Elizabeth.
42_1_6: And they were both righteous before God, walking blamelessly in all the commandments and ordinances of the Lord.
42_1_7: But they had no child, because Elizabeth was barren, and they were both well advanced in years.
42_1_8: So it was, that while he was serving as priest before God in the order of his division,
42_1_9: according to the custom of the priesthood, his lot fell to burn incense when he went into the temple of the Lord.
42_1_10: And the whole multitude of the people was praying outside at the hour of incense.
42_1_11: Then an angel of the Lord appeared to him, standing on the right side of the altar of incense.
42_1_12: And when Zacharias saw him, he was troubled, and fear fell upon him.
42_1_13: But the angel said to him, "Do not be afraid, Zacharias, for your prayer is heard; and your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you shall call his name John."
42_1_14: And you will have joy and gladness, and many will rejoice at his birth.
42_1_15: For he will be great in the sight of the Lord, and shall drink neither wine nor strong drink. He will also be filled with the Holy Spirit, even from his mother's womb.
42_1_16: And he will turn many of the children of Israel to the Lord their God.
42_1_17: He will also go before Him in the spirit and power of Elijah, 'to turn the hearts of the fathers to the children,' and the disobedient to the wisdom of the just, to make ready a people prepared for the Lord."
42_1_18: And Zacharias said to the angel, "How shall I know this? For I am an old man, and my wife is well advanced in years."
42_1_19: And the angel answered and said to him, "I am Gabriel, who stands in the presence of God, and was sent to speak to you and bring you these glad tidings."
42_1_20: But behold, you will be mute and not able to speak until the day these things take place, because you did not believe my words which will be fulfilled in their own time."
42_1_21: And the people waited for Zacharias, and marveled that he lingered so long in the temple.
42_1_22: But when he came out, he could not speak to them; and they perceived that he had seen a vision in the temple, for he beckoned to them and remained speechless.
42_1_23: So it was, as soon as the days of his service were completed, that he departed to his own house.
42_1_24: Now after those days his wife Elizabeth conceived; and she hid herself five months, saying,
42_1_25: "Thus the Lord has dealt with me, in the days when He looked on me, to take away my reproach among people."
42_1_26: Now in the sixth month the angel Gabriel was sent by God to a city of Galilee named Nazareth,
42_1_27: to a virgin betrothed to a man whose name was Joseph, of the house of David. The virgin's name was Mary.
42_1_28: And having come in, the angel said to her, "Greetings, favored one, the Lord is with you!"
42_1_29: But when she saw him, she was troubled at his saying, and considered what manner of greeting this was.
42_1_30: Then the angel said to her, "Do not be afraid, Mary, for you have found favor with God."
42_1_31: And behold, you will conceive in your womb and bring forth a Son, and shall call His name JESUS.
42_1_32: He will be great, and will be called the Son of the Highest; and the Lord God will give Him the throne of His father David.
42_1_33: And He will reign over the house of Jacob forever, and of His kingdom there will be no end."
42_1_34: Then Mary said to the angel, "How can this be, since I do not know a man?"
42_1_35: And the angel answered and said to her, "The Holy Spirit will come upon you, and the power of the Highest will overshadow you; therefore, also, that Holy One who is to be born will be called the Son of God."
42_1_36: Now indeed, Elizabeth your relative has also conceived a son in her old age; and this is now the sixth month for her who was called barren.
42_1_37: For with God nothing will be impossible."
42_1_38: Then Mary said, "Behold the maidservant of the Lord! Let it be to me according to your word." And the angel departed from her.
42_1_39: Now Mary arose in those days and went into the hill country with haste, to a city of Judah,
42_1_40: and entered the house of Zacharias and greeted Elizabeth.
42_1_41: And it happened, when Elizabeth heard the greeting of Mary, that the baby leaped in her womb; and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.
42_1_42: Then she spoke out with a loud voice and said, "Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the fruit of your womb!"
42_1_43: But why is this granted to me, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?
42_1_44: For indeed, as soon as the sound of your greeting reached my ears, the baby leaped in my womb for joy.
42_1_45: Blessed is she who believed, for there will be a fulfillment of those things which were told her from the Lord."
42_1_46: And Mary said: "My soul magnifies the Lord,
42_1_47: And my spirit has rejoiced in God my Savior."
42_1_48: For He has regarded the lowly state of His maidservant; For behold, henceforth all generations will call me blessed.
42_1_49: For He who is mighty has done great things for me, And holy is His name.
42_1_50: And His mercy is on those who fear Him From generation to generation."
42_1_51: He has shown strength with His arm; He has scattered the proud in the imagination of their hearts.
42_1_52: He has put down the mighty from their thrones, And exalted the lowly.
42_1_53: He has filled the hungry with good things, And the rich He has sent away empty.
42_1_54: He has helped His servant Israel, In remembrance of His mercy,
42_1_55: As He spoke to our fathers, To Abraham and to his seed forever."
42_1_56: And Mary remained with her about three months, and returned to her house.
42_1_57: Now Elizabeth's full time came for her to be delivered, and she brought forth a son.
42_1_58: When her neighbors and relatives heard how the Lord had shown great mercy to her, they rejoiced with her.
42_1_59: So it was, on the eighth day, that they came to circumcise the child; and they would have called him by the name of his father, Zacharias.
42_1_60: His mother answered and said, "No; he shall be called John."
42_1_61: But they said to her, "There is no one among your relatives who is called by this name."
42_1_62: So they made signs to his father—what he would have him called.
42_1_63: And he asked for a writing tablet, and wrote, saying, "His name is John." So they all marveled.
42_1_64: Immediately his mouth was opened and his tongue loosed, and he spoke, praising God.
42_1_65: Then fear came on all who dwelt around them; and all these sayings were discussed throughout all the hill country of Judea.
42_1_66: And all those who heard them kept them in their hearts, saying, "What kind of child will this be?" And the hand of the Lord was with him.
42_1_67: Now his father Zacharias was filled with the Holy Spirit, and prophesied, saying:
42_1_68: "Blessed is the Lord God of Israel, For He has visited and redeemed His people,
42_1_69: And has raised up a horn of salvation for us In the house of His servant David,
42_1_70: As He spoke by the mouth of His holy prophets, Who have been since the world began,
42_1_71: That we should be saved from our enemies And from the hand of all who hate us,
42_1_72: To perform the mercy promised to our fathers And to remember His holy covenant,
42_1_73: The oath which He swore to our father Abraham:
42_1_74: To grant us that we, Being delivered from the hand of our enemies, Might serve Him without fear,
42_1_75: In holiness and righteousness before Him all the days of our life.
42_1_76: "And you, child, will be called the prophet of the Highest; For you will go before the face of the Lord to prepare His ways,
42_1_77: To give knowledge of salvation to His people By the remission of their sins,
42_1_78: Through the tender mercy of our God, With which the Dayspring from on high has visited us;
42_1_79: To give light to those who sit in darkness and the shadow of death, To guide our feet into the way of peace."
42_1_80: So the child grew and became strong in spirit, and was in the deserts till the day of his manifestation to Israel.
42_2_1: And it came to pass in those days that a decree went out from Caesar Augustus that all the world should be registered.
42_2_2: This census first took place while Quirinius was governing Syria.
42_2_3: So all went to be registered, everyone to his own city.
42_2_4: Joseph also went up from Galilee, out of the city of Nazareth, into Judea, to the city of David, which is called Bethlehem, because he was of the house and lineage of David,
42_2_5: to be registered with Mary, his betrothed wife, who was with child.
42_2_6: So it was, that while they were there, the days were completed for her to be delivered.
42_2_7: And she gave birth to her firstborn Son, and wrapped Him in swaddling cloths, and laid Him in a manger, because there was no room for them in the inn.
42_2_8: Now there were in the same country shepherds staying out in the fields, keeping watch over their flock by night.
42_2_9: And behold, an angel of the Lord appeared to them, and the glory of the Lord shone around them, and they were greatly afraid.
42_2_10: Then the angel said to them, "Do not be afraid, for behold, I bring you good tidings of great joy which will be to all people.
42_2_11: For there is born to you this day in the city of David a Savior, who is Christ the Lord.
42_2_12: And this will be the sign to you: You will find a Babe wrapped in swaddling cloths, lying in a manger."
42_2_13: And suddenly there was with the angel a multitude of the heavenly host praising God and saying:
42_2_14: "Glory to God in the highest, And on earth peace, goodwill toward men!"
42_2_15: So it was, when the angels had gone away from them into heaven, that the shepherds said to one another, "Let us now go to Bethlehem and see this thing that has come to pass, which the Lord has made known to us."
42_2_16: And they came with haste and found Mary and Joseph, and the Babe lying in a manger.
42_2_17: Now when they had seen Him, they made widely known the saying which was told them concerning this Child.
42_2_18: And all those who heard it marveled at those things which were told them by the shepherds.
42_2_19: But Mary treasured all these things and pondered them in her heart.
42_2_20: Then the shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all the things that they had heard and seen, as it was told them.
42_2_21: And when eight days were completed for the circumcision of the Child, His name was called JESUS, the name given by the angel before He was conceived in the womb.
42_2_22: Now when the days of her purification according to the law of Moses were completed, they brought Him to Jerusalem to present Him to the Lord
42_2_23: (as it is written in the law of the Lord, "Every male who opens the womb shall be called holy to the Lord"),
42_2_24: and to offer a sacrifice according to what is said in the law of the Lord, "A pair of turtledoves or two young pigeons."
42_2_25: And behold, there was a man in Jerusalem whose name was Simeon, and this man was righteous and devout, waiting for the Consolation of Israel, and the Holy Spirit was upon him.
42_2_26: And it had been revealed to him by the Holy Spirit that he would not see death before he had seen the Lord's Christ.
42_2_27: So he came by the Spirit into the temple. And when the parents brought in the Child Jesus, to do for Him according to the custom of the law,
42_2_28: he took Him up in his arms and blessed God and said:
42_2_29: "Lord, now You are letting Your servant depart in peace, According to Your word;
42_2_30: For my eyes have seen Your salvation
42_2_31: Which You have prepared before the face of all peoples,
42_2_32: A light to bring revelation to the Gentiles, And the glory of Your people Israel."
42_2_33: And Joseph and His mother marveled at those things which were spoken of Him.
42_2_34: Then Simeon blessed them, and said to Mary His mother, "Behold, this Child is destined for the fall and rising of many in Israel, and for a sign which will be spoken against
42_2_35: (yes, a sword will pierce through your own soul also), that the thoughts of many hearts may be revealed."
42_2_36: Now there was one, Anna, a prophetess, the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher. She was advanced in years and had lived with a husband seven years after her marriage;
42_2_37: and she was a widow of about eighty-four years, who did not depart from the temple, but served God with fastings and prayers night and day.
42_2_38: And coming in that instant she gave thanks to the Lord, and spoke of Him to all those who looked for redemption in Jerusalem.
42_2_39: So when they had performed all things according to the law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee, to their own city, Nazareth.
42_2_40: And the Child grew and became strong in spirit, filled with wisdom; and the grace of God was upon Him.
42_2_41: His parents went to Jerusalem every year at the Feast of the Passover.
42_2_42: And when He was twelve years old, they went up to Jerusalem according to the custom of the feast.
42_2_43: When they had finished the days, as they were returning, the Boy Jesus lingered behind in Jerusalem. And Joseph and His mother did not know it;
42_2_44: but supposing Him to have been in the company, they went a day's journey, and sought Him among their relatives and acquaintances.
42_2_45: So when they did not find Him, they returned to Jerusalem, seeking Him.
42_2_46: Now so it was that after three days they found Him in the temple, sitting in the midst of the teachers, both listening to them and asking them questions.
42_2_47: And all who heard Him were astonished at His understanding and answers.
42_2_48: So when they saw Him, they were amazed; and His mother said to Him, "Son, why have You done this to us? Look, Your father and I have sought You anxiously."
42_2_49: And He said to them, "Why did you seek Me? Did you not know that I must be in My Father's house?"
42_2_50: But they did not understand the statement which He spoke to them.
42_2_51: Then He went down with them and came to Nazareth, and was subject to them, but His mother kept all these things in her heart.
42_2_52: And Jesus increased in wisdom and stature, and in favor with God and men.
42_3_1: Now in the fifteenth year of the reign of Tiberius Caesar, Pontius Pilate being governor of Judea, Herod being tetrarch of Galilee, his brother Philip tetrarch of Iturea and the region of Trachonitis, and Lysanias tetrarch of Abilene,
42_3_2: while Annas and Caiaphas were high priests, the word of God came to John the son of Zacharias in the wilderness.
42_3_3: And he went into all the region around the Jordan, preaching a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins,
42_3_4: as it is written in the book of the words of Isaiah the prophet, saying: "The voice of one crying in the wilderness: 'Prepare the way of the LORD; Make His paths straight.
42_3_5: Every valley shall be filled And every mountain and hill brought low; The crooked places shall be made straight And the rough ways smooth;
42_3_6: And all flesh shall see the salvation of God.'"
42_3_7: Then he said to the multitudes that came out to be baptized by him, "Brood of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?
42_3_8: Therefore bear fruits worthy of repentance, and do not begin to say to yourselves, 'We have Abraham as our father.' For I say to you that God is able to raise up children to Abraham from these stones.
42_3_9: And even now the ax is laid to the root of the trees. Therefore every tree which does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire."
42_3_10: So the people asked him, saying, "What shall we do then?"
42_3_11: He answered and said to them, "He who has two tunics, let him give to him who has none; and he who has food, let him do likewise."
42_3_12: Then tax collectors also came to be baptized, and said to him, "Teacher, what shall we do?"
42_3_13: And he said to them, "Collect no more than what is appointed for you."
42_3_14: Likewise the soldiers asked him, saying, "And what shall we do?" So he said to them, "Do not intimidate anyone or accuse falsely, and be content with your wages."
42_3_15: Now as the people were in expectation, and all reasoned in their hearts about John, whether he was the Christ or not,
42_3_16: John answered, saying to all, "I indeed baptize you with water; but One mightier than I is coming, whose sandal strap I am not worthy to loose. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
42_3_17: His winnowing fan is in His hand, and He will thoroughly clean out His threshing floor, and gather the wheat into His barn; but the chaff He will burn with unquenchable fire."
42_3_18: And with many other exhortations he preached to the people.
42_3_19: But Herod the tetrarch, being rebuked by him concerning Herodias, his brother Philip's wife, and for all the evils which Herod had done,
42_3_20: also added this, above all, that he shut John up in prison.
42_3_21: When all the people were baptized, it came to pass that Jesus also was baptized; and while He prayed, the heaven was opened.
42_3_22: And the Holy Spirit descended in bodily form like a dove upon Him, and a voice came from heaven which said, "You are My beloved Son; in You I am well pleased."
42_3_23: Now Jesus Himself began His ministry at about thirty years of age, being (as was supposed) the son of Joseph, the son of Heli,
42_3_24: the son of Matthat, the son of Levi, the son of Melchi, the son of Janna, the son of Joseph,
42_3_25: the son of Mattathiah, the son of Amos, the son of Nahum, the son of Esli, the son of Naggai,
42_3_26: the son of Maath, the son of Mattathiah, the son of Semei, the son of Joseph, the son of Judah,
42_3_27: the son of Joannas, the son of Rhesa, the son of Zerubbabel, the son of Shealtiel, the son of Neri,
42_3_28: the son of Melchi, the son of Addi, the son of Cosam, the son of Elmodam, the son of Er,
42_3_29: the son of Joshua, the son of Eliezer, the son of Jorim, the son of Matthat, the son of Levi,
42_3_30: the son of Simeon, the son of Judah, the son of Joseph, the son of Jonam, the son of Eliakim,
42_3_31: the son of Melea, the son of Menna, the son of Mattatha, the son of Nathan, the son of David,
42_3_32: the son of Jesse, the son of Obed, the son of Boaz, the son of Salmon, the son of Nahshon,
42_3_33: the son of Amminadab, the son of Admin, the son of Ram, the son of Hezron, the son of Perez, the son of Judah,
42_3_34: the son of Jacob, the son of Isaac, the son of Abraham, the son of Terah, the son of Nahor,
42_3_35: the son of Serug, the son of Reu, the son of Peleg, the son of Eber, the son of Shelah,
42_3_36: the son of Cainan, the son of Arphaxad, the son of Shem, the son of Noah, the son of Lamech,
42_3_37: the son of Methuselah, the son of Enoch, the son of Jared, the son of Mahalalel, the son of Cainan,
42_3_38: the son of Enosh, the son of Seth, the son of Adam, the son of God.
42_4_1: Then Jesus, being filled with the Holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan and was led by the Spirit into the wilderness,
42_4_2: being tempted for forty days by the devil. And in those days He ate nothing, and afterward, when they had ended, He was hungry.
42_4_3: And the devil said to Him, "If You are the Son of God, command this stone to become bread."
42_4_4: But Jesus answered him, saying, "It is written, 'Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word of God.'"
42_4_5: Then the devil, taking Him up on a high mountain, showed Him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment of time.
42_4_6: And the devil said to Him, "All this authority I will give You, and their glory; for this has been delivered to me, and I give it to whomever I wish.
42_4_7: Therefore, if You will worship before me, all will be Yours."
42_4_8: And Jesus answered and said to him, "Get behind Me, Satan! For it is written, 'You shall worship the LORD your God, and Him only you shall serve.'"
42_4_9: Then he brought Him to Jerusalem, set Him on the pinnacle of the temple, and said to Him, "If You are the Son of God, throw Yourself down from here.
42_4_10: For it is written: 'He shall give His angels charge over you, To keep you,'
42_4_11: and, 'In their hands they shall bear you up, Lest you dash your foot against a stone.'"
42_4_12: And Jesus answered and said to him, "It has been said, 'You shall not tempt the LORD your God.'"
42_4_13: Now when the devil had ended every temptation, he departed from Him until an opportune time.
42_4_14: Then Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit to Galilee, and news of Him went out through all the surrounding region.
42_4_15: And He taught in their synagogues, being glorified by all.
42_4_16: So He came to Nazareth, where He had been brought up. And as His custom was, He went into the synagogue on the Sabbath day, and stood up to read.
42_4_17: And He was handed the book of the prophet Isaiah. And when He had opened the book, He found the place where it was written:
42_4_18: "The Spirit of the LORD is upon Me, Because He has anointed Me To preach the gospel to the poor; He has sent Me to proclaim liberty to the captives And recovery of sight to the blind, To set at liberty those who are oppressed;
42_4_19: To proclaim the acceptable year of the LORD."
42_4_20: Then He closed the book, and gave it back to the attendant and sat down. And the eyes of all who were in the synagogue were fixed on Him.
42_4_21: And He began to say to them, "Today this Scripture is fulfilled in your hearing."
42_4_22: So all spoke well of Him, and marveled at the gracious words which proceeded out of His mouth. And they said, "Is this not Joseph's son?"
42_4_23: He said to them, "You will surely say this proverb to Me, 'Physician, heal yourself! Whatever we have heard done in Capernaum, do also here in Your country.'"
42_4_24: Then He said, "Assuredly, I say to you, no prophet is accepted in his own country.
42_4_25: But I tell you truly, many widows were in Israel in the days of Elijah, when the heaven was shut up three years and six months, and there was a great famine throughout all the land;
42_4_26: but to none of them was Elijah sent except to Zarephath, in the region of Sidon, to a woman who was a widow.
42_4_27: And many lepers were in Israel in the time of Elisha the prophet, and none of them was cleansed except Naaman the Syrian."
42_4_28: So all those in the synagogue, when they heard these things, were filled with wrath,
42_4_29: and rose up and thrust Him out of the city; and they led Him to the brow of the hill on which their city was built, that they might throw Him down over the cliff.
42_4_30: Then passing through the midst of them, He went His way.
42_4_31: Then He went down to Capernaum, a city of Galilee, and was teaching them on the Sabbaths.
42_4_32: And they were astonished at His teaching, for His word possessed authority.
42_4_33: Now in the synagogue there was a man who had the spirit of an unclean demon. And he cried out with a loud voice,
42_4_34: saying, "Let us alone! What have we to do with You, Jesus of Nazareth? Did You come to destroy us? I know who You are—the Holy One of God!"
42_4_35: But Jesus rebuked him, saying, "Be quiet and come out of him!" And when the demon had thrown him down in their midst, it came out of him and did not hurt him.
42_4_36: Then they were all amazed and spoke among themselves, saying, "What a word this is! For with authority and power He commands the unclean spirits, and they come out."
42_4_37: And the report about Him went out into every place in the surrounding region.
42_4_38: Now He arose from the synagogue and entered Simon's house. But Simon's wife's mother was sick with a high fever, and they made request of Him concerning her.
42_4_39: So He stood over her and rebuked the fever, and it left her. And immediately she arose and served them.
42_4_40: When the sun was setting, all those who had any that were sick with various diseases brought them to Him; and He laid His hands on every one of them and healed them.
42_4_41: And demons also came out of many, crying out and saying, "You are the Christ, the Son of God!" And He, rebuking them, did not allow them to speak, for they knew that He was the Christ.
42_4_42: Now when it was day, He departed and went into a deserted place. And the crowd sought Him and came to Him and tried to keep Him from leaving them;
42_4_43: but He said to them, "I must preach the kingdom of God to the other cities also, because for this purpose I have been sent."
42_4_44: And He was preaching in the synagogues of Galilee.
42_5_1: So it was, as the multitude pressed about Him to hear the word of God, that He stood by the Lake of Gennesaret,
42_5_2: and saw two boats standing by the lake; but the fishermen had gone from them and were washing their nets.
42_5_3: Then He got into one of the boats, which was Simon's, and asked him to put out a little from the land. And He sat down and taught the multitudes from the boat.
42_5_4: When He had finished speaking, He said to Simon, "Launch out into the deep and let down your nets for a catch."
42_5_5: But Simon answered and said to Him, "Master, we have toiled all night and caught nothing; nevertheless at Your word I will let down the net."
42_5_6: And when they had done this, they caught a great number of fish, and their net was beginning to break.
42_5_7: So they signaled to their partners in the other boat to come and help them. And they came and filled both the boats, so that they began to sink.
42_5_8: When Simon Peter saw it, he fell down at Jesus' knees, saying, "Depart from me, for I am a sinful man, O Lord!"
42_5_9: For he and all who were with him were astonished at the catch of fish which they had taken;
42_5_10: and so also were James and John, the sons of Zebedee, who were partners with Simon. And Jesus said to Simon, "Do not be afraid. From now on you will catch men."
42_5_11: So when they had brought their boats to land, they forsook all and followed Him.
42_5_12: And it happened when He was in a certain city, that behold, a man who was full of leprosy saw Jesus; and he fell on his face and implored Him, saying, "Lord, if You are willing, You can make me clean."
42_5_13: Then He put out His hand and touched him, saying, "I am willing; be cleansed." Immediately the leprosy left him.
42_5_14: And He charged him to tell no one, "But go and show yourself to the priest, and make an offering for your cleansing, as a testimony to them, just as Moses commanded."
42_5_15: However, the report went around concerning Him all the more; and great multitudes came together to hear, and to be healed by Him of their infirmities.
42_5_16: So He Himself often withdrew into the wilderness and prayed.
42_5_17: Now it happened on a certain day, as He was teaching, that there were Pharisees and teachers of the law sitting there, who had come out of every village of Galilee, Judea, and Jerusalem. And the power of the Lord was present to heal them.
42_5_18: Then behold, men brought on a bed a man who was paralyzed, whom they sought to bring in and lay before Him.
42_5_19: And when they could not find how they might bring him in, because of the crowd, they went up on the housetop and let him down with his stretcher through the tiling into the midst before Jesus.
42_5_20: When He saw their faith, He said to him, "Man, your sins are forgiven you."
42_5_21: And the scribes and the Pharisees began to reason, saying, "Who is this who speaks blasphemies? Who can forgive sins but God alone?"
42_5_22: But when Jesus perceived their thoughts, He answered and said to them, "Why are you reasoning in your hearts?"
42_5_23: Which is easier, to say, 'Your sins are forgiven you,' or to say, 'Rise up and walk'?
42_5_24: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins"—He said to the man who was paralyzed, "I say to you, arise, take up your bed, and go to your house."
42_5_25: Immediately he rose up before them, took up what he had been lying on, and departed to his own house, glorifying God.
42_5_26: And they were all amazed, and they glorified God and were filled with awe, saying, "We have seen extraordinary things today!"
42_5_27: After these things He went out and saw a tax collector named Levi, sitting at the tax office. And He said to him, "Follow Me."
42_5_28: So he left everything, rose up, and followed Him.
42_5_29: Then Levi gave Him a great banquet in his own house. And there were a great number of tax collectors and others who sat down with them.
42_5_30: And the Pharisees and their scribes complained against His disciples, saying, "Why do You eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners?"
42_5_31: Jesus answered and said to them, "Those who are well have no need of a physician, but those who are sick.
42_5_32: I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners, to repentance."
42_5_33: Then they said to Him, "Why do the disciples of John fast often and make prayers, and likewise those of the Pharisees, but Yours eat and drink?"
42_5_34: And He said to them, "Can you make the friends of the bridegroom fast while the bridegroom is with them?
42_5_35: But the days will come when the bridegroom will be taken away from them; then they will fast in those days."
42_5_36: Then He spoke a parable to them: "No one tears a piece from a new garment and puts it on an old one; otherwise the new makes a tear, and also the piece from the new does not match the old.
42_5_37: And no one puts new wine into old wineskins; or else the new wine will burst the wineskins and be spilled, and the wineskins will be ruined.
42_5_38: But new wine must be put into fresh wineskins, and both are preserved.
42_5_39: And no one, having drunk old wine, immediately desires new; for he says, 'The old is better.'"
42_6_1: Now it came to pass on a Sabbath that He went through the grainfields. And His disciples plucked the heads of grain and ate them, rubbing them in their hands.
42_6_2: And some of the Pharisees said to them, "Why are you doing what is not lawful to do on the Sabbath?"
42_6_3: But Jesus answering them said, "Have you not even read what David did when he was hungry, he and those who were with him:
42_6_4: how he went into the house of God, took and ate the bread of the Presence, which is not lawful for any but the priests to eat, and also gave some to those with him?"
42_6_5: And He said to them, "The Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath."
42_6_6: Now it came to pass on another Sabbath, also, that He entered the synagogue and taught. And a man was there whose right hand was withered.
42_6_7: So the scribes and Pharisees watched Him closely, whether He would heal on the Sabbath, so that they might find an accusation against Him.
42_6_8: But He knew their thoughts, and said to the man who had the withered hand, "Arise and stand here." And he arose and stood.
42_6_9: Then Jesus said to them, "I will ask you one thing: Is it lawful on the Sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to destroy?"
42_6_10: And when He had looked around at them all, He said to the man, "Stretch out your hand." And he did so, and his hand was restored.
42_6_11: But they were filled with rage and discussed with one another what they might do to Jesus.
42_6_12: Now it came to pass in those days that He went out to the mountain to pray, and continued all night in prayer to God.
42_6_13: And when it was day, He called His disciples to Himself; and from them He chose twelve whom He also named apostles:
42_6_14: Simon, whom He also named Peter, and Andrew his brother; James and John; Philip and Bartholomew;
42_6_15: Matthew and Thomas; James the son of Alphaeus, and Simon called the Zealot;
42_6_16: Judas the son of James, and Judas Iscariot who also became a traitor.
42_6_17: And He came down with them and stood on a level place with a great crowd of His disciples and a great multitude of people from all Judea and Jerusalem, and from the seacoast of Tyre and Sidon, who came to hear Him and be healed of their diseases,
42_6_18: as well as those who were tormented with unclean spirits. And they were healed.
42_6_19: And the whole multitude sought to touch Him, for power went out from Him and healed them all.
42_6_20: Then He lifted up His eyes toward His disciples, and said: "Blessed are you poor, for yours is the kingdom of God.
42_6_21: Blessed are you who hunger now, for you shall be filled. Blessed are you who weep now, for you shall laugh.
42_6_22: Blessed are you when men hate you, and when they exclude you, and revile you, and cast out your name as evil, for the Son of Man's sake.
42_6_23: Rejoice in that day and leap for joy! For indeed your reward is great in heaven, for in like manner their fathers did to the prophets.
42_6_24: But woe to you who are rich, for you have received your consolation.
42_6_25: Woe to you who are full, for you shall hunger. Woe to you who laugh now, for you shall mourn and weep.
42_6_26: Woe to you when all men speak well of you, for so did their fathers to the false prophets."
42_6_27: But I say to you who hear: Love your enemies, do good to those who hate you,
42_6_28: bless those who curse you, and pray for those who mistreat you.
42_6_29: To him who strikes you on the one cheek, offer the other also. And from him who takes away your cloak, do not withhold your tunic either.
42_6_30: Give to everyone who asks of you. And from him who takes away your goods do not ask them back.
42_6_31: And just as you want men to do to you, you also do to them likewise.
42_6_32: But if you love those who love you, what credit is that to you? For even sinners love those who love them.
42_6_33: And if you do good to those who do good to you, what credit is that to you? For even sinners do the same.
42_6_34: And if you lend to those from whom you hope to receive back, what credit is that to you? For even sinners lend to sinners to receive as much back.
