44_7_23: When he was forty years old, it came into his heart to visit his own people, the Israelites.
44_7_24: And seeing one of them being mistreated, he came to his rescue and avenged the oppressed man by striking down the Egyptian.
44_7_25: For he supposed that his brothers would understand that God was granting them deliverance through him, but they did not understand.
44_7_26: The next day he showed up while they were fighting and tried to reconcile them peacefully, saying, ‘Men, you are brothers. Why are you mistreating each other?’
44_7_27: But the one who was mistreating his neighbor pushed Moses aside, saying, ‘Who appointed you a ruler and a judge over us?
44_7_28: Do you want to kill me, the same way you killed the Egyptian yesterday?
44_7_29: When he heard this, Moses fled and became an exile in the land of Midian, where he became the father of two sons.
44_7_30: After forty years had passed, an angel appeared to him in the wilderness of Mount Sinai, in the flame of a burning bush.
44_7_31: When Moses saw it, he was amazed at the sight. As he was approaching to look at it, the voice of the Lord came:
44_7_32: saying, ‘I am the God of your ancestors — the God of Abraham, of Isaac, and of Jacob.’ Moses began to tremble and did not dare to look.
44_7_33: Then the Lord said to him, ‘Take off the sandals from your feet, because the place where you are standing is holy ground.
44_7_34: I have certainly seen the oppression of my people in Egypt; I have heard their groaning and have come down to set them free. And now, come, I will send you to Egypt.
44_7_35: This Moses, whom they rejected when they said, Who appointed you a ruler and a judge? — this one God sent as a ruler and a deliverer through the angel who appeared to him in the bush.
44_7_36: This man led them out and performed wonders and signs in the land of Egypt, at the Red Sea, and in the wilderness for forty years.
44_7_37: This is the Moses who said to the Israelites: God will raise up for you a prophet like me from among your brothers.
44_7_38: He is the one who was in the assembly in the wilderness, with the angel who spoke to him at Mount Sinai, and with our ancestors. He received living oracles to give to us.
44_7_39: Our ancestors were unwilling to obey him. Instead, they pushed him aside, and in their hearts turned back to Egypt.
44_7_40: saying to Aaron, ‘Make us gods who will go before us. As for this Moses who brought us out of the land of Egypt, we don’t know what’s happened to him.
44_7_41: They even made a calf in those days, offered sacrifice to the idol, and were celebrating what their hands had made.
44_7_42: But God turned away and gave them up to worship the stars of heaven, as it is written in the book of the prophets: House of Israel, did you bring me offerings and sacrifices for forty years in the wilderness?
44_7_43: You took up the tent of Moloch and the star of your god Rephan, the images that you made to worship. So I will send you into exile beyond Babylon.
44_7_44: Our ancestors had the tabernacle of the testimony in the wilderness, just as he who spoke to Moses commanded him to make it according to the pattern he had seen.
44_7_45: Our ancestors in turn received it and with Joshua brought it in when they dispossessed the nations that God drove out before them, until the days of David.
44_7_46: He found favor in God’s sight and asked that he might provide a dwelling place for the God of Jacob.
44_7_47: It was Solomon, rather, who built him a house.
44_7_48: However, the Most High does not dwell in temples made with hands, as the prophet says.
44_7_49: Heaven is My throne, and earth is My footstool. What kind of house will you build for Me? says the LORD, or what is the place of My rest?
44_7_50: Has My hand not made all these things?
44_7_51: You stiff-necked people, uncircumcised in heart and ears! You always resist the Holy Spirit; as your fathers did, so do you.
44_7_52: Which of the prophets did your fathers not persecute? And they killed those who foretold the coming of the Righteous One, of whom you now have become the betrayers and murderers.
44_7_53: You who received the law as delivered by angels and did not keep it.
44_7_54: When they heard these things, they were enraged, and they gnashed their teeth at him.
44_7_55: But he, full of the Holy Spirit, gazed into heaven and saw the glory of God, and Jesus standing at the right hand of God.
44_7_56: And he said, “Look! I see the heavens opened and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God!”
44_7_57: Then they cried out with a loud voice, stopped their ears, and rushed together at him.
44_7_58: And they cast him out of the city and stoned him. And the witnesses laid down their garments at the feet of a young man named Saul.
44_7_59: And they stoned Stephen as he was calling on God and saying, “Lord Jesus, receive my spirit.”
44_7_60: Then he knelt down and cried out with a loud voice, “Lord, do not hold this sin against them!” And when he had said this, he fell asleep.
44_8_1: Now Saul was consenting to his death. At that time a great persecution arose against the church which was at Jerusalem, and they were all scattered throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria, except the apostles.
44_8_2: And devout men carried Stephen to his burial and made great lamentation over him.
44_8_3: But Saul was ravaging the church, entering house after house, and dragging off men and women and committing them to prison.
44_8_4: Therefore, those who had been scattered went about preaching the word.
44_8_5: Then Philip went down to the city of Samaria and proclaimed the Messiah to them.
44_8_6: And the crowds with one accord paid attention to what was being said by Philip, when they heard him and saw the signs that he did.
44_8_7: For unclean spirits, crying out with a loud voice, came out of many who were possessed, and many who were paralyzed and lame were healed.
44_8_8: So there was great joy in that city.
44_8_9: But there was a certain man called Simon, who previously practiced sorcery in the city and astonished the people of Samaria, claiming that he was someone great.
44_8_10: To whom they all gave heed, from the least to the greatest, saying, “This man is the great power of God.”
44_8_11: And they paid attention to him because for a long time he had amazed them with his magic.
44_8_12: But when they believed Philip as he preached the good news about the kingdom of God and the name of Jesus Christ, both men and women were baptized.
44_8_13: Then Simon himself also believed; and when he was baptized he continued with Philip, and was amazed, seeing the signs and great miracles which were done.
44_8_14: Now when the apostles who were at Jerusalem heard that Samaria had received the word of God, they sent Peter and John to them.
44_8_15: Who, when they had come down, prayed for them that they might receive the Holy Spirit.
44_8_16: For he had not yet fallen on any of them, but they had only been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
44_8_17: Then they laid their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit.
44_8_18: And when Simon saw that the Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles’ hands, he offered them money.
44_8_19: Saying, “Give me this power also, so that anyone on whom I lay my hands may receive the Holy Spirit.”
44_8_20: But Peter said to him, “May your silver perish with you, because you thought you could obtain the gift of God with money!
44_8_21: You have no part or share in this matter, because your heart is not right before God.
44_8_22: Therefore repent of this wickedness of yours, and pray to the Lord that, if possible, your heart’s intent may be forgiven.
44_8_23: For I see you are poisoned by bitterness and bound by wickedness.
44_8_24: Then Simon answered and said, “Pray to the Lord for me, that none of the things which you have spoken may come upon me.”
44_8_25: So when they had testified and spoken the word of the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem, preaching the gospel in many villages of the Samaritans.
44_8_26: Now an angel of the Lord spoke to Philip, saying, “Arise and go toward the south along the road which goes down from Jerusalem to Gaza.” This is a desert.
44_8_27: So he arose and went. And behold, a man of Ethiopia, a eunuch of great authority under Candace the queen of the Ethiopians, who had charge of all her treasury, and had come to Jerusalem to worship.
44_8_28: was returning, and seated in his chariot, he was reading the prophet Isaiah.
44_8_29: Then the Spirit said to Philip, “Go over and join this chariot.”
44_8_30: So Philip ran to him and heard him reading Isaiah the prophet and said, “Do you understand what you are reading?”
44_8_31: And he said, “How can I, unless someone guides me?” And he invited Philip to come up and sit with him.
44_8_32: The place in the Scripture which he read was this: “He was led like a sheep to the slaughter, and as a lamb before its shearer is silent, so he opened not his mouth.
44_8_33: In his humiliation justice was denied him. Who can describe his generation? For his life is taken away from the earth.”
44_8_34: So the eunuch answered Philip and said, “I ask you, of whom does the prophet say this, of himself or of someone else?”
44_8_35: Then Philip opened his mouth, and beginning with this Scripture, he told him the good news about Jesus.
44_8_36: Now as they were going along the road they came to some water, and the eunuch said, “See, here is water! What prevents me from being baptized?”
44_8_37: Then Philip said, “If you believe with all your heart, you may.” And he answered and said, “I believe that Jesus Christ is the Son of God.”
44_8_38: So he commanded the chariot to stop, and both Philip and the eunuch went down into the water, and he baptized him.
44_8_39: Now when they came up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord carried Philip away, and the eunuch saw him no more, and went on his way rejoicing.
44_8_40: But Philip found himself at Azotus, and as he passed through he preached the gospel to all the towns until he came to Caesarea.
44_9_1: Then Saul, still breathing threats and murder against the disciples of the Lord, went to the high priest.
44_9_2: and asked him for letters to the synagogues at Damascus, so that if he found any belonging to the Way, men or women, he might bring them bound to Jerusalem.
44_9_3: As he traveled and was nearing Damascus, a light from heaven suddenly flashed around him.
44_9_4: Then he fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to him, “Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?”
44_9_5: And he said, “Who are you, Lord?” Then the Lord said, “I am Jesus, whom you are persecuting.
44_9_6: So he, trembling and astonished, said, “Lord, what do You want me to do?” Then the Lord said to him, “Get up and go into the city, and you will be told what you must do.”
44_9_7: And the men who were traveling with him stood speechless, hearing the voice but seeing no one.
44_9_8: Then Saul got up from the ground, and though his eyes were open, he could see nothing; so they took him by the hand and led him into Damascus.
44_9_9: And for three days he was without sight, and neither ate nor drank.
44_9_10: Now there was a disciple in Damascus named Ananias, and the Lord said to him in a vision, “Ananias.” And he said, “Here I am, Lord.”
44_9_11: So the Lord said to him, “Get up and go to the street called Straight, and at the house of Judas look for a man of Tarsus named Saul, for behold, he is praying,
44_9_12: and he has seen in a vision a man named Ananias come in and lay his hands on him so that he might regain his sight.”
44_9_13: Then Ananias answered, “Lord, I have heard from many about this man, how much harm he has done to your saints in Jerusalem.
44_9_14: And here he has authority from the chief priests to bind all who call on your name.”
44_9_15: But the Lord said to him, “Go, for he is a chosen instrument of mine to carry my name before the Gentiles and kings and the children of Israel.
44_9_16: For I will show him how much he must suffer for my name.”
44_9_17: And Ananias went his way and entered the house; and laying his hands on him he said, “Brother Saul, the Lord Jesus, who appeared to you on the road as you came, has sent me so that you may receive your sight and be filled with the Holy Spirit.”
44_9_18: Immediately something like scales fell from his eyes, and he regained his sight. Then he got up and was baptized.
44_9_19: So when he had received food, he was strengthened. Saul was with the disciples in Damascus for some time.
44_9_20: Immediately he began proclaiming Jesus in the synagogues, saying, “He is the Son of God.”
44_9_21: Then all who heard him were amazed and said, “Is not this the man who made havoc in Jerusalem of those who called upon this name? And has he not come here for this purpose, to bring them bound before the chief priests?”
44_9_22: But Saul grew stronger and kept confounding the Jews who lived in Damascus by proving that Jesus is the Messiah.
44_9_23: After many days had passed, the Jews conspired to kill him,
44_9_24: but Saul learned of their plot. So they were watching the gates day and night intending to kill him,
44_9_25: Then the disciples took him by night and lowered him in a large basket through an opening in the wall.
44_9_26: And when he arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the disciples, but they were all afraid of him, since they did not believe he was a disciple.
44_9_27: But Barnabas took him and brought him to the apostles and explained to them how Saul had seen the Lord on the road and that the Lord had talked to him, and how in Damascus he had spoken boldly in the name of Jesus.
44_9_28: So he was with them at Jerusalem, coming in and going out among them, preaching boldly in the name of the Lord.
44_9_29: And he spoke boldly in the name of the Lord Jesus and disputed against the Hellenists, but they were seeking to kill him.
44_9_30: When the brethren learned of this, they brought him down to Caesarea and sent him away to Tarsus.
44_9_31: Then the church throughout all Judea, Galilee, and Samaria had peace and was being built up, walking in the fear of the Lord and in the comfort of the Holy Spirit, and it multiplied.
44_9_32: Now it came to pass, as Peter went through all parts of the country, that he also came down to the saints who lived in Lydda.
44_9_33: There he found a man named Aeneas, who had been bedridden for eight years and was paralyzed.
44_9_34: And Peter said to him, “Aeneas, Jesus Christ heals you; arise and make your bed.” Then he arose immediately.
44_9_35: So all who lived in Lydda and Sharon saw him and turned to the Lord.
44_9_36: At Joppa there was a certain disciple named Tabitha, which is translated Dorcas; this woman was always doing good works and acts of charity.
44_9_37: But it happened in those days that she became sick and died; when they had washed her, they laid her in an upper room.
44_9_38: And since Lydda was near Joppa, and the disciples had heard that Peter was there, they sent two men to him, urging him not to delay in coming to them.
44_9_39: Then Peter arose and went with them; when he had come, they brought him to the upper room, and all the widows stood by him weeping and showing the tunics and garments which Dorcas had made while she was with them.
44_9_40: But Peter put them all outside, knelt down and prayed; and turning to the body he said, “Tabitha, arise.” And she opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter, she sat up.
44_9_41: Then he gave her his hand and helped her stand up; and when he had called the saints and widows, he presented her alive.
44_9_42: And it became known throughout all Joppa, and many believed in the Lord.
44_9_43: So it was that he stayed many days in Joppa with Simon, a tanner.
44_10_1: There was a certain man in Caesarea called Cornelius, a centurion of what was called the Italian Regiment.
44_10_2: He was a devout man and one who feared God with all his household, giving generously to the people and praying continually to God.
44_10_3: About the ninth hour of the day he saw clearly in a vision an angel of God coming in and saying to him, “Cornelius!”
44_10_4: And when he observed him, he was afraid and said, “What is it, Lord?” So he said to him, “Your prayers and your alms have ascended as a memorial before God.
44_10_5: Now send men to Joppa, and send for Simon whose surname is Peter.
44_10_6: He is lodging with Simon, a tanner, whose house is by the sea; he will tell you what you must do.”
44_10_7: And when the angel who spoke to him had departed, Cornelius called two of his household servants and a devout soldier from among those who waited on him continually.
44_10_8: So when he had explained everything to them, he sent them to Joppa.
44_10_9: The next day, as they were on their journey and approaching the city, Peter went up on the housetop to pray about the sixth hour.
44_10_10: Then he became very hungry and wanted to eat, but while they made ready, he fell into a trance.
44_10_11: And he saw heaven opened and an object like a great sheet bound at the four corners, descending to him and let down to the earth.
44_10_12: In it were all kinds of four-footed animals of the earth, wild beasts, creeping things, and birds of the air.
44_10_13: And a voice came to him, “Rise, Peter; kill and eat.”
44_10_14: But Peter said, “Not so, Lord! For I have never eaten anything common or unclean.”
44_10_15: And a voice spoke to him again the second time, “What God has cleansed you must not call common.”
44_10_16: This was done three times, and the object was taken up into heaven again.
44_10_17: Now while Peter wondered within himself what this vision which he had seen meant, behold, the men who had been sent from Cornelius had made inquiry for Simon’s house and stood before the gate.
44_10_18: And they called and asked whether Simon, whose surname was Peter, was lodging there.
44_10_19: While Peter thought about the vision, the Spirit said to him, “Behold, three men are seeking you.
44_10_20: Arise therefore, go down and go with them, doubting nothing; for I have sent them.”
44_10_21: Then Peter went down to the men who had been sent to him from Cornelius and said, “Yes, I am he whom you seek. For what reason have you come?”
44_10_22: And they said, “Cornelius the centurion, a just man, one who fears God and has a good reputation among all the nation of the Jews, was divinely instructed by a holy angel to summon you to his house and to hear words from you.”
44_10_23: Then he invited them in and lodged them; on the next day Peter went away with them, and some brethren from Joppa accompanied him.
44_10_24: And the following day they entered Caesarea; now Cornelius was waiting for them and had called together his relatives and close friends.
44_10_25: As Peter was coming in, Cornelius met him and fell down at his feet and worshiped him.
44_10_26: But Peter lifted him up, saying, “Stand up; I too am a man.”
44_10_27: And as he talked with him, he went in and found many persons gathered.
44_10_28: Then he said to them, “You know how unlawful it is for a Jewish man to associate with or visit anyone of another nation, but God has shown me that I should not call any person common or unclean.
44_10_29: Therefore I came without objection as soon as I was sent for. I ask, then, for what reason have you sent for me?”
44_10_30: So Cornelius said, “Four days ago at this hour, I was praying in my house at the ninth hour, and behold, a man stood before me in bright clothing,
44_10_31: and said, ‘Cornelius, your prayer has been heard, and your alms have been remembered before God.
44_10_32: Send therefore to Joppa and call for Simon who is called Peter. He is lodging in the house of Simon, a tanner, by the sea. When he comes, he will speak to you.’
44_10_33: So I sent for you immediately, and you have been kind enough to come. Now therefore, we are all present before God, to hear all the things commanded you by the Lord.”
44_10_34: Then Peter opened his mouth and said: “In truth I perceive that God shows no partiality.
44_10_35: But in every nation anyone who fears Him and does what is right is acceptable to Him.
44_10_36: The word which God sent to the children of Israel, preaching peace through Jesus Christ— He is Lord of all—
44_10_37: that word you know, which was proclaimed throughout all Judea, beginning from Galilee after the baptism that John proclaimed:
44_10_38: how God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Spirit and with power, and how he went about doing good and healing all who were oppressed by the devil, for God was with Him.
44_10_39: And we are witnesses of all that He did both in the country of the Jews and in Jerusalem, whom they killed by hanging Him on a tree.
44_10_40: Him God raised up on the third day, and showed Him openly,
44_10_41: not to all the people, but to us who had been chosen by God as witnesses, who ate and drank with Him after He arose from the dead.
44_10_42: And He commanded us to preach to the people, and to testify that He is the one appointed by God to be Judge of the living and the dead.
44_10_43: To Him all the prophets bear witness that everyone who believes in Him receives forgiveness of sins through His name.”
44_10_44: While Peter was still saying these things, the Holy Spirit fell on all who heard the word.
44_10_45: And the believers from among the circumcised who had come with Peter were amazed, because the gift of the Holy Spirit was poured out even on the Gentiles.
44_10_46: For they heard them speaking in other tongues and declaring the greatness of God. Then Peter responded,
44_10_47: “Can anyone withhold water and prevent these people from being baptized, who have received the Holy Spirit just as we have?”
44_10_48: And he commanded them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they asked him to stay for a few days.
44_11_1: Now the apostles and the brothers who were throughout Judea heard that the Gentiles also had received the word of God.
44_11_2: And when Peter went up to Jerusalem, the circumcision party criticized him, saying,
44_11_3: saying, “You went to uncircumcised men and ate with them!”
44_11_4: But Peter began to explain to them step by step, saying:
44_11_5: “I was in the city of Joppa praying, and in a trance I saw a vision, something like a great sheet descending, being let down from heaven by its four corners, and it came down to me.
44_11_6: When I looked closely and considered it, I saw the four-footed animals of the earth, the wild beasts, the reptiles, and the birds of the sky.
44_11_7: And I heard a voice telling me, ‘Get up, Peter; kill and eat.’
44_11_8: But I said, ‘By no means, Lord! For nothing impure or ritually unclean has ever entered my mouth.’
44_11_9: But the voice answered from heaven a second time, ‘What God has made clean, you must not call impure.’
44_11_10: Now this happened three times, and everything was drawn up again into heaven.
44_11_11: At that very moment, three men who had been sent to me from Caesarea arrived at the house where we were.
44_11_12: Then the Spirit told me to accompany them with no doubts at all. These six brothers also accompanied me, and we went into the man’s house.
44_11_13: And he reported to us how he had seen the angel standing in his house and saying, ‘Send to Joppa, and call for Simon, who is also named Peter.
44_11_14: He will speak a message to you by which you and all your household will be saved.’
44_11_15: And as I began to speak, the Holy Spirit came down on them, just as on us at the beginning.
44_11_16: Then I remembered the word of the Lord, how He said, ‘John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.’
44_11_17: If therefore God gave them the same gift as He gave us when we believed in the Lord Jesus Christ, who was I that I could stand in God's way?
44_11_18: When they heard these things they became silent; and they glorified God, saying, “Then to the Gentiles also God has granted repentance that leads to life.”
44_11_19: Now those who were scattered because of the persecution that arose over Stephen traveled as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, preaching the word to no one except Jews.
44_11_20: But there were some of them, men from Cyprus and Cyrene, who came to Antioch and began speaking to the Greeks, preaching the Lord Jesus.
44_11_21: And the hand of the Lord was with them, and a large number who believed turned to the Lord.
44_11_22: Then news about them reached the ears of the church in Jerusalem, and they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
44_11_23: When he came and saw the grace of God, he was glad, and encouraged them all to remain faithful to the Lord with steadfast purpose.
44_11_24: For he was a good man, full of the Holy Spirit and of faith, and a large number of people were added to the Lord.
44_11_25: Then Barnabas went to Tarsus to look for Saul.
44_11_26: And when he had found him, he brought him to Antioch; and for a whole year they assembled with the church and taught a great many people, and the disciples were first called Christians in Antioch.
44_11_27: In those days some prophets came down from Jerusalem to Antioch.
44_11_28: Then one of them, named Agabus, stood up and predicted by the Spirit that there would be a great famine throughout the Roman world, which also happened in the days of Claudius Caesar.
44_11_29: Then the disciples, each according to his ability, determined to send relief to the brothers living in Judea.
44_11_30: This they also did, sending it to the elders by means of Barnabas and Saul.
44_12_1: Now about that time King Herod laid violent hands on some who belonged to the church.
44_12_2: He killed James the brother of John with the sword.
44_12_3: And when he saw that it pleased the Jews, he proceeded to arrest Peter also; this was during the days of Unleavened Bread.
44_12_4: So when he had arrested him, he put him in prison, delivering him over to four squads of soldiers to guard him, intending after the Passover to bring him out to the people.
44_12_5: Peter was therefore kept in prison, but earnest prayer for him was made to God by the church.
44_12_6: And when Herod was about to bring him out, that night Peter was sleeping, bound with two chains between two soldiers, and the sentries before the door were guarding the prison.
44_12_7: Now behold, an angel of the Lord stood next to him, and a light shone in the cell; and he struck Peter on the side and woke him, saying, “Get up quickly!” And his chains fell off his hands.
44_12_8: Then the angel said to him, “Get dressed and put on your sandals.” And he did so. “Wrap your cloak around you and follow me,” the angel ordered.
44_12_9: So he went out and followed him, and he did not know that what was being done by the angel was real, but thought he was seeing a vision.
44_12_10: When they had passed the first and second guard, they came to the iron gate leading into the city, which opened for them by itself; and they went out and went along one street, and immediately the angel left him.
44_12_11: And when Peter came to himself, he said, “Now I know for certain that the Lord has sent His angel and rescued me from the hand of Herod and from all that the Jewish people were expecting.”
44_12_12: So, when he had considered this, he went to the house of Mary, the mother of John whose other name was Mark, where many were gathered together and were praying.
44_12_13: And as Peter knocked at the door of the outer gate, a servant girl named Rhoda came to answer.
44_12_14: When she recognized Peter’s voice, because of her joy she did not open the gate but ran in and announced that Peter was standing at the gate.
44_12_15: But they said to her, “You are out of your mind!” Yet she kept insisting that it was so. So they said, “It is his angel.”
44_12_16: Now Peter continued knocking; and when they opened the door and saw him, they were amazed.
44_12_17: But motioning to them with his hand to be silent, he described to them how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. “Tell these things to James and the brothers,” he said, and he left and went to another place.
44_12_18: Then, as soon as it was day, there was no little disturbance among the soldiers over what had become of Peter.
44_12_19: But when Herod had searched for him and did not find him, he examined the guards and commanded that they should be put to death; and he went down from Judea to Caesarea and stayed there.
44_12_20: Now Herod was very angry with the people of Tyre and Sidon; and they came to him with one accord, and having won over Blastus, the king's chamberlain, they asked for peace, because their country depended on the king's country for food.
44_12_21: So on an appointed day Herod, dressed in royal robes, sat on his throne and delivered an oration to them.
44_12_22: And the people kept shouting, “The voice of a god and not of a man!”
44_12_23: Then immediately an angel of the Lord struck him down because he did not give God the glory, and he was eaten by worms and breathed his last.
44_12_24: But the word of God flourished and multiplied.
44_12_25: And Barnabas and Saul returned from Jerusalem when they had fulfilled their ministry, taking along John who was called Mark.
44_13_1: Now in the church at Antioch there were prophets and teachers: Barnabas, Simeon who was called Niger, Lucius of Cyrene, Manaen a close friend of Herod the tetrarch, and Saul.
44_13_2: While they were ministering to the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, “Now separate to Me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them.”
44_13_3: Then, after they had fasted and prayed, they laid hands on them and sent them away.
44_13_4: So, being sent out by the Holy Spirit, they went down to Seleucia, and from there they sailed to Cyprus.
44_13_5: When they arrived in Salamis, they proclaimed the word of God in the synagogues of the Jews, and they also had John as their assistant.
44_13_6: When they had gone through the whole island as far as Paphos, they found a certain sorcerer, a false prophet, a Jew whose name was Bar-Jesus.
44_13_7: He was with the proconsul, Sergius Paulus, an intelligent man, who summoned Barnabas and Saul and sought to hear the word of God.
44_13_8: But Elymas the sorcerer (for that is the meaning of his name) opposed them, seeking to turn the proconsul away from the faith.
44_13_9: But Saul, who was also called Paul, filled with the Holy Spirit, stared straight at him.
44_13_10: And said, “You are full of all kinds of deceit and trickery, you son of the devil and enemy of all that is right. Won’t you ever stop perverting the straight paths of the Lord?
44_13_11: Now, look, the Lord’s hand is against you. You are going to be blind and will not see the sun for a time.” Immediately a mist and darkness fell on him, and he went around seeking someone to lead him by the hand.
44_13_12: Then the proconsul believed when he saw what had happened, because he was astonished at the teaching of the Lord.
44_13_13: Now when Paul and his companions set sail from Paphos, they came to Perga in Pamphylia, but John left them and returned to Jerusalem.
44_13_14: But going on from Perga, they arrived at Pisidian Antioch, and on the Sabbath day they went into the synagogue and sat down.
44_13_15: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the leaders of the synagogue sent word to them, saying, “Brothers, if you have any word of encouragement for the people, you can speak.”
44_13_16: So Paul stood up and motioned with his hand and said: “Men of Israel, and you who fear God, listen!
44_13_17: The God of this people Israel chose our fathers, made the people prosper during their stay in the land of Egypt, and led them out of it with a mighty arm.
44_13_18: And for about forty years he put up with them in the wilderness;
44_13_19: And after destroying seven nations in the land of Canaan, he gave them their land as an inheritance.
44_13_20: This all took about 450 years. After this, he gave them judges until Samuel the prophet.
44_13_21: Then they asked for a king, and God gave them Saul the son of Kish, a man of the tribe of Benjamin, for forty years.
44_13_22: After removing him, he raised up David as their king and testified about him: ‘I have found David the son of Jesse to be a man after my own heart, who will carry out all my will.’
44_13_23: From this man’s descendants, as he promised, God brought to Israel the Savior, Jesus.
44_13_24: Before his coming, John had proclaimed a baptism of repentance to all the people of Israel.
44_13_25: As John was finishing his course, he said, ‘Who do you think I am? I am not he. But behold, there comes One after me, the sandals of whose feet I am not worthy to untie.’
44_13_26: Brothers and sisters, children of Abraham’s race, and those among you who fear God, it is to us that the word of this salvation has been sent.
44_13_27: For those who live in Jerusalem and their rulers, because they did not recognize him nor understand the utterances of the prophets, which are read every Sabbath, fulfilled their words by condemning him.
44_13_28: And though they found no grounds for the death sentence, they asked Pilate to have him killed.
44_13_29: When they had carried out all that had been written about him, they took him down from the tree and put him in a tomb.
44_13_30: But God raised him from the dead.
44_13_31: And he appeared for many days to those who came up with him from Galilee to Jerusalem, who are now his witnesses to the people.
44_13_32: And we ourselves proclaim to you the good news of the promise that was made to our ancestors.
44_13_33: God has fulfilled this for us, their children, by raising up Jesus, as it is written in the second Psalm: ‘You are my Son; today I have become your Father.’
44_13_34: As to his raising him from the dead, never to return to decay, he has spoken in this way, ‘I will give you the holy and sure promises of David.’
44_13_35: Therefore he also says in another passage, ‘You will not let your Holy One see decay.’
44_13_36: For David, after serving God’s purpose in his own generation, fell asleep, was buried with his fathers, and decayed,
44_13_37: but the one God raised up did not decay.
44_13_38: Therefore, let it be known to you, brothers and sisters, that through this man forgiveness of sins is being proclaimed to you.
44_13_39: Everyone who believes is justified through him from everything that you could not be justified from through the law of Moses.
44_13_40: So beware that what is said in the prophets does not happen to you:
44_13_41: Look, you scoffers, marvel and vanish away, because I am doing a work in your days, a work that you will never believe, even if someone were to explain it to you.
44_13_42: So when the Jews went out of the synagogue, the Gentiles begged that these things might be preached to them the next Sabbath.
44_13_43: Now when the congregation had broken up, many of the Jews and devout converts to Judaism followed Paul and Barnabas, who, as they spoke with them, urged them to continue in the grace of God.
44_13_44: On the next Sabbath almost the whole city came together to hear the word of the Lord.
44_13_45: But when the Jews saw the crowds, they were filled with jealousy and began to contradict what was spoken by Paul, reviling him.
44_13_46: Then Paul and Barnabas grew bold and said, “It was necessary that the word of God should be spoken to you first; but since you reject it and judge yourselves unworthy of eternal life, behold, we turn to the Gentiles.
44_13_47: For so the Lord has commanded us: ‘I have made you a light for the Gentiles, that you may bring salvation to the ends of the earth.’
44_13_48: Now when the Gentiles heard this, they rejoiced and honored the word of the Lord, and as many as had been appointed to eternal life believed.
44_13_49: And the word of the Lord was spreading throughout the whole region.
44_13_50: But the Jews incited the prominent God-fearing women and the leading men of the city, stirred up persecution against Paul and Barnabas, and expelled them from their district.
44_13_51: But they shook off the dust from their feet against them and went to Iconium.
44_13_52: And the disciples were filled with joy and with the Holy Spirit.
44_14_1: Now it happened in Iconium that they went together to the synagogue of the Jews, and spoke in such a way that a great multitude both of the Jews and of the Greeks believed.
44_14_2: But the unbelieving Jews stirred up the Gentiles and poisoned their minds against the brothers.
44_14_3: Therefore they stayed there a long time, speaking boldly for the Lord, who bore witness to the word of His grace, granting signs and wonders to be done by their hands.
44_14_4: But the people of the city were divided; some sided with the Jews and some with the apostles.
44_14_5: And when an attempt was made by both the Gentiles and Jews, with their rulers, to mistreat them and to stone them,
44_14_6: they learned of it and fled to Lystra and Derbe, cities of Lycaonia, and to the surrounding region.
44_14_7: And there they continued to preach the gospel.
44_14_8: And in Lystra a certain man without strength in his feet was sitting, a cripple from his mother’s womb, who had never walked.
44_14_9: This man heard Paul speaking. Paul, observing him intently and seeing that he had faith to be made well,
44_14_10: said in a loud voice, “Stand up straight on your feet!” And he leaped and walked.
44_14_11: Now when the crowds saw what Paul had done, they lifted up their voices, saying in Lycaonian, “The gods have come down to us in human form!”
44_14_12: And they called Barnabas Zeus, and Paul Hermes, because he was the chief speaker.
44_14_13: Then the priest of Zeus, whose temple was just outside the town, brought bulls and wreaths to the gates because he intended, with the crowds, to offer sacrifice.
44_14_14: But when the apostles Barnabas and Paul heard this, they tore their robes and rushed into the crowd, crying out
44_14_15: and saying, “Men, why are you doing these things? We also are men of the same nature as you, and we bring you good news, that you should turn from these worthless things to the living God, who made the heaven, the earth, the sea, and all that is in them,
44_14_16: who in past generations allowed all nations to walk in their own ways.
44_14_17: Nevertheless He did not leave Himself without witness, for he did good by giving you rains from heaven and fruitful seasons, filling your hearts with food and gladness.”
44_14_18: And with these words they scarcely restrained the crowds from sacrificing to them.
44_14_19: Then Jews came from Antioch and Iconium, and having persuaded the crowds, they stoned Paul and dragged him out of the city, supposing that he was dead.
44_14_20: However, when the disciples gathered around him, he rose up and went into the city. And the next day he departed with Barnabas to Derbe.
44_14_21: And when they had preached the gospel to that city and made many disciples, they returned to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch,
44_14_22: strengthening the souls of the disciples, encouraging them to continue in the faith, and saying, “We must through many tribulations enter the kingdom of God.”
44_14_23: So when they had appointed elders for them in every church and prayed with fasting, they committed them to the Lord in whom they had believed.
44_14_24: And after they passed through Pisidia, they came to Pamphylia.
44_14_25: Now when they had spoken the word in Perga, they went down to Attalia.
44_14_26: From there they sailed back to Antioch where they had been commended to the grace of God for the work they had now completed.
44_14_27: Now when they had arrived and gathered the church together, they reported all that God had done with them, and how he had opened a door of faith to the Gentiles.
44_14_28: So they spent a considerable time with the disciples.
44_15_1: And certain men came down from Judea and began to teach the brothers, “Unless you are circumcised according to the custom prescribed by Moses, you cannot be saved.”
44_15_2: Therefore, when Paul and Barnabas had no small dissension and debate with them, they determined that Paul and Barnabas and some others should go up to Jerusalem to the apostles and elders about this question.
44_15_3: So, being sent on their way by the church, they passed through both Phoenicia and Samaria, describing in detail the conversion of the Gentiles, and brought great joy to all the brothers.
44_15_4: When they arrived at Jerusalem, they were welcomed by the church and the apostles and the elders, and they reported all that God had done with them.
44_15_5: But some of the believers who belonged to the party of the Pharisees rose up and said, “It is necessary to circumcise them and to order them to keep the law of Moses.”
44_15_6: The apostles and the elders were gathered together to consider this matter.
44_15_7: And after there had been much debate, Peter stood up and said to them, “Brothers, you know that in the early days God made a choice among you, that by my mouth the Gentiles should hear the word of the gospel and believe.
44_15_8: So God, who knows the heart, bore witness to them by giving them the Holy Spirit, just as He did to us,
44_15_9: and made no distinction between us and them, cleansing their hearts by faith.
44_15_10: Now therefore, why do you test God by putting a yoke on the neck of the disciples that neither our fathers nor we have been able to bear?
44_15_11: But we believe that we are saved through the grace of the Lord Jesus, in the same way as they also are.
44_15_12: Then all the assembly fell silent, and they listened to Barnabas and Paul as they related what signs and wonders God had done through them among the Gentiles.
44_15_13: After they stopped speaking, James responded: “Brothers and sisters, listen to me.
44_15_14: Simeon has reported how God first intervened to take from the Gentiles a people for his name.
44_15_15: And the words of the prophets agree with this, as it is written:
44_15_16: ‘After this I will return and rebuild the tent of David that has fallen; I will rebuild its ruins and restore it,
44_15_17: so that the rest of humanity may seek the Lord — even all the Gentiles who are called by my name — declares the Lord who makes these things.
44_15_19: Therefore, in my judgment, we should not cause difficulties for those among the Gentiles who turn to God,
44_15_20: but instead we should write to them to abstain from things polluted by idols, from sexual immorality, from eating anything that has been strangled, and from blood.
44_15_21: For since ancient times, Moses has had those who proclaim him in every city, and every Sabbath day he is read aloud in the synagogues.
44_15_22: Then it seemed good to the apostles and the elders, with the whole church, to choose men from among them and send them to Antioch with Paul and Barnabas: Judas, called Barsabbas, and Silas, both leading men among the brothers.
44_15_23: They wrote: “From the apostles and the elders, your brothers, to the brothers and sisters among the Gentiles in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia: Greetings.
44_15_24: Since we have heard that some without our authorization went out from us and troubled you with their words and unsettled your hearts,
44_15_25: it has seemed good to us, having come to one accord, to choose men and send them to you with our beloved Barnabas and Paul,
44_15_26: men who have risked their lives for the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
44_15_27: Therefore we have sent Judas and Silas, who will personally report the same things by word of mouth.
44_15_28: For it was the Holy Spirit’s decision — and ours — not to place further burdens on you beyond these requirements:
44_15_29: that you abstain from food offered to idols, from blood, from eating anything that has been strangled, and from sexual immorality. You will do well if you keep yourselves from these things. Farewell.”
44_15_30: So they were sent off and went down to Antioch, and after gathering the assembly, they delivered the letter.
44_15_31: When they read it, they rejoiced because of its encouragement.
44_15_32: Both Judas and Silas, who were also prophets themselves, encouraged the brothers and sisters and strengthened them with a long message.
44_15_33: After spending some time there, they were sent back in peace by the brothers and sisters to those who had sent them.
44_15_34: However, it seemed good to Silas to remain there.
44_15_35: But Paul and Barnabas, along with many others, remained in Antioch, teaching and proclaiming the word of the Lord.
44_15_36: After some time had passed, Paul said to Barnabas, “Let’s go back and visit the brothers and sisters in every town where we have preached the word of the Lord and see how they’re doing.”
44_15_37: Now Barnabas wanted to take along John Mark.
44_15_38: But Paul insisted that they should not take along this man who had deserted them in Pamphylia and had not gone on with them to the work.
44_15_39: They had such a sharp disagreement that they parted company, and Barnabas took Mark with him and sailed off to Cyprus.
44_15_40: But Paul chose Silas and departed, after being commended by the brothers and sisters to the grace of the Lord.
44_15_41: He traveled through Syria and Cilicia, strengthening the churches.
44_16_1: Then he came to Derbe and Lystra, and behold, a certain disciple was there, named Timothy, the son of a believing Jewish woman, but his father was Greek.
44_16_2: He was well spoken of by the brothers at Lystra and Iconium.
44_16_3: Paul wanted Timothy to accompany him, and he took him and circumcised him because of the Jews who were in that region, for they all knew that his father was Greek.
44_16_4: As they went on their way through the cities, they delivered to them for observance the decisions that had been reached by the apostles and elders who were in Jerusalem.
44_16_5: So the churches were strengthened in the faith and grew daily in numbers.
44_16_6: Now when they had gone through the region of Phrygia and Galatia, they were forbidden by the Holy Spirit to speak the word in Asia.
44_16_7: After they had come to Mysia, they tried to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus did not allow them.
44_16_8: So passing by Mysia, they went down to Troas.
44_16_9: And a vision appeared to Paul in the night: a man of Macedonia was standing there, urging him and saying, 'Come over to Macedonia and help us.'
44_16_10: Now after he had seen the vision, immediately we sought to go into Macedonia, concluding that God had called us to preach the gospel to them.
44_16_11: Therefore, sailing from Troas, we made a direct voyage to Samothrace, and the following day to Neapolis.
44_16_12: And from there to Philippi, a leading city of the district of Macedonia, a Roman colony. We stayed in that city for several days.
44_16_13: And on the Sabbath day we went outside the city gate by the river, where we expected to find a place of prayer, and we sat down and spoke to the women gathered there.
44_16_14: Now a certain woman named Lydia, a dealer in purple cloth from the city of Thyatira, who was a worshiper of God, was listening; the Lord opened her heart to respond to what Paul was saying.
44_16_15: And when she and her household were baptized, she urged us, saying, 'If you consider me a believer in the Lord, come and stay at my house.' And she persuaded us.
44_16_16: Now it happened, as we were going to the place of prayer, that a certain slave girl met us, who had a spirit of divination and brought her masters much profit by fortune-telling.
44_16_17: This girl followed Paul and us, crying out, 'These men are servants of the Most High God, who proclaim to you the way of salvation.'
44_16_18: And this she kept doing for many days. But Paul, greatly annoyed, turned and said to the spirit, 'I command you in the name of Jesus Christ to come out of her.' And it came out that very hour.
44_16_19: But when her owners saw that their hope of gain was gone, they seized Paul and Silas and dragged them into the marketplace before the authorities.
44_16_20: And they brought them to the magistrates, and said, 'These men are Jews, and they are disturbing our city.'
44_16_21: And they advocate customs that are not lawful for us as Romans to accept or practice.
44_16_22: Then the crowd joined in attacking them, and the magistrates tore the garments off them and gave orders to beat them with rods.
44_16_23: And when they had inflicted many blows upon them, they threw them into prison, ordering the jailer to keep them safely.
44_16_24: Having received such an order, he put them into the inner prison and secured their feet in the stocks.
44_16_25: But at midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God, and the prisoners were listening to them.
44_16_26: Suddenly there was such a violent earthquake that the foundations of the jail were shaken, and immediately all the doors were opened, and everyone’s chains came loose.
44_16_27: And the jailer woke up and saw the doors of the prison standing open, and he drew his sword and was going to kill himself, since he thought the prisoners had escaped.
44_16_28: But Paul called out in a loud voice, 'Don’t harm yourself, because we’re all here.'
44_16_29: Then he called for lights, rushed in, and fell down trembling before Paul and Silas.
44_16_30: And he brought them out and said, 'Sirs, what must I do to be saved?'
44_16_31: So they said, 'Believe in the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved — you and your household.'
44_16_32: Then they spoke the word of the Lord to him along with everyone in his house.
44_16_33: And he took them the same hour of the night and washed their wounds. Right away he and all his family were baptized.
44_16_34: Now when he had brought them into his house, he set food before them, and he rejoiced because he had come to believe in God with his entire household.
44_16_35: And when it was day, the magistrates sent the police, saying, 'Let those men go.'
44_16_36: So the jailer reported these words to Paul, saying, 'The magistrates have sent to let you go. Now therefore depart, and go in peace.'
44_16_37: But Paul said to them, 'They have beaten us publicly, uncondemned Romans, and have thrown us into prison; and now do they throw us out secretly? No! Let them come themselves and escort us out.'
44_16_38: And the police reported these words to the magistrates, and they were afraid when they heard that they were Roman citizens.
44_16_39: Then they came and pleaded with them and brought them out, and asked them to depart from the city.
44_16_40: So they went out of the prison and entered the house of Lydia; and when they had seen the brothers and sisters, they encouraged them and departed.
44_17_1: Now when they had passed through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they came to Thessalonica, where there was a synagogue of the Jews.
44_17_2: Then Paul, as was his custom, went in to them, and for three Sabbaths reasoned with them from the Scriptures.
44_17_3: explaining and proving that it was necessary for the Christ to suffer and rise again from the dead, and saying, “This Jesus whom I proclaim to you is the Christ.”
44_17_4: And some of them were persuaded and joined Paul and Silas, along with a large number of the devout Greeks and not a few of the leading women.
44_17_5: But the Jews who were not persuaded, becoming envious, took some of the wicked men from the marketplace, and gathering a mob, set all the city in an uproar and attacked the house of Jason, seeking to bring them out to the crowd.
44_17_6: But when they did not find them, they dragged Jason and some of the brothers before the city authorities, shouting, “These men who have turned the world upside down have come here also.
44_17_7: Jason has welcomed them, and they are all acting contrary to the decrees of Caesar, saying that there is another king, Jesus.”
44_17_8: And the crowd and city officials who heard these things were disturbed.
44_17_9: So when they had taken security from Jason and the rest, they let them go.
44_17_10: Then the brothers immediately sent Paul and Silas away by night to Berea, and when they arrived, they went into the synagogue of the Jews.
44_17_11: These were more noble-minded than those in Thessalonica, for they received the word with great eagerness, examining the Scriptures daily to see if these things were so.
44_17_12: Therefore many of them believed, along with a number of prominent Greek women and men.
44_17_13: But when the Jews from Thessalonica learned that the word of God was proclaimed by Paul at Berea also, they came there too, agitating and stirring up the crowds.
44_17_14: Then immediately the brothers sent Paul away to go to the sea; but Silas and Timothy remained there.
44_17_15: So those who conducted Paul brought him as far as Athens, and after receiving a command for Silas and Timothy to come to him as quickly as possible, they departed.
44_17_16: Now while Paul was waiting for them at Athens, his spirit was provoked within him as he saw that the city was full of idols.
44_17_17: Therefore he reasoned in the synagogue with the Jews and with the devout persons, and in the marketplace every day with those who happened to be there.
44_17_18: Then certain Epicurean and Stoic philosophers encountered him, and some said, “What does this babbler want to say?” Others said, “He seems to be a proclaimer of foreign gods,” because he preached to them Jesus and the resurrection.
44_17_19: And they took him and brought him to the Areopagus, saying, “May we know what this new teaching is that you are presenting?
44_17_20: For you bring some strange things to our ears; therefore we want to know what these things mean.”
44_17_21: Now all the Athenians and the foreigners residing there spent their time on nothing else but telling or hearing something new.
44_17_22: Then Paul stood in the midst of the Areopagus and said, “Men of Athens, I perceive that in every way you are very religious;
44_17_23: for as I was passing through and observing the objects of your worship, I even found an altar with this inscription: TO THE UNKNOWN GOD. Therefore, what you worship in ignorance, this I proclaim to you:
44_17_24: The God who made the world and everything in it, since He is Lord of heaven and earth, does not dwell in temples made by hands.
44_17_25: Nor is He served by human hands, as though He needed anything, since He Himself gives to all life and breath and everything.
44_17_26: And He made from one man every nation of mankind to live on all the face of the earth, having determined allotted periods and the boundaries of their dwelling place,
44_17_27: that they should seek God, and perhaps feel their way toward him and find him, though he is not far from each one of us;
44_17_28: for in Him we live and move and have our being, as even some of your own poets have said, ‘For we are also His offspring.’
44_17_29: Therefore, since we are the offspring of God, we ought not to think that the divine being is like gold or silver or stone, an image formed by the art and imagination of man.
44_17_30: Truly, these times of ignorance God overlooked, but now commands all people everywhere to repent,
44_17_31: because He has appointed a day on which He will judge the world in righteousness by the Man whom He has ordained. He has given assurance of this to all by raising Him from the dead.”
44_17_32: And when they heard of the resurrection of the dead, some mocked, while others said, “We will hear you again on this matter.”
44_17_33: So Paul went out from their midst.
44_17_34: However, some men joined him and believed, among whom also were Dionysius the Areopagite and a woman named Damaris and others with them.
44_18_1: After this, Paul left Athens and went to Corinth.
44_18_2: And he found a Jew named Aquila, a native of Pontus, who had recently come from Italy with his wife Priscilla because Claudius had commanded all the Jews to leave Rome.
44_18_3: So, because he was of the same trade, he stayed with them and worked; for they were tentmakers by trade.
44_18_4: And he reasoned in the synagogue every Sabbath and tried to persuade both Jews and Greeks.
44_18_5: When Silas and Timothy arrived from Macedonia, Paul devoted himself to preaching the word and testified to the Jews that Jesus is the Messiah.
44_18_6: But when they opposed and blasphemed, he shook out his garments and said to them, “Your blood is on your own heads! I am innocent. From now on I will go to the Gentiles.
44_18_7: And he departed from there and entered the house of a certain man named Titius Justus, a worshiper of God, whose house was next door to the synagogue.
44_18_8: Then Crispus, the ruler of the synagogue, believed in the Lord with all his household, and many of the Corinthians, hearing, believed and were baptized.
44_18_9: Now the Lord spoke to Paul in the night by a vision, 'Do not be afraid, but keep on speaking and do not be silent;
44_18_10: for I am with you, and no one will attack you to harm you, for I have many in this city who are my people.'
44_18_11: And he continued there a year and six months, teaching the word of God among them.
44_18_12: When Gallio was proconsul of Achaia, the Jews made a united attack on Paul and brought him before the tribunal,
44_18_13: saying, 'This man is persuading people to worship God contrary to the law.'
44_18_14: And when Paul was about to open his mouth, Gallio said to the Jews, 'If it were a matter of wrongdoing or a serious crime, O Jews, I would have reason to accept your complaint.
44_18_15: But if it is a question of words and names and your own law, see to it yourselves; for I do not want to be a judge of such matters.'
44_18_16: And he drove them from the tribunal.
44_18_17: Then all the Greeks seized Sosthenes, the ruler of the synagogue, and beat him in front of the tribunal, but Gallio paid no attention to any of this.
44_18_18: So Paul still remained a good while. Then he took leave of the brethren and sailed for Syria, and Priscilla and Aquila were with him. He had his hair cut off at Cenchrea, for he had taken a vow.
44_18_19: And he came to Ephesus and left them there; but he himself entered the synagogue and reasoned with the Jews.
44_18_20: When they asked him to stay longer, he declined.
44_18_21: but took leave of them, saying, 'I must by all means keep this coming feast in Jerusalem; but I will return again to you, God willing.' And he sailed from Ephesus.
44_18_22: And when he had landed at Caesarea, and gone up and greeted the church, he went down to Antioch.
44_18_23: After he had spent some time there, he departed and went over the region of Galatia and Phrygia in order, strengthening all the disciples.
44_18_24: Now a certain Jew named Apollos, born at Alexandria, an eloquent man and mighty in the Scriptures, came to Ephesus.
44_18_25: This man had been instructed in the way of the Lord; and being fervent in spirit, he spoke and taught accurately the things concerning Jesus, though he knew only the baptism of John.
44_18_26: So he began to speak boldly in the synagogue. When Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they took him aside and explained the way of God more accurately.
44_18_27: And when he desired to cross to Achaia, the brethren wrote, exhorting the disciples to receive him; and when he arrived, he greatly helped those who had believed through grace;
44_18_28: for he vigorously refuted the Jews publicly, demonstrating through the Scriptures that Jesus is the Messiah.
44_19_1: And it happened, while Apollos was at Corinth, that Paul, having passed through the upper regions, came to Ephesus and found some disciples.
44_19_2: he said to them, 'Did you receive the Holy Spirit when you believed?' So they said to him, 'We have not so much as heard whether there is a Holy Spirit.'
44_19_3: And he said to them, 'Into what then were you baptized?' So they said, 'Into John's baptism.'
44_19_4: Then Paul said, 'John indeed baptized with a baptism of repentance, telling the people to believe in the one who was to come after him, that is, in Jesus.'
44_19_5: When they heard this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
44_19_6: And when Paul had laid hands on them, the Holy Spirit came upon them, and they began to speak in other tongues and to prophesy.
44_19_7: Now there were about twelve men in all.
44_19_8: And he went into the synagogue and spoke boldly for three months, reasoning and persuading concerning the things of the kingdom of God.
44_19_9: But when some became stubborn and continued in unbelief, speaking evil of the Way before the congregation, he withdrew from them and took the disciples with him, reasoning daily in the hall of Tyrannus.
44_19_10: And this continued for two years, so that all the residents of Asia heard the word of the Lord, both Jews and Greeks.
44_19_11: Now God was performing extraordinary miracles by Paul’s hands,
44_19_12: so that even handkerchiefs or aprons that had touched his skin were carried away to the sick, and their diseases left them and the evil spirits came out of them.
44_19_13: Then some of the itinerant Jewish exorcists took it upon themselves to invoke the name of the Lord Jesus over those who had evil spirits, saying, 'I command you by the Jesus whom Paul preaches.'
44_19_14: Also there were seven sons of Sceva, a Jewish chief priest, who did so.
44_19_15: And the evil spirit answered and said, 'Jesus I know, and Paul I recognize; but who are you?'
44_19_16: Then the man in whom the evil spirit was leaped on them, overpowered them, and prevailed against them, so that they fled out of that house naked and wounded.
44_19_17: This became known both to all the residents of Ephesus, both Jews and Greeks; and fear fell on them all, and the name of the Lord Jesus was extolled.
44_19_18: And many who had become believers came confessing and disclosing their practices.
44_19_19: Also, many of those who had practiced magic arts brought their books together and burned them in the sight of all, and they counted the value of them and found it totaled fifty thousand pieces of silver.
44_19_20: So the word of the Lord continued to increase and prevail mightily.
44_19_21: When these things were accomplished, Paul resolved in the Spirit to pass through Macedonia and Achaia and go to Jerusalem, saying, 'After I have been there, I must also see Rome.'
44_19_22: So he sent into Macedonia two of those who assisted him, Timothy and Erastus, but he himself stayed in Asia for a while.
44_19_23: About that time there arose no little disturbance concerning the Way.
44_19_24: For a certain man named Demetrius, a silversmith, who made silver shrines of Artemis, brought no little business to the craftsmen.
44_19_25: He called them together with the workmen in similar trades and said, 'Men, you know that from this business we have our wealth.'
44_19_26: Moreover, you see and hear that not only in Ephesus, but throughout almost all Asia, this Paul has persuaded and turned away a considerable number of people, saying that gods made with hands are not gods.
44_19_27: So not only is this trade of ours in danger of falling into disrepute, but also the temple of the great goddess Artemis may be counted as nothing, and her magnificence come to the verge of ruin, whom all Asia and the world worship.
44_19_28: Now when they heard this, they were filled with rage and began to cry out, 'Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!'
44_19_29: So the city was filled with confusion, and they rushed all together into the theater, dragging along Gaius and Aristarchus, Macedonians who were Paul’s companions in travel.
44_19_30: And when Paul wanted to go in among the crowd, the disciples would not let him.
44_19_31: Then some of the Asiarchs, who were friends of his, sent to him pleading that he would not venture into the theater.
44_19_32: Some were shouting one thing and some another, for the assembly was in confusion, and most of them did not know why they had come together.
44_19_33: And they drew Alexander out of the multitude, the Jews putting him forward. And Alexander motioned with his hand and wanted to make a defense to the crowd.
44_19_34: But when they recognized that he was a Jew, they all shouted in unison for about two hours, 'Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!'
44_19_35: And when the town clerk had quieted the crowd, he said, 'Men of Ephesus, who is there who does not know that the city of the Ephesians is the temple guardian of the great Artemis, and of the sacred stone that fell from the sky?'
44_19_36: Therefore, since these things cannot be denied, you ought to keep calm and do nothing rash.
44_19_37: For you have brought these men here who are neither robbers of temples nor blasphemers of our goddess.
44_19_38: Therefore, if Demetrius and the craftsmen with him have a complaint against anyone, the courts are open, and there are proconsuls. Let them bring charges against one another.
44_19_39: But if you seek anything further, it must be decided in a legal assembly.
44_19_40: For we are in danger of being charged with rioting today, since there is no cause that we can give to justify this commotion.
44_19_41: And when he had said these things, he dismissed the assembly.
44_20_1: After the uproar had ceased, Paul called the disciples to himself, embraced them, and after saying farewell, departed to go to Macedonia.
44_20_2: Now when he had gone through those regions and had given them much encouragement, he came to Greece.
44_20_3: and stayed three months. And when a plot was made against him by the Jews as he was about to set sail for Syria, he decided to return through Macedonia.
44_20_4: And Sopater of Berea accompanied him to Asia—also Aristarchus and Secundus of the Thessalonians, and Gaius of Derbe, and Timothy, and Tychicus and Trophimus of Asia.
44_20_5: These men went on ahead and waited for us in Troas.
44_20_6: But we sailed away from Philippi after the Festival of Unleavened Bread, and in five days we reached them at Troas, where we stayed seven days.
44_20_7: Now on the first day of the week, when we were gathered together to break bread, Paul spoke to them, intending to depart on the next day, and he prolonged his message until midnight.
44_20_8: There were many lamps in the upper room where we were gathered.
44_20_9: And a young man named Eutychus, sitting on the window sill, sank into a deep sleep as Paul kept on talking. When he was overcome by sleep, he fell down from the third story and was picked up dead.
44_20_10: But Paul went down, bent over him, embraced him, and said, 'Don’t be alarmed, because he’s alive.'
44_20_11: Now when he had gone up and had broken bread and eaten, he talked a long time until dawn. Then he left.
44_20_12: And they brought the boy home alive and were greatly comforted.
44_20_13: Then we went ahead to the ship and sailed to Assos, where we were going to take Paul on board, for so he had given orders, intending himself to go on foot.
44_20_14: And when he met us at Assos, we took him on board and went on to Mitylene.
44_20_15: We sailed from there, and the next day came opposite Chios. The following day we arrived at Samos and stayed at Trogyllium. The next day we came to Miletus.
44_20_16: For Paul had decided to sail past Ephesus to avoid spending time in the province of Asia, because he was hurrying to be in Jerusalem, if possible, for the day of Pentecost.
44_20_17: From Miletus he sent to Ephesus and summoned the elders of the church.
44_20_18: And when they had come to him, he said to them: “You know, from the first day that I set foot in Asia, how I lived among you the whole time,
44_20_19: serving the Lord with all humility, with tears and trials that happened to me through the plotting of the Jews;
44_20_20: how I did not shrink from declaring to you anything that was profitable, but taught you publicly and from house to house,
44_20_21: testifying both to Jews and to Greeks of repentance toward God and faith in our Lord Jesus Christ.
44_20_22: And now, behold, I am going to Jerusalem, constrained by the Spirit, not knowing what will happen to me there,
44_20_23: except that the Holy Spirit testifies to me in every city that chains and afflictions await me.
44_20_24: But none of these things move me; nor do I count my life dear to myself, so that I may finish my race with joy, and the ministry which I received from the Lord Jesus, to testify to the gospel of the grace of God.
44_20_25: And indeed, now I know that none of you among whom I have gone about proclaiming the kingdom of God will see my face no more.
44_20_26: Therefore I declare to you this day that I am innocent of the blood of all of you,
44_20_27: for I did not shrink from declaring to you the whole counsel of God.
44_20_28: Therefore take heed to yourselves and to all the flock, among which the Holy Spirit has made you overseers, to shepherd the church of God which He purchased with His own blood.
44_20_29: For I know this, that after my departure fierce wolves will come in among you, not sparing the flock.
44_20_30: And from among your own selves will arise men speaking twisted things, to draw away the disciples after them.
44_20_31: Therefore be on the alert, remembering that for three years I did not cease night or day to warn everyone with tears.
44_20_32: So now, brethren, I commend you to God and to the word of His grace, which is able to build you up and give you an inheritance among all who are sanctified.
44_20_33: I have not coveted anyone’s silver or gold or clothing.
44_20_34: Yes, you yourselves know that these hands have provided for my necessities, and for those who were with me.
44_20_35: I have shown you in every way, by laboring like this, that you must help the weak and remember the words of the Lord Jesus, that He said, ‘It is more blessed to give than to receive.’
44_20_36: And when he had said these things, he knelt down and prayed with them all.
44_20_37: Then they all wept freely, and fell on Paul’s neck and kissed him,
44_20_38: sorrowing most of all for the words which he spoke, that they would see his face no more. And they accompanied him to the ship.
44_21_1: Now it came to pass, that when we had departed from them and set sail, running a straight course we came to Cos, the following day to Rhodes, and from there to Patara.
44_21_2: And finding a ship sailing over to Phoenicia, we went aboard and set sail.
44_21_3: When we had come in sight of Cyprus, leaving it on the left we sailed to Syria and landed at Tyre, for there the ship was to unload its cargo.
44_21_4: And finding disciples, we stayed there seven days. Through the Spirit they were telling Paul not to go on to Jerusalem.
44_21_5: When we had come to the end of those days, we departed and went on our way; and they all accompanied us, with wives and children, till we were out of the city. And we knelt down on the shore and prayed.
44_21_6: When we had taken our leave of one another, we boarded the ship, and they returned home.
44_21_7: And when we had finished our voyage from Tyre, we came to Ptolemais, greeted the brethren, and stayed with them one day.
44_21_8: On the next day we who were Paul’s companions departed and came to Caesarea, and entered the house of Philip the evangelist, who was one of the seven, and stayed with him.
44_21_9: Now this man had four virgin daughters who prophesied.
44_21_10: And as we stayed many days, a certain prophet named Agabus came down from Judea.
44_21_11: When he had come to us, he took Paul’s belt, bound his own hands and feet, and said, “Thus says the Holy Spirit, ‘So shall the Jews at Jerusalem bind the man who owns this belt, and deliver him into the hands of the Gentiles.’”
44_21_12: Now when we heard these things, both we and those from that place pleaded with him not to go up to Jerusalem.
44_21_13: Then Paul answered, “What do you mean by weeping and breaking my heart? For I am ready not only to be bound, but also to die at Jerusalem for the name of the Lord Jesus.”
44_21_14: So when he would not be persuaded, we ceased, saying, “The will of the Lord be done.”
44_21_15: And after those days we packed and went up to Jerusalem.
44_21_16: Also some of the disciples from Caesarea went with us and brought with them a certain Mnason of Cyprus, an early disciple, with whom we were to lodge.
44_21_17: And when we had come to Jerusalem, the brethren received us gladly.
44_21_18: On the following day Paul went in with us to James, and all the elders were present.
44_21_19: When he had greeted them, he reported in detail what God had done among the Gentiles through his ministry.
44_21_20: And when they heard it, they glorified God and said to him, “You see, brother, how many thousands there are among the Jews who have believed, and they are all zealous for the law;
44_21_21: but they have been informed about you that you teach all the Jews who are among the Gentiles to forsake Moses, telling them not to circumcise their children or walk according to our customs.
44_21_22: What then is to be done? They will certainly hear that you have come.
44_21_23: Therefore do what we tell you: We have four men who are under a vow;
44_21_24: Take these men and purify yourself along with them, and pay their expenses so that they may shave their heads; then everyone will know that what they were told about you amounts to nothing, but that you yourself are also careful about observing the law.
44_21_25: But concerning the Gentiles who have believed, we have written and decided that they should observe no such thing, except that they should keep themselves from food sacrificed to idols, from blood, from what is strangled, and from sexual immorality.
44_21_26: Then Paul took the men, and the next day, having purified himself along with them, entered the temple to announce the completion of the purification days when the offering would be made for each of them.
44_21_27: Now when the seven days were almost completed, the Jews from Asia, seeing him in the temple, stirred up the whole crowd and laid hands on him,
44_21_28: crying out, “Men of Israel, help! This is the man who teaches everyone everywhere against our people, our law, and this place; and furthermore, he also brought Greeks into the temple and has defiled this holy place.”
44_21_29: (For they had previously seen Trophimus the Ephesian with him in the city, and they supposed that Paul had brought him into the temple.)
44_21_30: And all the city was stirred up, and the people rushed together, seized Paul, and dragged him out of the temple; and immediately the doors were shut.
44_21_31: Now as they were seeking to kill him, word went up to the commander of the regiment that all Jerusalem was in chaos.
44_21_32: He immediately took soldiers and centurions and ran down to them; and when they saw the commander and the soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
44_21_33: Then the commander came near and took him, and commanded him to be bound with two chains; and he asked who he was and what he had done.
44_21_34: And some in the crowd were shouting one thing and some another; and since he could not learn the facts because of the uproar, he ordered him to be brought into the barracks.
44_21_35: When he reached the stairs, he had to be carried by the soldiers because of the violence of the crowd.
44_21_36: For the mass of people followed, yelling, “Get rid of him!”
44_21_37: Then as Paul was about to be led into the barracks, he said to the commander, “May I speak to you?” He replied, “Can you speak Greek?
44_21_38: Are you not the Egyptian who stirred up a revolt some time ago and led the four thousand men of the Assassins out into the wilderness?”
44_21_39: But Paul said, “I am a Jew from Tarsus, in Cilicia, a citizen of an important city; and I implore you, permit me to speak to the people.”
44_21_40: So when he had given him permission, Paul stood on the stairs and motioned with his hand to the people; and when there was a great hush, he addressed them in Aramaic, saying,
44_22_1: “Brothers and fathers, listen now to my defense before you.”
44_22_2: And when they heard that he was addressing them in the Hebrew language, they became even quieter; and he said:
44_22_3: “I am indeed a Jew, born in Tarsus of Cilicia, but brought up in this city at the feet of Gamaliel, educated according to the strict manner of the law of our fathers, being zealous for God just as all of you are today.
44_22_4: I persecuted this Way to the death, binding and putting both men and women into prisons,
44_22_5: as both the high priest and the whole council of elders can testify about me; after I received letters from them to the brothers, I traveled to Damascus to arrest those who were there and bring them to Jerusalem to be punished.
44_22_6: “Now it happened, as I journeyed and came near Damascus at about noon, suddenly a great light from heaven shone around me.
44_22_7: And I fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to me, ‘Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?’
44_22_8: So I answered, ‘Who are You, Lord?’ And He said to me, ‘I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom you are persecuting.’
44_22_9: And those who were with me indeed saw the light, but they did not hear the voice of Him who spoke to me.
44_22_10: So I said, ‘What shall I do, Lord?’ And the Lord said to me, ‘Get up and go into Damascus, and there you will be told everything that you have been assigned to do.’
44_22_11: And since I could not see because of the brightness of that light, I was led by the hand by those who were with me, and went into Damascus.
44_22_12: “Then a certain Ananias, a devout man according to the law, who had a good reputation with all the Jews living there,
44_22_13: came to me and standing by me said, ‘Brother Saul, receive your sight.’ And at that very hour I looked up at him.
44_22_14: Then he said, ‘The God of our ancestors has appointed you to know his will, to see the Righteous One, and to hear the words from his mouth;
44_22_15: for you will be a witness for him to all people of what you have seen and heard.
44_22_16: And now, why are you delaying? Get up and be baptized, and wash away your sins, calling on his name.’
44_22_17: “Now it happened, when I returned to Jerusalem and was praying in the temple, that I fell into a trance
44_22_18: and saw him telling me, ‘Hurry and get out of Jerusalem quickly, because they will not accept your testimony about me.’
44_22_19: So I said, ‘Lord, they know that in synagogue after synagogue I imprisoned and beat those who believed in you.
44_22_20: And when the blood of Your martyr Stephen was shed, I also was standing by consenting to his death, and guarding the clothes of those who were killing him.
44_22_21: Then He said to me, 'Go, for I will send you far away to the Gentiles.'
44_22_22: And they listened to him until this word, and then they raised their voices and said, 'Away with such a fellow from the earth, for he is not fit to live!'
44_22_23: Then, as they cried out and tore off their clothes and threw dust into the air,
44_22_24: the commander ordered him to be brought into the barracks, and said that he should be examined under scourging, so that he might know why they shouted so against him.
44_22_25: And as they bound him with thongs, Paul said to the centurion who stood by, 'Is it lawful for you to scourge a man who is a Roman citizen and uncondemned?'
44_22_26: When the centurion heard that, he went and told the commander, saying, 'Take care what you do, for this man is a Roman citizen.'
44_22_27: Then the commander came and said to him, 'Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?' He said, 'Yes.'
44_22_28: The commander answered, 'With a large sum I obtained this citizenship.' And Paul said, 'But I was born a citizen.'
44_22_29: Then immediately those who were about to examine him withdrew from him; and the commander was also afraid after he found out that he was a Roman citizen, and because he had bound him.
