44_22_30: The next day, because he wanted to know for certain why he was accused by the Jews, he released him from his bonds, and commanded the chief priests and all their council to appear, and brought Paul down and set him before them.
44_23_1: Then Paul, looking earnestly at the council, said, 'Men and brethren, I have lived in all good conscience before God until this day.'
44_23_2: And the high priest Ananias commanded those who stood by him to strike him on the mouth.
44_23_3: Then Paul said to him, 'God will strike you, you whitewashed wall! For you sit to judge me according to the law, and do you command me to be struck contrary to the law?'
44_23_4: And those who stood by said, 'Do you revile God’s high priest?'
44_23_5: Then Paul said, 'I did not know, brethren, that he was the high priest; for it is written,
44_23_6: But when Paul perceived that one part were Sadducees and the other Pharisees, he cried out in the council, 'Men and brethren, I am a Pharisee, the son of a Pharisee; concerning the hope and resurrection of the dead I am being judged!'
44_23_7: And when he had said this, a dissension arose between the Pharisees and the Sadducees; and the assembly was divided.
44_23_8: For Sadducees say that there is no resurrection—and no angel or spirit; but the Pharisees confess both.
44_23_9: Then there arose a loud outcry. And the scribes of the Pharisees’ party arose and protested, saying, 'We find no evil in this man; but if a spirit or an angel has spoken to him, let us not fight against God.'
44_23_10: Now when there arose a great dissension, the commander, fearing lest Paul might be pulled to pieces by them, commanded the soldiers to go down and take him by force from among them, and bring him into the barracks.
44_23_11: But the following night the Lord stood by him and said, 'Be of good cheer, Paul; for as you have testified for Me in Jerusalem, so you must also bear witness at Rome.'
44_23_12: And when it was day, some of the Jews banded together and bound themselves under an oath, saying that they would neither eat nor drink till they had killed Paul.
44_23_13: Now there were more than forty who had formed this conspiracy.
44_23_14: They came to the chief priests and elders, and said, 'We have bound ourselves under a great oath that we will eat nothing until we have killed Paul.'
44_23_15: Now you, therefore, together with the council, suggest to the commander that he be brought down to you tomorrow, as though you were going to make further inquiries concerning him; but we are ready to kill him before he comes near.'
44_23_16: So when Paul’s sister’s son heard of their ambush, he went and entered the barracks and told Paul.
44_23_17: Then Paul called one of the centurions to him and said, 'Take this young man to the commander, for he has something to tell him.'
44_23_18: So he took him and brought him to the commander and said, 'Paul the prisoner called me to him and asked me to bring this young man to you. He has something to say to you.'
44_23_19: Then the commander took him by the hand, went aside, and asked privately, 'What is it that you have to tell me?'
44_23_20: And he said, 'The Jews have agreed to ask that you bring Paul down to the council tomorrow, as though they were going to inquire more fully about him.'
44_23_21: But do not yield to them, for more than forty of them lie in wait for him, men who have bound themselves by an oath that they will neither eat nor drink till they have killed him; and now they are ready, waiting for the promise from you.'
44_23_22: So the commander let the young man depart, and commanded him, 'Tell no one that you have revealed these things to me.'
44_23_23: And he called for two centurions, saying, 'Prepare two hundred soldiers, seventy horsemen, and two hundred spearmen to go to Caesarea at the third hour of the night;
44_23_24: and provide mounts to set Paul on, and bring him safely to Felix the governor.'
44_23_25: He wrote a letter in the following manner:
44_23_26: Claudius Lysias, To the most excellent governor Felix: Greetings.
44_23_27: This man was seized by the Jews and was about to be killed by them. Coming with the troops I rescued him, having learned that he was a Roman citizen.
44_23_28: And when I wanted to know the reason they accused him, I brought him before their council.
44_23_29: I found out that he was accused concerning questions of their law, but had nothing charged against him deserving of death or chains.
44_23_30: And when it was disclosed to me that the Jews lay in wait for the man, I sent him immediately to you, and also commanded his accusers to state before you the charges against him. Farewell.
44_23_31: So the soldiers, as they were commanded, took Paul and brought him by night to Antipatris.
44_23_32: The next day they returned to the barracks, allowing the cavalry to go on with him.
44_23_33: When they came to Caesarea and had delivered the letter to the governor, they also presented Paul to him.
44_23_34: After he read it, he asked what province he was from. When he learned that he was from Cilicia,
44_23_35: he said, “I will give you a hearing when your accusers arrive.” And he commanded him to be kept in Herod’s Praetorium.
44_24_1: Now after five days Ananias the high priest came down with the elders and a certain orator named Tertullus. These men presented their case against Paul to the governor.
44_24_2: And when he was called upon, Tertullus began his accusation, saying: “Since through you we enjoy much peace, and since by your foresight, most excellent Felix, reforms are being made for this nation,
44_24_3: we acknowledge this in every way and everywhere, most excellent Felix, with utmost gratitude.
44_24_4: But, so that I will not burden you any further, I request that you would be kind enough to give us a brief hearing.
44_24_5: For we have found this man to be a plague, an agitator among all the Jews throughout the Roman world, and a ringleader of the sect of the Nazarenes.
44_24_6: He even tried to desecrate the temple, and so we apprehended him. By examining him yourself you will be able to discern the truth about these charges we are bringing against him.
44_24_7: But Lysias the commander came along, and with much violence took him out of our hands,
44_24_8: ordering his accusers to come before you. By examining him yourself concerning all these matters you will be able to ascertain the things of which we accuse him.
44_24_9: The Jews also joined in the attack, alleging that these things were true.
44_24_10: Then Paul, after the governor had nodded to him to speak, answered: “Knowing that for many years you have been a judge over this nation, I cheerfully make my defense,
44_24_11: You can verify for yourself that it is no more than twelve days since I went up to worship in Jerusalem.
44_24_12: And they didn’t find me arguing with anyone or causing a disturbance among the crowd, either in the temple or in the synagogues or anywhere in the city.
44_24_13: Neither can they prove the charges they are now making against me.
44_24_14: But I admit this to you: I worship the God of my ancestors according to the Way, which they call a sect, believing everything that is in accordance with the law and written in the prophets.
44_24_15: I have a hope in God, which these men themselves also accept, that there will be a resurrection, both of the righteous and the unrighteous.
44_24_16: Because of this, I always try to maintain a clear conscience before God and all people.
44_24_17: Now after many years I came to bring charitable gifts and offerings to my people,
44_24_18: while I was doing this, some Jews from Asia found me ritually purified in the temple, without a crowd and without any uproar.
44_24_19: It is they who ought to be here before you to bring charges, if they have anything against me.
44_24_20: Or let these men here state what wrongdoing they found in me when I stood before the Sanhedrin,
44_24_21: other than this one statement I shouted while standing among them, ‘Today I am on trial before you concerning the resurrection of the dead.’
44_24_22: Since Felix was well informed about the Way, he adjourned the hearing, saying, “When Lysias the commander comes down, I will decide your case.”
44_24_23: He ordered that the centurion keep Paul under guard, though he could have some freedom, and that he should not prevent any of his friends from meeting his needs.
44_24_24: Several days later, when Felix came with his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, he sent for Paul and listened to him on the subject of faith in Christ Jesus.
44_24_25: Now as he spoke about righteousness, self-control, and the judgment to come, Felix became afraid and replied, “Leave for now, but when I have an opportunity I’ll call for you.”
44_24_26: At the same time he was also hoping that Paul would offer him money. So he sent for him quite often and conversed with him.
44_24_27: But after two years had passed, Porcius Festus succeeded Felix, and because Felix wanted to do the Jews a favor, he left Paul in prison.
44_25_1: Three days after Festus arrived in the province, he went up to Jerusalem from Caesarea.
44_25_2: The chief priests and the leaders of the Jews presented their case against Paul to him; and they appealed,
44_25_3: asking for a favor against Paul, that Festus summon him to Jerusalem. They were, in fact, preparing an ambush along the road to kill him.
44_25_4: Festus, however, answered that Paul should be kept at Caesarea, and that he himself was about to go there shortly.
44_25_5: “Therefore,” he said, “let those of you who have authority go down with me and accuse him, if he has done anything wrong.”
44_25_6: When he had spent not more than eight or ten days among them, he went down to Caesarea. The next day, seated at the tribunal, he commanded Paul to be brought in.
44_25_7: When he arrived, the Jews who had come down from Jerusalem stood around him and brought many serious charges that they were not able to prove.
44_25_8: While he answered for himself, “Neither against the law of the Jews, nor against the temple, nor against Caesar have I committed any offense at all.”
44_25_9: But Festus, wanting to do the Jews a favor, answered Paul and said, “Are you willing to go up to Jerusalem and there be tried before me concerning these things?”
44_25_10: So Paul said, “I stand at Caesar’s judgment seat, where I ought to be tried. To the Jews I have done no wrong, as you very well know.”
44_25_11: For if I am a wrongdoer and have committed anything deserving of death, I do not object to dying; but if there is nothing to what these men accuse me of, no one can hand me over to them. I appeal to Caesar.”
44_25_12: Then Festus, when he had conferred with his council, answered, “You have appealed to Caesar? To Caesar you shall go!”
44_25_13: And after some days King Agrippa and Bernice came to Caesarea to pay their respects to Festus.
44_25_14: When they had been there many days, Festus laid Paul’s case before the king, saying: “There is a certain man left a prisoner by Felix,
44_25_15: about whom the chief priests and the elders of the Jews informed me, when I was in Jerusalem, asking for a sentence of condemnation against him.
44_25_16: To them I answered, ‘It is not the custom of the Romans to deliver any man to destruction before the accused meets his accusers face to face and has an opportunity to make his defense concerning the charges against him.’
44_25_17: Therefore when they had come together, without any delay, the next day I sat on the judgment seat and commanded the man to be brought in.
44_25_18: When the accusers stood up, they brought no charge against him of such evils as I supposed,
44_25_19: but had certain points of dispute with him about their own religion and about a certain Jesus, who was dead, but whom Paul asserted to be alive.
44_25_20: And because I was uncertain about such questions, I asked whether he was willing to go to Jerusalem and there be tried concerning these matters.
44_25_21: But when Paul appealed to be reserved for the decision of Augustus, I commanded him to be kept until I could send him to Caesar.”
44_25_22: Then Agrippa said to Festus, “I also would like to hear the man myself.” “Tomorrow,” he said, “you shall hear him.”
44_25_23: So the next day, when Agrippa and Bernice had come with great pomp and had entered the auditorium with the commanders and the prominent men of the city, at Festus’ command Paul was brought in.
44_25_24: And Festus said: “King Agrippa and all the men who are here present with us, you see this man about whom the whole Jewish community petitioned me, both in Jerusalem and here, crying out that he ought not to live any longer.
44_25_25: But when I found that he had committed nothing deserving of death, and that he himself had appealed to Augustus, I decided to send him.
44_25_26: I have nothing certain to write to my lord concerning him. Therefore I have brought him out before you, and especially before you, King Agrippa, so that after the examination has taken place I may have something to write.
44_25_27: For it seems unreasonable to send a prisoner and not to specify the charges against him.”
44_26_1: Then Agrippa said to Paul, “You are permitted to speak for yourself.” So Paul stretched out his hand and began his defense:
44_26_2: “I consider myself fortunate, King Agrippa, because today I shall answer for myself before you concerning all the things of which I am accused by the Jews,
44_26_3: especially because you are familiar with all the customs and controversies of the Jews. Therefore I beg you to listen to me patiently.
44_26_4: “My manner of life from my youth, which was spent from the beginning among my own nation and in Jerusalem, is known by all the Jews.
44_26_5: They have known for a long time, if they are willing to testify, that according to the strictest sect of our religion I lived a Pharisee.
44_26_6: And now I stand on trial because of the hope in what God promised to our ancestors.
44_26_7: To this promise our twelve tribes hope to attain, as they earnestly serve God night and day. For this hope’s sake, King Agrippa, I am accused by the Jews.
44_26_8: Why is it thought incredible by any of you that God raises the dead?
44_26_9: “Indeed, I myself was convinced that I ought to do many things contrary to the name of Jesus of Nazareth.
44_26_10: This I also did in Jerusalem, and many of the saints I locked up in prison, having received authority from the chief priests; and when they were put to death, I cast my vote against them.
44_26_11: And I punished them often in every synagogue and tried to make them blaspheme; and being exceedingly enraged against them, I persecuted them even to foreign cities.
44_26_12: “While thus occupied, as I journeyed to Damascus with authority and commission from the chief priests,
44_26_13: at midday, O king, along the road I saw a light from heaven, brighter than the sun, shining around me and those who journeyed with me.
44_26_14: And when we all had fallen to the ground, I heard a voice speaking to me in the Hebrew language, ‘Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting Me? It is hard for you to kick against the goads.’
44_26_15: So I said, ‘Who are You, Lord?’ And He said, ‘I am Jesus, whom you are persecuting.
44_26_16: But rise and stand on your feet; for I have appeared to you for this purpose, to appoint you as a servant and witness to the things in which you have seen me and to those in which I will appear to you,
44_26_17: I will rescue you from your people and from the Gentiles, to whom I am sending you,
44_26_18: to open their eyes, so that they may turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God, that they may receive forgiveness of sins and a share among those who are sanctified by faith in me.’
44_26_19: “Therefore, King Agrippa, I was not disobedient to the heavenly vision,
44_26_20: but declared first to those in Damascus and in Jerusalem, and throughout all the region of Judea, and then to the Gentiles, that they should repent and turn to God, performing deeds in keeping with their repentance.
44_26_21: For this reason the Jews seized me in the temple and tried to kill me.
44_26_22: Therefore, having obtained help from God, to this day I stand, witnessing both to small and great, saying nothing other than what the prophets and Moses said would come to pass—
44_26_23: that the Christ must suffer, and that, as the first to rise from the dead, he would proclaim light both to our people and to the Gentiles.
44_26_24: Now as he thus made his defense, Festus said with a loud voice, “Paul, you are out of your mind; your great learning is driving you mad!”
44_26_25: But he said, “I am not out of my mind, most excellent Festus, but I am speaking true and rational words.
44_26_26: For the king knows about these matters, and I can speak boldly to him, for I am convinced that none of these things has escaped his notice, since this was not done in a corner.
44_26_27: King Agrippa, do you believe the prophets? I know that you believe.”
44_26_28: Then Agrippa said to Paul, “In a short time would you persuade me to be a Christian?”
44_26_29: And Paul said, “Whether short or long, I would to God that not only you but also all who hear me today might become such as I am—except for these chains.”
44_26_30: When he had said these things, the king stood up, as well as the governor and Bernice and those who sat with them;
44_26_31: and when they had gone aside, they talked among themselves, saying, “This man is doing nothing to deserve death or imprisonment.”
44_26_32: Then Agrippa said to Festus, “This man could have been set free if he had not appealed to Caesar.”
44_27_1: And when it was decided that we should sail to Italy, they delivered Paul and some other prisoners to a centurion named Julius, of the Imperial Regiment.
44_27_2: So, entering a ship of Adramyttium, we put to sea, intending to sail to ports along the coast of Asia. Aristarchus, a Macedonian of Thessalonica, was with us.
44_27_3: And the next day we put in at Sidon, and Julius treated Paul kindly and allowed him to go to his friends to receive their care.
44_27_4: When we had put to sea from there, we sailed under the shelter of Cyprus because the winds were contrary.
44_27_5: And when we had sailed across the open sea along the coast of Cilicia and Pamphylia, we came to Myra in Lycia.
44_27_6: There the centurion found an Alexandrian ship sailing for Italy and put us on board.
44_27_7: When we had sailed slowly for many days and arrived with difficulty off Cnidus, the wind not permitting us to proceed, we sailed under the shelter of Crete off Salmone.
44_27_8: Passing it with difficulty, we came to a place called Fair Havens, near the city of Lasea.
44_27_9: Now when much time had been spent, and sailing was now dangerous because the Fast was already over, Paul advised them,
44_27_10: saying, “Men, I perceive that this voyage will end with disaster and much loss, not only of the cargo and ship, but also our lives.”
44_27_11: Nevertheless, the centurion was more persuaded by the helmsman and the owner of the ship than by what was being said by Paul.
44_27_12: And because the harbor was not suitable to winter in, the majority decided to set sail from there, hoping somehow to reach Phoenix, a harbor of Crete facing both southwest and northwest, and to winter there.
44_27_13: When a gentle south wind sprang up, they thought they had achieved their purpose, so they weighed anchor and sailed along the shore of Crete.
44_27_14: But soon a tempestuous wind, called the northeaster, struck down from the land.
44_27_15: So when the ship was caught and could not head into the wind, we let her drive.
44_27_16: And running under the shelter of a small island called Clauda, we were barely able to get control of the skiff.
44_27_17: When they had taken it on board, they used cables to undergird the ship; and fearing they would run aground on the Syrtis, they lowered the gear and thus were driven along.
44_27_18: And because we were being severely battered by the storm, they began to jettison the cargo the next day.
44_27_19: On the third day, they threw the ship’s tackle overboard with their own hands.
44_27_20: Now when neither sun nor stars appeared for many days, and the severe storm kept raging, finally all hope was fading that we would be saved.
44_27_21: But after long abstinence from food, then Paul stood in the midst of them and said, “Men, you should have listened to me and not have set sail from Crete and incurred this damage and loss.
44_27_22: And now I urge you to take heart, for there will be no loss of life among you, but only of the ship.
44_27_23: For last night an angel of the God to whom I belong and whom I serve stood by me,
44_27_24: saying, ‘Do not be afraid, Paul; you must stand before Caesar, and indeed God has graciously given you all those who are sailing with you.’
44_27_25: Therefore take heart, men, for I believe God that it will be just as it was told to me.
44_27_26: However, we must run aground on a certain island.”
44_27_27: Now when the fourteenth night had come, as we were being driven across the Adriatic Sea, about midnight the sailors suspected that they were nearing land.
44_27_28: And they took soundings and found it to be twenty fathoms; and when they had gone a little farther, they took soundings again and found it to be fifteen fathoms.
44_27_29: Then, fearing that we might run aground on the rocks, they dropped four anchors from the stern and prayed for daylight to come.
44_27_30: And as the sailors were seeking to escape from the ship, they had lowered the ship's boat into the sea under pretense of putting out anchors from the bow.
44_27_31: Paul said to the centurion and the soldiers, “Unless these men stay in the ship, you cannot be saved.”
44_27_32: Then the soldiers cut away the ropes holding the skiff and let it drop away.
44_27_33: And as day was about to dawn, Paul urged them all to take food, saying, “Today is the fourteenth day that you have continued in suspense and without food, having taken nothing.
44_27_34: Therefore I urge you to take some food, for this is for your survival, since not a hair will fall from the head of any of you.”
44_27_35: And when he had said these things, he took bread and gave thanks to God in the presence of them all; and when he had broken it, he began to eat.
44_27_36: Then they all were encouraged and took food themselves.
44_27_37: And in all, we were two hundred and seventy-six persons on the ship.
44_27_38: So when they had eaten enough, they began to lighten the ship by throwing the grain overboard into the sea.
44_27_39: When daylight came, they did not recognize the land but sighted a bay with a beach; they planned to run the ship ashore if they could.
44_27_40: And they cut off the anchors and left them in the sea, while loosening the ropes that tied the rudders; then they hoisted the foresail to the wind and made for the beach.
44_27_41: But striking a sandbar where two seas met, they ran the ship aground; the bow jammed fast and remained immovable, while the stern began to break up by the pounding of the waves.
44_27_42: And the soldiers’ plan was to kill the prisoners so that no one could swim away and escape.
44_27_43: But the centurion kept them from carrying out their plan because he wanted to save Paul, and so he ordered those who could swim to jump overboard first and get to land.
44_27_44: And the rest were to follow, some on planks and some on debris from the ship. In this way, everyone safely reached the shore.
44_28_1: Now when they had escaped, they then found out that the island was called Malta.
44_28_2: And the natives showed us unusual kindness, for they kindled a fire and welcomed us all, because it had begun to rain and was cold.
44_28_3: But when Paul had gathered a bundle of sticks and laid them on the fire, a viper came out because of the heat and fastened itself on his hand.
44_28_4: So when the natives saw the creature hanging from his hand, they said to one another, “No doubt this man is a murderer. Though he has escaped from the sea, Justice has not allowed him to live.”
44_28_5: But he shook the snake off into the fire and suffered no harm.
44_28_6: However, they were expecting that he would swell up or suddenly fall down dead. But after they had waited a long time and saw nothing unusual happen to him, they changed their minds and said that he was a god.
44_28_7: In that region there was an estate of the leading citizen of the island, whose name was Publius, who received us and entertained us hospitably for three days.
44_28_8: And it happened that the father of Publius lay sick with fever and dysentery. Paul went in to him and prayed, and laying his hands on him, he healed him.
44_28_9: So when this was done, the rest of those on the island who had diseases also came and were healed.
44_28_10: They also honored us greatly, and when we were about to sail, they put on board whatever we needed.
44_28_11: After three months we set sail in an Alexandrian ship that had wintered at the island, with the Twin Gods as its figurehead.
44_28_12: And putting in at Syracuse, we stayed there for three days.
44_28_13: From there, after making a circuit along the coast, we reached Rhegium. After one day a south wind sprang up, and the second day we came to Puteoli.
44_28_14: Where we found brothers and were invited to stay with them for seven days. And so we came to Rome.
44_28_15: And from there, when the brethren heard about us, they came to meet us as far as the Forum of Appius and the Three Taverns. When Paul saw them, he thanked God and took courage.
44_28_16: Now when we entered Rome, Paul was allowed to live by himself with the soldier who guarded him.
44_28_17: And it came to pass after three days that Paul called the leaders of the Jews together. So when they had come together, he said to them: “Brothers, though I have done nothing against our people or the customs of our fathers, yet I was delivered as a prisoner from Jerusalem into the hands of the Romans.
44_28_18: Who, when they had examined me, wanted to let me go, because there was no cause for putting me to death.
44_28_19: But when the Jews spoke against it, I was compelled to appeal to Caesar, not that I had anything of which to accuse my nation.
44_28_20: For this reason I have called for you, to see you and speak with you, because for the hope of Israel I am bound with this chain.”
44_28_21: Then they said to him, “We have received no letters from Judea about you, and none of the brothers coming here has reported or spoken any evil about you.
44_28_22: But we desire to hear from you what your views are, for concerning this sect, we know that it is spoken against everywhere.”
44_28_23: So when they had appointed a day for him, many came to him at his lodging. From morning till evening he expounded and testified about the kingdom of God, trying to persuade them concerning Jesus from both the Law of Moses and the Prophets.
44_28_24: And some were persuaded by what he said, but others did not believe.
44_28_25: So when they did not agree among themselves, they departed after Paul had made one statement: 'The Holy Spirit spoke rightly through Isaiah the prophet to your fathers,'
44_28_26: saying, 'Go to this people and say: You will indeed hear but never understand, and you will indeed see but never perceive;'
44_28_27: For the hearts of this people have grown dull, their ears are hard of hearing, and they have closed their eyes; otherwise they might see with their eyes and hear with their ears, understand with their heart and turn, and I would heal them.
44_28_28: Therefore let it be known to you that this salvation of God has been sent to the Gentiles; they will listen.
44_28_29: And when he had said these words, the Jews departed and had a great dispute among themselves.
44_28_30: Then Paul dwelt two whole years in his own rented house and welcomed all who came to him,
44_28_31: proclaiming the kingdom of God and teaching about the Lord Jesus Christ with all boldness and without hindrance.
19_1_1: Blessed is the man who walks not in the counsel of the wicked, nor stands in the path of sinners, nor sits in the seat of scoffers.
19_1_2: But his delight is in the law of the LORD, and in His law he meditates day and night.
19_1_3: He shall be like a tree planted by the rivers of water, that brings forth its fruit in its season, whose leaf also shall not wither; and whatever he does shall prosper.
19_1_4: The wicked are not so, but are like the chaff which the wind drives away.
19_1_5: Therefore the wicked will not stand in the judgment, nor sinners in the congregation of the righteous.
19_1_6: For the LORD knows the way of the righteous, but the way of the wicked shall perish.
19_2_1: Why do the nations rage, and the peoples plot a vain thing?
19_2_2: The kings of the earth set themselves, and the rulers take counsel together, against the LORD and against His Anointed, saying,
19_2_3: Let us break their bonds in pieces and cast away their cords from us.
19_2_4: He who sits in the heavens shall laugh; the Lord shall hold them in derision.
19_2_5: Then He shall speak to them in His wrath, and distress them in His deep displeasure:
19_2_6: Yet I have set My King on My holy hill of Zion.
19_2_7: I will declare the decree: The LORD has said to Me, 'You are My Son, today I have begotten You.
19_2_8: Ask of Me, and I will give You the nations for Your inheritance, and the ends of the earth for Your possession.
19_2_9: You shall break them with a rod of iron; You shall dash them to pieces like a potter’s vessel.
19_2_10: Now therefore, be wise, O kings; be instructed, you judges of the earth.
19_2_11: Serve the LORD with fear, and rejoice with trembling.
19_2_12: Kiss the Son, lest He be angry, and you perish in the way, when His wrath is kindled but a little. Blessed are all those who put their trust in Him.
19_3_1: LORD, how they have increased who trouble me! Many are they who rise up against me.
19_3_2: Many are they who say of me, "There is no help for him in God." Selah
19_3_3: But You, O LORD, are a shield for me, my glory and the One who lifts up my head.
19_3_4: I cried to the LORD with my voice, and He heard me from His holy hill. Selah
19_3_5: I lay down and slept; I awoke, for the LORD sustained me.
19_3_6: I will not be afraid of ten thousands of people who have set themselves against me all around.
19_3_7: Arise, O LORD; save me, O my God! For You have struck all my enemies on the cheekbone; You have broken the teeth of the wicked.
19_3_8: Salvation belongs to the LORD. Your blessing is upon Your people. Selah
19_4_1: Hear me when I call, O God of my righteousness! You have relieved me in my distress; have mercy on me, and hear my prayer.
19_4_2: How long, O you sons of men, will you turn my glory to shame? How long will you love worthlessness and seek falsehood? Selah
19_4_3: But know that the LORD has set apart for Himself him who is godly; the LORD will hear when I call to Him.
19_4_4: Be angry, and do not sin. Meditate within your heart on your bed, and be still. Selah
19_4_5: Offer the sacrifices of righteousness, and put your trust in the LORD.
19_4_6: There are many who say, "Who will show us any good?" LORD, lift up the light of Your countenance upon us.
19_4_7: You have put gladness in my heart, more than in the season that their grain and wine increased.
19_4_8: I will both lie down in peace, and sleep; for You alone, O LORD, make me dwell in safety.
19_5_1: Give ear to my words, O LORD, consider my meditation.
19_5_2: Give heed to the voice of my cry, my King and my God, for to You I will pray.
19_5_3: My voice You shall hear in the morning, O LORD; in the morning I will direct it to You, and I will look up.
19_5_4: For You are not a God who takes pleasure in wickedness, nor shall evil dwell with You.
19_5_5: The boastful shall not stand in Your sight; You hate all workers of iniquity.
19_5_6: You shall destroy those who speak falsehood; the LORD abhors the bloodthirsty and deceitful man.
19_5_7: But as for me, I will come into Your house in the abundance of Your steadfast love; In fear of You I will worship toward Your holy temple.
19_5_8: Lead me, O LORD, in Your righteousness because of my enemies; Make Your way straight before me.
19_5_9: For there is no faithfulness in their mouth; Their inward part is destruction; Their throat is an open grave; They flatter with their tongue.
19_5_10: Pronounce them guilty, O God! Let them fall by their own counsels; Cast them out in the multitude of their transgressions, For they have rebelled against You.
19_5_11: But let all those rejoice who put their trust in You; Let them ever shout for joy, because You defend them; Let those also who love Your name be joyful in You.
19_5_12: For You, O LORD, will bless the righteous; With favor You will surround him as with a shield.
19_6_1: O LORD, do not rebuke me in Your anger, Nor chasten me in Your hot displeasure.
19_6_2: Have mercy on me, O LORD, for I am weak; O LORD, heal me, for my bones are troubled.
19_6_3: My soul also is greatly troubled; But You, O LORD—how long?
19_6_4: Return, O LORD, deliver me! Oh, save me for Your mercies’ sake!
19_6_5: For in death there is no remembrance of You; In the grave who will give You thanks?
19_6_6: I am weary with my groaning; All night I make my bed swim; I drench my couch with my tears.
19_6_7: My eye wastes away because of grief; It grows old because of all my enemies.
19_6_8: Depart from me, all you workers of iniquity; For the LORD has heard the voice of my weeping.
19_6_9: The LORD has heard my supplication; The LORD will receive my prayer.
19_6_10: Let all my enemies be ashamed and greatly troubled; Let them turn back and be ashamed suddenly.
19_7_1: O LORD my God, in You I put my trust; Save me from all those who persecute me; And deliver me,
19_7_2: Lest they tear me like a lion, Rending me in pieces, while there is none to deliver.
19_7_3: O LORD my God, if I have done this: If there is iniquity in my hands,
19_7_4: If I have repaid evil to him who was at peace with me, Or have plundered my enemy without cause,
19_7_5: Let the enemy pursue me and overtake me; Yes, let him trample my life to the earth, And lay my honor in the dust. Selah
19_7_6: Arise, O LORD, in Your anger; Lift Yourself up because of the rage of my enemies; Rise up for me to the judgment You have commanded!
19_7_7: So the congregation of the peoples shall surround You; For their sakes, therefore, return on high.
19_7_8: The LORD shall judge the peoples; Judge me, O LORD, according to my righteousness, And according to my integrity within me.
19_7_9: Oh, let the wickedness of the wicked come to an end, But establish the just; For the righteous God tests the hearts and minds.
19_7_10: My defense is of God, Who saves the upright in heart.
19_7_11: God is a just judge, And God is angry with the wicked every day.
19_7_12: If he does not turn back, He will sharpen His sword; He bends His bow and makes it ready.
19_7_13: He also prepares for Himself instruments of death; He makes His arrows into fiery shafts.
19_7_14: Behold, the wicked brings forth iniquity; Yes, he conceives trouble and brings forth falsehood.
19_7_15: He made a pit and dug it out, And has fallen into the ditch which he made.
19_7_16: His trouble shall return upon his own head, And his violent dealing shall come down on his own crown.
19_7_17: I will praise the LORD according to His righteousness, And will sing praise to the name of the LORD Most High.
19_8_1: O LORD, our Lord, How excellent is Your name in all the earth, Who have set Your glory above the heavens!
19_8_2: Out of the mouth of babes and nursing infants You have ordained strength, Because of Your enemies, That You may silence the enemy and the avenger.
19_8_3: When I consider Your heavens, the work of Your fingers, The moon and the stars, which You have ordained,
19_8_4: What is man that You are mindful of him, And the son of man that You visit him?
19_8_5: For You have made him a little lower than the angels, And You have crowned him with glory and honor.
19_8_6: You have made him to have dominion over the works of Your hands; You have put all things under his feet,
19_8_7: All sheep and oxen— Even the beasts of the field,
19_8_8: The birds of the sky, and the fish of the sea that pass through the paths of the seas.
19_8_9: O LORD, our Lord, how majestic is Your name in all the earth!
19_9_1: I will praise You, O LORD, with my whole heart; I will tell of all Your marvelous works.
19_9_2: I will be glad and rejoice in You; I will sing praise to Your name, O Most High.
19_9_3: When my enemies turn back, they shall fall and perish at Your presence.
19_9_4: For You have maintained my just cause; You sat on the throne judging in righteousness.
19_9_5: You have rebuked the nations, You have destroyed the wicked; You have blotted out their name forever and ever.
19_9_6: O enemy, destructions are finished forever! And you have destroyed cities; even their memory has perished.
19_9_7: But the LORD shall endure forever; He has prepared His throne for judgment.
19_9_8: He shall judge the world in righteousness, and He shall administer judgment for the peoples in uprightness.
19_9_9: The LORD also will be a refuge for the oppressed, a refuge in times of trouble.
19_9_10: And those who know Your name will put their trust in You; for You, LORD, have not forsaken those who seek You.
19_9_11: Sing praises to the LORD, who dwells in Zion! Declare His deeds among the people.
19_9_12: When He avenges blood, He remembers them; He does not forget the cry of the humble.
19_9_13: Have mercy on me, O LORD! Consider my trouble from those who hate me, You who lift me up from the gates of death,
19_9_14: That I may tell of all Your praise in the gates of the daughter of Zion. I will rejoice in Your salvation.
19_9_15: The nations have sunk down in the pit which they made; in the net which they hid, their own foot is caught.
19_9_16: The LORD is known by the judgment He executes; the wicked is snared in the work of his own hands. Selah
19_9_17: The wicked shall be turned into hell, and all the nations that forget God.
19_9_18: For the needy shall not always be forgotten; the expectation of the poor shall not perish forever.
19_9_19: Arise, O LORD, do not let man prevail; let the nations be judged in Your sight.
19_9_20: Put them in fear, O LORD, that the nations may know themselves to be but men. Selah
19_10_1: Why do You stand afar off, O LORD? Why do You hide in times of trouble?
19_10_2: The wicked in his pride persecutes the poor; let them be caught in the plots which they have devised.
19_10_3: For the wicked boasts of his heart’s desire; he blesses the greedy and renounces the LORD.
19_10_4: The wicked in his proud countenance does not seek God; God is in none of his thoughts.
19_10_5: His ways are always prospering; Your judgments are far above, out of his sight; as for all his enemies, he sneers at them.
19_10_6: He has said in his heart, “I shall not be moved; I shall never be in adversity.”
19_10_7: His mouth is full of cursing and deceit and oppression; under his tongue is trouble and iniquity.
19_10_8: He sits in the lurking places of the villages; in the secret places he murders the innocent; his eyes are secretly fixed on the helpless.
19_10_9: He lies in wait secretly, as a lion in his den; he lies in wait to catch the poor; he catches the poor when he draws him into his net.
19_10_10: So he crouches, he lies low, that the helpless may fall by his strength.
19_10_11: He has said in his heart, “God has forgotten; He hides His face; He will never see.”
19_10_12: Arise, O LORD! O God, lift up Your hand! Do not forget the humble.
19_10_13: Why do the wicked renounce God? He has said in his heart, “You will not require an account.”
19_10_14: But You have seen, for You observe trouble and grief, to repay it by Your hand. The helpless commits himself to You; You are the helper of the fatherless.
19_10_15: Break the arm of the wicked and the evil man; seek out his wickedness until You find none.
19_10_16: The LORD is King forever and ever; the nations have perished out of His land.
19_10_17: LORD, You have heard the desire of the humble; You will prepare their heart; You will cause Your ear to hear,
19_10_18: To do justice to the fatherless and the oppressed, that the man of the earth may oppress no more.
19_11_1: In the LORD I put my trust; How can you say to my soul, “Flee as a bird to your mountain”?
19_11_2: For look! The wicked bend their bow, They make ready their arrow on the string, That they may shoot secretly at the upright in heart.
19_11_3: If the foundations are destroyed, What can the righteous do?
19_11_4: The LORD is in His holy temple, The LORD’s throne is in heaven; His eyes behold, His eyelids test the sons of men.
19_11_5: The LORD tests the righteous, But the wicked and the one who loves violence His soul hates.
19_11_6: Upon the wicked He will rain coals; Fire and brimstone and a burning wind Shall be the portion of their cup.
19_11_7: For the LORD is righteous, He loves righteousness; His countenance beholds the upright.
19_12_1: Help, LORD, for the godly man ceases! For the faithful disappear from among the sons of men.
19_12_2: They speak idly everyone with his neighbor; With flattering lips and a double heart they speak.
19_12_3: May the LORD cut off all flattering lips, And the tongue that speaks proud things,
19_12_4: Who have said, “With our tongue we will prevail; Our lips are our own; Who is lord over us?”
19_12_5: “For the oppression of the poor, for the sighing of the needy, Now I will arise,” says the LORD; “I will set him in the safety for which he yearns.”
19_12_6: The words of the LORD are pure words, Like silver tried in a furnace of earth, Purified seven times.
19_12_7: You shall keep them, O LORD, You shall preserve them from this generation forever.
19_12_8: The wicked prowl on every side, When vileness is exalted among the sons of men.
19_13_1: How long, O LORD? Will You forget me forever? How long will You hide Your face from me?
19_13_2: How long shall I take counsel in my soul, Having sorrow in my heart daily? How long will my enemy be exalted over me?
19_13_3: Consider and hear me, O LORD my God; Enlighten my eyes, Lest I sleep the sleep of death;
19_13_4: Lest my enemy say, “I have prevailed against him”; Lest those who trouble me rejoice when I am moved.
19_13_5: But I have trusted in Your mercy; My heart shall rejoice in Your salvation.
19_13_6: I will sing to the LORD, Because He has dealt bountifully with me.
19_14_1: The fool has said in his heart, “There is no God.” They are corrupt, They have done abominable works, There is none who does good.
19_14_2: The LORD looks down from heaven upon the children of men, To see if there are any who understand, who seek God.
19_14_3: They have all turned aside, They have together become corrupt; There is none who does good, No, not one.
19_14_4: Have all the workers of iniquity no knowledge, Who eat up my people as they eat bread, And do not call on the LORD?
19_14_5: There they are in great fear, For God is with the generation of the righteous.
19_14_6: You shame the counsel of the poor, But the LORD is his refuge.
19_14_7: Oh, that the salvation of Israel would come out of Zion! When the LORD brings back the captivity of His people, Let Jacob rejoice and Israel be glad.
19_15_1: LORD, who may abide in Your tabernacle? Who may dwell in Your holy hill?
19_15_2: He who walks uprightly, And works righteousness, And speaks the truth in his heart;
19_15_3: He who does not backbite with his tongue, Nor does evil to his neighbor, Nor does he take up a reproach against his friend;
19_15_4: In whose eyes a vile person is despised, But he honors those who fear the LORD; He who swears to his own hurt and does not change;
19_15_5: He who does not put out his money at usury, Nor does he take a bribe against the innocent. He who does these things shall never be moved.
19_16_1: Preserve me, O God, for in You I put my trust.
19_16_2: O my soul, you have said to the LORD, “You are my Lord, My goodness is nothing apart from You.”
19_16_3: As for the saints who are on the earth, “They are the excellent ones, in whom is all my delight.”
19_16_4: Their sorrows shall be multiplied who hasten after another god; Their drink offerings of blood I will not offer, Nor take up their names on my lips.
19_16_5: O LORD, You are the portion of my inheritance and my cup; You maintain my lot.
19_16_6: The lines have fallen to me in pleasant places; Yes, I have a good inheritance.
19_16_7: I will bless the LORD who has given me counsel; My heart also instructs me in the night seasons.
19_16_8: I have set the LORD always before me; because He is at my right hand, I shall not be shaken.
19_16_9: Therefore my heart is glad, and my whole being rejoices; my flesh also will rest in hope.
19_16_10: For You will not leave my soul in Sheol, nor will You allow Your Holy One to see decay.
19_16_11: You will show me the path of life; in Your presence is fullness of joy; at Your right hand are pleasures forevermore.
19_17_1: Hear a just cause, O LORD, attend to my cry; give ear to my prayer from lips free of deceit.
19_17_2: Let my vindication come from Your presence; let Your eyes look on the things that are upright.
19_17_3: You have tested my heart; You have visited me in the night; You have tried me and found nothing; I have purposed that my mouth shall not transgress.
19_17_4: Concerning the works of men, by the word of Your lips, I have kept away from the paths of the violent.
19_17_5: Uphold my steps in Your paths, that my footsteps may not slip.
19_17_6: I have called upon You, for You will hear me, O God; incline Your ear to me, and hear my speech.
19_17_7: Show Your marvelous lovingkindness by Your right hand, O You who save those who trust in You from those who rise up against them.
19_17_8: Keep me as the apple of Your eye; hide me under the shadow of Your wings,
19_17_9: From the wicked who oppress me, from my deadly enemies who surround me.
19_17_10: They have closed up their unfeeling hearts; with their mouths they speak proudly.
19_17_11: They have now surrounded us in our steps; they have set their eyes, crouching down to the earth,
19_17_12: As a lion is eager to tear his prey, and like a young lion lurking in secret places.
19_17_13: Arise, O LORD, confront him, cast him down; deliver my life from the wicked with Your sword,
19_17_14: With Your hand from men, O LORD, from men of the world who have their portion in this life, and whose belly You fill with Your treasure. They are satisfied with children, and leave the rest of their possession for their babes.
19_17_15: As for me, I will see Your face in righteousness; I shall be satisfied when I awake in Your likeness.
19_18_1: I will love You, O LORD, my strength.
19_18_2: The LORD is my rock and my fortress and my deliverer; my God, my strength, in whom I will trust; my shield and the horn of my salvation, my stronghold.
19_18_3: I will call upon the LORD, who is worthy to be praised; so shall I be saved from my enemies.
19_18_4: The pangs of death surrounded me, and the floods of ungodliness made me afraid.
19_18_5: The sorrows of Sheol surrounded me; the snares of death confronted me.
19_18_6: In my distress I called upon the LORD, and cried out to my God; He heard my voice from His temple, and my cry came before Him, even to His ears.
19_18_7: Then the earth shook and trembled; the foundations of the hills also quaked and were shaken, because He was angry.
19_18_8: Smoke went up from His nostrils, and devouring fire from His mouth; coals were kindled by it.
19_18_9: He bowed the heavens also, and came down with darkness under His feet.
19_18_10: And He rode upon a cherub, and flew; He flew upon the wings of the wind.
19_18_11: He made darkness His secret place; His canopy around Him was dark waters and thick clouds of the skies.
19_18_12: From the brightness before Him, His thick clouds passed with hailstones and coals of fire.
19_18_13: The LORD thundered from heaven, and the Most High uttered His voice, hailstones and coals of fire.
19_18_14: He sent out His arrows and scattered the foe, lightnings in abundance, and He vanquished them.
19_18_15: Then the channels of the sea were seen, the foundations of the world were uncovered at Your rebuke, O LORD, at the blast of the breath of Your nostrils.
19_18_16: He sent from above, He took me; He drew me out of many waters.
19_18_17: He delivered me from my strong enemy, from those who hated me, for they were too strong for me.
19_18_18: They confronted me in the day of my calamity, but the LORD was my support.
19_18_19: He also brought me out into a broad place; He delivered me because He delighted in me.
19_18_20: The LORD rewarded me according to my righteousness; according to the cleanness of my hands He has recompensed me.
19_18_21: For I have kept the ways of the LORD, and have not wickedly departed from my God.
19_18_22: For all His judgments were before me, and I did not put away His statutes from me.
19_18_23: I was also blameless before Him, and I kept myself from my iniquity.
19_18_24: Therefore the LORD has recompensed me according to my righteousness, according to the cleanness of my hands in His sight.
19_18_25: With the merciful You will show Yourself merciful; with a blameless man You will show Yourself blameless.
19_18_26: With the pure You will show Yourself pure; and with the devious You will show Yourself shrewd.
19_18_27: For You will save the humble people, but will bring down haughty looks.
19_18_28: For You will light my lamp; the LORD my God will enlighten my darkness.
19_18_29: For by You I can run against a troop, by my God I can leap over a wall.
19_18_30: As for God, His way is perfect; the word of the LORD is proven; He is a shield to all who trust in Him.
19_18_31: For who is God, except the LORD? And who is a rock, except our God?
19_18_32: It is God who arms me with strength, and makes my way perfect.
19_18_33: He makes my feet like the feet of deer, and sets me on my high places.
19_18_34: He teaches my hands to make war, so that my arms can bend a bow of bronze.
19_18_35: You have also given me the shield of Your salvation; Your right hand has held me up, Your gentleness has made me great.
19_18_36: You enlarged my path under me, so my feet did not slip.
19_18_37: I have pursued my enemies and overtaken them; neither did I turn back again till they were destroyed.
19_18_38: I have wounded them, so that they could not rise; they have fallen under my feet.
19_18_39: For You have armed me with strength for the battle; You have subdued under me those who rose up against me.
19_18_40: You have also given me the necks of my enemies, so that I destroyed those who hated me.
19_18_41: They cried out, but there was none to save; even to the LORD, but He did not answer them.
19_18_42: Then I beat them as fine as the dust before the wind; I cast them out like dirt in the streets.
19_18_43: You have delivered me from the strivings of the people; You have made me the head of the nations; a people I have not known shall serve me.
19_18_44: As soon as they hear of me they obey me; the foreigners submit to me.
19_18_45: The foreigners fade away, and come frightened from their hideouts.
19_18_46: The LORD lives! Blessed be my Rock! Let the God of my salvation be exalted.
19_18_47: It is God who avenges me, and subdues the peoples under me;
19_18_48: He delivers me from my enemies. You also lift me up above those who rise against me; You have delivered me from the violent man.
19_18_49: Therefore I will give thanks to You, O LORD, among the Gentiles, and sing praises to Your name.
19_18_50: Great deliverance He gives to His king, and shows mercy to His anointed, to David and his descendants forevermore.
19_19_1: The heavens declare the glory of God; and the firmament shows His handiwork.
19_19_2: Day unto day utters speech, and night unto night reveals knowledge.
19_19_3: There is no speech nor language where their voice is not heard.
19_19_4: Their line has gone out through all the earth, and their words to the end of the world. In them He has set a tabernacle for the sun,
19_19_5: Which is like a bridegroom coming out of his chamber, and rejoices like a strong man to run its race.
19_19_6: Its rising is from one end of heaven, and its circuit to the other end; and there is nothing hidden from its heat.
19_19_7: The law of the LORD is perfect, converting the soul; the testimony of the LORD is sure, making wise the simple;
19_19_8: The statutes of the LORD are right, rejoicing the heart; the commandment of the LORD is pure, enlightening the eyes;
19_19_9: The fear of the LORD is clean, enduring forever; the judgments of the LORD are true and righteous altogether.
19_19_10: More to be desired are they than gold, yea, than much fine gold; sweeter also than honey and the honeycomb.
19_19_11: Moreover by them Your servant is warned, and in keeping them there is great reward.
19_19_12: Who can understand his errors? Cleanse me from hidden faults.
19_19_13: Keep back Your servant also from presumptuous sins; let them not have dominion over me. Then I shall be blameless and innocent of great transgression.
19_19_14: Let the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart be acceptable in Your sight, O LORD, my rock and my Redeemer.
19_20_1: May the LORD answer you in the day of trouble; may the name of the God of Jacob protect you.
19_20_2: May He send you help from the sanctuary, and strengthen you out of Zion.
19_20_3: May He remember all your offerings, and accept your burnt sacrifice. Selah
19_20_4: May He grant you according to your heart’s desire, and fulfill all your purpose.
19_20_5: We will rejoice in your salvation, and in the name of our God we will set up our banners! May the LORD fulfill all your petitions.
19_20_6: Now I know that the LORD saves His anointed; He will answer him from His holy heaven with the saving strength of His right hand.
19_20_7: Some trust in chariots, and some in horses; but we will remember the name of the LORD our God.
19_20_8: They have bowed down and fallen; but we have risen and stand upright.
19_20_9: Save, LORD! May the King answer us when we call.
19_21_1: The king shall have joy in Your strength, O LORD; and in Your salvation how greatly shall he rejoice!
19_21_2: You have given him his heart’s desire, and have not withheld the request of his lips. Selah
19_21_3: For You meet him with the blessings of goodness; You set a crown of pure gold upon his head.
19_21_4: He asked life from You, and You gave it to him—length of days forever and ever.
19_21_5: His glory is great in Your salvation; honor and majesty You have placed upon him.
19_21_6: For You have made him most blessed forever; You have made him exceedingly glad with Your presence.
19_21_7: For the king trusts in the LORD, and through the mercy of the Most High he shall not be moved.
19_21_8: Your hand will find all Your enemies; Your right hand will find those who hate You.
19_21_9: You shall make them as a fiery oven in the time of Your anger; the LORD shall swallow them up in His wrath, and the fire shall devour them.
19_21_10: Their offspring You shall destroy from the earth, and their descendants from among the sons of men.
19_21_11: For they intended evil against You; they devised a plot which they are not able to perform.
19_21_12: Therefore You will make them turn their back; You will make ready Your arrows on Your string toward their faces.
19_21_13: Be exalted, O LORD, in Your own strength! We will sing and praise Your power.
19_22_1: My God, My God, why have You forsaken Me? Why are You so far from helping Me, and from the words of My groaning?
19_22_2: O My God, I cry in the daytime, but You do not hear; and in the night season, and am not silent.
19_22_3: But You are holy, enthroned in the praises of Israel.
19_22_4: Our fathers trusted in You; they trusted, and You delivered them.
19_22_5: They cried to You, and were delivered; they trusted in You, and were not ashamed.
19_22_6: But I am a worm, and no man; a reproach of men, and despised by the people.
19_22_7: All those who see Me ridicule Me; they shoot out the lip, they shake the head, saying,
19_22_8: “He trusted in the LORD, let Him rescue Him; let Him deliver Him, since He delights in Him!”
19_22_9: But You are He who took Me out of the womb; You made Me trust while on My mother’s breasts.
19_22_10: I was cast upon You from birth. From My mother’s womb You have been My God.
19_22_11: Be not far from Me, for trouble is near; for there is none to help.
19_22_12: Many bulls have surrounded Me; strong bulls of Bashan have encircled Me.
19_22_13: They gape at Me with their mouths, like a raging and roaring lion.
19_22_14: I am poured out like water, and all My bones are out of joint; My heart is like wax; it has melted within Me.
19_22_15: My strength is dried up like a potsherd, and My tongue clings to My jaws; You have brought Me to the dust of death.
19_22_16: For dogs have surrounded Me; a company of evildoers encircles Me. They pierced My hands and My feet;
19_22_17: I can count all My bones. They look and stare at Me;
19_22_18: They divide My garments among them, And for My clothing they cast lots.
19_22_19: But You, O LORD, do not be far from Me; O My Strength, hasten to help Me!
19_22_20: Deliver Me from the sword, My precious life from the power of the dog.
19_22_21: Save Me from the lion’s mouth And from the horns of the wild oxen! You have answered Me.
19_22_22: I will declare Your name to My brethren; In the midst of the assembly I will praise You.
19_22_23: You who fear the LORD, praise Him! All you descendants of Jacob, glorify Him, And fear Him, all you offspring of Israel!
19_22_24: For He has not despised nor abhorred the affliction of the afflicted; Nor has He hidden His face from Him; But when He cried to Him, He heard.
19_22_25: My praise shall be of You in the great assembly; I will pay My vows before those who fear Him.
19_22_26: The poor shall eat and be satisfied; Those who seek Him will praise the LORD. Let your heart live forever!
19_22_27: All the ends of the earth Shall remember and turn to the LORD, And all the families of the nations Shall worship before You.
19_22_28: For the kingdom is the LORD’s, And He rules over the nations.
19_22_29: All the prosperous of the earth Shall eat and worship; All those who go down to the dust Shall bow before Him, Even he who cannot keep himself alive.
19_22_30: A posterity shall serve Him. It will be recounted of the Lord to the next generation,
19_22_31: They will come and declare His righteousness to a people who will be born, That He has done this.
19_23_1: A Psalm of David. The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.
19_23_2: He makes me to lie down in green pastures; He leads me beside the still waters.
19_23_3: He restores my soul; He leads me in the paths of righteousness For His name’s sake.
19_23_4: Yea, though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil; For You are with me; Your rod and Your staff, they comfort me.
19_23_5: You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies; You anoint my head with oil; My cup runs over.
19_23_6: Surely goodness and mercy shall follow me All the days of my life; And I will dwell in the house of the LORD Forever.
19_24_1: A Psalm of David. The earth is the LORD’s, and all its fullness, The world and those who dwell therein.
19_24_2: For He has founded it upon the seas, And established it upon the waters.
19_24_3: Who may ascend into the hill of the LORD? Or who may stand in His holy place?
19_24_4: He who has clean hands and a pure heart, Who has not lifted up his soul to an idol, Nor sworn deceitfully.
19_24_5: He shall receive blessing from the LORD, And righteousness from the God of his salvation.
19_24_6: This is Jacob, the generation of those who seek Him, Who seek Your face. Selah
19_24_7: Lift up your heads, O you gates! And be lifted up, you everlasting doors! And the King of glory shall come in.
19_24_8: Who is this King of glory? The LORD strong and mighty, The LORD mighty in battle.
19_24_9: Lift up your heads, O you gates! Lift up, you everlasting doors! And the King of glory shall come in.
19_24_10: Who is this King of glory? The LORD of hosts, He is the King of glory. Selah
19_25_1: A Psalm of David. To You, O LORD, I lift up my soul.
19_25_2: O my God, I trust in You; Let me not be ashamed; Let not my enemies triumph over me.
19_25_3: Indeed, let no one who waits on You be ashamed; Let those be ashamed who deal treacherously without cause.
19_25_4: Show me Your ways, O LORD; Teach me Your paths.
19_25_5: Lead me in Your truth and teach me, For You are the God of my salvation; On You I wait all the day.
19_25_6: Remember, O LORD, Your tender mercies and Your lovingkindnesses, For they are from of old.
19_25_7: Do not remember the sins of my youth, nor my transgressions; According to Your mercy remember me, For Your goodness’ sake, O LORD.
19_25_8: Good and upright is the LORD; Therefore He teaches sinners in the way.
19_25_9: The humble He guides in justice, and the humble He teaches His way.
19_25_10: All the paths of the LORD are mercy and truth, to those who keep His covenant and His testimonies.
19_25_11: For Your name’s sake, O LORD, pardon my iniquity, for it is great.
19_25_12: Who is the man that fears the LORD? Him shall He teach in the way he should choose.
19_25_13: He himself shall dwell in prosperity, and his descendants shall inherit the land.
19_25_14: The secret of the LORD is with those who fear Him, and He will show them His covenant.
19_25_15: My eyes are ever toward the LORD, for He shall pluck my feet out of the net.
19_25_16: Turn Yourself to me, and have mercy on me, for I am lonely and afflicted.
19_25_17: The troubles of my heart have enlarged; bring me out of my distresses!
19_25_18: Look on my affliction and my pain, and forgive all my sins.
19_25_19: Consider my enemies, for they are many; and they hate me with cruel hatred.
19_25_20: Keep my soul, and deliver me; let me not be ashamed, for I put my trust in You.
19_25_21: Let integrity and uprightness preserve me, for I wait for You.
19_25_22: Redeem Israel, O God, out of all their troubles!
19_26_1: Vindicate me, O LORD, for I have walked in my integrity. I have also trusted in the LORD; I shall not slip.
19_26_2: Examine me, O LORD, and prove me; try my mind and my heart.
19_26_3: For Your lovingkindness is before my eyes, and I have walked in Your truth.
19_26_4: I have not sat with idolatrous mortals, nor will I go in with hypocrites.
19_26_5: I have hated the assembly of evildoers, and will not sit with the wicked.
19_26_6: I will wash my hands in innocence; so I will go about Your altar, O LORD,
19_26_7: That I may proclaim with the voice of thanksgiving, and tell of all Your wondrous works.
19_26_8: LORD, I have loved the habitation of Your house, and the place where Your glory dwells.
19_26_9: Do not gather my soul with sinners, nor my life with bloodthirsty men,
19_26_10: In whose hands is a sinister scheme, and whose right hand is full of bribes.
19_26_11: But as for me, I will walk in my integrity; redeem me and be merciful to me.
19_26_12: My foot stands in an even place; in the congregations I will bless the LORD.
19_27_1: The LORD is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear? The LORD is the strength of my life; of whom shall I be afraid?
19_27_2: When the wicked came against me to eat up my flesh, my enemies and foes, they stumbled and fell.
19_27_3: Though an army may encamp against me, my heart shall not fear; though war may rise against me, in this I will be confident.
19_27_4: One thing I have desired of the LORD, that will I seek: that I may dwell in the house of the LORD all the days of my life, to behold the beauty of the LORD, and to inquire in His temple.
19_27_5: For in the time of trouble He shall hide me in His pavilion; in the secret place of His tabernacle He shall hide me; He shall set me high upon a rock.
19_27_6: And now my head shall be lifted up above my enemies all around me; therefore I will offer sacrifices of joy in His tabernacle; I will sing, yes, I will sing praises to the LORD.
19_27_7: Hear, O LORD, when I cry with my voice! Have mercy also upon me, and answer me.
19_27_8: When You said, “Seek My face,” my heart said to You, “Your face, LORD, I will seek.”
19_27_9: Do not hide Your face from me; do not turn Your servant away in anger; You have been my help; do not leave me nor forsake me, O God of my salvation.
19_27_10: When my father and my mother forsake me, then the LORD will take care of me.
19_27_11: Teach me Your way, O LORD, and lead me in a smooth path, because of my enemies.
19_27_12: Do not deliver me to the will of my adversaries; for false witnesses have risen against me, and such as breathe out violence.
19_27_13: I would have lost heart, unless I had believed that I would see the goodness of the LORD in the land of the living.
19_27_14: Wait on the LORD; be of good courage, and He shall strengthen your heart; wait, I say, on the LORD!
19_28_1: To You I will cry, O LORD my Rock: Do not be silent to me, lest, if You are silent to me, I become like those who go down to the pit.
19_28_2: Hear the voice of my supplications when I cry to You for help, when I lift up my hands toward Your holy sanctuary.
19_28_3: Do not drag me away with the wicked and with the workers of iniquity, who speak peace to their neighbors, but evil is in their hearts.
19_28_4: Give them according to their deeds, and according to the wickedness of their endeavors; give them according to the work of their hands; render to them what they deserve.
19_28_5: Because they do not regard the works of the LORD, nor the operation of His hands, He shall destroy them and not build them up.
19_28_6: Blessed be the LORD, because He has heard the voice of my supplications!
19_28_7: The LORD is my strength and my shield; my heart trusted in Him, and I am helped; therefore my heart greatly rejoices, and with my song I will praise Him.
19_28_8: The LORD is their strength, and He is the saving refuge of His anointed.
19_28_9: Save Your people, and bless Your inheritance; shepherd them also, and bear them up forever.
19_29_1: Give unto the LORD, O you mighty ones, give unto the LORD glory and strength.
19_29_2: Give unto the LORD the glory due to His name; worship the LORD in the beauty of holiness.
19_29_3: The voice of the LORD is over the waters; the God of glory thunders; the LORD is over many waters.
19_29_4: The voice of the LORD is powerful; the voice of the LORD is full of majesty.
19_29_5: The voice of the LORD breaks the cedars, yes, the LORD splinters the cedars of Lebanon.
19_29_6: He makes them also skip like a calf, Lebanon and Sirion like a young wild ox.
19_29_7: The voice of the LORD divides the flames of fire.
19_29_8: The voice of the LORD shakes the wilderness; the LORD shakes the Wilderness of Kadesh.
19_29_9: The voice of the LORD makes the deer give birth, and strips the forests bare; and in His temple everyone says, “Glory!”
19_29_10: The LORD sat enthroned at the Flood, and the LORD sits as King forever.
19_29_11: The LORD will give strength to His people; the LORD will bless His people with peace.
19_30_1: I will extol You, O LORD, for You have lifted me up, and have not let my foes rejoice over me.
19_30_2: O LORD my God, I cried out to You, and You healed me.
19_30_3: O LORD, You brought my soul up from the grave; You have kept me alive, that I should not go down to the pit.
19_30_4: Sing praise to the LORD, you saints of His, and give thanks at the remembrance of His holy name.
19_30_5: For His anger is but for a moment, His favor is for life; weeping may endure for a night, but joy comes in the morning.
19_30_6: Now in my prosperity I said, “I shall never be moved.”
19_30_7: LORD, by Your favor You have made my mountain stand strong; You hid Your face, and I was troubled.
19_30_8: I cried out to You, O LORD; and to the LORD I made supplication:
19_30_9: “What profit is there in my blood, when I go down to the pit? Will the dust praise You? Will it declare Your truth?
19_30_10: Hear, O LORD, and have mercy on me; LORD, be my helper!”
19_30_11: You have turned for me my mourning into dancing; You have put off my sackcloth and clothed me with gladness,
19_30_12: To the end that my glory may sing praise to You and not be silent. O LORD my God, I will give thanks to You forever.
19_31_1: In You, O LORD, I put my trust; let me never be ashamed; deliver me in Your righteousness.
19_31_2: Bow down Your ear to me, deliver me speedily; be my rock of refuge, a fortress of defense to save me.
19_31_3: For You are my rock and my fortress; therefore, for Your name’s sake, lead me and guide me.
19_31_4: Pull me out of the net which they have secretly laid for me, for You are my strength.
19_31_5: Into Your hand I commit my spirit; You have redeemed me, O LORD God of truth.
19_31_6: I have hated those who regard useless idols; but I trust in the LORD.
19_31_7: I will be glad and rejoice in Your mercy, for You have considered my trouble; You have known my soul in adversities,
19_31_8: And have not shut me up into the hand of the enemy; You have set my feet in a wide place.
19_31_9: Be gracious to me, O LORD, for I am in distress; my eyes are worn out from grief, my soul and my body also.
19_31_10: For my life is spent with sorrow, and my years with sighing; my strength fails because of my iniquity, and my bones waste away.
19_31_11: I am a reproach among all my enemies, especially among my neighbors, and am repulsive to my acquaintances; those who see me in the street flee from me.
19_31_12: I am forgotten like a dead man, out of mind; I am like a broken vessel.
19_31_13: For I hear the slander of many; terror is on every side; while they take counsel together against me, they scheme to take away my life.
19_31_14: But as for me, I trust in You, O LORD; I say, “You are my God.”
19_31_15: My times are in Your hand; deliver me from the hand of my enemies and from those who persecute me.
19_31_16: Make Your face shine upon Your servant; save me for Your mercies’ sake.
19_31_17: Do not let me be ashamed, O LORD, for I have called upon You; let the wicked be ashamed; let them be silent in the grave.
19_31_18: Let the lying lips be put to silence, which speak insolent things proudly and contemptuously against the righteous.
19_31_19: Oh, how great is Your goodness, which You have laid up for those who fear You, which You have prepared for those who trust in You in the presence of the sons of men!
19_31_20: You shall hide them in the secret place of Your presence from the plots of man; You shall keep them secretly in a pavilion from the strife of tongues.
19_31_21: Blessed be the LORD, for He has shown me His marvelous kindness in a strong city!
19_31_22: For I said in my haste, “I am cut off from before Your eyes”; nevertheless You heard the voice of my supplications when I cried out to You.
19_31_23: Oh, love the LORD, all you His saints! For the LORD preserves the faithful, and fully repays the proud person.
19_31_24: Be of good courage, and He shall strengthen your heart, all you who hope in the LORD.
19_32_1: Blessed is he whose transgression is forgiven, whose sin is covered.
19_32_2: Blessed is the man to whom the LORD does not impute iniquity, and in whose spirit there is no deceit.
19_32_3: When I kept silent, my bones grew old through my groaning all the day long.
19_32_4: For day and night Your hand was heavy upon me; my vitality was turned into the drought of summer. Selah
19_32_5: I acknowledged my sin to You, and my iniquity I have not hidden. I said, “I will confess my transgressions to the LORD,” and You forgave the iniquity of my sin. Selah
19_32_6: For this cause everyone who is godly shall pray to You in a time when You may be found; surely in a flood of great waters they shall not come near him.
19_32_7: You are my hiding place; You shall preserve me from trouble; You shall surround me with songs of deliverance. Selah
19_32_8: I will instruct you and teach you in the way you should go; I will guide you with My eye.
19_32_9: Do not be like the horse or like the mule, which have no understanding, which must be harnessed with bit and bridle, else they will not come near you.
19_32_10: Many sorrows shall be to the wicked; but he who trusts in the LORD, mercy shall surround him.
19_32_11: Be glad in the LORD and rejoice, you righteous; and shout for joy, all you upright in heart!
19_33_1: Rejoice in the LORD, O you righteous! For praise from the upright is beautiful.
19_33_2: Praise the LORD with the harp; make melody to Him with an instrument of ten strings.
19_33_3: Sing to Him a new song; play skillfully with a shout of joy.
19_33_4: For the word of the LORD is right, and all His work is done in truth.
19_33_5: He loves righteousness and justice; the earth is full of the goodness of the LORD.
19_33_6: By the word of the LORD the heavens were made, and all the host of them by the breath of His mouth.
19_33_7: He gathers the waters of the sea together as a heap; He lays up the deep in storehouses.
19_33_8: Let all the earth fear the LORD; let all the inhabitants of the world stand in awe of Him.
19_33_9: For He spoke, and it was done; He commanded, and it stood fast.
19_33_10: The LORD brings the counsel of the nations to nothing; He makes the plans of the peoples of no effect.
19_33_11: The counsel of the LORD stands forever, the plans of His heart to all generations.
19_33_12: Blessed is the nation whose God is the LORD, the people He has chosen as His own inheritance.
19_33_13: The LORD looks from heaven; He sees all the sons of men.
19_33_14: From the place of His dwelling He looks on all the inhabitants of the earth.
19_33_15: He fashions their hearts individually; He considers all their works.
19_33_16: No king is saved by the multitude of an army; a mighty man is not delivered by great strength.
19_33_17: A horse is a vain hope for safety; neither shall it deliver any by its great strength.
19_33_18: Behold, the eye of the LORD is on those who fear Him, on those who hope in His steadfast love.
19_33_19: To deliver their soul from death, and to keep them alive in famine.
19_33_20: Our soul waits for the LORD; He is our help and our shield.
19_33_21: For our heart shall rejoice in Him, because we have trusted in His holy name.
19_33_22: Let Your mercy, O LORD, be upon us, just as we hope in You.
19_34_1: I will bless the LORD at all times; His praise shall continually be in my mouth.
19_34_2: My soul shall make its boast in the LORD; the humble shall hear of it and be glad.
19_34_3: Oh, magnify the LORD with me, and let us exalt His name together.
19_34_4: I sought the LORD, and He heard me, and delivered me from all my fears.
19_34_5: They looked to Him and were radiant, and their faces were not ashamed.
19_34_6: This poor man cried out, and the LORD heard him, and saved him out of all his troubles.
19_34_7: The angel of the LORD encamps all around those who fear Him, and delivers them.
19_34_8: Oh, taste and see that the LORD is good; blessed is the man who trusts in Him!
19_34_9: Oh, fear the LORD, you His saints! There is no want to those who fear Him.
19_34_10: The young lions lack and suffer hunger; but those who seek the LORD shall not lack any good thing.
19_34_11: Come, you children, listen to me; I will teach you the fear of the LORD.
19_34_12: Who is the man who desires life, and loves many days, that he may see good?
19_34_13: Keep your tongue from evil, and your lips from speaking deceit.
19_34_14: Depart from evil and do good; seek peace and pursue it.
19_34_15: The eyes of the LORD are on the righteous, and His ears are open to their cry.
19_34_16: The face of the LORD is against those who do evil, to cut off the remembrance of them from the earth.
19_34_17: The righteous cry out, and the LORD hears, and delivers them out of all their troubles.
19_34_18: The LORD is near to those who have a broken heart, and saves such as have a contrite spirit.
19_34_19: Many are the afflictions of the righteous, but the LORD delivers him out of them all.
19_34_20: He guards all his bones; not one of them is broken.
19_34_21: Evil shall slay the wicked, and those who hate the righteous shall be condemned.
19_34_22: The LORD redeems the soul of His servants, and none of those who trust in Him shall be condemned.
19_35_1: Plead my cause, O LORD, with those who strive with me; fight against those who fight against me.
19_35_2: Take hold of shield and buckler, and stand up for my help.
19_35_3: Also draw out the spear, and stop those who pursue me. Say to my soul, “I am your salvation.”
19_35_4: Let those be put to shame and brought to dishonor who seek after my life; let those be turned back and brought to confusion who plot my hurt.
19_35_5: Let them be like chaff before the wind, and let the angel of the LORD chase them.
19_35_6: Let their way be dark and slippery, and let the angel of the LORD pursue them.
19_35_7: For without cause they have hidden their net for me in a pit, which they have dug without cause for my life.
19_35_8: Let destruction come upon him unexpectedly, and let his net that he has hidden catch himself; into that very destruction let him fall.
19_35_9: And my soul shall be joyful in the LORD; it shall rejoice in His salvation.
19_35_10: All my bones shall say, "LORD, who is like You, delivering the poor from him who is too strong for him, the poor and the needy from him who robs him?"
19_35_11: Malicious witnesses rise up; they ask me things that I do not know.
19_35_12: They repay me evil for good, to the sorrow of my soul.
19_35_13: But as for me, when they were sick, my clothing was sackcloth; I humbled myself with fasting, and my prayer returned to my own heart.
19_35_14: I paced about as though he were my friend or brother; I bowed down heavily, as one who mourns for his mother.
19_35_15: But in my adversity they rejoiced and gathered together; attackers gathered against me, and I did not know it; they tore at me and did not cease.
19_35_16: With ungodly mockers at feasts, they gnashed at me with their teeth.
19_35_17: Lord, how long will You look on? Rescue me from their destructions, my precious life from the lions.
19_35_18: I will give You thanks in the great assembly; I will praise You among many people.
19_35_19: Let them not rejoice over me who are wrongfully my enemies, nor let them wink with the eye who hate me without a cause.
19_35_20: For they do not speak peace, but they devise deceitful matters against the quiet ones in the land.
19_35_21: They also opened their mouth wide against me, and said, "Aha, aha! Our eyes have seen it."
19_35_22: This You have seen, O LORD; do not keep silence. O Lord, do not be far from me.
19_35_23: Stir up Yourself, and awake to my vindication, to my cause, my God and my Lord.
19_35_24: Vindicate me, O LORD my God, according to Your righteousness; and let them not rejoice over me.
19_35_25: Let them not say in their hearts, "Ah, so we would have it!" Let them not say, "We have swallowed him up."
19_35_26: Let them be ashamed and brought to mutual confusion who rejoice at my hurt; let them be clothed with shame and dishonor who exalt themselves against me.
19_35_27: Let them shout for joy and be glad, who favor my righteous cause; and let them say continually, "Let the LORD be magnified, who has pleasure in the prosperity of His servant."
19_35_28: And my tongue shall speak of Your righteousness and of Your praise all the day long.
