19_119_169: Let my cry come before You, O LORD; give me understanding according to Your word.
19_119_170: Let my supplication come before You; deliver me according to Your word.
19_119_171: My lips shall utter praise, for You teach me Your statutes.
19_119_172: My tongue shall speak of Your word, for all Your commandments are righteousness.
19_119_173: Let Your hand become my help, for I have chosen Your precepts.
19_119_174: I long for Your salvation, O LORD, and Your law is my delight.
19_119_175: Let my soul live, and it shall praise You; and let Your judgments help me.
19_119_176: I have gone astray like a lost sheep; seek Your servant, for I do not forget Your commandments.
19_120_1: A Song of Ascents. In my distress I cried to the LORD, and He heard me.
19_120_2: Deliver my soul, O LORD, from lying lips and from a deceitful tongue.
19_120_3: What shall be given to you, or what shall be done to you, you false tongue?
19_120_4: Sharp arrows of the warrior, with coals of the broom tree!
19_120_5: Woe is me, that I dwell in Meshech, that I dwell among the tents of Kedar!
19_120_6: My soul has dwelt too long with one who hates peace.
19_120_7: I am for peace; but when I speak, they are for war.
19_121_1: A Song of Ascents. I will lift up my eyes to the hills— from whence comes my help?
19_121_2: My help comes from the LORD, who made heaven and earth.
19_121_3: He will not allow your foot to be moved; He who keeps you will not slumber.
19_121_4: Behold, He who keeps Israel shall neither slumber nor sleep.
19_121_5: The LORD is your keeper; the LORD is your shade at your right hand.
19_121_6: The sun shall not strike you by day, nor the moon by night.
19_121_7: The LORD shall preserve you from all evil; He shall preserve your soul.
19_121_8: The LORD shall preserve your going out and your coming in from this time forth, and even forevermore.
19_122_1: A Song of Ascents. Of David. I was glad when they said to me, “Let us go into the house of the LORD.”
19_122_2: Our feet have been standing within your gates, O Jerusalem!
19_122_3: Jerusalem is built as a city that is compact together,
19_122_4: Where the tribes go up, the tribes of the LORD, to the Testimony of Israel, to give thanks to the name of the LORD.
19_122_5: For thrones are set there for judgment, the thrones of the house of David.
19_122_6: Pray for the peace of Jerusalem: “May they prosper who love you.
19_122_7: Peace be within your walls, prosperity within your palaces.”
19_122_8: For the sake of my brethren and companions, I will now say, “Peace be within you.”
19_122_9: Because of the house of the LORD our God I will seek your good.
19_123_1: A Song of Ascents. Unto You I lift up my eyes, O You who dwell in the heavens.
19_123_2: Behold, as the eyes of servants look to the hand of their masters, as the eyes of a maid to the hand of her mistress, so our eyes look to the LORD our God, until He has mercy on us.
19_123_3: Have mercy on us, O LORD, have mercy on us! For we are exceedingly filled with contempt.
19_123_4: Our soul is exceedingly filled with the scorn of those who are at ease, with the contempt of the proud.
19_124_1: A Song of Ascents. Of David. “If it had not been the LORD who was on our side,” let Israel now say—
19_124_2: “If it had not been the LORD who was on our side, when men rose up against us,
19_124_3: Then they would have swallowed us alive, when their wrath was kindled against us;
19_124_4: Then the waters would have overwhelmed us, the stream would have gone over our soul;
19_124_5: Then the swollen waters would have gone over our soul.”
19_124_6: Blessed be the LORD, who has not given us as prey to their teeth.
19_124_7: Our soul has escaped as a bird from the snare of the fowlers; the snare is broken, and we have escaped.
19_124_8: Our help is in the name of the LORD, who made heaven and earth.
19_125_1: A Song of Ascents. Those who trust in the LORD are like Mount Zion, which cannot be moved, but abides forever.
19_125_2: As the mountains surround Jerusalem, so the LORD surrounds His people from this time forth and forever.
19_125_3: For the scepter of wickedness shall not rest on the land allotted to the righteous, lest the righteous reach out their hands to iniquity.
19_125_4: Do good, O LORD, to those who are good, and to those who are upright in their hearts.
19_125_5: As for such as turn aside to their crooked ways, the LORD shall lead them away with the workers of iniquity. Peace be upon Israel!
19_126_1: A Song of Ascents. When the LORD brought back the captivity of Zion, we were like those who dream.
19_126_2: Then our mouth was filled with laughter, and our tongue with singing. Then they said among the nations, “The LORD has done great things for them.”
19_126_3: The LORD has done great things for us, and we are glad.
19_126_4: Bring back our captivity, O LORD, as the streams in the South.
19_126_5: Those who sow in tears shall reap in joy.
19_126_6: He who continually goes forth weeping, bearing seed for sowing, shall doubtless come again with rejoicing, bringing his sheaves with him.
19_127_1: A Song of Ascents. Of Solomon. Unless the LORD builds the house, they labor in vain who build it; unless the LORD guards the city, the watchman stays awake in vain.
19_127_2: It is vain for you to rise up early, to sit up late, to eat the bread of sorrows; for so He gives His beloved sleep.
19_127_3: Behold, children are a heritage from the LORD, the fruit of the womb is a reward.
19_127_4: Like arrows in the hand of a warrior, so are the children of one’s youth.
19_127_5: Happy is the man who has his quiver full of them; they shall not be ashamed, but shall speak with their enemies in the gate.
19_128_1: A Song of Ascents. Blessed is every one who fears the LORD, who walks in His ways.
19_128_2: When you eat the labor of your hands, you shall be happy, and it shall be well with you.
19_128_3: Your wife shall be like a fruitful vine in the very heart of your house, your children like olive plants all around your table.
19_128_4: Behold, thus shall the man be blessed who fears the LORD.
19_128_5: The LORD bless you out of Zion, and may you see the good of Jerusalem all the days of your life.
19_128_6: Yes, may you see your children’s children. Peace be upon Israel!
19_129_1: Song of Ascents. “Many a time they have afflicted me from my youth,” let Israel now say—
19_129_2: “Many a time they have afflicted me from my youth; yet they have not prevailed against me.
19_129_3: The plowers plowed on my back; they made their furrows long.”
19_129_4: The LORD is righteous; He has cut in pieces the cords of the wicked.
19_129_5: Let all those who hate Zion be put to shame and turned back.
19_129_6: Let them be as the grass on the housetops, which withers before it grows up,
19_129_7: With which the reaper does not fill his hand, nor he who binds sheaves, his arms.
19_129_8: Neither let those who pass by them say, “The blessing of the LORD be upon you; We bless you in the name of the LORD!”
19_130_1: A Song of Ascents. Out of the depths I have cried to You, O LORD;
19_130_2: Lord, hear my voice! Let Your ears be attentive to the voice of my pleas for mercy.
19_130_3: If You, LORD, should mark iniquities, O Lord, who could stand?
19_130_4: But there is forgiveness with You, that You may be revered.
19_130_5: I wait for the LORD, my soul waits, and in His word I do hope.
19_130_6: My soul waits for the Lord more than those who watch for the morning—yes, more than those who watch for the morning.
19_130_7: O Israel, hope in the LORD; for with the LORD there is steadfast love, and with Him is abundant redemption.
19_130_8: And He shall redeem Israel from all his iniquities.
19_131_1: A Song of Ascents. Of David. LORD, my heart is not haughty, nor my eyes lofty. Neither do I concern myself with great matters, nor with things too profound for me.
19_131_2: Surely I have calmed and quieted my soul, like a weaned child with his mother; like a weaned child is my soul within me.
19_131_3: O Israel, hope in the LORD from this time forth and forevermore.
19_132_1: A Song of Ascents. LORD, remember David and all his afflictions;
19_132_2: How he swore to the LORD, and vowed to the Mighty One of Jacob:
19_132_3: Surely I will not go into the chamber of my house, or go up to the comfort of my bed;
19_132_4: I will not give sleep to my eyes or slumber to my eyelids,
19_132_5: Until I find a place for the LORD, a dwelling place for the Mighty One of Jacob.
19_132_6: Behold, we heard of it in Ephrathah; we found it in the fields of Jaar.
19_132_7: Let us go into His tabernacle; let us worship at His footstool.
19_132_8: Arise, O LORD, to Your resting place, You and the ark of Your strength.
19_132_9: Let Your priests be clothed with righteousness, and let Your saints shout for joy.
19_132_10: For Your servant David’s sake, do not turn away the face of Your Anointed.
19_132_11: The LORD has sworn in truth to David; He will not turn from it: “I will set upon your throne the fruit of your body.
19_132_12: If your sons will keep My covenant and My testimony which I shall teach them, their sons also shall sit upon your throne forevermore.
19_132_13: For the LORD has chosen Zion; He has desired it for His dwelling place:
19_132_14: This is My resting place forever; here I will dwell, for I have desired it.
19_132_15: I will abundantly bless her provision; I will satisfy her poor with bread.
19_132_16: I will also clothe her priests with salvation, and her saints shall shout aloud for joy.
19_132_17: There I will make the horn of David grow; I will prepare a lamp for My Anointed.
19_132_18: His enemies I will clothe with shame, but upon Himself His crown shall flourish.
19_133_1: A Song of Ascents. Of David. Behold, how good and how pleasant it is for brethren to dwell together in unity!
19_133_2: It is like the precious oil upon the head, running down on the beard, the beard of Aaron, running down on the edge of his garments.
19_133_3: It is like the dew of Hermon, descending upon the mountains of Zion; for there the LORD commanded the blessing—life forevermore.
19_134_1: A Song of Ascents. Behold, bless the LORD, all you servants of the LORD, who by night stand in the house of the LORD!
19_134_2: Lift up your hands in the sanctuary, and bless the LORD.
19_134_3: The LORD who made heaven and earth bless you from Zion!
19_135_1: Praise the LORD! Praise the name of the LORD; praise Him, O you servants of the LORD!
19_135_2: You who stand in the house of the LORD, in the courts of the house of our God,
19_135_3: Praise the LORD, for the LORD is good; sing praises to His name, for it is pleasant.
19_135_4: For the LORD has chosen Jacob for Himself, Israel for His special treasure.
19_135_5: For I know that the LORD is great, and our Lord is greater than all gods.
19_135_6: Whatever the LORD pleases He does, in heaven and on earth, in the seas and all the depths.
19_135_7: He causes the clouds to rise from the ends of the earth; He makes lightning for the rain and brings the wind from His storehouses.
19_135_8: He struck down the firstborn of Egypt, both of man and beast.
19_135_9: He sent signs and wonders into your midst, O Egypt, against Pharaoh and all his servants.
19_135_10: He struck down many nations and slew mighty kings—
19_135_11: Sihon king of the Amorites, Og king of Bashan, and all the kingdoms of Canaan—
19_135_12: And gave their land as a heritage, a heritage to Israel His people.
19_135_13: Your name, O LORD, endures forever, Your fame, O LORD, throughout all generations.
19_135_14: For the LORD will judge His people, and He will have compassion on His servants.
19_135_15: The idols of the nations are silver and gold, the work of human hands.
19_135_16: They have mouths, but they do not speak; eyes they have, but they do not see;
19_135_17: They have ears, but they do not hear; nor is there any breath in their mouths.
19_135_18: Those who make them are like them; so is everyone who trusts in them.
19_135_19: Bless the LORD, O house of Israel! Bless the LORD, O house of Aaron!
19_135_20: Bless the LORD, O house of Levi! You who fear the LORD, bless the LORD!
19_135_21: Blessed be the LORD out of Zion, who dwells in Jerusalem! Praise the LORD!
19_136_1: Oh, give thanks to the LORD, for He is good! For His faithful love endures forever.
19_136_2: Oh, give thanks to the God of gods! For His faithful love endures forever.
19_136_3: Oh, give thanks to the Lord of lords! For His faithful love endures forever.
19_136_4: To Him who alone does great wonders, for His faithful love endures forever;
19_136_5: To Him who by wisdom made the heavens, for His faithful love endures forever;
19_136_6: To Him who laid out the earth above the waters, for His faithful love endures forever;
19_136_7: To Him who made great lights, for His faithful love endures forever—
19_136_8: The sun to rule by day, for His faithful love endures forever;
19_136_9: The moon and stars to rule by night, for His faithful love endures forever.
19_136_10: To Him who struck Egypt in their firstborn, for His faithful love endures forever;
19_136_11: And brought out Israel from among them, for His faithful love endures forever;
19_136_12: With a strong hand, and with an outstretched arm, for His faithful love endures forever;
19_136_13: To Him who divided the Red Sea in two, for His faithful love endures forever;
19_136_14: And made Israel pass through the midst of it, for His faithful love endures forever;
19_136_15: But overthrew Pharaoh and his army in the Red Sea, for His faithful love endures forever;
19_136_16: To Him who led His people through the wilderness, for His faithful love endures forever;
19_136_17: To Him who struck down great kings, for His faithful love endures forever;
19_136_18: And slew famous kings, for His faithful love endures forever—
19_136_19: Sihon king of the Amorites, for His faithful love endures forever;
19_136_20: And Og king of Bashan, for His faithful love endures forever—
19_136_21: And gave their land as a heritage, for His faithful love endures forever;
19_136_22: A heritage to Israel His servant, for His faithful love endures forever.
19_136_23: Who remembered us in our lowly state, for His faithful love endures forever;
19_136_24: And rescued us from our enemies, for His faithful love endures forever.
19_136_25: He gives food to all flesh, for His faithful love endures forever.
19_136_26: Oh, give thanks to the God of heaven! For His faithful love endures forever.
19_137_1: By the rivers of Babylon, there we sat down and wept when we remembered Zion.
19_137_2: We hung our harps upon the willows in the midst of it.
19_137_3: For there our captors demanded of us a song, and our tormentors requested mirth, saying, “Sing us one of the songs of Zion!”
19_137_4: How shall we sing the LORD’s song in a foreign land?
19_137_5: If I forget you, O Jerusalem, let my right hand forget its skill!
19_137_6: If I do not remember you, let my tongue cling to the roof of my mouth—if I do not exalt Jerusalem above my chief joy.
19_137_7: Remember, O LORD, against the sons of Edom the day of Jerusalem, who said, “Raze it, raze it, to its very foundation!”
19_137_8: O daughter of Babylon, who are to be destroyed, happy is the one who repays you as you have served us!
19_137_9: Happy is the one who takes and dashes your little ones against the rock!
19_138_1: A Psalm of David. I will praise You with my whole heart; before the gods I will sing praises to You.
19_138_2: I will worship toward Your holy temple, and praise Your name for Your lovingkindness and Your truth; for You have magnified Your word above all Your name.
19_138_3: In the day when I cried out, You answered me, and made me bold with strength in my soul.
19_138_4: All the kings of the earth shall praise You, O LORD, when they hear the words of Your mouth.
19_138_5: Yes, they shall sing of the ways of the LORD, for great is the glory of the LORD.
19_138_6: Though the LORD is on high, yet He regards the lowly; but the proud He knows from afar.
19_138_7: Though I walk in the midst of trouble, You will revive me; You will stretch out Your hand against the wrath of my enemies, and Your right hand will save me.
19_138_8: The LORD will perfect that which concerns me; Your mercy, O LORD, endures forever; do not forsake the works of Your hands.
19_139_1: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. O LORD, You have searched me and known me.
19_139_2: You know my sitting down and my rising up; You understand my thought afar off.
19_139_3: You comprehend my path and my lying down, and are acquainted with all my ways.
19_139_4: For there is not a word on my tongue, but behold, O LORD, You know it altogether.
19_139_5: You have hedged me behind and before, and laid Your hand upon me.
19_139_6: Such knowledge is too wonderful for me; it is high, I cannot attain it.
19_139_7: Where can I go from Your Spirit? Or where can I flee from Your presence?
19_139_8: If I ascend into heaven, You are there; if I make my bed in Sheol, behold, You are there.
19_139_9: If I take the wings of the morning, and dwell in the uttermost parts of the sea,
19_139_10: Even there Your hand shall lead me, and Your right hand shall hold me.
19_139_11: If I say, “Surely the darkness shall fall on me,” even the night shall be light about me;
19_139_12: Indeed, the darkness shall not hide from You, but the night shines as the day; the darkness and the light are both alike to You.
19_139_13: For You formed my inward parts; You covered me in my mother’s womb.
19_139_14: I will praise You, for I am fearfully and wonderfully made; marvelous are Your works, and that my soul knows very well.
19_139_15: My frame was not hidden from You, when I was made in secret, and skillfully wrought in the lowest parts of the earth.
19_139_16: Your eyes saw my substance, being yet unformed. And in Your book they all were written, the days fashioned for me, when as yet there were none of them.
19_139_17: How precious also are Your thoughts to me, O God! How great is the sum of them!
19_139_18: If I should count them, they would be more in number than the sand; when I awake, I am still with You.
19_139_19: Oh, that You would slay the wicked, O God! Depart from me, therefore, you bloodthirsty men.
19_139_20: For they speak against You wickedly; Your enemies take Your name in vain.
19_139_21: Do I not hate those who hate You, O LORD? And do I not loathe those who rise up against You?
19_139_22: I hate them with perfect hatred; I count them my enemies.
19_139_23: Search me, O God, and know my heart; Try me, and know my anxious thoughts;
19_139_24: And see if there is any wicked way in me, And lead me in the way everlasting.
19_140_1: Deliver me, O LORD, from evil men; Preserve me from violent men,
19_140_2: Who plan evil things in their hearts; They continually gather together for war.
19_140_3: They sharpen their tongues like a serpent; The poison of asps is under their lips. Selah
19_140_4: Keep me, O LORD, from the hands of the wicked; Preserve me from violent men, Who have purposed to make my steps stumble.
19_140_5: The proud have hidden a snare for me, and cords; They have spread a net by the wayside; They have set traps for me. Selah
19_140_6: I said to the LORD: “You are my God; Hear the voice of my supplications, O LORD.
19_140_7: O GOD the Lord, the strength of my salvation, You have covered my head in the day of battle.
19_140_8: Do not grant, O LORD, the desires of the wicked; Do not further his wicked scheme, Lest they be exalted. Selah
19_140_9: As for the head of those who surround me, Let the evil of their lips cover them;
19_140_10: Let burning coals fall upon them; Let them be cast into the fire, Into deep pits, that they rise not up again.
19_140_11: Let not a slanderer be established in the earth; Let evil hunt the violent man to overthrow him.
19_140_12: I know that the LORD will maintain The cause of the afflicted, And justice for the poor.
19_140_13: Surely the righteous shall give thanks to Your name; The upright shall dwell in Your presence.
19_141_1: LORD, I cry out to You; Make haste to me! Give ear to my voice when I cry out to You.
19_141_2: Let my prayer be set before You as incense, The lifting up of my hands as the evening sacrifice.
19_141_3: Set a guard, O LORD, over my mouth; Keep watch over the door of my lips.
19_141_4: Do not incline my heart to any evil thing, To practice wicked works With men who work iniquity; And do not let me eat of their delicacies.
19_141_5: Let the righteous strike me; It shall be a kindness. And let him rebuke me; It shall be as excellent oil; Let my head not refuse it. For still my prayer is against the deeds of the wicked.
19_141_6: Their judges are overthrown by the sides of the cliff, And they hear my words, for they are sweet.
19_141_7: Our bones are scattered at the mouth of the grave, As when one plows and breaks up the earth.
19_141_8: But my eyes are upon You, O GOD the Lord; In You I take refuge; Do not leave my soul destitute.
19_141_9: Keep me from the snares they have laid for me, And from the traps of the workers of iniquity.
19_141_10: Let the wicked fall into their own nets, While I escape safely.
19_142_1: I cry out to the LORD with my voice; With my voice to the LORD I make my supplication.
19_142_2: I pour out my complaint before Him; I declare before Him my trouble.
19_142_3: When my spirit was overwhelmed within me, Then You knew my path. In the way in which I walk They have secretly set a snare for me.
19_142_4: Look on my right hand and see, For there is no one who acknowledges me; Refuge has failed me; No one cares for my soul.
19_142_5: I cried out to You, O LORD: I said, “You are my refuge, My portion in the land of the living.
19_142_6: Attend to my cry, For I am brought very low; Deliver me from my persecutors, For they are stronger than I.
19_142_7: Bring my soul out of prison, That I may praise Your name; The righteous shall surround me, For You shall deal bountifully with me.
19_143_1: Hear my prayer, O LORD, Give ear to my supplications! In Your faithfulness answer me, And in Your righteousness.
19_143_2: Do not enter into judgment with Your servant, For in Your sight no one living is righteous.
19_143_3: For the enemy has persecuted my soul; He has crushed my life to the ground; He has made me dwell in darkness, Like those who have long been dead.
19_143_4: Therefore my spirit is overwhelmed within me; My heart within me is distressed.
19_143_5: I remember the days of old; I meditate on all Your works; I muse on the work of Your hands.
19_143_6: I spread out my hands to You; My soul longs for You like a thirsty land. Selah
19_143_7: Answer me quickly, O LORD; my spirit fails! Do not hide Your face from me, lest I be like those who go down to the pit.
19_143_8: Cause me to hear Your lovingkindness in the morning, for in You do I trust. Reveal to me the way I should walk, for I lift up my soul to You.
19_143_9: Deliver me, O LORD, from my enemies; I take refuge in You.
19_143_10: Teach me to do Your will, for You are my God; let Your good Spirit lead me on level ground.
19_143_11: Revive me, O LORD, for Your name’s sake! For Your righteousness’ sake bring my soul out of trouble.
19_143_12: In Your mercy cut off my enemies, and destroy all those who afflict my soul; for I am Your servant.
19_144_1: Blessed be the LORD my Rock, who trains my hands for war, and my fingers for battle—
19_144_2: My lovingkindness and my fortress, my high tower and my deliverer, my shield and the One in whom I take refuge, who subdues my people under me.
19_144_3: LORD, what is man, that You take knowledge of him? Or the son of man, that You are mindful of him?
19_144_4: Man is like a breath; his days are like a passing shadow.
19_144_5: Bow down Your heavens, O LORD, and come down; touch the mountains, and they shall smoke.
19_144_6: Flash forth lightning and scatter them; shoot out Your arrows and destroy them.
19_144_7: Stretch out Your hand from above; rescue me and deliver me out of great waters, from the hand of foreigners,
19_144_8: Whose mouth speaks lying words, and whose right hand is a right hand of falsehood.
19_144_9: I will sing a new song to You, O God; on a harp of ten strings I will sing praises to You,
19_144_10: The One who gives salvation to kings, who delivers David His servant from the deadly sword.
19_144_11: Rescue me and deliver me from the hand of foreigners, whose mouth speaks lying words, and whose right hand is a right hand of falsehood—
19_144_12: That our sons may be as plants grown up in their youth; that our daughters may be as pillars, sculptured in palace style;
19_144_13: That our barns may be full, supplying all kinds of produce; that our sheep may bring forth thousands and ten thousands in our fields;
19_144_14: That our oxen may be well laden; that there be no breaking in or going out; that there be no outcry in our streets.
19_144_15: Happy are the people who are in such a state; happy are the people whose God is the LORD!
19_145_1: I will extol You, my God, O King; and I will bless Your name forever and ever.
19_145_2: Every day I will bless You, and I will praise Your name forever and ever.
19_145_3: Great is the LORD, and greatly to be praised; and His greatness is unsearchable.
19_145_4: One generation shall praise Your works to another, and shall declare Your mighty acts.
19_145_5: I will meditate on the glorious splendor of Your majesty, and on Your wondrous works.
19_145_6: Men shall speak of the might of Your awesome acts, and I will declare Your greatness.
19_145_7: They shall utter the memory of Your great goodness, and shall sing of Your righteousness.
19_145_8: The LORD is gracious and full of compassion, slow to anger and great in mercy.
19_145_9: The LORD is good to all, and His tender mercies are over all His works.
19_145_10: All Your works shall praise You, O LORD, and Your saints shall bless You.
19_145_11: They shall speak of the glory of Your kingdom, and talk of Your power,
19_145_12: To make known to the sons of men His mighty acts, and the glorious majesty of His kingdom.
19_145_13: Your kingdom is an everlasting kingdom, and Your dominion endures throughout all generations. The LORD is faithful in all His words and gracious in all His actions.
19_145_14: The LORD upholds all who fall, and raises up all who are bowed down.
19_145_15: The eyes of all look expectantly to You, and You give them their food in due season.
19_145_16: You open Your hand and satisfy the desire of every living thing.
19_145_17: The LORD is righteous in all His ways, gracious in all His works.
19_145_18: The LORD is near to all who call upon Him, to all who call upon Him in truth.
19_145_19: He will fulfill the desire of those who fear Him; He also will hear their cry and save them.
19_145_20: The LORD preserves all who love Him, but He will destroy all the wicked.
19_145_21: My mouth shall speak the praise of the LORD, and let all flesh bless His holy name forever and ever.
19_146_1: Praise the LORD! Let all that I am praise the LORD.
19_146_2: While I live I will praise the LORD; I will sing praises to my God as long as I have my being.
19_146_3: Do not put your trust in princes, nor in a son of man, in whom there is no salvation.
19_146_4: When his breath departs, he returns to the earth; on that very day his plans perish.
19_146_5: Happy is he who has the God of Jacob for his help, whose hope is in the LORD his God.
19_146_6: Who made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that is in them; who keeps faith forever.
19_146_7: Who executes justice for the oppressed, who gives food to the hungry. The LORD sets the prisoners free.
19_146_8: The LORD opens the eyes of the blind; the LORD raises those who are bowed down; the LORD loves the righteous.
19_146_9: The LORD watches over the strangers; He relieves the fatherless and widow, but the way of the wicked He brings to ruin.
19_146_10: The LORD shall reign forever—your God, O Zion, to all generations. Praise the LORD!
19_147_1: Praise the LORD! For it is good to sing praises to our God; for it is pleasant, and praise is beautiful.
19_147_2: The LORD builds up Jerusalem; He gathers together the outcasts of Israel.
19_147_3: He heals the brokenhearted and binds up their wounds.
19_147_4: He counts the number of the stars; He calls them all by name.
19_147_5: Great is our Lord, and mighty in power; His understanding is beyond measure.
19_147_6: The LORD lifts up the humble; He casts the wicked down to the ground.
19_147_7: Sing to the LORD with thanksgiving; sing praises on the harp to our God.
19_147_8: Who covers the heavens with clouds, who prepares rain for the earth, who makes grass to grow on the mountains.
19_147_9: He gives to the beast its food, and to the young ravens that cry.
19_147_10: He does not delight in the strength of the horse; He takes no pleasure in the legs of a man.
19_147_11: The LORD takes pleasure in those who fear Him, in those who hope in His steadfast love.
19_147_12: Praise the LORD, O Jerusalem! Praise your God, O Zion!
19_147_13: For He has strengthened the bars of your gates; He has blessed your children within you.
19_147_14: He makes peace in your borders and fills you with the finest wheat.
19_147_15: He sends out His command to the earth; His word runs very swiftly.
19_147_16: He gives snow like wool; He scatters the frost like ashes.
19_147_17: He casts out His hail like morsels; who can stand before His cold?
19_147_18: He sends out His word and melts them; He causes His wind to blow, and the waters flow.
19_147_19: He declares His word to Jacob, His statutes and His judgments to Israel.
19_147_20: He has not dealt thus with any nation; and as for His judgments, they have not known them. Praise the LORD!
19_148_1: Praise the LORD! Praise the LORD from the heavens; praise Him in the heights!
19_148_2: Praise Him, all His angels; praise Him, all His hosts!
19_148_3: Praise Him, sun and moon; praise Him, all you stars of light!
19_148_4: Praise Him, you heavens of heavens, and you waters above the heavens!
19_148_5: Let them praise the name of the LORD, for He commanded and they were created.
19_148_6: He also established them forever and ever; He made a decree which shall not pass away.
19_148_7: Praise the LORD from the earth, you great sea creatures and all the depths;
19_148_8: Fire and hail, snow and clouds; stormy wind, fulfilling His word;
19_148_9: Mountains and all hills, fruit trees and all cedars;
19_148_10: Beasts and all cattle, creeping things and flying birds;
19_148_11: Kings of the earth and all peoples, princes and all judges of the earth;
19_148_12: Young men and maidens together, old men and children.
19_148_13: Let them praise the name of the LORD, for His name alone is exalted; His glory is above the earth and heaven.
19_148_14: And He has exalted the horn of His people, the praise of all His saints—of the children of Israel, a people near to Him. Praise the LORD!
19_149_1: Praise the LORD! Sing to the LORD a new song, and His praise in the assembly of the faithful.
19_149_2: Let Israel rejoice in their Maker; let the children of Zion be joyful in their King.
19_149_3: Let them praise His name with dancing; let them sing praises to Him with the timbrel and harp.
19_149_4: For the LORD takes pleasure in His people; He adorns the humble with salvation.
19_149_5: Let the saints be joyful in glory; let them sing aloud on their beds.
19_149_6: Let the high praises of God be in their mouth, and a two-edged sword in their hand,
19_149_7: To execute vengeance on the nations, and punishments on the peoples;
19_149_8: To bind their kings with chains, and their nobles with fetters of iron;
19_149_9: To execute on them the written judgment—this honor have all His saints. Praise the LORD!
19_150_1: Praise the LORD! Praise God in His sanctuary; praise Him in His mighty firmament!
19_150_2: Praise Him for His mighty acts; praise Him according to His excellent greatness!
19_150_3: Praise Him with the sound of the trumpet; praise Him with the lute and harp!
19_150_4: Praise Him with the timbrel and dance; praise Him with stringed instruments and flutes!
19_150_5: Praise Him with loud cymbals; praise Him with clashing cymbals!
19_150_6: Let everything that has breath praise the LORD. Praise the LORD!
20_1_1: The proverbs of Solomon, the son of David, king of Israel:
20_1_2: To know wisdom and instruction, to understand words of insight,
20_1_3: To receive the instruction of wisdom, justice, judgment, and equity;
20_1_4: To give prudence to the simple, to the young man knowledge and discretion—
20_1_5: A wise man will hear and increase learning, and a man of understanding will attain wise counsel,
20_1_6: To understand a proverb and an enigma, the words of the wise and their riddles.
20_1_7: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge, but fools despise wisdom and instruction.
20_1_8: My son, hear the instruction of your father, and do not forsake the law of your mother;
20_1_9: For they will be a graceful ornament on your head, and chains about your neck.
20_1_10: My son, if sinners entice you, do not consent.
20_1_11: If they say, “Come with us, let us lie in wait to shed blood; let us lurk secretly for the innocent without cause;
20_1_12: Let us swallow them alive like Sheol, and whole, like those who go down to the Pit;
20_1_13: We shall find all kinds of precious possessions, we shall fill our houses with spoil;
20_1_14: Cast in your lot among us, let us all have one purse”—
20_1_15: My son, do not walk in the way with them, keep your foot from their path;
20_1_16: For their feet run to evil, and they make haste to shed blood.
20_1_17: Surely, in vain the net is spread in the sight of any bird;
20_1_18: But they lie in wait for their own blood, they lurk secretly for their own lives.
20_1_19: So are the ways of everyone who is greedy for gain; it takes away the life of its owners.
20_1_20: Wisdom calls aloud outside; she raises her voice in the open squares.
20_1_21: She cries out in the chief concourses, at the openings of the gates in the city she speaks her words:
20_1_22: “How long, you simple ones, will you love simplicity? For scorners delight in their scorning, and fools hate knowledge.
20_1_23: Turn at my rebuke; surely I will pour out my spirit on you; I will make my words known to you.
20_1_24: Because I have called and you refused, I have stretched out my hand and no one regarded,
20_1_25: Because you disdained all my counsel, and would have none of my rebuke,
20_1_26: I also will laugh at your calamity; I will mock when your terror comes,
20_1_27: When your terror comes like a storm, and your destruction comes like a whirlwind, when distress and anguish come upon you.
20_1_28: “Then they will call on me, but I will not answer; they will seek me diligently, but they will not find me.
20_1_29: Because they hated knowledge and did not choose the fear of the LORD,
20_1_30: They would have none of my counsel and despised my every rebuke.
20_1_31: Therefore they shall eat the fruit of their own way, and be filled to the full with their own fancies.
20_1_32: For the turning away of the simple will slay them, and the complacency of fools will destroy them;
20_1_33: But whoever listens to me will dwell safely, and will be secure, without fear of evil.”
20_2_1: My son, if you receive my words, and treasure my commands within you,
20_2_2: So that you incline your ear to wisdom, and apply your heart to understanding;
20_2_3: Yes, if you cry out for discernment, and lift up your voice for understanding,
20_2_4: If you seek her as silver, and search for her as for hidden treasures;
20_2_5: Then you will understand the fear of the LORD, and find the knowledge of God.
20_2_6: For the LORD gives wisdom; from His mouth come knowledge and understanding;
20_2_7: He stores up sound wisdom for the upright; He is a shield to those who walk uprightly;
20_2_8: He guards the paths of justice and protects the way of His faithful ones.
20_2_9: Then you will understand righteousness and justice, equity, and every good path.
20_2_10: For wisdom will enter your heart, and knowledge will be pleasant to your soul.
20_2_11: Discretion will watch over you; understanding will guard you.
20_2_12: To deliver you from the way of evil, from the man who speaks perverse things,
20_2_13: From those who leave the paths of uprightness to walk in the ways of darkness;
20_2_14: Who rejoice in doing evil and delight in the perversity of the wicked;
20_2_15: Whose ways are crooked and who are devious in their paths;
20_2_16: To deliver you from the immoral woman, from the seductress who flatters with her words,
20_2_17: Who forsakes the companion of her youth and forgets the covenant of her God.
20_2_18: For her house leads down to death, and her paths to the dead;
20_2_19: None who go to her return, nor do they regain the paths of life—
20_2_20: So you may walk in the way of goodness and keep to the paths of righteousness.
20_2_21: For the upright will dwell in the land, and the blameless will remain in it;
20_2_22: But the wicked will be cut off from the earth, and the unfaithful will be uprooted from it.
20_3_1: My son, do not forget my law, but let your heart keep my commands;
20_3_2: For length of days and long life and peace they will add to you.
20_3_3: Let not mercy and truth forsake you; bind them around your neck, write them on the tablet of your heart,
20_3_4: And so find favor and high esteem in the sight of God and man.
20_3_5: Trust in the LORD with all your heart, and lean not on your own understanding;
20_3_6: In all your ways acknowledge Him, and He shall direct your paths.
20_3_7: Do not be wise in your own eyes; fear the LORD and depart from evil.
20_3_8: It will be health to your flesh and strength to your bones.
20_3_9: Honor the LORD with your possessions, and with the firstfruits of all your increase;
20_3_10: So your barns will be filled with plenty, and your vats will overflow with new wine.
20_3_11: My son, do not despise the chastening of the LORD, nor detest His correction;
20_3_12: For whom the LORD loves He corrects, just as a father the son in whom he delights.
20_3_13: Happy is the man who finds wisdom, and the man who gains understanding;
20_3_14: For her proceeds are better than the profits of silver, and her gain than fine gold.
20_3_15: She is more precious than rubies, and all the things you may desire cannot compare with her.
20_3_16: Length of days is in her right hand, in her left hand riches and honor.
20_3_17: Her ways are ways of pleasantness, and all her paths are peace.
20_3_18: She is a tree of life to those who take hold of her, and happy are all who retain her.
20_3_19: The LORD by wisdom founded the earth; by understanding He established the heavens;
20_3_20: By His knowledge the depths were broken up, and clouds drop down the dew.
20_3_21: My son, let them not depart from your eyes—keep sound wisdom and discretion;
20_3_22: So they will be life to your soul and grace to your neck.
20_3_23: Then you will walk safely in your way, and your foot will not stumble.
20_3_24: When you lie down, you will not be afraid; yes, you will lie down and your sleep will be sweet.
20_3_25: Do not be afraid of sudden terror, nor of trouble from the wicked when it comes;
20_3_26: For the LORD will be your confidence, and will keep your foot from being caught in a snare.
20_3_27: Do not withhold good from those to whom it is due, when it is in your power to do so.
20_3_28: Do not say to your neighbor, “Go, and come back, and tomorrow I will give it,” when you have it with you.
20_3_29: Do not devise evil against your neighbor, for he dwells trustingly beside you.
20_3_30: Do not strive with a man without cause, if he has done you no harm.
20_3_31: Do not envy a man of violence, and choose none of his ways.
20_3_32: For the devious are an abomination to the LORD, but His secret counsel is with the upright.
20_3_33: The curse of the LORD is on the house of the wicked, but He blesses the home of the just.
20_3_34: Surely He scorns the scornful, but gives grace to the humble.
20_3_35: The wise shall inherit glory, but shame shall be the legacy of fools.
20_4_1: Hear, my children, the instruction of a father, and give attention to know understanding.
20_4_2: For I give you good doctrine; do not forsake my law.
20_4_3: When I was my father’s son, tender and the only one in the sight of my mother.
20_4_4: He also taught me, and said to me: “Let your heart retain my words; keep my commands, and live.
20_4_5: Get wisdom! Get understanding! Do not forget, nor turn away from the words of my mouth.
20_4_6: Do not forsake her, and she will preserve you; love her, and she will keep you.
20_4_7: Wisdom is the principal thing; therefore get wisdom. And in all your getting, get understanding.
20_4_8: Exalt her, and she will promote you; she will bring you honor, when you embrace her.
20_4_9: She will place on your head an ornament of grace; a crown of glory she will deliver to you.
20_4_10: Hear, my son, and receive my sayings, and the years of your life will be many.
20_4_11: I have taught you in the way of wisdom; I have led you in right paths.
20_4_12: When you walk, your steps will not be hindered, and when you run, you will not stumble.
20_4_13: Take firm hold of instruction, do not let go; keep her, for she is your life.
20_4_14: Do not enter the path of the wicked, and do not walk in the way of evil.
20_4_15: Avoid it, do not travel on it; turn away from it and pass on.
20_4_16: For they do not sleep unless they have done evil; and their sleep is taken away unless they make someone fall.
20_4_17: For they eat the bread of wickedness, and drink the wine of violence.
20_4_18: But the path of the just is like the shining sun, that shines ever brighter unto the perfect day.
20_4_19: The way of the wicked is like darkness; they do not know what makes them stumble.
20_4_20: My son, give attention to my words; incline your ear to my sayings.
20_4_21: Do not let them depart from your eyes; keep them in the midst of your heart.
20_4_22: For they are life to those who find them, and health to all their flesh.
20_4_23: Keep your heart with all diligence, for out of it spring the issues of life.
20_4_24: Put away from you a deceitful mouth, and put perverse lips far from you.
20_4_25: Let your eyes look straight ahead, and your eyelids look right before you.
20_4_26: Ponder the path of your feet, and let all your ways be established.
20_4_27: Do not turn to the right or the left; remove your foot from evil.
20_5_1: My son, pay attention to my wisdom; lend your ear to my understanding.
20_5_2: That you may preserve discretion, and your lips may keep knowledge.
20_5_3: For the lips of an immoral woman drip honey, and her mouth is smoother than oil.
20_5_4: But in the end she is as bitter as wormwood, sharp as a double-edged sword.
20_5_5: Her feet go down to death, her steps take hold of Sheol.
20_5_6: She does not ponder the path of life; her ways are unstable, and she does not know it.
20_5_7: Therefore hear me now, my children, and do not depart from the words of my mouth.
20_5_8: Remove your way far from her, and do not go near the door of her house.
20_5_9: Lest you give your honor to others, and your years to the merciless.
20_5_10: Lest strangers be filled with your wealth, and your labors go to the house of a foreigner.
20_5_11: And you mourn at last, when your flesh and your body are consumed.
20_5_12: And say: “How I have hated instruction, and my heart despised correction!
20_5_13: I have not obeyed the voice of my teachers, nor inclined my ear to those who instructed me!
20_5_14: I was on the verge of total ruin, in the midst of the assembly and congregation.”
20_5_15: Drink water from your own cistern, and running water from your own well.
20_5_16: Should your fountains be dispersed abroad, streams of water in the streets?
20_5_17: Let them be only your own, and not for strangers with you.
20_5_18: Let your fountain be blessed, and rejoice with the wife of your youth.
20_5_19: As a loving deer and a graceful doe, let her breasts satisfy you at all times; and always be enraptured with her love.
20_5_20: For why should you, my son, be enraptured by an immoral woman, and be embraced in the arms of a seductress?
20_5_21: For the ways of man are before the eyes of the LORD, and He ponders all his paths.
20_5_22: His own iniquities entrap the wicked man, and he is caught in the cords of his sin.
20_5_23: He shall die for lack of instruction, and in the greatness of his folly he shall go astray.
20_6_1: My son, if you become surety for your friend, if you have shaken hands in pledge for a stranger,
20_6_2: You are snared by the words of your mouth; you are taken by the words of your mouth.
20_6_3: So do this, my son, and deliver yourself; for you have come into the hand of your friend: go and humble yourself; plead with your friend.
20_6_4: Give no sleep to your eyes, nor slumber to your eyelids.
20_6_5: Deliver yourself like a gazelle from the hand of the hunter, and like a bird from the hand of the fowler.
20_6_6: Go to the ant, you sluggard! Consider her ways and be wise,
20_6_7: Which, having no captain, overseer or ruler,
20_6_8: Provides her supplies in the summer, and gathers her food in the harvest.
20_6_9: How long will you slumber, O sluggard? When will you rise from your sleep?
20_6_10: A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to sleep—
20_6_11: So shall your poverty come on you like a prowler, and your need like an armed man.
20_6_12: A worthless person, a wicked man, walks with a perverse mouth;
20_6_13: He winks with his eyes, he shuffles his feet, he points with his fingers;
20_6_14: Perversity is in his heart, he devises evil continually, he sows discord.
20_6_15: Therefore his calamity shall come suddenly; suddenly he shall be broken without remedy.
20_6_16: These six things the LORD hates, yes, seven are an abomination to Him:
20_6_17: A proud look, a lying tongue, hands that shed innocent blood,
20_6_18: A heart that devises wicked plans, feet that are swift in running to evil,
20_6_19: A false witness who speaks lies, and one who sows discord among brethren.
20_6_20: My son, keep your father’s command, and do not forsake the law of your mother.
20_6_21: Bind them continually upon your heart; tie them around your neck.
20_6_22: When you roam, they will lead you; when you sleep, they will keep you; and when you awake, they will speak with you.
20_6_23: For the commandment is a lamp, and the law a light; reproofs of instruction are the way of life.
20_6_24: To keep you from the evil woman, from the flattering tongue of a seductress.
20_6_25: Do not lust after her beauty in your heart, nor let her allure you with her eyelids.
20_6_26: For by means of a harlot a man is reduced to a crust of bread; and an adulteress will prey upon his precious life.
20_6_27: Can a man take fire to his bosom, and his clothes not be burned?
20_6_28: Can one walk on hot coals, and his feet not be seared?
20_6_29: So is he who goes in to his neighbor’s wife; whoever touches her shall not be innocent.
20_6_30: People do not despise a thief if he steals to satisfy himself when he is starving.
20_6_31: Yet when he is found, he must restore sevenfold; he may have to give up all the substance of his house.
20_6_32: Whoever commits adultery with a woman lacks understanding; he who does so destroys his own soul.
20_6_33: Wounds and dishonor he will get, and his reproach will not be wiped away.
20_6_34: For jealousy is a husband’s fury; therefore he will not spare in the day of vengeance.
20_6_35: He will accept no recompense, nor will he be appeased though you give many gifts.
20_7_1: My son, keep my words, and treasure my commands within you.
20_7_2: Keep my commands and live, and my law as the apple of your eye.
20_7_3: Bind them on your fingers; write them on the tablet of your heart.
20_7_4: Say to wisdom, “You are my sister,” and call understanding your nearest kin.
20_7_5: That they may keep you from the immoral woman, from the seductress who flatters with her words.
20_7_6: For at the window of my house I looked through my lattice,
20_7_7: And saw among the simple, I perceived among the youths, a young man devoid of understanding,
20_7_8: Passing along the street near her corner; and he took the path to her house
20_7_9: In the twilight, in the evening, in the black and dark night.
20_7_10: And there a woman met him, with the attire of a harlot, and a crafty heart.
20_7_11: She was loud and rebellious, her feet would not stay at home.
20_7_12: At times she was outside, at times in the open square, lurking at every corner.
20_7_13: So she caught him and kissed him; with an impudent face she said to him:
20_7_14: “I have peace offerings with me; today I have paid my vows.
20_7_15: So I came out to meet you, diligently to seek your face, and I have found you.
20_7_16: I have spread my bed with tapestry, colored coverings of Egyptian linen.
20_7_17: I have perfumed my bed with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.
20_7_18: Come, let us take our fill of love until morning; let us delight ourselves with love.
20_7_19: For my husband is not at home; he has gone on a long journey;
20_7_20: He has taken a bag of money with him, and will come home on the appointed day.”
20_7_21: With her enticing speech she caused him to yield, with her flattering lips she seduced him.
20_7_22: Immediately he went after her, as an ox goes to the slaughter, or as a fool to the correction of the stocks,
20_7_23: Till an arrow struck his liver. As a bird hastens to the snare, he did not know it would cost his life.
20_7_24: Now therefore, listen to me, my children; pay attention to the words of my mouth:
20_7_25: Do not let your heart turn aside to her ways, do not stray into her paths;
20_7_26: For she has cast down many wounded, and all her slain are a mighty throng.
20_7_27: Her house is the way to Sheol, descending to the chambers of death.
20_8_1: Does not wisdom cry out, and understanding lift up her voice?
20_8_2: She takes her stand on the top of the high hill, beside the way, where the paths meet.
20_8_3: She cries out by the gates, at the entry of the city, at the entrance of the doors:
20_8_4: To you, O men, I call, and my voice is to the sons of men.
20_8_5: O you simple ones, understand prudence, and you fools, be of an understanding heart.
20_8_6: Listen, for I will speak of excellent things, and from the opening of my lips will come right things;
20_8_7: For my mouth will speak truth; wickedness is an abomination to my lips.
20_8_8: All the words of my mouth are with righteousness; nothing crooked or perverse is in them.
20_8_9: They are all plain to him who understands, and right to those who find knowledge.
20_8_10: Receive my instruction, and not silver, and knowledge rather than choice gold;
20_8_11: For wisdom is better than rubies, and all the things one may desire cannot be compared with her.
20_8_12: I, wisdom, dwell with prudence, and find out knowledge and discretion.
20_8_13: The fear of the LORD is to hate evil; pride and arrogance and the evil way and the perverse mouth I hate.
20_8_14: Counsel is mine, and sound wisdom; I am understanding, I have strength.
20_8_15: By me kings reign, and rulers decree justice.
20_8_16: By me princes rule, and nobles, all the judges of the earth.
20_8_17: I love those who love me, and those who seek me diligently will find me.
20_8_18: Riches and honor are with me, enduring riches and righteousness.
20_8_19: My fruit is better than gold, yes, than fine gold, and my revenue than choice silver.
20_8_20: I traverse the way of righteousness, in the midst of the paths of justice,
20_8_21: That I may cause those who love me to inherit wealth, that I may fill their treasuries.
20_8_22: The LORD possessed me at the beginning of His way, before His works of old.
20_8_23: I have been established from everlasting, from the beginning, before there was ever an earth.
20_8_24: When there were no depths I was brought forth, when there were no fountains abounding with water.
20_8_25: Before the mountains were settled, before the hills, I was brought forth;
20_8_26: While as yet He had not made the earth or the fields, or the primal dust of the world.
20_8_27: When He prepared the heavens, I was there, when He drew a circle on the face of the deep,
20_8_28: When He established the clouds above, when He strengthened the fountains of the deep,
20_8_29: When He assigned to the sea its limit, so that the waters would not transgress His command, when He marked out the foundations of the earth,
20_8_30: Then I was beside Him as a master craftsman; and I was daily His delight, rejoicing always before Him,
20_8_31: Rejoicing in His inhabited world, and my delight was with the sons of men.
20_8_32: Now therefore, listen to me, my children, for blessed are those who keep my ways.
20_8_33: Hear instruction and be wise, and do not disdain it.
20_8_34: Blessed is the man who listens to me, watching daily at my gates, waiting at the posts of my doors.
20_8_35: For whoever finds me finds life, and obtains favor from the LORD;
20_8_36: But he who sins against me wrongs his own soul; all those who hate me love death.
20_9_1: Wisdom has built her house, she has hewn out her seven pillars;
20_9_2: She has slaughtered her meat, she has mixed her wine, she has also furnished her table.
20_9_3: She has sent out her maidens, she calls out from the highest places of the city.
20_9_4: “Whoever is simple, let him turn in here!” To him who lacks understanding, she says,
20_9_5: “Come, eat of my bread and drink of the wine I have mixed.
20_9_6: Forsake foolishness and live, and go in the way of understanding.
20_9_7: He who corrects a scoffer gets shame for himself, and he who rebukes a wicked man only harms himself.
20_9_8: Do not correct a scoffer, lest he hate you; rebuke a wise man, and he will love you.
20_9_9: Give instruction to a wise man, and he will be still wiser; teach a just man, and he will increase in learning.
20_9_10: The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom, and the knowledge of the Holy One is understanding.
20_9_11: For by me your days will be multiplied, and years of life will be added to you.
20_9_12: If you are wise, you are wise for yourself, and if you scoff, you will bear it alone.
20_9_13: A foolish woman is clamorous; she is simple, and knows nothing.
20_9_14: For she sits at the door of her house, on a seat by the highest places of the city,
20_9_15: To call to those who pass by, who go straight on their way:
20_9_16: “Whoever is simple, let him turn in here”; and as for him who lacks understanding, she says to him,
20_9_17: “Stolen water is sweet, and bread eaten in secret is pleasant.”
20_9_18: But he does not know that the dead are there, that her guests are in the depths of hell.
20_10_1: The proverbs of Solomon: A wise son makes a glad father, but a foolish son is the grief of his mother.
20_10_2: Treasures of wickedness profit nothing, but righteousness delivers from death.
20_10_3: The LORD will not allow the righteous soul to famish, but He casts away the desire of the wicked.
20_10_4: He who has a slack hand becomes poor, but the hand of the diligent makes rich.
20_10_5: He who gathers in summer is a wise son; he who sleeps in harvest is a son who causes shame.
20_10_6: Blessings are on the head of the righteous, but violence covers the mouth of the wicked.
20_10_7: The memory of the righteous is blessed, but the name of the wicked will rot.
20_10_8: The wise in heart will receive commands, but a prating fool will fall.
20_10_9: He who walks with integrity walks securely, but he who perverts his ways will become known.
20_10_10: He who winks with the eye causes trouble, but a prating fool will fall.
20_10_11: The mouth of the righteous is a well of life, but violence covers the mouth of the wicked.
20_10_12: Hatred stirs up strife, but love covers all sins.
20_10_13: Wisdom is found on the lips of him who has understanding, but a rod is for the back of him who is devoid of understanding.
20_10_14: Wise people store up knowledge, but the mouth of the foolish is near destruction.
20_10_15: The rich man’s wealth is his strong city; the destruction of the poor is their poverty.
20_10_16: The labor of the righteous leads to life, the wages of the wicked to sin.
20_10_17: He who keeps instruction is in the way of life, but he who refuses correction goes astray.
20_10_18: Whoever hides hatred has lying lips, and whoever spreads slander is a fool.
20_10_19: In the multitude of words sin is not lacking, but he who restrains his lips is wise.
20_10_20: The tongue of the righteous is choice silver; the heart of the wicked is worth little.
20_10_21: The lips of the righteous feed many, but fools die for lack of wisdom.
20_10_22: The blessing of the LORD makes one rich, and He adds no sorrow with it.
20_10_23: To do evil is like sport to a fool, but a man of understanding has wisdom.
20_10_24: The fear of the wicked will come upon him, and the desire of the righteous will be granted.
20_10_25: When the whirlwind passes by, the wicked is no more, but the righteous has an everlasting foundation.
20_10_26: As vinegar to the teeth and smoke to the eyes, so is the lazy man to those who send him.
20_10_27: The fear of the LORD prolongs days, but the years of the wicked will be shortened.
20_10_28: The hope of the righteous will be gladness, but the expectation of the wicked will perish.
20_10_29: The way of the LORD is strength for the upright, but destruction will come to the workers of iniquity.
20_10_30: The righteous will never be removed, but the wicked will not inhabit the earth.
20_10_31: The mouth of the righteous brings forth wisdom, but the perverse tongue will be cut out.
20_10_32: The lips of the righteous know what is acceptable, but the mouth of the wicked what is perverse.
20_11_1: Dishonest scales are an abomination to the LORD, but a just weight is His delight.
20_11_2: When pride comes, then comes shame; but with the humble is wisdom.
20_11_3: The integrity of the upright will guide them, but the perversity of the unfaithful will destroy them.
20_11_4: Riches do not profit in the day of wrath, but righteousness delivers from death.
20_11_5: The righteousness of the blameless will direct his way aright, but the wicked will fall by his own wickedness.
20_11_6: The righteousness of the upright will deliver them, but the unfaithful will be caught by their lust.
20_11_7: When a wicked man dies, his expectation will perish, and the hope of the unjust perishes.
20_11_8: The righteous is delivered from trouble, and it comes to the wicked instead.
20_11_9: The hypocrite with his mouth destroys his neighbor, but through knowledge the righteous will be delivered.
20_11_10: When it goes well with the righteous, the city rejoices; and when the wicked perish, there is jubilation.
20_11_11: By the blessing of the upright the city is exalted, but it is overthrown by the mouth of the wicked.
20_11_12: He who is devoid of wisdom despises his neighbor, but a man of understanding holds his peace.
20_11_13: A talebearer reveals secrets, but he who is of a faithful spirit conceals a matter.
20_11_14: Where there is no counsel, the people fall; but in the multitude of counselors there is safety.
20_11_15: He who is surety for a stranger will suffer, but one who hates being surety is secure.
20_11_16: A gracious woman retains honor, but ruthless men retain riches.
20_11_17: The merciful man does good for his own soul, but he who is cruel troubles his own flesh.
20_11_18: The wicked man does deceptive work, but he who sows righteousness will have a sure reward.
20_11_19: As righteousness leads to life, so he who pursues evil pursues it to his own death.
20_11_20: Those who are of a perverse heart are an abomination to the LORD, but the blameless in their ways are His delight.
20_11_21: Though they join forces, the wicked will not go unpunished; but the posterity of the righteous will be delivered.
20_11_22: As a ring of gold in a swine’s snout, so is a lovely woman who lacks discretion.
20_11_23: The desire of the righteous is only good, but the expectation of the wicked is wrath.
20_11_24: There is one who scatters, yet increases more; and there is one who withholds more than is right, but it leads to poverty.
20_11_25: The generous soul will be made rich, and he who waters will also be watered himself.
20_11_26: The people will curse him who withholds grain, but blessing will be on the head of him who sells it.
20_11_27: He who earnestly seeks good finds favor, but trouble will come to him who seeks evil.
20_11_28: He who trusts in his riches will fall, but the righteous will flourish like foliage.
20_11_29: He who troubles his own house will inherit the wind, and the fool will be servant to the wise of heart.
20_11_30: The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life, and he who wins souls is wise.
20_11_31: If the righteous will be recompensed on the earth, how much more the ungodly and the sinner.
20_12_1: Whoever loves instruction loves knowledge, but he who hates correction is stupid.
20_12_2: A good man obtains favor from the LORD, but a man of wicked intentions He will condemn.
20_12_3: No one is established by wickedness, but the root of the righteous cannot be moved.
20_12_4: An excellent wife is the crown of her husband, but she who causes shame is like rottenness in his bones.
20_12_5: The thoughts of the righteous are just, but the counsels of the wicked are deceitful.
20_12_6: The words of the wicked are a deadly ambush, but the mouth of the upright will deliver them.
20_12_7: The wicked are overthrown and are no more, but the house of the righteous will stand.
20_12_8: A man is praised for his wisdom, but he who is of a perverse heart will be despised.
20_12_9: Better is the one who is slighted but has a servant than he who honors himself but lacks bread.
20_12_10: A righteous man regards the life of his animal, but the tender mercies of the wicked are cruel.
20_12_11: He who tills his land will be satisfied with bread, but he who follows worthless pursuits lacks understanding.
20_12_12: The wicked covet the catch of evil men, but the root of the righteous yields fruit.
20_12_13: The wicked is ensnared by the transgression of his lips, but the righteous will escape from trouble.
20_12_14: A man will be satisfied with good by the fruit of his mouth, and the recompense of a man’s hands will be rendered to him.
20_12_15: The way of a fool is right in his own eyes, but he who heeds counsel is wise.
20_12_16: A fool’s wrath is known at once, but a prudent man covers shame.
20_12_17: He who speaks truth declares righteousness, but a false witness utters deceit.
20_12_18: There is one who speaks like the piercings of a sword, but the tongue of the wise brings healing.
20_12_19: Truthful lips shall be established forever, but a lying tongue is but for a moment.
20_12_20: Deceit is in the heart of those who devise evil, but counselors of peace have joy.
20_12_21: No grave trouble will overtake the righteous, but the wicked shall be filled with evil.
20_12_22: Lying lips are an abomination to the LORD, but those who deal truthfully are His delight.
20_12_23: A prudent man conceals knowledge, but the heart of fools proclaims foolishness.
20_12_24: The hand of the diligent will rule, but the lazy man will be put to forced labor.
20_12_25: Anxiety in the heart of man causes depression, but a good word makes it glad.
20_12_26: The righteous should choose his friends carefully, for the way of the wicked leads them astray.
20_12_27: The lazy man does not roast what he took in hunting, but diligence is man’s precious possession.
20_12_28: In the way of righteousness is life, and in its pathway there is no death.
20_13_1: A wise son heeds his father’s instruction, but a scoffer does not listen to rebuke.
20_13_2: A man shall eat well by the fruit of his mouth, but the soul of the unfaithful feeds on violence.
20_13_3: He who guards his mouth preserves his life, but he who opens wide his lips shall have destruction.
20_13_4: The soul of a lazy man desires, and has nothing; but the soul of the diligent shall be made rich.
20_13_5: A righteous man hates lying, but a wicked man is loathsome and comes to shame.
20_13_6: Righteousness guards him whose way is blameless, but wickedness overthrows the sinner.
20_13_7: There is one who makes himself rich, yet has nothing; and one who makes himself poor, yet has great riches.
20_13_8: The ransom of a man’s life is his riches, but the poor does not hear rebuke.
20_13_9: The light of the righteous rejoices, but the lamp of the wicked will be put out.
20_13_10: By pride comes nothing but strife, but with the well-advised is wisdom.
20_13_11: Wealth gained by dishonesty will be diminished, but he who gathers by labor will increase.
20_13_12: Hope deferred makes the heart sick, but when the desire comes, it is a tree of life.
20_13_13: He who despises the word will be destroyed, but he who fears the commandment will be rewarded.
20_13_14: The teaching of the wise is a fountain of life, turning one away from the snares of death.
20_13_15: Good understanding gains favor, but the way of the treacherous is hard.
20_13_16: Every prudent man acts with knowledge, but a fool flaunts his folly.
20_13_17: A wicked messenger falls into trouble, but a faithful envoy brings healing.
20_13_18: Poverty and shame will come to him who disdains correction, but he who regards a rebuke will be honored.
20_13_19: A desire accomplished is sweet to the soul, but it is an abomination to fools to depart from evil.
20_13_20: He who walks with wise men will be wise, but the companion of fools will suffer harm.
20_13_21: Evil pursues sinners, but to the righteous, good shall be repaid.
20_13_22: A good man leaves an inheritance to his children's children, but the wealth of the sinner is stored up for the righteous.
20_13_23: Much food is in the fallow ground of the poor, but for lack of justice there is waste.
20_13_24: He who spares his rod hates his son, but he who loves him disciplines him diligently.
20_13_25: The righteous eats to the satisfying of his soul, but the stomach of the wicked shall be in want.
20_14_1: The wise woman builds her house, but the foolish pulls it down with her hands.
20_14_2: He who walks in his uprightness fears the LORD, but he who is devious in his ways despises Him.
20_14_3: In the mouth of a fool is a rod of pride, but the lips of the wise will preserve them.
20_14_4: Where no oxen are, the trough is clean, but much increase comes by the strength of an ox.
20_14_5: A faithful witness does not lie, but a false witness will utter lies.
20_14_6: A scoffer seeks wisdom and does not find it, but knowledge is easy to him who understands.
20_14_7: Go from the presence of a foolish man, when you do not perceive in him the lips of knowledge.
20_14_8: The wisdom of the prudent is to understand his way, but the folly of fools is deceit.
20_14_9: Fools mock at sin, but among the upright there is favor.
20_14_10: The heart knows its own bitterness, and a stranger does not share its joy.
20_14_11: The house of the wicked will be overthrown, but the tent of the upright will flourish.
20_14_12: There is a way that seems right to a man, but its end is the way of death.
20_14_13: Even in laughter the heart may sorrow, and the end of mirth may be grief.
20_14_14: The backslider in heart will be filled with his own ways, but a good man will be satisfied from above.
20_14_15: The simple believes every word, but the prudent considers well his steps.
20_14_16: A wise man fears and departs from evil, but a fool rages and is self-confident.
20_14_17: A quick-tempered man acts foolishly, and a man of wicked intentions is hated.
20_14_18: The simple inherit folly, but the prudent are crowned with knowledge.
20_14_19: The evil will bow before the good, and the wicked at the gates of the righteous.
20_14_20: The poor man is hated even by his own neighbor, but the rich has many friends.
20_14_21: He who despises his neighbor sins, but he who has mercy on the poor, happy is he.
20_14_22: Do they not go astray who devise evil? But mercy and truth belong to those who devise good.
20_14_23: In all labor there is profit, but idle chatter leads only to poverty.
20_14_24: The crown of the wise is their riches, but the foolishness of fools is folly.
20_14_25: A true witness delivers souls, but a deceitful witness speaks lies.
20_14_26: In the fear of the LORD there is strong confidence, and His children will have a place of refuge.
20_14_27: The fear of the LORD is a fountain of life, to turn one away from the snares of death.
20_14_28: In a multitude of people is a king’s honor, but in the lack of people is the downfall of a prince.
20_14_29: He who is slow to anger has great understanding, but he who is quick-tempered exalts folly.
20_14_30: A tranquil heart is life to the body, but envy is rottenness to the bones.
20_14_31: He who oppresses the poor insults his Maker, but he who is kind to the needy honors Him.
20_14_32: The wicked is overthrown by his wrongdoing, but the righteous has a refuge in his death.
20_14_33: Wisdom rests in the heart of him who has understanding, but in the heart of fools it is made known.
20_14_34: Righteousness exalts a nation, but sin is a disgrace to any people.
20_14_35: The king’s favor is toward a wise servant, but his wrath is against him who causes shame.
20_15_1: A gentle answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.
20_15_2: The tongue of the wise uses knowledge rightly, but the mouth of fools pours forth foolishness.
20_15_3: The eyes of the LORD are in every place, keeping watch on the evil and the good.
20_15_4: A soothing tongue is a tree of life, but perverseness in it breaks the spirit.
20_15_5: A fool despises his father’s instruction, but he who receives correction is prudent.
20_15_6: In the house of the righteous there is much treasure, but trouble accompanies the income of the wicked.
20_15_7: The lips of the wise disperse knowledge, but the heart of the fool does not do so.
20_15_8: The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to the LORD, but the prayer of the upright is His delight.
20_15_9: The way of the wicked is an abomination to the LORD, but He loves him who follows righteousness.
20_15_10: Harsh discipline is for him who forsakes the way, and he who hates correction will die.
20_15_11: Sheol and Abaddon lie open before the LORD; how much more the hearts of the sons of men.
20_15_12: A scoffer does not love one who corrects him, nor will he go to the wise.
20_15_13: A merry heart makes a cheerful countenance, but by sorrow of the heart the spirit is broken.
20_15_14: The heart of him who has understanding seeks knowledge, but the mouth of fools feeds on foolishness.
20_15_15: All the days of the afflicted are evil, but he who is of a merry heart has a continual feast.
20_15_16: Better is a little with the fear of the LORD than great treasure with trouble.
20_15_17: Better is a dinner of herbs where love is than a fatted calf with hatred.
20_15_18: A hot-tempered man stirs up strife, but he who is slow to anger calms contention.
20_15_19: The way of the lazy man is like a hedge of thorns, but the way of the upright is a highway.
20_15_20: A wise son makes a father glad, but a foolish man despises his mother.
20_15_21: Folly is joy to him who lacks discernment, but a man of understanding walks uprightly.
20_15_22: Without counsel, plans go awry, but in the multitude of counselors they are established.
20_15_23: A man has joy by the answer of his mouth, and a word spoken in due season, how good it is!
20_15_24: The way of life winds upward for the wise, that he may turn away from hell below.
20_15_25: The LORD will destroy the house of the proud, but He will establish the boundary of the widow.
20_15_26: The thoughts of the wicked are an abomination to the LORD, but the words of the pure are pleasant.
20_15_27: He who is greedy for gain troubles his own house, but he who hates bribes will live.
20_15_28: The heart of the righteous studies how to answer, but the mouth of the wicked pours forth evil.
20_15_29: The LORD is far from the wicked, but He hears the prayer of the righteous.
20_15_30: The light of the eyes rejoices the heart, and a good report makes the bones healthy.
20_15_31: The ear that hears the rebukes of life will abide among the wise.
20_15_32: He who disdains instruction despises his own soul, but he who heeds rebuke gets understanding.
20_15_33: The fear of the LORD is the instruction of wisdom, and before honor is humility.
20_16_1: The preparations of the heart belong to man, but the answer of the tongue is from the LORD.
20_16_2: All the ways of a man are pure in his own eyes, but the LORD weighs the motives.
20_16_3: Commit your works to the LORD, and your plans will be established.
20_16_4: The LORD has made everything for its purpose, even the wicked for the day of disaster.
20_16_5: Everyone proud in heart is an abomination to the LORD; be assured, none will go unpunished.
20_16_6: In mercy and truth atonement is provided for iniquity; and by the fear of the LORD one departs from evil.
20_16_7: When a man’s ways please the LORD, He makes even his enemies to be at peace with him.
20_16_8: Better is a little with righteousness than vast revenues without justice.
20_16_9: A man’s heart plans his way, but the LORD directs his steps.
20_16_10: Divination is on the lips of the king; his mouth must not transgress in judgment.
20_16_11: Honest weights and scales are the LORD’s; all the weights in the bag are His work.
20_16_12: It is an abomination for kings to commit wickedness, for a throne is established by righteousness.
20_16_13: Righteous lips are the delight of kings, and they love him who speaks what is right.
20_16_14: As messengers of death is the king’s wrath, but a wise man will appease it.
20_16_15: In the light of the king’s face is life, and his favor is like a cloud of the latter rain.
20_16_16: How much better to get wisdom than gold! And to get understanding is to be chosen rather than silver.
20_16_17: The highway of the upright is to depart from evil; he who keeps his way preserves his soul.
20_16_18: Pride goes before destruction, and a haughty spirit before a fall.
20_16_19: Better to be of a humble spirit with the lowly than to divide the spoil with the proud.
20_16_20: He who heeds the word wisely will find good, and whoever trusts in the LORD, happy is he.
20_16_21: The wise in heart will be called prudent, and sweetness of the lips increases learning.
20_16_22: Understanding is a wellspring of life to him who has it, but the correction of fools is folly.
20_16_23: The heart of the wise teaches his mouth, and adds learning to his lips.
20_16_24: Pleasant words are like a honeycomb, sweetness to the soul and health to the bones.
20_16_25: There is a way that seems right to a man, but its end is the way of death.
20_16_26: The person who labors, labors for himself, for his hungry mouth drives him on.
20_16_27: An ungodly man digs up evil, and it is on his lips like a burning fire.
20_16_28: A perverse man sows strife, and a whisperer separates the best of friends.
20_16_29: A violent man entices his neighbor and leads him in a way that is not good.
20_16_30: He winks his eye to devise perverse things; he purses his lips and brings about evil.
20_16_31: The silver-haired head is a crown of glory, if it is found in the way of righteousness.
20_16_32: He who is slow to anger is better than the mighty, and he who rules his spirit than he who takes a city.
20_16_33: The lot is cast into the lap, but its every decision is from the LORD.
20_17_1: Better is a dry morsel with quietness than a house full of feasting with strife.
20_17_2: A wise servant will rule over a son who causes shame, and will share an inheritance among the brothers.
20_17_3: The refining pot is for silver and the furnace for gold, but the LORD tests the hearts.
20_17_4: An evildoer gives heed to false lips; a liar listens eagerly to a spiteful tongue.
20_17_5: He who mocks the poor reproaches his Maker; he who is glad at calamity will not go unpunished.
20_17_6: Children’s children are the crown of old men, and the glory of children is their father.
20_17_7: Excellent speech is not becoming to a fool, much less lying lips to a prince.
20_17_8: A bribe is like a precious stone in the eyes of its possessor; wherever he turns, he prospers.
20_17_9: He who covers a transgression seeks love, but he who repeats a matter separates close friends.
20_17_10: Rebuke is more effective for a wise man than a hundred blows on a fool.
20_17_11: An evil man seeks only rebellion; therefore a cruel messenger will be sent against him.
20_17_12: Let a man meet a bear robbed of her cubs rather than a fool in his folly.
20_17_13: Whoever rewards evil for good, evil will not depart from his house.
20_17_14: The beginning of strife is like releasing water; therefore stop contention before a quarrel starts.
20_17_15: He who justifies the wicked, and he who condemns the just, both are an abomination to the LORD.
20_17_16: Why is there in the hand of a fool the purchase price of wisdom, since he has no heart for it?
20_17_17: A friend loves at all times, and a brother is born for adversity.
20_17_18: A man devoid of understanding shakes hands in a pledge and becomes surety for his friend.
20_17_19: He who loves transgression loves strife, and he who exalts his gate seeks destruction.
20_17_20: He who has a deceitful heart finds no good, and he who has a perverse tongue falls into evil.
20_17_21: He who begets a scoffer does so to his sorrow, and the father of a fool has no joy.
20_17_22: A merry heart does good, like medicine, but a broken spirit dries the bones.
20_17_23: A wicked man accepts a bribe behind the back to pervert the ways of justice.
20_17_24: Wisdom is in the sight of him who has understanding, but the eyes of a fool are on the ends of the earth.
20_17_25: A foolish son is a grief to his father, and bitterness to her who bore him.
20_17_26: To punish the righteous is not good, nor to strike princes for their uprightness.
20_17_27: He who has knowledge spares his words, and a man of understanding is of a calm spirit.
20_17_28: Even a fool is counted wise when he holds his peace; when he shuts his lips, he is considered perceptive.
20_18_1: A man who isolates himself seeks his own desire; he rages against all wise judgment.
20_18_2: A fool has no delight in understanding, but in expressing his own heart.
20_18_3: When the wicked comes, contempt comes also; and with dishonor comes reproach.
20_18_4: The words of a man’s mouth are deep waters; the wellspring of wisdom is a flowing brook.
20_18_5: It is not good to show partiality to the wicked, or to overthrow the righteous in judgment.
20_18_6: A fool’s lips enter into contention, and his mouth calls for blows.
20_18_7: A fool’s mouth is his destruction, and his lips are the snare of his soul.
20_18_8: The words of a talebearer are like tasty trifles, and they go down into the inmost body.
20_18_9: He who is slothful in his work is a brother to him who is a great destroyer.
20_18_10: The name of the LORD is a strong tower; the righteous run to it and are safe.
20_18_11: The rich man’s wealth is his strong city, and like a high wall in his own esteem.
20_18_12: Before destruction the heart of a man is haughty, and before honor is humility.
20_18_13: He who answers a matter before he hears it, it is folly and shame to him.
20_18_14: The spirit of a man will sustain him in sickness, but who can bear a broken spirit?
20_18_15: The heart of the prudent acquires knowledge, and the ear of the wise seeks knowledge.
