20_18_16: A man’s gift makes room for him, and brings him before great men.
20_18_17: The first one to plead his cause seems right, until his neighbor comes and examines him.
20_18_18: Casting lots causes contentions to cease, and keeps the mighty apart.
20_18_19: A brother offended is harder to win than a strong city, and contentions are like the bars of a castle.
20_18_20: A man's stomach shall be satisfied from the fruit of his mouth; from the produce of his lips he shall be filled.
20_18_21: Death and life are in the power of the tongue, and those who love it will eat its fruit.
20_18_22: He who finds a wife finds a good thing, and obtains favor from the LORD.
20_18_23: The poor man uses entreaties, but the rich answers roughly.
20_18_24: A man who has friends must himself be friendly, but there is a friend who sticks closer than a brother.
20_19_1: Better is the poor who walks in his integrity than one who is perverse in his lips, and is a fool.
20_19_2: It is not good for a soul to be without knowledge, and he sins who hastens with his feet.
20_19_3: The foolishness of a man twists his way, and his heart frets against the LORD.
20_19_4: Wealth makes many friends, but the poor is separated from his friend.
20_19_5: A false witness will not go unpunished, and he who speaks lies will not escape.
20_19_6: Many entreat the favor of the nobility, and every man is a friend to one who gives gifts.
20_19_7: All the brothers of the poor hate him; how much more do his friends go far from him! He may pursue them with words, yet they abandon him.
20_19_8: He who gets wisdom loves his own soul; he who keeps understanding will find good.
20_19_9: A false witness will not go unpunished, and he who speaks lies shall perish.
20_19_10: Luxury is not fitting for a fool, much less for a servant to rule over princes.
20_19_11: The discretion of a man makes him slow to anger, and his glory is to overlook a transgression.
20_19_12: The king’s wrath is like the roaring of a lion, but his favor is like dew on the grass.
20_19_13: A foolish son is the ruin of his father, and the contentions of a wife are a continual dripping.
20_19_14: Houses and riches are an inheritance from fathers, but a prudent wife is from the LORD.
20_19_15: Laziness casts one into a deep sleep, and an idle person will suffer hunger.
20_19_16: He who keeps the commandment keeps his soul, but he who is careless of his ways will die.
20_19_17: He who has pity on the poor lends to the LORD, and He will pay back what he has given.
20_19_18: Chasten your son while there is hope, and do not set your heart on his destruction.
20_19_19: A man of great wrath will suffer punishment; for if you rescue him, you will have to do it again.
20_19_20: Listen to counsel and receive instruction, that you may be wise in your latter days.
20_19_21: There are many plans in a man’s heart, nevertheless the LORD’s counsel—that will stand.
20_19_22: What is desired in a man is kindness, and a poor man is better than a liar.
20_19_23: The fear of the LORD leads to life, and he who has it will abide in satisfaction; he will not be visited with evil.
20_19_24: A lazy man buries his hand in the bowl, and will not so much as bring it to his mouth again.
20_19_25: Strike a scoffer, and the simple will become wary; rebuke one who has understanding, and he will discern knowledge.
20_19_26: He who mistreats his father and chases away his mother is a son who causes shame and brings reproach.
20_19_27: Cease listening to instruction, my son, and you will stray from the words of knowledge.
20_19_28: A disreputable witness scorns justice, and the mouth of the wicked devours iniquity.
20_19_29: Judgments are prepared for scoffers, and beatings for the backs of fools.
20_20_1: Wine is a mocker, strong drink is a brawler, and whoever is led astray by it is not wise.
20_20_2: The wrath of a king is like the roaring of a lion; whoever provokes him to anger sins against his own life.
20_20_3: It is honorable for a man to stop striving, since any fool can start a quarrel.
20_20_4: The lazy man will not plow because of winter; he will beg during harvest and have nothing.
20_20_5: Counsel in the heart of man is like deep water, but a man of understanding will draw it out.
20_20_6: Most men will proclaim each his own goodness, but who can find a faithful man?
20_20_7: The righteous man walks in his integrity; blessed are his children after him.
20_20_8: A king who sits on the throne of judgment winnows all evil with his eyes.
20_20_9: Who can say, “I have made my heart clean, I am pure from my sin”?
20_20_10: Diverse weights and diverse measures, they are both alike, an abomination to the LORD.
20_20_11: Even a child is known by his deeds, whether what he does is pure and right.
20_20_12: The hearing ear and the seeing eye, the LORD has made them both.
20_20_13: Do not love sleep, lest you come to poverty; open your eyes, and you will be satisfied with bread.
20_20_14: “It is good for nothing,” cries the buyer; but when he has gone his way, then he boasts.
20_20_15: There is gold and a multitude of rubies, but the lips of knowledge are a precious jewel.
20_20_16: Take the garment of one who is surety for a stranger, and hold it as a pledge when it is for a seductress.
20_20_17: Bread gained by deceit is sweet to a man, but afterward his mouth will be filled with gravel.
20_20_18: Plans are established by counsel; by wise counsel wage war.
20_20_19: He who goes about as a talebearer reveals secrets; therefore do not associate with one who flatters with his lips.
20_20_20: Whoever curses his father or his mother, his lamp will be put out in deep darkness.
20_20_21: An inheritance gained hastily at the beginning will not be blessed at the end.
20_20_22: Do not say, “I will recompense evil”; wait for the LORD, and He will save you.
20_20_23: Diverse weights are an abomination to the LORD, and dishonest scales are not good.
20_20_24: A man’s steps are of the LORD; how then can a man understand his own way?
20_20_25: It is a snare for a man to devote rashly something as holy, and afterward to reconsider his vows.
20_20_26: A wise king sifts out the wicked, and brings the threshing wheel over them.
20_20_27: The spirit of a man is the lamp of the LORD, searching all the inner depths of his heart.
20_20_28: Mercy and truth preserve the king, and by lovingkindness he upholds his throne.
20_20_29: The glory of young men is their strength, and the splendor of old men is their gray head.
20_20_30: Blows that hurt cleanse away evil, as do stripes the inner depths of the heart.
20_21_1: The king’s heart is in the hand of the LORD, like the rivers of water; He turns it wherever He wishes.
20_21_2: Every way of a man is right in his own eyes, but the LORD weighs the hearts.
20_21_3: To do righteousness and justice is more acceptable to the LORD than sacrifice.
20_21_4: A haughty look, a proud heart, and the plowing of the wicked are sin.
20_21_5: The plans of the diligent lead surely to plenty, but those of everyone who is hasty, surely to poverty.
20_21_6: Getting treasures by a lying tongue is the fleeting fantasy of those who seek death.
20_21_7: The violence of the wicked will destroy them, because they refuse to do justice.
20_21_8: The way of a guilty man is perverse; but as for the pure, his work is right.
20_21_9: Better to dwell in a corner of a housetop, than in a house shared with a contentious woman.
20_21_10: The soul of the wicked desires evil; his neighbor finds no favor in his eyes.
20_21_11: When the scoffer is punished, the simple is made wise; but when the wise is instructed, he receives knowledge.
20_21_12: The righteous God wisely considers the house of the wicked, overthrowing the wicked for their wickedness.
20_21_13: Whoever shuts his ears to the cry of the poor will also cry himself and not be heard.
20_21_14: A gift in secret pacifies anger, and a bribe behind the back, strong wrath.
20_21_15: It is a joy for the just to do justice, but destruction will come to the workers of iniquity.
20_21_16: A man who wanders from the way of understanding will rest in the assembly of the dead.
20_21_17: He who loves pleasure will become a poor man; he who loves wine and luxury will not be rich.
20_21_18: The wicked shall be a ransom for the righteous, and the traitor for the upright.
20_21_19: Better to dwell in the wilderness than with a quarrelsome and angry woman.
20_21_20: There is precious treasure and oil in the dwelling of the wise, but a foolish man squanders it.
20_21_21: He who follows righteousness and unfailing love finds life, righteousness, and honor.
20_21_22: A wise man scales the city of the mighty and brings down the trusted stronghold.
20_21_23: Whoever guards his mouth and tongue keeps his soul from troubles.
20_21_24: A proud and haughty man — “Scoffer” is his name; he acts with arrogant pride.
20_21_25: The desire of the lazy man kills him, for his hands refuse to labor.
20_21_26: He covets greedily all day long, but the righteous gives and does not hold back.
20_21_27: The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination; how much more when he brings it with wicked intent!
20_21_28: A false witness shall perish, but the man who hears him will speak endlessly.
20_21_29: A wicked man hardens his face, but the upright establishes his way.
20_21_30: There is no wisdom or understanding or counsel against the LORD.
20_21_31: The horse is prepared for the day of battle, but deliverance is of the LORD.
20_22_1: A good name is to be chosen rather than great riches, loving favor rather than silver and gold.
20_22_2: The rich and the poor have this in common, the LORD is the maker of them all.
20_22_3: A prudent man foresees evil and hides himself, but the simple pass on and are punished.
20_22_4: By humility and the fear of the LORD are riches and honor and life.
20_22_5: Thorns and snares are in the way of the perverse; he who guards his soul will be far from them.
20_22_6: Train up a child in the way he should go, and when he is old he will not depart from it.
20_22_7: The rich rules over the poor, and the borrower is servant to the lender.
20_22_8: He who sows iniquity will reap sorrow, and the rod of his anger will fail.
20_22_9: He who has a generous eye will be blessed, for he gives of his bread to the poor.
20_22_10: Cast out the scoffer, and contention will leave; yes, strife and reproach will cease.
20_22_11: He who loves purity of heart and has grace on his lips, the king will be his friend.
20_22_12: The eyes of the LORD preserve knowledge, but He overthrows the words of the faithless.
20_22_13: The lazy man says, “There is a lion outside! I shall be slain in the streets!”
20_22_14: The mouth of an immoral woman is a deep pit; he who is abhorred by the LORD will fall there.
20_22_15: Foolishness is bound up in the heart of a child; the rod of correction will drive it far from him.
20_22_16: He who oppresses the poor to increase his riches, and he who gives to the rich, will surely come to poverty.
20_22_17: Incline your ear and hear the words of the wise, and apply your heart to my knowledge;
20_22_18: For it is a pleasant thing if you keep them within you; let them all be fixed upon your lips,
20_22_19: So that your trust may be in the LORD; I have instructed you today, even you.
20_22_20: Have I not written to you excellent things of counsels and knowledge,
20_22_21: That I may make you know the certainty of the words of truth, that you may answer words of truth to those who send to you?
20_22_22: Do not rob the poor because he is poor, nor oppress the afflicted at the gate;
20_22_23: For the LORD will plead their cause, and plunder the soul of those who plunder them.
20_22_24: Make no friendship with an angry man, and with a furious man do not go,
20_22_25: Lest you learn his ways and set a snare for your soul.
20_22_26: Do not be one of those who shakes hands in a pledge, or one who puts up security for debts.
20_22_27: If you have nothing with which to pay, why should he take your bed from under you?
20_22_28: Do not remove the ancient landmark which your fathers have set.
20_22_29: Do you see a man who excels in his work? He will stand before kings; he will not stand before obscure men.
20_23_1: When you sit down to eat with a ruler, consider carefully what is before you;
20_23_2: And put a knife to your throat if you are a man given to appetite.
20_23_3: Do not desire his delicacies, for they are deceptive food.
20_23_4: Do not overwork to be rich; because of your own understanding, cease!
20_23_5: Will you set your eyes on that which is not? For riches certainly make themselves wings; they fly away like an eagle toward heaven.
20_23_6: Do not eat the bread of a miser, nor desire his delicacies;
20_23_7: For as he thinks in his heart, so is he. “Eat and drink!” he says to you, but his heart is not with you.
20_23_8: The morsel you have eaten, you will vomit up, and waste your pleasant words.
20_23_9: Do not speak in the hearing of a fool, for he will despise the wisdom of your words.
20_23_10: Do not remove the ancient landmark, nor enter the fields of the fatherless;
20_23_11: For their Redeemer is mighty; he will plead their cause against you.
20_23_12: Apply your heart to instruction, and your ears to words of knowledge.
20_23_13: Do not withhold correction from a child, for if you beat him with a rod, he will not die.
20_23_14: You shall beat him with a rod, and deliver his soul from Sheol.
20_23_15: My son, if your heart is wise, my heart will rejoice—indeed, I myself;
20_23_16: Yes, my inmost being will rejoice when your lips speak right things.
20_23_17: Do not let your heart envy sinners, but be zealous for the fear of the LORD all the day;
20_23_18: For surely there is a hereafter, and your hope will not be cut off.
20_23_19: Hear, my son, and be wise; and guide your heart in the way.
20_23_20: Do not mix with winebibbers, or with gluttonous eaters of meat;
20_23_21: For the drunkard and the glutton will come to poverty, and drowsiness will clothe a man with rags.
20_23_22: Listen to your father who begot you, and do not despise your mother when she is old.
20_23_23: Buy the truth, and do not sell it, also wisdom and instruction and understanding.
20_23_24: The father of the righteous will greatly rejoice, and he who begets a wise child will delight in him.
20_23_25: Let your father and your mother be glad, and let her who bore you rejoice.
20_23_26: My son, give me your heart, and let your eyes observe my ways.
20_23_27: For a harlot is a deep pit, and a seductress is a narrow well.
20_23_28: She also lies in wait as for a victim, and increases the unfaithful among men.
20_23_29: Who has woe? Who has sorrow? Who has contentions? Who has complaints? Who has wounds without cause? Who has redness of eyes?
20_23_30: Those who linger long at the wine, those who go in search of mixed wine.
20_23_31: Do not look on the wine when it is red, when it sparkles in the cup, when it swirls around smoothly;
20_23_32: At the last it bites like a serpent, and stings like a viper.
20_23_33: Your eyes will see strange things, and your heart will utter perverse things.
20_23_34: Yes, you will be like one who lies down in the midst of the sea, or like one who lies at the top of the mast, saying:
20_23_35: “They have struck me, but I was not hurt; they have beaten me, but I did not feel it. When shall I awake, that I may seek another drink?”
20_24_1: Do not be envious of evil men, nor desire to be with them;
20_24_2: For their heart devises violence, and their lips talk of trouble.
20_24_3: Through wisdom a house is built, and by understanding it is established.
20_24_4: By knowledge the rooms are filled with all precious and pleasant riches.
20_24_5: A wise man is strong, and a man of knowledge increases strength.
20_24_6: For by wise counsel you will wage your own war, and in a multitude of counselors there is safety.
20_24_7: Wisdom is too lofty for a fool; he does not open his mouth in the gate.
20_24_8: He who plots to do evil will be called a schemer.
20_24_9: The devising of foolishness is sin, and the scoffer is an abomination to men.
20_24_10: If you faint in the day of adversity, your strength is small.
20_24_11: Deliver those who are drawn toward death, and hold back those stumbling to the slaughter.
20_24_12: If you say, “Surely we did not know this,” does not He who weighs the hearts consider it? He who keeps your soul, does He not know it? And will He not render to each man according to his deeds?
20_24_13: My son, eat honey because it is good, and the honeycomb which is sweet to your taste.
20_24_14: So shall the knowledge of wisdom be to your soul; if you have found it, there is a prospect, and your hope will not be cut off.
20_24_15: Do not lie in wait, O wicked man, against the dwelling of the righteous; do not plunder his resting place.
20_24_16: For a righteous man may fall seven times and rise again, but the wicked shall fall by calamity.
20_24_17: Do not rejoice when your enemy falls, and do not let your heart be glad when he stumbles.
20_24_18: Lest the LORD see it, and it displease Him, and He turn away His wrath from him.
20_24_19: Do not fret because of evildoers, nor be envious of the wicked.
20_24_20: For there will be no prospect for the evil man; the lamp of the wicked will be put out.
20_24_21: My son, fear the LORD and the king; do not associate with those given to change.
20_24_22: For their calamity will rise suddenly, and who knows the ruin those two can bring?
20_24_23: These things also belong to the wise: it is not good to show partiality in judgment.
20_24_24: He who says to the wicked, “You are righteous,” him the people will curse; nations will abhor him.
20_24_25: But those who rebuke the wicked will have delight, and a good blessing will come upon them.
20_24_26: He who gives a right answer kisses the lips.
20_24_27: Prepare your outside work, make it fit for yourself in the field; and afterward build your house.
20_24_28: Do not be a witness against your neighbor without cause, for would you deceive with your lips?
20_24_29: Do not say, “I will do to him just as he has done to me; I will render to the man according to his work.”
20_24_30: I went by the field of the lazy man, and by the vineyard of the man devoid of understanding;
20_24_31: And there it was, all overgrown with thorns; its surface was covered with nettles; its stone wall was broken down.
20_24_32: When I saw it, I considered it well; I looked on it and received instruction:
20_24_33: A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to rest;
20_24_34: So shall your poverty come like a prowler, and your need like an armed man.
20_25_1: These also are proverbs of Solomon which the men of Hezekiah king of Judah copied:
20_25_2: It is the glory of God to conceal a matter, but the glory of kings is to search out a matter.
20_25_3: As the heavens for height and the earth for depth, so the heart of kings is unsearchable.
20_25_4: Take away the dross from silver, and it will go to the silversmith for jewelry.
20_25_5: Take away the wicked from before the king, and his throne will be established in righteousness.
20_25_6: Do not exalt yourself in the presence of the king, and do not stand in the place of the great;
20_25_7: For it is better that he say to you, “Come up here,” than that you should be put lower in the presence of the prince, whom your eyes have seen.
20_25_8: Do not go hastily to court; for what will you do in the end, when your neighbor has put you to shame?
20_25_9: Debate your case with your neighbor, and do not disclose the secret to another.
20_25_10: Lest he who hears it expose your shame, and your reputation be ruined.
20_25_11: A word fitly spoken is like apples of gold in settings of silver.
20_25_12: Like an earring of gold and an ornament of fine gold is a wise rebuker to an obedient ear.
20_25_13: Like the cold of snow in time of harvest is a faithful messenger to those who send him, for he refreshes the soul of his masters.
20_25_14: Whoever falsely boasts of giving is like clouds and wind without rain.
20_25_15: By long forbearance a ruler is persuaded, and a gentle tongue breaks a bone.
20_25_16: Have you found honey? Eat only as much as you need, lest you be filled with it and vomit.
20_25_17: Seldom set foot in your neighbor’s house, lest he become weary of you and hate you.
20_25_18: A man who bears false witness against his neighbor is like a club, a sword, and a sharp arrow.
20_25_19: Confidence in an unfaithful man in time of trouble is like a bad tooth and a foot out of joint.
20_25_20: Like one who takes away a garment in cold weather, and like vinegar on soda, is one who sings songs to a heavy heart.
20_25_21: If your enemy is hungry, give him bread to eat; and if he is thirsty, give him water to drink.
20_25_22: For so you will heap coals of fire on his head, and the LORD will reward you.
20_25_23: The north wind brings forth rain, and a backbiting tongue an angry countenance.
20_25_24: It is better to dwell in a corner of a housetop, than in a house shared with a contentious woman.
20_25_25: As cold water to a weary soul, so is good news from a far country.
20_25_26: A righteous man who falters before the wicked is like a murky spring and a polluted well.
20_25_27: It is not good to eat much honey; so to seek one’s own glory is not glory.
20_25_28: Whoever has no rule over his own spirit is like a city broken down, without walls.
20_26_1: As snow in summer and rain in harvest, so honor is not fitting for a fool.
20_26_2: Like a flitting sparrow, like a flying swallow, so a curse without cause shall not alight.
20_26_3: A whip for the horse, a bridle for the donkey, and a rod for the fool’s back.
20_26_4: Do not answer a fool according to his folly, lest you also be like him.
20_26_5: Answer a fool according to his folly, lest he be wise in his own eyes.
20_26_6: He who sends a message by the hand of a fool cuts off his own feet and drinks violence.
20_26_7: Like the legs of the lame that hang limp is a proverb in the mouth of fools.
20_26_8: Like one who binds a stone in a sling is he who gives honor to a fool.
20_26_9: Like a thorn that goes into the hand of a drunkard is a proverb in the mouth of fools.
20_26_10: The great God who formed everything gives the fool his hire and the transgressor his wages.
20_26_11: As a dog returns to his own vomit, so a fool repeats his folly.
20_26_12: Do you see a man wise in his own eyes? There is more hope for a fool than for him.
20_26_13: The lazy man says, “There is a lion in the road! A fierce lion is in the streets!”
20_26_14: As a door turns on its hinges, so does the lazy man on his bed.
20_26_15: The lazy man buries his hand in the bowl; it wearies him to bring it back to his mouth.
20_26_16: The lazy man is wiser in his own eyes than seven men who can answer sensibly.
20_26_17: He who passes by and meddles in a quarrel not his own is like one who takes a dog by the ears.
20_26_18: Like a madman who throws firebrands, arrows, and death,
20_26_19: is the man who deceives his neighbor, and says, “I was only joking!”
20_26_20: Without wood, the fire goes out; and where there is no whisperer, strife ceases.
20_26_21: As charcoal to hot embers and wood to fire, so is a quarrelsome man to kindle strife.
20_26_22: The words of a whisperer are like delicious morsels, and they go down into the innermost parts of the body.
20_26_23: Fervent lips with a wicked heart are like earthenware covered with silver dross.
20_26_24: He who hates disguises it with his lips and harbors deceit within himself.
20_26_25: When he speaks kindly, do not believe him, for there are seven abominations in his heart.
20_26_26: Though his hatred is covered by deceit, his wickedness will be revealed before the assembly.
20_26_27: Whoever digs a pit will fall into it, and he who rolls a stone will have it roll back on him.
20_26_28: A lying tongue hates those it crushes, and a flattering mouth works ruin.
20_27_1: Do not boast about tomorrow, for you do not know what a day may bring forth.
20_27_2: Let another man praise you, and not your own mouth; a stranger, and not your own lips.
20_27_3: A stone is heavy and sand is weighty, but a fool’s wrath is heavier than both of them.
20_27_4: Wrath is cruel and anger a torrent, but who can stand before jealousy?
20_27_5: Open rebuke is better than love carefully concealed.
20_27_6: Faithful are the wounds of a friend, but the kisses of an enemy are deceitful.
20_27_7: A satisfied soul loathes the honeycomb, but to a hungry soul every bitter thing is sweet.
20_27_8: Like a bird that wanders from its nest is a man who wanders from his place.
20_27_9: Ointment and perfume delight the heart, and the sweetness of a man’s friend gives delight by hearty counsel.
20_27_10: Do not forsake your own friend or your father’s friend, nor go to your brother’s house in the day of your calamity; better is a neighbor nearby than a brother far away.
20_27_11: My son, be wise, and make my heart glad, that I may answer him who reproaches me.
20_27_12: A prudent man foresees evil and hides himself; the simple pass on and are punished.
20_27_13: Take the garment of him who is surety for a stranger, and hold it in pledge when he is surety for a seductress.
20_27_14: He who blesses his friend with a loud voice, rising early in the morning, it will be counted a curse to him.
20_27_15: A continual dripping on a very rainy day and a contentious woman are alike.
20_27_16: Whoever restrains her restrains the wind and grasps oil with his right hand.
20_27_17: As iron sharpens iron, so a man sharpens the countenance of his friend.
20_27_18: Whoever keeps the fig tree will eat its fruit; so he who waits on his master will be honored.
20_27_19: As in water face reflects face, so a man’s heart reveals the man.
20_27_20: Hell and Destruction are never full; so the eyes of man are never satisfied.
20_27_21: The refining pot is for silver and the furnace for gold, and a man is valued by what others say of him.
20_27_22: Though you grind a fool in a mortar with a pestle along with crushed grain, yet his foolishness will not depart from him.
20_27_23: Be diligent to know the state of your flocks, and attend to your herds;
20_27_24: For riches are not forever, nor does a crown endure to all generations.
20_27_25: When the hay is removed, and the tender grass shows itself, and the herbs of the mountains are gathered in,
20_27_26: The lambs will provide your clothing, and the goats the price of a field;
20_27_27: You shall have enough goats’ milk for your food, for the food of your household, and the nourishment of your maidservants.
20_28_1: The wicked flee when no one pursues, but the righteous are bold as a lion.
20_28_2: Because of the transgression of a land, many are its princes; but by a man of understanding and knowledge right will be prolonged.
20_28_3: A poor man who oppresses the poor is like a driving rain which leaves no food.
20_28_4: Those who forsake the law praise the wicked, but such as keep the law contend with them.
20_28_5: Evil men do not understand justice, but those who seek the LORD understand it completely.
20_28_6: Better is the poor who walks in his integrity than one perverse in his ways, though he be rich.
20_28_7: Whoever keeps the law is a discerning son, but a companion of gluttons shames his father.
20_28_8: One who increases his possessions by usury and extortion gathers it for him who will pity the poor.
20_28_9: One who turns away his ear from hearing the law, even his prayer is an abomination.
20_28_10: Whoever causes the upright to go astray in an evil way, he himself will fall into his own pit; but the blameless will inherit good.
20_28_11: The rich man is wise in his own eyes, but the poor who has understanding searches him out.
20_28_12: When the righteous rejoice, there is great glory; but when the wicked arise, men hide themselves.
20_28_13: He who covers his sins will not prosper, but whoever confesses and forsakes them will have mercy.
20_28_14: Happy is the man who is always reverent, but he who hardens his heart will fall into calamity.
20_28_15: Like a roaring lion and a charging bear is a wicked ruler over poor people.
20_28_16: A ruler who lacks understanding is a great oppressor, but he who hates covetousness will prolong his days.
20_28_17: A man burdened with bloodshed will flee into a pit; let no one help him.
20_28_18: Whoever walks blamelessly will be saved, but he who is perverse in his ways will suddenly fall.
20_28_19: He who tills his land will have plenty of bread, but he who follows frivolity will have poverty enough!
20_28_20: A faithful man will abound with blessings, but he who hastens to be rich will not go unpunished.
20_28_21: To show partiality is not good, because for a piece of bread a man will transgress.
20_28_22: A man with an evil eye hastens after riches, and does not consider that poverty will come upon him.
20_28_23: He who rebukes a man will find more favor afterward than he who flatters with the tongue.
20_28_24: Whoever robs his father or his mother, and says, “It is no transgression,” the same is companion to a destroyer.
20_28_25: He who is of a proud heart stirs up strife, but he who trusts in the LORD will be prospered.
20_28_26: He who trusts in his own heart is a fool, but whoever walks wisely will be delivered.
20_28_27: He who gives to the poor will not lack, but he who hides his eyes will have many curses.
20_28_28: When the wicked arise, men hide themselves; but when they perish, the righteous increase.
20_29_1: He who is often rebuked, and hardens his neck, will suddenly be destroyed, and that without remedy.
20_29_2: When the righteous are in authority, the people rejoice; but when a wicked man rules, the people groan.
20_29_3: Whoever loves wisdom makes his father rejoice, but a companion of harlots wastes his wealth.
20_29_4: The king establishes the land by justice, but he who receives bribes overthrows it.
20_29_5: A man who flatters his neighbor spreads a net for his feet.
20_29_6: By transgression an evil man is snared, but the righteous sings and rejoices.
20_29_7: The righteous considers the cause of the poor, but the wicked does not understand such knowledge.
20_29_8: Scoffers set a city aflame, but wise men turn away wrath.
20_29_9: If a wise man contends with a foolish man, whether the fool rages or laughs, there is no peace.
20_29_10: The bloodthirsty hate the blameless, but the upright seek his well-being.
20_29_11: A fool vents all his feelings, but a wise man holds them back.
20_29_12: If a ruler pays attention to lies, all his servants become wicked.
20_29_13: The poor man and the oppressor have this in common: the LORD gives light to the eyes of both.
20_29_14: The king who judges the poor with truth, his throne will be established forever.
20_29_15: The rod and rebuke give wisdom, but a child left to himself brings shame to his mother.
20_29_16: When the wicked are multiplied, transgression increases; but the righteous will see their fall.
20_29_17: Discipline your son, and he will give you rest; yes, he will give delight to your soul.
20_29_18: Where there is no prophetic vision, the people cast off restraint; but happy is he who keeps the law.
20_29_19: A servant will not be corrected by mere words; for though he understands, he will not respond.
20_29_20: Do you see a man hasty in his words? There is more hope for a fool than for him.
20_29_21: He who pampers his servant from childhood will have him as a son in the end.
20_29_22: An angry man stirs up strife, and a hot-tempered man abounds in transgression.
20_29_23: A man's pride will bring him low, but the humble in spirit will retain honor.
20_29_24: Whoever is a partner with a thief hates his own life; he hears the curse but reveals nothing.
20_29_25: The fear of man brings a snare, but whoever trusts in the LORD shall be safe.
20_29_26: Many seek the ruler’s favor, but justice for man comes from the LORD.
20_29_27: An unjust man is an abomination to the righteous, and he who is upright in the way is an abomination to the wicked.
20_30_1: The words of Agur the son of Jakeh, his utterance. This man declared to Ithiel—to Ithiel and Ucal:
20_30_2: Surely I am more stupid than any man, and do not have the understanding of a man.
20_30_3: I neither learned wisdom nor have knowledge of the Holy One.
20_30_4: Who has ascended into heaven, or descended? Who has gathered the wind in His fists? Who has bound the waters in a garment? Who has established all the ends of the earth? What is His name, and what is His Son’s name, if you know?
20_30_5: Every word of God is pure; He is a shield to those who put their trust in Him.
20_30_6: Do not add to His words, lest He rebuke you, and you be found a liar.
20_30_7: Two things I request of You (Deprive me not before I die):
20_30_8: Remove falsehood and lies far from me; give me neither poverty nor riches—feed me with the food allotted to me;
20_30_9: Lest I be full and deny You, and say, “Who is the LORD?” Or lest I be poor and steal, and profane the name of my God.
20_30_10: Do not malign a servant to his master, lest he curse you, and you be found guilty.
20_30_11: There is a generation that curses its father, and does not bless its mother.
20_30_12: There is a generation that is pure in its own eyes, yet is not washed from its filthiness.
20_30_13: There is a generation—oh, how lofty are their eyes! And their eyelids are lifted up.
20_30_14: There is a generation whose teeth are like swords, and whose fangs are like knives, to devour the poor from off the earth, and the needy from among men.
20_30_15: The leech has two daughters—Give and Give! There are three things that are never satisfied, four never say, “Enough!”:
20_30_16: The grave, the barren womb, the earth that is not satisfied with water—and the fire never says, “Enough!”
20_30_17: The eye that mocks his father, and scorns obedience to his mother, the ravens of the valley will pick it out, and the young eagles will eat it.
20_30_18: There are three things which are too wonderful for me, yes, four which I do not understand:
20_30_19: The way of an eagle in the air, the way of a serpent on a rock, the way of a ship in the midst of the sea, and the way of a man with a virgin.
20_30_20: This is the way of an adulterous woman: She eats and wipes her mouth, and says, “I have done no wickedness.”
20_30_21: For three things the earth is perturbed, yes, for four it cannot bear up:
20_30_22: For a servant when he reigns, a fool when he is filled with food,
20_30_23: A hateful woman when she is married, and a maidservant who succeeds her mistress.
20_30_24: There are four things which are little on the earth, but they are exceedingly wise:
20_30_25: The ants are a people not strong, yet they prepare their food in the summer;
20_30_26: The rock badgers are a feeble folk, yet they make their homes in the crags;
20_30_27: The locusts have no king, yet they all advance in ranks;
20_30_28: The spider skillfully grasps with its hands, and it is in kings’ palaces.
20_30_29: There are three things which are majestic in pace, yes, four which are stately in walk:
20_30_30: A lion, which is mighty among beasts and does not turn away from any.
20_30_31: A strutting rooster, a male goat also, and a king whose troops are with him.
20_30_32: If you have been foolish in exalting yourself, or if you have devised evil, put your hand on your mouth.
20_30_33: For as the churning of milk produces butter, and wringing the nose produces blood, so the forcing of wrath produces strife.
20_31_1: The words of King Lemuel, the utterance which his mother taught him.
20_31_2: What, my son? And what, son of my womb? And what, son of my vows?
20_31_3: Do not give your strength to women, nor your ways to that which destroys kings.
20_31_4: It is not for kings, O Lemuel, it is not for kings to drink wine, nor for princes intoxicating drink.
20_31_5: Lest they drink and forget the law, and pervert the justice of all the afflicted.
20_31_6: Give strong drink to him who is perishing, and wine to those who are bitter of heart.
20_31_7: Let him drink and forget his poverty, and remember his misery no more.
20_31_8: Open your mouth for the speechless, in the cause of all who are appointed to die.
20_31_9: Open your mouth, judge righteously, and plead the cause of the poor and needy.
20_31_10: Who can find a virtuous wife? For her worth is far above rubies.
20_31_11: The heart of her husband safely trusts her; so he will have no lack of gain.
20_31_12: She does him good and not evil all the days of her life.
20_31_13: She seeks wool and flax, and willingly works with her hands.
20_31_14: She is like the merchant ships, she brings her food from afar.
20_31_15: She also rises while it is yet night, and provides food for her household, and a portion for her maidservants.
20_31_16: She considers a field and buys it; from her profits she plants a vineyard.
20_31_17: She girds herself with strength, and strengthens her arms.
20_31_18: She perceives that her merchandise is good, and her lamp does not go out by night.
20_31_19: She stretches out her hands to the distaff, and her hand holds the spindle.
20_31_20: She extends her hand to the poor, yes, she reaches out her hands to the needy.
20_31_21: She is not afraid of snow for her household, for all her household is clothed with scarlet.
20_31_22: She makes tapestry for herself; her clothing is fine linen and purple.
20_31_23: Her husband is known in the gates, when he sits among the elders of the land.
20_31_24: She makes linen garments and sells them, and supplies sashes for the merchants.
20_31_25: Strength and honor are her clothing; she shall rejoice in time to come.
20_31_26: She opens her mouth with wisdom, and on her tongue is the law of kindness.
20_31_27: She watches over the ways of her household, and does not eat the bread of idleness.
20_31_28: Her children rise up and call her blessed; her husband also, and he praises her:
20_31_29: “Many daughters have done well, but you excel them all.”
20_31_30: Charm is deceitful and beauty is passing, but a woman who fears the LORD, she shall be praised.
20_31_31: Give her of the fruit of her hands, and let her own works praise her in the gates.
8_1_1: In the days when the judges ruled in Israel, there was a famine in the land. A certain man of Bethlehem in Judah went to live in the country of Moab, he and his wife and his two sons.
8_1_2: The man's name was Elimelech, his wife's name was Naomi, and the names of his two sons were Mahlon and Chilion — Ephrathites of Bethlehem in Judah. They entered the country of Moab and settled there.
8_1_3: Then Elimelech, Naomi's husband, died; and she was left with her two sons.
8_1_4: Her sons took Moabite women as their wives; the name of one was Orpah and the name of the other Ruth. And they lived there about ten years.
8_1_5: Then both Mahlon and Chilion also died, and Naomi was left without her two sons and her husband.
8_1_6: Then she arose with her daughters-in-law to return from the country of Moab, for she had heard in Moab that the LORD had visited his people and given them food.
8_1_7: So she set out from the place where she was, and her two daughters-in-law with her; and they went on the way to return to the land of Judah.
8_1_8: And Naomi said to her two daughters-in-law, "Go, return each to her mother's house. May the LORD show kindness to you, as you have shown to the dead and to me.
8_1_9: May the LORD grant that you each find rest in the house of a husband." So she kissed them, and they wept loudly.
8_1_10: And they said to her, "No, we insist on returning with you to your people."
8_1_11: But Naomi said, "Return home, my daughters; why will you go with me? Am I able to have any more sons in my womb who could become your husbands?
8_1_12: Return, my daughters; go your way, for I am too old to have a husband. Even if I thought there was still hope, and I were to have a husband tonight and also bear sons,
8_1_13: would you wait for them until they were grown? Would you restrain yourselves from marrying? No, my daughters; for it is far more bitter for me than for you, because the LORD's hand has gone out against me!"
8_1_14: Then they wept aloud again; and Orpah kissed her mother-in-law, but Ruth clung tightly to her.
8_1_15: And Naomi said, "Look, your sister-in-law has gone back to her people and to her gods; follow after your sister-in-law."
8_1_16: But Ruth said, "Do not plead with me to leave you or to turn back from following you; for wherever you go, I will go, and wherever you live, I will live. Your people shall be my people, and your God, my God.
8_1_17: Where you die, I will die, and there I will be buried. May the LORD punish me severely if anything but death parts you and me."
8_1_18: When Naomi saw that Ruth was determined to go with her, she said no more to her.
8_1_19: So the two of them went on until they came to Bethlehem. And when they came to Bethlehem, the whole town was stirred because of them; and the women said, "Can this be Naomi?"
8_1_20: But she said to them, "Do not call me Naomi; call me Mara, for the Almighty has made life very bitter for me.
8_1_21: I went away full, and the LORD has brought me back empty. Why do you call me Naomi, since the LORD has testified against me and the Almighty has afflicted me?"
8_1_22: So Naomi returned, and with her Ruth the Moabitess, her daughter-in-law, who returned from the country of Moab. And they came to Bethlehem at the beginning of barley harvest.
8_2_1: Now Naomi had a relative on her husband's side — a prominent man of noble character from the family of Elimelech. His name was Boaz.
8_2_2: And Ruth the Moabitess said to Naomi, "Please let me go to the field and glean among the heads of grain after him in whose sight I may find favor." And she said to her, "Go, my daughter."
8_2_3: So she left and went and gleaned in the field after the reapers. And as it happened, she found herself in the portion of the field belonging to Boaz, who was of the family of Elimelech.
8_2_4: Just then Boaz arrived from Bethlehem and greeted the harvesters, "The LORD be with you!" And they answered him, "The LORD bless you!"
8_2_5: Then Boaz said to his servant who was in charge of the harvesters, "Whose young woman is this?"
8_2_6: The servant in charge of the harvesters answered, "She is the young Moabite woman who came back with Naomi from the country of Moab.
8_2_7: She asked, 'Please let me glean and gather after the reapers among the sheaves.' So she came and has been on her feet from early morning until now, except that she rested a little in the shelter."
8_2_8: Then Boaz said to Ruth, "Listen carefully, my daughter. Do not go to glean in another field, nor go from here, but stay close by my young women.
8_2_9: Keep your eyes on the field they are reaping, and follow after them. I have ordered the young men not to touch you. And when you are thirsty, go to the water jars and drink from what the young men have drawn."
8_2_10: So she fell on her face, bowing to the ground, and said to him, "Why have I found favor in your eyes, that you should take notice of me, since I am a foreigner?"
8_2_11: Boaz answered her, "Everything you have done for your mother-in-law since the death of your husband has been fully reported to me — how you left your father and mother and your native land and came to a people you did not previously know.
8_2_12: May the LORD repay you for what you have done, and may you receive a full reward from the LORD, the God of Israel, under whose wings you have come to take refuge."
8_2_13: Then she said, "I have found favor in your sight, my lord; for you have comforted me and spoken kindly to your servant, though I am not like one of your servants."
8_2_14: At mealtime Boaz said to her, "Come here, eat some bread and dip your piece in the vinegar." So she sat beside the harvesters, and he served her roasted grain; and she ate and was satisfied, and had some left over.
8_2_15: When she rose to glean, Boaz commanded his young men, saying, "Let her glean even among the sheaves, and do not humiliate her.
8_2_16: And pull out some stalks from the bundles for her and leave them purposely for her to glean, and do not rebuke her."
8_2_17: So she gleaned in the field until evening. Then she beat out what she had gleaned, and it was about an ephah of barley.
8_2_18: Then she took it up and went into the city, and her mother-in-law saw what she had gleaned. She also brought out and gave to her what she had left over after being satisfied.
8_2_19: And her mother-in-law said to her, "Where did you glean today? And where did you work? Blessed be the man who took notice of you." So she told her mother-in-law with whom she had worked, and said, "The man's name with whom I worked today is Boaz."
8_2_20: Then Naomi said to her daughter-in-law, "May he be blessed by the LORD, who has not abandoned his kindness to the living or the dead!" Naomi also said to her, "This man is one of our close relatives — one of our family redeemers."
8_2_21: Ruth the Moabitess said, "He also told me, 'Stay close to my servants until they have finished all my harvest.'"
8_2_22: And Naomi said to Ruth her daughter-in-law, "It is good, my daughter, that you go out with his young women, so that you will not be harassed in another field."
8_2_23: So she stayed close to the young women of Boaz, gleaning until the end of the barley harvest and the wheat harvest; and she lived with her mother-in-law.
8_3_1: Then Naomi her mother-in-law said to her, "My daughter, should I not seek rest and security for you, so that it may be well with you?
8_3_2: Now is not Boaz our relative, with whose young women you were? Tonight he will be winnowing barley at the threshing floor.
8_3_3: Therefore wash yourself and anoint yourself with perfumed oil, put on your best garment and go down to the threshing floor; but do not make yourself known to the man until he has finished eating and drinking.
8_3_4: When he lies down, notice the place where he lies; then go and uncover his feet and lie down; and he will tell you what you should do."
8_3_5: And she said to her, "I will do everything you say."
8_3_6: So she went down to the threshing floor and did according to all that her mother-in-law had instructed her.
8_3_7: After Boaz had eaten and drunk, and his heart was merry, he went to lie down at the end of the heap of grain. Then she came quietly, uncovered his feet, and lay down.
8_3_8: At midnight the man was startled and turned over; and behold, a woman was lying at his feet!
8_3_9: He said, "Who are you?" She answered, "I am Ruth, your servant. Spread your wings over your servant, for you are a family redeemer."
8_3_10: Then he said, "May you be blessed by the LORD, my daughter! You have shown more kindness now than before, in that you did not go after younger men, whether poor or rich.
8_3_11: And now, my daughter, do not fear. I will do for you all that you ask, for all the people of my town know that you are a woman of noble character.
8_3_12: Now it is true that I am a family redeemer; however, there is a redeemer closer than I.
8_3_13: Stay this night, and in the morning, if he will redeem you, good — let him do it. But if he is not willing to redeem you, then as the LORD lives, I will redeem you myself. Lie down until morning."
8_3_14: So she lay at his feet until morning, but rose before one could recognize another. Then Boaz said, "Do not let it be known that a woman came to the threshing floor."
8_3_15: Also he said, "Bring the shawl that is on you and hold it out." So she held it out, and he measured six measures of barley and laid it on her. Then she went into the city.
8_3_16: When she came to her mother-in-law, Naomi asked, "What happened, my daughter?" Then Ruth told her everything the man had done for her.
8_3_17: She said, "He gave me these six measures of barley, for he said, 'Do not go back to your mother-in-law empty-handed.'"
8_3_18: Then Naomi said, "Wait, my daughter, until you find out how the matter will go; for the man will not rest until he has settled it today."
8_4_1: Now Boaz went up to the gate and sat down there; and just then the family redeemer of whom Boaz had spoken came by. So Boaz said, "Come aside, friend, sit down here." And he came over and sat down.
8_4_2: Then Boaz took ten men of the elders of the city and said, "Sit down here." So they sat down.
8_4_3: Then he said to the family redeemer, "Naomi, who has come back from the country of Moab, is selling the piece of land which belonged to our relative Elimelech.
8_4_4: And I thought to inform you, saying, 'Buy it back in the presence of those sitting here and the elders of my people. If you will redeem it, redeem it; but if you will not, tell me, that I may know; for there is no one besides you to redeem it, and I am next after you.'" And he said, "I will redeem it."
8_4_5: Then Boaz said, "On the day you buy the field from the hand of Naomi, you also acquire Ruth the Moabitess, the widow of the deceased, in order to perpetuate the name of the dead in his inheritance."
8_4_6: And the family redeemer said, "I cannot redeem it for myself, or I will jeopardize my own inheritance. Take my right of redemption yourself, for I cannot redeem it."
8_4_7: Now this was the custom in former times in Israel concerning redeeming and exchanging, to confirm any transaction: a man removed his sandal and gave it to the other party, and this was the manner of legally binding a matter in Israel.
8_4_8: Therefore the family redeemer said to Boaz, "Buy it for yourself." And he removed his sandal.
8_4_9: And Boaz said to the elders and all the people, "You are witnesses this day that I have bought from the hand of Naomi all that belonged to Elimelech and all that belonged to Chilion and Mahlon.
8_4_10: Moreover, Ruth the Moabitess, the widow of Mahlon, I have acquired as my wife, to perpetuate the name of the dead in his inheritance, so that the name of the dead may not be cut off from among his relatives or from the gate of his hometown. You are witnesses this day."
8_4_11: And all the people who were at the gate, and the elders, said, "We are witnesses. May the LORD make the woman who is coming into your house like Rachel and Leah, who together built the house of Israel; and may you be powerful in Ephrathah and your name well known in Bethlehem.
8_4_12: May your house be like the house of Perez, whom Tamar bore to Judah, because of the offspring which the LORD will give you by this young woman."
8_4_13: So Boaz took Ruth and she became his wife. And when he went in to her, the LORD enabled her to conceive, and she bore a son.
8_4_14: Then the women said to Naomi, "Blessed be the LORD, who has not left you this day without a family redeemer; and may his name become well known in Israel!
8_4_15: May he be to you a restorer of life and a sustainer of your old age; for your daughter-in-law, who loves you and is better to you than seven sons, has given birth to him."
8_4_16: Then Naomi took the child and laid him on her lap, and became his nurse.
8_4_17: And the neighbor women said, "A son has been born to Naomi!" And they named him Obed. He was the father of Jesse, the father of David.
8_4_18: Now these are the generations of Perez: Perez fathered Hezron,
8_4_19: Hezron fathered Ram, and Ram fathered Amminadab,
8_4_20: Amminadab fathered Nahshon, and Nahshon fathered Salmon,
8_4_21: Salmon fathered Boaz, and Boaz fathered Obed,
8_4_22: Obed fathered Jesse, and Jesse fathered David.
6_1_1: After the death of Moses the servant of the LORD, the LORD spoke to Joshua the son of Nun, Moses’ assistant, saying:
6_1_2: “Moses My servant is dead. Now therefore, arise, go over this Jordan, you and all this people, to the land which I am giving to them—the children of Israel.
6_1_3: Every place that the sole of your foot will tread upon I have given you, as I said to Moses.
6_1_4: From the wilderness and this Lebanon as far as the great river, the River Euphrates, all the land of the Hittites, and to the Great Sea toward the going down of the sun, shall be your territory.
6_1_5: No man shall be able to stand before you all the days of your life; as I was with Moses, so I will be with you. I will not leave you nor forsake you.
6_1_6: Be strong and of good courage, for to this people you shall divide as an inheritance the land which I swore to their fathers to give them.
6_1_7: Only be strong and very courageous, that you may observe to do according to all the law which Moses My servant commanded you; do not turn from it to the right hand or to the left, that you may prosper wherever you go.
6_1_8: This Book of the Law shall not depart from your mouth, but you shall meditate in it day and night, that you may observe to do according to all that is written in it. For then you will make your way prosperous, and then you will have good success.
6_1_9: Have I not commanded you? Be strong and of good courage; do not be afraid, nor be dismayed, for the LORD your God is with you wherever you go.”
6_1_10: Then Joshua commanded the officers of the people, saying,
6_1_11: “Pass through the camp and command the people, saying, ‘Prepare provisions for yourselves, for within three days you will cross over this Jordan, to go in to possess the land which the LORD your God is giving you to possess.’ ”
6_1_12: And to the Reubenites, the Gadites, and half the tribe of Manasseh Joshua spoke, saying,
6_1_13: “Remember the word which Moses the servant of the LORD commanded you, saying, ‘The LORD your God is giving you rest and is giving you this land.’
6_1_14: Your wives, your little ones, and your livestock shall remain in the land which Moses gave you on this side of the Jordan. But you shall pass before your brethren armed, all your mighty men of valor, and help them,
6_1_15: Until the LORD has given your brethren rest, as He gave you, and they also have taken possession of the land which the LORD your God is giving them. Then you shall return to the land of your possession and enjoy it, which Moses the LORD’s servant gave you on this side of the Jordan toward the sunrise.”
6_1_16: So they answered Joshua, saying, “All that you command us we will do, and wherever you send us we will go.
6_1_17: Just as we heeded Moses in all things, so we will heed you. Only the LORD your God be with you, as He was with Moses.
6_1_18: Whoever rebels against your command and does not heed your words, in all that you command him, shall be put to death. Only be strong and of good courage.”
6_2_1: Now Joshua the son of Nun sent out two men from Acacia Grove to spy secretly, saying, “Go, view the land, especially Jericho.” So they went, and came to the house of a harlot named Rahab, and lodged there.
6_2_2: And it was told the king of Jericho, saying, “Behold, men have come here tonight from the children of Israel to search out the country.”
6_2_3: So the king of Jericho sent to Rahab, saying, “Bring out the men who have come to you, who have entered your house, for they have come to search out all the country.”
6_2_4: Then the woman took the two men and hid them. So she said, “Yes, the men came to me, but I did not know where they were from.
6_2_5: And it happened as the gate was being shut, when it was dark, that the men went out. Where the men went I do not know; pursue them quickly, for you may overtake them.”
6_2_6: (But she had brought them up to the roof and hidden them with the stalks of flax, which she had laid in order on the roof.)
6_2_7: Then the men pursued them by the road to the Jordan, to the fords. And as soon as those who pursued them had gone out, they shut the gate.
6_2_8: Now before they lay down, she came up to them on the roof,
6_2_9: And said to the men: “I know that the LORD has given you the land, that the terror of you has fallen on us, and that all the inhabitants of the land are fainthearted because of you.
6_2_10: For we have heard how the LORD dried up the water of the Red Sea for you when you came out of Egypt, and what you did to the two kings of the Amorites who were on the other side of the Jordan, Sihon and Og, whom you utterly destroyed.
6_2_11: And as soon as we heard these things, our hearts melted; neither did there remain any more courage in anyone because of you, for the LORD your God, He is God in heaven above and on earth beneath.
6_2_12: Now therefore, I beg you, swear to me by the LORD, since I have shown you kindness, that you also will show kindness to my father’s house, and give me a true token,
6_2_13: And spare my father, my mother, my brothers, my sisters, and all that they have, and deliver our lives from death.”
6_2_14: So the men answered her, “Our lives for yours, if none of you tell this business of ours. And it shall be, when the LORD has given us the land, that we will deal kindly and truly with you.”
6_2_15: Then she let them down by a rope through the window, for her house was on the city wall; she dwelt on the wall.
6_2_16: And she said to them, “Get to the mountain, lest the pursuers meet you. Hide there three days, until the pursuers have returned. Afterward you may go your way.”
6_2_17: So the men said to her: “We will be blameless of this oath of yours which you have made us swear,
6_2_18: Unless, when we come into the land, you bind this line of scarlet cord in the window through which you let us down, and unless you bring your father, your mother, your brothers, and all your father’s household to your own home.
6_2_19: So it shall be that whoever goes outside the doors of your house into the street, his blood shall be on his own head, and we will be guiltless. And whoever is with you in the house, his blood shall be on our head if a hand is laid on him.
6_2_20: And if you tell this business of ours, then we will be free from your oath which you made us swear.”
6_2_21: Then she said, “According to your words, so be it.” And she sent them away, and they departed. And she bound the scarlet cord in the window.
6_2_22: They departed and went to the mountain, and stayed there three days until the pursuers returned. The pursuers sought them all along the way, but did not find them.
6_2_23: So the two men returned, descended from the mountain, and crossed over; and they came to Joshua the son of Nun, and told him all that had befallen them.
6_2_24: And they said to Joshua, “Truly the LORD has delivered all the land into our hands, for indeed all the inhabitants of the country are fainthearted because of us.”
6_3_1: Then Joshua rose early in the morning; and they set out from Acacia Grove and came to the Jordan, he and all the children of Israel, and lodged there before they crossed over.
6_3_2: After three days, that the officers went through the camp;
6_3_3: And they commanded the people, saying, “When you see the ark of the covenant of the LORD your God, and the priests, the Levites, bearing it, then you shall set out from your place and go after it.
6_3_4: Yet there shall be a space between you and it, about two thousand cubits by measure. Do not come near it, that you may know the way by which you must go, for you have not passed this way before.”
6_3_5: And Joshua said to the people, “Sanctify yourselves, for tomorrow the LORD will do wonders among you.”
6_3_6: Then Joshua spoke to the priests, saying, “Take up the ark of the covenant and cross over before the people.” So they took up the ark of the covenant and went before the people.
6_3_7: And the LORD said to Joshua, “This day I will begin to exalt you in the sight of all Israel, that they may know that, as I was with Moses, so I will be with you.
6_3_8: You shall command the priests who bear the ark of the covenant, saying, ‘When you have come to the edge of the water of the Jordan, you shall stand in the Jordan.’ ”
6_3_9: So Joshua said to the children of Israel, “Come here, and hear the words of the LORD your God.”
6_3_10: And Joshua said, “By this you shall know that the living God is among you, and that He will without fail drive out from before you the Canaanites and the Hittites and the Hivites and the Perizzites and the Girgashites and the Amorites and the Jebusites:
6_3_11: Behold, the ark of the covenant of the Lord of all the earth is crossing over before you into the Jordan.
6_3_12: Now therefore, take for yourselves twelve men from the tribes of Israel, one man from every tribe.
6_3_13: And it shall come to pass, as soon as the soles of the feet of the priests who bear the ark of the LORD, the Lord of all the earth, shall rest in the waters of the Jordan, that the waters of the Jordan shall be cut off, the waters that come down from upstream, and they shall stand as a heap.”
6_3_14: When the people set out from their camp to cross over the Jordan, with the priests bearing the ark of the covenant before the people,
6_3_15: And as those who bore the ark came to the Jordan, and the feet of the priests who bore the ark dipped in the edge of the water (for the Jordan overflows all its banks during the whole time of harvest),
6_3_16: That the waters which came down from upstream stood still, and rose in a heap very far away at Adam, the city that is beside Zaretan. So the waters that went down into the Sea of the Arabah, the Salt Sea, failed, and were cut off; and the people crossed over opposite Jericho.
6_3_17: Then the priests who bore the ark of the covenant of the LORD stood firm on dry ground in the midst of the Jordan; and all Israel crossed over on dry ground, until all the people had crossed completely over the Jordan.
6_4_1: When all the people had completely crossed over the Jordan, the LORD spoke to Joshua, saying:
6_4_2: “Take for yourselves twelve men from the people, one man from every tribe,
6_4_3: And command them, saying, ‘Take for yourselves twelve stones from here, out of the midst of the Jordan, from the place where the priests’ feet stood firm. You shall carry them over with you and leave them in the lodging place where you lodge tonight.’ ”
6_4_4: Then Joshua called the twelve men whom he had appointed from the children of Israel, one man from every tribe;
6_4_5: And Joshua said to them: “Cross over before the ark of the LORD your God into the midst of the Jordan, and each one of you take up a stone on his shoulder, according to the number of the tribes of the children of Israel,
6_4_6: That this may be a sign among you when your children ask in time to come, saying, ‘What do these stones mean to you?’
6_4_7: Then you shall answer them that the waters of the Jordan were cut off before the ark of the covenant of the LORD; when it crossed over the Jordan, the waters of the Jordan were cut off. And these stones shall be for a memorial to the children of Israel forever.”
6_4_8: And the children of Israel did so, just as Joshua commanded, and took up twelve stones from the midst of the Jordan, as the LORD had spoken to Joshua, according to the number of the tribes of the children of Israel, and carried them over with them to the place where they lodged, and laid them down there.
6_4_9: Then Joshua set up twelve stones in the midst of the Jordan, in the place where the feet of the priests who bore the ark of the covenant stood; and they are there to this day.
6_4_10: So the priests who bore the ark stood in the midst of the Jordan until everything was finished that the LORD had commanded Joshua to speak to the people, according to all that Moses had commanded Joshua; and the people hurried and crossed over.
6_4_11: When all the people had completely crossed over, the ark of the LORD and the priests crossed over in the presence of the people.
6_4_12: And the men of Reuben, the men of Gad, and half the tribe of Manasseh crossed over armed before the children of Israel, as Moses had spoken to them.
6_4_13: About forty thousand prepared for war crossed over before the LORD for battle, to the plains of Jericho.
6_4_14: On that day the LORD exalted Joshua in the sight of all Israel; and they feared him, as they had feared Moses, all the days of his life.
6_4_15: Then the LORD spoke to Joshua, saying,
6_4_16: “Command the priests who bear the ark of the Testimony to come up from the Jordan.”
6_4_17: Joshua therefore commanded the priests, saying, “Come up from the Jordan.”
6_4_18: When the priests who bore the ark of the covenant of the LORD had come from the midst of the Jordan, and the soles of the priests’ feet touched the dry land, the waters of the Jordan returned to their place and overflowed all its banks as before.
6_4_19: Now the people came up from the Jordan on the tenth day of the first month, and they camped in Gilgal on the east border of Jericho.
6_4_20: And those twelve stones which they took out of the Jordan, Joshua set up in Gilgal.
6_4_21: Then he spoke to the children of Israel, saying: “When your children ask their fathers in time to come, saying, ‘What are these stones?’
6_4_22: Then you shall let your children know, saying, ‘Israel crossed over this Jordan on dry land’;
6_4_23: For the LORD your God dried up the waters of the Jordan before you until you had crossed over, as the LORD your God did to the Red Sea, which He dried up before us until we had crossed over,
6_4_24: That all the peoples of the earth may know the hand of the LORD, that it is mighty, that you may fear the LORD your God forever.”
6_5_1: When all the kings of the Amorites who were on the west side of the Jordan, and all the kings of the Canaanites who were by the sea, heard that the LORD had dried up the waters of the Jordan from before the children of Israel until we had crossed over, their heart melted; and there was no spirit in them any longer because of the children of Israel.
6_5_2: At that time the LORD said to Joshua, “Make flint knives for yourself, and circumcise the sons of Israel again the second time.”
6_5_3: So Joshua made flint knives for himself, and circumcised the sons of Israel at the hill of the foreskins.
6_5_4: And this is the reason why Joshua circumcised them: All the people who came out of Egypt who were males, all the men of war, had died in the wilderness on the way, after they had come out of Egypt.
6_5_5: For all the people who came out had been circumcised, but all the people born in the wilderness, on the way as they came out of Egypt, had not been circumcised.
6_5_6: For the children of Israel walked forty years in the wilderness, till all the people who were men of war, who came out of Egypt, were consumed, because they did not obey the voice of the LORD—to whom the LORD swore that He would not show them the land which the LORD had sworn to their fathers that He would give us, “a land flowing with milk and honey.”
6_5_7: Then Joshua circumcised their sons whom He raised up in their place; for they were uncircumcised, because they had not been circumcised on the way.
6_5_8: When they had finished circumcising all the people, they stayed in their places in the camp till they were healed.
6_5_9: Then the LORD said to Joshua, “This day I have rolled away the reproach of Egypt from you.” Therefore the name of the place is called Gilgal to this day.
6_5_10: Now the children of Israel camped in Gilgal, and kept the Passover on the fourteenth day of the month at twilight on the plains of Jericho.
6_5_11: And they ate of the produce of the land on the day after the Passover, unleavened bread and parched grain, on the very same day.
6_5_12: Then the manna ceased on the day after they had eaten the produce of the land; and the children of Israel no longer had manna, but they ate the food of the land of Canaan that year.
6_5_13: When Joshua was by Jericho, he lifted his eyes and looked, and behold, a Man stood opposite him with His sword drawn in His hand. And Joshua went to Him and said to Him, “Are You for us or for our adversaries?”
6_5_14: So He said, “No, but as Commander of the army of the LORD I have now come.” And Joshua fell on his face to the earth and worshiped, and said to Him, “What does my Lord say to His servant?”
6_5_15: Then the Commander of the LORD’s army said to Joshua, “Take your sandal off your foot, for the place where you stand is holy.” And Joshua did so.
6_6_1: Now Jericho was securely shut up because of the children of Israel; none went out, and none came in.
6_6_2: And the LORD said to Joshua: “See! I have given Jericho into your hand, its king, and the mighty men of valor.
6_6_3: You shall march around the city, all you men of war; you shall go all around the city once. This you shall do six days.
6_6_4: And seven priests shall bear seven trumpets of rams’ horns before the ark. But the seventh day you shall march around the city seven times, and the priests shall blow the trumpets.
6_6_5: It shall come to pass, when they make a long blast with the ram’s horn, and when you hear the sound of the trumpet, that all the people shall shout with a great shout; then the wall of the city will fall down flat. And the people shall go up every man straight before him.”
6_6_6: Then Joshua the son of Nun called the priests and said to them, “Take up the ark of the covenant, and let seven priests bear seven trumpets of rams’ horns before the ark of the LORD.”
6_6_7: And he said to the people, “Proceed, and march around the city, and let him who is armed advance before the ark of the LORD.”
6_6_8: When Joshua had spoken to the people, the seven priests bearing the seven trumpets of rams’ horns before the LORD advanced and blew the trumpets, and the ark of the covenant of the LORD followed them.
6_6_9: The armed men went before the priests who blew the trumpets, and the rear guard came after the ark, while the priests continued blowing the trumpets.
6_6_10: Now Joshua had commanded the people, saying, “You shall not shout or make any noise with your voice, nor shall a word proceed out of your mouth, until the day I say to you, ‘Shout!’ Then you shall shout.”
6_6_11: So he had the ark of the LORD circle the city, going around it once. Then they came into the camp and lodged in the camp.
6_6_12: And Joshua rose early in the morning, and the priests took up the ark of the LORD.
6_6_13: Then seven priests bearing seven trumpets of rams’ horns before the ark of the LORD went on continually and blew with the trumpets. And the armed men went before them. But the rear guard came after the ark of the LORD, while the priests continued blowing the trumpets.
6_6_14: And the second day they marched around the city once and returned to the camp. So they did six days.
6_6_15: But On the seventh day that they rose early, about the dawning of the day, and marched around the city seven times in the same manner. On that day only they marched around the city seven times.
6_6_16: And the seventh time it happened, when the priests blew the trumpets, that Joshua said to the people: “Shout, for the LORD has given you the city!
6_6_17: Now the city shall be doomed by the LORD to destruction, it and all who are in it. Only Rahab the harlot shall live, she and all who are with her in the house, because she hid the messengers that we sent.
6_6_18: And you, by all means abstain from the accursed things, lest you become accursed when you take of the accursed things, and make the camp of Israel a curse, and trouble it.
6_6_19: But all the silver and gold, and vessels of bronze and iron, are consecrated to the LORD; they shall come into the treasury of the LORD.”
6_6_20: So the people shouted when the priests blew the trumpets. And it happened when the people heard the sound of the trumpet, and the people shouted with a great shout, that the wall fell down flat. Then the people went up into the city, every man straight before him, and they took the city.
6_6_21: And they utterly destroyed all that was in the city, both man and woman, young and old, ox and sheep and donkey, with the edge of the sword.
6_6_22: But Joshua had said to the two men who had spied out the country, “Go into the harlot’s house, and from there bring out the woman and all that she has, as you swore to her.”
6_6_23: And the young men who had been spies went in and brought out Rahab, her father, her mother, her brothers, and all that she had. So they brought out all her relatives and left them outside the camp of Israel.
6_6_24: But they burned the city and all that was in it with fire. Only the silver and gold, and the vessels of bronze and iron, they put into the treasury of the house of the LORD.
6_6_25: And Joshua spared Rahab the harlot, her father’s household, and all that she had. So she dwells in Israel to this day, because she hid the messengers whom Joshua sent to spy out Jericho.
6_6_26: Then Joshua charged them at that time, saying, “Cursed be the man before the LORD who rises up and builds this city Jericho; he shall lay its foundation with his firstborn, and with his youngest he shall set up its gates.”
6_6_27: So the LORD was with Joshua, and his fame spread throughout all the country.
6_7_1: But the children of Israel committed a trespass regarding the accursed things, for Achan the son of Carmi, the son of Zabdi, the son of Zerah, of the tribe of Judah, took of the accursed things; so the anger of the LORD burned against the children of Israel.
6_7_2: Now Joshua sent men from Jericho to Ai, which is beside Beth Aven, on the east side of Bethel, and spoke to them, saying, “Go up and spy out the country.” So the men went up and spied out Ai.
6_7_3: And they returned to Joshua and said to him, “Do not let all the people go up, but let about two or three thousand men go up and attack Ai. Do not weary all the people there, for the people of Ai are few.”
6_7_4: So about three thousand men went up there from the people, but they fled before the men of Ai.
6_7_5: And the men of Ai struck down about thirty-six men, for they chased them from before the gate as far as Shebarim, and struck them down on the descent; therefore the hearts of the people melted and became like water.
6_7_6: Then Joshua tore his clothes, and fell to the earth on his face before the ark of the LORD until evening, he and the elders of Israel; and they put dust on their heads.
6_7_7: And Joshua said, “Alas, Lord GOD, why have You brought this people over the Jordan at all—to deliver us into the hand of the Amorites, to destroy us? Oh, that we had been content, and dwelt on the other side of the Jordan!
6_7_8: O Lord, what shall I say when Israel turns its back before its enemies?
6_7_9: For the Canaanites and all the inhabitants of the land will hear it, and surround us, and cut off our name from the earth. Then what will You do for Your great name?”
6_7_10: So the LORD said to Joshua: “Get up! Why do you lie thus on your face?
6_7_11: Israel has sinned, and they have also transgressed My covenant which I commanded them. For they have even taken some of the accursed things, and have both stolen and deceived; and they have also put it among their own stuff.
6_7_12: Therefore the children of Israel could not stand before their enemies, but turned their backs before their enemies, because they have become doomed to destruction. Neither will I be with you anymore, unless you destroy the accursed from among you.
6_7_13: Get up, sanctify the people, and say, ‘Sanctify yourselves for tomorrow, because thus says the LORD God of Israel: “There is an accursed thing in your midst, O Israel; you cannot stand before your enemies until you take away the accursed thing from among you.”
6_7_14: In the morning therefore you shall be brought according to your tribes. And it shall be that the tribe which the LORD takes shall come according to families; and the family which the LORD takes shall come by households; and the household which the LORD takes shall come man by man.
6_7_15: Then it shall be that he who is taken with the accursed thing shall be burned with fire, he and all that he has, because he has transgressed the covenant of the LORD, and because he has done a disgraceful thing in Israel.’ ”
6_7_16: So Joshua rose early in the morning and brought Israel by their tribes, and the tribe of Judah was taken.
6_7_17: He brought the clan of Judah, and he took the family of the Zarhites; and he brought the family of the Zarhites man by man, and Zabdi was taken.
6_7_18: Then he brought his household man by man, and Achan the son of Carmi, the son of Zabdi, the son of Zerah, of the tribe of Judah, was taken.
6_7_19: Now Joshua said to Achan, “My son, I beg you, give glory to the LORD God of Israel, and make confession to Him, and tell me now what you have done; do not hide it from me.”
6_7_20: And Achan answered Joshua and said, “Indeed I have sinned against the LORD God of Israel, and this is what I have done:
6_7_21: When I saw among the spoils a beautiful Babylonian garment, two hundred shekels of silver, and a wedge of gold weighing fifty shekels, I coveted them and took them. And there they are, hidden in the earth in the midst of my tent, with the silver under it.”
6_7_22: So Joshua sent messengers, and they ran to the tent; and there it was, hidden in his tent, with the silver under it.
6_7_23: And they took them from the midst of the tent, brought them to Joshua and to all the children of Israel, and laid them out before the LORD.
6_7_24: Then Joshua, and all Israel with him, took Achan the son of Zerah, the silver, the garment, the wedge of gold, his sons, his daughters, his oxen, his donkeys, his sheep, his tent, and all that he had, and they brought them to the Valley of Achor.
6_7_25: And Joshua said, “Why have you troubled us? The LORD will trouble you this day.” So all Israel stoned him with stones; and they burned them with fire after they had stoned them with stones.
6_7_26: Then they raised over him a great heap of stones, still there to this day. So the LORD turned from the fierceness of His anger. Therefore the name of that place has been called the Valley of Achor to this day.
6_8_1: Now the LORD said to Joshua: “Do not be afraid, nor be dismayed; take all the people of war with you, and arise, go up to Ai. See, I have given into your hand the king of Ai, his people, his city, and his land.
6_8_2: And you shall do to Ai and its king as you did to Jericho and its king. Only its spoil and its cattle you shall take as booty for yourselves. Lay an ambush for the city behind it.”
6_8_3: So Joshua arose, and all the people of war, to go up against Ai; and Joshua chose thirty thousand mighty men of valor and sent them away by night.
6_8_4: And he commanded them, saying: “Behold, you shall lie in ambush against the city, behind the city. Do not go very far from the city, but all of you be ready.
6_8_5: Then I and all the people who are with me will approach the city; and it will come about, when they come out against us as at the first, that we shall flee before them.
6_8_6: For they will come out after us till we have drawn them from the city, for they will say, ‘They are fleeing before us as at the first.’ Therefore we will flee before them.
6_8_7: Then you shall rise from the ambush and seize the city, for the LORD your God will deliver it into your hand.
6_8_8: And it will be, when you have taken the city, that you shall set the city on fire. According to the commandment of the LORD you shall do. See, I have commanded you.”
6_8_9: Joshua therefore sent them out; and they went to lie in ambush, and stayed between Bethel and Ai, on the west side of Ai; but Joshua lodged that night among the people.
6_8_10: Then Joshua rose up early in the morning and mustered the people, and went up, he and the elders of Israel, before the people to Ai.
6_8_11: And all the people of war who were with him went up and drew near; and they came before the city and camped on the north side of Ai. Now a valley lay between them and Ai.
