12_18_35: Who among all the gods of the lands have delivered their countries from my hand, that the LORD should deliver Jerusalem from my hand?’ ”
12_18_36: But the people held their peace and answered him not a word; for the king’s commandment was, “Do not answer him.”
12_18_37: Then Eliakim the son of Hilkiah, who was over the household, Shebna the scribe, and Joah the son of Asaph, the recorder, came to Hezekiah with their clothes torn, and told him the words of the Rabshakeh.
12_19_1: And so it was, when King Hezekiah heard it, that he tore his clothes, covered himself with sackcloth, and went into the house of the LORD.
12_19_2: Then he sent Eliakim, who was over the household, Shebna the scribe, and the elders of the priests, covered with sackcloth, to Isaiah the prophet, the son of Amoz.
12_19_3: And they said to him, “Thus says Hezekiah: ‘This day is a day of trouble, and rebuke, and blasphemy; for the children have come to birth, but there is no strength to bring them forth.
12_19_4: It may be that the LORD your God will hear all the words of the Rabshakeh, whom his master the king of Assyria has sent to reproach the living God, and will rebuke the words which the LORD your God has heard. Therefore lift up your prayer for the remnant that is left.’ ”
12_19_5: So the servants of King Hezekiah came to Isaiah.
12_19_6: And Isaiah said to them, “Thus you shall say to your master, ‘Thus says the LORD: “Do not be afraid of the words which you have heard, with which the servants of the king of Assyria have blasphemed Me.
12_19_7: Surely I will send a spirit upon him, and he shall hear a rumor and return to his own land; and I will cause him to fall by the sword in his own land.” ’ ”
12_19_8: Then the Rabshakeh returned and found the king of Assyria warring against Libnah, for he heard that he had departed from Lachish.
12_19_9: And the king heard concerning Tirhakah king of Ethiopia, “Look, he has come out to make war with you.” So he again sent messengers to Hezekiah, saying,
12_19_10: “Thus you shall speak to Hezekiah king of Judah, saying: ‘Do not let your God in whom you trust deceive you, saying, “Jerusalem shall not be given into the hand of the king of Assyria.”
12_19_11: Look! You have heard what the kings of Assyria have done to all lands by utterly destroying them; and shall you be delivered?
12_19_12: Have the gods of the nations delivered those whom my fathers have destroyed, Gozan and Haran and Rezeph, and the people of Eden who were in Telassar?
12_19_13: Where is the king of Hamath, the king of Arpad, and the king of the city of Sepharvaim, Hena, and Ivah?’ ”
12_19_14: And Hezekiah received the letter from the hand of the messengers, and read it; and Hezekiah went up to the house of the LORD, and spread it before the LORD.
12_19_15: Then Hezekiah prayed before the LORD, and said: “O LORD God of Israel, the One who dwells between the cherubim, You are God, You alone, of all the kingdoms of the earth. You have made heaven and earth.
12_19_16: Incline Your ear, O LORD, and hear; open Your eyes, O LORD, and see; and hear the words of Sennacherib, which he has sent to reproach the living God.
12_19_17: Truly, LORD, the kings of Assyria have laid waste the nations and their lands,
12_19_18: and have cast their gods into the fire; for they were not gods, but the work of men’s hands—wood and stone. Therefore they destroyed them.
12_19_19: Now therefore, O LORD our God, I pray, save us from his hand, that all the kingdoms of the earth may know that You are the LORD God, You alone.”
12_19_20: Then Isaiah the son of Amoz sent to Hezekiah, saying, “Thus says the LORD God of Israel: ‘Because you have prayed to Me against Sennacherib king of Assyria, I have heard.’
12_19_21: This is the word which the LORD has spoken concerning him: ‘The virgin, the daughter of Zion, Has despised you, laughed you to scorn; The daughter of Jerusalem Has shaken her head behind your back!
12_19_22: ‘Whom have you reproached and blasphemed? Against whom have you raised your voice, And lifted up your eyes on high? Against the Holy One of Israel.
12_19_23: By your messengers you have reproached the Lord and said: “With the multitude of my chariots I have come up to the heights of the mountains, to the farthest recesses of Lebanon; I will cut down its tall cedars and its choice cypress trees; I will enter its farthest borders, to its fruitful forest.
12_19_24: I have dug and drunk strange water, and with the soles of my feet I have dried up all the brooks of defense.
12_19_25: Did you not hear long ago how I made it, from ancient times that I formed it? Now I have brought it to pass, that you should be for crushing fortified cities into heaps of ruins.
12_19_26: Therefore their inhabitants had little power; they were dismayed and confounded; they were as the grass of the field and the green herb, as the grass on the housetops and grain blighted before it is grown.
12_19_27: But I know your dwelling place, your going out and your coming in, and your rage against Me.
12_19_28: Because your rage against Me and your tumult have come up to My ears, therefore I will put My hook in your nose and My bridle in your lips, and I will turn you back by the way which you came.
12_19_29: This shall be a sign to you: You shall eat this year such as grows of itself, and in the second year what springs from the same; also in the third year sow and reap, plant vineyards and eat the fruit of them.
12_19_30: And the remnant who have escaped of the house of Judah shall again take root downward, and bear fruit upward.
12_19_31: For out of Jerusalem shall go a remnant, and those who escape from Mount Zion. The zeal of the LORD of hosts will do this.
12_19_32: Therefore thus says the LORD concerning the king of Assyria: ‘He shall not come into this city, nor shoot an arrow there, nor come before it with shield, nor build a siege mound against it.
12_19_33: By the way that he came, by the same shall he return; and he shall not come into this city,’ says the LORD.
12_19_34: For I will defend this city, to save it for My own sake and for My servant David’s sake.
12_19_35: And it came to pass on a certain night that the angel of the LORD went out, and killed in the camp of the Assyrians one hundred and eighty-five thousand; and when people arose early in the morning, there were the corpses—all dead.
12_19_36: So Sennacherib king of Assyria departed and went away, returned home, and remained at Nineveh.
12_19_37: Now it came to pass, as he was worshiping in the temple of Nisroch his god, that his sons Adrammelech and Sharezer struck him down with the sword; and they escaped into the land of Ararat. Then Esarhaddon his son reigned in his place.
12_20_1: In those days Hezekiah was sick and near death. And Isaiah the prophet, the son of Amoz, went to him and said to him, “Thus says the LORD: ‘Set your house in order, for you shall die, and not live.’”
12_20_2: Then he turned his face toward the wall, and prayed to the LORD, saying,
12_20_3: Remember now, O LORD, I pray, how I have walked before You in truth and with a loyal heart, and have done what was good in Your sight. And Hezekiah wept bitterly.
12_20_4: And it happened, before Isaiah had gone out into the middle court, that the word of the LORD came to him, saying,
12_20_5: Return and tell Hezekiah the leader of My people, ‘Thus says the LORD, the God of David your father: I have heard your prayer, I have seen your tears; surely I will heal you. On the third day you shall go up to the house of the LORD.
12_20_6: And I will add to your days fifteen years. I will deliver you and this city from the hand of the king of Assyria; and I will defend this city for My own sake, and for the sake of My servant David.
12_20_7: Then Isaiah said, “Take a lump of figs.” So they took and laid it on the boil, and he recovered.
12_20_8: And Hezekiah said to Isaiah, “What is the sign that the LORD will heal me, and that I shall go up to the house of the LORD the third day?”
12_20_9: Then Isaiah said, “This is the sign to you from the LORD, that the LORD will do the thing which He has spoken: shall the shadow go forward ten degrees or go backward ten degrees?”
12_20_10: And Hezekiah answered, “It is an easy thing for the shadow to go down ten degrees; no, but let the shadow go backward ten degrees.”
12_20_11: So Isaiah the prophet cried out to the LORD, and He brought the shadow ten degrees backward, by which it had gone down on the sundial of Ahaz.
12_20_12: At that time Berodach-Baladan the son of Baladan, king of Babylon, sent letters and a present to Hezekiah, for he heard that Hezekiah had been sick.
12_20_13: And Hezekiah was attentive to them, and showed them all the house of his treasures—the silver and gold, the spices and precious ointment, and all his armory—all that was found among his treasures. There was nothing in his house or in all his dominion that Hezekiah did not show them.
12_20_14: Then Isaiah the prophet went to King Hezekiah, and said to him, “What did these men say, and from where did they come to you?” So Hezekiah said, “They came from a far country, from Babylon.”
12_20_15: And he said, “What have they seen in your house?” So Hezekiah answered, “They have seen all that is in my house; there is nothing among my treasures that I have not shown them.”
12_20_16: Then Isaiah said to Hezekiah, “Hear the word of the LORD:
12_20_17: Behold, the days are coming when all that is in your house, and what your fathers have accumulated until this day, shall be carried to Babylon; nothing shall be left, says the LORD.
12_20_18: And they shall take away some of your sons who will descend from you, whom you will beget; and they shall be eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon.
12_20_19: So Hezekiah said to Isaiah, “The word of the LORD which you have spoken is good!” For he said, “Will there not be peace and truth at least in my days?”
12_20_20: Now the rest of the acts of Hezekiah—all his might, and how he made a pool and a tunnel and brought water into the city—are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?
12_20_21: So Hezekiah rested with his fathers. Then Manasseh his son reigned in his place.
12_21_1: Manasseh was twelve years old when he became king, and he reigned fifty-five years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Hephzibah.
12_21_2: And he did evil in the sight of the LORD, according to the abominations of the nations whom the LORD had cast out before the children of Israel.
12_21_3: For he rebuilt the high places which Hezekiah his father had destroyed; he raised up altars for Baal, and made a wooden image, as Ahab king of Israel had done; and he worshiped all the host of heaven and served them.
12_21_4: He also built altars in the house of the LORD, of which the LORD had said, “In Jerusalem I will put My name.”
12_21_5: And he built altars for all the host of heaven in the two courts of the house of the LORD.
12_21_6: Also he made his son pass through the fire, practiced soothsaying, used witchcraft, and consulted spiritists and mediums. He did much evil in the sight of the LORD, to provoke Him to anger.
12_21_7: He even set a carved image of Asherah that he had made, in the house of which the LORD had said to David and to Solomon his son, “In this house and in Jerusalem, which I have chosen out of all the tribes of Israel, I will put My name forever.
12_21_8: And I will not make the feet of Israel wander anymore from the land which I gave their fathers—only if they are careful to do according to all that I have commanded them, and according to all the law that My servant Moses commanded them.”
12_21_9: But they paid no attention, and Manasseh seduced them to do more evil than the nations whom the LORD had destroyed before the children of Israel.
12_21_10: And the LORD spoke by His servants the prophets, saying,
12_21_11: Because Manasseh king of Judah has done these abominations (he has acted more wickedly than all the Amorites who were before him, and has also made Judah sin with his idols),
12_21_12: therefore thus says the LORD God of Israel: ‘Behold, I am bringing such calamity upon Jerusalem and Judah, that whoever hears of it, both his ears will tingle.
12_21_13: And I will stretch over Jerusalem the measuring line of Samaria and the plummet of the house of Ahab; I will wipe Jerusalem as one wipes a dish, wiping it and turning it upside down.
12_21_14: So I will forsake the remnant of My inheritance and deliver them into the hand of their enemies; and they shall become victims of plunder to all their enemies,
12_21_15: because they have done evil in My sight and have provoked Me to anger since the day their fathers came out of Egypt, even to this day.
12_21_16: Moreover, Manasseh shed very much innocent blood, till he had filled Jerusalem from one end to another, besides his sin by which he made Judah sin, in doing evil in the sight of the LORD.
12_21_17: Now the rest of the acts of Manasseh—all that he did, and the sin that he committed—are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?
12_21_18: So Manasseh rested with his fathers and was buried in the garden of his own house, in the garden of Uzza. Then his son Amon reigned in his place.
12_21_19: Amon was twenty-two years old when he became king, and he reigned two years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Meshullemeth, the daughter of Haruz of Jotbah.
12_21_20: And he did evil in the sight of the LORD, as his father Manasseh had done.
12_21_21: So he walked in all the ways that his father had walked; and he served the idols that his father had served, and worshiped them.
12_21_22: He forsook the LORD God of his fathers and did not walk in the way of the LORD.
12_21_23: Then the servants of Amon conspired against him and killed the king in his own house.
12_21_24: But the people of the land executed all those who had conspired against King Amon. Then the people of the land made his son Josiah king in his place.
12_21_25: Now the rest of the acts of Amon which he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?
12_21_26: And he was buried in his tomb in the garden of Uzza. Then Josiah his son reigned in his place.
12_22_1: Josiah was eight years old when he became king, and he reigned thirty-one years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Jedidah, the daughter of Adaiah of Bozkath.
12_22_2: And he did what was right in the sight of the LORD, and walked in all the ways of his father David; he did not turn aside to the right hand or to the left.
12_22_3: Now it came to pass, in the eighteenth year of King Josiah, that the king sent Shaphan the scribe, the son of Azaliah, the son of Meshullam, to the house of the LORD, saying:
12_22_4: “Go up to Hilkiah the high priest, that he may count the money which has been brought into the house of the LORD, which the doorkeepers have gathered from the people.
12_22_5: And let them deliver it into the hand of those doing the work, who are the overseers in the house of the LORD; let them give it to those who are in the house of the LORD doing the work, to repair the damages of the house—
12_22_6: to carpenters and builders and masons—and to buy timber and hewn stone to repair the house.
12_22_7: However there need be no accounting made with them of the money delivered into their hand, because they deal faithfully.”
12_22_8: Then Hilkiah the high priest said to Shaphan the scribe, “I have found the Book of the Law in the house of the LORD.” And Hilkiah gave the book to Shaphan, and he read it.
12_22_9: So Shaphan the scribe went to the king, bringing the king word, saying, “Your servants have gathered the money that was found in the house, and have delivered it into the hand of those who do the work, who oversee the house of the LORD.”
12_22_10: Then Shaphan the scribe showed the king, saying, “Hilkiah the priest has given me a book.” And Shaphan read it before the king.
12_22_11: Now it happened, when the king heard the words of the Book of the Law, that he tore his clothes.
12_22_12: Then the king commanded Hilkiah the priest, Ahikam the son of Shaphan, Achbor the son of Michaiah, Shaphan the scribe, and Asaiah a servant of the king, saying,
12_22_13: “Go, inquire of the LORD for me, for the people and for all Judah, concerning the words of this book that has been found; for great is the wrath of the LORD that is aroused against us, because our fathers have not obeyed the words of this book, to do according to all that is written concerning us.”
12_22_14: So Hilkiah the priest, Ahikam, Achbor, Shaphan, and Asaiah went to Huldah the prophetess, the wife of Shallum the son of Tikvah, the son of Harhas, keeper of the wardrobe. (She dwelt in Jerusalem in the Second Quarter.) And they spoke with her.
12_22_15: Then she said to them, “Thus says the LORD God of Israel, ‘Tell the man who sent you to Me,
12_22_16: “Thus says the LORD: ‘Behold, I will bring calamity on this place and on its inhabitants—all the words of the book which the king of Judah has read—
12_22_17: because they have forsaken Me and burned incense to other gods, that they might provoke Me to anger with all the works of their hands. Therefore My wrath shall be aroused against this place and shall not be quenched.’ ” ’
12_22_18: But as for the king of Judah, who sent you to inquire of the LORD, in this manner you shall speak to him, ‘Thus says the LORD God of Israel: “Concerning the words which you have heard—
12_22_19: because your heart was tender, and you humbled yourself before the LORD when you heard what I spoke against this place and against its inhabitants, that they would become a desolation and a curse, and you tore your clothes and wept before Me, I also have heard you,” says the LORD.
12_22_20: “Surely, therefore, I will gather you to your fathers, and you shall be gathered to your grave in peace; and your eyes shall not see all the calamity which I will bring on this place.” ’ ” So they brought back word to the king.
12_23_1: Now the king sent them to gather all the elders of Judah and Jerusalem to him.
12_23_2: The king went up to the house of the LORD with all the men of Judah, and with him all the inhabitants of Jerusalem—the priests and the prophets and all the people, both small and great. And he read in their hearing all the words of the Book of the Covenant which had been found in the house of the LORD.
12_23_3: Then the king stood by a pillar and made a covenant before the LORD, to follow the LORD and to keep His commandments and His testimonies and His statutes, with all his heart and all his soul, to perform the words of this covenant that were written in this book. And all the people took a stand for the covenant.
12_23_4: And the king commanded Hilkiah the high priest, the priests of the second order, and the doorkeepers, to bring out of the temple of the LORD all the articles that were made for Baal, for Asherah, and for all the host of heaven; and he burned them outside Jerusalem in the fields of Kidron, and carried their ashes to Bethel.
12_23_5: Then he removed the idolatrous priests whom the kings of Judah had ordained to burn incense on the high places in the cities of Judah and in the places all around Jerusalem, and those who burned incense to Baal, to the sun, to the moon, to the constellations, and to all the host of heaven.
12_23_6: And he brought out the wooden image from the house of the LORD, to the Brook Kidron outside Jerusalem, burned it at the Brook Kidron and ground it to ashes, and threw its ashes on the graves of the common people.
12_23_7: Then he tore down the ritual booths of the perverted persons that were in the house of the LORD, where the women wove hangings for the wooden image.
12_23_8: And he brought all the priests from the cities of Judah, and defiled the high places where the priests had burned incense, from Geba to Beersheba; also he broke down the high places at the gates which were at the entrance of the Gate of Joshua the governor of the city, which were to the left of the city gate.
12_23_9: Nevertheless the priests of the high places did not come up to the altar of the LORD in Jerusalem, but they ate unleavened bread among their brethren.
12_23_10: And he defiled Topheth, which is in the Valley of the Son of Hinnom, that no man might make his son or his daughter pass through the fire to Molech.
12_23_11: Then he removed the horses that the kings of Judah had dedicated to the sun, at the entrance to the house of the LORD, by the chamber of Nathan-Melech, the officer who was in the court; and he burned the chariots of the sun with fire.
12_23_12: The altars that were on the roof, the upper chamber of Ahaz, which the kings of Judah had made, and the altars which Manasseh had made in the two courts of the house of the LORD, the king broke down and pulverized there, and threw their dust into the Brook Kidron.
12_23_13: Then the king defiled the high places that were east of Jerusalem, which were on the south of the Mount of Corruption, which Solomon king of Israel had built for Ashtoreth the abomination of the Sidonians, for Chemosh the abomination of the Moabites, and for Milcom the abomination of the people of Ammon.
12_23_14: And he broke in pieces the sacred pillars and cut down the wooden images, and filled their places with the bones of men.
12_23_15: Moreover the altar that was at Bethel, and the high place which Jeroboam the son of Nebat, who made Israel sin, had made, both that altar and the high place he broke down; and he burned the high place and crushed it to powder, and burned the wooden image.
12_23_16: As Josiah turned, he saw the tombs that were there on the mountain. And he sent and took the bones out of the tombs and burned them on the altar, and defiled it according to the word of the LORD which the man of God proclaimed, who proclaimed these words.
12_23_17: Then he said, “What gravestone is this that I see?” So the men of the city told him, “It is the tomb of the man of God who came from Judah and proclaimed these things which you have done against the altar of Bethel.”
12_23_18: And he said, “Let him alone; let no one move his bones.” So they let his bones alone, with the bones of the prophet who came from Samaria.
12_23_19: Now Josiah also took away all the shrines of the high places that were in the cities of Samaria, which the kings of Israel had made to provoke the LORD to anger; and he did to them according to all the deeds he had done in Bethel.
12_23_20: He executed all the priests of the high places who were there, on the altars, and burned human bones on them; and he returned to Jerusalem.
12_23_21: Then the king commanded all the people, saying, “Keep the Passover to the LORD your God, as it is written in this Book of the Covenant.”
12_23_22: Such a Passover surely had never been held since the days of the judges who judged Israel, nor in all the days of the kings of Israel and the kings of Judah.
12_23_23: But in the eighteenth year of King Josiah this Passover was held before the LORD in Jerusalem.
12_23_24: Moreover Josiah put away those who consulted mediums and spiritists, the household gods and idols, all the abominations that were seen in the land of Judah and in Jerusalem, that he might perform the words of the law which were written in the book that Hilkiah the priest found in the house of the LORD.
12_23_25: Now before him there was no king like him, who turned to the LORD with all his heart, with all his soul, and with all his might, according to all the Law of Moses; nor after him did any arise like him.
12_23_26: Nevertheless the LORD did not turn from the fierceness of His great wrath, with which His anger was aroused against Judah, because of all the provocations with which Manasseh had provoked Him.
12_23_27: And the LORD said, “I will also remove Judah from My sight, as I have removed Israel, and will cast off this city Jerusalem which I have chosen, and the house of which I said, ‘My name shall be there.’”
12_23_28: Now the rest of the acts of Josiah, and all that he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?
12_23_29: In his days Pharaoh Necho king of Egypt went to the aid of the king of Assyria, to the River Euphrates; and King Josiah went against him. And Pharaoh Necho killed him at Megiddo when he confronted him.
12_23_30: Then his servants moved his body in a chariot from Megiddo, brought him to Jerusalem, and buried him in his own tomb. And the people of the land took Jehoahaz the son of Josiah, anointed him, and made him king in his father’s place.
12_23_31: Jehoahaz was twenty-three years old when he became king, and he reigned three months in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Hamutal the daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah.
12_23_32: And he did evil in the sight of the LORD, according to all that his fathers had done.
12_23_33: Now Pharaoh Necho put him in prison at Riblah in the land of Hamath, that he might not reign in Jerusalem; and he imposed on the land a tribute of one hundred talents of silver and a talent of gold.
12_23_34: Then Pharaoh Necho made Eliakim the son of Josiah king in place of his father Josiah, and changed his name to Jehoiakim. And Pharaoh took Jehoahaz and went to Egypt, and he died there.
12_23_35: So Jehoiakim gave the silver and gold to Pharaoh; but he taxed the land to give money according to the command of Pharaoh; he exacted the silver and gold from the people of the land, from every one according to his assessment, to give it to Pharaoh Necho.
12_23_36: Jehoiakim was twenty-five years old when he became king, and he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Zebudah the daughter of Pedaiah of Rumah.
12_23_37: And he did evil in the sight of the LORD, according to all that his fathers had done.
12_24_1: In his days Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came up, and Jehoiakim became his vassal for three years. Then he turned and rebelled against him.
12_24_2: And the LORD sent against him raiding bands of Chaldeans, bands of Syrians, bands of Moabites, and bands of the people of Ammon; He sent them against Judah to destroy it, according to the word of the LORD which He had spoken by His servants the prophets.
12_24_3: Surely at the commandment of the LORD this came upon Judah, to remove them from His sight because of the sins of Manasseh, according to all that he had done,
12_24_4: and also because of the innocent blood that he had shed; for he had filled Jerusalem with innocent blood, which the LORD would not pardon.
12_24_5: Now the rest of the acts of Jehoiakim, and all that he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?
12_24_6: So Jehoiakim rested with his fathers. Then Jehoiachin his son reigned in his place.
12_24_7: And the king of Egypt did not come out of his land anymore, for the king of Babylon had taken all that belonged to the king of Egypt from the Brook of Egypt to the River Euphrates.
12_24_8: Jehoiachin was eighteen years old when he became king, and he reigned in Jerusalem three months. His mother’s name was Nehushta the daughter of Elnathan of Jerusalem.
12_24_9: And he did evil in the sight of the LORD, according to all that his father had done.
12_24_10: At that time the servants of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came up against Jerusalem, and the city was besieged.
12_24_11: And Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came against the city, as his servants were besieging it.
12_24_12: Then Jehoiachin king of Judah, his mother, his servants, his princes, and his officers went out to the king of Babylon; and the king of Babylon, in the eighth year of his reign, took him prisoner.
12_24_13: And he carried out from there all the treasures of the house of the LORD and the treasures of the king’s house, and he cut in pieces all the articles of gold which Solomon king of Israel had made in the temple of the LORD, as the LORD had said.
12_24_14: Also he carried into captivity all Jerusalem: all the captains and all the mighty men of valor, ten thousand captives, and all the craftsmen and smiths. None remained except the poorest people of the land.
12_24_15: And he carried Jehoiachin captive to Babylon. The king’s mother, the king’s wives, his officers, and the mighty of the land he carried into captivity from Jerusalem to Babylon.
12_24_16: All the valiant men, seven thousand, and craftsmen and smiths, one thousand, all who were strong and fit for war, these the king of Babylon brought captive to Babylon.
12_24_17: Then the king of Babylon made Mattaniah, Jehoiachin’s uncle, king in his place, and changed his name to Zedekiah.
12_24_18: Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king, and he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Hamutal the daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah.
12_24_19: He also did evil in the sight of the LORD, according to all that Jehoiakim had done.
12_24_20: For because of the anger of the LORD this happened in Jerusalem and Judah, that He finally cast them out from His presence. Then Zedekiah rebelled against the king of Babylon.
12_25_1: Now it came to pass in the ninth year of his reign, in the tenth month, on the tenth day of the month, that Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon and all his army came against Jerusalem and encamped against it; and they built a siege wall against it all around.
12_25_2: So the city was besieged until the eleventh year of King Zedekiah.
12_25_3: By the ninth day of the fourth month the famine had become so severe in the city that there was no food for the people of the land.
12_25_4: Then the city wall was broken through, and all the men of war fled at night by way of the gate between two walls, which was by the king’s garden, even though the Chaldeans were still encamped all around against the city. And the king went by way of the plain.
12_25_5: But the army of the Chaldeans pursued the king, and they overtook him in the plains of Jericho. All his army was scattered from him.
12_25_6: So they took the king and brought him up to the king of Babylon at Riblah, and they pronounced judgment on him.
12_25_7: Then they killed the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes, put out the eyes of Zedekiah, bound him with bronze fetters, and took him to Babylon.
12_25_8: And in the fifth month, on the seventh day of the month (which was the nineteenth year of King Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon), Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard, a servant of the king of Babylon, came to Jerusalem.
12_25_9: He burned the house of the LORD and the king’s house; all the houses of Jerusalem, that is, all the houses of the great, he burned with fire.
12_25_10: And all the army of the Chaldeans who were with the captain of the guard broke down the walls of Jerusalem all around.
12_25_11: Then Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard carried away captive the rest of the people who remained in the city and the defectors who had deserted to the king of Babylon, with the rest of the multitude.
12_25_12: But the captain of the guard left some of the poorest of the land to be vinedressers and farmers.
12_25_13: The bronze pillars that were in the house of the LORD, and the carts and the bronze Sea that were in the house of the LORD, the Chaldeans broke in pieces and carried their bronze to Babylon.
12_25_14: They also took away the pots, the shovels, the trimmers, the spoons, and all the bronze utensils used in the temple service.
12_25_15: The firepans and the basins, the things of solid gold and solid silver, the captain of the guard took away.
12_25_16: The two pillars, one Sea, and the carts, which Solomon had made for the house of the LORD, the bronze of all these articles was beyond measure.
12_25_17: The height of one pillar was eighteen cubits, and the capital on it was of bronze. The height of the capital was three cubits, and the network and pomegranates all around the capital were all of bronze. The second pillar was the same, with a network.
12_25_18: And the captain of the guard took Seraiah the chief priest, Zephaniah the second priest, and the three doorkeepers.
12_25_19: He also took out of the city an officer who had charge of the men of war, five men of the king’s close associates who were found in the city, the chief recruiting officer of the army, who mustered the people of the land, and sixty men of the people of the land who were found in the city.
12_25_20: So Nebuzaradan, captain of the guard, took these and brought them to the king of Babylon at Riblah.
12_25_21: Then the king of Babylon struck them and put them to death at Riblah in the land of Hamath. Thus Judah was carried away captive from its own land.
12_25_22: Then he made Gedaliah the son of Ahikam, the son of Shaphan, governor over the people who remained in the land of Judah, whom Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon had left.
12_25_23: Now when all the captains of the armies, they and their men, heard that the king of Babylon had made Gedaliah governor, they came to Gedaliah at Mizpah—Ishmael the son of Nethaniah, Johanan the son of Careah, Seraiah the son of Tanhumeth the Netophathite, and Jaazaniah the son of a Maachathite, they and their men.
12_25_24: And Gedaliah took an oath before them and their men, and said to them, “Do not be afraid of the servants of the Chaldeans. Dwell in the land and serve the king of Babylon, and it shall be well with you.”
12_25_25: But it happened in the seventh month that Ishmael the son of Nethaniah, the son of Elishama, of the royal family, came with ten men and struck and killed Gedaliah, the Jews, as well as the Chaldeans who were with him at Mizpah.
12_25_26: And all the people, small and great, and the captains of the armies, arose and went to Egypt; for they were afraid of the Chaldeans.
12_25_27: Now it came to pass in the thirty-seventh year of the captivity of Jehoiachin king of Judah, in the twelfth month, on the twenty-seventh day of the month, that Evil-Merodach king of Babylon, in the year that he began to reign, released Jehoiachin king of Judah from prison.
12_25_28: He spoke kindly to him, and gave him a more prominent seat than those of the kings who were with him in Babylon.
12_25_29: So Jehoiachin changed from his prison garments, and he ate bread regularly before the king all the days of his life.
12_25_30: And as for his provisions, there was a regular ration given him by the king, a portion for each day, all the days of his life.
13_1_4: Noah, Noah’s sons: Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
13_1_5: The sons of Japheth were Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras.
13_1_6: The sons of Gomer were Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah.
13_1_7: The sons of Javan were Elishah, Tarshish, Kittim, and Rodanim.
13_1_8: The sons of Ham were Cush, Mizraim, Put, and Canaan.
13_1_9: The sons of Cush were Seba, Havilah, Sabta, Raama, and Sabteca. The sons of Raama were Sheba and Dedan.
13_1_10: Cush begot Nimrod; he began to be a mighty one on the earth.
13_1_11: Mizraim begot Ludim, Anamim, Lehabim, Naphtuhim,
13_1_12: Pathrusim, Casluhim (from whom came the Philistines and the Caphtorim).
13_1_13: Canaan begot Sidon, his firstborn, and Heth,
13_1_14: the Jebusite, the Amorite, and the Girgashite,
13_1_15: the Hivite, the Arkite, and the Sinite,
13_1_16: the Arvadite, the Zemarite, and the Hamathite.
13_1_17: The sons of Shem were Elam, Asshur, Arpachshad, Lud, Aram, Uz, Hul, Gether, and Meshech.
13_1_18: Arpachshad begot Shelah, and Shelah begot Eber.
13_1_19: To Eber were born two sons: the name of one was Peleg, for in his days the earth was divided; and his brother’s name was Joktan.
13_1_20: Joktan begot Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah,
13_1_23: Ophir, Havilah, and Jobab. All these were the sons of Joktan.
13_1_28: The sons of Abraham were Isaac and Ishmael.
13_1_29: These are their genealogies: The firstborn of Ishmael was Nebaioth; then Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam,
13_1_31: Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah. These were the sons of Ishmael.
13_1_32: Now the sons born to Keturah, Abraham’s concubine, were Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah. The sons of Jokshan were Sheba and Dedan.
13_1_33: The sons of Midian were Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah. All these were the children of Keturah.
13_1_34: And Abraham begot Isaac. The sons of Isaac were Esau and Israel.
13_1_35: The sons of Esau were Eliphaz, Reuel, Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
13_1_36: And the sons of Eliphaz were Teman, Omar, Zephi, Gatam, and Kenaz; and by Timna, Amalek.
13_1_37: The sons of Reuel were Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah.
13_1_38: The sons of Seir were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah, Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan.
13_1_39: And the sons of Lotan were Hori and Homam; Lotan’s sister was Timna.
13_1_40: The sons of Shobal were Alian, Manahath, Ebal, Shephi, and Onam. The sons of Zibeon were Aiah and Anah.
13_1_41: The son of Anah was Dishon. The sons of Dishon were Hamran, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.
13_1_42: The sons of Ezer were Bilhan, Zaavan, and Jaakan. The sons of Dishan were Uz and Aran.
13_1_43: Now these were the kings who reigned in the land of Edom before a king reigned over the children of Israel: Bela the son of Beor, and the name of his city was Dinhabah.
13_1_44: And when Bela died, Jobab the son of Zerah of Bozrah reigned in his place.
13_1_45: When Jobab died, Husham of the land of the Temanites reigned in his place.
13_1_46: And when Husham died, Hadad the son of Bedad, who attacked Midian in the field of Moab, reigned in his place. The name of his city was Avith.
13_1_47: When Hadad died, Samlah of Masrekah reigned in his place.
13_1_48: And when Samlah died, Saul of Rehoboth-by-the-River reigned in his place.
13_1_49: When Saul died, Baal-Hanan the son of Achbor reigned in his place.
13_1_50: And when Baal-Hanan died, Hadad reigned in his place; and the name of his city was Pai. His wife’s name was Mehetabel the daughter of Matred, the daughter of Mezahab.
13_1_51: Hadad died also. And the chiefs of Edom were Chief Timnah, Chief Aliah, Chief Jetheth,
13_1_52: Chief Aholibamah, Chief Elah, Chief Pinon,
13_1_53: Chief Kenaz, Chief Teman, Chief Mibzar,
13_1_54: Chief Magdiel, and Chief Iram. These were the chiefs of Edom.
13_2_1: These were the sons of Israel: Reuben, Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, Zebulun,
13_2_2: Dan, Joseph, Benjamin, Naphtali, Gad, and Asher.
13_2_3: The sons of Judah were Er, Onan, and Shelah. These three were born to him by the daughter of Shua, the Canaanitess. Er, the firstborn of Judah, was wicked in the sight of the LORD; so He killed him.
13_2_4: And Tamar, his daughter-in-law, bore him Perez and Zerah. All the sons of Judah were five.
13_2_5: The sons of Perez were Hezron and Hamul.
13_2_6: The sons of Zerah were Zimri, Ethan, Heman, Calcol, and Dara—five of them in all.
13_2_7: The son of Carmi was Achar, the troubler of Israel, who transgressed in the accursed thing.
13_2_8: The son of Ethan was Azariah.
13_2_9: Also the sons of Hezron who were born to him were Jerahmeel, Ram, and Chelubai.
13_2_10: Ram begot Amminadab, and Amminadab begot Nahshon, leader of the children of Judah;
13_2_11: Nahshon begot Salma, and Salma begot Boaz;
13_2_12: Boaz begot Obed, and Obed begot Jesse;
13_2_13: Jesse begot Eliab his firstborn, Abinadab the second, Shimea the third,
13_2_14: Nethanel the fourth, Raddai the fifth,
13_2_15: Ozem the sixth, and David the seventh.
13_2_16: Now their sisters were Zeruiah and Abigail. And the sons of Zeruiah were Abishai, Joab, and Asahel—three.
13_2_17: Abigail bore Amasa; and the father of Amasa was Jether the Ishmaelite.
13_2_18: Caleb the son of Hezron had children by Azubah, his wife, and by Jerioth. Now these were her sons: Jesher, Shobab, and Ardon.
13_2_19: When Azubah died, Caleb took Ephrath as his wife, who bore him Hur.
13_2_20: And Hur begot Uri, and Uri begot Bezalel.
13_2_21: Now afterward Hezron went in to the daughter of Machir the father of Gilead, whom he married when he was sixty years old; and she bore him Segub.
13_2_22: Segub begot Jair, who had twenty-three cities in the land of Gilead.
13_2_23: Geshur and Syria took from them the towns of Jair, with Kenath and its towns—sixty towns. All these belonged to the sons of Machir the father of Gilead.
13_2_24: After Hezron died in Caleb Ephrathah, Hezron’s wife Abijah bore him Ashhur the father of Tekoa.
13_2_25: The sons of Jerahmeel, the firstborn of Hezron, were Ram, the firstborn, and Bunah, Oren, Ozem, and Ahijah.
13_2_26: Jerahmeel had another wife, whose name was Atarah; she was the mother of Onam.
13_2_27: The sons of Ram, the firstborn of Jerahmeel, were Maaz, Jamin, and Eker.
13_2_28: The sons of Onam were Shammai and Jada. The sons of Shammai were Nadab and Abishur.
13_2_29: And the name of the wife of Abishur was Abihail, and she bore him Ahban and Molid.
13_2_30: The sons of Nadab were Seled and Appaim; Seled died without children.
13_2_31: The son of Appaim was Ishi, the son of Ishi was Sheshan, and Sheshan’s son was Ahlai.
13_2_32: The sons of Jada, the brother of Shammai, were Jether and Jonathan; Jether died without children.
13_2_33: The sons of Jonathan were Peleth and Zaza. These were the sons of Jerahmeel.
13_2_34: Now Sheshan had no sons, only daughters. And Sheshan had an Egyptian servant whose name was Jarha.
13_2_35: Sheshan gave his daughter to Jarha his servant as wife, and she bore him Attai.
13_2_36: Attai begot Nathan, and Nathan begot Zabad;
13_2_37: Zabad begot Ephlal, and Ephlal begot Obed;
13_2_38: Obed begot Jehu, and Jehu begot Azariah;
13_2_39: Azariah begot Helez, and Helez begot Eleasah;
13_2_40: Eleasah begot Sismai, and Sismai begot Shallum;
13_2_41: Shallum begot Jekamiah, and Jekamiah begot Elishama.
13_2_42: The descendants of Caleb the brother of Jerahmeel were Mesha, his firstborn, who was the father of Ziph, and the sons of Mareshah the father of Hebron.
13_2_43: The sons of Hebron were Korah, Tappuah, Rekem, and Shema.
13_2_44: Shema begot Raham the father of Jorkoam, and Rekem begot Shammai.
13_2_45: And the son of Shammai was Maon, and Maon was the father of Beth Zur.
13_2_46: Ephah, Caleb’s concubine, bore Haran, Moza, and Gazez; and Haran begot Gazez.
13_2_47: And the sons of Jahdai were Regem, Jotham, Geshan, Pelet, Ephah, and Shaaph.
13_2_48: Maachah, Caleb’s concubine, bore Sheber and Tirhanah.
13_2_49: She also bore Shaaph the father of Madmannah, Sheva the father of Machbenah and the father of Gibea. And the daughter of Caleb was Achsah.
13_2_50: These were the descendants of Caleb: The sons of Hur, the firstborn of Ephrathah, were Shobal the father of Kirjath Jearim,
13_2_51: Salma the father of Bethlehem, and Hareph the father of Beth Gader.
13_2_52: And Shobal the father of Kirjath Jearim had descendants: Haroeh, and half of the families of Manuhoth.
13_2_53: The families of Kirjath Jearim were the Ithrites, the Puthites, the Shumathites, and the Mishraites. From these came the Zorathites and the Eshtaolites.
13_2_54: The sons of Salma were Bethlehem, the Netophathites, Atroth Beth Joab, half of the Manahethites, and the Zorites.
13_2_55: And the families of the scribes who dwelt at Jabez were the Tirathites, the Shimeathites, and the Suchathites. These were the Kenites who came from Hammath, the father of the house of Rechab.
13_3_1: Now these were the sons of David who were born to him in Hebron: The firstborn was Amnon, by Ahinoam the Jezreelitess; the second, Daniel, by Abigail the Carmelitess;
13_3_2: the third, Absalom the son of Maacah, the daughter of Talmai, king of Geshur; the fourth, Adonijah the son of Haggith;
13_3_3: the fifth, Shephatiah, by Abital; the sixth, Ithream, by his wife Eglah.
13_3_4: These six were born to him in Hebron. There he reigned seven years and six months, and in Jerusalem he reigned thirty-three years.
13_3_5: And these were born to him in Jerusalem: Shimea, Shobab, Nathan, and Solomon—four by Bathshua the daughter of Ammiel.
13_3_6: Also there were Ibhar, Elishama, Eliphelet,
13_3_8: Elishama, Eliada, and Eliphelet—nine in all.
13_3_9: These were all the sons of David, besides the sons of the concubines, and Tamar their sister.
13_3_10: Solomon’s son was Rehoboam; Abijah was his son, Asa his son, Jehoshaphat his son,
13_3_11: Joram his son, Ahaziah his son, Joash his son,
13_3_12: Amaziah his son, Azariah his son, Jotham his son,
13_3_13: Ahaz his son, Hezekiah his son, Manasseh his son,
13_3_14: Amon his son, and Josiah his son.
13_3_15: The sons of Josiah were Johanan the firstborn, the second Jehoiakim, the third Zedekiah, and the fourth Shallum.
13_3_16: The sons of Jehoiakim were Jeconiah his son and Zedekiah his son.
13_3_17: And the sons of Jeconiah were Assir, Shealtiel his son,
13_3_18: and Malchiram, Pedaiah, Shenazzar, Jecamiah, Hoshama, and Nedabiah.
13_3_19: The sons of Pedaiah were Zerubbabel and Shimei. The sons of Zerubbabel were Meshullam, Hananiah, Shelomith their sister,
13_3_20: and Hashubah, Ohel, Berechiah, Hasadiah, and Jushab-Hesed—five in all.
13_3_21: The sons of Hananiah were Pelatiah and Jeshaiah, the sons of Rephaiah, the sons of Arnan, the sons of Obadiah, and the sons of Shechaniah.
13_3_22: The son of Shechaniah was Shemaiah. The sons of Shemaiah were Hattush, Igal, Bariah, Neariah, and Shaphat—six in all.
13_3_23: The sons of Neariah were Elioenai, Hezekiah, and Azrikam—three in all.
13_3_24: The sons of Elioenai were Hodaviah, Eliashib, Pelaiah, Akkub, Johanan, Delaiah, and Anani—seven in all.
13_4_1: The sons of Judah were Perez, Hezron, Carmi, Hur, and Shobal.
13_4_2: And Reaiah the son of Shobal begot Jahath, and Jahath begot Ahumai and Lahad. These were the families of the Zorathites.
13_4_3: These were the sons of the father of Etam: Jezreel, Ishma, and Idbash; and the name of their sister was Hazelelponi;
13_4_4: and Penuel was the father of Gedor, and Ezer was the father of Hushah. These were the sons of Hur, the firstborn of Ephrathah the father of Bethlehem.
13_4_5: And Ashhur the father of Tekoa had two wives, Helah and Naarah.
13_4_6: Naarah bore him Ahuzzam, Hepher, Temeni, and Haahashtari. These were the sons of Naarah.
13_4_7: The sons of Helah were Zereth, Zohar, and Ethnan;
13_4_8: and Koz begot Anub, Zobebah, and the families of Aharhel the son of Harum.
13_4_9: Now Jabez was more honorable than his brothers, and his mother called his name Jabez, saying, “Because I bore him in pain.”
13_4_10: And Jabez called on the God of Israel saying, “Oh, that You would bless me indeed, and enlarge my territory, that Your hand would be with me, and that You would keep me from evil, that I may not cause pain!” So God granted him what he requested.
13_4_11: Chelub the brother of Shuhah begot Mehir, who was the father of Eshton.
13_4_12: And Eshton begot Beth-Rapha, Paseah, and Tehinnah the father of Ir-Nahash. These were the men of Rechah.
13_4_13: The sons of Kenaz were Othniel and Seraiah. The sons of Othniel were Hathath,
13_4_14: and Meonothai who begot Ophrah. Seraiah begot Joab the father of Ge Harashim, for they were craftsmen.
13_4_15: The sons of Caleb the son of Jephunneh were Iru, Elah, and Naam. The son of Elah was Kenaz.
13_4_16: The sons of Jehallelel were Ziph, Ziphah, Tiria, and Asarel.
13_4_17: The sons of Ezrah were Jether, Mered, Epher, and Jalon. And Mered’s wife bore Miriam, Shammai, and Ishbah the father of Eshtemoa.
13_4_18: His wife Jehudijah bore Jered the father of Gedor, Heber the father of Sochoh, and Jekuthiel the father of Zanoah. These were the sons of Bithiah, the daughter of Pharaoh, whom Mered took.
13_4_19: The sons of Hodiah’s wife, the sister of Naham, were the fathers of Keilah the Garmite and of Eshtemoa the Maachathite.
13_4_20: The sons of Shimon were Amnon, Rinnah, Ben-Hanan, and Tilon. The sons of Ishi were Zoheth and Ben-Zoheth.
13_4_21: The sons of Shelah the son of Judah were Er the father of Lecah, Laadah the father of Mareshah, and the families of the house of the linen workers at Beth-ashbea.
13_4_22: Also Jokim, the men of Cozeba, and Joash; Saraph, who ruled in Moab, and Jashubi-Lehem. Now the records are ancient.
13_4_23: These were the potters and those who dwelt at Netaim and Gederah; there they dwelt with the king for his work.
13_4_24: The sons of Simeon were Nemuel, Jamin, Jarib, Zerah, and Shaul.
13_4_25: Shallum his son, Mibsam his son, and Mishma his son.
13_4_26: The sons of Mishma were Hamuel his son, Zacchur his son, and Shimei his son.
13_4_27: Shimei had sixteen sons and six daughters; but his brothers did not have many children, nor did any of their families multiply as much as the children of Judah.
13_4_28: They dwelt at Beersheba, Moladah, Hazar Shual,
13_4_31: Beth Marcaboth, Hazar Susim, Beth Biri, and at Shaaraim. These were their cities until the reign of David.
13_4_32: And their villages were Etam, Ain, Rimmon, Tochen, and Ashan—five cities—
13_4_33: and all the villages that were around these cities as far as Baal. These were their dwelling places, and they maintained their genealogy:
13_4_34: Meshobab, Jamlech, and Joshah the son of Amaziah;
13_4_35: Joel, and Jehu the son of Joshibiah, the son of Seraiah, the son of Asiel;
13_4_36: Elioenai, Jaakobah, Jeshohaiah, Asaiah, Adiel, Jesimiel, and Benaiah;
13_4_37: Ziza the son of Shiphi, the son of Allon, the son of Jedaiah, the son of Shimri, the son of Shemaiah—
13_4_38: these mentioned by name were leaders in their families, and their father’s house increased greatly.
13_4_39: So they went to the entrance of Gedor, as far as the east side of the valley, to seek pasture for their flocks.
13_4_40: And they found rich, good pasture, and the land was broad, quiet, and peaceful; for some Hamites formerly lived there.
13_4_41: These recorded by name came in the days of Hezekiah king of Judah; and they attacked their tents and the Meunites who were found there, and utterly destroyed them, as it is to this day. So they dwelt in their place, because there was pasture for their flocks there.
13_4_42: Now some of them, five hundred men of the sons of Simeon, went to Mount Seir, having as their captains Pelatiah, Neariah, Rephaiah, and Uzziel, the sons of Ishi.
13_4_43: And they defeated the rest of the Amalekites who had escaped. They have dwelt there to this day.
13_5_1: Now the sons of Reuben the firstborn of Israel—he was indeed the firstborn, but because he defiled his father’s bed, his birthright was given to the sons of Joseph, the son of Israel, so that the genealogy is not listed according to the birthright;
13_5_2: yet Judah prevailed over his brothers, and from him came a ruler, although the birthright was Joseph’s—
13_5_3: the sons of Reuben the firstborn of Israel were Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi.
13_5_4: The sons of Joel were Shemaiah his son, Gog his son, Shimei his son,
13_5_5: Micah his son, Reaiah his son, Baal his son,
13_5_6: and Beerah his son, whom Tiglath-Pileser king of Assyria carried into captivity. He was leader of the Reubenites.
13_5_7: And his brethren by their families, when the genealogy of their generations was registered: the chief, Jeiel, and Zechariah,
13_5_8: and Bela the son of Azaz, the son of Shema, the son of Joel, who dwelt in Aroer, as far as Nebo and Baal Meon.
13_5_9: Eastward they settled as far as the entrance of the wilderness this side of the River Euphrates, because their cattle had multiplied in the land of Gilead.
13_5_10: Now in the days of Saul they made war with the Hagrites, who fell by their hand; and they dwelt in their tents throughout the entire area east of Gilead.
13_5_11: And the children of Gad dwelt next to them in the land of Bashan as far as Salcah:
13_5_12: Joel was the chief, Shapham the next, then Jaanai and Shaphat in Bashan,
13_5_13: and their brethren of their father’s house: Michael, Meshullam, Sheba, Jorai, Jachan, Zia, and Eber—seven in all.
13_5_14: These were the children of Abihail the son of Huri, the son of Jaroah, the son of Gilead, the son of Michael, the son of Jeshishai, the son of Jahdo, the son of Buz;
13_5_15: Ahi the son of Abdiel, the son of Guni, was chief of their father’s house.
13_5_16: And the Gadites dwelt in Gilead, in Bashan and in its villages, and in all the common-lands of Sharon within their borders.
13_5_17: All these were registered by genealogies in the days of Jotham king of Judah, and in the days of Jeroboam king of Israel.
13_5_18: The sons of Reuben, the Gadites, and half the tribe of Manasseh had forty-four thousand seven hundred and sixty valiant men, men able to bear shield and sword, to shoot with the bow, and skillful in war, who went to war.
13_5_19: They made war with the Hagrites, Jetur, Naphish, and Nodab.
13_5_20: And they were helped against them, and the Hagrites were delivered into their hand, and all who were with them, for they cried out to God in the battle. He heeded their prayer, because they put their trust in Him.
13_5_21: Then they took away their livestock—fifty thousand of their camels, two hundred and fifty thousand of their sheep, and two thousand of their donkeys—also one hundred thousand of their men;
13_5_22: for many fell dead, because the war was God’s. And they dwelt in their place until the captivity.
13_5_23: So the children of the half-tribe of Manasseh dwelt in the land. Their numbers increased from Bashan to Baal Hermon, that is, to Senir, or Mount Hermon.
13_5_24: These were the heads of their fathers’ houses: Epher, Ishi, Eliel, Azriel, Jeremiah, Hodaviah, and Jahdiel. They were mighty men of valor, famous men, and heads of their fathers’ houses.
13_5_25: And they were unfaithful to the God of their fathers, and played the harlot after the gods of the peoples of the land, whom God had destroyed before them.
13_5_26: So the God of Israel stirred up the spirit of Pul king of Assyria, that is, Tiglath-Pileser king of Assyria. He carried the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh into captivity. He took them to Halah, Habor, Hara, and the river of Gozan to this day.
13_6_1: The sons of Levi were Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
13_6_2: The sons of Kohath were Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.
13_6_3: The children of Amram were Aaron, Moses, and Miriam. And the sons of Aaron were Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
13_6_4: Eleazar begot Phinehas, and Phinehas begot Abishua;
13_6_5: Abishua begot Bukki, and Bukki begot Uzzi;
13_6_6: Uzzi begot Zerahiah, and Zerahiah begot Meraioth;
13_6_7: Meraioth begot Amariah, and Amariah begot Ahitub;
13_6_8: Ahitub begot Zadok, and Zadok begot Ahimaaz;
13_6_9: Ahimaaz begot Azariah, and Azariah begot Johanan;
13_6_10: Johanan begot Azariah (it was he who ministered as priest in the temple that Solomon built in Jerusalem);
13_6_11: Azariah begot Amariah, and Amariah begot Ahitub;
13_6_12: Ahitub begot Zadok, and Zadok begot Shallum;
13_6_13: Shallum begot Hilkiah, and Hilkiah begot Azariah;
13_6_14: Azariah begot Seraiah, and Seraiah begot Jehozadak.
13_6_15: Jehozadak went into captivity when the LORD carried Judah and Jerusalem into captivity by the hand of Nebuchadnezzar.
13_6_16: The sons of Levi were Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
13_6_17: These are the names of the sons of Gershon: Libni and Shimei.
13_6_18: The sons of Kohath were Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.
13_6_19: The sons of Merari were Mahli and Mushi. Now these are the families of the Levites according to their fathers:
13_6_20: Of Gershon were Libni his son, Jahath his son, Zimmah his son,
13_6_21: Joah his son, Iddo his son, Zerah his son, and Jeatherai his son.
13_6_22: The sons of Kohath were Amminadab his son, Korah his son, Assir his son,
13_6_23: Elkanah his son, Ebiasaph his son, Assir his son,
13_6_24: Tahath his son, Uriel his son, Uzziah his son, and Shaul his son.
13_6_25: The sons of Elkanah were Amasai and Ahimoth.
13_6_26: As for Elkanah, the sons of Elkanah were Zophai his son, Nahath his son,
13_6_27: Eliab his son, Jeroham his son, and Elkanah his son.
13_6_28: The sons of Samuel were Joel the firstborn, and Abijah the second.
13_6_29: The sons of Merari were Mahli, Libni his son, Shimei his son, Uzzah his son,
13_6_30: Shimea his son, Haggiah his son, and Asaiah his son.
13_6_31: Now these are the men whom David appointed over the service of song in the house of the LORD, after the ark came to rest.
13_6_32: They were ministering with music before the dwelling place of the tabernacle of meeting, until Solomon had built the house of the LORD in Jerusalem, and they served in their office according to their order.
13_6_33: And these are the ones who ministered with their sons: Of the sons of the Kohathites were Heman the singer, the son of Joel, the son of Samuel,
13_6_34: the son of Elkanah, the son of Jeroham, the son of Eliel, the son of Toah,
13_6_35: the son of Zuph, the son of Elkanah, the son of Mahath, the son of Amasai,
13_6_36: the son of Elkanah, the son of Joel, the son of Azariah, the son of Zephaniah,
13_6_37: the son of Tahath, the son of Assir, the son of Ebiasaph, the son of Korah,
13_6_38: the son of Izhar, the son of Kohath, the son of Levi, the son of Israel.
13_6_39: And his brother Asaph, who stood at his right hand, was Asaph the son of Berachiah, the son of Shimea,
13_6_40: the son of Michael, the son of Baaseiah, the son of Malchijah,
13_6_41: the son of Ethni, the son of Zerah, the son of Adaiah,
13_6_42: the son of Ethan, the son of Zimmah, the son of Shimei,
13_6_43: the son of Jahath, the son of Gershon, the son of Levi.
13_6_44: Their brethren, the sons of Merari, on the left hand, were Ethan the son of Kishi, the son of Abdi, the son of Malluch,
13_6_45: the son of Hashabiah, the son of Amaziah, the son of Hilkiah,
13_6_46: the son of Amzi, the son of Bani, the son of Shamer,
13_6_47: the son of Mahli, the son of Mushi, the son of Merari, the son of Levi.
13_6_48: And their brethren, the Levites, were appointed to every kind of service of the tabernacle of the house of God.
13_6_49: But Aaron and his sons offered sacrifices on the altar of burnt offering and on the altar of incense, for all the work of the Most Holy Place, and to make atonement for Israel, according to all that Moses the servant of God had commanded.
13_6_50: Now these are the sons of Aaron: Eleazar his son, Phinehas his son, Abishua his son,
13_6_51: Bukki his son, Uzzi his son, Zerahiah his son,
13_6_52: Meraioth his son, Amariah his son, Ahitub his son,
13_6_53: Zadok his son, and Ahimaaz his son.
13_6_54: Now these are their dwelling places throughout their settlements in their territory, for they were given by lot to the sons of Aaron, of the family of the Kohathites:
13_6_55: They gave them Hebron in the land of Judah, with its surrounding pasturelands.
13_6_56: But the fields of the city and its villages they gave to Caleb the son of Jephunneh.
13_6_57: And to the sons of Aaron they gave one of the cities of refuge, Hebron; also Libnah with its pasturelands, Jattir, Eshtemoa with its pasturelands,
13_6_58: Hilen with its pasturelands, Debir with its pasturelands,
13_6_59: Ashan with its pasturelands, and Beth Shemesh with its pasturelands.
13_6_60: And from the tribe of Benjamin: Geba with its pasturelands, Alemeth with its pasturelands, and Anathoth with its pasturelands. All their cities among their families were thirteen.
13_6_61: To the rest of the family of the tribe of the Kohathites they gave by lot ten cities from half the tribe of Manasseh.
13_6_62: And to the sons of Gershon, throughout their families, they gave thirteen cities from the tribe of Issachar, from the tribe of Asher, from the tribe of Naphtali, and from the tribe of Manasseh in Bashan.
13_6_63: To the sons of Merari, throughout their families, they gave twelve cities from the tribe of Reuben, from the tribe of Gad, and from the tribe of Zebulun.
13_6_64: So the children of Israel gave these cities with their pasturelands to the Levites.
13_6_65: And they gave by lot from the tribe of the children of Judah, from the tribe of the children of Simeon, and from the tribe of the children of Benjamin these cities which are called by their names.
13_6_66: Now some of the families of the sons of Kohath were given cities as their territory from the tribe of Ephraim.
13_6_67: And they gave them one of the cities of refuge, Shechem with its pasturelands, in the mountains of Ephraim, also Gezer with its pasturelands,
13_6_68: Jokmeam with its pasturelands, Beth Horon with its pasturelands,
13_6_69: Aijalon with its pasturelands, and Gath Rimmon with its pasturelands.
13_6_70: And from the half-tribe of Manasseh: Aner with its pasturelands and Bileam with its pasturelands, for the rest of the family of the sons of Kohath.
13_6_71: From the family of the half-tribe of Manasseh the sons of Gershon were given Golan in Bashan with its pasturelands and Ashtaroth with its pasturelands.
13_6_72: And from the tribe of Issachar: Kedesh with its pasturelands, Daberath with its pasturelands,
13_6_73: Ramoth with its pasturelands, and Anem with its pasturelands.
13_6_74: And from the tribe of Asher: Mashal with its pasturelands, Abdon with its pasturelands,
13_6_75: Hukok with its pasturelands, and Rehob with its pasturelands.
13_6_76: And from the tribe of Naphtali: Kedesh in Galilee with its pasturelands, Hammon with its pasturelands, and Kirjathaim with its pasturelands.
13_6_77: From the tribe of Zebulun the rest of the children of Merari were given Rimmon with its pasturelands and Tabor with its pasturelands.
13_6_78: And on the other side of the Jordan, across from Jericho, on the east side of the Jordan, they were given from the tribe of Reuben: Bezer in the wilderness with its pasturelands, Jahzah with its pasturelands,
13_6_79: Kedemoth with its pasturelands, and Mephaath with its pasturelands.
13_6_80: And from the tribe of Gad: Ramoth in Gilead with its pasturelands, Mahanaim with its pasturelands,
13_6_81: Heshbon with its pasturelands, and Jazer with its pasturelands.
13_7_1: The sons of Issachar were Tola, Puah, Jashub, and Shimron—four in all.
13_7_2: The sons of Tola were Uzzi, Rephaiah, Jeriel, Jahmai, Jibsam, and Shemuel, heads of their father’s house. The sons of Tola were mighty men of valor in their generations; their number in the days of David was twenty-two thousand six hundred.
13_7_3: The son of Uzzi was Izrahiah, and the sons of Izrahiah were Michael, Obadiah, Joel, and Ishiah. All five of them were chief men.
13_7_4: And with them, by their generations, according to their fathers’ houses, were thirty-six thousand troops ready for war; for they had many wives and sons.
13_7_5: Now their brethren among all the families of Issachar were mighty men of valor, listed by their genealogies, eighty-seven thousand in all.
13_7_6: The sons of Benjamin were Bela, Becher, and Jediael—three in all.
13_7_7: The sons of Bela were Ezbon, Uzzi, Uzziel, Jerimoth, and Iri—five in all. They were heads of their fathers’ houses, and they were listed by their genealogies, twenty-two thousand and thirty-four mighty men of valor.
13_7_8: The sons of Becher were Zemirah, Joash, Eliezer, Elioenai, Omri, Jerimoth, Abijah, Anathoth, and Alemeth. All these are the sons of Becher.
13_7_9: And they were recorded by genealogy according to their generations, heads of their fathers’ houses, twenty thousand two hundred mighty men of valor.
13_7_10: The son of Jediael was Bilhan, and the sons of Bilhan were Jeush, Benjamin, Ehud, Chenaanah, Zethan, Tharshish, and Ahishahar.
13_7_11: All these sons of Jediael were heads of their fathers’ houses; there were seventeen thousand two hundred mighty men of valor fit to go out for war and battle.
13_7_12: Shuppim and Huppim were the sons of Ir, and Hushim was the son of Aher.
13_7_13: The sons of Naphtali were Jahziel, Guni, Jezer, and Shallum, the sons of Bilhah.
13_7_14: The descendants of Manasseh: his Syrian concubine bore him Machir the father of Gilead, the father of Asriel.
13_7_15: Machir took as his wife the sister of Huppim and Shuppim, whose name was Maachah. The name of Gilead’s grandson was Zelophehad, but Zelophehad begot only daughters.
13_7_16: Maachah the wife of Machir bore a son, and she called his name Peresh. The name of his brother was Sheresh, and his sons were Ulam and Rakem.
13_7_17: The son of Ulam was Bedan. These were the descendants of Gilead the son of Machir, the son of Manasseh.
13_7_18: His sister Hammoleketh bore Ishhod, Abiezer, and Mahlah.
13_7_19: The sons of Shemida were Ahian, Shechem, Likhi, and Aniam.
13_7_20: The sons of Ephraim were Shuthelah, Bered his son, Tahath his son, Eladah his son, Tahath his son,
13_7_21: Zabad his son, Shuthelah his son, and Ezer and Elead. The men of Gath who were born in that land killed them because they came down to raid their cattle.
13_7_22: Then Ephraim their father mourned many days, and his brethren came to comfort him.
13_7_23: And when he went in to his wife, she conceived and bore a son; and he called his name Beriah, because tragedy had come upon his house.
13_7_24: Now his daughter was Sheerah, who built Lower and Upper Beth Horon and Uzzen Sheerah;
13_7_25: And Rephah was his son, as well as Resheph, and Telah his son, Tahan his son,
13_7_26: Laadan his son, Ammihud his son, Elishama his son,
13_7_27: Nun his son, and Joshua his son.
13_7_28: Now their possessions and dwelling places were Bethel and its towns: to the east Naaran, to the west Gezer and its towns, and Shechem and its towns, as far as Ayyah and its towns;
13_7_29: And by the borders of the children of Manasseh were Beth Shean and its towns, Taanach and its towns, Megiddo and its towns, Dor and its towns. In these dwelt the children of Joseph, the son of Israel.
13_7_30: The sons of Asher were Imnah, Ishvah, Ishvi, Beriah, and their sister Serah.
13_7_31: The sons of Beriah were Heber and Malchiel, who was the father of Birzaith.
13_7_32: And Heber begot Japhlet, Shomer, Hotham, and their sister Shua.
13_7_33: The sons of Japhlet were Pasach, Bimhal, and Ashvath. These were the children of Japhlet.
13_7_34: The sons of Shemer were Ahi, Rohgah, Jehubbah, and Aram.
13_7_35: And the sons of his brother Helem were Zophah, Imna, Shelesh, and Amal.
13_7_36: The sons of Zophah were Suah, Harnepher, Shual, Beri, Imrah,
13_7_37: Bezer, Hod, Shamma, Shilshah, Ithran, and Beera.
13_7_38: The sons of Jether were Jephunneh, Pispa, and Ara.
13_7_39: The sons of Ulla were Arah, Hanniel, and Rizia.
13_7_40: All these were the children of Asher, heads of their fathers’ houses, choice men, mighty men of valor, chief leaders. And they were recorded by genealogies among the army fit for battle; their number was twenty-six thousand.
13_8_1: Now Benjamin begot Bela his firstborn, Ashbel the second, Aharah the third,
13_8_2: Nohah the fourth, and Rapha the fifth.
13_8_3: The sons of Bela were Addar, Gera, Abihud,
13_8_6: These are the sons of Ehud, who were the heads of the fathers’ houses of the inhabitants of Geba, and who forced them to move to Manahath:
13_8_7: Naaman, Ahijah, and Gera who forced them to move. He begot Uzza and Ahihud.
13_8_8: Also Shaharaim had children in the country of Moab, after he had sent away Hushim and Baara his wives.
13_8_9: By Hodesh his wife he begot Jobab, Zibia, Mesha, Malcam,
13_8_10: Jeuz, Sachiah, and Mirmah. These were his sons, heads of their fathers’ houses.
13_8_11: And by Hushim he begot Abitub and Elpaal.
13_8_12: The sons of Elpaal were Eber, Misham, and Shemed, who built Ono and Lod with its towns;
13_8_13: And Beriah and Shema, who were heads of their fathers’ houses of the inhabitants of Aijalon, who drove out the inhabitants of Gath.
13_8_16: Michael, Ishpah, and Joha were the sons of Beriah.
13_8_18: Ishmerai, Izliah, and Jobab were the sons of Elpaal.
13_8_21: Adaiah, Beraiah, and Shimrath were the sons of Shimei.
13_8_25: Iphdeiah, and Penuel were the sons of Shashak.
13_8_27: Jaareshiah, Elijah, and Zichri were the sons of Jeroham.
13_8_28: These were the heads of the fathers' houses by their generations, chief men who lived in Jerusalem.
13_8_29: Jeiel, the father of Gibeon, lived in Gibeon, and his wife's name was Maacah.
13_8_30: His firstborn son was Abdon, then Zur, Kish, Baal, Nadab,
13_8_32: and Mikloth, who begot Shimeah. They also dwelt alongside their relatives in Jerusalem, with their brethren.
13_8_33: Ner begot Kish, Kish begot Saul, and Saul begot Jonathan, Malchishua, Abinadab, and Esh-Baal.
13_8_34: The son of Jonathan was Merib-Baal, and Merib-Baal begot Micah.
13_8_35: The sons of Micah were Pithon, Melech, Tarea, and Ahaz.
13_8_36: Ahaz begot Jehoaddah; Jehoaddah begot Alemeth, Azmaveth, and Zimri; and Zimri begot Moza.
13_8_37: Moza begot Binea, Raphah his son, Eleasah his son, and Azel his son.
13_8_38: Azel had six sons whose names were these: Azrikam, Bocheru, Ishmael, Sheariah, Obadiah, and Hanan. All these were the sons of Azel.
13_8_39: And the sons of Eshek his brother were Ulam his firstborn, Jeush the second, and Eliphelet the third.
13_8_40: The sons of Ulam were mighty men of valor—archers. They had many sons and grandsons, one hundred and fifty in all. These were all sons of Benjamin.
13_9_1: So all Israel was recorded by genealogies, and indeed, they were inscribed in the book of the kings of Israel. But Judah was carried away captive to Babylon because of their unfaithfulness.
13_9_2: And the first inhabitants who dwelt in their possessions in their cities were Israelites, priests, Levites, and the Nethinim.
13_9_3: Now in Jerusalem the children of Judah dwelt, and some of the children of Benjamin, and of the children of Ephraim and Manasseh:
13_9_4: Uthai the son of Ammihud, the son of Omri, the son of Imri, the son of Bani, of the descendants of Perez, the son of Judah.
13_9_5: Of the Shilonites: Asaiah the firstborn and his sons.
13_9_6: Of the sons of Zerah: Jeuel, and their brethren—six hundred and ninety.
13_9_7: Of the sons of Benjamin: Sallu the son of Meshullam, the son of Hodaviah, the son of Hassenuah;
13_9_8: Ibneiah the son of Jeroham; Elah the son of Uzzi, the son of Michri; Meshullam the son of Shephatiah, the son of Reuel, the son of Ibnijah;
13_9_9: and their brethren, according to their generations—nine hundred and fifty-six. All these men were heads of a father's house in their fathers' houses.
13_9_10: Of the priests: Jedaiah, Jehoiarib, and Jachin;
13_9_11: Azariah the son of Hilkiah, the son of Meshullam, the son of Zadok, the son of Meraioth, the son of Ahitub, the officer over the house of God;
13_9_12: Adaiah the son of Jeroham, the son of Pashur, the son of Malchijah; Maasai the son of Adiel, the son of Jahzerah, the son of Meshullam, the son of Meshillemith, the son of Immer;
13_9_13: and their brethren, heads of their fathers' houses—one thousand seven hundred and sixty. They were very able men for the work of the service of the house of God.
13_9_14: Of the Levites: Shemaiah the son of Hasshub, the son of Azrikam, the son of Hashabiah, of the sons of Merari;
13_9_15: Bakbakkar, Heresh, Galal, and Mattaniah the son of Micah, the son of Zichri, the son of Asaph;
13_9_16: Obadiah the son of Shemaiah, the son of Galal, the son of Jeduthun; and Berechiah the son of Asa, the son of Elkanah, who lived in the villages of the Netophathites.
13_9_17: And the gatekeepers were Shallum, Akkub, Talmon, Ahiman, and their brethren. Shallum was the chief.
13_9_18: Until then they had been gatekeepers for the camps of the children of Levi at the King's Gate on the east.
13_9_19: Shallum the son of Kore, the son of Ebiasaph, the son of Korah, and his brethren, from his father's house, the Korahites, were in charge of the work of the service, gatekeepers of the tabernacle. Their fathers had been keepers of the entrance to the camp of the LORD.
13_9_20: And Phinehas the son of Eleazar had been the officer over them in time past; the LORD was with him.
13_9_21: Zechariah the son of Meshelemiah was keeper of the door of the tabernacle of meeting.
13_9_22: All those chosen as gatekeepers were two hundred and twelve. They were recorded by their genealogy, in their villages. David and Samuel the seer had appointed them to their trusted office.
13_9_23: So they and their children were in charge of the gates of the house of the LORD, the house of the tabernacle, by assignment.
13_9_24: The gatekeepers were assigned to the four directions: the east, west, north, and south.
13_9_25: And their brethren in their villages had to come with them from time to time for seven days.
13_9_26: For in this trusted office were four chief gatekeepers; they were Levites. And they had charge over the chambers and treasuries of the house of God.
13_9_27: And they lodged all around the house of God because they had the responsibility, and they were in charge of opening it every morning.
13_9_28: Now some of them were in charge of the serving vessels, for they brought them in and took them out by count.
13_9_29: Some of them were appointed over the furnishings and over all the implements of the sanctuary, and over the fine flour and the wine and the oil and the incense and the spices.
13_9_30: And some of the sons of the priests made the ointment of the spices.
13_9_31: Mattithiah of the Levites, the firstborn of Shallum the Korahite, had the trusted office over the things that were baked in the pans.
13_9_32: And some of their brethren of the sons of the Kohathites were in charge of preparing the showbread for every Sabbath.
13_9_33: These are the singers, heads of the fathers' houses of the Levites, who lodged in the chambers, and were free from other duties; for they were employed in that work day and night.
13_9_34: These heads of the fathers' houses of the Levites were heads throughout their generations. They dwelt at Jerusalem.
13_9_35: Jeiel the father of Gibeon, whose wife's name was Maacah, dwelt at Gibeon.
13_9_36: His firstborn son was Abdon, then Zur, Kish, Baal, Ner, Nadab,
13_9_38: And Mikloth begot Shimeam. They also dwelt alongside their relatives in Jerusalem, with their brethren.
13_9_39: Ner begot Kish, Kish begot Saul, and Saul begot Jonathan, Malchishua, Abinadab, and Esh-Baal.
13_9_40: The son of Jonathan was Merib-Baal, and Merib-Baal begot Micah.
13_9_41: The sons of Micah were Pithon, Melech, Tahrea, and Ahaz.
13_9_42: And Ahaz begot Jarah; Jarah begot Alemeth, Azmaveth, and Zimri; and Zimri begot Moza.
13_9_43: Moza begot Binea, Rephaiah his son, Eleasah his son, and Azel his son.
13_9_44: And Azel had six sons whose names were these: Azrikam, Bocheru, Ishmael, Sheariah, Obadiah, and Hanan; these were the sons of Azel.
13_10_1: Now the Philistines fought against Israel; and the men of Israel fled from before the Philistines, and fell slain on Mount Gilboa.
13_10_2: Then the Philistines followed hard after Saul and his sons. And the Philistines killed Jonathan, Abinadab, and Malchishua, Saul’s sons.
13_10_3: The battle became fierce against Saul. The archers hit him, and he was wounded by the archers.
13_10_4: Then Saul said to his armorbearer, “Draw your sword, and thrust me through with it, lest these uncircumcised men come and abuse me.” But his armorbearer would not, for he was greatly afraid. Therefore Saul took a sword and fell on it.
13_10_5: And when his armorbearer saw that Saul was dead, he also fell on his sword and died.
13_10_6: So Saul and his three sons died, and all his house died together.
13_10_7: And when all the men of Israel who were in the valley saw that they had fled and that Saul and his sons were dead, they forsook their cities and fled; then the Philistines came and dwelt in them.
13_10_8: So it happened the next day, when the Philistines came to strip the slain, that they found Saul and his sons fallen on Mount Gilboa.
13_10_9: And they stripped him and took his head and his armor, and sent word throughout the land of the Philistines to proclaim the news in the temple of their idols and among the people.
13_10_10: Then they put his armor in the temple of their gods, and fastened his head in the temple of Dagon.
13_10_11: And when all Jabesh Gilead heard all that the Philistines had done to Saul,
13_10_12: all the valiant men arose and took the body of Saul and the bodies of his sons; and they brought them to Jabesh, and buried their bones under the tamarisk tree at Jabesh, and fasted seven days.
13_10_13: So Saul died for his unfaithfulness which he had committed against the LORD, because he did not keep the word of the LORD, and also because he consulted a medium for guidance.
13_10_14: But he did not inquire of the LORD; therefore He killed him, and turned the kingdom over to David the son of Jesse.
13_11_1: Then all Israel came together to David at Hebron, saying, “Indeed we are your bone and your flesh.
13_11_2: Also, in time past, even when Saul was king, you were the one who led Israel out and brought them in; and the LORD your God said to you, ‘You shall shepherd My people Israel, and be ruler over My people Israel.’”
13_11_3: Therefore all the elders of Israel came to the king at Hebron, and David made a covenant with them at Hebron before the LORD. And they anointed David king over Israel, according to the word of the LORD by Samuel.
13_11_4: And David and all Israel went to Jerusalem, which is Jebus, where the Jebusites were, the inhabitants of the land.
13_11_5: But the inhabitants of Jebus said to David, “You shall not come in here!” Nevertheless David took the stronghold of Zion (that is, the City of David).
13_11_6: Now David said, “Whoever attacks the Jebusites first shall be chief and captain.” And Joab the son of Zeruiah went up first, and became chief.
13_11_7: Then David dwelt in the stronghold; therefore they called it the City of David.
13_11_8: And he built the city around it, from the Millo to the surrounding area. Joab repaired the rest of the city.
13_11_9: So David went on and became great, and the LORD of hosts was with him.
13_11_10: Now these were the heads of the mighty men whom David had, who strengthened themselves with him in his kingdom, with all Israel, to make him king, according to the word of the LORD concerning Israel.
13_11_11: And this is the number of the mighty men whom David had: Jashobeam the son of a Hachmonite, chief of the captains; he had lifted up his spear against three hundred, killed by him at one time.
13_11_12: After him was Eleazar the son of Dodo, the Ahohite, who was one of the three mighty men.
13_11_13: He was with David at Pasdammim. Now there the Philistines were gathered for battle, and there was a piece of ground full of barley. So the people fled from the Philistines.
13_11_14: But they stationed themselves in the middle of that field, defended it, and killed the Philistines. So the LORD brought about a great victory.
13_11_15: Now three of the thirty chief men went down to the rock to David, into the cave of Adullam; and the army of the Philistines encamped in the Valley of Rephaim.
13_11_16: David was then in the stronghold, and the garrison of the Philistines was then in Bethlehem.
13_11_17: And David said with longing, “Oh, that someone would give me a drink of water from the well of Bethlehem, which is by the gate!”
13_11_18: So the three broke through the camp of the Philistines, drew water from the well of Bethlehem that was by the gate, and took it and brought it to David. Nevertheless David would not drink it, but poured it out to the LORD.
13_11_19: And he said, “Far be it from me, O my God, that I should do this! Shall I drink the blood of these men who have put their lives in jeopardy? For at the risk of their lives they brought it.” Therefore he would not drink it. These things were done by the three mighty men.
13_11_20: Abishai the brother of Joab was chief of another three. He had lifted up his spear against three hundred men, killed them, and won a name among these three.
13_11_21: Of the three he was more honored than the other two men. Therefore he became their captain. However he did not attain to the first three.
