23_37_20: Now therefore, O LORD our God, save us from his hand, that all the kingdoms of the earth may know that You are the LORD, You alone.
23_37_21: Then Isaiah the son of Amoz sent to Hezekiah, saying, “Thus says the LORD God of Israel, ‘Because you have prayed to Me against Sennacherib king of Assyria,
23_37_22: this is the word which the LORD has spoken concerning him: “The virgin, the daughter of Zion, Has despised you, laughed you to scorn; The daughter of Jerusalem Has shaken her head behind your back!
23_37_23: Whom have you reproached and blasphemed? Against whom have you raised your voice, And lifted up your eyes in pride? Against the Holy One of Israel.
23_37_24: By your servants you have reproached the Lord, And said, ‘By the multitude of my chariots I have come up to the height of the mountains, To the far recesses of Lebanon; I will cut down its tall cedars And its choice cypress trees; I will enter its remotest height, To its fruitful forest.
23_37_25: I have dug wells and drunk water, And with the soles of my feet I have dried up All the rivers of Egypt.
23_37_26: Did you not hear long ago How I made it, From ancient times that I formed it? Now I have brought it to pass, That you should be For crushing fortified cities into heaps of ruins.
23_37_27: Therefore their inhabitants had little power; They were dismayed and confounded; They were as the grass of the field And the green herb, As the grass on the housetops And grain blighted before it is grown.
23_37_28: But I know your dwelling place, Your going out and your coming in, And your rage against Me.
23_37_29: Because your rage against Me and your arrogance Have come up to My ears, Therefore I will put My hook in your nose And My bridle in your lips, And I will turn you back By the way which you came.
23_37_30: This shall be a sign to you: You shall eat this year such as grows of itself, And the second year what springs from the same; Also in the third year sow and reap, Plant vineyards and eat the fruit of them.
23_37_31: And the remnant who have escaped of the house of Judah Shall again take root downward, And bear fruit upward.
23_37_32: For out of Jerusalem shall go a remnant, And those who escape from Mount Zion. The zeal of the LORD of hosts will do this.
23_37_33: Therefore thus says the LORD concerning the king of Assyria: ‘He shall not come into this city, Nor shoot an arrow there, Nor come before it with shield, Nor build a siege mound against it.
23_37_34: By the way that he came, By the same shall he return; And he shall not come into this city,’ Says the LORD.
23_37_35: ‘For I will defend this city, to save it For My own sake and for My servant David’s sake.’
23_37_36: Then the angel of the LORD went out, and killed in the camp of the Assyrians one hundred and eighty-five thousand; and when people arose early in the morning, there were the corpses—all dead.
23_37_37: So Sennacherib king of Assyria departed and went away, returned home, and remained at Nineveh.
23_37_38: Now it came to pass, as he was worshiping in the house of Nisroch his god, that his sons Adrammelech and Sharezer struck him down with the sword; and they escaped into the land of Ararat. Then Esarhaddon his son reigned in his place.
23_38_1: In those days Hezekiah was sick and near death. And Isaiah the prophet, the son of Amoz, went to him and said to him, “Thus says the LORD: ‘Set your house in order, for you shall die and not live.’
23_38_2: Then Hezekiah turned his face toward the wall, and prayed to the LORD,
23_38_3: and said, “Remember now, O LORD, I pray, how I have walked before You in truth and with a loyal heart, and have done what is good in Your sight.” And Hezekiah wept bitterly.
23_38_4: And the word of the LORD came to Isaiah, saying,
23_38_5: Go and tell Hezekiah, ‘Thus says the LORD, the God of David your father: “I have heard your prayer, I have seen your tears; surely I will add to your days fifteen years.
23_38_6: I will deliver you and this city from the hand of the king of Assyria, and I will defend this city.”
23_38_7: And this is the sign to you from the LORD, that the LORD will do this thing which He has spoken:
23_38_8: Behold, I will bring the shadow on the sundial, which has gone down with the sun on the sundial of Ahaz, ten degrees backward.” So the sun returned ten degrees on the dial by which it had gone down.
23_38_9: This is the writing of Hezekiah king of Judah, when he had been sick and had recovered from his sickness:
23_38_10: I said, “In the prime of my life I shall go to the gates of Sheol; I am deprived of the remainder of my years.”
23_38_11: I said, “I shall not see YAH, The LORD in the land of the living; I shall observe man no more among the inhabitants of the world.
23_38_12: My life span is gone, Taken from me like a shepherd’s tent; I have cut off my life like a weaver. He cuts me off from the loom; From day until night You make an end of me.
23_38_13: I have considered until morning— Like a lion, So He breaks all my bones; From day until night You make an end of me.
23_38_14: Like a crane or a swallow, so I chattered; I mourned like a dove; My eyes fail from looking upward. O LORD, I am oppressed; Undertake for me!
23_38_15: What shall I say? He has both spoken to me, And He Himself has done it. I shall walk carefully all my years In the bitterness of my soul.
23_38_16: O Lord, by these things men live; And in all these things is the life of my spirit; So You will restore me and make me live.
23_38_17: Indeed it was for my own peace That I had great bitterness; But You have lovingly delivered my soul from the pit of corruption, For You have cast all my sins behind Your back.
23_38_18: For Sheol cannot thank You, Death cannot praise You; Those who go down to the pit cannot hope for Your truth.
23_38_19: The living, the living man, he shall praise You, As I do this day; The father shall make known Your truth to the children.
23_38_20: The LORD was ready to save me; Therefore we will sing my songs with stringed instruments All the days of our life, in the house of the LORD.
23_38_21: Now Isaiah had said, “Let them take a lump of figs, and apply it as a poultice on the boil, and he shall recover.”
23_38_22: And Hezekiah had said, “What is the sign that I shall go up to the house of the LORD?”
23_39_1: At that time Merodach-Baladan the son of Baladan, king of Babylon, sent letters and a present to Hezekiah, for he heard that he had been sick and had recovered.
23_39_2: And Hezekiah was pleased with them, and showed them the house of his treasures—the silver and gold, the spices and precious ointment, and all his armory—all that was found among his treasures. There was nothing in his house or in all his dominion that Hezekiah did not show them.
23_39_3: Then Isaiah the prophet went to King Hezekiah, and said to him, “What did these men say, and from where did they come to you?” So Hezekiah said, “They came to me from a far country, from Babylon.”
23_39_4: And he said, “What have they seen in your house?” So Hezekiah answered, “They have seen all that is in my house; there is nothing among my treasures that I have not shown them.”
23_39_5: Then Isaiah said to Hezekiah, “Hear the word of the LORD of hosts:
23_39_6: ‘Behold, the days are coming when all that is in your house, and what your fathers have accumulated until this day, shall be carried to Babylon; nothing shall be left,’ says the LORD.
23_39_7: ‘And they shall take away some of your sons who will descend from you, whom you will beget; and they shall be eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon.’ ”
23_39_8: So Hezekiah said to Isaiah, “The word of the LORD which you have spoken is good!” For he said, “At least there will be peace and truth in my days.”
23_40_1: “Comfort, yes, comfort My people!” Says your God.
23_40_2: “Speak comfort to Jerusalem, and cry out to her, That her warfare is ended, That her iniquity is pardoned;  For she has received from the LORD’s hand Double for all her sins.”
23_40_3: The voice of one crying in the wilderness:  “Prepare the way of the LORD;  Make straight in the desert A highway for our God.
23_40_4: Every valley shall be exalted And every mountain and hill brought low;  The crooked places shall be made straight And the rough places smooth;
23_40_5: The glory of the LORD shall be revealed, And all flesh shall see it together; For the mouth of the LORD has spoken.”
23_40_6: The voice said, “Cry out!” And he said, “What shall I cry?”  “All flesh is grass, And all its loveliness is like the flower of the field.
23_40_7: The grass withers, the flower fades, Because the breath of the LORD blows upon it; Surely the people are grass.
23_40_8: The grass withers, the flower fades, But the word of our God stands forever.”
23_40_9: O Zion, You who bring good tidings, Get up into the high mountain; O Jerusalem, You who bring good tidings, Lift up your voice with strength, Lift it up, be not afraid; Say to the cities of Judah, “Behold your God!”
23_40_10: Behold, the Lord GOD shall come with a strong hand, And His arm shall rule for Him; Behold, His reward is with Him, And His work before Him.
23_40_11: He will feed His flock like a shepherd; He will gather the lambs with His arm, And carry them in His bosom, And gently lead those who are with young.
23_40_12: Who has measured the waters in the hollow of His hand, Measured heaven with a span And calculated the dust of the earth in a measure? Weighed the mountains in scales And the hills in a balance?
23_40_13: Who has directed the Spirit of the LORD, Or as His counselor has taught Him?
23_40_14: With whom did He take counsel, and who instructed Him, And taught Him in the path of justice? Who taught Him knowledge, And showed Him the way of understanding?
23_40_15: Behold, the nations are as a drop in a bucket, And are counted as the small dust on the scales; Look, He lifts up the isles as a very little thing.
23_40_16: And Lebanon is not sufficient to burn, Nor its beasts sufficient for a burnt offering.
23_40_17: All nations before Him are as nothing, And they are counted by Him less than nothing and worthless.
23_40_18: To whom then will you liken God? Or what likeness will you compare to Him?
23_40_19: The workman molds an image, The goldsmith overspreads it with gold, And the silversmith casts silver chains.
23_40_20: Whoever is too impoverished for such a contribution Chooses a tree that will not rot; He seeks for himself a skillful workman  To prepare a carved image that will not totter.
23_40_21: Have you not known? Have you not heard? Has it not been told you from the beginning? Have you not understood from the foundations of the earth?
23_40_22: It is He who sits above the circle of the earth, And its inhabitants are like grasshoppers, Who stretches out the heavens like a curtain, And spreads them out like a tent to dwell in.
23_40_23: He brings the princes to nothing; He makes the judges of the earth useless.
23_40_24: Scarcely shall they be planted, Scarcely shall they be sown, Scarcely shall their stock take root in the earth, When He will also blow on them, And they will wither, And the whirlwind will take them away like stubble.
23_40_25: “To  whom then will you liken Me, Or to whom shall I be equal?” says the Holy One.
23_40_26: Lift up your eyes on high, And see who has created these things, Who brings out their host by number;  He calls them all by name, By the greatness of His might And the strength of His power; Not one is missing.
23_40_27: Why do you say, O Jacob, And speak, O Israel: “My way is hidden from the LORD, And my just claim is passed over by my God”?
23_40_28: Have you not known? Have you not heard? The everlasting God, the LORD, The Creator of the ends of the earth, Neither faints nor is weary.  His understanding is unsearchable.
23_40_29: He gives power to the weak, And to those who have no might He increases strength.
23_40_30: Even the youths shall faint and be weary, And the young men shall utterly fall,
23_40_31: But those who wait on the LORD  Shall renew their strength; They shall mount up with wings like eagles, They shall run and not be weary, They shall walk and not faint.
23_41_1: “Keep silence before Me, O coastlands, And let the people renew their strength! Let them come near, then let them speak; Let us come near together for judgment.
23_41_2: “Who raised up one from the east? Who in righteousness called him to His feet? Who gave the nations before him, And made him rule over kings? Who gave them as the dust to his sword, As driven stubble to his bow?
23_41_3: Who pursued them, and passed safely By the way that he had not gone with his feet?
23_41_4: Who has performed and done it, calling the generations from the beginning? ‘I, the LORD, am the first; and with the last I am He.’
23_41_5: The coastlands saw it and feared, the ends of the earth were afraid; they drew near and came.
23_41_6: Everyone helped his neighbor, and said to his brother, “Be of good courage!”
23_41_7: So the craftsman encouraged the goldsmith; he who smooths with the hammer inspired him who strikes the anvil, saying, “It is ready for the soldering”; then he fastened it with pegs, that it might not totter.
23_41_8: But you, Israel, are My servant, Jacob whom I have chosen, the descendants of Abraham My friend.
23_41_9: You whom I have taken from the ends of the earth, and called from its farthest regions, and said to you, ‘You are My servant, I have chosen you and have not cast you away:
23_41_10: Fear not, for I am with you; be not dismayed, for I am your God. I will strengthen you, yes, I will help you, I will uphold you with My righteous right hand.’
23_41_11: Behold, all those who were incensed against you shall be ashamed and disgraced; they shall be as nothing, and those who strive with you shall perish.
23_41_12: You shall seek them and not find them—those who contended with you. Those who war against you shall be as nothing, as a nonexistent thing.
23_41_13: For I, the LORD your God, will hold your right hand, saying to you, ‘Fear not, I will help you.’
23_41_14: Fear not, you worm Jacob, you men of Israel! I will help you,” says the LORD and your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel.
23_41_15: Behold, I will make you into a new threshing sledge with sharp teeth; you shall thresh the mountains and beat them small, and make the hills like chaff.
23_41_16: You shall winnow them, the wind shall carry them away, and the whirlwind shall scatter them; you shall rejoice in the LORD, and glory in the Holy One of Israel.
23_41_17: The poor and needy seek water, but there is none, their tongues fail for thirst. I, the LORD, will hear them; I, the God of Israel, will not forsake them.
23_41_18: I will open rivers in desolate heights, and fountains in the midst of the valleys; I will make the wilderness a pool of water, and the dry land springs of water.
23_41_19: I will plant in the wilderness the cedar and the acacia tree, the myrtle and the oil tree; I will set in the desert the cypress tree and the pine and the box tree together,
23_41_20: That they may see and know, and consider and understand together, that the hand of the LORD has done this, and the Holy One of Israel has created it.
23_41_21: Present your case,” says the LORD. “Bring forth your strong reasons,” says the King of Jacob.
23_41_22: Let them bring forth and show us what will happen; let them show the former things, what they were, that we may consider them, and know the latter end of them; or declare to us things to come.
23_41_23: Show the things that are to come hereafter, that we may know that you are gods; yes, do good or do evil, that we may be dismayed and see it together.
23_41_24: Indeed you are nothing, and your work is nothing; he who chooses you is an abomination.
23_41_25: I have raised up one from the north, and he shall come; from the rising of the sun he shall call on My name; and he shall come against princes as though mortar, as the potter treads clay.
23_41_26: Who has declared from the beginning, that we may know? And former times, that we may say, ‘He is righteous’? Surely there is no one who shows, surely there is no one who declares, surely there is no one who hears your words.
23_41_27: The first time I said to Zion, ‘Look, there they are!’ And I will give to Jerusalem one who brings good tidings.
23_41_28: For I looked, and there was no man; I looked among them, but there was no counselor, who, when I asked of them, could answer a word.
23_41_29: Indeed they are all worthless; their works are nothing; their molded images are wind and confusion.
23_42_1: Behold! My Servant whom I uphold, My Elect One in whom My soul delights! I have put My Spirit upon Him; He will bring forth justice to the Gentiles.
23_42_2: He will not cry out, nor raise His voice, nor cause His voice to be heard in the street.
23_42_3: A bruised reed He will not break, and smoking flax He will not quench; He will bring forth justice for truth.
23_42_4: He will not fail nor be discouraged, till He has established justice in the earth; and the coastlands shall wait for His law.”
23_42_5: Thus says God the LORD, who created the heavens and stretched them out, who spread forth the earth and that which comes from it, who gives breath to the people on it, and spirit to those who walk on it:
23_42_6: I, the LORD, have called You in righteousness, and will hold Your hand; I will keep You and give You as a covenant to the people, as a light to the Gentiles,
23_42_7: To open blind eyes, to bring out prisoners from the prison, those who sit in darkness from the prison house.
23_42_8: I am the LORD, that is My name; and My glory I will not give to another, nor My praise to carved images.
23_42_9: Behold, the former things have come to pass, and new things I declare; before they spring forth I tell you of them.”
23_42_10: Sing to the LORD a new song, and His praise from the ends of the earth, you who go down to the sea, and all that is in it, you coastlands and you inhabitants of them!
23_42_11: Let the wilderness and its cities lift up their voice, the villages that Kedar inhabits. Let the inhabitants of Sela sing, let them shout from the top of the mountains.
23_42_12: Let them give glory to the LORD, and declare His praise in the coastlands.
23_42_13: The LORD shall go forth like a mighty man; He shall stir up His zeal like a man of war. He shall cry out, yes, shout aloud; He shall prevail against His enemies.
23_42_14: I have held My peace a long time, I have been still and restrained Myself. Now I will cry like a woman in labor, I will pant and gasp at once.
23_42_15: I will lay waste the mountains and hills, and dry up all their vegetation; I will make the rivers into coastlands, and I will dry up the pools.
23_42_16: I will bring the blind by a way they did not know; I will lead them in paths they have not known. I will make darkness light before them, and crooked places straight. These things I will do for them, and not forsake them.
23_42_17: They shall be turned back, they shall be utterly ashamed, who trust in carved images, who say to the molded images, ‘You are our gods.’
23_42_18: Hear, you deaf; and look, you blind, that you may see.
23_42_19: Who is blind but My servant, or deaf as My messenger whom I send? Who is blind as he who is perfect, and blind as the LORD’s servant?
23_42_20: Seeing many things, but you do not observe; opening the ears, but he does not hear.
23_42_21: The LORD is well pleased for His righteousness’ sake; He will exalt the law and make it glorious.
23_42_22: But this is a people robbed and plundered; all of them are snared in holes, and they are hidden in prison houses; they are for prey, and no one delivers; for plunder, and no one says, “Restore!”
23_42_23: Who among you will give ear to this? Who will listen and hear for the time to come?
23_42_24: Who gave Jacob for plunder, and Israel to the robbers? Was it not the LORD, He against whom we have sinned? For they would not walk in His ways, nor were they obedient to His law.
23_42_25: Therefore He has poured on him the fury of His anger and the strength of battle; it has set him on fire all around, yet he did not know; and it burned him, yet he did not take it to heart.
23_43_1: But now, thus says the LORD, who created you, O Jacob, and He who formed you, O Israel: “Fear not, for I have redeemed you; I have called you by your name; you are Mine.
23_43_2: When you pass through the waters, I will be with you; and through the rivers, they shall not overflow you. When you walk through the fire, you shall not be burned, nor shall the flame scorch you.
23_43_3: For I am the LORD your God, the Holy One of Israel, your Savior; I gave Egypt for your ransom, Ethiopia and Seba in your place.
23_43_4: Since you were precious in My sight, you have been honored, and I have loved you; therefore I will give men for you, and people for your life.
23_43_5: Fear not, for I am with you; I will bring your descendants from the east, and gather you from the west;
23_43_6: I will say to the north, ‘Give them up!’ and to the south, ‘Do not keep them back!’ Bring My sons from afar, and My daughters from the ends of the earth—
23_43_7: Everyone who is called by My name, whom I have created for My glory; I have formed him, yes, I have made him.
23_43_8: Bring out the blind people who have eyes, and the deaf who have ears.
23_43_9: Let all the nations be gathered together, and let the people be assembled. Who among them can declare this, and show us former things? Let them bring out their witnesses, that they may be justified; or let them hear and say, “It is truth.”
23_43_10: You are My witnesses,” says the LORD, “and My servant whom I have chosen, that you may know and believe Me, and understand that I am He. Before Me there was no God formed, nor shall there be after Me.
23_43_11: I, even I, am the LORD, and besides Me there is no savior.
23_43_12: I have declared and saved, I have proclaimed, and there was no foreign god among you; therefore you are My witnesses,” says the LORD, “that I am God.
23_43_13: Indeed before the day was, I am He; and there is no one who can deliver out of My hand; I work, and who will reverse it?
23_43_14: Thus says the LORD, your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: “For your sake I will send to Babylon, and bring them all down as fugitives— the Chaldeans, who rejoice in their ships.
23_43_15: I am the LORD, your Holy One, the Creator of Israel, your King.
23_43_16: Thus says the LORD, who makes a way in the sea and a path through the mighty waters,
23_43_17: Who brings forth the chariot and horse, the army and the power (they shall lie down together, they shall not rise; they are extinguished, they are quenched like a wick):
23_43_18: Do not remember the former things, nor consider the things of old.
23_43_19: Behold, I will do a new thing, now it shall spring forth; shall you not know it? I will even make a road in the wilderness and rivers in the desert.
23_43_20: The beast of the field will honor Me, the jackals and the ostriches, because I give waters in the wilderness and rivers in the desert, to give drink to My people, My chosen.
23_43_21: This people I have formed for Myself; they shall declare My praise.
23_43_22: But you have not called upon Me, O Jacob; and you have been weary of Me, O Israel.
23_43_23: You have not brought Me the sheep for your burnt offerings, nor have you honored Me with your sacrifices. I have not caused you to serve with grain offerings, nor wearied you with incense.
23_43_24: You have bought Me no sweet cane with money, nor have you satisfied Me with the fat of your sacrifices; but you have burdened Me with your sins, you have wearied Me with your iniquities.
23_43_25: I, even I, am He who blots out your transgressions for My own sake; and I will not remember your sins.
23_43_26: Put Me in remembrance; let us contend together; state your case, that you may be acquitted.
23_43_27: Your first father sinned, and your mediators have transgressed against Me.
23_43_28: Therefore I will profane the princes of the sanctuary; I will give Jacob to the curse, and Israel to reproaches.
23_44_1: Yet hear now, O Jacob My servant, and Israel whom I have chosen.
23_44_2: Thus says the LORD who made you and formed you from the womb, who will help you: Fear not, O Jacob My servant; and you, Jeshurun, whom I have chosen.
23_44_3: For I will pour water on him who is thirsty, and streams on the dry ground; I will pour My Spirit on your descendants, and My blessing on your offspring;
23_44_4: They will spring up among the grass like willows by the watercourses.
23_44_5: One will say, ‘I am the LORD’s’; another will call himself by the name of Jacob; another will write with his hand, ‘The LORD’s,’ and name himself by the name of Israel.
23_44_6: Thus says the LORD, the King of Israel, and his Redeemer, the LORD of hosts: ‘I am the First and I am the Last; besides Me there is no God.
23_44_7: And who can proclaim as I do? Then let him declare it and set it in order for Me, since I appointed the ancient people. And the things that are coming and shall come, let them show these to them.
23_44_8: Do not fear, nor be afraid; have I not told you from that time, and declared it? You are My witnesses. Is there a God besides Me? Indeed there is no other Rock; I know not one.’
23_44_9: Those who make an image, all of them are useless, and their precious things shall not profit; they are their own witnesses; they neither see nor know, that they may be ashamed.
23_44_10: Who would form a god or mold an image that profits him nothing?
23_44_11: Surely all his companions would be ashamed; and the workmen, they are mere men. Let them all be gathered together, let them stand up; yet they shall fear, they shall be ashamed together.
23_44_12: The blacksmith with the tongs works one in the coals, fashions it with hammers, and works it with the strength of his arms. Even so, he is hungry, and his strength fails; he drinks no water and is faint.
23_44_13: The craftsman stretches out his rule, he marks one out with chalk; he fashions it with a plane, he marks it out with the compass, and makes it like the figure of a man, according to the beauty of a man, that it may remain in the house.
23_44_14: He cuts down cedars for himself, and takes the cypress and the oak; he secures it for himself among the trees of the forest. He plants a pine, and the rain nourishes it.
23_44_15: Then it shall be for a man to burn, for he will take some of it and warm himself; yes, he kindles it and bakes bread; indeed he makes a god and worships it; he makes it a carved image, and falls down to it.
23_44_16: He burns half of it in the fire; with this half he eats meat; he roasts a roast, and is satisfied. He even warms himself and says, “Ah! I am warm, I have seen the fire.”
23_44_17: And the rest of it he makes into a god, his carved image. He falls down before it and worships it, prays to it and says, “Deliver me, for you are my god!”
23_44_18: They do not know nor understand; for He has shut their eyes, so that they cannot see, and their hearts, so that they cannot understand.
23_44_19: And no one considers in his heart, nor is there knowledge nor understanding to say, “I have burned half of it in the fire, yes, I have also baked bread on its coals; I have roasted meat and eaten it; and shall I make the rest of it an abomination? Shall I fall down before a block of wood?”
23_44_20: He feeds on ashes; a deceived heart has turned him aside; and he cannot deliver his soul, nor say, “Is there not a lie in my right hand?”
23_44_21: Remember these, O Jacob, and Israel, for you are My servant; I have formed you, you are My servant; O Israel, you will not be forgotten by Me!
23_44_22: I have blotted out, like a thick cloud, your transgressions, and like a cloud, your sins. Return to Me, for I have redeemed you.
23_44_23: Sing, O heavens, for the LORD has done it! Shout, you lower parts of the earth; break forth into singing, you mountains, O forest, and every tree in it! For the LORD has redeemed Jacob, and glorified Himself in Israel.
23_44_24: Thus says the LORD, your Redeemer, and He who formed you from the womb: “I am the LORD, who makes all things, who stretches out the heavens all alone, who spreads abroad the earth by Myself;
23_44_25: Who frustrates the signs of the babblers, and drives diviners mad; who turns wise men backward, and makes their knowledge foolishness;
23_44_26: Who confirms the word of His servant, and performs the counsel of His messengers; who says to Jerusalem, ‘You shall be inhabited,’ to the cities of Judah, ‘You shall be built,’ and I will raise up her waste places;
23_44_27: Who says to the deep, ‘Be dry! And I will dry up your rivers’;
23_44_28: Who says of Cyrus, ‘He is My shepherd, and he shall perform all My pleasure, saying to Jerusalem, “You shall be built,” and to the temple, “Your foundation shall be laid.” ’
23_45_1: Thus says the LORD to His anointed, to Cyrus, whose right hand I have held— to subdue nations before him and loose the armor of kings, to open before him the double doors, so that the gates will not be shut:
23_45_2: I will go before you and make the crooked places straight; I will break in pieces the gates of bronze and cut the bars of iron.
23_45_3: I will give you the treasures of darkness and hidden riches of secret places, that you may know that I, the LORD, who call you by your name, am the God of Israel.
23_45_4: For Jacob My servant’s sake, and Israel My elect, I have even called you by your name; I have named you, though you have not known Me.
23_45_5: I am the LORD, and there is no other; there is no God besides Me. I will gird you, though you have not known Me,
23_45_6: That they may know from the rising of the sun to its setting that there is none besides Me. I am the LORD, and there is no other;
23_45_7: I form the light and create darkness, I make peace and create calamity; I, the LORD, do all these things.
23_45_8: Rain down, you heavens, from above, and let the skies pour down righteousness; let the earth open, let them bring forth salvation, and let righteousness spring up together. I, the LORD, have created it.
23_45_9: Woe to him who strives with his Maker! Let the potsherd strive with the potsherds of the earth! Shall the clay say to him who forms it, ‘What are you making?’ Or shall your handiwork say, ‘He has no hands’?
23_45_10: Woe to him who says to his father, ‘What are you begetting?’ Or to the woman, ‘What have you brought forth?’
23_45_11: Thus says the LORD, The Holy One of Israel, and his Maker: “Ask Me of things to come concerning My sons; and concerning the work of My hands, you command Me.
23_45_12: I have made the earth, and created man on it. I—My hands—stretched out the heavens, and all their host I have commanded.
23_45_13: I have raised him up in righteousness, and I will direct all his ways; he shall build My city and let My exiles go free, not for price nor reward,” says the LORD of hosts.
23_45_14: Thus says the LORD:  “The labor of Egypt and merchandise of Cush And of the Sabeans, men of stature, Shall come over to you, and they shall be yours; They shall walk behind you, They shall come over in chains; And they shall bow down to you. They will make supplication to you, saying, ‘Surely God is in you, And there is no other;  There is no other God.’ ”
23_45_15: Truly You are God, who hide Yourself, O God of Israel, the Savior!
23_45_16: They shall be ashamed And also disgraced, all of them; They shall go in confusion together, Who are makers of idols.
23_45_17: But Israel shall be saved by the LORD With an everlasting salvation; You shall not be ashamed or disgraced Forever and ever.
23_45_18: For thus says the LORD,  Who created the heavens, Who is God, Who formed the earth and made it, Who has established it, Who did not create it in vain, Who formed it to be inhabited:  “I am the LORD, and there is no other.
23_45_19: I have not spoken in secret, In a dark place of the earth; I did not say to the seed of Jacob, ‘Seek Me in vain’;  I, the LORD, speak righteousness, I declare things that are right.
23_45_20: “Assemble yourselves and come; Draw near together, You who have escaped from the nations.  They have no knowledge, Who carry the wood of their carved image, And pray to a god that cannot save.
23_45_21: Tell and bring forth your case; Yes, let them take counsel together.  Who has declared this from ancient time? Who has told it from that time? Have not I, the LORD?  And there is no other God besides Me, A just God and a Savior; There is none besides Me.
23_45_22: “Look to Me, and be saved,  All you ends of the earth! For I am God, and there is no other.
23_45_23: I have sworn by Myself; The word has gone out of My mouth in righteousness, And shall not return, That to Me every knee shall bow,  Every tongue shall take an oath.
23_45_24: He shall say, ‘Surely in the LORD I have righteousness and strength. To Him men shall come, And all shall be ashamed Who are incensed against Him.
23_45_25: In the LORD all the descendants of Israel Shall be justified, and shall glory.’ ”
23_46_1: Bel bows down, Nebo stoops; Their idols were on the beasts and on the cattle. Your carriages were heavily loaded,  A burden to the weary beast.
23_46_2: They stoop, they bow down together; They could not deliver the burden,  But have themselves gone into captivity.
23_46_3: “Listen to Me, O house of Jacob, And all the remnant of the house of Israel,  Who have been upheld by Me from birth, Who have been carried from the womb:
23_46_4: Even to your old age, I am He, And even to gray hairs I will carry you! I have made, and I will bear; Even I will carry, and will deliver you.
23_46_5: “To  whom will you liken Me, and make Me equal And compare Me, that we should be alike?
23_46_6: They lavish gold out of the bag, And weigh silver on the scales; They hire a goldsmith, and he makes it a god; They prostrate themselves, yes, they worship.
23_46_7: They bear it on the shoulder, they carry it And set it in its place, and it stands; From its place it shall not move. Though one cries out to it, yet it cannot answer Nor save him out of his trouble.
23_46_8: “Remember this, and show yourselves men;  Recall to mind, O you transgressors.
23_46_9: Remember the former things of old, For I am God, and there is no other; I am God, and there is none like Me,
23_46_10: Declaring the end from the beginning, And from ancient times things that are not yet done, Saying, ‘My counsel shall stand, And I will do all My pleasure,’
23_46_11: Calling a bird of prey from the east, The man who executes My counsel, from a far country. Indeed I have spoken it; I will also bring it to pass. I have purposed it; I will also do it.
23_46_12: “Listen to Me, you stubborn-hearted,  Who are far from righteousness:
23_46_13: I bring My righteousness near, it shall not be far off; My salvation shall not linger. And I will place salvation in Zion, For Israel My glory.
23_47_1: “Come down and sit in the dust, O virgin daughter of Babylon; Sit on the ground without a throne, O daughter of the Chaldeans! For you shall no more be called Tender and delicate.
23_47_2: Take the millstones and grind meal. Remove your veil, Take off the skirt, Uncover the thigh, Pass through the rivers.
23_47_3: Your nakedness shall be uncovered, Yes, your shame will be seen;  I will take vengeance, And I will not arbitrate with a man.”
23_47_4: As for our Redeemer, the LORD of hosts is His name, The Holy One of Israel.
23_47_5: “Sit in silence, and go into darkness, O daughter of the Chaldeans;  For you shall no longer be called The Lady of Kingdoms.
23_47_6: I was angry with My people;  I have profaned My inheritance, And given them into your hand. You showed them no mercy;  On the elderly you laid your yoke very heavily.
23_47_7: And you said, ‘I shall be a lady forever,’ So that you did not take these things to heart,  Nor remember the latter end of them.
23_47_8: “Therefore hear this now, you who are given to pleasures, Who dwell securely, Who say in your heart, ‘I am, and there is no one else besides me; I shall not sit as a widow, Nor shall I know the loss of children’;
23_47_9: But these two things shall come to you  In a moment, in one day: The loss of children, and widowhood. They shall come upon you in their fullness Because of the multitude of your sorceries, For the great abundance of your enchantments.
23_47_10: “For you have trusted in your wickedness; You have said, ‘No one sees me’; Your wisdom and your knowledge have warped you; And you have said in your heart, ‘I am, and there is no one else besides me.’
23_47_11: Therefore evil shall come upon you; You shall not know from where it arises. And trouble shall fall upon you; You will not be able to put it off. And desolation shall come upon you suddenly, Which you shall not know.
23_47_12: “Stand now with your enchantments And the multitude of your sorceries, In which you have labored from your youth— Perhaps you will be able to profit, Perhaps you will prevail.
23_47_13: You are wearied in the multitude of your counsels; Let now the astrologers, the stargazers, And the monthly prognosticators Stand up and save you From what shall come upon you.
23_47_14: Behold, they shall be as stubble, The fire shall burn them; They shall not deliver themselves From the power of the flame; It shall not be a coal to be warmed by, Nor a fire to sit before!
23_47_15: Thus shall they be to you With whom you have labored,  Your merchants from your youth; They shall wander each one to his quarter. No one shall save you.
23_48_1: “Hear this, O house of Jacob, Who are called by the name of Israel, And have come forth from the wellsprings of Judah; Who swear by the name of the LORD, And make mention of the God of Israel, But not in truth or in righteousness;
23_48_2: For they call themselves after the holy city, And lean on the God of Israel; The LORD of hosts is His name:
23_48_3: “I have declared the former things from the beginning; They went forth from My mouth, and I caused them to hear it. Suddenly I did them, and they came to pass.
23_48_4: Because I knew that you were obstinate, And your neck was an iron sinew, And your brow bronze,
23_48_5: Even from the beginning I have declared it to you; Before it came to pass I proclaimed it to you, Lest you should say, ‘My idol has done them, And my carved image and my molded image Have commanded them.’
23_48_6: “You have heard; See all this. And will you not declare it? I have made you hear new things from this time, Even hidden things, and you did not know them.
23_48_7: They are created now and not from the beginning; And before this day you have not heard them, Lest you should say, ‘Of course I knew them.’
23_48_8: Surely you did not hear, Surely you did not know; Surely from long ago your ear was not opened. For I knew that you would deal very treacherously, And were called a transgressor from the womb.
23_48_9: “For My name’s sake I will defer My anger, And for My praise I will restrain it from you, So that I do not cut you off.
23_48_10: Behold, I have refined you, but not as silver; I have tested you in the furnace of affliction.
23_48_11: For My own sake, for My own sake, I will do it; For how should My name be profaned? And I will not give My glory to another.
23_48_12: “Listen to Me, O Jacob, And Israel, My called: I am He, I am the First, I am also the Last.
23_48_13: Indeed My hand has laid the foundation of the earth, And My right hand has stretched out the heavens; When I call to them, They stand up together.
23_48_14: “All of you, assemble yourselves, and hear! Who among them has declared these things? The LORD loves him; He shall do His pleasure on Babylon, And His arm shall be against the Chaldeans.
23_48_15: I, even I, have spoken; Yes, I have called him, I have brought him, and his way will prosper.
23_48_16: “Come near to Me, hear this: I have not spoken in secret from the beginning; From the time that it was, I was there. And now the Lord GOD and His Spirit Have sent Me.”
23_48_17: Thus says the LORD, your Redeemer, The Holy One of Israel: “I am the LORD your God, Who teaches you to profit, Who leads you by the way you should go.
23_48_18: Oh, that you had heeded My commandments! Then your peace would have been like a river, And your righteousness like the waves of the sea.
23_48_19: Your descendants also would have been like the sand, And the offspring of your body like the grains of sand; His name would not have been cut off Nor destroyed from before Me.”
23_48_20: Go forth from Babylon! Flee from the Chaldeans! With a voice of singing, Declare, proclaim this, Utter it to the end of the earth; Say, “The LORD has redeemed His servant Jacob!”
23_48_21: And they did not thirst When He led them through the deserts; He caused the waters to flow from the rock for them; He also split the rock, and the waters gushed out.
23_48_22: “There is no peace,” says the LORD, “for the wicked.”
23_49_1: “Listen, O coastlands, to Me, And take heed, you peoples from afar! The LORD has called Me from the womb; From the matrix of My mother He has made mention of My name.
23_49_2: And He has made My mouth like a sharp sword; In the shadow of His hand He has hidden Me, And made Me a polished shaft; In His quiver He has hidden Me.”
23_49_3: “And He said to me, ‘You are My servant, O Israel, In whom I will be glorified.’
23_49_4: Then I said, ‘I have labored in vain, I have spent my strength for nothing and in vain; Yet surely my just reward is with the LORD, And my work with my God.’ ”
23_49_5: “And now the LORD says, Who formed Me from the womb to be His Servant, To bring Jacob back to Him, So that Israel is gathered to Him (For I shall be glorious in the eyes of the LORD, And My God shall be My strength),
23_49_6: Indeed He says, ‘It is too small a thing that You should be My Servant To raise up the tribes of Jacob, And to restore the preserved ones of Israel; I will also give You as a light to the Gentiles, That You should be My salvation to the ends of the earth.’ ”
23_49_7: Thus says the LORD, The Redeemer of Israel, their Holy One, To Him whom man despises, To Him whom the nation abhors, To the Servant of rulers: “Kings shall see and arise, Princes also shall worship, Because of the LORD who is faithful, The Holy One of Israel; And He has chosen You.”
23_49_8: Thus says the LORD: “In an acceptable time I have heard You, And in the day of salvation I have helped You; I will preserve You and give You As a covenant to the people, To restore the earth, To cause them to inherit the desolate heritages;
23_49_9: That You may say to the prisoners, ‘Go forth,’ To those who are in darkness, ‘Show yourselves.’ “They shall feed along the roads, And their pastures shall be on all desolate heights.
23_49_10: They shall neither hunger nor thirst, Neither heat nor sun shall strike them; For He who has mercy on them will lead them, Even by the springs of water He will guide them.
23_49_11: I will make each of My mountains a road, And My highways shall be elevated.
23_49_12: Surely these shall come from afar; Look! Those from the north and the west, And these from the land of Sinim.”
23_49_13: Sing, O heavens! Be joyful, O earth! And break out in singing, O mountains! For the LORD has comforted His people, And will have mercy on His afflicted.
23_49_14: But Zion said, “The LORD has forsaken me, And my Lord has forgotten me.”
23_49_15: “Can a woman forget her nursing child, And not have compassion on the son of her womb? Surely they may forget, Yet I will not forget you.
23_49_16: See, I have inscribed you on the palms of My hands; Your walls are continually before Me.
23_49_17: Your sons shall make haste; Your destroyers and those who laid you waste Shall go away from you.
23_49_18: Lift up your eyes, look around and see; All these gather together and come to you. As I live,” says the LORD, “You shall surely clothe yourselves with them all as an ornament, And bind them on you as a bride does.
23_49_19: For your waste and desolate places, and the land of your destruction, will even now be too small for the inhabitants; and those who swallowed you up will be far away.
23_49_20: The children you will have, after you have lost the others, will say again in your ears, ‘The place is too small for me; give me a place where I may dwell.’
23_49_21: Then you will say in your heart, ‘Who has begotten these for me, since I have lost my children and am desolate, a captive, and wandering to and fro? And who has brought these up? There I was, left alone; but these, where were they?’
23_49_22: Thus says the Lord GOD: “Behold, I will lift My hand in an oath to the nations, and set up My standard for the peoples; they shall bring your sons in their arms, and your daughters shall be carried on their shoulders;
23_49_23: Kings shall be your foster fathers, and their queens your nursing mothers; they shall bow down to you with their faces to the earth, and lick up the dust of your feet. Then you will know that I am the LORD, for they shall not be ashamed who wait for Me.”
23_49_24: Shall the prey be taken from the mighty, or the captives of a tyrant be delivered?
23_49_25: But thus says the LORD: “Even the captives of the mighty shall be taken away, and the prey of the terrible be delivered; for I will contend with him who contends with you, and I will save your children.
23_49_26: I will feed those who oppress you with their own flesh, and they shall be drunk with their own blood as with sweet wine. All flesh shall know that I, the LORD, am your Savior, and your Redeemer, the Mighty One of Jacob.”
23_50_1: Thus says the LORD: “Where is the certificate of your mother’s divorce, whom I have put away? Or which of My creditors is it to whom I have sold you? For your iniquities you have sold yourselves, and for your transgressions your mother has been put away.
23_50_2: Why, when I came, was there no man? Why, when I called, was there none to answer? Is My hand shortened at all that it cannot redeem? Or have I no power to deliver? Indeed with My rebuke I dry up the sea, I make the rivers a wilderness; their fish stink because there is no water, and die of thirst.
23_50_3: I clothe the heavens with blackness, and I make sackcloth their covering.”
23_50_4: The Lord GOD has given Me the tongue of the learned, that I should know how to speak a word in season to him who is weary. He awakens Me morning by morning, He awakens My ear to hear as the learned.
23_50_5: The Lord GOD has opened My ear; and I was not rebellious, nor did I turn away.
23_50_6: I gave My back to those who struck Me, and My cheeks to those who plucked out the beard; I did not hide My face from shame and spitting.
23_50_7: For the Lord GOD will help Me; therefore I will not be disgraced; therefore I have set My face like a flint, and I know that I will not be ashamed.
23_50_8: He is near who justifies Me; who will contend with Me? Let us stand together. Who is My adversary? Let him come near Me.
23_50_9: Surely the Lord GOD will help Me; who is he who will condemn Me? Indeed they will all grow old like a garment; the moth will eat them up.
23_50_10: Who among you fears the LORD? Who obeys the voice of His Servant? Who walks in darkness and has no light? Let him trust in the name of the LORD and rely upon his God.
23_50_11: Look, all you who kindle a fire, who encircle yourselves with sparks: walk in the light of your fire and in the sparks you have kindled— this you shall have from My hand: you shall lie down in torment.
23_51_1: Listen to Me, you who follow after righteousness, you who seek the LORD: look to the rock from which you were hewn, and to the hole of the pit from which you were dug.
23_51_2: Look to Abraham your father, and to Sarah who bore you; for I called him alone, and blessed him and increased him.”
23_51_3: For the LORD will comfort Zion, He will comfort all her waste places; He will make her wilderness like Eden, and her desert like the garden of the LORD; joy and gladness will be found in it, thanksgiving and the voice of melody.
23_51_4: Listen to Me, My people; and give ear to Me, O My nation: for law will proceed from Me, and I will make My justice rest as a light of the peoples.
23_51_5: My righteousness is near, My salvation has gone forth, and My arms will judge the peoples; the coastlands will wait upon Me, and on My arm they will trust.
23_51_6: Lift up your eyes to the heavens, and look on the earth beneath. For the heavens will vanish away like smoke, the earth will grow old like a garment, and those who dwell in it will die in like manner; but My salvation will be forever, and My righteousness will not be abolished.
23_51_7: Listen to Me, you who know righteousness, you people in whose heart is My law: do not fear the reproach of men, nor be afraid of their insults.
23_51_8: For the moth will eat them up like a garment, and the worm will eat them like wool; but My righteousness will be forever, and My salvation from generation to generation.”
23_51_9: Awake, awake, put on strength, O arm of the LORD! Awake as in the ancient days, in the generations of old. Are You not the arm that cut Rahab apart, and wounded the serpent?
23_51_10: Are You not the One who dried up the sea, the waters of the great deep; that made the depths of the sea a road for the redeemed to cross over?
23_51_11: So the ransomed of the LORD shall return, and come to Zion with singing, with everlasting joy on their heads. They shall obtain joy and gladness; sorrow and sighing shall flee away.
23_51_12: I, even I, am He who comforts you. Who are you that you should be afraid of a man who will die, and of the son of a man who will be made like grass?
23_51_13: And you forget the LORD your Maker, who stretched out the heavens and laid the foundations of the earth; you have feared continually every day because of the fury of the oppressor, when he has prepared to destroy. And where is the fury of the oppressor?
23_51_14: The captive exile hastens, that he may be loosed, that he should not die in the pit, and that his bread should not fail.
23_51_15: But I am the LORD your God, who divided the sea whose waves roared— the LORD of hosts is His name.
23_51_16: And I have put My words in your mouth; I have covered you with the shadow of My hand, that I may plant the heavens, lay the foundations of the earth, and say to Zion, ‘You are My people.’”
23_51_17: Awake, awake! Stand up, O Jerusalem, you who have drunk at the hand of the LORD the cup of His fury; you have drunk the dregs of the cup of trembling, and drained it out.
23_51_18: There is no one to guide her among all the sons she has brought forth; nor is there any who takes her by the hand among all the sons she has brought up.
23_51_19: These two things have come to you; who will be sorry for you?— desolation and destruction, famine and sword— by whom will I comfort you?
23_51_20: Your sons have fainted, they lie at the head of all the streets, like an antelope in a net; they are full of the fury of the LORD, the rebuke of your God.
23_51_21: Therefore please hear this, you afflicted, and drunk but not with wine.
23_51_22: Thus says your Lord, The LORD and your God, Who pleads the cause of His people: “See, I have taken out of your hand The cup of trembling, The dregs of the cup of My fury; You shall no longer drink it.
23_51_23: But I will put it into the hand of those who afflict you, Who have said to you, ‘Lie down, that we may walk over you.’ And you have laid your body like the ground, And as the street, for those who walk over.”
23_52_1: Awake, awake! Put on your strength, O Zion; Put on your beautiful garments, O Jerusalem, the holy city! For the uncircumcised and the unclean Shall no longer come to you.
23_52_2: Shake yourself from the dust, arise; Sit down, O Jerusalem!  Loose yourself from the bonds of your neck, O captive daughter of Zion!
23_52_3: For thus says the LORD:  “You have sold yourselves for nothing, And you shall be redeemed without money.”
23_52_4: For thus says the Lord GOD: “My people went down at first Into Egypt to dwell there; Then the Assyrian oppressed them without cause.
23_52_5: Now therefore, what have I here,” says the LORD, “That My people are taken away for nothing? Those who rule over them Make them wail,” says the LORD, “And My name is blasphemed continually every day.
23_52_6: Therefore My people shall know My name; Therefore they shall know in that day That I am He who speaks: ‘Behold, it is I.’ ”
23_52_7: How beautiful upon the mountains Are the feet of him who brings good news, Who proclaims peace, Who brings glad tidings of good things, Who proclaims salvation, Who says to Zion,  “Your God reigns!”
23_52_8: Your watchmen shall lift up their voices, With their voices they shall sing together; For they shall see eye to eye When the LORD brings back Zion.
23_52_9: Break forth into joy, sing together, You waste places of Jerusalem! For the LORD has comforted His people, He has redeemed Jerusalem.
23_52_10: The LORD has made bare His holy arm In the eyes of all the nations; And all the ends of the earth shall see The salvation of our God.
23_52_11: Depart! Depart! Go out from there, Touch no unclean thing; Go out from the midst of her,  Be clean, You who bear the vessels of the LORD.
23_52_12: For you shall not go out with haste, Nor go by flight;  For the LORD will go before you,  And the God of Israel will be your rear guard.
23_52_13: Behold, My Servant shall deal prudently;  He shall be exalted and extolled and be very high.
23_52_14: Just as many were astonished at you, So His visage was marred more than any man, And His form more than the sons of men;
23_52_15: So shall He sprinkle many nations. Kings shall shut their mouths at Him; For what had not been told them they shall see, And what they had not heard they shall consider.
23_53_1: Who has believed our report? And to whom has the arm of the LORD been revealed?
23_53_2: For He shall grow up before Him as a tender plant, And as a root out of dry ground. He has no form or comeliness; And when we see Him, There is no beauty that we should desire Him.
23_53_3: He is despised and rejected by men, A Man of sorrows and acquainted with grief. And we hid, as it were, our faces from Him; He was despised, and we did not esteem Him.
23_53_4: Surely He has borne our griefs And carried our sorrows; Yet we esteemed Him stricken, Smitten by God, and afflicted.
23_53_5: But He was wounded for our transgressions, He was bruised for our iniquities; The chastisement for our peace was upon Him, And by His stripes we are healed.
23_53_6: All we like sheep have gone astray; We have turned, every one, to his own way; And the LORD has laid on Him the iniquity of us all.
23_53_7: He was oppressed and He was afflicted, Yet He opened not His mouth;  He was led as a lamb to the slaughter, And as a sheep before its shearers is silent, So He opened not His mouth.
23_53_8: He was taken from prison and from judgment, And who will declare His generation? For He was cut off from the land of the living; For the transgressions of My people He was stricken.
23_53_9: And they made His grave with the wicked— But with the rich at His death, Because He had done no violence, Nor was any deceit in His mouth.
23_53_10: Yet it pleased the LORD to bruise Him; He has put Him to grief. When You make His soul an offering for sin, He shall see His seed, He shall prolong His days, And the pleasure of the LORD shall prosper in His hand.
23_53_11: He shall see the labor of His soul, and be satisfied. By His knowledge My righteous Servant shall justify many, For He shall bear their iniquities.
23_53_12: Therefore I will divide Him a portion with the great,  And He shall divide the spoil with the strong, Because He poured out His soul unto death, And He was numbered with the transgressors, And He bore the sin of many, And made intercession for the transgressors.
23_54_1: “Sing, O barren, You who have not borne! Break forth into singing, and cry aloud, You who have not labored with child! For more are the children of the desolate Than the children of the married woman,” says the LORD.
23_54_2: “Enlarge  the place of your tent, And let them stretch out the curtains of your dwellings; Do not spare; Lengthen your cords, And strengthen your stakes.
23_54_3: For you shall expand to the right and to the left, And your descendants will inherit the nations, And make the desolate cities inhabited.
23_54_4: “Do  not fear, for you will not be ashamed; Neither be disgraced, for you will not be put to shame; For you will forget the shame of your youth, And will not remember the reproach of your widowhood anymore.
23_54_5: For your Maker is your husband, The LORD of hosts is His name; And your Redeemer is the Holy One of Israel; He is called the God of the whole earth.
23_54_6: For the LORD has called you Like a woman forsaken and grieved in spirit, Like a youthful wife when you were refused,” Says your God.
23_54_7: “For  a mere moment I have forsaken you, But with great mercies I will gather you.
23_54_8: With a little wrath I hid My face from you for a moment;  But with everlasting kindness I will have mercy on you,” Says the LORD, your Redeemer.
23_54_9: “For this is like the waters of Noah to Me; For as I have sworn That the waters of Noah would no longer cover the earth, So have I sworn That I would not be angry with you, nor rebuke you.
23_54_10: For the mountains shall depart And the hills be removed,  But My kindness shall not depart from you, Nor shall My covenant of peace be removed,” Says the LORD, who has mercy on you.
23_54_11: “O you afflicted one, Tossed with tempest, and not comforted, Behold, I will lay your stones with colorful gems, And lay your foundations with sapphires.
23_54_12: I will make your pinnacles of rubies, your gates of crystal, and all your walls of precious stones.
23_54_13: All your children shall be taught by the LORD, and great shall be the peace of your children.
23_54_14: In righteousness you shall be established; you shall be far from oppression, for you shall not fear; and from terror, for it shall not come near you.
23_54_15: Indeed they shall surely assemble, but not because of Me. Whoever assembles against you shall fall for your sake.
23_54_16: Behold, I have created the blacksmith who blows the coals in the fire, who brings forth an instrument for his work; and I have created the spoiler to destroy.
23_54_17: No weapon formed against you shall prosper, and every tongue which rises against you in judgment you shall condemn. This is the heritage of the servants of the LORD, and their righteousness is from Me,” says the LORD.
23_55_1: Ho! Everyone who thirsts, come to the waters; and you who have no money, come, buy and eat. Yes, come, buy wine and milk without money and without price.
23_55_2: Why do you spend money for what is not bread, and your wages for what does not satisfy? Listen carefully to Me, and eat what is good, and let your soul delight itself in abundance.
23_55_3: Incline your ear, and come to Me. Hear, and your soul shall live; and I will make an everlasting covenant with you— the sure mercies of David.
23_55_4: Indeed I have given him as a witness to the people, a leader and commander for the people.
23_55_5: Surely you shall call a nation you do not know, and nations who do not know you shall run to you, because of the LORD your God, and the Holy One of Israel; for He has glorified you.
23_55_6: Seek the LORD while He may be found, call upon Him while He is near.
23_55_7: Let the wicked forsake his way, and the unrighteous man his thoughts; let him return to the LORD, and He will have mercy on him; and to our God, for He will abundantly pardon.
23_55_8: For My thoughts are not your thoughts, nor are your ways My ways,” says the LORD.
23_55_9: For as the heavens are higher than the earth, so are My ways higher than your ways, and My thoughts than your thoughts.
23_55_10: For as the rain comes down, and the snow from heaven, and do not return there, but water the earth, and make it bring forth and bud, that it may give seed to the sower and bread to the eater,
23_55_11: So shall My word be that goes forth from My mouth; it shall not return to Me void, but it shall accomplish what I please, and it shall prosper in the thing for which I sent it.
23_55_12: For you shall go out with joy, and be led out with peace; the mountains and the hills shall break forth into singing before you, and all the trees of the field shall clap their hands.
23_55_13: Instead of the thorn shall come up the cypress tree, and instead of the brier shall come up the myrtle tree; and it shall be to the LORD for a name, for an everlasting sign that shall not be cut off.
23_56_1: Thus says the LORD: “Keep justice, and do righteousness, for My salvation is about to come, and My righteousness to be revealed.
23_56_2: Blessed is the man who does this, and the son of man who lays hold on it; who keeps from defiling the Sabbath, and keeps his hand from doing any evil.”
23_56_3: Do not let the son of the foreigner who has joined himself to the LORD speak, saying, “The LORD has utterly separated me from His people”; nor let the eunuch say, “Here I am, a dry tree.”
23_56_4: For thus says the LORD: “To the eunuchs who keep My Sabbaths, and choose what pleases Me, and hold fast My covenant,
23_56_5: Even to them I will give in My house and within My walls a place and a name better than that of sons and daughters; I will give them an everlasting name that shall not be cut off.
23_56_6: Also the sons of the foreigner who join themselves to the LORD, to serve Him, and to love the name of the LORD, to be His servants— everyone who keeps from defiling the Sabbath, and holds fast My covenant—
23_56_7: Even them I will bring to My holy mountain, and make them joyful in My house of prayer. Their burnt offerings and their sacrifices will be accepted on My altar; for My house shall be called a house of prayer for all nations.”
23_56_8: The Lord GOD, who gathers the outcasts of Israel, says, “Yet I will gather to him others besides those who are gathered to him.”
23_56_9: All you beasts of the field, come to devour, all you beasts in the forest.
23_56_10: His watchmen are blind, they are all ignorant; they are all dumb dogs, they cannot bark; sleeping, lying down, loving to slumber.
23_56_11: Yes, they are greedy dogs which never have enough. And they are shepherds who cannot understand; they all look to their own way, every one for his own gain, from his own territory.
23_56_12: “Come,” one says, “I will bring wine, and we will fill ourselves with intoxicating drink; tomorrow will be as today, and much more abundant.”
23_57_1: The righteous perishes, and no man takes it to heart; merciful men are taken away, while no one considers that the righteous is taken away from evil.
23_57_2: He shall enter into peace; they shall rest in their beds, each one walking in his uprightness.
23_57_3: But come here, you sons of the sorceress, you offspring of the adulterer and the harlot!
23_57_4: Whom do you ridicule? Against whom do you make a wide mouth and stick out the tongue? Are you not children of transgression, offspring of falsehood,
23_57_5: Inflaming yourselves with gods under every green tree, slaying the children in the valleys, under the clefts of the rocks?
23_57_6: Among the smooth stones of the stream is your portion; they, they, are your lot! Even to them you have poured a drink offering, you have offered a grain offering. Should I receive comfort in these?
23_57_7: On a lofty and high mountain you have set your bed; even there you went up to offer sacrifice.
23_57_8: Also behind the doors and their posts you have set up your remembrance; for you have uncovered yourself to those other than Me, and have gone up to them; you have enlarged your bed and made a covenant with them; you have loved their bed, where you saw their nudity.
23_57_9: You went to the king with ointment, and increased your perfumes; you sent your messengers far off, and even descended to Sheol.
23_57_10: You are wearied in the length of your way; yet you did not say, ‘There is no hope.’ You found renewed strength for your hand; therefore you were not grieved.
23_57_11: And of whom have you been afraid, or feared, that you have lied and not remembered Me, nor taken it to your heart? Is it not because I have held My peace from of old that you do not fear Me?
23_57_12: I will declare your righteousness and your deeds, for they will not profit you.
23_57_13: When you cry out, let your collection of idols deliver you. But the wind will carry them all away, a breath will take them. But he who puts his trust in Me shall possess the land, and shall inherit My holy mountain.
23_57_14: And one shall say, “Heap it up! Heap it up! Prepare the way, take the stumbling block out of the way of My people.”
23_57_15: For thus says the High and Lofty One who inhabits eternity, whose name is Holy: “I dwell in the high and holy place, with him who has a contrite and humble spirit, to revive the spirit of the humble, and to revive the heart of the contrite ones.
23_57_16: For I will not contend forever, nor will I always be angry; for the spirit would grow faint before Me, and the souls which I have made.
23_57_17: For the iniquity of his covetousness I was angry and struck him; I hid and was angry, and he went on backsliding in the way of his heart.
23_57_18: I have seen his ways, and will heal him; I will also lead him, and restore comforts to him and to his mourners.
23_57_19: I create the fruit of the lips: Peace, peace to him who is far off and to him who is near,” says the LORD, “and I will heal him.”
23_57_20: But the wicked are like the troubled sea, when it cannot rest, whose waters cast up mire and dirt.
23_57_21: There is no peace,” says my God, “for the wicked.
23_58_1: Cry aloud, spare not; lift up your voice like a trumpet; tell My people their transgression, and the house of Jacob their sins.
23_58_2: Yet they seek Me daily, and delight to know My ways, as a nation that did righteousness, and did not forsake the ordinance of their God. They ask of Me the ordinances of justice; they take delight in approaching God.
23_58_3: ‘Why have we fasted,’ they say, ‘and You have not seen? Why have we afflicted our souls, and You take no notice?’ In fact, in the day of your fast you find pleasure, and exploit all your laborers.
23_58_4: Indeed you fast for strife and debate, and to strike with the fist of wickedness. You will not fast as you do this day, to make your voice heard on high.
23_58_5: Is it a fast that I have chosen, a day for a man to afflict his soul? Is it to bow down his head like a bulrush, and to spread out sackcloth and ashes? Would you call this a fast, and an acceptable day to the LORD?
23_58_6: Is this not the fast that I have chosen: to loose the bonds of wickedness, to undo the heavy burdens, to let the oppressed go free, and that you break every yoke?
23_58_7: Is it not to share your bread with the hungry, and that you bring to your house the poor who are cast out; when you see the naked, that you cover him, and not hide yourself from your own flesh?
23_58_8: Then your light shall break forth like the morning, your healing shall spring forth speedily, and your righteousness shall go before you; the glory of the LORD shall be your rear guard.
23_58_9: Then you shall call, and the LORD will answer; you shall cry, and He will say, ‘Here I am.’ If you take away the yoke from your midst, the pointing of the finger, and speaking wickedness,
23_58_10: If you extend your soul to the hungry and satisfy the afflicted soul, then your light shall dawn in the darkness, and your darkness shall be as the noonday.
23_58_11: The LORD will guide you continually, and satisfy your soul in drought, and strengthen your bones; you shall be like a watered garden, and like a spring of water, whose waters do not fail.
23_58_12: Those from among you shall build the old waste places; you shall raise up the foundations of many generations; and you shall be called the Repairer of the Breach, the Restorer of Streets to Dwell In.
23_58_13: If you turn away your foot from the Sabbath, from doing your pleasure on My holy day, and call the Sabbath a delight, the holy day of the LORD honorable, and shall honor Him, not doing your own ways, nor finding your own pleasure, nor speaking your own words,
23_58_14: Then you shall delight yourself in the LORD; and I will cause you to ride on the high hills of the earth, and feed you with the heritage of Jacob your father. The mouth of the LORD has spoken.”
23_59_1: Behold, the LORD’s hand is not shortened, that it cannot save; nor His ear heavy, that it cannot hear.
23_59_2: But your iniquities have separated you from your God; and your sins have hidden His face from you, so that He will not hear.
23_59_3: For your hands are defiled with blood, and your fingers with iniquity; your lips have spoken lies, your tongue has muttered perversity.
23_59_4: No one calls for justice, nor does any plead for truth. They trust in empty words and speak lies; they conceive evil and bring forth iniquity.
23_59_5: They hatch vipers’ eggs and weave the spider’s web; he who eats of their eggs dies, and from that which is crushed a viper breaks out.
23_59_6: Their webs will not become garments, nor will they cover themselves with their works; their works are works of iniquity, and the act of violence is in their hands.
23_59_7: Their feet run to evil, and they make haste to shed innocent blood; their thoughts are thoughts of iniquity; wasting and destruction are in their paths.
23_59_8: The way of peace they have not known, and there is no justice in their ways; they have made themselves crooked paths; whoever takes that way shall not know peace.
23_59_9: Therefore justice is far from us, nor does righteousness overtake us; we look for light, but there is darkness! For brightness, but we walk in blackness!
23_59_10: We grope for the wall like the blind, and we grope as if we had no eyes; we stumble at noonday as at twilight; we are as dead men in desolate places.
23_59_11: We all growl like bears, and moan sadly like doves; we look for justice, but there is none; for salvation, but it is far from us.
23_59_12: For our transgressions are multiplied before You, and our sins testify against us; for our transgressions are with us, and as for our iniquities, we know them:
23_59_13: In transgressing and lying against the LORD, and departing from our God, speaking oppression and revolt, conceiving and uttering from the heart words of falsehood.
23_59_14: Justice is turned back, and righteousness stands afar off; for truth is fallen in the street, and equity cannot enter.
23_59_15: So truth fails, And he who departs from evil makes himself a prey. Then the LORD saw it, and it displeased Him That there was no justice.
23_59_16: He saw that there was no man, And wondered that there was no intercessor;  Therefore His own arm brought salvation for Him; And His own righteousness, it sustained Him.
23_59_17: For He put on righteousness as a breastplate, And a helmet of salvation on His head; He put on the garments of vengeance for clothing, And was clad with zeal as a cloak.
23_59_18: According to their deeds, accordingly He will repay, Fury to His adversaries, Recompense to His enemies; The coastlands He will fully repay.
23_59_19: So shall they fear The name of the LORD from the west, And His glory from the rising of the sun; When the enemy comes in like a flood, The Spirit of the LORD will lift up a standard against him.
23_59_20: “The  Redeemer will come to Zion, And to those who turn from transgression in Jacob,” Says the LORD.
23_59_21: “As  for Me,” says the LORD, “this is My covenant with them: My Spirit who is upon you, and My words which I have put in your mouth, shall not depart from your mouth, nor from the mouth of your descendants, nor from the mouth of your descendants’ descendants,” says the LORD, “from this time and forevermore.”
23_60_1: Arise, shine; For your light has come! And the glory of the LORD is risen upon you.
23_60_2: For behold, the darkness shall cover the earth, And deep darkness the people; But the LORD will arise over you, And His glory will be seen upon you.
23_60_3: The Gentiles shall come to your light, And kings to the brightness of your rising.
23_60_4: “Lift  up your eyes all around, and see: They all gather together, they come to you; Your sons shall come from afar, And your daughters shall be nursed at your side.
23_60_5: Then you shall see and become radiant, And your heart shall swell with joy; Because the abundance of the sea shall be turned to you, The wealth of the Gentiles shall come to you.
23_60_6: The multitude of camels shall cover your land, The dromedaries of Midian and Ephah; All those from Sheba shall come; They shall bring gold and incense, And they shall proclaim the praises of the LORD.
23_60_7: All the flocks of Kedar shall be gathered together to you, The rams of Nebaioth shall minister to you; They shall ascend with acceptance on My altar, And I will glorify the house of My glory.
23_60_8: “Who are these who fly like a cloud, And like doves to their roosts?
23_60_9: Surely the coastlands shall wait for Me; And the ships of Tarshish will come first,  To bring your sons from afar,  Their silver and their gold with them, To the name of the LORD your God, And to the Holy One of Israel,  Because He has glorified you.
23_60_10: “The  sons of foreigners shall build up your walls,  And their kings shall minister to you; For in My wrath I struck you,  But in My favor I have had mercy on you.
23_60_11: Therefore your gates shall be open continually; They shall not be shut day or night, That men may bring to you the wealth of the Gentiles, And their kings in procession.
23_60_12: For the nation and kingdom which will not serve you shall perish, And those nations shall be utterly ruined.
23_60_13: “The  glory of Lebanon shall come to you, The cypress, the pine, and the box tree together, To beautify the place of My sanctuary; And I will make the place of My feet glorious.
23_60_14: Also the sons of those who afflicted you Shall come bowing to you, And all those who despised you shall fall prostrate at the soles of your feet; And they shall call you The City of the LORD,  Zion of the Holy One of Israel.
23_60_15: “Whereas you have been forsaken and hated, So that no one went through you, I will make you an eternal excellence, A joy of many generations.
23_60_16: You shall drink the milk of the Gentiles,  And milk the breast of kings; You shall know that I, the LORD, am your Savior And your Redeemer, the Mighty One of Jacob.
23_60_17: “Instead of bronze I will bring gold, Instead of iron I will bring silver, Instead of wood, bronze, And instead of stones, iron. I will also make your officers peace, And your magistrates righteousness.
23_60_18: Violence shall no longer be heard in your land, Neither wasting nor destruction within your borders; But you shall call your walls Salvation, And your gates Praise.
23_60_19: “The sun shall no longer be your light by day, Nor for brightness shall the moon give light to you; But the LORD will be to you an everlasting light, And your God your glory.
23_60_20: Your sun shall no longer go down, Nor shall your moon withdraw itself; For the LORD will be your everlasting light, And the days of your mourning shall be ended.
23_60_21: Also your people shall all be righteous;  They shall inherit the land forever,  The branch of My planting,  The work of My hands, That I may be glorified.
23_60_22: A little one shall become a thousand, And a small one a strong nation. I, the LORD, will hasten it in its time.”
23_61_1: “The Spirit of the Lord GOD is upon Me, Because the LORD has anointed Me To preach good tidings to the poor; He has sent Me to heal the brokenhearted, To proclaim liberty to the captives, And the opening of the prison to those who are bound;
23_61_2: To proclaim the acceptable year of the LORD, And the day of vengeance of our God;  To comfort all who mourn,
23_61_3: To console those who mourn in Zion,  To give them beauty for ashes, The oil of joy for mourning, The garment of praise for the spirit of heaviness; That they may be called trees of righteousness,  The planting of the LORD, that He may be glorified.”
23_61_4: And they shall rebuild the old ruins, They shall raise up the former desolations, And they shall repair the ruined cities, The desolations of many generations.
23_61_5: Strangers shall stand and feed your flocks, And the sons of the foreigner Shall be your plowmen and your vinedressers.
23_61_6: But you shall be named the priests of the LORD, They shall call you the servants of our God.  You shall eat the riches of the Gentiles, And in their glory you shall boast.
23_61_7: Instead of your shame you shall have double honor, And instead of confusion they shall rejoice in their portion. Therefore in their land they shall possess double; Everlasting joy shall be theirs.
23_61_8: “For I, the LORD, love justice;  I hate robbery for burnt offering; I will direct their work in truth,  And will make with them an everlasting covenant.
23_61_9: Their descendants shall be known among the Gentiles, And their offspring among the people. All who see them shall acknowledge them,  That they are the posterity whom the LORD has blessed.”
23_61_10: I will greatly rejoice in the LORD, My soul shall be joyful in my God; For He has clothed me with the garments of salvation, He has covered me with the robe of righteousness,  As a bridegroom decks himself with ornaments, And as a bride adorns herself with her jewels.
23_61_11: For as the earth brings forth its bud, As the garden causes the things that are sown in it to spring forth, So the Lord GOD will cause righteousness and praise to spring forth before all the nations.
23_62_1: For Zion’s sake I will not hold My peace, And for Jerusalem’s sake I will not rest, Until her righteousness goes forth as brightness, And her salvation as a burning torch.
23_62_2: The nations shall see your righteousness, And all kings your glory. You shall be called by a new name, Which the mouth of the LORD will name.
23_62_3: You shall also be a crown of beauty in the hand of the LORD, And a royal diadem in the hand of your God.
23_62_4: You shall no longer be termed Forsaken, Nor shall your land any more be termed Desolate; But you shall be called My Delight Is in Her, and your land Married; For the LORD delights in you, And your land shall be married.
23_62_5: For as a young man marries a young woman, So shall your sons marry you; And as the bridegroom rejoices over the bride, So shall your God rejoice over you.
23_62_6: I have set watchmen on your walls, O Jerusalem; They shall never hold their peace day or night. You who remind the LORD, do not keep silent,
23_62_7: And give Him no rest till He establishes And till He makes Jerusalem a praise in the earth.
23_62_8: The LORD has sworn by His right hand And by the arm of His strength: “Surely I will no longer give your grain As food for your enemies; And the sons of the foreigner shall not drink your new wine, For which you have labored.
23_62_9: But those who have gathered it shall eat it, And praise the LORD; Those who have brought it together shall drink it in My holy courts.”
23_62_10: Go through, Go through the gates! Prepare the way for the people; Build up, Build up the highway! Remove the stones, Lift up a banner for the peoples!
23_62_11: Indeed the LORD has proclaimed To the end of the world: “Say to the daughter of Zion, ‘Surely your salvation is coming; Behold, His reward is with Him, And His recompense before Him.’”
23_62_12: And they shall call them The Holy People, The Redeemed of the LORD; And you shall be called Sought Out, A City Not Forsaken.
